PN CN208426695-U
TI Traditional Chinese medicine supercritical extraction device, has teeth provided with outer ring of rotating disk, turntable fixed with meshed gear, rotating shaft fixed with reaction tube, and cooling device connected with pressure pump.
AU LIU X
AE JIANGSU XUZHOU PHARM VOCATIONAL COLLEGE (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019119899
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a supercritical extraction device for traditional Chinese medicine, belonging to the field of medical Chemical Industry, comprising a carbon dioxide storage tank, extracting kettle, a separating kettle and the collecting bottle; the carbon dioxide connecting with thermostat, the other end said thermostat connected with the input end of the extracting kettle; the output end of the extraction kettle connected with the input end of the separating kettle, two output ends of the separation kettle are respectively connected with the collecting bottle and cooler, the other end of the cooling device connected with the pressure pump, the other end of said pressurizing pump connected to the carbon dioxide storage tank, the extracting kettle of the utility model is provided with a reaction part by supercritical carbon dioxide, can drive the reaction tube of the rotating, through the through hole on the reaction tube can make the traditional Chinese medicine material with supercritical carbon dioxide in the reaction tube fully contact, so as to increase the utilization rate of the medicine on the basis of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and saves the resource.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN109061819-A; CN109061819-B
TI Optical cable fiber dividing box, has box body formed with air path, and carbon dioxide compression tank fixed with right side of box body, where box body stores compressed carbon dioxide to realize fire extinguishing process.
AU LI X
AE LI X (LIXX-Individual)
   HEBEI YUTIAN COMMUNICATION EQUIP CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 201900701A
AB    NOVELTY - The box has a box body (1) whose left side is formed with a through hole. A right side wall of the box body is formed with a through hole that is fixed with a carbon dioxide compression tank (6). The box body is formed with an air path (9). An end of the air passage is fixed with a power unit (4). A discharging unit (5) is fixed with a top left side of the box body. A power supply unit (2) is fixed with the box body. A sealing plate (8) is fixedly connected with a baffle (7). The carbon dioxide compression tank is fixed with a right side of the box body. The box body stores compressed carbon dioxide to realize fire extinguishing process.
   USE - Optical cable fiber dividing box.
   ADVANTAGE - The box has high extinguishing efficiency, and ensures better sun-shading effect and heat-insulation effect and reduces firing range.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side sectional view of an optical cable fiber dividing box.

   Power supply unit (2)

PN CN207980801-U
TI Indoor fresh air circulation device has circulating absorber that is provided with liquid outlet which is respectively connected to liquid inlet of water storage tank and liquid inlet of carbon dioxide absorption tank.
AU XU W
AE HANGZHOU SHENNONG HUSBANDRY CO LTD (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 201883926R
AB    NOVELTY - The invention claims an indoor circulating air device, comprising an air inlet pipeline, an air outlet pipeline, a fan, a circulating absorber, carbon dioxide absorption liquid tank and the water storage tank; the fan is installed on the air inlet pipeline; the air inlet and the air inlet pipe of the circulation absorber is connected, the air outlet is connected with an outlet pipeline of circulating absorber; the liquid outlet of the carbon dioxide absorption liquid tank is connected with the spraying device circulating in the absorber by the power pump, the liquid outlet of the water storage tank is connected with the spraying device circulating in the absorber by the power pump; outlet of the circulating absorber are respectively liquid inlet of the liquid inlet and a carbon dioxide absorption tank of the storage tank are connected. The utility model has the following beneficial effects: it can quickly and efficiently remove dust in the closed cultivation room, ammonia gas and carbon dioxide and so on, and does not influence the temperature in the cultivation, carbon dioxide absorption liquid can be recycled, energy-saving and environment-friendly.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. TI Device for delivering underwater carbon dioxide to            e.g. fish aquarium, has breather patch located on            sealed container, and attachment mechanism for securing            device in underwater environment to supplement carbon            dioxide to underwater.
AU BABCOCK G
   BABCOCK G W
   BABCOCK GARRETT W
AE BABCOCK G (BABC-Individual)

GA 201880744D
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a food substrate for feeding                organism mixed with organism in a sealed container                (18). A breather patch (8) is located on the sealed                container, where the breather patch comprises                sufficient filtering capabilities to allow carbon                dioxide molecules to leave the sealed container and                oxygen molecules to enter the sealed container and                the breather patch comprises sufficient filter                capabilities to prevent water molecules from                entering the sealed container. An attachment                mechanism secures a device in an underwater                environment to supplement carbon dioxide to the                underwater environment. A clip (2) serves as a                selectively applied sealing device to restrict air                flow to the organism through the breather                patch.
   USE - Device for delivering underwater carbon                dioxide to underwater environments e.g. fish                aquarium.
   ADVANTAGE - The device protects the device for longer                periods of use to ensure performance of a bag. The                device improves production efficiency to access                oxygen needed to survive through a breather                patch.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a                double bag of a carbon dioxide delivery device and                system installed in an aquarium.

PN CN108640115-A; CN108640115-B
TI Solid carbon dioxide forming machine comprises die which is arranged on extrusion die cavity far away from one end of first power device and second power device that drives die connection rod into reciprocating motion.
AU YAN D
   MEI L
   XU H
   CHEN K
AE UNIV XIAMEN TECHNOLOGY (UXLG-C)
GA 201881324R
AB    NOVELTY - The solid carbon dioxide forming machine comprises an extrusion cavity (20), a compression assembly, a first power device (21), a piston rod (22) and a piston (23) which are orderly connected with each other. The piston is arranged in the inner portion of the extrusion cavity. The top portion of a material storage tower (10) is provided with a feeding port. A die (30) is arranged on the extrusion cavity far away from one end of the first power device and a second power device that drives a die connection rod into reciprocating motion. The closing or opening of extruding die cavity is carried out in the blocking state, and a snowflake-shaped carbon dioxide is formed from the extrusion.
   USE - Solid carbon dioxide forming machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The working hours required for the manufacturing process is reduced and the working efficiency of extrusion is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a control method of solid carbon dioxide forming machine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic sectional view of the solid carbon dioxide forming machine.
   Material storage tower (10)
   Extrusion cavity (20)
   First power device (21)
   Piston rod (22)
   Piston (23)
   Die (30)

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a vacuum tank (1) provided with                a heat sink (13). A temperature adjusting system                comprises a heating system and a cooling system. A                vacuum-pumping system reduces or increases pressure                in the vacuum tank. The vacuum measuring system                measures inner pressure of the vacuum tank. A                cooling system cools the vacuum tank and comprises                a liquid nitrogen pipeline (2). A heating system                heats product to-be tested. Two ends of the liquid                nitrogen pipeline are respectively formed with a                liquid nitrogen inlet and a liquid nitrogen outlet                that are fixed with the heat sink. A carbon dioxide                injection system comprises a carbon dioxide                injection port and a carbon dioxide mass flow meter                (3). A carbon dioxide injection plate (5) is                located in a buffer balance plate (4).

PT P
PN WO2018085377-A1; JP2019534158-W; US2020055003-A1; US11000810-B2
TI Membrane for e.g. separating e.g. carbon dioxide            from feed gas stream, contains polymer layer comprising            oxidatively stable carrier e.g. quaternary ammonium            hydroxide carrier and quaternary ammonium fluoride            carrier, and borate additive.
AU HO W S W
   VAKHARIA V
   SALIM W
AE OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
   OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
   OHIO STATE INNOVATION FOUND (OHIS-C)
GA 201836343J
AB    NOVELTY - A membrane comprises a support layer, and a                selective polymer layer arranged on the support                layer. The selective polymer layer comprises an                oxidatively stable carrier and a borate additive                dispersed within a hydrophilic polymer matrix. The                oxidatively stable carrier is a quaternary ammonium                hydroxide carrier and/or a quaternary ammonium                fluoride carrier.
   USE - Membrane for separating gas e.g. carbon                dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, and hydrogen chloride                from feed gas stream (all claimed). Can also be                used for purification of synthesis gas to produce                high-purity hydrogen, carbon dioxide removal from                biogas, natural gas, shale gas and confined space                air, and carbon dioxide capture from flue gas in                coal- and natural gas-fired power plants for                sequestration and enhanced oil recovery.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane exhibits improved carbon                dioxide/hydrogen selectivity. The properties of the                selective polymer layer can be altered due to the                use of hydrophobic components. The catalytic effect                of the borate additives improves the carbon dioxide                permeance through hydroxide and fluoride-containing                membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) separation of a gas (g1) from a feed gas                stream; and
   (2) manufacture of membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The borate additive                  comprises a borate salt or a boric acid. The                  borate salt is chosen from sodium tetraborate,                  sodium tetrafluoroborate, potassium tetraborate,                  potassium tetrafluoroborate, lithium tetraborate,                  lithium tetrafluoroborate, cesium tetraborate,                  cesium tetrafluoroborate, sodium dihydrogen                  borate, potassium dihydrogen borate, lithium                  dihydrogen borate, sodium potassium hydrogen                  borate, sodium lithium hydrogen borate, potassium                  lithium hydrogen borate, tetramethyl quaternary                  ammonium tetraborate, tetraethyl quaternary                  ammonium tetraborate, tetrapropyl quaternary                  ammonium tetraborate, tetrabutyl quaternary                  ammonium tetraborate, tetramethyl quaternary                  ammonium tetrafluoroborate, tetraethyl quaternary                  ammonium tetrafluoroborate, tetrapropyl                  quaternary ammonium tetrafluoroborate and/or                  tetrabutyl quaternary ammonium tetrafluoroborate.                  The boric acid is tetrafluoroboric acid and/or                  boric acid. The feed gas stream comprises                  hydrogen, carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide,                  hydrogen chloride, carbon monoxide, nitrogen,                  methane, steam, sulfur oxides and/or nitrogen                  oxides. The feed gas comprises a gas (g2)                  selected from nitrogen, hydrogen and/or carbon                  monoxide. The method uses a sweep gas comprising                  a gas selected from air, steam, nitrogen, argon                  and/or helium. Preferred Properties: The gas (g1)                  exhibits a permeance of at least 50 GPU at 120                  degrees C, preferably 50-300 GPU at 120 degrees                  C. The membrane exhibits gas (g1)/gas (g2)                  selectivity of 20-300 at 120 degrees C. The feed                  gas has a temperature of at least 100 degrees                  C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The quaternary                  ammonium hydroxide carrier is a small molecule                  quaternary ammonium hydroxide and/or a quaternary                  ammonium hydroxide-containing polymer. The small                  molecule quaternary ammonium hydroxide is chosen                  from tetramethyl quaternary ammonium hydroxide,                  tetraethyl quaternary ammonium hydroxide,                  tetrapropyl quaternary ammonium hydroxide and/or                  tetrabutyl quaternary ammonium hydroxide. The                  quaternary ammonium fluoride carrier is a small                  molecule quaternary ammonium fluoride and/or a                  quaternary ammonium fluoride-containing polymer.                  The small molecule quaternary ammonium fluoride                  is chosen from tetramethyl quaternary ammonium                  fluoride, tetraethyl quaternary ammonium                  fluoride, tetrapropyl quaternary ammonium                  fluoride, tetrabutyl quaternary ammonium                  fluoride, cesium fluoride, potassium fluoride,                  sodium fluoride and/or lithium fluoride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The quaternary                  ammonium hydroxide-containing polymer is chosen                  from poly(diallyldimethyl-quaternary ammonium                  hydroxide), poly(vinylbenzyltrimethyl quaternary                  ammonium hydroxide),                  poly(2-vinyl-l-methylpyridinium hydroxide),                  poly(acrylamido-N-propyltrimethyl quaternary                  ammonium hydroxide),                  poly(2-methacryloxyethyltrimethyl quaternary                  ammonium hydroxide),                  poly(3-chloro-2-hydroxypropyl-2-methacryloxyethyldimethyl                  quaternary ammonium hydroxide) and/or their                  copolymers and blends. The quaternary ammonium                  fluoride-containing polymer is chosen from                  poly(diallyldimethyl-quaternary ammonium                  fluoride), poly(vinylbenzyltrimethyl quaternary                  ammonium fluoride),                  poly(2-vinyl-l-methylpyridinium fluoride),                  poly(acrylamido-N-propyltrimethyl quaternary                  ammonium fluoride),                  poly(2-methacryloxyethyltrimethyl quaternary                  ammonium fluoride),                  poly(3-chloro-2-hydroxypropyl-2-methacryloxyethyldimethylquaternaryammonium                  fluoride) and/or their copolymers and blends. The                  hydrophilic polymer matrix comprises a                  crosslinked hydrophilic polymer, polyvinyl                  alcohol, or polyvinyl alcohol-polysiloxane. The                  support layer comprises a gas permeable polymer                  arranged on a base. The gas permeable polymer                  comprises a polymer chosen from polyamide,                  polyimide, polypyrrole, polyester, sulfone-based                  polymer, nitrile-based polymer, polymeric                  organosilicone, fluorinated polymers, polyolefin                  and/or their copolymers and blends, preferably                  polyethersulfone or polysulfone. The membrane                  further comprises a permeable layer arranged                  between the support layer and the selective                  polymer layer. The permeable layer comprises a                  gas permeable polymer chosen from                  poly(dimethylsiloxane),                  poly(trimethylsilyl)propyne,                  poly(4-methyl-1-pentene) and/or their copolymers                  and blends.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: The base comprises a                  nonwoven fabric. The nonwoven fabric comprises                  fibers formed from a polyester.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); F03 (Weaving - including finished products (D03).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); X11 (Power Generation and High Power Machines)
MC A12-W11A; A12-W11F; F03-C02A; E05-S; E05-T; E10-A22G; E10-J02D1; E11-Q01A; E11-Q02B; E31-B03C; E31-B03D; E31-D05; E31-F01A; E31-F01B; E31-H02; E31-N05C; E31-Q; E33; H04-E; H04-F02E; H04-F03; J01-E03E; J01-H02; X11-A08

PT P
PN RU2652049-C1
TI Method of gasocyclic injection of liquid carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions in oil producing well.
AU VOLKOV V A
   BELIKOVA V G
   PROKHOROV P E
   AFANASEV S V
   TURAPIN A N
   KEROSIROV V M
AE DELTA PROM INNOVATIONS CO LTD (DELT-Non-standard)
GA 201841787M
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the production of hard-to-recover oil and gas condensate reserves by means of carbon dioxide. Method involves injecting liquid carbon dioxide into the production well. Before injecting liquid carbon dioxide into the well, a first rim of the mixture of the "Delta ARPHDI" composition is injected into the well, containing a mixture of secondary oil products of pyrolysis and hydrocarbon crude processing processes, and dimethyl carbonate in equal volume ratios in an amount of not less than 5-20% of the daily volume of produced fluid of the well. Then liquid carbon dioxide is injected at a temperature of at least Tcrit=31.1 degrees C and a pressure of at least Pcrit=7.38MPa for supercritical conditions in a formation. Then a second rim of the mixture of the "Delta ARPHDI" composition ratios in an amount of not less than 5-20% of the daily volume of the produced fluid of the well. Number of injection cycles in this sequence in the production well must be not less than 3. Impregnation of the well zone is carried out. Then, oil is extracted from the same production well.
   USE - Oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Increase in the efficiency of extraction of residual oil reserves by using carbon dioxide geologically accumulated during technological processes.
1 cl, 3 tbl, 3 ex

AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a production device                of outer wall decorative plate based on carbon                dioxide curing technology, relating to the                technical field of building material production                device, the production device comprises a stirring                module, a reverse module and a carbon dioxide                curing module. the carbon dioxide curing module                comprises a machine frame, a conveyor, a protective                cover, a carbon dioxide storage bottle, the outer                wall decorative plate needing carbon dioxide                mineralization interval is placed on the conveyor,                the conveyor is provided with an outer wall                decorative plate into the protective cover for                carbon dioxide mineralization maintenance                treatment; the stirring module is used for stirring                the concrete; the mould reversing module comprises                a mould box and a transfer mechanical hand, the                mould box is set below the stirring module, the                mould box top part with an opening, the transfer                manipulator is set on the ground. solidifying the                carbon dioxide in the protective cover, the carbon                dioxide is permanently solidified in the concrete,                shortening the concrete initial setting time of the                outer wall decorative plate, improving the                compression strength and reducing the dosage of the                cement.

TI Hydrocarbon production system comprises hydrocarbon production device which produces hydrocarbon from hydrogen and carbon dioxide in atmosphere, and heat exchanger supplies heat obtained from carbon dioxide separation device to hydrocarbon production device via heat medium.

AB    NOVELTY - Hydrocarbon production system (300) comprises a hydrocarbon production device which produces a hydrocarbon from hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the atmosphere, a carbon dioxide separation device which adsorbs and separates carbon dioxide in exhaust gas, and a heat exchanger (14) which supplies heat obtained from the carbon dioxide separation device to the hydrocarbon production device via a heat medium (10), where the hydrocarbon production device comprises a first reactor (4) which houses a storage component which is capable of storing carbon dioxide in the atmosphere and comprising at least one of a metal oxide and a metal hydroxide, and a synthetic catalyst (5) which uses hydrogen and carbon dioxide to synthesize a hydrocarbon, a first hydrogen supply unit (20) which supplies hydrogen to the first reactor, and a flow rate control unit which controls the flow rate of the atmosphere supplied to the first reactor and the flow rate of hydrogen, respectively.
   USE - Hydrocarbon production system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has improved energy efficiency and processing performance.

AU ZHU C
AE HEFEI SENKE WINE IND CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 2021B2014D
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model relates to the technical field of carbon dioxide tester, specifically to a carbon dioxide tester with high sensitivity and quality detection, comprising a first carbon dioxide tester main body, the bottom end of the first carbon dioxide tester main body is provided with a first stabilizing mechanism, the first stabilizing mechanism comprises a first groove; the inner part of the first screw hole is inserted with a second inserting rod; the inner part of the first connecting block is provided with a seventh groove. The utility model avoids the condition that the first carbon dioxide tester main body drops from the desktop to the ground, so that the use time of the first carbon dioxide tester main body is longer; it protects the normal use of the first carbon dioxide tester main body, avoids the damage of the instrument, so that the work of the user is normal; so as to improve the working efficiency of the user, making it more stable in the process of detecting, avoiding the effect of measuring the deflection of the wine bottle, so that the measurement result is more accurate.

TI Portable enhanced medicament and cosmetic formula            dispenser device, comprises carbon dioxide source and            temperature regulator that controls temperature of            carbon dioxide, and temperature regulator raises            temperature to target temperature.

   FORMICA P M
   NIEDBALA R S
   YOUNG L C
AE CRYOCONCEPTS LP (CRYO-Non-standard)
   CRYOCONCEPTS LP (CRYO-Non-standard)
GA 2021A35407
AB    NOVELTY - A portable enhanced medicament and cosmetic                formula dispenser device comprises a carbon dioxide                source and a temperature regulator that controls a                temperature of the carbon dioxide. A cartridge                having a topical substance having at least one of a                group of the medicament or the cosmetic formula for                enhancing with carbon dioxide to create carbon                dioxide enhanced topical substance, where the                device applies the enhanced substance to a client.                A base unit 101 and a hand-held dispenser                containing the source and regulator, respectively.                The temperature regulator raises or lowers the                temperature to a target temperature.
   USE - Portable enhanced medicament and cosmetic                formula dispenser device.
   ADVANTAGE - The containers are refillable in the hand-held                dispensers to allow full integration of the                containers, dispenser tips, and mixing cavities to                the dispensers. The dispenser tip is smooth for                burn cream topical substances to prevent harm to                burned skin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for enhancing delivery of a topical                substance.

   Base unit (101)
   Carbon dioxide source tank (105)

PN CN109724941-A
TI Method for detecting carbon dioxide high temperature gas concentration, involves placing test sample into neural network at different gas concentration to measure carbon dioxide gas concentration and calculate measurement error.
AU LI Y
   CHENG W
   XU G
   HAN X
   ZHANG W
AE DATANG CHANGSHAN THERMAL POWER PLANT (DATA-Non-standard)
   UNIV NORTH CHINA ELECTRIC POWER (UYHD-C)
GA 2019427912
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves absorbing a laser spectrum signal at specific frequency. An original laser spectrum signal is obtained by fitting a non- absorbed portion of the laser spectrum signal. A difference between an absorption part of a spectrum signal and the original spectrum signal is calculated. Statistics characteristic parameters are normalized as an input of a radial basis function (RBF) radial base neural network. Carbon dioxide gas concentration is normalized as an output of the RBF neural network. A carbon dioxide gas concentration measuring model is established based on the RBF neural network. A training sample of network training is selected. A test sample is placed into the trained RBF neural network at different gas concentration to measure carbon dioxide gas concentration and calculate a measurement error.
   USE - Method for detecting carbon dioxide high temperature gas concentration based on RBF neural network.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing accurate measuring of the carbon dioxide gas concentration and error analysis in a quick and easy manner.

TI System for production of methanol/methane, has            electrolysis plant producing hydrogen and oxygen from            water molecules, and closed cultivation container for            aquatic organisms forming carbon dioxide which is            combined with hydrogen from plant.
AU MIKALSEN T E
   MLKALSEN T E
AE MIKALSEN T E (MIKA-Individual)
   MLKALSEN T E (MLKA-Individual)
   MIKALSEN T E (MIKA-Individual)
   MIKALSEN T E (MIKA-Individual)
   MIKALSEN T E (MIKA-Individual)
   MIKALSEN T E (MIKA-Individual)
GA 201830854A
AB    NOVELTY - System has an electrolysis section (1)                producing hydrogen (3) and oxygen (2) from cleaving                of water molecules, and further consists of a                closed cultivation/breeding container/pond (5) for                aquatic organisms forming carbon dioxide through                their metabolism to be liberated into the water                surrounding the organisms forming carbon                dioxide-rich water. The carbon dioxide-rich water                is transported to a carbon dioxide-liberating                section. The liberated gaseous carbon dioxide is                transported to a reactor and combined with hydrogen                from the electrolysis plant for forming methanol                and/or methane.
   USE - System for producing methanol and methane                using carbon dioxide-rich water originating from                land-based plant e.g. closed fish farming plant for                cultivating/breeding aquatic organisms such as fish                e.g. carp, mullet, bass, pike and trout, fresh                water crayfish, fresh water pearl clams and fresh                water scallops, and for disinfection or                slaughtering of edible aquatic animals (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces methanol and                methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - System has an electrolysis section producing                hydrogen and oxygen from cleaving of water                molecules, and further consists of a closed                cultivation/breeding container/pond for aquatic                organisms creating carbon dioxide through their                metabolism to be liberated into the water                surrounding the organisms forming carbon                dioxide-rich water. The carbon dioxide-rich water                is transported to a carbon dioxide-liberating                section forming gaseous carbon dioxide and carbon                dioxide-poor water. The liberated gaseous carbon                dioxide is transported to a reactor and combined                with hydrogen from the electrolysis plant for                forming methanol and/or methane as an end product.                The methanol/methane is isolated and exited from                the system. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) production of methanol/methane using the                system, which involves electrolyzing water for                forming hydrogen gas and oxygen gas, isolating                carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide-rich water                originating from the animal's breathing of the                water in the plant for forming carbon dioxide to be                sequestered from the breeding water, and returning                the sequestered carbon dioxide to the plant for                forming methanol and/or methane by combining the                carbon dioxide with 3 or 4 moles of hydrogen                originating from the electrolysis of water;                and
   (2) use of carbon dioxide-rich water                originating from the plant for cultivating/breeding                aquatic organisms for isolating carbon dioxide to                be reacted with hydrogen originating from the                electrolysis of water for forming methanol and/or                methane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the system                for producing methanol/methane.
   Electrolysis (1)
   Oxygen (2)
   Hydrogen (3)
   Closed cultivation/breeding container/pond                (5)
   Fermenter (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The oxygen liberated from                  the cleaving of water molecules, is added to the                  water in the cultivation/breeding plant for                  aquatic animals, or to the carbon dioxide-poor                  water originating from the carbon                  dioxide-liberating section prior to its                  reintroduction to the cultivation/breeding plant.                  The carbon dioxide-rich water from a land-based                  breeding plant for aquatic animals, is used for                  producing methanol and/or methane by combining                  the carbon dioxide with hydrogen originating from                  electrolysis of water and further returning the                  byproduct oxygen originating from the                  electrolysis of the water to the water in the                  breeding plant. The water is recirculated after                  liberating carbon dioxide. The oxygen liberated                  from the electrolysis of the water is introduced                  into the water in the cultivation/breeding plant                  for aquatic animals. Hot water originating from                  the electrolysis of water is supplied to the                  cultivation/breeding plant.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

PT P
PN WO2021102385-A1; AU2020386642-A1; CA3162284-A1; AU2020386642-A2; KR2022121806-A; EP4061507-A1
TI System for removing carbon dioxide from carbon            dioxide laden gas mixture, comprises porous substrate            supported on carbon dioxide removal structures, and            chamber comprising inlet for connecting to carbon            dioxide-laden flue gas source.
AU EISENBERGER P
   PING E
   SAKWA-NOVAK M
   ERIC P
   MILES S
AE EISENBERGER P (EISE-Individual)
   GLOBAL THERMOSTAT OPERATIONS LLC (GLOB-Non-standard)
   EISENBERGER P (EISE-Individual)
   GLOBAL THERMOSTAT OPERATIONS LLC (GLOB-Non-standard)
   GLOBAL THERMOSTAT OPERATIONS LLC (GLOB-Non-standard)
   EISENBERGER P (EISE-Individual)
GA 202157271C
AB    NOVELTY - A system comprises a group of carbon dioxide                removal structures (21,22), an endless loop support                for the removal structures, and a sealable                regeneration box (25) at one location along the                loop support. Each group comprises a porous solid                substrate supported on the structure. Each porous                substrate has a sorbent supported within its pores.                The sorbent adsorbs or binds to carbon dioxide to                remove carbon dioxide from a gas mixture. The loop                support is arranged so as to permit movement of the                removal structures of each group along a closed                curve, and is exposed to a stream of a carbon                dioxide-laden gas mixture. The removal structure is                sealably placed in the sealable regeneration box to                seal the removal structure. The carbon dioxide                sorbed by the sorbent is stripped from the sorbent                and captured, and the sorbent is regenerated.
   USE - System for removing carbon dioxide from carbon                dioxide laden gas mixture (claimed) for fuel                burning power plants, cement manufacturing plants,                steelmaking plants and petroleum oil                refinery.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces capex per ton by increasing                overall production of carbon dioxide per direct air                capture (DAC) plant by 30-50%, reduces capital cost                of flue gas capture component by using same capital                plant with DAC, reduces energy used per ton of                carbon dioxide, has increased efficiency by                increasing amount of carbon dioxide captured during                each cycle. The method is economical due to mere                rotation of structures into the box as part of its                rotational movement thereby avoiding the vertical                movement, the loss of time and the additional                capital cost of the elevators.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A system comprises a group of carbon dioxide                removal structures (21,22), an endless loop support                for the removal structures, and a sealable                regeneration box (25) at one location along the                loop support. Each group comprises a porous solid                substrate supported on the structure. Each porous                substrate has a sorbent supported within its pores.                The sorbent adsorbs or binds to carbon dioxide to                remove carbon dioxide from a gas mixture. The loop                support is arranged so as to permit movement of the                removal structures of each group along a closed                curve, and is exposed to a stream of a carbon                dioxide-laden gas mixture. The removal structure is                sealably placed in the sealable regeneration box to                seal the removal structure. The carbon dioxide                sorbed by the sorbent is stripped from the sorbent                and captured, and the sorbent is regenerated. Each                of the removal structures supporting the porous                substrates in a position such that the sorbent is                exposed to a flow of carbon dioxide laden gas                mixture so as to allow the removal of carbon                dioxide from the gas mixture. The number of removal                structures and the number of regeneration boxes is                directly determined by a ratio of the adsorption                time (for removing carbon dioxide from the gas                mixture) and the regeneration time (for stripping                carbon dioxide from the sorbent on the porous                substrate). The adsorption time is the time to                adsorb carbon dioxide from the gas mixture on the                sorbent from a base level to a desired level on the                sorbent. The regeneration time is the time to strip                carbon dioxide from the desired level back to the                base level on the sorbent. The system further                comprises a chamber at a location along the track.                The chamber is designed to sealingly receive each                capture structure as it moves along the track. The                chamber comprises an inlet designed to be connected                to a source of carbon dioxide-laden flue gas, and                an outlet for carbon dioxide-lean flue gas after at                least a portion of carbon dioxide is adsorbed by                the sorbent. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                removal of carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide laden                gas mixture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a top view of the                system.
   Capture structures (21,22)
   Induced draft fans (23,26)
   Regeneration boxes (25,27)
   Flue gas adsorption housings                (121,122,221,222)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The sorbent is a                  polymeric amine.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN114917175-A
TI Anti-aging composition in cosmetics, comprises            pearl fermentation liquid and essential oil, where            essential oil is mixture of Angelica essential oil and            licorice essential oil and rest are cosmetic bases,            pearl fermentation liquid is obtained by pulverizing            pearls to obtain ultra-fine pearl powder.
AU YAN G
   HU M
   WANG J
   YANG A
   MIAO X
AE ZHEJIANG OSM GROUP DEQING BIOTECH CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   FANSHENLAN TECHNOLOGY SHANGHAI CO LTD (FANS-Non-standard)
GA 2022B0357P
AB    NOVELTY - Anti-aging composition comprises 10 wt.% pearl                fermentation liquid and 3 wt.% essential oil, where                the essential oil is the mixture of Angelica                essential oil and licorice essential oil in the                ratio of 1:1 and the rest are cosmetic bases. The                pearl fermentation liquid is obtained by                pulverizing the pearls to obtain ultra-fine pearl                powder, mixing and stirring pearl powder with water                to make a mixed solution, passing excess carbon                dioxide into the bottom of the mixed solution or                adding 10-30 wt.% weak acid solution to dissolve,                inserting 1-10 wt.% microbial expansion bacterial                liquid into the obtained supernatant, placing in a                fermenter at 28-37&#8451;, and cultivating at                100-200 rpm for 24-36 hours. The culture solution                is inactivated, filtered using filter film to                obtain sterile filtrate, mixed the filtrate with                the freeze-drying protective agent, freeze-dried,                and harvested the freeze-dried powder with a                moisture content of 2-5%.
   USE - Anti-aging composition used in cosmetics                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The anti-aging composition has effect of                delaying aging, improves skin antioxidant capacity,                reduce skin lipofuscin content, scavenge free                radicals in the body and/or reduce lipid                peroxidative damage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Anti-aging composition comprises 10 wt.% pearl                fermentation liquid and 3 wt.% essential oil, where                the essential oil is the mixture of Angelica                essential oil and licorice essential oil in the                ratio of 1:1 and the rest are cosmetic bases. The                pearl fermentation liquid is obtained by                pulverizing the pearls to obtain ultra-fine pearl                powder, mixing and stirring pearl powder with water                to make a mixed solution, passing excess carbon                dioxide into the bottom of the mixed solution or                adding 10-30 wt.% weak acid solution to dissolve,                inserting 1-10 wt.% microbial expansion bacterial                liquid into the obtained supernatant, placing in a                fermenter at 28-37&#8451;, and cultivating at                100-200 rpm for 24-36 hours. The culture solution                is inactivated, filtered using filter film to                obtain sterile filtrate, mixed the filtrate with                the freeze-drying protective agent, freeze-dried,                and harvested the freeze-dried powder with a                moisture content of 2-5%. The Angelica essential                oil is the volatile oil obtained by supercritical                carbon dioxide extraction and molecular                distillation extraction with Angelica sinensis as                raw material. The licorice essential oil is a                volatile oil obtained by extracting licorice                (Glycyrrhiza uralensis Fisch.) as a raw material                through supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The weak acid solution                  is selected from acetic acid, citric acid, lactic                  acid, malic acid. The microorganism is selected                  from brewer's yeast, grape juice yeast or                  Lactobacillus rhamnosus, Bacillus subtilis and                  Bacillus licheniformis. The weak acid solution is                  lactic acid, acetic acid or citric acid, and the                  microorganism is Saccharomyces cerevisiae,                  Bacillus subtilis or Bacillus licheniformis.                  Preferred Conditions: The pearl fermentation                  liquid is prepared by cleaning the pearls, drying                  in the sun and pulverize them, and passing them                  through a jet mill to obtain ultra-fine pearl                  powder, mixing 1kg pearl powder with deionized                  water at a mass ratio of 1:5-20 and stirring at                  30-70&#8451; through an internal stirring                  rotor or stirring blade at a speed of 50-100 rpm,                  passing excess carbon dioxide into the bottom of                  the mixed solution for 0.5-1.5 hours or adding                  10-30 wt.% weak acid solution by weight to                  dissolve. The supernatant obtained by filtration                  is inserted into 1-10 wt.% microbial expansion                  culture liquid, placed in a fermenter at                  28-37&#8451;, and cultured at 100-200 rpm for                  24-36 hours after inactivation stirred at                  85-100&#8451; for 20-50 minutes, press                  filtered through a 0.22 &#956; filter film to                  obtain sterile filtrate. The filtrate and the                  freeze-drying protective agent are mixed in a                  ratio of 100:0.5-2 (volume/mass), pre-frozen in                  liquid nitrogen or -70-85&#8451;, and then                  placed in a vacuum freeze-drying chamber for                  freeze-drying for 12-20 hours, the freeze-dried                  powder with moisture content of 2-5% is finally                  harvested. The lyophilized powder is canned in a                  brown vial for storage at 1-10&#8451;. The                  cosmetic base is a cream base. The angelica                  essential oil is obtained by pulverizing the                  Chinese medicinal material Angelica sinensis                  according to 10-20 mesh, then extracting,                  concentrating and vacuum drying with ethanol                  solution to prepare the infusion of Angelica                  sinensis. The ethanol is used to extract twice                  according to the ratio of solid to liquid ratio                  of 1:3.5. The first extraction is 3.5 hours, and                  the second extraction is 2.5 hours. The weighed                  medicinal powder is placed in an emery cloth bag,                  placed into the extractor, sealed the top cover,                  turned on the power of the thermostat, opened the                  valve of the carbon dioxide cylinder, and let the                  gas pass through the dryer, cooled condenser,                  plunger pump along the pipeline, entered the                  pressurized buffer bottle after the pressure of                  the pressurized buffer bottle greatly exceeds the                  experimental pressure, opened the valve V1 to                  increase the pressure of the extractor to the                  experimental pressure, closed the valve V1,                  pre-infiltered for 10-15 minutes, and extracted                  the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, separator                  I pressure is 20-25Mpa, temperature of                  35-50&#8451;, separator II pressure is                  4.5-5.5MPa, temperature of 45&#8451;, carbon                  dioxide flow is 35kg/hours, extraction time is                  1.5-2 hours, the obtained dark green angelica                  essential oil paste. The Angelica paste is used                  as raw material, extracted with 95.0% ethanol,                  the ratio of Angelica paste and ethanol as 1:9,                  filtered to remove portion of the wax, let the                  filtrate stand overnight, filtered again the next                  day. The filtrate is placed in under the                  condition of 50&#8451;, vacuum degree                  -0.015MPa, ethanol is extracted by vacuum, and                  the dark brown substance obtained is Angelica                  sinensis. The obtained Angelica sinensis is                  refined by molecular distillation process, where                  the distillation temperature is 115&#8451;,                  vacuum degree is -0.015 mPa, Angelica sinensis                  flux of 1.0ml/minutes, scraping speed of                  380r/minutes. The licorice essential oil is                  prepared by pulverizing the Chinese medicinal                  material licorice into coarse powder that has                  passed through a 10-20 mesh sieve, passing                  through water once, drying in the sun,                  pulverizing 75 meshes for use, and packing the                  weighed licorice in an emery cloth bag. The                  coarse powder of medicinal materials is put into                  the extractor, the top cover is sealed, the power                  supply of the constant temperature tank is turned                  on, and the valve of the carbon dioxide cylinder                  is opened, so that the gas passes through the                  desiccator along the pipeline, the cooling                  condenser, the plunger pump, and enters the                  pressurized buffer bottle after the pressure of                  the pressurized buffer bottle greatly exceeds the                  experimental pressure, opened the valve V1 to                  increase the pressure of the extractor to the                  experimental pressure, closed the valve V1,                  pre-infiltration for 10-15 minutes, and                  supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction.                  The carbon dioxide fluid flow rate is 1L/minutes,                  the extraction temperature is 30-35&#8451;,                  the extraction pressure is 35-40MPa, the                  separation temperature is 50-60&#8451;, the                  separation pressure is 4.5-5.5MPa, and the                  extraction time is 2-3 hours to obtain a paste                  with licorice essential oil. The licorice paste                  is used as raw material, extracted with 95.0%                  ethanol, according to the weight ratio of                  licorice paste and ethanol as 1:9, filtered to                  remove portion of the wax, let the filtrate stand                  overnight, filtered again the next day, and                  filtered the filtrate under the conditions of                  50&#8451; and vacuum degree of -0.015MPa. The                  ethanol is extracted by vacuum to obtain                  product.

PN CN114128821-A
TI Extracting of tea polyphenol, comprises e.g.            adding chitosan flocculation liquid to enzymatic            hydrolysis treatment solution, stirring and separating            to obtain primary tea polyphenol filtrate, adding            ethanol, and stirring and separating to obtain            secondary tea polyphenol filtrate.
AU ZHAO Y
   ZHANG X
   YANG S
   CHEN G
AE WUXI JIANGDA BAITAI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (WUXI-Non-standard)
GA 202238812W
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting of tea polyphenol, comprises                carrying enzymatic hydrolysis treatment by: adding                tea powder to distilled water, heating, keeping                temperature, adding composite wall-breaking enzyme                to solution, carrying out enzymolysis treatment on                the tea powder, carrying the enzymatic hydrolysis                to obtain an enzymatic hydrolysis treatment                solution; adding chitosan flocculation liquid to                the enzymatic hydrolysis treatment solution,                stirring and separating to obtain primary tea                polyphenol filtrate; adding ethanol to the primary                tea polyphenol filtrate, stirring and separating to                obtain secondary tea polyphenol filtrate; using                carbon dioxide extractant to extract caffeine from                the secondary tea polyphenol filtrate, dynamically                extracting caffeine, separating the carbon dioxide                extractant, and remaining liquid is the refined tea                polyphenol filtrate; concentrating and drying the                purified tea polyphenol filtrate to obtain tea                polyphenol powder.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting of tea                polyphenol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: obtains tea polyphenol powder with                high purity and high extraction rate through                concentration and drying; is simple, and suitable                for large scale industrial production; has high                extraction rate of tea polyphenols; and can fully                recovers high-value by-products in production                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting of tea polyphenol, comprises                carrying enzymatic hydrolysis treatment by: adding                tea powder to distilled water, the weight ratio of                the tea powder to the distilled water is 1:(10-20),                heating to 20-60degreesC, keeping temperature,                adding composite wall-breaking enzyme to solution,                carrying out enzymolysis treatment on the tea                powder, the weight of the composite wall-breaking                enzyme is 0.1-0.3 wt.% weight of tea powder,                carrying the enzymatic hydrolysis for 0.5-3 hours                under condition of pH = 4-7 to obtain an enzymatic                hydrolysis treatment solution; adding chitosan                flocculation liquid to the enzymatic hydrolysis                treatment solution, the chitosan content in the                chitosan flocculating liquid is 0.1-0.5 wt.%, the                weight ratio of chitosan flocculation liquid to tea                powder is (1-3):1, stirring and separating to                obtain a primary tea polyphenol filtrate; adding                ethanol to the primary tea polyphenol filtrate,                weight ratio of the ethanol to the tea powder is                (0.1-0.5):1, stirring and separating to obtain                secondary tea polyphenol filtrate; using carbon                dioxide extractant to extract caffeine from the                secondary tea polyphenol filtrate, dynamically                extracting caffeine under the conditions of                20-40degreesC and 25-29MPa for 50-70 minutes,                separating the carbon dioxide extractant, and                remaining liquid is the refined tea polyphenol                filtrate; concentrating and drying the purified tea                polyphenol filtrate to obtain tea polyphenol                powder.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The composite                  wall-breaking enzyme is cellulase, pectinase and                  neutral protease in the weight ratio of                  1:(1-2):(2-3). The crushed particle size is 5-100                  mum. Preferred Method: In the enzymatic                  hydrolysis treatment step, heating to                  45-50degreesC. In enzymatic hydrolysis treatment                  step, enzymatic hydrolysis treatment time is                  1-1.5 hours. The pH value in the enzymatic                  hydrolysis treatment step is 6. In the                  concentrating and drying step, using ethyl                  acetate as extractant for extraction, and                  concentrating under reduced pressure under vacuum                  conditions. Before the enzymatic hydrolysis                  treatment step, the tea raw material is crushed                  at -70--10degreesC to obtain tea powder. The                  temperature during the crushing is -30 to                  -20degreesC.

PN US2022062864-A1
TI Synthesizing catalyst comprises adding catalytic metal comprising tin, bismuth, or indium in its metallic form to molten lithium metal, atomically dispersing catalytic metal in molten lithium and forming lithium catalytic metal-solid.
AU XU H
   XU T
   LIU D
AE UCHICAGO ARGONNE LLC (UYCG-C)
GA 2022312724
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing (M1) a catalyst comprises: adding a catalytic metal comprising tin, bismuth, or indium in its metallic form to molten lithium metal; atomically dispersing the catalytic metal in the molten lithium; forming a lithium catalytic metal-solid; converting a portion of lithium in the lithium catalytic metal solid to lithium hydroxide forming a catalytic metal-lithium hydroxide solid; mixing the catalytic metal-lithium hydroxide solid with a conductive support material to form a mixture, where the conductive support material is carbonaceous with a porous network and having catalytic metal decorated throughout the porous network; removing lithium hydroxide from the mixture leaving a mixture of catalytic metal and the conductive support material; and drying the mixture of catalytic metal and the conductive support material to produce the catalyst containing the catalytic metal atomically dispersed over the conductive support material.
   USE - The methods are useful for synthesizing a catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods provide catalyst that has a Faradaic efficiency of at least 90% in converting of carbon dioxide to formate at -0.6 V (RHE), a Faradaic efficiency of at least 90% in converting of carbon dioxide to acetic acid at -0.6 V (RHE), a Faradaic efficiency of at least 90% in converting of carbon dioxide to ethanol at 0.5 V (RHE), high selectivity and low over potential toward to the electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to chemicals.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for synthesizing (M2) a catalyst, comprising adding at least one catalytic material comprising a metal M comprising e.g. lithium, sodium, aluminum, potassium, calcium, copper, zinc, silver, platinum, gold, or bismuth, or a metal oxide comprising oxides of a metal M' comprising e.g. titanium, iron, cobalt, nickel, zinc, tungsten or rhenium to molten lithium metal, atomically dispersing the catalytic material in the molten lithium, forming a lithium catalytic metal-solid, converting a portion of lithium in the lithium catalytic metal solid to lithium hydroxide forming a catalytic metal-lithium hydroxide solid, mixing the catalytic metal-lithium hydroxide solid with a conductive support material to form a mixture, where the conductive support material is carbonaceous with a porous network and having catalytic metal decorated throughout the porous network, removing lithium hydroxide from the mixture leaving a mixture of catalytic metal and the conductive support material and drying the mixture of catalytic metal and the conductive support material to produce the catalyst containing the catalytic metal atomically dispersed over the conductive support material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The converting the portion of the lithium-catalytic metal solid to catalytic metal-lithium hydroxide solid comprises reacting lithium in the lithium catalytic metal solid with moist air. The method (M1) further comprises mixing the catalytic metal-lithium hydroxide solid with the conductive support material using a mechanical method. The removing the metal hydroxide comprises a drop-wise washing of the catalytic metal lithium metal hydroxide solid with water thus removing lithium. The washing comprises forming an alkaline water solution and modifying the carbonaceous support with oxygenated species serving as anchoring sites for the catalytic metal.Preferred Components: The molten lithium metal is less than or equal to300degrees Celsius. The catalyst metal is a 0.1-55 wt.% indium, a 0.2-50 wt % bismuth, and 0.1-55 wt.% tin. The final catalyst has a 0.1-55 wt.% of tin. The catalytic metal is a bimetallic compound.
   EXAMPLE - 0.29 Lithium was added into a nickel crucible and heated to 220degrees Celsius to form molten lithium. Then, 0.042 mmol (~5 mg) of tin foil were added into the molten lithium. An ultrasonic homogenizer was used to ensure a homogeneous dispersion of the bulk tin foil into single atoms and prevent them from precipitation and reaggregation in the molten lithium while the molten lithium was maintained at 220degrees Celsius for 2 hours. The melted metals were then quickly poured onto a clean 316-stainless steel plates to quench to solid solution. After cooling, the tin-lithium amalgam was taken out from the glovebox in ambience and cut into small pieces, which were then slowly converted from lithium to lithium hydroxide under humidified air at ambient temperature. The obtained tin in lithium hydroxide powder was homogeneously mixed with 2.5 g carbon black support by grinding the mixture with an agate mortar and pestle. The resulting mixture was filtered with copious amount of de-ionized water to leach off lithium hydroxide. Finally, the filtered carbon-tin mixture was dried under vacuum at 60degrees Celsius for 24 hours to form 0.2 wt.% tin over carbon.

TI Cold pressing processing of selenium-enriched tea oil comprises crushing and processing high quality selenium-enriched Camellia oleifera seed, spiral-cold pressing, separating cold pressed oil and tea bran, extracting selenium-enriched tea oil and filtering.
AU YUAN C
AE HUNAN KANGDUOLI GREASE CO LTD (HUNA-Non-standard)
GA 202223266R
AB    NOVELTY - Cold pressing processing of selenium-enriched tea oil comprises (a) providing high quality selenium-enriched Camellia oleifera seed, where the high quality selenium-enriched Camellia oleifera seed has high oil content characteristic, (b) crushing and processing the high quality selenium-enriched Camellia oleifera seed, (c) spiral-cold pressing the crushed high quality selenium-enriched Camellia oleifera seed, separating the cold pressed oil and tea bran, where in the spiral-cold pressing process, the cold pressing temperature is less than 60degrees Celsius, (d) extracting the selenium-enriched tea oil in the tea bran slag based on supercritical extraction method, obtaining extracting oil and (e) respectively filtering the cold pressed oil and the extracting oil, where the obtained oil liquid is respectively as selenium-enriched tea oil A and selenium-enriched tea oil B.
   USE - The method is useful for cold pressing processing of selenium-enriched tea oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has high oil yield and good economic benefits; fully utilizes the tea oil in the tea seeds based on supercritical extraction; reduces the loss of raw materials; improves the processing efficiency; does not have solvent residue, and extracts oil with high safety and quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cold pressing processing of selenium-enriched tea oil comprises (a) providing high quality selenium-enriched Camellia oleifera seed, where the high quality selenium-enriched Camellia oleifera seed has high oil content characteristic, (b) crushing and processing the high quality selenium-enriched Camellia oleifera seed, (c) spiral-cold pressing the crushed high quality selenium-enriched Camellia oleifera seed, separating the cold pressed oil and tea bran, where in the spiral-cold pressing process, the cold pressing temperature is less than 60degrees Celsius, (d) extracting the selenium-enriched tea oil in the tea bran slag based on supercritical extraction method, obtaining extracting oil, where the specific operation of the supercritical extraction method is taking the tea bran sheet slag, heating and crushing and sieving, obtaining the extracting raw material, providing an extraction kettle, separating kettle and carbon dioxide fluid, liquefying the carbon dioxide gas to obtain the carbon dioxide fluid, taking the extracting raw material in the extracting kettle, removing the air in the extracting kettle for sealing, collecting the extraction product, placing the extraction product in the separation kettle, heating to remove carbon dioxide to obtain the extraction oil and (e) respectively filtering the cold pressed oil and the extracting oil, where the obtained oil liquid is respectively as selenium-enriched tea oil A and selenium-enriched tea oil B.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method of obtaining the high quality selenium-enriched tea seed comprises selecting oil tea variety with oil content not less than 50%, planting in the selenium-enriched area, collecting Camellia oleifera seed, stacking and retting the Camellia oleifera seed for 6-7 days, drying for 4-5 days until the Camellia oleifera shell is separated from the seed, drying the seed, keeping the water content more than 15%, removing impurities in the selenium-enriched Camellia oleifera seed to obtain pure Camellia oleifera seed. The environment standard of the selenium-enriched region is that the altitude is more than 200 m, and the pH value of the soil is 5-6.5. In the process of stacking and retting, mixing the oil tea seed with lime to carry out retting treatment to realize ripening. The spiral-cold pressing process is carried out by spiral pressing, grinding the selenium-enriched oil-tea seed into tea bran sheet, and squeezing out the tea oil. The low temperature condition is that the temperature is less than 8degrees Celsius.

TI Producing liquid fuel and/or chemicals from            carbonaceous material using recycled conditioned            syngas, involves providing steam reformer, introducing            carbonaceous material into steam reformer along with            superheated steam.
AU ATTWOOD B C
   MILLER J K
   LEO D M
   BURCIAGA D A
   NEWPORT D G
   CHANDRAN R
AE THERMOCHEM RECOVERY INT INC (THER-Non-standard)
   THERMOCHEM RECOVERY INT INC (THER-Non-standard)
GA 2021A6678Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing liquid fuel and/or chemicals from a                carbonaceous material, involves providing a steam                reformer having a multiple of pulse combustion heat                exchangers in thermal contact with a particulate                heat transfer material; introducing carbonaceous                material into the steam reformer along with                superheated steam; combusting a first portion of                conditioned syngas in the pulse combustion heat                exchangers to indirectly heat the particulate heat                transfer material and cause the carbonaceous                material to react with the superheated steam and                produce a first reactor product gas which comprises                carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and                hydrocarbons; reacting a second portion of                conditioned syngas and an oxygen-containing gas to                produce a combustion stream in a hydrocarbon                reformer, comprising carbon dioxide and excess                oxygen-containing gas, and reacting the combustion                stream with a portion of the first reactor product                gas to generate additional hydrogen and carbon                monoxide.
   USE - Method for producing liquid fuel and/or                chemicals from a carbonaceous material using                recycled conditioned syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces syngas, reduces greenhouse                gas emissions, reduces air and water pollution,                promote economic development, expands energy supply                options, increases energy security, decreases                dependence on imported oil, and strengthen rural                economies.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing liquid fuel and/or chemicals from a                carbonaceous material, involves providing a steam                reformer having a multiple of pulse combustion heat                exchangers in thermal contact with a particulate                heat transfer material; introducing carbonaceous                material into the steam reformer along with                superheated steam; combusting a first portion of                conditioned syngas in the pulse combustion heat                exchangers to indirectly heat the particulate heat                transfer material and cause the carbonaceous                material to react with the superheated steam and                produce a first reactor product gas which comprises                carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and                hydrocarbons; reacting a second portion of                conditioned syngas and an oxygen-containing gas to                produce a combustion stream in a hydrocarbon                reformer, comprising carbon dioxide and excess                oxygen-containing gas, and reacting the combustion                stream with a portion of the first reactor product                gas to generate additional hydrogen and carbon                monoxide, to produce an improved syngas having a                higher concentration of carbon monoxide and a lower                concentration of hydrocarbons than in the first                reactor product gas; subjecting the improved syngas                to one or more gas clean-up steps to produce new                conditioned syngas; transferring a first portion of                the new conditioned syngas to a synthesis system to                produce tail gas and from the group consisting of                liquid fuel and chemicals; and recycling a second                portion of the new conditioned syngas for use as                the first and second portion of conditioned                syngas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The upgraded liquid                  fuels comprises jet fuel, gasoline and diesel.                  The synthesis system produces one or more liquid                  fuels selected from mixed alcohols, dimethyl                  ether, and fischer-tropsch products. The                  synthesis system contains catalyst. The synthesis                  system comprises a bioreactor containing                  microorganisms; and the method comprises                  producing ethanol. The synthesis system comprises                  a bioreactor containing microorganisms; and the                  method comprises producing one or more chemicals                  from the group consisting of 3-hydroxypropionate,                  mevalonate, mevalonic acid, isoprene, aromatics,                  benzoate (p-hydroxyl, 2-amino, dihydroxy),                  salicylate, 1-propanol, 1,2-propanediol,                  (R)-1,2-propanediol, (S)-1,2-propanediol, mixed                  isomers of 1,2-propanediol, acetoin, methyl ethyl                  ketone, branched-chain amino acids, valine,                  leucine, isoleucine, succinate, lactate,                  2,3-butanediol, (R,R)-2,3-butanediol,                  meso-2,3-butanediol, mixed isomers of                  2,3-butanediol, citramalate, 1,3-butanediol,                  (R)-1,3-butanediol, (S)-1,3-butanediol, mixed                  isomers of 1,3-butanediol, 3-hydroxybutyrate,                  (R)-3-hydroxybutyrate, (S)-3-hydroxybutyrate,                  mixed isomers of 3-hydroxybutyrate, butyrate,                  acetone, isopropanol, acetate, 1,3-butadiene,                  biopolymers, isobutene, and long chain alcohols.                  The hydrocarbon reformer comprises a down-flow,                  non-catalytic pressure vessel.Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises compressing the new conditioned syngas,                  before transferring its first portion to the                  synthesis system and recycling a second portion                  of the new conditioned syngas. The method further                  comprises reacting, in a carbon trim cell, char                  present in the first reactor product gas and                  transferred from the steam reformer, with steam                  and an oxygen-containing gas to form a second                  reactor product gas; and, in the hydrocarbon                  reformer, reacting a second portion of                  conditioned syngas and an oxygen-containing gas                  with portion of the first reactor product gas,                  and also with a portion of the second reactor                  product gas to produce the improved syngas;                  subjecting the improved syngas to a first gas                  clean-up process to produce a primary conditioned                  syngas; compressing the primary conditioned                  syngas; and subjecting the primary conditioned                  syngas to a second gas clean-up process to form a                  secondary conditioned syngas; where the new                  conditioned syngas comprises the secondary                  conditioned syngas; removing, from the primary                  conditioned syngas, one or more contaminants                  selected from the group consisting of carbonyl                  sulfide, hydrogen sulfide, mercury, arsenic, lead                  and cadmium, to produce the secondary conditioned                  syngas; transferring a third portion of the new                  conditioned syngas to a hydrogen separation                  system to produce hydrogen and a first off-gas                  stream; transferring a portion of the first                  off-gas stream to a gas turbine system to produce                  electricity; transferring to the pulse combustion                  heat exchangers, both the first portion of                  conditioned syngas and a portion of the first                  off-gas stream, and combusting; transferring to                  the hydrocarbon reformer, both the second portion                  of conditioned syngas and a portion of the first                  off-gas stream, to form the improved                  syngas.

PT P
PN CN113045798-A; CN113045798-B
TI Asphalt recovery method for styrene butadiene styrene (SBS) modified asphalt mixture, by placing SBS modified asphalt mixture in container, soaking SBS modified asphalt mixture in mixed solvent, leaving still, performing high-speed centrifugation, when solvent starts to be distilled, and aerating.
AU ZHANG W
   HE W
   GUAN Y
AE SINOROAD JIANGSU TESTING TECHNOLOGY CO (SINO-Non-standard)
   SINOROAD TESTING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 2021786252
AB    NOVELTY - Asphalt recovery method for styrene butadiene styrene (SBS) modified asphalt mixture, involves (i) placing SBS modified asphalt mixture in a container, using trichloroethylene and tetrahydrofuran as a mixed solvent to soak SBS modified asphalt mixture, and stirring every 1-2 hours to obtain modified asphalt solution, (ii) leaving still modified asphalt solution for 12 hours, (iii) taking middle and upper layer solution of the modified asphalt solution obtained in step (ii) and performing high-speed centrifugation, (iv) pouring centrifuged solution to a distillation flask, assembling a test instrument according to Absen method, adjusting temperature of a constant temperature oil bath to 155 degrees C, and introducing carbon dioxide gas, and (v) when the mixed solvent starts to be distilled out in drops, setting carbon dioxide aeration rate to 1600 plus minus 50 ml/minute and aerating for 40 minutes, stopping heating, and pouring the recycled asphalt in the distillation flask to a container.
   USE - Asphalt recovery method for SBS modified asphalt mixture.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Asphalt recovery method for styrene butadiene styrene (SBS) modified asphalt mixture, involves (i) placing SBS modified asphalt mixture in a container, using trichloroethylene and tetrahydrofuran at a volume ratio of (10-11):1 as a mixed solvent to soak the SBS modified asphalt mixture at room temperature for 12-15 hours, so that the liquid level of the mixed solvent is higher than that of SBS and the highest point of the modified asphalt mixture is 2-4 cm, and stirring every 1-2 hours to obtain a modified asphalt solution, (ii) leaving still the modified asphalt solution obtained in step (i) for 12 hours, (iii) taking the middle and upper layer solution of the modified asphalt solution obtained in step (ii) and performing high-speed centrifugation at 3000 rpm for 30 minutes, (iv) pouring the centrifuged solution to a distillation flask, assembling a test instrument according to Absen method, adjusting the temperature of a constant temperature oil bath to 155 degrees C, and introducing carbon dioxide gas at a ventilation rate of 1000 ml/minute until the mixed solvent is distilled out in drops, and (v) when the mixed solvent starts to be distilled out in drops, setting the carbon dioxide aeration rate to 1600 plus minus 50 ml/minute and aerating for 40 minutes, stopping heating, and pouring the recycled asphalt in the distillation flask to a container.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves placing the SBS modified asphalt mixture in front of the container, and heating the SBS modified asphalt mixture to a loose state. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the liquid level of the mixed solvent is 2 cm higher than the highest point of the SBS modified asphalt mixture, and the soaking of the SBS modified asphalt mixture is carried out for 12 hours, during which stirring is carried out for every 1 hour. Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of trichloroethylene and tetrahydrofuran in step (i) is 10:1.
