PN WO2021058508-A1; CN114450085-A; CA3150148-A1; KR2022069988-A; EP4034297-A1; IN202217023127-A; BR112022005125-A2
TI Catalytic system useful for granules, moldings or            extrudates, comprises transition metal compound on a            solid carrier e.g. surface-reacted calcium carbonate,            and nickel, ruthenium, gold, palladium, platinum, iron            and copper.
AU FTOUNI J
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 202133013Q
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytic system (S1) comprises transition                metal compound on a solid carrier, where (a) the                solid carrier is surface-reacted calcium carbonate,                the surface-reacted calcium carbonate is reaction                product of natural ground calcium carbonate or                precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide                and at least one H3O+ ion donors, the carbon                dioxide is formed in situ by the H3O+ ion donors                treatment and/or is supplied from external source,                and (b) the transition metal compound consisting of                nickel, ruthenium, gold, palladium, platinum, iron                and/or copper, and the content of transition metal                element on the surface of the solid carrier is                0.1-30 wt.% based on the dry weight of the solid                carrier.
   USE - The catalytic system is useful for granules,                moldings or extrudates (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) method (M1) for manufacturing catalytic                system comprising transition metal compound on a                solid carrier, comprising (a) providing at least                one solid carrier, where the solid carrier is                surface-reacted calcium carbonate, the                surface-reacted calcium carbonate is reaction                product of natural ground calcium carbonate or                precipitated calcium carbonate with carbon dioxide                and at least one H3O+ ion donors, the carbon                dioxide is formed in situ by the H3O+ ion donors                treatment and/or is supplied from an external                source, (b) providing at least one transition metal                reagent comprising nickel ions, ruthenium ions,                gold ions, palladium ions, platinum ions, iron ions                and/or copper ions, content of ions is 0.1-30 wt.%                based on the dry weight of the solid carrier, (c)                contacting the at least one step (a) solid carrier                and step (b) transition metal reagent provided to                obtain mixture comprising solid carrier and                transition metal reagent, (d) calcining the step                (c) mixture at 250-500 degrees C, and (e) reducing                the obtained calcined catalytic system under                hydrogen atmosphere at 100-500 degrees C to obtain                catalytic system comprising transition metal                compound on the solid carrier, where the transition                metal compound consisting of nickel, ruthenium,                gold, palladium, platinum, iron and/or                copper;
   (2) use of catalytic system comprising (A)                providing at least one reactant, (B) providing                catalytic system, (C) subjecting provided at least                one reactant to chemical reaction under air, oxygen                atmosphere, hydrogen atmosphere or inert atmosphere                at 75-300 degrees C in presence of catalytic                system; and
   (3) use of solid carrier loaded with a                transition metal compound as catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the catalytic                  system (S1): the natural ground calcium carbonate                  consisting of marble, chalk and/or limestone or                  the precipitated calcium carbonate consisting of                  precipitated calcium carbonates having                  aragonitic, vateritic and/or calcitic crystal                  form; the at least one H3O+ ion donor consisting                  of hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous                  acid, phosphoric acid, citric acid, oxalic acid,                  an acidic salt, acetic acid and/or formic acid,                  preferably at least one H3O+ ion donor consisting                  of hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous                  acid, phosphoric acid, oxalic acid, dihydrogen                  phosphate, being at least partially neutralised                  by a cation e.g. lithium, sodium and/or                  potassium, hydrogenphosphate being at least                  partially neutralised by cation selected from                  lithium (I) ion, sodium (I) ion, potassium (I)                  ion, magnesium (II) ion and/or calcium (II) ion,                  preferably the at least one H3O+ ion donor                  consisting of hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid,                  sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid and/or oxalic                  acid, preferably phosphoric acid; the solid                  carrier has: (i) volume median particle size                  (d5o) of 0.1-75 mu m, preferably 1.5-15 mu m,                  and/or (ii) a volume top cut particle size (d98)                  of 0.2-150 mu m, preferably 3-30 mu m, and/or                  (iii) a specific surface area of 10-200 m2/g,                  preferably 30-100 m2/g, measured using nitrogen                  and the Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) method; the                  transition metal compound consisting of nickel,                  ruthenium, gold, iron and/or copper, preferably                  nickel, ruthenium and/or gold; and the content of                  the transition metal element on the surface of                  the solid carrier is 0.25-25 wt.%, preferably                  2.5-5 wt.% based on the dry weight of the solid                  carrier. In the method (M1): the transition metal                  reagent consisting of ammonium nickel(II)                  sulfate, nickel(II) acetate, nickel(II) bromide,                  nickel(II) chloride, nickel(II) fluoride,                  nickel(II) hydroxide, nickel(II) iodide, nickel                  nitrate, nickel(II) perchlorate, nickel                  sulfamate, nickel(II) sulfate, potassium                  nickel(IV) paraperiodate, potassium tetracyano                  nickelate, hexaammineruthenium(II) chloride,                  hexaammineruthenium(III) chloride,                  pentaamine(nitrogen)ruthenium(II) chloride,                  ruthenium(III) chloride, ruthenium(III) nitrosyl                  nitrate, ruthenium(III) iodide, ruthenium In the use of catalytic system: further                  comprises step (D) recovering and optionally                  recycling the catalytic system following the                  chemical reaction of step (C). Preferred Method:                  The method (M1): the calcination step (d) is                  performed under air, nitrogen atmosphere, argon                  atmosphere and/or oxygen atmosphere at 275-475                  degrees C, preferably 350-400 degrees C; further                  comprises step (f) providing solvent and                  contacting the at least one solid carrier and/or                  step (b) transition metal reagent before or                  during step (c) in any order and preferably the                  solvent is non-polar solvent and/or polar                  solvent, preferably pentane, cyclopentane,                  hexane, cyclohexane, benzene, toluene,                  1,4-dioxane, chloroform, diethyl ether and/or                  dichloromethane, and/or the polar solvent                  consisting of tetrahydrofuran, ethyl acetate,                  acetone, dimethylformamide, acetonitrile,                  dimethyl sulfoxide, nitromethane, propylene                  carbonate, formic acid, n-butanol, isopropanol,                  n-propanol, ethanol, methanol, acetic acid and/or                  water, preferably water; further comprises step                  (g) removing at least part of the step (c)                  solvent and before step (d) by evaporation and/or                  filtration and/or centrifugation and/or spray                  drying to obtain concentrated mixture; step (h)                  of thermally treating the step (c) mixture or the                  step (g) concentrated mixture at 25-200 degrees                  C, preferably 100-150 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN112029158-A; CN112029158-B
TI Method for preparing chitosan-based composite responsive membrane, involves reacting chitosan, acetic acid, ethanol solution and succinic anhydride, preparing mixed solution, adjusting pH, adding plasticizer, centrifuging and casting.
AU WAN X
   WANG X
   LIU M
   CHEN G
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2020C4095D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a chitosan-based composite responsive membrane with carbon dioxide response behavior, involves (a) adding chitosan and acetic acid to the ethanol solution, stirring well, freezing treatment, standing still, adding succinic anhydride, mixing evenly to obtain the mixture, stirring to react, neutralizing, centrifuging to collect the precipitate, washing, and freeze-drying to obtain N-succinylated chitosan, (b) mixing the alkalized chitosan-isopropanol mixture with propylene oxide, neutralizing, washing, and freeze-drying, (c) preparing N-succinylated chitosan described and the hydroxypropyl chitosan into solutions with solvents, mixing the propyl chitosan solution and stirring uniformly to obtain a mixed solution, adjusting the pH, adding a plasticizer and a mixing indicator to the mixed solution, stirring and treating, ultrasonically dispersing uniformly, and centrifuging, and (d) casting the mixed solution and drying.
   USE - The method is used for preparing a chitosan-based composite responsive membrane with carbon dioxide response behavior, which is used as an indicator label for the freshness of fruits and vegetables (claimed) in the field of safety instructions for the preservation of fruits and vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is carried out in a simple manner. The chitosan-based composite membrane has excellent carbon dioxide response behavior, has excellent stability and is less affected by changes in the surrounding environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing a chitosan-based composite responsive membrane with carbon dioxide response behavior, involves (a) adding chitosan and acetic acid to the ethanol solution, stirring well, freezing treatment, standing still, adding succinic anhydride, mixing evenly to obtain the mixture, stirring to react, neutralizing, centrifuging to collect the precipitate, washing, and freeze-drying to obtain N-succinylated chitosan, (b) mixing the alkalized chitosan-isopropanol mixture with propylene oxide to obtain the reaction raw materials, stirring to react, neutralizing, washing, and freeze-drying to obtain hydroxypropyl chitosan, (c) respectively preparing N-succinylated chitosan described and the hydroxypropyl chitosan into solutions with solvents to obtain N-succinylated chitosan solution and hydroxypropyl chitosan, mixing the propyl chitosan solution and stirring uniformly to obtain a mixed solution, adjusting the pH of the mixed solution to be neutral, adding a plasticizer and a mixing indicator to the mixed solution, stirring and treating, ultrasonically dispersing uniformly, and centrifuging to take the supernatant which is defoamed to obtain a mixed solution of N-succinylated chitosan/hydroxypropyl chitosan/plasticizer/indicator, and (d) casting the mixed solution of N-succinylated chitosan/hydroxypropyl chitosan/plasticizer/indicator, and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent in step (b) is water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In step (a), the ethanol solution is a solution obtained by uniformly mixing ethanol and water, and the volume ratio of ethanol to water is 1:(1-3):1, the mass of acetic acid is 1-10% of the mass of chitosan, the mass-volume ratio of the chitosan to the ethanol solution is 1:30-1:50 g/ml, the temperature of the freezing treatment is -10 to -20 degrees C, and the freezing treatment time is 2-6 hours, and the standing time is 20-50 minutes. The amount of succinic anhydride in step (a) is 1-5 times that of chitosan amino group. The temperature of the stirring reaction is 20-60 degrees C, and the time of the stirring reaction is 5-9 hours. The preparation of the alkalized chitosan-isopropanol mixture in step (b) includes adding chitosan powder to the sodium hydroxide solution and swelling to obtain alkalized chitosan, adding the alkalized chitosan to isopropanol and mixing evenly to obtain the alkalized chitosan to obtain isopropanol mixture, where the concentration of the sodium hydroxide solution is 6-10 mol/l, the mass-volume ratio of the chitosan and the sodium hydroxide solution is 1:20-1:50 g/ml, and the mass-volume ratio of the chitosan to isopropanol is 1:10-1:30 g/ml. In the reaction raw material of step (b), the mass ratio of propylene oxide to chitosan is 5:1-20:1. The temperature of the stirring reaction in step (2) is 40-80 degrees C, and the time of the stirring reaction is 12-24 hours. In step (c), the plasticizer is glycerin, the mixed indicator is a reagent obtained by uniformly mixing bromothymol blue and methyl red indicators, and the mass ratio of bromothymol blue and methyl red is 1:1, the mass fraction of the plasticizer in the mixed solution is 5-20%, the mass fraction of the mixed indicator in the mixed solution is 0.6-1.6%, and the time of the stirring treatment is 1-5 hours. The drying temperature in step (d) is 40-60 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The mass concentration of the N-succinylated chitosan solution is 0.5-2%, the mass concentration of the hydroxypropyl chitosan solution is 0.5-2%, and the mass ratio of the hydroxypropyl chitosan and N-succinylated chitosan is 0.3-1:1.

PN CN109206035-A
TI Preparing hollow porous microspheres useful for piezoelectric power generation asphalt concrete, comprises e.g. adding glucose to deionized water, stirring, adding into the reaction kettle and heating to carry out hydrothermal reaction.
AU CHEN Q
   SI W
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM ENG SCI & TECHNIC (XKLI-C)
GA 2019080904
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing hollow porous microspheres comprises (i) adding glucose to deionized water, stirring uniformly, adding into the reaction kettle, heating to carry out hydrothermal reaction, cooling to room temperature after completion of the reaction, filtering, washing and drying to obtain a nano carbon spheres of particle size of 150-700 nm, (ii) obtaining a colorless transparent solution, (iii) obtaining a uniform mixed fluid, (iv) obtaining a carbon sphere/barium titanate complex microsphere and (v) subjecting the carbon sphere/barium titanate complex microsphere to temperature rising calcinations, generating the carbon dioxide gas by calcining the carbon sphere, where the carbon dioxide gas breaks through the barium titanate covered on the surface of the carbon sphere, forming pores on the surface of barium titanate to obtain hollow porous nanomaterials and cooling to obtain hollow porous microspheres for piezoelectrically generated asphalt concrete.
   USE - The hollow porous microspheres is useful for piezoelectric power generation asphalt concrete (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing hollow porous microspheres comprises (i) adding glucose to deionized water, stirring uniformly, adding into the reaction kettle, heating to carry out hydrothermal reaction, cooling to room temperature after completion of the reaction, filtering, washing and drying to obtain a particle size of Nano carbon spheres of 150-700 nm, (ii) adding cerium hydroxide to deionized water, stirring,adding the uniformly obtained solution to an ethanol solution of tetrabutyl titanate and stirring uniformly to obtain a colorless transparent solution, (iii) adding the nano carbon sphere prepared in the step (i) into anhydrous ethanol, uniformly dispersing the suspension to obtain a suspension, dropping the suspension to the colorless transparent solution prepared in the step (ii) and stirring uniformly to obtain a uniform mixed fluid, (iv) adding the mixed fluid prepared in the step (iii) into the high pressure reaction vessel, sealing and heating the reaction, cooling to room temperature after the reaction, washing and drying to obtain a carbon sphere/barium titanate complex microsphere and (v) subjecting the carbon sphere/barium titanate complex microsphere to temperature rising calcinations, generating the carbon dioxide gas by calcining the carbon sphere, where the carbon dioxide gas breaks through the barium titanate covered on the surface of the carbon sphere, forming pores on the surface of barium titanate to obtain hollow porous nanomaterials and cooling to obtain hollow porous microspheres for piezoelectrically generated asphalt concrete. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the hollow porous microsphere is obtained by the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the raw materials comprises 15-20 pts. wt. glucose and 80-85 pts. wt. deionized water. In the step (ii), the raw material comprises 2-4 pts. wt. barium hydroxide, 46-56 pts. wt. deionized water, 2-5 pts. wt. tetrabutyl titanate and 40-45 pts. wt. ethanol. In the step (iii), the raw material comprises 4-7 pts. wt. nano carbon ball, 15-18 pts. wt. absolute ethanol and 75-81 pts. wt. colorless transparent solution. In the step (i), the hydro-thermal reaction temperature is 180-200 degrees C and the reaction time is 8-12 hours. In the step (iv), the high pressure reaction kettle is a carbon steel reaction vessel, a stainless steel reaction vessel, an enamel reaction vessel or a steel liner reactor. In the step (iv), the temperature of the heating reaction is 230-250 degrees C and the reaction time is 22-24 hours. In the step (iv), the drying is a vacuum oven at 70-80 degrees C for 6-8 hours. In the step (v), the heating rate of the temperature rising calcination is 1-2 degrees C/minute, the temperature is to 450-480 degrees C and the calcination maintaining time is 30-40 minutes.

PT P
PN CN108047721-A; CN108047721-B
TI High-temperature resistant steam baking pad contains dimethyl silicone rubber, methyl vinyl silicone rubber, silica sol, glass fiber, cellulose, bentonite, 2,5-dimethyl-2,5-di(tert-butylperoxy)hexane, zinc gluconate and nanotitanium dioxide.
AU ZHUANG J
AE FENGYANG JIASONG NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (FENG-Non-standard)
GA 201841380L
AB    NOVELTY - A high-temperature resistant steam baking pad comprises 40-50 pts. wt. dimethyl silicone rubber, 35-45 pts. wt. methyl vinyl silicone rubber, 30-35 pts. wt. silica sol, 12-15 pts. wt. glass fiber, 8-11 pts. wt. cellulose, 2-3 pts. wt. bentonite, 2.0-2.5 pts. wt. 2,5-dimethyl-2,5-di(tert-butylperoxy)hexane (DBPMH), 1.2-1.4 pts. wt. stearate, 0.6-0.9 pt. wt. zinc gluconate and 0.3-0.5 pt. wt. nanotitanium dioxide. The processing of silica sol and nanotitanium dioxide comprises dispersing nanotitanium dioxide in silica sol, stirring, placing into sealed container, storing, taking out silica sol, aging at room temperature, supercritical drying, dipping gel in water, reducing temperature of drying device, introducing liquid carbon dioxide as replaced solvent, setting temperature and pressure of drier to supercritical condition, slowly releasing carbon dioxide gas and collecting modified silicon dioxide aerogel; and vulcanizing using t-butyl peroxide as vulcanizing agent and press molding.
   USE - High-temperature resistant steam baking pad.
   ADVANTAGE - The pad has improved high-temperature resistance and prolonged service life.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A high-temperature resistant steam baking pad comprises 40-50 pts. wt. dimethyl silicone rubber, 35-45 pts. wt. methyl vinyl silicone rubber, 30-35 pts. wt. silica sol, 12-15 pts. wt. glass fiber, 8-11 pts. wt. cellulose, 2-3 pts. wt. bentonite, 2.0-2.5 pts. wt. DBPMH, 1.2-1.4 pts. wt. stearate, 0.6-0.9 pt. wt. zinc gluconate and 0.3-0.5 pt. wt. nanotitanium dioxide. The glass fiber and carboxymethyl cellulose are pre-oxidized at 350-400 degrees C, mixing glass fiber, carboxymethyl cellulose and molten silicone rubber, high temperature carbonizing at 1200-1300 degrees C and graphitizing at 2000 degrees C in argon atmosphere. The processing of silica sol and nanotitanium dioxide comprises dispersing nanotitanium dioxide in silica sol, stirring, placing into sealed container, storing at 60-65 degrees C for 7-9 hours, taking out silica sol, aging at room temperature for 10-12 hours, supercritical drying, dipping hydrogel in water, reducing temperature of drying device to 2-4 degrees C, introducing liquid carbon dioxide as replaced solvent, setting temperature and pressure of drier to supercritical condition, slowly releasing carbon dioxide gas and collecting modified silicon dioxide aerogel if temperature and pressure is reduced to indoor condition; and vulcanizing at 200-230 degrees C for 8-10 minutes using t-butyl peroxide as vulcanizing agent and press molding.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The preparation of nanotitanium dioxide comprise washing 99.5-99.9% pure metatitanic acid purity with water and reacting with 80-90% sulfuric acid in mol ratio of 1:1.2-1.3 to obtain titanyl sulfate solution; adding 2-3 by weight volume of 40-50% sodium hydroxide solution to hydrolyze and generate titanium oxide-dihydroxide and carrying out hydrothermal reaction at 150-170 degrees C for 4-6 hours under 2-3KPa; washing reactant with anhydrous ethanol and de-ionized water for 6-8 times, vacuum drying product at 60-65 degrees C for 2-3 hours to obtain titanium dioxide with grain diameter of 10-30 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The silica sol is prepared by treating rice husk ash by physical and chemical method. The physical and chemical method comprises carbonizing rice husk ash at 500-600 degrees C, filtering extract, concentrating in acid-base pressure tank, adding 0.2-0.4% dilute sulfuric acid, carrying out reaction until no sol longer forms and washing with water. Preferred Method: The supercritical drying is carried out at 30-34 degrees C under 7-7.5 MPa.

PN CN113062712-A; CN113062712-B
TI Processing biological prevention for carbon dioxide storage in deep stratum comprises e.g. collecting geological data of carbon dioxide storage layer, clarifying way of carbon dioxide leakage, arranging injection wells and production wells.
AU LIANG W
   GUO H
   LIU Y
   LI Z
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 202180482D
AB    NOVELTY - Processing biological prevention for carbon dioxide storage in deep stratum comprises (I) collecting the geological data of carbon dioxide storage layer and clarifying the way of carbon dioxide leakage, (ii) arranging injection wells and production wells in the formation above the carbon dioxide storage layer, (iii) detecting the total amount of hydrogen sources in the formation, (iv) injecting the biological fluid and external hydrogenation source into the selected formation through the injection, (v) monitoring the methane and carbon dioxide content in the selected stratum in real time and regularly checking the composition of the microbial community, (vi) assessing the degree of leakage and biotransformation of the stored carbon dioxide and adjusting the flora structure, and (vii) extracting the methane produced by production well. The biological liquid comprises microbial liquid and nutrient solution. The the external hydrogenation source comprises hydrogen-rich organic liquid.
   USE - The method is useful for processing biological prevention for carbon dioxide storage in deep stratum.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly and suitable for carbon dioxide emission reduction; saves the energy; utilizes the ability of microorganisms to transform carbon dioxide; and can convert the leaked carbon dioxide into methane or organic matter above the storage layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The microbial liquid comprises hydrogen-trophic methanogens that reduce carbon dioxide, alpha-Proteobacteria, beta-Proteobacteria hydrogen-producing acetogens that degrade organic matter and supply hydrogen.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))

An injection system is connected with a sealing cavity system and injects certain amount of carbon dioxide through the injection well. The production well discharges oil to an outside. An information collecting system is provided with a gas detector and an information data processing computer (16). The gas detector uniformly arranged on a sealing box inner space clamping system. TI System for monitoring and detection of carbon            dioxide or carbon monoxide levels in room and            triggering alarms, has master monitor that receives            data from remote monitor, and triggers first alarm            status when concentration of gas in first area and            second area exceeds first predefined alarm            threshold.
AU LONGLEY R V
AE ELEVEN ELEVEN TECHNOLOGIES LLC (ELEV-Non-standard)
GA 202287831S
AB    NOVELTY - The gas monitoring and alarm system comprises                a master monitor (104) coupled to a master sensor,                which senses a concentration of a gas in a first                area and sends data about the concentration of the                gas to the master monitor. A remote monitor is                communicatively coupled to the master monitor. The                remote monitor is coupled to a remote sensor to                sense the concentration of gas in a second area.                The remote monitor receives data about the                concentration of the gas in the second area from                the remote sensor, and sends the data to the master                monitor. The master monitor receives the data from                the remote monitor, and triggers a first alarm                status when the concentration of gas in the first                area and the second area exceeds a first predefined                alarm threshold.
   USE - System for monitoring and detection of gas,                such as carbon dioxide or carbon monoxide levels in                the room, residential homes, and business                facilities, and triggering alarms when high levels                of gases are detected. Can also be used in business                facilities that offer food and drink utilize                soda-dispensing machines that use carbon dioxide to                carbonate beverages.
   ADVANTAGE - The gas monitoring and alarm system activates                various safety measures to prevent or limit injury                once gas levels reach an unsafe level, and power                backup system allows the system to continue to                alert occupants, including emergency responders,                that harmful concentrations of the gas are present,                even during power outages.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a relay                interface of the gas monitoring and alarm                system
   Primary Monitor (102)
   Master Monitor (104)

PT P
PN US2022022993-A1
TI Method for preventing esophageal fistula formation after intra-cardiac catheter ablation of left atrium, has feedback control system that is configured to regulate pressure and flow rate of gas from gas supply to needle.
AU WONG F
   KUHN M
   BRAHMSTEDT C
   COHN W
AE ETHICON INC (ETHI-C)
GA 202217584C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves delivering an injection catheter (110) to a region proximate the posterior left atrium wall. The injection catheter is anchored to the cardiac tissue. The needle of injection catheter is advanced into the fibro-fatty tissue that separates the posterior left atrium wall from the esophagus. A dose of gas is delivered at a controlled rate through the needle into the fibro-fatty tissue to create a thicker, gas infused insulation layer. The injection catheter is removed when the dose is delivered.
   USE - Method for preventing esophageal fistula formation during intra-cardiac ablation of left atrium of patient with cardiac arrhythmias and atrial fibrillation.
   ADVANTAGE - The system injects sufficient volumes of carbon dioxide gas into the layer of connective tissue that sits between the esophagus and the back wall of the left atrium to create a protective layer of insulation that will prevent thermal injury to the esophagus while intentionally creating full-thickness burns in the atrium. The system provides adequate thermal insulation, thus preventing it from being injured. The carbon dioxide is utilized instead of air to leverage carbon dioxide's water solubility. The 3-6 millimeter layer of fibro-fatty tissue is converted into a thicker layer of gas infused tissue that will surround the esophagus. The risk of potential injury is essentially non-existent because a gas is being injected, the needle to be utilized may be small enough e.g. on the order of a 27-gauge needle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a system for the prevention of esophageal fistula during intra-cardiac ablation.
   Electronically adjustable pressure regulator (104)
   Solenoid valve (106)
   Flowmeter (108)
   Injection catheter (110)
   Microprocessor (112)

PT P
PN CN113904013-A
TI Recycling lithium-extracting waste water in waste lithium battery recycling processing involves collecting and storing lithium extracting waste water, removing impurities from waste water through filter, and extracting lithium waste water.
AU YANG Y
   HE Y
   QIU C
   CUI X
   LIU Y
   WANG J
   ZHEN A
   KONG F
   LING C
   ZHANG T
   LV Y
AE ZHEJIANG TIANNENG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202213756G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for recycling lithium-extracting waste water in a waste lithium battery recycling process, involves (a) collecting and storing lithium extraction waste water, (b) passing the waste water through a filter to remove impurities, (c) entering the filtered waste water into the P507 extraction system through the flow meter, (d) performing saponification of extraction waste water and P507 extractant in the mixing chamber based on a certain ratio, adding sodium carbonate, and reacting P507 extractant with sodium carbonate to form sodium-P507 extractant, water and carbon dioxide, and mixing for greater than 3.5 minutes, (e) collecting the carbon dioxide produced by the saponification by the gas collecting device and used for the hydrogenation process of refining lithium carbonate by reacting the lithium carbonate, carbon dioxide and water to form lithium bicarbonate, and (d) after the extraction waste water is saponified, the P507 raffinate enters the degreasing and deweighting process.
   USE - Method for recycling lithium-extracting waste water in a waste lithium battery recycling treatment process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables recycling lithium extracting waste water without complicated processing, heating and filtering directly on the P507 extracting line. The waste water content is high chemical oxygen demand (COD), sodium ion, and carbonate will not generate negative influence to the system. The method uses the characteristics of high pH and sodium ion in the lithium-extracting waste water to replace the liquid alkali for P507 extraction line saponification, which can reduce the waste water discharge, reduce the dosage of the liquid-alkali, can save the consumption of acid in the acidification process, and reach the purpose of reducing emission and reducing consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (a), the lithium extraction wastewater is placed in storage tanks and pipelines, and thermal insulation or heating device is added, and the temperature is controlled at greater than 30degrees Celsius.Preferred Conditions: In step (b), the filter is a bag filter or a precision filter. In step (c), by adjusting the flow meter, the ratio of lithium extraction wastewater to P507 extractant is adjusted to be 1:(3-5). In step (d), the saponification process will release heat, and a heat exchanger or forced cooling device is added to the saponification section.

AU NIU Y
   CHEN H
   DUO F
   ZHANG W
   LAN J
AE UNIV XINXIANG (UNXX-C)
GA 202210763P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid comprises (i) mixing substrate I i.e. furan compound (I), 1,8-naphthalimide, auxiliary agent, solvent and electrolyte and adding to the cathode chamber of the electrolytic cell in an oxygen-free environment, stirring and dissolving to obtain cathode chamber reaction solution, and (ii) mixing solvent, triethylamine and electrolyte and then adding to the anode chamber of the electrolytic cell to obtain anode chamber reaction solution, passing carbon dioxide into the electrolytic cell, and connecting direct current, carrying out carboxylation reaction under the condition of visible light, and acidifying the reacted cathode chamber reaction solution to obtain 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes easily raw materials; has low production cost and mild reaction conditions; and is suitable for industrial production; avoids usage of metal catalysts; and adopts the non-edible furfural derivative.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid comprises (i) mixing substrate I i.e. furan compound of formula (I), 1,8-naphthalimide, auxiliary agent, solvent and electrolyte and adding to the cathode chamber of the electrolytic cell in an oxygen-free environment, stirring and dissolving to obtain cathode chamber reaction solution, and (ii) mixing solvent, triethylamine and electrolyte and then adding to the anode chamber of the electrolytic cell to obtain anode chamber reaction solution, passing carbon dioxide into the electrolytic cell, and connecting direct current, carrying out carboxylation reaction under the condition of visible light, and acidifying the reacted cathode chamber reaction solution to obtain 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.
   R = H, carboxy group, or 1-10C alkyl formate;
   X = H, fluoro, chloro, bromo or iodo;
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - In step (i), the molar ratio between substrate I, 1,8-naphthalimide and auxiliary agent is 1:0.05-2:1-4. In step (ii), the irradiation time of the visible light is 6-16 hours, the current range of the direct current is 0.2-1 mA, and the reaction temperature of the carboxylation reaction is 20-80degrees Celsius.

TI Graphite-phase carbon nitride composite material            useful in preparing ethylene, propylene and butane by            carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide hydrogenation            reaction, comprises sheet-shaped heptaazine ring, and            loaded with nano metal particles.
AU ZHANG J
   ZHANG Y
   ZHAO T
   MA L
   GAO X
   MA Q
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 202172116J
AB    NOVELTY - Graphite-phase carbon nitride composite                material comprises sheet-shaped heptaazine ring.                The material surface is surrounded by hydroxy and                amino functional group to form functional                group-carbon nitride. The material is loaded with                nano metal particle which is polymerized on surface                of functional group-carbon nitride to form                amorphous coating sheet. The carbon nitride                comprises 1-20 wt.% metal particles. The material                is prepared by (a) roasting melamine, cooling to                room temperature and grinding to obtain powder A;                (b) stirring powder A in hydrogen peroxide                solution, filtering and drying to obtain powder B;                (c) stirring powder B in aqueous ammonia, heating                in reaction kettle, setting time, taking out,                filtering and drying to obtain powder C; and (d)                preparing metal salt solution, taking powder B and                powder C at mass ratio of 0-1:0.5-5, mechanically                mixing to obtain powder D, adding metal salt                solution into powder D, stirring, drying, roasting                and grinding to obtain target material.
   USE - The material is useful in preparing ethylene,                propylene and butane by carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide hydrogenation reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material has unique electronic structure                and good chemical stability; uses the iron oxide as                main active phase of the carbon monoxide                hydrogenation reaction, and uses the surface                modified carbon nitride loaded iron catalyst for                preparing olefin by hydrogenation reaction to                provide a reliable solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for use                method of graphite-phase carbon nitride composite                material in preparing ethylene, propylene and                butane by carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide                hydrogenation reaction, comprising performing                operation using fixed bed reactor at 280-380                degrees C, 1-3 MPa, and 1000-3000 h-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal is iron,                  cobalt, nickel and ruthenium, and metal                  lanthanum, manganese, potassium, cerium and                  sodium are added selectively. In the step (b),                  the concentration of the hydrogen peroxide is 1-5                  mol/l. In the step (c), the mass fraction of                  aqueous ammonia is 1-20 wt.%. Preferred Method:                  In the step (d), the powder B, when the ratio of                  powder C is less than or equal to 1, the material                  is used for carbon monoxide hydrogenation, and                  atmosphere condition is hydrogen/carbon monoxide                  is 1-3, when the ratio of powder C is 1-2, the                  material is used for carbon monoxide and carbon                  dioxide mixed atmosphere hydrogenation, and                  atmosphere condition is carbon monoxide/carbon                  dioxide is 0.5-50 and hydrogen/(carbon monoxide                  and carbon dioxide) is 1-3, and when the ratio of                  powder C is 2-5, the material is used for carbon                  dioxide hydrogenation, and atmosphere condition                  is hydrogen/carbon dioxide is 1-3. In the step                  (a), the roasting is carried out at 300-600                  degrees C at a heating speed of 2-10 degrees                  C/minute for 2-6 hours. In the step (c), the                  stirring time is 2-4 hours, the heating                  temperature is 120-180 degrees C, and reaction                  time is 2-4 hours.

PN CN112076587-A
TI Food-grade carbon dioxide purifying device and adsorbent, comprises a fixed purifying box body and a sliding filter plate, where the right upper position of the front end of the fixed purifying box body is provided with an operating table.
AU CAO X
AE GUANGDONG HUIXING AIR LIQUEFACTION CO LT (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020C7978J
AB    NOVELTY - Food-grade carbon dioxide purifying device and adsorbent comprises a fixed purifying box body (1) and a sliding filter plate (6). The right upper position of front end of fixed purifying box body is provided with an operating table (7). The outer wall of upper and lower sides of left front end of fixed purifying box body is provided with a rotating connecting block (2). The middle position of two rotating connecting blocks is provided with a rotating door (3). The rotating door is located at left front side position of fixed purifying box body. The left front side of fixed purifying box body is provided with a sealing plate (5). The sealing plate is located at right side of rotating door. The middle inner position of front side of sealing plate is sleeved with fixing lock. The inner wall of left and right sides of fixing purifying box body is provided with screw thread sliding fixing table. The screw thread sliding fixing table is set to be eight.
   USE - Food grade carbon dioxide purifying device and adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The sliding block in the sliding filter plate is in a vertical state with the sliding groove of the screw thread sliding fixing table, so as to not make the sliding filter plate slide, and will not damage the fixed filter plate, which not only improves the using space of the purification, but also reduces the damage rate of the filter plate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the device.
   Fixed purifying box body (1)
   Rotating connecting block (2)
   Rotating door (3)
   Sealing plate (5)
   Sliding filter plate (6)
   Operating table (7)

PN CN111579261-A; CN111579261-B
TI Device for simulating gas composition, has upper computer that controls carbon dioxide mass flow meter and vacuum chamber whose gas composition ratio in ensured by opening degrees of nitrogen mass, argon mass and hydrogen mass flow meters.
AU WANG Y
   SAI J
   ZHAO Y
   ZHANG H
   HAN L
   GAO B
   YU P
   MENG N
AE XIAN OPTICS & PRECISION MECHANICS INST (CAXO-C)
   XIAN OPTICS & PRECISION MECHANICS INST (CAXO-C)
GA 2020846739
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a decompression chamber (16) whose output end is connected to the input end of the gas ionization system (22) through a fourth vacuum valve (21). The second pumping unit is used for pumping a vacuum in the decompression chamber to reduce the gas pressure in it. The gas ionization system is used to ionize the gas molecules entering it to form ions. The mass spectrometry system (23) is used to screen the ions and send them to the ion collection system. The ion collection system generates corresponding current signals according to the received ion current concentrations of various gases, and sends them to the upper computer. The gas concentration is configured to control the opening of the carbon dioxide mass flow meter (5), nitrogen mass flow meter (6), argon mass flow meter (7), and hydrogen mass flow meter (8). The gas composition ratio in the vacuum chamber (10) is ensured.
   USE - Device for simulating gas composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The device is close to the actual simulation of the Martian atmospheric state, and provides technical support for the Mars probe to conduct real and accurate environmental simulation tests on the ground.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for simulating active surface gas components.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a device for simulating gas composition. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide mass flow meter (5)
   Nitrogen mass flow meter (6)
   Argon mass flow meter (7)
   Hydrogen mass flow meter (8)
   Vacuum chamber (10)
   Decompression chamber (16)
   Fourth vacuum valve (21)
   Gas ionization system (22)
   Mass spectrometry system (23)

PT P
PN US2020239311-A1; US11299395-B2
TI Graphitic carbon nitride nanostructure used as            catalyst for oxidizing carbon monoxide, and reducing            carbon dioxide to hydrocarbon, is doped atomically with            one or more metal elements.
AU EID K A M
   ABDULLAH A M
AE UNIV QATAR (UYQA-Non-standard)
GA 202071044R
AB    NOVELTY - Graphitic carbon nitride nanostructure is                doped atomically with one or more metal                elements.
   USE - Graphitic carbon nitride nanostructure used as                catalyst for oxidizing carbon monoxide and reducing                carbon dioxide to hydrocarbons (all claimed), and                for practical and large-scale gas conversion for                household or industrial applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The graphitic carbon nitride nanostructure is                prepared with high mass production from inexpensive                and natural abundant precursors. It possess                physicochemical benefits, including unique thermal                stability (up to 600 degrees C in air), dynamic                chemical stability in different organic/inorganic                solvents, high electron density, massive defects,                and nontoxicity which are highly required merits in                practical gas conversion applications. It provides                conversion of CO2 and CO gasses into useful                hydrocarbon fuels under ambient reaction conditions                using active, stable, inexpensive, earth-abundant,                and reduced greenhouse-gas emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for preparing metal-doped carbon                nitride (gC3N4) nanostructure, which involves                providing a metal salt solution comprising one or                more metal salts and a first solvent, adding                melamine to the metal salt solution followed by                adding an acid solution, optionally while stirring                at a first temperature to form a precipitate;                washing the precipitate with a second solvent; and                drying the washed precipitate at a second                temperature to obtain the desired product;
   (2) a method for oxidizing carbon monoxide                (CO), which involves providing a CO gas mixture                comprising 1-100% CO; providing a catalyst                comprising the graphitic-like carbon nitride                nanostructure to contact the CO gas mixture; and                performing a CO oxidation reaction; and
   (3) a method for reducing carbon dioxide                (CO2), which involves providing a CO2 gas mixture                comprising greater than or equal to 1% CO;                providing a catalyst comprising the graphitic-like                carbon nitride nanostructure to contact the CO2 gas                mixture; and performing a CO2 reduction                reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The nanostructure                  comprises nanotubes, nanowires, nanorods, and/or                  nanofibers. The metal elements are in the form of                  dopants, single atoms, nanoparticles, ions,                  oxide, or their combination. The nanostructure is                  doped atomically with metal selected from gold                  (Au), palladium (Pd), copper (Cu), and platinum                  (Pt), preferably doped atomically with Pd and a                  second metal element selected from Au, Cu, and                  Pt. The nanostructure is selected from carbon                  nitride nanotube doped with Au and Pd (Au/Pd/g                  C3N4NT), a carbon nitride nanofiber doped with Au                  and Pd (Au/Pd/gC3N4NF), a carbon nitride nanorod                  doped with Pt and Pd (Pt/Pd/CN nanorod), and a                  carbon nitride nanotube doped with Pd and Cu                  (Pd/Cu/gC3N4NF). The CO and CO2 conversion is                  enhanced using other metal support selected from                  metal oxides, molecular sieves, carbon supports,                  ceramic-based materials, clay-based materials,                  and promoters, where the metal oxides is selected                  from titanium dioxide, ceric dioxide, ferric                  oxide, silica, or ferroso ferric oxide, the                  molecular sieves is a zeolite, the carbon                  supports is selected from diamond, graphene,                  cellulose, lignin, and carbon nanotube, the                  ceramic-based materials comprises bioglass and                  hydroxyapatite, the clay-based materials                  comprises bentonite and halloysite, and the                  promotors comprises potassium hydroxide,                  hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, and acetic acid.                  The metal salt is selected from the noble metals                  group, the transition metals, and their                  combination, preferaby hydrogen tetrachloroaurate                  (III) trihydrate, chloroauric acid, potassium                  tetrachloropalladate (II), sodium                  tetrachloropalladate, potassium                  tetrachloroplatinate (II) , sodium                  tetrachloroplatinate(II), copper(II) chloride                  dihydrate, copper(II) chloride, and their                  combination. The first solvent is selected from                  ethylene glycol, isopropanol, and water. The                  second solvent is selected from ethanol and                  isopropanol. The acid is selected from nitric                  acid, hydrochloric acid, and their combination.                  The nanostructure is prepared from a                  nitrogen-rich precursor comprising urea,                  thiourea, cyanuric acid-based, cyandiamide,                  pyridine, or guanidine hydrochloride. Preferred                  Composition: The nanostructure is doped with                  1-1.2 wt. % Au/Pd, or 1-1.2 wt. % Pd/Cu, or 1-1.2                  wt. % Pt/Pd. Preferred Conditions: The                  nanostructure is one-dimensional (1-D). The                  nanostructure is functionalized with one or more                  metal-based nanoparticles, single atoms, metal                  oxide nanoparticles, or hybrid nanoparticles. The                  nanostructure is a catalyst for a carbon monoxide                  (CO) oxidation reaction and/or a carbon dioxide                  (CO2) reduction reaction. The nanostructure is                  porous, having surface area of 300-350 m2/g, an                  average pore size/diameter of 45-65 nm, and a                  pore volume of 0.45-0.65 cubic centimeter/g. The                  nanostructure is a catalyst for oxidizing CO in a                  CO gas mixture and/or reducing CO2 in a CO2 gas                  mixture at room temperature to 300 degrees C,                  where CO gas mixture contains 1-100% of CO, and                  the CO2 gas mixture contains 1-100% of CO2. The                  method further comprises annealing the powder.                  The first temperature is room temperature. The                  second temperature is 70-100 degrees C. The CO is                  oxidized to CO2. CO2 is reduced to HCO2H and CO                  is oxidized to CO2. The CO oxidation or the CO2                  reduction is performed under a condition where a                  UV-light is on the catalyst. The method of                  reducing CO2 further comprises converting CO or                  CO2 to a hydrocarbon.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PN US2020217786-A1; CN111412989-A; JP2020112364-A; US11009454-B2
TI Fourier transform infrared spectrometer used for calibration of oscillation wavelength of semiconductor laser, has oscillation wavelength calibrator that locates absorption peak of carbon dioxide from peaks.
AU WAKUDA S
   WAKUTA S
AE SHIMADZU CORP (SHMA-C)
   SHIMADZU CORP (SHMA-C)
   SHIMADZU CORP (SHMA-C)
   SHIMADZU CORP (SHMA-C)
GA 202062030P
AB    NOVELTY - The infrared spectrometer (1) has a main interferometer (10) that includes a multiwavelength infrared light source (11), a beam splitter (12), a fixed mirror (13) and a movable mirror (14). A control interferometer includes a semiconductor laser (21). A first detector (16) is configured to detect infrared interference light. A second detector (26) is configured to detect monochromatic interference light. A spectrum creator (32) is configured to determine an optical path difference between an optical path via the fixed mirror and an optical path via the movable mirror. An oscillation wavelength calibrator (34) is configured to locate an absorption peak of carbon dioxide from peaks in the spectrum created by the spectrum creator, and to compare a wavenumber or wavelength of the absorption peak with a true absorption wavenumber or wavelength of carbon dioxide to determine a calibrated oscillation wavelength of the semiconductor laser.
   USE - Fourier transform infrared spectrometer used for calibration of oscillation wavelength of semiconductor laser in control interferometer.
   ADVANTAGE - The oscillation wavelength precise calibrator is configured to perform the precise calibration so as to minimize the difference between the obtained wavenumber or wavelength and the true absorption wavenumber or wavelength for each of the absorption peaks of water vapor. The spectrum created by using the oscillation wavelength calibrated by the oscillation wavelength calibrator has a high level of accuracy of the oscillation wavelength than before the calibration.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the Fourier transform infrared spectrometer.
   Fourier transform infrared spectrometer (1)

PT P
PN CN111256531-A; CN111256531-B
TI Body structure for ultra-critical carbon dioxide gas gun, has barrel whose rear end is in fixed sealing engagement with front end of acceleration section outer ring, where barrel receives and launches projectiles including outer diameter.
AU LIU H
   GUO B
   ZHANG F
   LI J
AE INST ENG THERMOPHYSICS CHINESE (CAET-C)
GA 2020535975
AB    NOVELTY - The structure has an acceleration section outer ring whose rear end is connected with an outlet pipeline at a front end of a liquid container in a sealing manner. An inner ring of the acceleration section is arranged in a middle of the outer ring of the acceleration section, and comprises contraction and expansion sections that extend along axis, where length of the expansion section is greater than length of the contraction section. A transition section is located between the contraction section and the expansion section, and forms a throat unit. The outer ring of the acceleration section is provided with a back flushing air hole positioned at upstream of the inner ring of the acceleration section in axis direction. A barrel assembly comprises a barrel including a rear end in coaxial fixed sealing engagement with a front end of the acceleration section outer ring. The barrel receives and launches projectiles including outer diameter corresponding to inner diameter of the barrel.
   USE - Body structure for an ultra-critical carbon dioxide gas gun.
   ADVANTAGE - The barrel includes the rear end in coaxial fixed sealing engagement with the front end of the acceleration section outer ring. The barrel receives and launches the projectiles including the outer diameter corresponding to the inner diameter of the barrel so as to improve exit speed of a shell and reduce length of the barrel, thus providing a simple structure, safety and reliability, fast filling, portability and high efficiency. The structure is suitable for emergency rescue.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of a body structure for an ultra-critical carbon dioxide gas gun.

PN CN111021071-A
TI Mosquito-repellent waterproof finishing agent used for fabric, comprises mosquito repellent, coupling agent, high-methyl hydrogen silicone oil, catalyst, cyclodextrin and organic solvent.
AU MO S
   HE J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY ZHIJIANG COLLEG (UYZJ-C)
GA 202033897L
AB    NOVELTY - Mosquito-repellent waterproof finishing agent comprises 5-15 pts. wt. mosquito repellent, 0.5-3 pts. wt. coupling agent, 15-30 pts. wt. high-methyl hydrogen silicone oil, 0.2-2 pts. wt. catalyst, 5-20 pts. wt. cyclodextrin and 30-85 pts. wt. organic solvent.
   USE - The mosquito-repellent waterproof finishing agent is used for fabric (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The finishing agent provides fabric with high mosquito-repellent effect and waterproof performance. The finishing method improves the whole set of technological process of anhydrous dyeing and finishing, and solves the ecological environment pollution problem of printing and dyeing enterprises to some extent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a supercritical carbon dioxide anhydrous finishing method using the mosquito-repellent waterproof finishing agent, involving (a) pre-cooling the autoclave, and taking out after cooling to below -20 degrees C, (b) opening the lid of the autoclave, adding the fabric and the anti-mosquito waterproof finishing agent, and covering the autoclave lid, where the amount of the finishing agent added is 1-5% of the fabric weight, (c) slowly feeding the carbon dioxide into the autoclave so that the density of carbon dioxide is 0.4-0.8 g/cm3, closing the charging valve and covering with the anti-blocking cover, (d) placing the autoclave into an oil bath, immersing the oil bath medium in the autoclave, and fixing the autoclave with a autoclave clamp, (e) heating the oil bath medium at more than 50 degrees C, opening the oil bath, stirring for 0.5-3 hours, and cooling to room temperature, (f) taking out the autoclave, unscrewing the anti-blocking cover, and opening the charging valve to reduce the pressure to equilibrium, and (g) opening the lid of the autoclave and taking out the processed materials.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Components: The mosquito repellent includes N,N-diethyl-m-toluamide and/or permethrin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is one or a combination of toluene, xylene, petroleum jelly, dichloroethane, chloroform, acetone, ethyl acetate and ethyl acetoacetate. The oil bath medium is glycerin. The coupling agent is vinyl silane, amino silane, epoxy silane, mercapto silane, methacryloxy silane, methyl hydroxy silicone oil, typically vinyl triethoxy silane, vinyltrimethoxysilane, vinyltris( beta -methoxyethoxy)silane, gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane, ( gamma -glycidoxypropyl)trimethoxysilane, gamma -(methacryloyloxy)propyltrimethoxysilane, N-( beta -aminoethyl)- gamma -aminopropylmethyldimethoxysilane, N-( beta -aminoethyl)- gamma -aminopropyltrimethoxysilane, and/or N-( beta -aminoethyl)- gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane. The catalyst is an organic tin nitrate comprising one or more of diethyltin acetate, dibutyltin acetate, dioctyltin acetate, dibutyltin dilaurate and nickel acetylacetonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The cyclodextrin is beta -cyclodextrin, dimethyl- beta -cyclodextrin, hydroxypropyl- beta -cyclodextrin, monochlorotriazine- beta -cyclodextrin, hydroxyethyl- beta -cyclodextrin, alpha -cyclodextrin and its derivatives, and/or gamma -cyclodextrin and its derivatives. The high-methyl hydrogen silicone oil is one or more of methyl siloxanes with hydrogen groups or branched-chain hydrogen groups with a hydrogen content of 1.2-1.8%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Conditions: In step (e), the temperature of the oil bath medium is increased to 50-120 degrees C, and the heating rate is 1-5 degrees C/minute.
   ACTIVITY - Insect Repellent.

TI Producing hydrogen comprises contacting reformer            feed of methane with reforming catalyst under reforming            conditions, to provide reformer product comprising            hydrogen produced from reforming of portion of methane            by reaction with oxidant.
AU MARKER T
   WANGEROW J
   ORTIZ-TORAL P
   LINCK M
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
GA 202021839J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing (P1) hydrogen, comprises: contacting                a reformer feed comprising methane with a reforming                catalyst under reforming conditions, to provide a                reformer product comprising hydrogen produced from                reforming of at least a portion of the methane by                reaction with an oxidant, where the reforming                catalyst comprises a noble metal on a solid support                comprising cerium oxide and less than 0.5 wt.%                silicon, and the reformer feed comprises one or                both of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide.
   USE - The process is useful for producing                hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The process utilizes number of components                commonly present in methane-containing process                streams that can maintained without conventional                upgrading (pretreating) steps, thus improving                process economics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) producing (P2) hydrogen, comprising (a)                combining a reformer feed comprising methane and                one or both of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide                with a steam-containing feed to provide a combined                feed, and (b) feeding the combined feed to a                reforming reactor containing a reforming catalyst                to reform at least a portion of the methane by                reaction with at least a portion of water in the                steam-containing feed, where the noble metal is                present in elemental form; and
   (2) producing (P3) hydrogen, comprising (i)                contacting a process stream comprising methane,                carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide with a sulfur                guard bed to provide a sweetened process stream,                having a decreased concentration of total sulfur,                relative to the process stream, (ii) separating                hydrogen from the sweetened process stream to                provide a reformer feed having a decreased hydrogen                gas concentration, relative to the sweetened                process stream, (iii) combining the reformer feed                with a steam-containing feed to provide a combined                feed, and (iv) the step (b) as per se, where Lewis                acid sites represent at least 25% of the total acid                sites of the solid support.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (P1) further                  comprises converting at least a portion of the                  carbon monoxide in the reformer product to                  additional hydrogen has by a water-gas shift                  (WGS) reaction, to provide a WGS product having                  an increased hydrogen gas concentration, relative                  to the reformer product, where the WGS product                  comprises at least 70 mol.% hydrogen gas, on a                  water-free basis. In the process (P2), the                  reformer feed is obtained from a                  methane-containing process stream including                  natural gas, a coal or biomass gasification                  effluent, a biomass digester effluent, an                  effluent of a renewable hydrocarbon fuel                  production process and/or a well head effluent.                  The effluent of the renewable hydrocarbon fuel                  production process (P2) is a hydropyrolysis                  gaseous mixture recovered from biomass                  hydropyrolysis. In the process (P2), (A) before                  step (a), the reformer feed is not subjected to                  at least one upgrading step consisting of (a1)                  hydrotreating to convert higher than 2C olefinic                  hydrocarbons, (b1) a WGS reaction to convert                  carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide, and (c1)                  carbon dioxide removal, and/or (B) before the                  step (b), the combined feed is not subjected to                  pre-reforming to convert higher than 2C                  paraffinic hydrocarbons, where the reformer feed                  comprises at least 10 mol.ppm of total sulfur.                  Preferred Components: In the process (P1): the                  reformer feed comprises both carbon monoxide and                  carbon dioxide, in a combined concentration of                  1-25 mol.%; the oxidant comprises water and                  further before to the contacting, the reformer                  feed is combined with a separate steam-containing                  feed comprising all or a portion of the water;                  the reformer feed comprises less than 25 mol.%                  methane; the reformer feed further comprises one                  or both of at least one higher than 2C paraffinic                  hydrocarbons and at least higher than 2C olefinic                  hydrocarbons; the reformer feed comprises the                  higher than 2C paraffinic hydrocarbons including                  ethane, propane, butane and/or pentane; the                  reformer feed comprises ethane and propane, in a                  combined concentration of 2-35 mol.%; the                  reformer feed comprises both the higher than 2C                  paraffinic hydrocarbons and the higher than 2C                  olefinic hydrocarbons, where the higher than 2C                  olefinic hydrocarbons are ethylene, propylene,                  butene and/or pentene; the reformer feed                  comprises ethane and ethylene, in a combined                  concentration of 3-30 mol.%; the methane and the                  one or both of the higher than 2C paraffinic                  hydrocarbons and the higher than 2C olefinic                  hydrocarbons are reformed with a hydrocarbon                  conversion of at least 90%; the reforming                  conditions include a temperature of 649-816                  degrees C, a gauge pressure of 0-517 kPa, a                  weight hourly space velocity of 0.1-2.5/hour, and                  molar steam/carbon ratio of 2-4.5; the noble                  metal comprises platinum, rhodium, ruthenium,                  palladium, silver, osmium, iridium or gold; the                  reforming catalyst comprises at least two noble                  metals consisting of platinum, rhodium,                  ruthenium, palladium, silver, osmium, iridium or                  gold; and the reformer product comprises at least                  5 mol.% cobalt.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN US2020061534-A1; US11358096-B2
TI Method for binding hazardous gaseous ammonia from organic waste streams, involves concentrating certain bound solution by removing portion of solvent of certain bound solution by means of crystallization and precipitation in reaction vessel.
AU VAN WAKEREN P A P
   GRISKONIS E
   NAKAS T
   FRIEDMAN M J
AE VAN WAKEREN P A P (VWAK-Individual)
   GRISKONIS E (GRIS-Individual)
   NAKAS T (NAKA-Individual)
   FRIEDMAN M J (FRIE-Individual)
   NAKAS T (NAKA-Individual)
   GRISKONIS E (GRIS-Individual)
   VAN WAKEREN P A P (VWAK-Individual)
GA 202017538V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a quantity of ammonia from an organic source in gaseous form, a quantity of carbon dioxide and a primary reaction vessel. The gaseous ammonia is dissolved in water to form an ammonia solution. The quantity of carbon dioxide is injected through micro-sized bubbles into the primary reaction vessel at ambient temperature and under ambient pressure. The micro-sized carbon dioxide bubbles are mixed with the ammonia solution. The primary reaction of vessel is at ambient temperature and under ambient pressure in order to produce an ammonia-bound solution which comprises ammonia salts selected from the group consisting of ammonium bicarbonate, ammonium carbonate and ammonium carbamate. The ammonia-bound solution is concentrated by removing a portion of the solvent of the ammonia-bound solution by means of crystallization and precipitation within the same reaction vessel and without the use of reverse osmosis or evaporation of water.
   USE - Method for binding hazardous gaseous ammonia from organic waste streams.
   ADVANTAGE - The capture of gaseous ammonia is required by law, to reduce environmental impact of farming operations and other ammonia emitting industries. The ammonia-bound solution is a liquid solution that allows the transportation of the ammonia to be efficient locally or from site to site. The ammonia-bound solution remains stable and easily stored or transported for future processing and applications. The quantity of adjusting reagent is utilized to increase the ease which the ammonia evaporates during the aeration of the agricultural waste. The method introduces and increases or optimizes the farm revenue, as well as reduces its environmental impact.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for binding hazardous gaseous ammonia from organic waste streams.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN110496619-A; CN110496619-B
TI Preparing copper hydride catalyst comprises e.g.            providing copper salt precursor and a borane complex,            dissolving in an organic solvent separately, and            reacting the copper salt precursor and borane complex            to generate reaction product.
AU YANG H
   MA L
   ZOU L
   HU W
   ZOU Z
AE SHANGHAI ADVANCED RES INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAR-C)
GA 2019A2338T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing copper hydride catalyst comprises                providing a copper salt precursor and a borane                complex, and dissolving in an organic solvent                separately, reacting the copper salt precursor and                a borane complex under certain conditions to                generate reaction product, separating, purifying,                and drying the reaction product to obtain copper                hydride catalyst.
   USE - The copper hydride derived catalyst is useful                for electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction to                prepare diatomic carbon chemicals (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and does not require                complicated surface plasma treatment and heat                treatment to regulate the crystalline phase                structure of the copper-based catalyst; and                suitable for large-scale production. The copper                hydride derived catalyst: has excellent                electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction                performance, ethylene selectivity, and multi-carbon                product selectivity; and achieves industrial-grade                current density electrolysis and catalytic                stability. The copper hydride catalyst has rough                active surface and can spontaneously derived into                series of different copper-based crystalline phase                catalysts in air or oxygen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) copper hydride catalyst;
   (2) preparing copper hydride derived catalyst                comprising providing the copper hydride catalyst,                exposing the copper hydride catalyst to air or                oxygen, releasing hydrogen at -20 degrees C-80                degrees C to generate copper, forming copper                hydride/copper mixed crystal phase, and forming                copper crystal phase after completely releasing                hydrogen, oxidizing the surface of the copper                crystal phase to form copper/copper oxide mixed                crystal phase, continuously oxidizing to form                copper oxide crystal phase, and oxidizing to form                cuprous oxide/copper oxide mixed crystal                phase;
   (3) copper hydride derived catalyst; and
   (4) use method of copper hydride derived                catalyst comprising applying copper hydride derived                catalyst to the electrochemical carbon dioxide                reduction of industrial grade current density to                prepare diatomic carbon chemicals, where the                electrolytic cell comprises flow electrolytic                cell.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper salt                  precursor is copper chloride, cuprous chloride,                  copper bromide, copper iodide, copper nitrate,                  copper acetate, copper sulfate, or copper                  acetylacetonate. The borane complex is is borane                  tert-butylamine, borane tetrahydrofuran complex,                  borane dimethyl sulfide complex, borane pyridine                  complex, catecholborane, binaborane,                  2-methylpyridineborane, N, N-diethylaniline                  borane, triethylboron, or diethyl (3-pyridyl)                  borane. The organic solvent is ethanol, N,                  N-dimethylformamide, N, N-dimethylacetamide,                  dimethyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran, methanol,                  benzene, chloroform, or carbon tetrachloride. The                  molar ratio of the copper salt precursor to the                  borane complex is 0.25-20, and the molar                  concentration of the copper salt precursor is 1                  mmol/l-1 mol/l. The reaction atmosphere                  generating the reaction product is argon,                  nitrogen or air. Preferred Method: The reaction                  temperature for forming the reaction product is                  -20 degrees C-80 degrees C. The reaction                  conditions for generating the reaction product                  are ultrasonication, stirring or allowing to                  stand for few minutes. The separating process                  comprises performing liquid-solid separation                  using suction filtration or centrifugation. The                  purification comprieses purifying by adopting                  multiple washing solvent dispersion washing,                  where the washing solvent is ethanol, N,                  N-dimethylformamide, N, N-dimethylacetamide,                  dimethyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran, methanol,                  benzene, chloroform or carbon tetrachloride. The                  drying process comprises using environments of                  vacuum, argon, or nitrogen for drying, when the                  vacuum environment is used for drying, the range                  of vacuum degree is 0-100 kPa. In the process,                  the gas diffusion electrode comprises three-layer                  structure, in which, the middle layer is 10-40                  wt.% polytetrafluoroethylene treated carbon paper                  as gas diffusion layer, drop-coating or                  spray-coating or spin-coating the copper hydride                  derived catalyst as catalytic layer on one side                  of the intermediate layer, brushing the                  polytetrafluoroethylene hydrophobic layer or                  adhering polytetrafluoroethylene polymer film on                  the other side of the intermediate layer.
