TI Inactivated vaccine for porcine infectious pleuropneumonia useful for preparing the medicine for preventing or treating porcine infectious pleuropneumonia, comprises a mixture of an inactivated bacteria.
AU HU Y
AE UNIV YICHUN (UYYC-C)
GA 201933642B
AB    NOVELTY - Inactivated vaccine for porcine infectious pleuropneumonia comprises a mixture of an inactivated bacteria of Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae and a solution of astragalus polysaccharide adjuvant.
   USE - The inactivated porcine infectious pleuropneumoniae vaccine is useful for preparing the medicine for preventing or treating porcine infectious pleuropneumonia (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the inactivated vaccine for porcine infectious pleuropneumonia, comprising (i) rejuvenating a strain of porcine infectious Pleuropneumoniae isolated and identified, inoculating the cells to TSB liquid medium at 1% by volume of the culture medium and culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain a first-stage seed liquid, (ii) inoculating the first-stage seed liquid prepared in the step (i) into the TSB liquid medium at 1% of the medium volume, culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain a second-stage seed liquid, (iii) inoculating the secondary seed solution prepared in step (ii) into TSB liquid medium at 1% of the medium volume, collecting the culture medium by incubating in a carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 24 hours, obtaining a solution of 4x108-4x109 CPU/ml Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae by diluting with phosphate buffered saline, (iv) adding the formaldehyde solution to the Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae prepared in step (iii), shaking at 37 degrees C, and inactivating for 24 hours to obtain Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae inactivated bacterial solution, (v) dissolving the Astragalus polysaccharide, obtaining a solution of Astragalus polysaccharide concentration of 1000-2000 mg/l, sterilizing, cooling and obtaining 1000-2000 mg/l Astragalus polysaccharide adjuvant solution and (vi) mixing the inactivated bacteria of Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae prepared and the solution of the polysaccharide adjuvant in a volume ratio of 1:1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The final concentration of the inactivated bacteria of Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae in the inactivated vaccine against porcine infectious pleuropneumonia is 2x108-2x109 CPU/ml and the final concentration of the polyose adjuvant solution is 500-1000 mg/l. The final concentration of the actinomycete in the porcine infectious pleuropneumonia inactivated vaccine is 2 x 109 CPU/mL, and the final concentration of the polyose adjuvant solution is 500 mg/l. In the step (iii), the concentration of the Actinobacillus pleuropneumoniae bacteria in solution is 4x109 CPU/ml and the step (v), the concentration of astragalus polyose adjuvant solution is 1000 mg/l. In the step (iv), the concentration of the formaldehyde solution is 0.4-0.5 %, preferably 0.4%. Preferred Method: The porcine infectious pleuropne pneumonia inactivated vaccine is administered at a dose of 2-3, preferably 2 ml/mouse.

PN WO2019051035-A1; US2020014026-A1; CN110679013-A; KR2020052263-A; EP3679613-A1; US10797308-B2; JP2020533729-W; US2021104733-A1; EP3679613-A4
TI Formation of silicon material for anode of battery, involves performing carbon-dioxide-thermic oxidation on metal silicide to form composite of one or more metal oxides, silicon, and carbon, and removing metal oxides from composite.
AU SONG M
   CHA Y
   HWAN C Y
AE UNIV WASHINGTON STATE (UNWS-C)
   UNIV WASHINGTON STATE (UNWS-C)
GA 201924229A
AB    NOVELTY - Formation of silicon material involves reacting a metal or a mixture of multiple metals with silicon in a solid state reaction to form a metal silicide, performing carbon-dioxide-thermic oxidation on metal silicide with carbon dioxide to form a composite of one or more metal oxides, silicon, and carbon, and contacting the composite with an acid to remove the metal oxides from the composite to produce particles of silicon material. The particles of silicon material individually comprise silicon formed in the thermic oxidation process, and macro-scale pores provided in silicon. The macro-scale pores correspond to the removed metal oxide and have surfaces coated with the carbon from the performed thermic oxidation process.
   USE - Formation of silicon material used for anode of battery (all claimed) e.g. lithium-ion battery. Uses include but are not limited to consumer electronics, defense application, automotive application, and aerospace application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces silicon material which is capable of improving charging capacity and charging/discharging cycle property of the battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for battery (100), which comprises an electrode (106), an electrolyte (108) comprising metal ions, in electrical communication with electrode (106), and an electrode (104) spaced apart from electrode (106) and in electrical communication with electrode (106) through the electrolyte. The electrode (104) contains the particles of silicon material individually comprising the crystalline silicon in a matrix, and macro-scale pores distributed in the matrix of crystalline silicon. The macro-scale pores have a size of more than 100 nm.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the battery incorporating the anode having the carbon-coated macro-porous silicon material.
   Lithium-ion battery (100)
   Container (102)
   Anode (104)
   Cathode (106)
   Electrolyte (108)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Reaction of metal or mixture of multiple metals with silicon is carried out by reacting metal or mixture of multiple metals and silicon with the metal or mixture of multiple metals being in excess of stoichiometric proportion to form metal silicide along with excess of metal or mixture of metal, and performing carbon-dioxide-thermic oxidation process by reacting the excess metal or mixture of metal to form additional one or more metal oxides and additional carbon. Carbon-dioxide-thermic oxidation process is carried out by flowing a gas of carbon dioxide across the formed metal silicide at 600-800 degrees C for 50-600 minutes. Formation of silicon material preferably involves reacting 2 molecules of magnesium with silicon to form magnesium silicide, and performing carbon-dioxide-thermic oxidation with magnesium silicide to form the composite of 2 molecules of magnesium oxide, silicon, and carbon, or reacting calcium with 2 molecules of silicon to form calcium disilicide, and performing carbon-dioxide-thermic oxidation process with 2 molecules of calcium disilicide to form the composite of 2 molecules of calcium oxide, 4 molecules of silicon, and carbon, and contacting the composite with the acid to remove magnesium oxide or calcium oxide from the composite to produce particles with a layered two-dimensional silicon morphology. Preferred Conditions: Reaction of metal or mixture of multiple metals with silicon is carried out in an inert reaction environment. Preferred Components: The metal or mixture of multiple metals is at least one of magnesium, calcium, or barium. The metal silicide is at least one of magnesium disilicide, calcium disilicide, and barium disilicide. The carbon is in the amorphous form and/or semi-crystalline or crystalline form. The electrode (106) is made of lithium cobalt oxide, lithium iron phosphate, lithium manganese oxide, lithium nickel oxide, lithium iron phosphate fluoride, lithium-nickel-cobalt aluminate (LiNiCoAlO2) comprising 80% nickel, 15% cobalt, and 5% aluminum, lithium cobalt oxide comprising 100% cobalt, lithium manganese oxide comprising 100% manganese, lithium-nickel-manganese-cobalt oxide comprising 33.3% nickel, 33.3% manganese, and 33.3% cobalt, lithium iron phosphate comprising 100% iron, or lithium-cobalt-nickel-manganese oxide (LiCo1/3Ni1/3Mn1/3O2). The electrolyte comprises lithium hexafluorophosphate, lithium hexafluoroarsenate, lithium perchlorate, lithium tetrafluoroborate, or lithium triflate.

   QI X
   LU Q
   GAO Y
   SONG S
AE BEIJING NIUHE RUICHEN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   BEIJING NIUHE RUICHEN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201895947A
AB    NOVELTY - Drag reducing agent comprises fluorinated                acrylate-styrene prepared by polymerization of a                fluorinated acrylate polymer, a sulfonated                polystyrene, sulfonated styrene block copolymer,                where mass ratio of fluorinated acrylate polymer,                sulfonated polystyrene, and sulfonated styrene                block copolymer is (55-65):(20-30):(10-20),                preferably 60:25:15.
   USE - Drag reducing agent for carbon dioxide dry                fracturing in carbon dioxide fracturing fluid                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The drag reducing agent has good damping                effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing drag reducing agent, which                involves:
   (A) using butyl methacrylate, isobornyl                methacrylate and methyl methacrylate as mixed                monomers, polymerizing reaction at 75-80 degrees C                for 1-3 hours, and then adding dodecafluoroheptyl                methacrylate at 75-80 degrees C for 1-3 hours to                prepare a fluorinated acrylate polymer, where mass                ratio of the butyl methacrylate, isobornyl                methacrylate, methyl methacrylate and                dodecafluoroheptyl methacrylate is                40:10:25:25;
   (B) concentrating organic solution of                polystyrene with sulfuric acid, mixing and reacting                at 40 degrees C for 10 hours to prepare a                sulfonated polystyrene, where mass ratio of the                polystyrene to the concentrated sulfuric acid is                90:10;
   (C) mixing appropriate amount of water, the                fluorinated acrylate polymer and the styrene and                stirring uniformly to obtain a mixture; and
   (D) adjusting the pH of the mixture to 7-10,                then adding sulfonated polystyrene and initiator,                and carrying out polymerization at 75-80 degrees C                for 1-2 hours to obtain fluorination                acrylate-styrene-sulfonated styrene block                copolymer, where initiator a combination of one or                more of azobisisobutyronitrile, sodium persulfate,                and sodium thiosulfate, more preferably sodium                persulfate, and sodium thiosulfate, where mass                ratio of the two is preferably (60-70):(30-40),                amount of the initiator is 0.08-0.12%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The fluorinated                  acrylate-styrene-sulfonated styrene block                  copolymer is prepared by arranging an appropriate                  amount of water, fluorinating acrylate polymer                  and styrene are mixed and stirred uniformly to                  obtain mixture, adjusting the pH of the mixture                  to 7-10, then adding sulfonated polystyrene and                  initiator, at 75-80 degrees C and polymerizing                  for 1-2 hours to obtain fluorinated                  acrylate-styrene-sulfonated styrene block                  copolymer. The sulfonated polystyrene is prepared                  by mixing and reacting an organic solution of                  polystyrene with concentrated sulfuric acid to                  prepare sulfonated polystyrene, where mass ratio                  of the polystyrene to concentrated sulfuric acid                  is 90:10. The solvent in the organic solution of                  the polystyrene comprises dichloroethane, or                  toluene and butyl a mixture of alcohols, solvent                  is a mixture of toluene and butanol, the mass                  ratio of the toluene and butanol is                  (30-40):(60-70). The organic solution of the                  polystyrene is prepared by adding polystyrene to                  a solvent and completely dissolving at not                  greater than 60 degrees C, preferably organic                  solution of the polystyrene is mixed with                  concentrated sulfuric acid and reacted for not                  greater than 40 degrees C for 10 hours. The                  fluorinated acrylate polymer is prepared by                  polymerizing butyl methacrylate, isobornyl                  methacrylate, and methyl acrylate as a mixed                  monomer, and then dodecafluoroheptyl methacrylate                  is added to continue the polymerization reaction                  to prepare the fluorinated acrylate polymer,                  preferably, butyl methacrylate, methyl isobornyl                  acrylate and methyl methacrylate are polymruized                  as mixed monomer at 75-80 degrees C for 1-3                  hours, and then the polymerized                  dodecafluoroheptyl methacrylate at 75-80 degrees                  C for 1-3 hours. The polymerization reaction is                  carried out in the presence of an initiator,                  preferably initiator includes                  azobisisobutyronitrile, sodium persulfate, and                  sodium thiosulfate or combination, preferably,                  the initiator is added in an amount of 0.05 to                  0.08%, more preferably 0.05-0.06%. Preferred                  Composition: The initiator comprises one or                  combination of azobisisobutyronitrile, sodium                  persulfate, and sodium thiosulfate, preferably                  amount of initiator added is from 0.05-0.08 wt.%,                  more preferably from 0.05-0.06 wt.%. The mass                  ratio of the butyl methacrylate, isobornyl                  methacrylate, methyl methacrylate and                  dodecafluoroheptyl methacrylate is 40:10:25:25.                  The amount of fluorinated                  acrylate-styrene-sulfonated styrene block                  copolymer in the carbon dioxide dry fracturing                  drag reducing agent is is 50-80 mg/L.

PN CN108126722-A; CN108126722-B
TI Preparing coal-tungsten/tungsten carbide composite catalyst used to hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol comprises adding ammonium tungstate into deionized water, heating, adding into coal sample, adding e.g. tungsten and calcining.
AU MA B
   ZHANG R
   LIN K
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
   UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
GA 201846500U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing coal-tungsten/tungsten carbide composite catalyst comprises (a) grinding raw coal, acid treatment, drying and sieving to obtain 100-400 meshes coal sample, (b) adding 2-10 g, preferably 5-8 g ammonium tungstate into 100-300 ml deionized water, heating to 70-100 degrees C until the solution turns into a clear solution, (c) taking the coal sample, adding the clear solution while stirring at 70-100 degrees C, adding tungsten and carbon in a mass ratio of 0.725:0.2-0.725:2 under a stirring state, maintaining for 20-60 minutes, transferring to an oven, drying at 80-150 degrees C for 12-36 hours, and (d) grinding and sieving the sample to obtain 100-400 meshes, placing into a quartz boat, transferring into a tubular furnace, calcining under inert gas atmosphere at a flow rate of 30-80 ml/minute for 0.5-12 hours at 900-1300 degrees C and heating from room temperature to a calcining temperature at a rate of 3-10 degrees C/minute.
   USE - The coal-tungsten/tungsten carbide composite catalyst is useful for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to methanol, hydrogenation dehydrogenation of ethylene and other coal chemical industries, in photocatalytic decomposition of water to hydrogen and photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: uses easily available components with high quality; uses low cost coal as carbon source; has simple carbon source pre-treatment; and ensures one-step direct synthesis of metal tungsten and tungsten carbide, which can be directly combined together to prepare the composite catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for coal W/WC composite catalyst obtained by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw coal includes at least two coals having 2-7% in Taixi coal, Yangchangwan coal or Jinfeng coal. The mass ratio of tungsten and carbon is 0.725:1. The inert gas comprises argon gas and/or nitrogen. Preferred Method: The acid treatment comprises acid treatment using 10-20 mol/l nitric acid, hydrochloric acid or sulfuric acid, where the coal is boiled in acid for more than 0.5 hours. The raw coal in the step (a): is sieved to 200-300 meshes coal. The sample in the step (d) is sieved to 200-300 meshes. The step (d) comprises grinding for 0.5-3 hours, preferably 1 hour; and calcining by heating at a rate of 5 degrees C/minute. The step (d) comprises calcining at 1000 degrees C for 3 hours.

An operation of a                second UV module is selectively controlled                according to a first comparison result. The                concentration of the carbon dioxide is compared                with the size of the second preset concentration. A                second ultraviolet module is controlled to operate                at a first power, if the concentration of the                carbon dioxide is less than the second preset                concentration. The concentration                of carbon dioxide is increased to increase the                third operation time, so as to achieve better                sterilization effect and to save electric energy.                The operation time is increased along with the                increase of the concentration of the carbon                dioxide.

PN CN113774412-A
TI Preparing isocyanate by utilizing carbon dioxide resource, comprises e.g. hydrogenating carbon dioxide, reacting obtained formic acid and alcohol, then oxidative dehydrogenating with amine, thermally decomposing obtained carbamate, and reacting amine and carbon dioxide.
AU WANG Z
   NING C
   WANG J
AE SHENZHEN YOUWEI TECHNOLOGY HOLDING GROUP (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E8631V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing isocyanate (I) by utilizing carbon dioxide resource, comprises (i) hydrogenating carbon dioxide to obtain formic acid, (ii) reacting obtained formic acid and alcohol (II) to obtain formate (III), (iii) performing oxidative dehydrogenation of formate and amine (IV), then recovering and recycling the by-product hydrogen to obtain carbamate (V), (iv) performing thermal decomposition of carbamate to obtain isocyanate and re-releases alcohol, and (v) reacting amine and carbon dioxide to obtain isocyanate product, and by-product water, where each of the above reaction steps can be carried out in batches, or continuously.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing isocyanate by utilizing carbon dioxide resource.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly, safe and efficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing isocyanate of formula (Q-NCO) (I) by utilizing carbon dioxide resource, comprises (i) hydrogenating carbon dioxide to obtain formic acid, (ii) reacting obtained formic acid and alcohol of formula (ROH) (II) to obtain formate of formula (HCO2R) (III), (iii) performing oxidative dehydrogenation of formate and amine of formula (Q-NH2) (IV), then recovering and recycling the by-product hydrogen to obtain carbamate of formula (Q-NH-C(O)-OR) (V), (iv) performing thermal decomposition of carbamate to obtain isocyanate and re-releases alcohol (ROH), and (v) reacting amine (Q-NH2) and carbon dioxide to obtain isocyanate product, and by-product water, where each of the above reaction steps can be carried out in batches, or continuously.
   R = 4-24C (hetero)aryl (optionally substituted), or 1-24C linear or branched alkane;
   Q = n-valent mono-functional or multifunctional 2-24C aliphatic or (hetero) aromatic primary amine; and
   n = 1-6.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method of carbamate comprises (either) performing electrochemical electrolytic dehydrogenation, i.e. direct electrolysis of mixture of amine (Q-NH2) and formate (HCO2R), (or) electrolyzing N,O-hemiacetal intermediate as shown in the equation as given in the specification. The preparation method of carbamate, comprises (either) performing chemical molecular oxidant oxidation i.e. directly oxidizing the mixture of amine (Q-NH2) and formate (HCO2R), (or) carrying out oxidation of N,O-hemiacetal intermediate to obtain carbamate as shown in following equation as given in the specification. The method comprises (a) hydrogenating carbon dioxide to obtain formic acid, (b) reacting formic acid with alcohol of formula (R0OH) (VI) to obtain formate of formula (HCO2R0) (VII), (c) condensing formate and amine (Q-NH2) to obtain formamide of formula (QNH-C(O)H) (VIII) and recycling the by-product alcohol (R0OH), (d) performing electrochemical direct electrolytic dehydrogenation of formamide to obtain isocyanate with re-release of hydrogen, and (e) reacting amine and carbon dioxide to obtain isocyanate product, and water by-product, where each of the reaction steps can be carried out in batches, or operated continuously. The steps (i) and (ii) in preparation process are preferably implemented in combined manner i.e. carbon dioxide hydrogenation in the presence of alcohol (ROH) directly prepares formate, and carbon dioxide is directly hydrogenated to obtain methyl formate, where alcohol (ROH) is methanol.Preferred Components: The anode/cathode (+/-) electrode material system includes platinum/platinum, platinum/lead, platinum/carbon, lead-silver/lead, carbon/carbon, carbon/platinum, carbon/lead, carbon/nickel, carbon/iron, lead dioxide/lead, nickel/lead, nickel-steel/lead, nickel/nickel, nickel/molybdenum, nickel/nickel oxide, nickel/iron, stannic oxide/titanium, graphite/graphite, 4,4-dimethyl-4-silapentane-1-ammonium trifluoroacetate/lead, diamond/nickel, diamond/diamond, platinum-titanium/steel, lead/mercury-lead, lead-silver/mercury-copper, and lead/mercury; and electrolysis method includes potentiostatic and/or galvanostatic electrolysis. The amine (Q-NH2) includes e.g. aniline, cyclohexanamine, phenylmethanamine, butan-1-amine, naphthalen-1-amine, naphthalen-2-amine, (1,1'-biphenyl)-4-amine, benzene-1,2-diamine, benzene-1,4-diamine, and 4,4'-methylenedianiline. The oxidant (O) is the terminal oxidant hydrogen peroxide, air, oxygen, ozone, quinone, alkyl peroxide (ROOH), or carboxylic acid peroxide (RCO3H); and catalytic oxidant system formed by the terminal oxidant of at least one of above and metal or non-metal oxidation catalyst.R0 = hydrogen or R.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R, R0 = methyl or ethyl.

AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a concrete mixer vehicle                (110) comprising a vehicle exhaust (114) and a                mixer tank (116). A vehicle exhaust capture system                unit (120) is located onboard the concrete mixer                vehicle. The vehicle exhaust capture system unit                comprises fluid pathways to fluidly couple the                vehicle exhaust and the mixer tank. The mixer tank                houses an uncured cementitious material mixture                (115), where a portion of carbon dioxide emission                generated by the concrete mixer vehicle and output                by the vehicle exhaust is introduced into the mixer                tank and mixes with the uncured cementitious                material mixture when the concrete mixer vehicle                generates carbon dioxide emission for carbonizing                the uncured cementitious material mixture and                forming calcium carbonate in the mixer tank.
   USE - Vehicle carbon dioxide capture and utilization                system.

PT P
PN RU2677524-C1
TI Liquid carbon dioxide pumping into oil well mobile system comprises, liquid mixture rims into production well independent injection line.
AU VOLKOV V A
   BELIKOVA V G
   PROKHOROV P E
   TURAPIN A N
   KEROSIROV V M
   VAKHRAMOV D V
AE DELTA PROM CO LTD (DELT-Non-standard)
GA 201915574C
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to the oil and gas industry, namely, to the liquid carbon dioxide into the oil producing well injection devices. Mobile system includes the liquid carbon dioxide receiving and storing device. It contains the temperature-controlled tank, feedback loop, evaporator, auxiliary pump and the high-pressure pump. Device comprises pressure in the temperature-controlled tank automatic monitoring and control system, pressure and temperature in the liquid carbon dioxide discharge line automatic monitoring and control system. There are automatic valves with electric actuators for the liquid carbon dioxide flow in the feedback loop and in the high pressure line control, as well as the liquid carbon dioxide heating device. Latter is located in the wellbore and provides the working agent at the bottomhole temperature automatic control and maintenance of not less than the carbon dioxide critical temperature Tcr=31.1 degrees C. To ensure the carbon dioxide supercritical state in the wellbore and further in the formation for the high-viscosity oil extraction, the liquid carbon dioxide injection is carried out at a temperature of at least Tcr=31.1 degrees C and pressure of not less than Pcr=7.38 MPa. At that, the mobile system additionally comprises the liquid mixture rims into the production well independent injection line: the "Delta ASPGO" composition and the dimethyl carbonate. They are injected before and after the liquid carbon dioxide injection. These rims are pumped from the TsA-320 type independent mobile unit, equipped with the components mixer using the centrifugal pump and the high-pressure pump of up to 20 MPa.
   USE - Oil and gas industry.
   ADVANTAGE - Reduction in energy costs, elimination of the gas hydrates formation risk, intensification of the hard-to-recover high-viscosity oil reserves production.
1 cl, 1 dwg
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); Q49 (Mining (E21))
MC H01-B03B3; H01-C10; H01-C11; H01-D03; H01-D06C; H01-D08; H01-D12; Q49-A; Q49-V35

PT P
PN KR1920886-B1
TI Chemical healing method for second damage prevention of fire damaged concrete structure, involves chemically healing fire-damaged concrete structure to repair and reinforce fire-damaged concrete structure.
AU HEO Y S
AE KOREA CIVIL ENG & BUILDING TECHNOLOGY (KOCT-C)
GA 2018932158
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves confirming (S110) a crack in the cracked portion of the fire damaged concrete structure. A carbon dioxide injector is prepared (S120) for injecting carbon dioxide at a high concentration into the cracked portion. The crack size of the cracked portion of the fire damaged concrete structure is compared (S130) with a reference value. The high concentration carbon dioxide is injected (S140) into the cracked portion. The carbon dioxide in the fire-damaged concrete structure is forced (S150) to penetrate the carbon dioxide at a high concentration so as to remove the carbon dioxide. The micro voids in the fire damaged concrete structure are filled simultaneously with the shrinkage reaction, when the carbonation reaction penetrates the cracked portion in the fire damaged concrete structure. The fire-damaged concrete structure is chemically healed (S160) to repair and reinforce the fire-damaged concrete structure.
   USE - Chemical healing method for second damage prevention of fire damaged concrete structure (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - By injecting the high-concentration carbon dioxide into a cracked portion of a fire-damaged concrete structure, it is possible to remove the cause of the secondary fracture behavior of the fire damaged concrete structure to prevent the expansion reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the chemical healing method for second damage prevention of fire damaged concrete structure. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for confirming a crack in the cracked portion (S110)
   Step for preparing the carbon dioxide injector (S120)
   Step for comparing the crack size of the cracked portion with a reference value (S130)
   Step for injecting the high concentration carbon dioxide into the cracked portion (S140)
   Step for forcing the carbon dioxide present in the fire-damaged concrete structure (S150)
   Step for chemically healing the fire-damaged concrete structure (S160)

PN CN109812937-A; CN109812937-B
TI Method for controlling carbon dioxide concentration, involves comparing received real-time carbon dioxide concentration value with preset carbon dioxide concentration value, and increasing speed of machine.
AU ZHENG Z
AE AUX AIR CONDITIONING CO LTD (AUXS-C)
GA 201948741S
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide concentration controlling method involves comparing a received real-time carbon dioxide concentration value with a preset carbon dioxide concentration value, calculating a difference between the real-time carbon dioxide concentration value and the preset carbon dioxide concentration value if the real-time carbon dioxide concentration value is greater than the preset carbon dioxide concentration value, sending the first signal according to the difference in a state in which the air conditioning system is operating, sending the second signal according to the difference, where the first signal is used to control the rotational speed of the motor of the fresh air system to a rotational speed value corresponding to the difference, and the second signal is used to control the compression of the air conditioning system, and increasing the speed of the machine to a value of the speed that matches the difference.
   USE - Method for controlling carbon dioxide concentration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a control device
   (2) an air conditioner.

TI Carbon-dioxide-reducing film comprises an electrically conductive material including at least one selected from a group consist of carbon nanotubes, nanographenes, and carbon paper and a proton-permeable high-molecular-weight material.
AU MANABE T
   IMANAKA Y
   ANAZAWA T
   AMADA H
AE FUJITSU LTD (FUIT-C)
   FUJITSU LTD (FUIT-C)
GA 201900179J
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon-dioxide-reducing film comprises an electrically conductive material including at least one selected from a group consist of carbon nanotubes, nanographenes, and carbon paper; a carbon-dioxide adsorbent, and a proton-permeable high-molecular-weight material.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide-reducing film.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for manufacturing carbon-dioxide-reducing film, which involves coating a substrate with a composite material including an electrically conductive material, a carbon-dioxide adsorbent, and a proton-permeable high-molecular-weight material and heating the substrate at a temperature from 100-250 degrees C; and
   (2) a carbon-dioxide-reducing apparatus comprises a carbon-dioxide-reducing electrode configured to include a carbon-dioxide-reducing film and to serve as a cathode electrode, the carbon-dioxide-reducing film configured to include an electrically conductive material including at least one selected from a group consist of carbon nanotubes, nanographenes, and carbon paper, a carbon-dioxide adsorbent, and a proton-permeable high-molecular-weight material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon-dioxide-reducing film comprises the electrically conductive material configured to be at least one selected from a group consisting of single-walled carbon nanotubes having carboxy groups on the surfaces and multi-walled carbon nanotubes having carboxy groups on the surfaces and carbon-dioxide adsorbent configured to include a metal that reduces carbon dioxide and a ligand that coordinate with the metal. The metal is at least one selected from a group consisting of nickel, copper, zinc, magnesium, iron, and cobalt. The ligand has at least one selected from a group consist of a carboxy group, a hydroxy group, an amino group, an imino group, a thiol group, a carboxylate group, and an oxime group. The ligand has at least one aromatic hydrocarbon ring, and two pairs of a carboxy group and a hydroxy group, the two pairs being bonded to the at least one aromatic hydrocarbon ring, where a carboxy group of one pair of a carboxy group and a hydroxy group is bonded to one carbon atom of two adjacent carbon atoms of the at least one aromatic hydrocarbon ring, and a hydroxy group of the one pair is bonded to the other carbon atom, and the other pair is identical. The proton-permeable high-molecular-weight material has a skeleton containing fluorine atoms and carbon atoms, and a perfluoro side chain having a sulfonate group. The substrate includes a proton-permeable high-molecular-weight material. Preferred Conditions: The content of the electrically conductive material is 1% by mass to 10% by mass relative to the carbon-dioxide adsorbent. The content of the proton-permeable high-molecular-weight material is 20% by mass to 70% by mass.

PT P
PN US9975080-B1
TI Removing carbon dioxide from combustible fuel, comprises exposing the fuel comprising carbon dioxide to ionic liquid having cation pyridinium having allyl bond and anion, and subsequent to first step, delivering the fuel for combustion.
AU LUEBKE D
   NULWALA H
   KAIL B
   SHI F
   THOMPSON R L
   SIEFERT N
AE US DEPT ENERGY (USAT-C)
GA 2018395790
AB    NOVELTY - Removing carbon dioxide from a combustible fuel, comprises: (a) exposing the fuel comprising carbon dioxide to at least one ionic liquid, where the ionic liquid comprises a cation pyridinium and an anion, the cation pyridinium comprises at least one allyl bond, and the ionic liquid is operative to absorb at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the fuel by carbon dioxide absorbent complex formation; and (b) subsequent to (a), delivering the fuel for combustion.
   USE - The methods are useful for removing carbon dioxide from a combustible fuel (claimed), capturing carbon dioxide, purifying hydrogen and natural gas sweetening.
   ADVANTAGE - The methods: utilize the ionic liquid that is sulfur tolerant, exhibits excellent carbon dioxide solubility, low water solubility, low vapor pressure, high thermal stability and selectivity over hydrogen, low viscosity and resistance to hydrogen sulfide; and provide enhanced carbon dioxide absorption in a cost effective way.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) removing carbon dioxide from a combustible fuel, comprising (a1) contacting a combustible gas with comprising of allyl pyridinium bistriflimide absorption stage to remove at least one gas contaminant from the gas, (b1) discharging from the absorption stage, where the gas has gas contaminant removed, (c1) subsequent to (a), at least one of decompressing or heating the ionic liquid containing the absorbed at least one gas contaminant to remove at least one gas contaminant, and (d1) subsequent to (c), returning the ionic liquid to the absorption stage in (a);
   (2) removing carbon dioxide from a combustible fuel, comprising (a2) exposing the fuel comprising carbon dioxide to at least one ionic liquid, where the ionic liquid comprises 1-allyl-pyridinium cation-1,1,1-trifluoro-N-(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)methanesulfonamide cation of formula (I), the ionic liquid comprises a cation and an anion, the cation contains at least one allyl bond, and the ionic liquid is operative to absorb at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the fuel by carbon dioxide absorbent complex formation, and (b2) subsequent to (a2), delivering the fuel for combustion;
   (3) removing carbon dioxide from a combustible fuel, comprising (a3) exposing the fuel comprising carbon dioxide to at least one ionic liquid, where the ionic liquid is allyl-trioctyl-phosphonium cation-bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)amide anion, where the ionic liquid comprises a cation and an anion, the cation contains at least one allyl bond and the ionic liquid is operative to absorb at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the fuel by carbon dioxide absorbent complex formation, and (b3) subsequent to (a3), delivering the fuel for combustion;
   (4) removing carbon dioxide from a combustible fuel, comprising (a4) exposing the fuel comprising carbon dioxide to at least two ionic liquids, where the two ionic liquids comprise an anion and cation, the two ionic liquids is (I) and the two ionic liquid is operative to absorb at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the fuel by carbon dioxide absorbent complex formation, and (b4) subsequent to (a4), delivering the fuel for combustion; and
   (5) removing carbon dioxide from a combustible fuel, comprising (a5) exposing the fuel comprising carbon dioxide to at least two ionic liquids, where the two ionic liquids comprise an anion and cation, at least one of the two ionic liquids is allyl-trioctyl-phosphonium cation-bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)amide anion, at least one of the two ionic liquid is operative to absorb at least a portion of the carbon dioxide from the fuel by carbon dioxide absorbent complex formation, and (b5) subsequent to (a5), delivering the fuel for combustion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: (c) subsequent to (a), treating the ionic liquid to desorb the carbon dioxide to obtain a regenerated ionic liquid, where the treating comprises at least one of heating or depressurizing the ionic liquid; (d) subsequent to (c), repeating steps (a)-(b) with the regenerated ionic liquid and further fuel; and at least two ionic liquids, where the two ionic liquids comprise an anion and a cation. In step (a), the ionic liquid has increased carbon dioxide absorption after exposure to hydrogen sulfide. Preferred Components: In step (a), the combustible fuel: containing carbon dioxide comprises a gaseous mixture; further comprises syngas; further comprises natural gas; and further comprises syngas and hydrogen sulfide. In step (a4), one of the two ionic liquids comprises allyl-trioctyl-phosphonium cation bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)amide anion.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN JP2021102198-A
TI Carbon dioxide separation device used for e.g.,            air cleaner comprises electrolyte layer containing            non-volatile electrolyte solution and redox compound, a            pair of electrode, and voltage application unit.
AU INUI Y
   TAKEDA M
   IWASAKI H
AE KURARAY CO LTD (KURS-C)
GA 202179853W
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide separation device (10)                comprises electrolyte layer (13), a pair of                electrode (11, 12) provided on both sides of the                electrolyte layer, and voltage application unit                (14) which applies a voltage between a pair of                electrodes. The pair of electrode is each an                electrode which can permeate gas. The electrolyte                layer contains non-volatile electrolyte solution                and redox compound. The redox compound is                non-volatile and is a carbon dioxide compound in                which carbon dioxide is adsorbed by electrolytic                oxidation and carbon dioxide is desorbed by                electrolytic reduction.
   USE - A carbon dioxide separation device used for                air cleaner, air-conditioner and carbon-dioxide                concentration apparatus (all claimed). Can be used                in private vehicle, bus, electric train, airplane,                and space station.
   ADVANTAGE - A carbon dioxide separation device can simply                separate carbon dioxide from gas containing carbon                dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic sectional view                of the structure of carbon dioxide separation                device.
   10Carbon dioxide separation device
   11, 12Electrode
   13Electrolyte layer
   14Voltage application unit
   15, 16Flow paths
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Devices: The pair of electrode                  consists of 1st electrode of the side which takes                  in carbon dioxide from gas containing carbon                  dioxide, and 2nd electrode of the side which                  discharges carbon dioxide from the electrolyte                  layer. The voltage application unit applies a                  voltage between the pair of electrodes so that                  the electric potential of 1st electrode may                  become lower than the electric potential of 2nd                  electrode. Preferred Components: The electrolyte                  layer consists of a gel containing the                  electrolyte solution and redox compound. The                  electrolyte solution is an ionic liquid. The pair                  of electrode is &#8805; 1 chosen from porous                  metal, a graphite, a carbon nanotube, and carbon                  fiber. Preferred Parameters: The boiling point in                  a normal pressure of redox compound is                  &#8805; 200&#176; C.

PN CN112221344-A; CN112221344-B
TI Carbon dioxide catalytic reduction device comprises friction nano generator and micro plasma assembly, micro plasma assembly includes discharge electrode and working electrode plate, loading opposite side of the working electrode plate.
AU CHENG G
   ZHANG B
   XIANG X
   LI S
   ZHAO K
   DU Z
AE UNIV HENAN (UYEH-C)
GA 2021091607
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide catalytic reduction device comprises friction nano generator and micro plasma assembly. The micro plasma assembly includes discharge electrode and working electrode plate. The discharge electrode and working electrode plate are respectively connected with two output ends of the triboelectric nanogenerator by wires. The opposite side of the working electrode plate and the discharge electrode is loaded with carbon dioxide reduction catalyst.
   USE - Carbon dioxide catalytic reduction device.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide catalytic reduction device realizes catalytic reduction reaction of carbon dioxide molecule at low temperature by mechanical energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for performing carbon dioxide catalytic reduction, which involves placing the micro plasma assembly of the carbon dioxide catalytic reduction device in environment containing carbon dioxide, making friction nano generator work to output voltage, driving micro plasma assembly to generate micro plasma, micro-plasma and carbon dioxide catalyst on the surface of the working electrode plate catalytically reducing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The friction nanogenerator is independent-layer, single-electrode, translational or sliding friction nanogenerator. The carbon dioxide reduction catalyst includes silicon oxide, nickel oxide, iron oxide, titanium oxide, zinc oxide, copper oxide, indium oxide, aluminum oxide, cobalt oxide, vanadium oxide, chromium oxide, germanium oxide, iron oxide, manganese oxide, zirconium oxide, bismuth trioxide, calcium oxide, cerium oxide, strontium titanate, magnesium oxide, cobalt tetroxide, gallium oxide and barium titanate. Preferred Conditions: The loading amount of the carbon dioxide reduction catalyst is 1-10mg/cm2. The volume content of carbon dioxide in the environment is above 1%.

PT P
PN WO2021007388-A1; US2022275268-A1
TI Recovering hydrocarbons involves injecting into            subterranean formation composition comprising carbon            dioxide and carbon-dioxide-soluble compound under            conditions favorable to diffusion into and adsorption            of carbon-dioxide-soluble compound.
AU ENICK R M
   TAPRIYAL D
   GOODMAN HANSON A
   CRANDALL D
   GOODMAN H A
AE UNIV PITTSBURGH COMMONWEALTH SYSTEM HIGH (UPIT-C)
   US DEPT ENERGY (USAT-C)
   US DEPT ENERGY (USAT-C)
   UNIV PITTSBURGH COMMONWEALTH SYSTEM HIGH (UPIT-C)
GA 202106462E
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering hydrocarbons involves injecting                into subterranean formation a composition                comprising carbon dioxide and                carbon-dioxide-soluble compound under conditions                favorable to diffusion into and adsorption of the                carbon-dioxide-soluble compound on the subterranean                formation. The oil-wetability is decreased or water                wetability or carbon dioxide-philicity is increased                in a portion the subterranean formation.
   USE - Method for recovering hydrocarbons from a                subterranean formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method recovers the hydrocarbons from                subterranean formation in a simple, cost-effective                and eco-friendly manner with improved mobility                control or conformance control, improved by                lowering pressure to provide alteration of                wettability while limiting the effect on                interfacial tension.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for altering a subterranean formation, which                involves injecting into the subterranean formation                a composition comprising carbon dioxide and                carbon-dioxide-soluble compound under conditions                favorable to diffusion into and adsorption of the                carbon-dioxide-soluble compound on the subterranean                formation to decrease the oil-wettability or                increase water wettability or                carbon-dioxide-philicity of the portion of the                subterranean formation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The                  carbon-dioxide-soluble compound is a surfactant,                  an amphiphilic polymer, or a ketone which is                  carbon-dioxide-soluble at a pressure under which                  the composition enters the subterranean                  formation. The ketone is acetone, a pentanone or                  a butanone. The carbon-dioxide-soluble compound                  is a surfactant or an amphiphilic polymer which                  comprises hydrophobic segment which is                  carbon-dioxide-philic which is attached to the                  other segment. The hydrophobic segment is                  carbon-dioxide-philic comprises a linear or                  branched alkyl group, an aromatic group, a cyclic                  group, an alkyl group attached to an aromatic or                  cyclic group, an oligomer of propylene glycol, an                  oligomer of propylene glycol attached to an alkyl                  group. The other segment is hydrophilic. The                  other segment is a polymer or oligomer of an                  alkylene oxide, ethylene oxide or propylene                  oxide. The carbon- dioxide-soluble compound is                  water soluble or oil soluble. The subterranean                  formation is an unconventional formation, a                  low-permeability formation or a moderate-                  permeability formation.

PN CN211367554-U
TI Carbon dioxide culture box gas source pipeline in-situ sterilizing device comprises an air pump, a sterilizing filter, an air inlet valve, an exhaust valve and a carbon dioxide control valve connected with the carbon dioxide culture box.
AU YE D
   XIA X
   ZHAO Z
   CHEN J
AE ZHEJIANG TAILIN MEDICAL ENG CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)

   (2) reduction (P2) of carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide, which involves contacting carbon dioxide (gas) with the catalyst compound (solid).

PN US2020391157-A1; AU2019250214-A1; TW202045240-A; TW758610-B1; US11413576-B2
TI Treating gas involves collecting gas comprising            carbon dioxide and fine particulate matter, rinsing gas            with water to obtain rinsed gas, then contacting with            basic solution in way of co-current flow to absorb            carbon dioxide in rinsed gas.
AU LIN C
   LIN C J
AE LIN C (LINC-Individual)
   LIN C (LINC-Individual)
   LIN C J (LINC-Individual)
   LIN C (LINC-Individual)
GA 2020C6029A
AB    NOVELTY - Treating a gas comprises (a) collecting a gas                comprising carbon dioxide and fine particulate                matter; (b) rinsing the gas with water to obtain a                rinsed gas; and (c) contacting the rinsed gas with                a basic solution in a way of co-current flow to                absorb the carbon dioxide in the rinsed gas by the                basic solution to obtain a treated gas and a weak                basic solution, where the pH value of the basic                solution is 9-14, and the pH of the weak basic                solution is 8-8.5.
   USE - The method is useful for treating a gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the content of both fine                particulate matter and carbon dioxide in the gas in                order to achieve the effect of reducing air                pollution; decreases the emission of greenhouse gas                and clarifies the air quality; is economical; and                is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microalgae in step                  (d) is Botryococcus braunii, Chlorella sp.,                  Crypthecodinium cohnii, Cylindrotheca sp.,                  Dunaliella primolecta, Isochrysis sp.,                  Monalanthus salina, Nannochloris sp.,                  Nannochloropsis sp., Neochloris oleoabundans,                  Nitzschia sp., Phaeodactylum tricomutum,                  Schizochytrium sp., Tetraselmis suecica,                  Arthrospira maxima, and/or Arthrospira                  platensis.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (c) is conducted                  at the temperature below 90 degrees C. The method                  comprises step (d) adding the weak basic solution                  to a microalgae culture tank containing                  microalgae. The method comprises step (e)                  evaporating the moisture content in the weak                  basic solution to obtain a solid sodium hydrogen                  carbonate. The step (c) includes preliminarily                  contacting the rinsed gas with the basic solution                  in the way of co-current flow, to absorb the                  carbon dioxide in the rinsed gas by the basic                  solution to obtain a preliminary treated gas and                  a first weak basic solution, and again contacting                  the preliminary treated gas with the basic                  solution in the way of co-current flow to absorb                  the carbon dioxide in the preliminary treated gas                  by the basic solution to obtain the treated gas                  and a second weak basic solution, where the weak                  basic solution comprises the first weak basic                  solution and the second basic weak solution. In                  step (c): the total contacting time of the basic                  solution with the rinsed gas is more than 5                  seconds; the concentration of the basic solution                  is 1-2 wt.%; the flow rate ratio of the basic                  solution to the rinsed gas is 1:200-1:800.                  Preferred Components: The basic solution is                  sodium hydroxide solution.

PN CN111348627-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide synthesis method for preparing zinc-tellurium nanoparticles, by adding zinc salt, tellurium-containing compound and reducing agent to deionized water, increasing temperature, filtering and washing.
AU WANG Y
AE SHANGHAI CUILI ELECTRONIC TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202063038C
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide synthesis method for preparing zinc- tellurium nanoparticles, involves (a) adding zinc salt, strong base, tellurium-containing compound and reducing agent to the deionized water to dissolve to obtain a solution, pumping the solution into a high-pressure reactor of a supercritical device, pumping-out the air, pumping-in carbon dioxide, stopping ventilation after 20-30 minutes, increasing the temperature to 50-70 degrees C, continuously pressurizing and pumping carbon dioxide into am autoclave, when the pressure reaches 120-150 bar, the constant temperature and pressure for 1.5-2 hours, after pressure relief, filtering the precipitate and washing to obtain zinc- tellurium nanoparticles.
   USE - The supercritical carbon dioxide synthesis method is used for preparing zinc- tellurium nanoparticles (claimed) which is used in a photo-recovery batteries, UV light-emitting diodes, and sensors.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts common raw materials, has high output, and synthesizes zinc-tellurium nanoparticles with uniform particle diameters.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The zinc salt comprises zinc chloride, zinc sulfate, and zinc acetate, and the concentration in the solution is 1-5 mmol/l, and the strong base comprises sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide, and the concentration is 0.1-0.5 mol/l. Preferred Components: The tellurium-containing compound comprises tellurium dioxide, sodium tellurite, and/or potassium tellurite, and the reducing agent comprises sodium borohydride, potassium borohydride, and/or hydrazine hydrate, where the molar concentration is 10-20 times that of zinc salt. Preferred Method: The method comprises carrying-out washing alternately with deionized water and ethanol, using centrifuge sedimentation or suction filtration equipment to filter after washing, redispersing the filtered product in deionized water or ethanol, filtering repeatedly until the pH of the filtrate is 7, and washing with ethanol for the last time to facilitate drying, performing drying in a vacuum oven at 50-80 degrees C, where the vacuum drying condition is that the degree of vacuum is not greater than 133 Pa.

   USE - Catalyst used for dehydrogenation of ethane to ethylene by carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has small pores, high hydrothermal stability and high specific surface area. It has surface protonic acid centers and exchangeable cations, and good affinity for carbon dioxide.    DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the catalyst comprising subjecting molecular sieve carrier to calcination dehydration pretreatment; adding molecular sieve carrier to chromium salt aqueous solution at 60-80 degrees C, and collecting solid; and drying solid and calcining to obtain catalyst.

Preferred Method: The drying is carried out at 80-110 degrees C for 8-20 hours. The baking is done at 450-600 degrees C for 2-20 hours.

TI Processing semi-water gas shift gas to ammonia synthesis gas comprises carrying out desulfurization of the shifted gas, adding pure oxygen, and establishing catalytic conversion of carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide.
AU WANG W
   YANG H
   ZHONG J
AE YANG H (YANG-Individual)
GA 201875869T
AB    NOVELTY - Processing semi-water gas shift gas to ammonia synthesis gas comprises carrying out desulfurization of the shifted gas, where the gas composition comprises 57 wt.% hydrogen, 24 wt.% nitrogen, 0.1 wt.% oxygen, 1.5 wt.% or less of carbon monoxide, 18 wt.% or more of carbon dioxide, adding 0.3 wt.% pure oxygen or the same amount of air into the oxygen required for carbon monoxide conversion, and establishing catalytic conversion of carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide, converting carbon monoxide at concentration of 0.3-1.9 wt.% to carbon dioxide, where the residual concentration of carbon monoxide is less than 1 ppm, sending the conversion gas into the fine deoxidation step, sending pressure swing adsorption, and removing the carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide to below 10 ppm, and which becomes ammonia synthesis gas.
   USE - The method is useful for processing semi-water gas shift gas to ammonia synthesis gas.

TI Regeneration of gas sorbent in capture process,            involves receiving to-be-regenerated solid sorbent of            carbon dioxide gas, generating heat by combusting fuel            with oxidizer, and regenerating sorbent using generated            heat.
AU CHEN D
   ROUT K R
   STRAND A O R
   STRAND A
AE FJELL BIODRY AS (FJEL-Non-standard)
   FJELL BIODRY AS (FJEL-Non-standard)
   FJELL BIODRY AS (FJEL-Non-standard)
GA 201871836E
AB    NOVELTY - Regeneration of gas sorbent in capture                process, involves receiving a to-be-regenerated                solid sorbent (a) of carbon dioxide gas, generating                heat by combusting a fuel with an oxidizer in                presence of catalyst, regenerating the sorbent                using generated heat to indirectly heat the sorbent                so that the sorbent releases carbon dioxide gas,                and outputting the regenerated sorbent (b) and                released carbon dioxide. The capture process                involves recirculating the sorbent between a gas                capturing system and regenerating reactor                system.
   USE - Regeneration of gas sorbent used for reducing                carbon dioxide in gas capture system (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the efficiency                of gas capture system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) regenerator system;
   (2) gas capture system; and
   (3) method for capturing gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the gas                capture system.
   Heat exchanger (1)
   Combustor (9)
   Moving bed calciner (11)
   Transport pipe (16)
   Cooler (19)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sorbent (a) and                  sorbent (b) comprise a metal carbonate,                  preferably calcium carbonate, a natural and/or                  synthetic metal oxide, preferably calcium oxide,                  and a binder. The amount of metal oxide in the                  sorbent (a) is greater than in the sorbent (b).                  The amount of metal oxide in the sorbent (a) is                  less than in the sorbent (b). The sorbent                  comprises several different sorbents for                  respective several different gases. The oxidizer                  is a gas, preferably carbonaceous gas comprising                  oxygen, such as air or flue gas. The catalyst is                  nickel, cobalt, ruthenium, rhenium, palladium or                  platinum, preferably mixture of nickel and                  cobalt. The generated heat is transferred by a                  heat loop to regenerate the sorbent. The heat                  loop comprises one or more of liquefied helium,                  air, carbon dioxide or sodium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fuel is a                  carbonaceous based fuel comprising one or more of                  methane, natural gas, synthesis gas, biogas,                  gasified coal or coal dust.

PN CN108299146-A; CN108299146-B
TI Method for isomerizing alpha-pinene, involves adjusting carbon dioxide flow and separator pressure, transferring alpha-pinene, subjecting reaction product to tubular reaction, entering reaction product into separator to complete separation.
AU LI J
   FU H
   XIE Y
   YANG Y
   GONG X
   ZHOU X
   WANG H
   SU Y
   HONG Y
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
   XIAMEN ZHONGKUN CHEM CO LTD (XIAM-Non-standard)
GA 201859072U
AB    NOVELTY - The method for isomerizing alpha -pinene, involves (1) setting the temperature of the preheater, reactor and heat exchanger, continuously flowing carbon dioxide to the system before the preheater, and adjusting the carbon dioxide flow and the separator pressure in the system to jointly control the fluid in the reactor, maintaining stable pressure and temperature, (2) transferring alpha -pinene to carbon dioxide before the inlet of the preheater by a transfer pump, forming mixture of alpha -pinene and carbon dioxide, passing the mixture through preheater into the tubular reactor, subjecting reaction product to tubular reaction, connecting outlet of reactor the heat exchanger, and entering the reaction product into separator to complete the separation, after heat recovery in the heat exchanger.
   USE - Method for isomerizing alpha -pinene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables isomerization of alpha-pinene with single pass conversion rate of 95.7% and selectivity of 39.2%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide is set at a pressure of 7 MPa. The separator is set at pressure of 6 MPa. Preferred Process: The separator is used to separate carbon dioxide and reaction products, and carbon dioxide is reused.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The step (1) comprises controlling temperature of preheater at 100-300 degrees C, temperature of reactor at 300-700 degrees C and temperature of heat exchanger at 50-100 degrees C. In step (S1), the residence time is 18 seconds. Preferred Process: The composition in the mixture of alpha-pinene and carbon dioxide is determined based on product yield and set conditions.

TI Method for recovery of hydrocarbons from Fischer-Tropsch tail gas, involves providing hydrocarbon rich Fischer-Tropsch tail gas which includes hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide.
AU SMIT J
AE SASOL SOUTH AFRICA PTY LTD (SASO-C)
   SASOL SOUTH AFRICA LTD (SASO-C)
   SASOL SOUTH AFRICA LTD (SASO-C)
   SASOL SOUTH AFRICA LTD (SASO-C)
GA 201841098J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a hydrocarbon rich Fischer-Tropsch tail gas which includes hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide. The hydrocarbon rich Fischer-Tropsch tail gas (22) is compressed to provide a compressed hydrocarbon rich Fischer-Tropsch tail gas. The compressed hydrocarbon rich Fischer-Tropsch tail gas is contacted with a lean oil to recover the hydrocarbons and to produce hydrocarbon rich oil. The carbon dioxide is stripped from the hydrocarbon rich oil at a pressure which is below the pressure at which the hydrocarbon rich Fischer-Tropsch tail gas is contacted with the lean oil.
   USE - Method for recovery of hydrocarbons from Fischer-Tropsch tail gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide can easily be stripped from the hydrocarbon rich condensate at the lower pressure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of hydrocarbons from Fischer-Tropsch tail gas.
   Carbon dioxide stipper (20)
   Hydrocarbon rich Fischer-Tropsch tail gas (22)
   Fischer-tropsch Hydrocarbon Condensate (24)
   Tail gas (36)
   Hydrocarbon rich condensate (66)

   ZHANG G

GA 202234697Y

   USE - Method for mineralizing carbon monoxide with                titanium-containing blast furnace slag or                chlorinated tailings and red gypsum, used in                industrial solid waste treatment and carbon dioxide                emission reduction applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient utilization of                titanium and aluminum elements in                titanium-containing blast furnace slag, chlorinated                tailing slag and red gypsum and mineralization and                fixation of carbon dioxide, and is green and                environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves adding the formed pellets to muffle                  furnace or rotary kiln with exhaust gas                  absorption, controlling the temperature of muffle                  furnace or rotary kiln at 350-450&#176;C,                  filling tail gas absorption device with calcium                  oxide, and controlling roasting time for 30-90                  minutes, where the mass ratio of pellet and                  liquid volume during leaching process is 1:(2-5)                  (g/mL), after leaching is completed, filtering                  and washing with water for several times to                  obtain leaching residue of calcium and leaching                  solution containing titanium, aluminum and                  magnesium, adding calcium-containing leaching                  residue and ammonium bicarbonate solution to                  mineralization reactor, adding aqueous ammonia to                  adjust pH, where the mass ratio of carbonate and                  calcium is 3:1, and the pH is greater than 10,                  controlling the mineralization reaction                  temperature at 30-50&#176;C for 60-180                  minutes, placing the leaching solution containing                  titanium, aluminum and magnesium in cooling                  crystallization device, and controlling cooling                  temperature at 5-20&#176;C, and crystallizing                  for 6-24 hours, hydrolyzing the crystallization                  mother liquor, and controlling hydrolysis                  temperature at 80-100&#8451;, and controlling                  hydrolysis pH to 7 to obtain metatitanic acid,                  calcining metatitanic acid at about                  1000&#176;C to obtain titanium white, passing                  leaching solution of removing titanium and                  aluminum components into carbon dioxide and to                  obtain ammonia, and controlling mineralization                  temperature at 30-50&#8451; for 30-120                  minutes, and filtering the obtained mineralized                  slurry to obtain solid magnesium carbonate,                  mineralization mother liquor containing ammonia,                  sulfate and carbonate. The mineralized mother                  liquor is evaporated to obtain solid ammonium                  sulfate. The titanium-containing blast furnace                  slag or the chlorinated tailings, red gypsum and                  ammonium sulfate are mixed and pelletized.

   USE - The method for preapring the catalyst useful in methane-carbon dioxide reforming reaction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high reaction activity, and the active metal is not easy to sinter and carbon deposit.

In the step (ii), in the solution of the active metal salt, the mass-volume ratio of the active metal salt to water is 10-15 g/100 ml. In the step (iii), the calcination is performed at a temperature of 600-800degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours. In the step (iv), the hydrothermal reaction is perrformed at 35-200degrees Celsius, at a pressure of 0-3 MPa for 0.2-10hours. In the step (v), the reduction reaction is performed at 400-850degrees Celsius for 1-5 hours.Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the active metal salt is nickel nitrate, nickel acetate, cobalt nitrate, cobalt acetate, ferric nitrate, chloroplatinic acid, ammonium chlororhodate, ammonium tetrachloropalladate, silver acetate and/or chloroauric acid. The average particle diameter of the aluminium nitride powder is 350-450 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The methane and carbon dioxide are reacted under the catalysis of the aluminum nitride-based catalyst. The reaction temperature is 600-950degrees Celsius, the reaction pressure is 0-10 bar and the reaction time is 100-300 hours.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The zirconium-based metal organic framework material has a grain diameter of 0.06-5 mu m, a specific surface area of 600-1300 m2/g, and a regular octahedral microstructure. Preferred Method: The microwave-assisted synthesis involves dissolving zirconium oxychloride octahydrate in N,N-dimethylformamide, stirring under water bath condition of 25 degrees C, adding fumaric acid, stirring to obtain a solution containing 0.01-0.6 mol/L zirconium oxychloride octahydrate and 0.01-0.6 mol/L fumaric acid, adding formic acid to the solution, preparing into a mixed solution, stirring, adding the mixed solution to a microwave reactor, carrying out microwave reaction at 60-140 degrees C for 0.1-6 hours, washing the reaction product, filtering, and drying. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of zirconium oxychloride and formic acid in the mixed solution is 1:10-1:250.

   USE - Propane low temperature catalytic combustion catalyst with high thermal stability used for completely oxidizing propane catalytic combustion to carbon dioxide (claimed).

   (B) obtaining carrier mullite SmMn2O5 by deposition precipitation method;
   (C) taking mullite and cobalt nitrate as raw material; and
   (D) obtaining Co3O4/SmMn2O5 of the needed load amount by deposition precipitation method, where the mass ratio of the mullite and the cobalt nitrate is determined according to the load ratio of the Co3O4/SmMn2O5.

The mass ratio of the samarium nitrate hexahydrate to the tetrahydrate manganese acetate is 1:1. The mass ratio of the cobalt nitrate to the mullite is 3:5. Preferred Process: The method for preparing propane low temperature catalytic combustion catalyst with high thermal stability further involves centrifuging the product obtained by the deposition precipitation method, water washing, alcohol washing, drying to remove impurity, then calcining at high temperature to obtain the product.

PN CN112544420-A
TI Preparing selenium-enriched extract used for e.g. lowering blood fat, by performing soilless cultivation of live seedlings of selenium-enriched plants, vacuum freeze-drying, and extracting using carbon dioxide and hydrophilic ionic liquid.
AU WU Q
   LIU F
AE WUHAN YUCHEN INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 2021368808
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a selenium-enriched extract using selenium-enriched plants as raw materials involves (a) performing soilless cultivation of live seedlings of selenium-enriched plants in imitating the wild environment, (b) vacuum freeze-drying the cultivated selenium-rich plant live seedlings, and (c) subjecting the dried selenium-enriched plant live seedlings to microwave extraction by carbon dioxide and hydrophilic ionic liquid to obtain the selenium-enriched extract.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a selenium-enriched extract, which is used for preparing beverages and product for providing antioxidation effect, lowering blood fat, scavenging free radicals, improving immunity, and calming the nerves (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method achieves the mutual solubility of hydrophilic ionic liquids with lipophilic and hydrophilic organics, and improves the extraction rate and the extraction efficiency by synergistic extraction with the organic extracting liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves performing soilless cultivation of selenium-enriched plant seedlings in a wild environment, preferably in the foam house of the greenhouse, and equipping the large shed foam house with a physical sterilization system, a spray mechanism, a fill light, a carbon dioxide generator, a ventilation exchange fan, an electric field generator and a heating mechanism. The cultivation process involves selecting seeds, soaking seeds to promote germination, cultivating by adjusting the temperature suitable for germinating seed, promoting seed germination, and growing seedlings. The vacuum freeze-drying is performed by selecting live seedlings of selenium-rich plants, cleaning, placing the plant seedlings in boiling water or hot steam for heat treatment, cooling, dehydrating, placing the dehydrated selenium-rich plant seedlings in the quick-freezing room, freezing and crushing into powder. The microwave extraction is performed by preparing hydrophilic ionic liquid, passing carbon dioxide into the hydrophilic ionic liquid to obtain a highly polar ionic liquid, mixing the selenium-rich plant live seedling powder, performing microwave extraction, stopping the introduction of carbon dioxide, converting the highly polar ionic liquid back to the hydrophilic ionic liquid, obtaining the extract, which is separated into an organic layer and an ionic liquid layer, removing the solvent from the organic layer to obtain an organic substance, adding deionized water to the ionic liquid layer, fully shaking to extract hydrophilic substances to obtain a water body fluid, concentrating to obtain a concentrated water extract, vortexing, centrifuging the concentrated water extract and organic matter, taking the supernatant to obtain the selenium-enriched extract A, combining the lower organic phases and spray-drying to obtain the selenium-rich extract B. Preferred Components: The greenhouse foam house comprises several cultivation rooms comprising a greenhouse, a foam layer and a shelf. The greenhouse is supported by the shelf, and the foam layer is located inside the greenhouse. The shelf comprises several longitudinal frames and several inclined beams, which are detachably spliced together to form a house structure.
   ACTIVITY - Antilipemic; Immunostimulant; Tranquilizer.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA.    AREFEV YU A

AE CENTRAL AEROHYDRODYNAMIC INSTITUTE (AERO-Non-standard)
GA 2021160621

Sublimation is carried out in a homogeneous mode of the fluidised bed, feeding compressed gas into the heat exchanger chamber at preset pressure at supercritical flow at constant flow rate.
   USE - Heat exchange.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is increase in cooling capacity due to intensive evaporation of carbon dioxide in boiling layer and operation of the fluidised bed in a homogeneous mode due to stabilization of compressed gas flow supplied to the heat exchanger, at critical pressure drop at the inlet, which ensures uniform blowing of the test object.

   LI X (LIXX-Individual)

AB    NOVELTY - Nanocomposite catalyst comprises: a support; a                multiplicity of nanoscale metal oxide clusters                coupled to the support; and at least one metal atom                coupled to each of the nanoscale metal oxide                clusters.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalyzing a                reaction (claimed) comprising carbon monoxide                oxidation and water-gas-shift, reforming carbon                dioxide and methanol, and oxidation of natural                gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is metal oxide for stable metal                single atom and cluster catalysts.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) fabricating (M1) the nanocomposite                catalyst, comprising forming nanoscale metal oxide                clusters comprising a first metal on a support, and                depositing at least one metal atom comprising a                second metal on the nanoscale metal oxide clusters;                and
   (2) catalyzing (M2) a reaction, comprising                contacting the nanocomposite with reactants, where                the reaction comprises carbon oxide oxidation,                water-gas-shift reaction, reforming of carbon                dioxide and methanol, or oxidation of natural                gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The support comprises:                  a refractory material having a surface area of at                  least 50 m2/g or at least 100 m2/g; and silica,                  alumina, magnesia, zirconia, cordierite, mullite                  and/or perovskite, where the support is powdered.                  The nanoscale metal oxide clusters comprise                  cerium oxide (CeO(x)), cobalt oxide (CoO(x)),                  iron oxide (FeO(x)), titanium oxide (TiO(x)),                  copper oxide (CuO(x)), nickel oxide (NiO(x)),                  manganese oxide (MnO(x)), neiobium oxide (NbO(x))                  and/or zirconium oxide (ZrO(x)), (where x is not                  defined) preferably cerium dioxide, cobalt                  tetraoxide, ferric oxide, titanium dioxide,                  copper oxide, neiobium oxide, manganese dioxide,                  niobium pentoxide and/or zirconium dioxide. The                  metal atom comprises at least one transition                  metal atom, and at least one precious metal atom,                  where the metal atom comprises platinum,                  platinum, rhodium, gold, ruthenium and/or                  iridium. The high-surface-area support is free of                  direct contact with the metal atom. The support                  comprises silicon dioxide and the metal oxide                  clusters comprise CeO(x), CoO(x), CuO(x) and/or                  FeO(x). The metal atoms comprise metal clusters                  having 2-100 metal atoms. The nanoscale metal                  oxide clusters have a dimension of 0.5-10 nm. In                  the method (M1), the support is free of direct                  contact with the second metal.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
