TI Meter blocking preventing method, involves adding absorption liquid with low concentration to absorption tower by absorption pump, and leading ammonia gas and carbon dioxide gas into the absorption tower.
AU HAN L
   LIU Y
   BIAN X
   QIN Z
AE ANHUI JINMEI ZHONGNENG CHEM CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202106893H
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves adding absorption liquid with low concentration to an absorption tower by an absorption pump, and leading ammonia gas and carbon dioxide gas into the absorption tower. Liquid level and pressure in the absorption tower are measured at a short pipe by an instrument. High concentration methylamine solution is diluted at the short pipe. The high concentration of methylamine solution is prevented from crystallizing on a surface of a pressure measuring membrane. A valve is closed when the concentration of the ammonium liquid in the absorption tower is low.
   USE - The method is useful for preventing meter blockage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method solves the problem that the crystallization temperature of ammonium formate liquid is high in the existing technology; it is easy to form crystal on the pressure measuring film, which causes the problem that the measuring data is not accurate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of meter blocking preventing.

PT P
PN CN112195560-A
TI Preparing natural antibacterial facial mask base cloth comprises e.g. firstly using raspberries as raw materials, extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, mixing with deacetyl konjac glucomannan, and electrospinning.
AU SONG R
AE SONG R (SONG-Individual)
GA 202106730V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing natural antibacterial facial mask base cloth comprises (i) firstly using raspberries as raw materials, extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide fluid to obtain raspberry extract, mixing with deacetyl konjac glucomannan to make spinning solution, and electrospinning to obtain fiber I, (ii) modifying the fullerene with silk fibroin to obtain the intermediate product, mixing with lactic acid, polymerizing to obtain modified fullerene, melting and spinning to obtain fiber II, and (iii) finally mixing the fiber I and fiber II to form a spun-laced non-woven fabric.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing natural antibacterial facial mask base cloth.
   ADVANTAGE - The cloth: has excellent liquid retention properties and antibacterial properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a natural antibacterial facial mask base cloth prepared by above mentioned method; and
   (2) a natural antibacterial facial mask prepared by the mask base cloth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the extracting with supercritical carbon dioxide fluid is carried out at 45-55 degrees C under 9-10 MP with 20-23 l/hour carbon dioxide flow rate for 2-3 hours. The deacetyl konjac glucomannan is prepared by firstly adding 1 pts. wt. konjac glucomannan to 7-9 pts. wt. 30-40 vol.% ethanol aqueous solution, adding 0.8-1 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide, heating at 30-40 degrees C, allowing to stand to swell for 50-70 minutes, adjusting the pH value to 6-8, filtering and taking the solid, washing the solid for 2-3 times with 30-40 vol.% ethanol aqueous solution, and drying to constant weight at 70-90 degrees C. The spinning solution is prepared by adding raspberry extract and deacetyl konjac glucomannan to N,N-dimethylformamide and uniformly ultrasonically oscillating, where the dosage of N,N-dimethylformamide is 4-5 times the weight of raspberry extract. The electrospinning is carried out taking the wire outlet is a No. 6 needle with a voltage of 15-18 kV, the distance from a nozzle to a receiver is 14-16 cm, the jet flow rate is 0.3-0.4 ml/hour, and the relative humidity is below 40%. In the step (ii), the intermediate product is prepared by firstly adding 1 pts. wt. silk fibroin and 0.8-1 pts. wt. sodium hydroxide to 8-10 pts. wt. 30-40 vol.% ethanol aqueous solution, uniformly stirring to obtain the silk fibroin solution, dropping the silk fibroin solution to 10-12 pts. wt. 60-70 wt.% fullerene methanol solution, stirring and carrying out the reaction for 6-8 hours, centrifuging and taking the precipitate, washing with absolute ethanol for 3-4 times, and drying. The modified fullerene is prepared by firstly adding 1 pts. wt. intermediate product to 2-3 pts. wt. lactic acid, adding 0.5-0.8 pts. wt. anhydrous stannous chloride, introducing nitrogen gas, allowing to stand for 5-8 minutes, vacuumizing, carrying out the reaction at 110-120 degrees C for 1-2 hours, continuously heating at 155-165 degrees C, incubating and stirring for 3-4 hours, adding the obtained reaction product into dichloromethane, uniformly stirring, dropping deionized water to obtain precipitation, filtering and drying. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the mass ratio of raspberry extract to konjac glucomannan is 1:0.2-0.3.
   ACTIVITY - Antibacterial.

PN CN111704632-A
TI Recycling waste circuit board flame retardant comprises separating waste circuit board from components to obtain light board, coarsely crushing to obtain scraps, transferring to liquid nitrogen tank, freezing, grinding and recovering.
AU JIANG L
   WU D
   ZHANG W
   MIAO C
   HU G
   LUO T
   ZHANG S
   FANG H
   LI H
AE ANHUI CHAOYUE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (ANHU-Non-standard)
   ANHUI PROVINCIAL BUREAU COAL GEOLOGY EXP (ANHU-Non-standard)
   UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 202097597L
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling waste circuit board flame retardant comprises (1) separating waste circuit board from the components to obtain light board, coarsely crushing to obtain scraps, (2) transferring scraps obtained in (1) to a liquid nitrogen tank, freezing at 200 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, and grinding and pulverizing to obtain a powder, (3) separating powder by electrostatic separation to obtain resin powder and metal powder, and (4) placing resin powder into the mixer of the supercritical reaction equipment, feeding liquid carbon dioxide into the mixer from a cylinder through a plunger pump, mixing, contacting, transferring mixture to the separation tank for solid-liquid separation, recovering carbon dioxide from the liquid extract to obtain the required crude flame retardant.
   USE - The method is useful for recycling waste circuit board flame retardant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The particle size of the coarsely pulverized scraps is 10-15 mm. In the step (2), the particle size of the powder after fine pulverization is less than or equal to 0.2 mm. Preferred Method: In the step (4), the supercritical extraction condition are: adjusting the pressure to 20 MPa in the mixer, and extracting at 40-50 degrees C for 40-60 minutes. In the step (4), adding methanol equivalent to 5-8% of resin powder is added as an additive.

PN CN111457385-A; CN212157171-U
TI Device for processing plasma continuous gasification cracking of medical waste, has gasification cracking furnace main body that is provided with first plasma torch, and ash outlet that is provided with sealed ash discharging device.
AU WANG Z
   ZHANG B
AE SHANGHAI DISHENG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTIO (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202072648X
AB    NOVELTY - The device comprises a gasification cracking furnace main body is provided with a first plasma torch. A feeding port is provided with a first push rod. An ash outlet is provided with a sealed ash discharging device. A medical waste is provided in the gasification cracking furnace, which is provided according to the position of the distance plasma torch and the air inlet opening. An oxidizing area that generates a large amount of carbon dioxide. The medical waste is provided in the reducing area, which is used in reduction reaction with the heat generated by the oxidation area and the carbon dioxide.

PN CN111188053-A; CN111188053-B
TI Preparing carbonate by using by-product of Kolbe coupling comprises carrying out Kolbe coupling reaction of carboxylate at anode of electrolytic cell and by-produced carbon dioxide undergoes reduction reaction with alcohol.
AU DU W
   CHEN C
   LIU S
   LI J
   ZHAO W
   HUANG Z
   SONG J
   LIU Z
   LI Y
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 202046542U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbonate by using by-product of Kolbe coupling comprises carrying out the Kolbe coupling reaction of the carboxylate at the anode of the electrolytic cell and the by-produced carbon dioxide undergoes a reduction reaction with the alcohol at the cathode of the electrolytic cell to produce carbonate. The mass ratio of the carboxylate to alcohol is 1:1-1:10, more preferably 1:3-1:5.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbonate by using by-product of Kolbe coupling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly and economical; has high product yield and easy electrolyzer structure; is simple and has low equipment investment and good industrialization prospects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a cathode material for an electrolytic cell for the reaction of preparing carbonate by using the by-product of Kolbe coupling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carboxylate is sodium n-valerate, sodium n-hexanoate, sodium n-heptanoate, sodium n-octanoate, sodium n-nonanoate, sodium n-decanoate, sodium n-undecanoate, potassium monomethyl glutarate, hexyl monomethyl potassium diacid, potassium monomethyl pimelate, potassium monomethyl suberate, sodium monohexyl glutarate, sodium monoethyl adipate, sodium monoethyl adipate and suberic acid sodium monoethyl ester, preferably sodium n-octanoate, sodium n-hexanoate, potassium monomethyl adipate, potassium monomethyl adipate, sodium monohexyl glutarate. The alcohols are methanol, ethanol, isopropanol, butanol, 4-methylbenzyl alcohol, 4-chlorobenzyl alcohol, benzyl alcohol and 2-phenylethanol, preferably methanol, ethanol, isopropyl alcohol, propanol, butanol and benzyl alcohol. The carbon dioxide in the reduction reaction comes from the anode reaction, no additional carbon dioxide is required. The reaction adopts the diaphragmless electrolysis method and/or the raw material carboxylate and alcohol are added to the electrolytic cell at the same time. The anode material is platinum, rhodium, iridium, copper, zinc, graphite, platinum-plated titanium, platinum-plated tantalum, platinum-iridium alloy containing 90 wt.% platinum, 10 wt.% iridium, preferably platinum, graphite, titanium-plated platinum, platinum-iridium alloys containing 90 wt.% platinum and 10 wt.% iridium. The cathode material is a base alloy and a coating covered on the surface. The base material is an alloy composed of lead, antimony, tin, and cadmium, where the total mass of the alloy composed of lead, antimony, tin, and cadmium, the mass fraction of lead and cadmium is 80-99%, preferably 90-95%. The mass ratio of lead to cadmium is 1:0.5-1.5, preferably 1:0.8-1.2. The sum of the mass fractions of antimony and tin is 1-20%, preferably 5-10%, where the mass ratio of antimony to tin is 1:0.5-1.5, preferably 1:0.8-1.2. The surface covering coating is rare earth metal elements, preferably binary metals and/or ternary metals in lanthanum, scandium, yttrium, cerium, praseodymium, samarium, terbium and scandium. The binary metals are lanthanum and scandium, lanthanum and yttrium and ternary metals are lanthanum cerium praseodymium, lanthanum samarium terbium and lanthanum samarium scandium.

PN CN111054098-A
TI Regenerating solvent containing acidic gas, where acidic gas comprises carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, and sulfur dioxide, involves separating acidic gas from solvent by microwave heating the solvent containing the acidic gas.
AU MAO S
   WANG D
   CHEN X
   HUANG H
   HUANG Z
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 2020367058
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating solvent containing acidic gas involves separating acidic gas from solvent by microwave heating the solvent containing the acidic gas.
   USE - Method for regenerating solvent containing acidic gas, where acidic gas comprises carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide, and sulfur dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to regenerate solvent containing acidic gas, which heats solvent by microwave, changes gas-liquid equilibrium condition inside solvent, and separates acidic gas from solvent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for regenerating solvent containing acidic gas, which comprises a tank body, a first clapboard and a second clapboard arranged perpendicular to axial direction in tank body, where tank body is divided into inlet zone, outlet zone and regeneration zone from top to bottom by first clapboard and second clapboard, and regeneration zone comprises a regeneration gas collection cavity, a microwave emission tube and a degassing pipe which are provided along the axial direction in regeneration gas collection cavity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The microwave power of microwave heating treatment is 10-120kW, preferably 15-60kW based on amount of solvent 1t/h, and treatment time is 1-10 minutes, preferably 1-5 minutes. The solvent containing acidic gas is regenerated by using a regeneration device. The method involves allowing a solvent containing acidic gas to enter inlet zone through solvent inlet, flowing into degassing pipe through inlet on the first partition, receiving microwave treatment in degassing pipe to remove acidic gas, and passing gas from upper outlet of second partition into outlet area and discharging it through the solvent outlet to obtain a solvent with separated acidic gas. The gas removed from the degassing pipe enters regeneration gas collection cavity through side wall of degassing pipe, and is then discharged through regeneration gas outlet. Preferred Components: The solvent comprises desulfurization and decarbonization absorbent, preferably one or more of methyldiethanolamine, monoethanolamine, piperazine, polyethylene glycol dimethyl ether, hydroxyethylethylenediamine, hexaethyltetramine, diethylenetriamine, diethylenediamine, ethanolamine, sulfolane, and potassium carbonate.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Condition: The optimum temperature for growth is 1 to 30 degrees C for flowers, 1 to 30 degrees C for vegetables, and 1 to 30 degrees C for fruit trees. The temperature in the house is below the optimum temperature for growth, it is -10 degrees C to less than 1 degrees C, and when it exceeds the optimum temperature for growth, nanobubble water is irrigated at room temperature at temperatures in the range of more than 30 degrees C and 45 degrees C, respectively.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Complex catalyst comprises a zirconium-based oxide and a modified acidic molecular sieve.
AU LIU S
   ZHU W
   LIU Z
   NI Y
   LIU H
   LIU Y
   MA X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201987383G
AB    NOVELTY - Complex catalyst comprises a zirconium-based oxide and a modified acidic molecular sieve, the mass content of the zirconium-based oxide is 10-90 wt.%, the mass content of the modified acidic molecular sieve is 10-90 wt.% and the modified acidic molecular sieve is an acidic molecular sieve treated by a pre-adsorbed alkali.
   USE - Used as complex catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the complex catalyst, comprising (i) obtaining zirconium-based oxide, (ii) obtaining a modified acidic molecular sieve and (iii) combining the components containing the zirconium-based oxide in the step (i) and the modified acidic molecular sieve in the step (ii) by ultrasonic assisted chemical compounding and/or physical compounding; and
   (2) preparing the ethylene, comprising passing a raw material gas containing a synthesis gas through a reactor equipped with a complex catalyst to obtain ethylene, where the synthesis gas includes carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and the molar ratio carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide is 1:0.2-4:0-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic alkali-containing gas includes a carrier gas and an organic base, the carrier gas is nitrogen, helium, carbon dioxide, argon or hydrogen, the organic base is trimethylamine, diethylamine, triethylamine, pyridazine, pyrimidine, pyrazine, pyridine, imidazole, N-methylimidazole, N-ethylimidazole, N-propylimidazole or N-isopropylimidazole and the volume fraction of the organic base in the organic base-containing gas is 0.1-10%. The zirconium-based oxide is of formula (XaMb(ZrO2)(1-a-b) (I).X = oxide of at least one elements including Si, Al, Ti, Ce or La;M = oxide of at least one of Cu, Ag, Zn, Mn, Y, Nb, Ga, In or Cr;a = 0.02-0.9; andb = 0-0.8.Preferred Method: The step of pre-adsorbing alkali treatment comprises contacting the acidic molecular sieve with a gas containing an organic base for pre-adsorption alkali treatment, where the temperature of the pre-adsorption alkali treatment is 150-350 degrees C and the pre-adsorption alkali treatment time is 0.5-4 hours and the mass space velocity of the organic base-containing gas is 300-6000 ml/g/hour. The step of pre-adsorbing alkali treatment comprises activating the acidic molecular sieve in an inert gas atmosphere, adjusting the temperature to a pre-adsorption alkali treatment temperature, contacting with a gas containing an organic base to carry out pre-adsorption alkali treatment, saturating the adsorption and purging the mixture to room temperature to obtain a modified acidic molecular sieve, where the activation temperature is 300-500 degrees C and the activation time is 3-5 hours. The acidic molecules are screened from molecular sieves having a MOR topology, the molecular sieve having a FER topology, a eutectic molecular sieve comprises a MOR topology and a FER topology, a mixed crystal molecular sieve includes a MOR topology and a FER topology, the molecular sieve having a MOR topology is an H-MOR molecular sieve having a skeleton atom silicon/aluminum ratio of 4-60 and the molecular sieve having the FER topology is an H-ZSM-35 molecular sieve having a skeleton atomic silicon/aluminum ratio of 5-50. In the step (i), the zirconium-based oxide obtained by preparing by a coprecipitation method, a dipping method or a mechanical mixing method, the coprecipitation method comprises mixing a solution containing the X element, the M' element, and the zirconium element with a solution containing a precipitant in a cocurrent manner under stirring, where the control system having a pH of 7-9, aging, separating the solid phase, washing, drying and calcining to obtain the zirconium-based oxide. The impregnation method includes immersing the zirconia powder in a salt solution containing the X element and the M' element or immersing the zirconia powder and the oxide of X in a salt solution containing the M' element or zirconia powder and the oxide of M' are immersed in a salt solution containing X element, the solvent is removed, dried, and calcined to obtain the zirconium-based oxide, where the M' is copper, silver, zinc, manganese, yttrium, niobium, Ga, indium or chromium, the aging time in the coprecipitation method is 2-4 hours and calcined at 400-600 degrees C for 1-6 hours, the dipping method having a immersion time of 1-6 hours, the drying condition of 60-200 degrees C for 1-10 hours, and the calcination condition of 400-600 degrees C for 1-6 hours, where the X element, M' element and zirconium element in the solution are independently derived from the nitrate, hydrochloride, acetate of the X element, the M' element and the zirconium element, acetylacetonate or sulfate. In the step (iii), the ultrasonic assisted chemical compounding comprises ultrasonically dissolving a solution containing a zirconium-based oxide and a modified acidic molecular sieve, separating the solid phase by solid-liquid separation and drying the solid phase to obtain the complex catalyst. The physical compounding comprises compounding a mixture containing a zirconium-based oxide and a modified acidic molecular sieve by at least one of mechanical mixing, ball milling, and vibrating to obtain the complex catalyst, where the time of the ultrasound in the ultrasonic assisted chemical compounding method is 10 minutes to 3 hours, the drying temperature is 60-150 degrees C and the calcination temperature is 300-650 degrees C. The reaction temperature is 250-380 degrees C, the pressure is 1-8 MPa, the gas mass space velocity is 300-10000 ml/g/hour, the raw material gas further comprises an inert gas, the inert gas is nitrogen, argon, helium or methane, the volume content of the inert gas in the mixed gas is /~ 10% and the reactor is a fixed bed reactor, a fluidized bed reactor or a moving bed reactor.

PN CN110165151-A
TI Preparation of high-capacity graphene aerogel negative electrode material includes weighing lithium titanate precursor powder, adding graphene/carbon nanotube dispersion liquid and performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction drying.
AU CHE C
AE BEIJING OUMEIZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY RES (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201976912M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-capacity graphene aerogel negative electrode material comprises weighing 30-50 mg lithium titanate precursor powder, adding 200 mL graphene/carbon nanotube dispersion liquid for three times, dispersing for 0.8-1 hours with ultrasonic waves to obtain mixed solution, sealing mixed solution in PTFE high-pressure reaction kettle, reacting at 180-200 degrees C for 10-12 hours, cooling to room temperature to obtain graphene hydrogel/carbon nanotube/lithium titanate, cleaning graphene hydrogel/carbon nanotube/lithium titanate particles with absolute ethyl alcohol, drying graphene hydrogel/carbon nanotube/lithium titanate particles, performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction drying to obtain graphene aerogel/carbon nanotube/lithium titanate particles and heating graphene aerogel/carbon nanotube/lithium titanate particles at 1000-1200 degrees C for 3-5 hours.
   USE - The method is for preparation of high-capacity graphene aerogel negative electrode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has good electrochemical performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of lithium titanate precursor powder comprises weighing 5-10 mg lithium hydroxide, adding 100-300 ml deionized water, ultrasonically dispersing for 20-30 minutes, adding 10-20 ml 50 wt.% oxalic acid to obtain lithium source/oxalic acid solution, weighing 10-30 mg tetrabutyl titanate, adding 100-300 ml deionized water, ultrasonically dispersing for 20-30 minutes to obtain titanium source solution, adding titanium source solution to lithium source/oxalic acid solution, performing microwave treatment on mixed solution for 10-20 minutes and drying mixed solution at 200-300 degrees C. The preparation of graphene/carbon nanotube dispersion liquid comprises mixing 10-30 mg graphene powder and 5-20 mg carbon nanotube powder, placing in sand mill, grinding with sand grinding medium zirconia ball for 0.5-1 hour, adding 500 ml deionized water, ultrasonically dispersing for 20-30 minutes and standing for 30 minutes. The preparation of graphene powder comprises weighing 1-6 mg graphene oxide powder, adding 100-200 ml deionized water, ultrasonically dispersing for 0.5 hour to obtain graphene oxide solution, pouring graphene oxide solution in stainless steel reaction kettle, reacting at 100-200 degrees C for 10-24 hours, cooling stainless steel reaction kettle to room temperature and placing powder in vacuum drying oven. The preparation of graphene oxide powder comprises using 25-100 ml 98 wt.% concentrated sulfuric acid, adding 1-2 g potassium nitrate, mixing, adding 2 g graphite in ice bath, mixing for 30-60 minutes, adding 20-30 g 50 wt.% potassium permanganate aqueous solution, mixing for 30-60 minutes in ice bath, mixing in water bath at 20-30 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, adding 200 ml deionized water, sending to water bath at 90-110 degrees C, mixing for 10-30 minutes, adding 500 ml deionized water and 40 ml 20-30 wt.% hydrogen peroxide, mixing for 10 minutes, centrifuging, washing mixture to neutral and drying.

AU HAIDER M H
   SUBRAMANI C
   KARIM K
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 201968622C
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst precursor is produced by mixing 1st suspension comprising catalyst support and solvent comprising water, with cobalt salt and/or manganese salt to form 2nd suspension comprising catalyst support, and cobalt and/or manganese, and mixing 2nd suspension to produce catalyst precursor.
   USE - Production of catalyst precursor for catalyst used for producing greater than or equal to 2C hydrocarbons (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst made from the catalyst precursor has high olefin selectivity while simultaneously having a low selectivity towards the production of carbon dioxide ( less than or equal to 8%, preferably less than or equal to 5% carbon dioxide selectivity).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) catalyst produced by the method;
   (2) production of greater than or equal to 2C hydrocarbons which involves contacting syngas with the catalyst; and
   (3) composition comprising catalyst support, solvent comprising water, cobalt salt and/or manganese salt, and urea.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further includes drying the catalyst precursor and calcining the catalyst precursor to produce a catalyst. Preferred Compositions: Catalyst support comprises alumina (Al2O3), silica (SiO2), titania (TiO2), cerium(IV) oxide (CeO2), aluminum phosphate (AlPO4), zirconia (ZrO2), magnesium oxide (MgO), thorium oxide (ThO2), boehmite, silicon-carbide, molybdenum-carbide, aluminosilicate, kaolin, zeolite, and/or molecular sieve, preferably SiO2. Concentration of catalyst support in 1st suspension is greater than or equal to 0.005 g support per ml of solvent. Catalyst has structure of formula CoMnxS1yOz.S1=catalyst support;x=0.8-1.2;y=0.01-5; andz=undefined number determined by valence requirements of Co, Mn, and S1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Solvent has temperature of 20-95 degrees C, preferably 70-95 degrees C. The 1st suspension has a pH of less than or equal to 4. Step of drying is performed at 75-175 degrees C. Calcining is performed at 350-650 degrees C. Preferred Component: Solvent is essentially free from organic solvents. Preferred Process: Mixing of 2nd suspension with urea comprises adding urea to the 2nd suspension from an aqueous solution.

PN CN109970496-A
TI Preparing fertilizer by placing domestic sludge in closed container, fermenting, adding anaerobic sludge to domestic raw sludge, mixing, adding carbon dioxide, additive materials and anaerobic sludge, stirring, stacking and fermenting.
AU PAN X
AE PAN X (PANX-Individual)
GA 201961472J
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing fertilizer using sludge, involves (a) placing domestic sludge in a closed container, leaving still for more than two days, and fermenting to produce anaerobic sludge, (b) adding 20-30 %mass anaerobic sludge to domestic raw sludge, uniformly mixing, leaving still for 24-48 hours, adding 5-10 %mass carbon dioxide and additive material, and (c) continuously adding 20-30 %mass anaerobic sludge, uniformly stirring, stacking and fermenting at 15-50 degrees C for 15-20 days.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing fertilizer using sludge.
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared fertilizer increases the size of apple than the previous year, supplements sufficient water, and ensures high yield of the crops with excellent taste. The size of the potatoes are consistent, and the greenhouse vegetables such as strawberry and blueberry appears in market one week to half a month in advance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The step (b) involves stirring the mixture for 10-15 minutes, using sealing strip to isolate the sludge from the outside air, shaking 3-5 times a day, making the inner gas to flow up and down, and continuously adding anaerobic sludge after 7-10 days. Preferred Components: The additive material comprises rice husk, 3-4 cm plant straw and/or Chinese herbal medicinal residue. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of domestic raw sludge to additive material is (1-3):1. Preferred Conditions: In step (a), the domesticated anaerobic sludge is a viscous liquid with no lumps inside and is used as anaerobic sludge. The obtained anaerobic sludge is darker than the domestic sludge before acclimation, where the upper surface of the anaerobic sludge is lighter in color and the lower layer of sludge is darker in color. The anaerobic sludge is black in the middle of the upper layer and dark black in the bottom. The anaerobic sludge after acclimation in step (a) contains agglomerates, which are pulverized to 60-80 mesh and used as a raw material of the domestic sludge. In step (b), the mixing time is 10-20 minutes. In step (c), the stirring time is 10-15 minutes, and when the fermentation temperature of the stack reaches 55-75 degrees C, the stack starts to collapse and the temperature of the stack is lowered to 20-40 degrees C.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

PN CN108516573-A; CN108516573-B
TI Preparing micron-sized regular hexagonal calcium carbonate comprises e.g. adding calcium chloride into aqueous ammonia-ammonium chloride buffer solution, stirring, adding soluble zinc salt, introducing carbon dioxide gas and reacting.
AU JIN P
   ZHANG Y
   CHEN Y
   KE C
   SUN C
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 201873510Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing micron-sized regular hexagonal calcium carbonate comprises adding calcium chloride into aqueous ammonia-ammonium chloride buffer solution having pH of 10, stirring until the calcium chloride is completely dissolves, then adding soluble zinc salt, so that the concentration of calcium ions in the obtained solution is 0.2-0.8 mol/l, adding 0.5-3 wt.% soluble zinc salt (based on mass of calcium chloride), then continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the obtained solution, and reacting at 60-70 degrees C for 1-2 hours, and centrifuging and washing to obtain final product.
   USE - The hexagonal calcium carbonate is useful: for rubber as filler, plastic, paper making, dope and ink industries; for organic synthesis, metallurgy, glass and asbestos in production; and as dentifrice, toothpaste and other raw material of cosmetic.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple; and has high yield.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The soluble zinc salt is zinc chloride or zinc nitrate. The calcium ion concentration in the resulting solution is 0.3-0.5 mol/l. The addition amount of soluble zinc salt is 1-3% by mass of the calcium chloride.

PN CN108503175-A
TI Catalytic regeneration of oil sludge by performing anaerobic thermal cracking of sludge to obtain combustible gas, combustion oil, and waste residue, and removing hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, water, and pentane from combustible gas.
AU HEI J
   HEI X
AE NINGXIA BLACK GOLD TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201872393L
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytic regeneration of oil sludge involves performing anaerobic thermal cracking of sludge to obtain combustible gas, combustion oil, and waste residue, and removing hydrogen sulfide, carbon dioxide, water, and pentane from combustible gas, converting combustible gas and oxygen into synthesis gas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, performing Fischer-Tropsch reaction of synthesis gas under action of a catalyst to form a liquid fuel. The fuel is refined to obtain one of liquefied petroleum gas, naphtha, kerosene, and diesel.
   USE - The method is used for regenerating oil sludge to to obtain at least one of liquefied petroleum gas, naphtha, at least one of the kerosene and diesel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to regenerate clay catalyst which is fully combined with oxygen-free cracking Fischer-Tropsch reaction, and reduces sulfur content of prepared liquid phase oil product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The anaerobic thermal cracking involves heating the sludge to any value between 100-120 degrees C to removed moisture in the sludge, heated sludge treated in any value between 200-400 degrees C to obtain combustible gas, heated sludge treated to any value between 480-520 degrees C to obtain combustion oil. The method for regenerating clay catalyst further involves performing microcrystalline foaming treatment on combustion oil and waste residue to obtain a novel energy-saving material for microcrystalline foaming. Preferred Condition: The oxygen is obtained by separating oxygen from air. The temperature of Fischer-Tropsch reaction is value between 300-500 degrees C, and Fischer-Tropsch reaction catalyst is iron. The temperature of Fischer-Tropsch reaction is any value between 200-240 degrees C, and Fischer-Tropsch reaction catalyst is cobalt. The molar percentage of hydrogen and carbon monoxide is 2:1. The temperature of synthesis gas to Fischer- Tropsch reaction temperature are same.

PT P
PN CN108070177-A; CN108070177-B
TI Preparing impact resistant automobile exterior board comprises e.g. mixing zinc oxide with water, introducing carbon dioxide gas into mixture, adding oleic acid, placing obtained powder under condition of calcination and injection molding.
AU FANG X
   ZHENG L
AE QUZHOU COLLEGE TECHNOLOGY (QUZH-C)
GA 201842313F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing impact resistant automobile exterior board comprises (i) mixing zinc oxide with water at 50-80 degrees C to obtain mixture M1, (ii) introducing carbon dioxide gas into mixture M1, adding oleic acid, stirring, heating at 90-100 degrees C and reacting for 4-10 hours to obtain mixture M2, (iii) placing mixture M2 at 300-400 degrees C under drying condition to obtain powder M3, (iv) placing powder M3 at 450-600 degrees C under condition of calcination to obtain calcined M4, and (v) mixing polyethylene resin, acrylonitrile butadiene styrene resin, calcined M4, diatomite, paraffin wax, tribasic lead sulfate and white carbon black and injection molding to obtain impact resistant automobile exterior board.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing impact resistant automobile exterior board.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for impact resistant automobile exterior board obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), used amount of zinc oxide is 20-50 pts. wt. (based on 100 pts. wt. water), and mixing process is ultrasonic mixing. In step (ii), used amount of oleic acid is 5-15 pts. wt. (based on 100 pts. wt. mixture M1). In step (v), composition of raw materials are 100 pts. wt. polyethylene resin, 30-50 pts. wt. acrylonitrile butadiene styrene resin, 10-30 pts. wt. calcined M4, 5-15 pts. wt. diatomite, 3-10 pts. wt. paraffin wax, 1-5 pts. wt. tribasic lead sulfate and 5-15 pts. wt. white carbon black. The exterior board further comprises processing auxiliary agent. The processing auxiliary agent is antioxidant, anti-ultraviolet agent or plasticizer. Preferred Method: In step (ii), dropping rate of oleic acid is 60-100 drops/minute. In step (iv), calcination time is 6-10 hours.
DC A95 (Transport - including vehicle parts, tyres and armaments.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.)

TI Method for producing lutein ester from marigold            extract, involves adding marigold extract to            supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank for            extraction and purification and reacting with ethyl            acetate and absolute ethanol.
AU NING A
AE HANDAN MEIKANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HAND-Non-standard)
GA 2018390503
AB    NOVELTY - The method for producing high purity lutein                ester from marigold extract, involves (A) adding                marigold extract to supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction tank for extraction and purification to                obtain high purity marigold extract (I), (B)                introducing high purity marigold extract (I) into                stainless steel tank and adding ethyl acetate, the                content of ethyl acetate is 5-10 times that of                high-purity marigold extract (I), heating with                stirring at 30-45 degrees C for 30-45 minutes and                centrifuging at high-speed to obtain resulting                precipitate (II), (C) adding absolute ethanol                solution to stainless steel tank and mixing                precipitate (II), the content of absolute ethanol                is 5-6 times that of precipitate (II), stirring,                extracting and separating after high-speed                centrifugation and collecting precipitate (III),                adding precipitate (III) into stainless steel dish,                dispersing evenly and vacuum drying.
   USE - Method for producing lutein ester from                marigold extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables producing lutein ester with                high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The steps (B) and (C)                  involves centrifuging at 10000-15000 rpm.

PT P
PN US2018093308-A1
TI Inhibiting methane production in environmental medium involves providing antimethanogenic reagent to environmental medium to inhibit methane production of indigenous symbiotic methanogens located in environmental medium.
AU MUELLER J
   SCALZI M
   KARACHALIOS A
AE INNOVATIVE ENVIRONMENTAL TECH INC (INNO-Non-standard)
GA 2018259791
AB    NOVELTY - Methane production in environmental medium is inhibited by providing antimethanogenic reagent (AMR) to environmental medium to inhibit methane production of indigenous symbiotic methanogens located in environmental medium.
   USE - Inhibition of methane production in environmental medium.
   ADVANTAGE - Limiting methanogens in the environmental medium allows the slower-growing, halo-respiring bacteria that are utilized to dechlorinate containments to utilize the hydrogen donors (either naturally occurring or provided via fermentable substrates provided as part of a reduction process). The AMRs are harmless to the halo-respiring bacteria and can be provided alone or along with various organic hydrogen donors, zero-valent iron, or other reduced metals to enhance biodegradation (reductive dechlorination) of targeted contaminants.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide-methane reduction pathway that occurs within methanogens.
   Reduction of carbon dioxide to formyl level (110)
   Reduction of formyl group to formaldehyde level (120)
   Reduction of methylene group to methyl level (130)
   Converting methyl group to methane (140)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: AMR disrupts enzyme and coenzyme systems that are integral parts of methanogenesis process of methanogens. AMR interferes with biosynthesis of pseudomurein by indigenous symbiotic methanogens.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: Environmental medium, that is subject to reductive dechlorination, is soil, sediment, and water in ground water. Preferred Process: Fermentable substrate is provided to environmental medium to provide organic hydrogen donor. Providing antimethanogenic reagent (AMR) and providing fermentable substrate are done concurrently as a combination or sequentially. Zero valent iron or other reduced metals are provided into environmental medium. AMR is provided into environmental medium by injection.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Antimethanogenic reagent (AMR) is synthetic compound including diallyl disulfide, diallyl trisulfide, and/or ethyl propionate. AMR includes essential oil including garlic oil, lemongrass oil, cinnamon bark oil, and/or cinnamon bark powder. AMR is a combination of synthetic compounds, naturally occurring statins, and essential oils. Fermentable substrate includes acetate, propionate, butyrate, benzoate, lactate, formate, methanol, toluene, molasses, cheese whey, corn steep liquor, corn oil, peanut oil, coconut oil, vegetable oil, fish oil, soybean oil, hydrogenated cottonseed oil beads, solid food shortening, beef tallow, melted corn oil margarine, filamentous plant material, chitin, and/or hydrogenated soybean. Fermentable substrate is carbohydrate including glucose and/or glucose-producing compound.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)

The catalyst includes 0.1-1.0 wt.% platinum (Pt), 0.5-5.0 wt.% tin oxide (SnO2), and 0.1-5.0 wt.%. The reaction temperature of the method is 500-650degrees Celsius, and the operation pressure is 0.05-0.15 MPa.

PT P
PN US2019135715-A1; WO2019086981-A1; CA3019803-A1; AU2018360335-A1; US10647635-B2; KR2020076695-A; IN202037018875-A; AR113833-A1; CN111527060-A; EP3707115-A1; SG11202004064-A1; BR112020008679-A2; JP2021501773-W
TI Controlling carbon dioxide output from oxidative            dehydrogenation process comprises e.g. introducing gas            mixture comprising lower alkane and oxygen, and            optionally steam and inert diluent, into ethane            oxidative dehydrogenation reactor.
AU SIMANZHENKOV V
   GOODARZNIA S
AE NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM CORP (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
   NOVA CHEM INT SA (NVCH-C)
GA 201939684K
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling the carbon dioxide output from an                oxidative dehydrogenation process comprises: (i)                introducing a gas mixture comprising a lower alkane                and oxygen, and optionally at least one of steam                and inert diluent, into at least one ethane                oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) reactor containing                the same or different ODH catalysts, provided that                if more than one ODH reactor is present then at                least one of the ODH catalysts is capable of                utilizing carbon dioxide as an oxidizing agent,                under conditions to produce a product stream from                at least one ODH reactor comprising corresponding                alkene, and optionally at least one of unreacted                lower alkane, unreacted oxygen, carbon dioxide,                carbon monoxide, inert diluent, and acetic acid;                (ii) measuring carbon dioxide levels in at least                one the product streams; and (iii) either e.g. (a)                introducing steam, or increasing amount of steam                introduced, into the at least one ODH reactor in an                amount sufficient to decrease the carbon dioxide                output levels.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling the                carbon dioxide output from an oxidative                dehydrogenation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Controlling the carbon dioxide output from an                oxidative dehydrogenation process comprises: (i)                introducing a gas mixture comprising a lower alkane                and oxygen, and optionally at least one of steam                and inert diluent, into at least one ethane                oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) reactor containing                the same or different ODH catalysts, provided that                if more than one ODH reactor is present then at                least one of the ODH catalysts is capable of                utilizing carbon dioxide as an oxidizing agent,                under conditions to produce a product stream from                at least one ODH reactor comprising corresponding                alkene, and optionally at least one of unreacted                lower alkane, unreacted oxygen, carbon dioxide,                carbon monoxide, inert diluent, and acetic acid;                (ii) measuring carbon dioxide levels in at least                one the product streams; and (iii) either (a)                introducing steam, or increasing amount of steam                introduced, into the at least one ODH reactor in an                amount sufficient to decrease the carbon dioxide                output levels when the measured carbon dioxide                level is above a predetermined target carbon                dioxide level, (b) decreasing the flow rate of                steam introduced into the at least one ODH reactor                to increase the carbon dioxide output levels when                steam was introduced in step (i) and the measured                carbon dioxide level is below the predetermined                target carbon dioxide level, (c) increasing the                volumetric ratio of oxygen to ethane in the gas                mixture introduced into the at least one ODH                reactors to a degree to decrease the carbon dioxide                levels when the measured carbon dioxide level is                above a predetermined target carbon dioxide level,                or (d) decreasing the volumetric ratio of oxygen to                ethane in the gas mixture introduced into the at                least one ODH reactor to a degree to increase the                carbon dioxide level when the measured carbon                dioxide level is below a predetermined target                carbon dioxide level.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The lower alkane is                  ethane and the corresponding alkene is ethylene.                  The ODH reactor is a fixed bed reactor. The fixed                  bed ODH reactor comprises heat dissipative                  particles having a thermal conductivity greater                  that the catalyst. The ODH reactor is a fluidized                  bed reactor. The ODH catalysts are a mixed metal                  oxide. The ODH catalysts comprise a mixed metal                  oxide of formula (MoaVbTecNbdPdeOf) (II) where                  a-f are the relative atomic amounts of the                  elements molybdenum, vanadium, tellurium,                  niobium, palladium or oxygen, and when a is 1, b                  is 0.01-1, c is 0.01-1, d is 0.01-1, e is 0-0.1                  and f is a number to satisfy the valence state of                  the catalyst. The inert diluent is carbon                  dioxide. The steam comprises up to 60 wt.% of the                  gas mixture. The conditions in at least one ODH                  reactor are at temperatures of 300-450 degrees C,                  pressures of 0.5-150 psig (3.447-689.47 kPag),                  and a residence time of the lower alkane in the                  reactor of 0.002-30 seconds. The gas mixture has                  a gas hourly space velocity of 500-30000 per h.                  The gas mixture has a weight hourly space                  velocity of 0.5-50 per hour. The gas mixture has                  a linear velocity of 5-1500 cm/second. The                  reactor is maintained at a temperature of below                  340 degrees C. The steam added into at least one                  ODH reactor is from 0-20 wt.% and is increased to                  35-60 wt.% and results in an absolute decrease in                  carbon dioxide output, measured as normalized                  product selectivity of 2.5-15 wt.%. The steam                  added into at least one ODH reactor is 35-60 wt.%                  and is decreased to 0-20 wt.% and results in an                  absolute increase in carbon dioxide output,                  measured as normalized product selectivity, of                  2.5-15 wt.%. The reactor is maintained at a                  temperature above 350 degrees C. The steam added                  into at least one ODH reactor is of 0-10 wt.% and                  increased to 40-60 wt.% and results in an                  absolute decrease in carbon dioxide output,                  measured as normalized product selectivity, of at                  least 0.5 wt.%, where steam added into at least                  one ODH reactor is 40-60 wt.% and decreased to                  0-10 wt.% and results in an absolute increase in                  carbon dioxide output, measured as normalized                  product selectivity, of at least 0.5 wt.%. The                  vol.% of oxygen in the gas mixture is 20%, and                  the volumetric ratio of oxygen:ethane in the gas                  mixture is 0.4 and is changed to a volumetric                  ratio of oxygen:ethane to 0.6 and results in an                  absolute decrease in carbon dioxide output,                  measured as normalized product selectivity, of at                  least 2.5 wt.%. The vol.% of oxygen in the gas                  mixture is so that the feed mixture stays outside                  of the flammable envelop of hydrocarbon feed                  mixture, and the volumetric ratio of                  oxygen:ethane in the gas mixture is 0.1 and is                  changed to max ratio allowed before the feed                  mixture enters the flammable envelop zone of the                  hydrocarbon feed mixture and results in an                  absolute change in carbon dioxide output,                  measured as normalized product selectivity, of at                  least 0.5 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Building material comprises combination of fibers that are either of carbon dioxide-negative organic origin, produced synthetically from carbon dioxide, or molten stone that absorbs carbon dioxide and comprises natural stone.
AU 
AE ARNOLD U (ARNO-Individual)
   BRUECK T (BRUE-Individual)
   KUSE K (KUSE-Individual)
GA 202108327G
AB    NOVELTY - Building material prepared from sustainable raw materials comprises a combination of fibers that are either of carbon dioxide-negative organic origin or are produced synthetically from carbon dioxide or are prepared from molten stone that absorbs carbon dioxide and comprises natural stone that cut into slabs, when weathered, binds to sufficient carbon dioxide from surrounding air and the carbon dioxide remains permanently bound in stone material itself and its waste stone dust material.
   USE - As building material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material can be produced and disposed or recycled at low cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fibers are bamboo fibers, hemp fibers, flax fibers, carbon fibers of biogenic origin, stone fibers or their mixtures. The stone material and its production-related stone dust waste comprises granite, gneiss, gabbro, serpentine, dunite, olive stone, basalt or their mixtures. The surface of built-in natural stone slab material is exposed to ambient air, which is not coated with a layer of paint or plaster on outside and preferably also inside of room, but is exposed to ambient air. The stone dust is applied as a fertilizer on agricultural land on ground.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PT P
PN CN109231695-A
TI Processing of beneficiation wastewater by entering beneficiation wastewater containing barium ions in collecting well, conducting centralized collection and initial stable adjustment, and entering effluent into coarse grid.
AU TU R
AE TU R (TURR-Individual)
GA 201909816U
AB    NOVELTY - Processing of beneficiation wastewater includes entering beneficiation wastewater containing barium ions in collecting well, conducting centralized collection and initial stable adjustment, entering the effluent of the collecting well into coarse grid, removing large diameter solid in wastewater, entering the effluent in coarse grid into a primary sedimentation tank, further removing the insoluble material in the wastewater, entering the effluent of the primary sedimentation tank into a pH regulating tank, regulating the pH at 6.0-7.0 to meet the carbon dioxide vortex convection slag separation reactor pH requirement, entering the effluent of the pH regulating tank in carbon dioxide vortex convection slag separation reactor, removing barium ions in the wastewater, and entering the effluent of the carbon dioxide vortex convection slag separation reactor in aeration tank.
   USE - Processing of beneficiation wastewater.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing of beneficiation wastewater comprises entering beneficiation wastewater containing barium ions in collecting well, conducting centralized collection and initial stable adjustment, entering the effluent of the collecting well into coarse grid, removing large diameter solid in wastewater, entering the effluent in coarse grid into a primary sedimentation tank, further removing the insoluble material in the wastewater, entering the effluent of the primary sedimentation tank into a pH regulating tank, regulating the pH at 6.0-7.0 to meet the carbon dioxide vortex convection slag separation reactor pH requirement, entering the effluent of the pH regulating tank in carbon dioxide vortex convection slag separation reactor, removing barium ions in the wastewater, entering the effluent of the carbon dioxide vortex convection slag separation reactor in aeration tank, subjecting the remaining chemical oxygen demand (COD) in wastewater to primary oxidative decomposition treatment by aerobic aeration, entering the effluent of the aeration tank in biological oxidation filter tank, entering the effluent of the biological oxidation filter tank in secondary sedimentation tank, entering the effluent of the secondary sedimentation tank in clean water tank, and discharging the clean water through a wastewater pipeline. The carbon dioxide vortex convection slag separation reactor has left side lower portion provided with a water inlet valve, right side upper portion provided with a discharging valve, and bottom provided with 5 stirring blades, and center installed with hollow carbon dioxide aeration reverse stirring screw, which has hollow portion connected to carbon dioxide storage tank by gas tube. The hollow carbon dioxide aeration reverse stirring screw rod body is uniformly provided with multiple ventilating micropores. The gas of the screw hollow portion enters into the wastewater in bubble form. The top of the hollow carbon dioxide aeration stirring screw rod is connected to driving motor. The top of the reactor is provided with 1 set of floating suction separation device, which has lower end provided with four scum suction pipes having an opening extending into the reactor below the liquid level. The ore beneficiation wastewater scum suction separation device right side is provided with a waste slag outlet. The hollow carbon dioxide aeration reversely stirring screw enables the forward vortex to generate wastewater in the reactor, and the reactor center begins to do reverse rotation under the driving of the driving motor so as to apply a reverse rotation power in the center of forward rotation, making it generate reverse vortex. The carbon dioxide vortex convection slag separation reactor has effective volume of 245 m3, stirring blade working voltage of 380 V, and rotation speed of 270 revolutions/minute.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN KR2101021-B1; KR2021017997-A
TI Automatic building control system comprises e.g. receiving unit for receiving fine dust prediction information and carbon dioxide concentration information from a carbon dioxide sensing device installed in interior space of building.
AU MYEON J C
AE MYEON J C (MYEO-Individual)
GA 2020325133
AB    NOVELTY - Automatic building control system comprises a receiving unit for receiving carbon dioxide concentration information from a carbon dioxide sensing device installed in the interior space of the building and fine dust prediction information from an external electronic device, and a control unit for opening and closing a window or driving a ventilation device installed in a building based on the carbon dioxide concentration information and the fine dust prediction information to implement ventilation of the building. The fine dust prediction information is greater than or equal to a preset fine dust threshold at a first point in the future. The control unit includes a first ventilation mode which implements ventilation when a preset time or the carbon dioxide concentration in the internal space is greater than or equal to a preset carbon dioxide threshold in the previous period of the second time point and a second ventilation mode for implementing ventilation.
   USE - The automatic building control system is useful for reducing fine dust through predictive control.
   ADVANTAGE - The system effectively reduces fine dust concentration inside a building even when the fine dust concentration outside is high.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Automatic building control system comprises a receiving unit (100) for receiving carbon dioxide concentration information from a carbon dioxide sensing device installed in the interior space of the building and fine dust prediction information from an external electronic device, and a control unit (200) for opening and closing a window or driving a ventilation device installed in a building based on the carbon dioxide concentration information and the fine dust prediction information to implement ventilation of the building. The fine dust prediction information is greater than or equal to a preset fine dust threshold at a first point in the future based on the second time point which is before the first time point. The control unit includes a first ventilation mode which implements ventilation when a preset time or the carbon dioxide concentration in the internal space is greater than or equal to a preset carbon dioxide threshold in the previous period of the second time point and a second ventilation mode for implementing ventilation which is implemented when the concentration of carbon dioxide in the internal space is less than the carbon dioxide threshold in the period from the second time point to the first time point. The second time point is changed by the control unit based on the fine dust prediction information.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic configuration diagram of the automatic building control system for reducing fine dust through predictive control (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Receiving unit (100)
   Control unit (200)
   Transmitter (300)
   Memory unit (400)

PT P
PN CN109680125-A; CN109680125-B
TI Vacuum refining using carbon dioxide as lifting gas comprises passing liquid carbon dioxide, carrying out no steel water debugging, plugging, lifting the ladle, sampling, adding aluminum, turning off lifting gas and breaking vacuum.
AU LI M
   LI Q
   YIN Y
   TAO B
   ZHOU H
   ZHAO B
   CHU J
AE NANJING IRON & STEEL CO LTD (FUXN-C)
GA 2019410952
AB    NOVELTY - Vacuum refining using carbon dioxide as lifting gas comprises (i) passing liquid carbon dioxide into a vaporizer by adopting Dewar to supply carbon dioxide, passing in the buffer tank and maintaining the pressure; (ii) carrying out no steel water debugging and ensuring the smooth switching of the lifting gas; (iii) reducing roasting oxygen lance tube outer standby position, plugging the plug and processing the molten steel; (iv) passing the ladle in the station, opening the bottom blow for pre-blowing argon, lifting the ladle, measuring slag thickness, temperature measurement and sampling; (v) manually lifting the hydraulic jacking platform, manually clearing the lift height, continuously lifting the ladle and ensuring the nozzle is immersed in the molten steel surface; (vi) processing to blow carbon dioxide, maintaining the vacuum and controlling the actual blowing flow rate of carbon dioxide; and (vii) adding aluminum, stopping processing, turning off lifting gas and breaking vacuum.
   USE - The method is useful for vacuum refining using carbon dioxide as lifting gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Vacuum refining using carbon dioxide as lifting gas comprises (i) passing liquid carbon dioxide into a vaporizer through a cryogenic liquid pump by adopting Dewar to supply carbon dioxide, passing in the buffer tank and maintaining the pressure at 3-3.5 Mpa; (ii) carrying out no steel water debugging and ensuring the smooth switching of the lifting gas, where the carbon dioxide and argon shares the same blowpipe; (iii) reducing roasting oxygen lance tube outer standby position, plugging the plug and processing the molten steel for 15 minutes, moving the moving elbow to waiting processing station, confirming the moving elbow movement finished and starting pretreatment 10 minutes before the molten steel is processed; (iv) passing the ladle in the station, opening the bottom blow for pre-blowing argon, lifting the ladle to a height of 110 cm, measuring slag thickness, temperature measurement and sampling; (v) manually lifting the hydraulic jacking platform, manually clearing the lift height when the lower edge of the nozzle contacts the slag surface, continuously lifting the ladle and ensuring the nozzle is immersed in the molten steel surface below 500 mm; (vi) processing to blow carbon dioxide when the vacuum is less than 50 mBar, maintaining the vacuum to less than 2.5 mbar and controlling the actual blowing flow rate of carbon dioxide to 90-99 Nm3/hour according to the molten steel in the vacuum tank; and (vii) adding aluminum 1-2 minutes before the break, stopping processing after the vacuum treatment and the holding time meet the process requirements, turning off the lifting gas and breaking vacuum.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), carbon dioxide is supplied using 6-8 Dewar tanks in parallel. In the step (ii), the device debugging without molten steel comprises (a) purging the device and piping to ensure that it is unobstructed; (b) opening the carbon dioxide pipeline valve, cutting off the argon pipeline valve, adjusting the gas flow to the refining required flow and continuously spraying at the flow for 15 minutes; (c) checking the air tightness of the pipeline under normal working pressure and flow, pressure, flow and stability of equipment operation; and (d) verifying whether nitrogen-argon switching, carbon dioxide-argon switching, and flow regulation can be achieved to ensure that the normal gas supply to argon can be switched when the carbon dioxide gas path fails. In the step (vi), when the carbon dioxide is sprayed as the lifting gas and when the pressure of the blow hole increases the argon is switched to prevent the spray gun from being clogged. In the step (vii), the vacuum treatment is maintained for 15-20 minutes and breaking vacuum, lowering the ladle when the vacuum degree is greater than 900 mbar and feeding, temperature measuring, sampling, adding the heat insulating agent and performing the soft blowing to make the molten steel temperature, soft blowing and composition meet the requirements. Preferred Components: The aluminum content of the inbound steel is controlled to be less than or equal to 0.028%. The total stroke of the nozzle immersion depth is controlled to be less than 700 mm. The argon hot mass flowmeter is used to measure the injection flow rate of carbon dioxide. The conversion formula is the actual carbon dioxide flow rate is equal to 0.508 x thermal mass flow meter shows the flow rate.

TI Preparing carbon dioxide-based ionic polyurea useful as functional material in the field of flexible electronic devices and/or biomedicine, comprises polymerizing diamine-functionalized ionic liquid and carbon dioxide as monomers.
AU ZHU Z
   YAN F
   ZHOU Y
   OU X
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2021B80275
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon dioxide-based ionic polyurea, comprises polymerizing diamine-functionalized ionic liquid and carbon dioxide as monomers to obtain carbon dioxide-based ionic polyurea.
   USE - The carbon dioxide-based ionic polyurea is useful as functional material in the field of flexible electronic devices and/or biomedicine (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The polyurea has excellent ion conductivity and biological antibacterial properties, simple preparation method; and is environmentally friendly, and ensures resource utilization of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbon dioxide-based ionic polyurea obtained by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cation in diamine-functionalized ionic liquid is imidazole diamine ions of formula (I), pyridine diamine ions compound of formula (II) or quaternary ammonium diamine ions compound of formula (III). The anion in diamine-functionalized ionic liquid is chloride ions, bromide ion, iodide ion, tetrafluoroborate ion, hexafluorophosphate ion, trifluoromethanesulfonate ion or bistrifluoromethanesulfonimide ion. The organic catalyst is 4-methyloxycholine, triazabicyclo, diazabicyclo or triethylamine; inorganic catalyst is potassium carbonate, cesium carbonate, potassium hydroxide, cesium hydroxide monohydrate or potassium t-butoxide.n = number of connection C i.e. 2-18.Preferred Method: The polymerization reaction is carried out in the presence of an organic catalyst or an inorganic catalyst. When the polymerization reaction takes place in the presence of organic catalyst or inorganic catalyst, where molar content of the organic catalyst or inorganic catalyst is 0-20 mol% of the diamine ionic liquid, but is not zero. The conditions of the polymerization reaction are: the reaction is carried out for 2-24 hours at carbon dioxide pressure of 0.1-20 MPa and 60-250degrees Celsius. The method comprises (i) dissolving ionic liquid functionalized in diamine using suitable organic solvent into a 0.1-2 mol/l solution, (ii) adding diamine ionic liquid solution, organic catalyst or inorganic catalyst having molar weight of ionic liquid of 0-20 mol% and performing ultrasonic treatment to obtain uniformly mixed precursor solution, and (iii) injecting precursor solution into the reaction kettle, purging carbon dioxide to remove the air, raising it to 60-250degrees Celsius, and then passing in 0.1-20 MPa carbon dioxide, reacting for 2-24 hours to obtain carbon dioxide-based ion polyurea.

TI Producing biomethane that is scrubbed of carbon            dioxide and of water from feed gas stream comprising            methane and carbon dioxide involves compressing feed            gas stream with compressor to produce compressed feed            gas stream, cooling compressed feed gas stream to            condense at least one portion of water.

GA 2022C1481L
AB    NOVELTY - Producing biomethane that is scrubbed of                carbon dioxide and of water from feed gas stream                comprising methane and carbon dioxide involves                compressing the feed gas stream with a compressor                to produce compressed feed gas stream, cooling                compressed feed gas stream to condense at least one                portion of water in the compressed feed gas stream,                removing at least one portion of the condensed                water in the feed gas stream to produce a                water-depleted compressed feed gas stream,                separating the water-depleted compressed feed gas                stream with a membrane separation unit into a                permeate (10) enriched in carbon dioxide and a                biomethane stream scrubbed of carbon dioxide, and                recovering the biomethane stream scrubbed of carbon                dioxide from the membrane separation unit.
   USE - Method for producing biomethane that is                scrubbed of carbon dioxide and of water from feed                gas stream comprising methane and carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The production of biomethane helps the                territories to acquire greater energy                self-sufficiency, and has cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing biomethane that is scrubbed of                carbon dioxide and of water from feed gas stream                comprising methane and carbon dioxide involves                compressing the feed gas stream with a compressor                to produce compressed feed gas stream, cooling                compressed feed gas stream to condense at least one                portion of water in the compressed feed gas stream,                removing at least one portion of the condensed                water in the feed gas stream to produce a                water-depleted compressed feed gas stream,                separating the water-depleted compressed feed gas                stream with a membrane separation unit into a                permeate enriched in carbon dioxide and a                biomethane stream scrubbed of carbon dioxide,                recovering the biomethane stream scrubbed of carbon                dioxide from the membrane separation unit, removing                at least one portion of water remaining in the                recovered biomethane stream scrubbed of carbon                dioxide with an adsorption purification unit                comprising an adsorber to produce biomethane                scrubbed of carbon dioxide and water, and                recovering the biomethane scrubbed of carbon                dioxide and water from the adsorption purification                unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a plant for performing                method for producing biomethane that is scrubbed of                carbon dioxide and of water from feed gas stream                comprising methane and carbon dioxide.

PN US2020171424-A1; US11161073-B2
TI Purification of carbon dioxide for forming carbon dioxide-rich product, involves contacting feed gas stream of carbon dioxide and nitrogen and aqueous phase stream in countercurrent flow in which gas feed stage having preset temperature.
AU METTLER M S
   MHADESHWAR A B
   BILLIMORIA R M
AE EXXONMOBIL RES & ENG CO (ESSO-C)
GA 2020488823
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of carbon dioxide from gas containing nitrogen involves intimately contacting a feed gas stream comprising carbon dioxide and nitrogen gases and an aqueous phase stream in countercurrent flow to form carbon dioxide-rich hydrate in the aqueous phase. The temperature (Tf) is maintained at a gas feed stage (f) in the countercurrent flow, and temperature (T2) which is lower than Tf and maintained at stage (n) which is greater than stage (f), and temperature (T1) which is greater than or equal to Tf and maintained at stage (m) which is less than or equal to stage (f). The temperature (T2) is in range from the incipient vapor temperature for carbon dioxide to the incipient hydrate temperature for carbon dioxide at the operating pressure of the process. The temperature (T1) is a temperature at or below a temperature of convergence of the incipient carbon dioxide hydrate formation and incipient carbon dioxide vapor formation curves at the operating pressure of the process.
   USE - Purification of carbon dioxide from gas containing nitrogen, such as combustion gases such as power plant flue gases, for forming carbon dioxide rich products for long term storage locations such as geologic formations and marine hydrate reservoirs and nitrogen rich gas products for driving turbine and other pressure differential engine to generate electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables the effective purification of carbon dioxide from gas containing nitrogen provides the carbon dioxide rich products and nitrogen rich gas products capable to store and transport for use in further industrial process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The purification process is performed by separating gas phase from aqueous phase and collecting a hydrate slurry formed in aqueous slurry, and comprising hydrate particles enriched in carbon dioxide. The feed gas stream, and aqueous phase stream comprises hydrate promoter.

The liquid is supplied to the gas cylinder. The cylinder is filled with drug carbon dioxide. The gas cylinder is removed and placed on the review electronic scale to weigh the weight, when the weight or pressure of the cylinder reaches the set value. AB    NOVELTY - Natural gas liquefaction system comprises a vapor-liquid separator (34) configured to remove carbon dioxide in liquid state from natural gas containing carbon dioxide, a precooling device configured to use the removed carbon dioxide to precool the natural gas containing carbon dioxide, and a natural gas liquefying device (43) configured to cool the natural gas from which the carbon dioxide has been removed, so as to produce liquefied natural gas (LNG).
   USE - The system is used in floating production plant for purifying, liquefying, storing, and shipping natural gas on ocean e.g. floating-LNG, and used in stationary offshore production plant that is fixed to the seabed or an LNG production plant at an onshore gas field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reuses the removed liquid carbon dioxide to precool natural gas, hence the requisite energy for removing carbon dioxide from natural gas and requisite energy for producing LNG from natural gas is reduced compared to removing carbon dioxide by amine-based absorption only.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing liquefied natural gas, comprising (i) removing carbon dioxide in liquid state from natural gas containing carbon dioxide, (ii) using the removed carbon dioxide to precool the natural gas containing carbon dioxide, and (iii) cooling the natural gas from which the carbon dioxide has been removed, so as to produce LNG.

PN CN108179996-A
TI Replacing natural gas hydrate by liquid carbon            dioxide under in situ condition useful for engineering            mining, comprises e.g. controlling reaction            temperature, mixing liquid carbon dioxide with water            and decomposing methane hydrate.
AU ZHANG F
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2018503306
AB    NOVELTY - Replacing natural gas hydrate by liquid carbon                dioxide under in situ condition comprises (i)                controlling reaction temperature and pressure to                273.15-287.15 K and 4.5-8.5 MPa respectively and                mixing liquid carbon dioxide in the fracture of the                sufficient gas hydrate reservoir with the water in                the fracture to form a mixed hydrate mainly                comprising carbon dioxide; (ii) decomposing methane                hydrate, injecting liquid carbon dioxide                continuously to produce gas, decomposing adjacent                natural gas hydrate to produce methane-rich natural                gas and free water to maintain carbon dioxide-rich                hydrate and simultaneously producing natural gas                hydrate to produce methane-rich natural gas                continuosly; and (iii) controlling the gas recovery                rate of the reaction to 0.5-50 standard/hour per                cubic meter of natural gas hydrate to ensure                reaction until the reaction is completed.
   USE - The method is useful for engineering                mining.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high replacement effect and                long reaction time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The natural gas                  hydrate has a porous structure, and the carbon                  dioxide reaction is a liquid structure under                  pressure and temperature conditions. The carbon                  dioxide-methane mixed hydrate in step (i) is in a                  four-phase structure in which water-liquid is                  rich in carbon dioxide-carbon dioxide                  hydrate-methane gas. The structure used for                  controlling the reaction pressure in step (i) is                  a constant pressure valve. Preferred Method: The                  step (ii) comprises controlling injecting amount                  of liquid carbon dioxide and gas production rate                  to maintain the heat release of the carbon                  dioxide hydrate formation process and the heat                  absorption of the methane hydrate decomposition                  process to ensure the hydrate accumulation during                  the decomposition and generation process without                  destroying macro structure.
