PN WO2018015827-A1
TI Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce syngas-containing composition used for producing e.g. methanol, involves contacting copper-manganese-aluminum metal oxide catalyst with hydrogen and carbon dioxide at preset temperature.
AU MAMEDOV A
   REA C
   SHAIKH S
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   MAMEDOV A (MAME-Individual)
   REA C (REAC-Individual)
   SHAIKH S (SHAI-Individual)
GA 201807433F
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce syngas-containing composition comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, involves contacting a copper-manganese-aluminum mixed metal oxide catalyst (112) with hydrogen and carbon dioxide at a temperature of at least 600 degrees C, and a pressure greater than atmospheric pressure.
   USE - Hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce syngas-containing composition which is used as intermediate or feed material in subsequent synthesis e.g. aromatics production, hydroformylation of olefins, carbonylation of methanol, and carbonylation of olefins to produce chemical products e.g. methanol (all claimed), t-butyl methyl ether, ammonia, fertilizers, 2-ethyl hexanol, formaldehyde, acetic acid, and 1,4-butanediol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently converts carbon dioxide to syngas-containing composition, with reduced production of methane.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to produce syngas-containing composition.
   System (100)
   Combined reactant gas source (102)
   Reactor (104)
   Reaction zone (110)
   Copper-manganese-aluminum mixed metal oxide catalyst (112)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method is carried out at a temperature of preferably 615-625 degrees C, and a pressure of 0.5-6 MPa, preferably 2.5-3.5 MPa. The hydrogen gas flow rate is 75-110 mL/minute, and the carbon dioxide gas flow rate is 20-30 mL/minute. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide is 1:1-4:1. The syngas-containing composition comprises 5 mol% or less, preferably 2-4 mol% methane, and 15 mol% or less carbon dioxide. The copper-manganese-aluminum mixed metal oxide catalyst comprises 1-10 wt.%, preferably 5 wt.% copper, 1-30 wt.%, preferably 10 wt.% manganese, and 60-98 wt.%, preferably 83-87 wt.% aluminum oxide. The volume ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide during contact with the copper-manganese-aluminum mixed metal oxide catalyst is at least 3:1 or 4:1. Preferred Property: The catalyst has a surface area of 200-300 m2/g, preferably 254 m2/g.

PN CN110141947-A
TI Process for reducing emission of coke oven flue gas carbon dioxide involves entering coke oven flue gas generated by coke oven heating system into flue gas desulfurization and denitration system to remove nitrogen oxide and sulfur dioxide.
AU WANG M
   WU S
AE ACRE COKING & REFRACTORY ENG CONSULTING (CMEG-C)
GA 201974566B
AB    NOVELTY - The process for reducing emission of coke oven flue gas carbon dioxide involves (1) entering coke oven flue gas generated by coke oven heating system into flue gas desulfurization and denitration system to remove nitrogen oxide and sulfur dioxide from coke oven flue gas, (2) recovering heat, entering coke oven flue gas into carbon dioxide absorption system for absorbing carbon dioxide, discharging through outlet, entering ammonium bicarbonate solution formed by reaction of aqueous ammonia with carbon dioxide into carbon dioxide desorption system, (3) reacting ammonium bicarbonate solution with sulfuric acid to form ammonium sulfate and dissolving in aqueous solution, precipitating carbon dioxide gas, entering precipitated carbon dioxide gas into high-temperature carbonization system, reacting with coal char material, entering generated carbon monoxide into gas collection and purification system, subjecting solution to desorption reaction to saturator of ammonium sulfate unit.
   USE - Process for reducing emission of coke oven flue gas carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reduction of emission of coke oven flue gas carbon dioxide under atmosphere, and improves the resource utilization level of coke production waste gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The process for reducing emission of coke oven flue gas carbon dioxide involves (1) entering coke oven flue gas generated by coke oven heating system into flue gas desulfurization and denitration system to remove nitrogen oxide and sulfur dioxide from the coke oven flue gas, (2) recovering heat from coke oven flue gas after desulfurization and denitrification by flue gas waste heat recovery system, entering coke oven flue gas into carbon dioxide absorption system after temperature drop, absorbing carbon dioxide in coke oven flue gas by using aqueous ammonia, removing the carbon dioxide, discharging through coke oven flue gas outlet, entering ammonium bicarbonate solution formed by reaction of aqueous ammonia with carbon dioxide into carbon dioxide desorption system, (3) reacting ammonium bicarbonate solution with sulfuric acid in reactor in carbon dioxide desorption system to form ammonium sulfate and dissolving in aqueous solution, and simultaneously precipitating the carbon dioxide gas, entering precipitated carbon dioxide gas into carbonization chamber of high-temperature carbonization system of coal, reacting with coal char material in carbonization chamber, entering generated carbon monoxide into gas collection and purification system along with the gas separated by dry distillation process, subjecting solution to desorption reaction to the saturator of ammonium sulfate unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The aqueous ammonia is product aqueous ammonia obtained by ammonia distillation system. In the carbon dioxide absorption tower, coke oven flue gas is in countercurrent contact with the aqueous ammonia entering the carbon dioxide absorption tower, the carbon dioxide in the flue gas is chemically reacted with ammonia in aqueous ammonia to form ammonium bicarbonate and is dissolved in the bottom solution of carbon dioxide absorption tower, and sent to carbon dioxide desorption system through pipeline from the bottom outlet.

PT P
PN US2018371506-A1; US10920249-B2
TI Performing carbon dioxide sequestration involves dissolving carbon dioxide into aqueous solution to form aqueous carbon dioxide solution defined by mineral undersaturation level, combining carbon dioxide solution with sequestration agent.
AU SUBHAS A V
   BERELSON W M
   ROLLINS N E
   ADKINS J F
   EREZ J
AE CALIFORNIA INST OF TECHNOLOGY (CALY-C)
   UNIV SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA (USCA-C)
   YISSUM RES DEV CO HEBREW UNIV JERUSALEM (YISS-C)
GA 2018A4268F
AB    NOVELTY - Performing carbon dioxide sequestration involves dissolving carbon dioxide into an aqueous solution to form an aqueous carbon dioxide solution defined by a mineral undersaturation level, combining the aqueous carbon dioxide solution with a sequestration agent, titrating a hydrating catalyst into the aqueous carbon dioxide solution such that within a first catalysis region a mixture of catalyst and aqueous carbon dioxide solution is formed, the first catalysis region encompassing a second interfacial catalysis region located within the laminar boundary layer at the interface between the mixture and the carbonate sequestration agent, and reacting the aqueous carbon dioxide solution with the catalyst within the first catalysis region to produce protons in proximity to the second interfacial catalysis region such that the protons dissolve the sequestration agent.
   USE - Method for performing carbon dioxide sequestration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method further involves reacting the carbon dioxide within the aqueous carbon dioxide solution with the dissolved sequestration agent within the second interfacial catalysis region to produce an effluent comprising bicarbonate, and where within the second interfacial catalysis region the dissolution of the sequestration agent is enhanced such that the overall rate of dissolution of the sequestration agent within the catalytic region is higher than the rate of the uncatalyzed dissolution of the sequestration agent when exposed to an aqueous carbon dioxide solution having the same mineral under saturation level. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an apparatus for sequestering carbon dioxide, which comprises a reactor vessel defining an enclosed volume, a source of a catalyst, a sequestration agent, a carbon di-oxide gas, and an aqueous solution, one input in fluid communication between the one source and the enclosed volume of the one reactor vessel, and one output in fluid communication with the enclosed volume of the one reactor vessel, where the one input is arranged such that the carbon dioxide gas and aqueous solution combine to form an aqueous carbon dioxide solution, and where the aqueous carbon dioxide solution and catalyst are delivered as a mixture within the enclosed volume of the one reactor within a first catalytic region encompassing a second interfacial catalytic region disposed about the sequestration agent and being located within a laminar flow boundary at the interface between the mixture and the carbonate sequestration agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The sequestration agent is selected from the group consisting of a metal carbonate, or a silicate mineral. The sequestration agent is calcium carbonate and the catalyst is one of either carbonic anhydrase or a carbonic anhydrase analog. The overall rate of dissolution of the carbonate sequestration agent is an order of magnitude higher than the rate of the uncatalyzed dissolution of the carbonate sequestration agent when exposed to an aqueous carbon dioxide solution having the same mineral undersaturation level. The mineral undersaturation level is held at less than 0.5. The aqueous solution has a circum-neutral pH, where the aqueous solution is a brine solution. The aqueous carbon dioxide solution is combined in measured aliquots such that the mineral undersaturation level is maintained at a constant level. The stirring forms a diffusion boundary layer around the second interfacial catalysis region the diffusion boundary defining a volume around the interfacial region of the sequestration agent on the order of 10 microns. The catalyst operates to catalyze the protolysis of water in the aqueous solution and hydrate the carbon di-oxide within the solution. The rate of dissolution is diffusion rate limited. The pressure is increased or the temperature is decreased to increase mineral undersaturation. The sequestration agent and catalyst is physically confined within the first catalytic region. Preferred Process: The method further involves placing the first catalysis region and the second interfacial catalysis region under a pressure of 500 pound per square inch such that the dissolution of the sequestration agent is increased relative to the unpressurized dissolution rate of the sequestration agent at the same mineral undersaturation, maintaining the second interfacial catalysis region at a temperature not greater than 200 degrees C, reacting with a condition agent the aqueous solution to reduce surface poisoning ions in the aqueous carbon dioxide solution, stirring the aqueous solution within at least the first catalysis region such that a mixing zone forms where the aqueous carbon dioxide solution and catalyst intermingle and where the mixing zone is within the first catalysis region, roughening the surface of the sequestration agent such that the grain size of the sequestration agent is no greater than 100 micrometer, collecting and filtering the effluent from the reaction to capture one of catalyst or unreacted aqueous carbon dioxide solution, and reintroducing the catalyst and unreacted aqueous carbon dioxide solution into the first catalysis region.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide entrained recycled aggregate                is obtained by entraining carbon dioxide in                recycled aggregate by exposing recycled aggregate                to carbon dioxide in a carbonation chamber at a                predetermined carbonation pressure and for a                predetermined carbonation time; and maintaining                carbon dioxide entrained recycled aggregate under                pressure after the carbonation time and prior to                its use in the concrete mix.
   USE - The method is useful for obtaining carbon                dioxide entrained recycled aggregate for use in a                concrete mix.
   ADVANTAGE - The increased density of the carbon dioxide                entrained recycled aggregate improves its                compressive strength. Concrete formed from a                concrete mix including the carbon dioxide entrained                recycled aggregate also exhibits an improved                compressive strength compared to concrete formed                from an equivalent concrete mix including                non-carbon dioxide entrained recycled aggregate.                The concrete mix produced using the carbon dioxide                entrained recycled aggregate utilizes recycled                construction waste to form new concrete, which                reduces the amount of waste being added to                landfill.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:

PN RD680034-A
TI Method for establishing power of carbon dioxide laser, involves providing oxygen sensor for measuring partial pressure of oxygen in laser, where carbon dioxide lasers are used widely in industry to generate beams of infrared light.
AU 
AE ANONYMOUS (ANON-C)
GA 2020C5358F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing an oxygen sensor for measuring the partial pressure of the oxygen in a laser, where the carbon dioxide lasers are used widely in an industry to generate the beams of infrared light. The carbon dioxide lasers are provided for utilizing an emission of radiation from the carbon dioxide molecules to create the infrared light. The carbon dioxide molecules are split into carbon monoxide and oxygen according to a following formula. The reaction time for such reactions is provided in an order of minutes for the lasers with a large total gas volume, where the small lasers are sealed. The dissociation of carbon dioxide is resulted in gradual power degradation of the laser.
   USE - Method for establishing the power of a carbon dioxide laser.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen sensor is provided for measuring the partial pressure of the oxygen in a laser, where the carbon dioxide lasers are used widely in an industry to generate the beams of infrared light, and hence ensures establishing the power of carbon dioxide laser by monitoring the concentration of oxygen gas in a laser in an effective manner and adjusts the partial pressure of carbon dioxide in a carbon dioxide laser.

   USE - Method for comparing carbon dioxide immiscible flooding stages.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables comparing the carbon dioxide immiscible flooding stages with effectively increasing amplitude.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide non-phase phase mixing comparing device.

PN CN114544463-A
TI Method for evaluating storage rate of carbon            dioxide in crack reservoir geologic body, involves            obtaining carbon dioxide oil displacement recovery rate            according to change relation curve and calculating            carbon dioxide storage rate.
AU DING Y
   RUI Z
   ZHAO Y
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
GA 202275558J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining rock core in a                target oil deposit geologic body. Stratum water                with constant pressure and flow speed is injected                to the rock core. Matrix permeability of the rock                core is calculated. The rock core is divided into                semi-rock cores. Confining pressure of the                semi-rock cores is adjusted. Crack opening degree                between the semi-rock cores is controlled.                Effective permeability of the crack rock core is                calculated. Crack permeability of the rock core is                calculated according to matrix permeability and                effective permeability to obtain crack guide                capacity. Carbon dioxide oil displacement recovery                rate is obtained according to change relation                curve. Carbon dioxide storage rate is                calculated.
   USE - Method for evaluating storage rate of carbon                dioxide in a crack reservoir geologic body.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing displacement                fluid oil extraction simulation experiment to                obtain crude oil extraction degree-injection carbon                dioxide volume relation curve, obtaining recovery                rate of crude oil and breakthrough time of the                displacing fluid to quickly and accurately compare                different displacing fluid displacement effect and                providing guidance suggestion for optimizing crack                oil reservoir displacing mode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device                for evaluating storage rate of carbon dioxide in a                crack reservoir geologic body.

PN CN107983112-A; CN107983112-B
TI Carbon dioxide capture and separation cycle involves carbonizing carbon dioxide-containing flue gas, capturing carbon dioxide with calcium-based absorbent filler in rotary-type fixed bed, combusting, and regenerating absorbent.
AU RONG N
   FANG T
   ZHU S
   WANG Y
   MA J
   WANG G
   HU H
   MA X
   TIAN X
   PAN W
   WANG J
   LI W
AE UNIV ANHUI JIANZHU (UYAH-C)
GA 201837918J
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide capture and separation cycle involves adding carbon dioxide-containing flue gas generated by fossil fuel utilization to carbonization reaction zone from flue gas side inlet passage, capturing carbon dioxide by carbonization reaction zone with a calcium-based absorbent filler provided in a rotary-type fixed bed, generating calcium carbonate, continuously rotating fixed bed rotor around the rotating shaft, rotating carbonated filler on flue gas side to fuel gas side, performing combustion reaction of fuel gas and oxygen, passing resultant high-temperature flue gas through fixed bed packing, obtaining high carbon dioxide-enriched fuel gas at fuel gas side outlet passage, regenerating calcium-based absorbent filler from calcium carbonate, adding partially deactivated absorbent to a steam activation reaction zone, generating calcium hydroxide, discharging excess steam, converting filler to calcium hydroxide and regenerating carbon oxide absorbent.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capture and separation cycle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The temperature of flue gas carbonization reaction is adjusted to 600-700 degrees C. The carbon dioxide-free flue gas is discharged from flue gas side outlet passage. The heat generated by processing the flue gas in fixed bed packing is utilized for endothermic calcium carbonate decomposition reaction. The activation reaction temperature is 300-400 degrees C. The calcium hydroxide conversion is carried out at 400-650 degrees C. The combustion reaction of fuel gas and oxygen is carried out at 900-1100 degrees C. The reaction zone high-temperature gas is utilized as heat for calcium hydroxide dehydration.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The particle diameter of calcium-based absorbent is 1 mm or more. Preferred Components: The calcium-based absorbent particles are chosen from limestone, dolomite and calcium oxide. The high-temperature regeneration gas is nitrogen or air.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The fuel gas is chosen from methane, natural gas, carbon monoxide, hydrogen and synthesis gas.

TI Method for producing natural gas substitute and carbon dioxide product, involves recycling gaseous fraction to form separation insert, and utilizing liquid fraction to form carbon dioxide product.
AU HAGENFELDT P
   MANIUT C
   MERCER J
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 201822267T
AB    NOVELTY - The method (100) involves cooling a separation insert at a first pressure level to a second temperature level below first temperature level, and partially condensed to obtain a first liquid fraction and a first gaseous fraction. The first liquid fraction is expanded to a second liquid fraction and a second gaseous fraction to a second pressure level above first pressure level. The first gaseous fraction is used to form natural gas substitute. The second gaseous fraction is recycled to form separation insert. The second liquid fraction is used to form carbon dioxide product.
   USE - Method for producing natural gas substitute and carbon dioxide product.
   ADVANTAGE - The need for washing water units is prevented so that biological contamination is reduced. Hence good control and management of the contaminants which are removed in washing units is achieved. The high-purity natural gas substitute is produced, and the quality of the liquid carbon dioxide is ensured by purification process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method involves providing separation insert at first pressure level and first temperature level using a feed gas mixture containing methane and carbon dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a plant for producing natural gas substitute and carbon dioxide product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the process for producing natural gas substitute and carbon dioxide product.
   Step for producing natural gas substitute and carbon dioxide product (100)
   Step for feeding gas stream to booster (101)
   Step for feeding precompressed gas stream to secondary compression (102)
   Step for feeding dried gas stream to optional treatment (105)
   Step for cooling purified gas stream (106)

PN CN114717364-A
TI Controlling three-gas incubator involves            determining leakage amount of carbon dioxide in current            period based on flow of first electromagnetic valve and            flow of second electromagnetic valve, and determining            compensation duration of first electromagnetic valve in            current period.
AU HU W
   WANG P
   DUAN Z
   XU Z
   ZHENG W
   JU H
   CHEN H
AE QINGDAO HAIER BIOMEDICAL CO LTD (HIER-C)
   QINGDAO HAIER BIOMEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (HIER-C)
GA 202297502S
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling three-gas incubator involves                determining the leakage amount of the carbon                dioxide in the current period based on the flow of                the first electromagnetic valve and the flow of the                second electromagnetic valve under the condition                that the system is in a steady state, determining                the compensation duration of the first                electromagnetic valve in the current period based                on the carbon dioxide leakage amount, and                controlling the first electromagnetic valve to                operate based on the compensation time length. The                three-gas incubator comprises first electromagnetic                valve for controlling the delivery of carbon                dioxide into the incubator body and a second                electromagnetic valve for controlling the delivery                of nitrogen into the incubator body.
   USE - Method for controlling three-gas incubator for                placing multiple cultures such as cells and                microorganisms at one time in the technical field                of biological medical treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to increase the opening                degree of first electromagnet through compensation                duration to compensate the leakage amount of carbon                dioxide in the current period, so that the static                error of the concentration of the carbon dioxide is                reduced, and the accuracy of controlling the gas                concentration of the three-gas incubator is                improved when the concentration of oxygen and                carbon dioxide is adjusted by the three-gas                incubator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a device for controlling triple gas                incubator, which comprises processor and a memory                storing program instructions, where processor is                configured to perform a method for triple gas                incubator control when executing the program                instructions; and
   (2) a three-gas incubator, which comprises                first electromagnetic valve and a second                electromagnetic valve, where first electromagnetic                valve is used for controlling the carbon dioxide to                be conveyed into the incubator body, and a device                for controlling three-gas incubator.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step of determining                  the amount of carbon dioxide leakage in the                  current cycle based on the flow rate of the first                  solenoid valve and the flow rate of the second                  solenoid valve specifically involves obtaining a                  first flow and first opening time of first                  electromagnetic valve in a current period, and                  second flow and second opening time of second                  electromagnetic valve in current period,                  determining the gas leakage amount in the current                  period based on first flow, second flow, first                  time length and second time length, and                  determining the carbon dioxide leakage amount                  based on gas leakage amount and current carbon                  dioxide concentration in box body. The first time                  length is determined through a                  proportional-integral-derivative (PID) algorithm                  based on the current carbon dioxide concentration                  and the set carbon dioxide concentration under                  the condition of being at the starting time node                  of the current period, and second time length is                  determined through a PID algorithm based on the                  current oxygen degree and the set oxygen                  concentration in the box body. The step of                  determining the compensation time length of the                  first solenoid valve in the current cycle based                  on the leakage amount of the carbon dioxide                  specifically involves calculating the ratio of                  the leakage amount of the carbon dioxide to the                  first flow amount, and determining the ratio as                  the compensation duration. The step of                  controlling operation of first solenoid valve                  based on compensation period specifically                  involves increasing the opening duration of the                  first electromagnetic valve in the current period                  by the compensation duration, and controlling the                  first electromagnetic valve to operate based on                  the compensated opening duration in the current                  period. The system is determined to be in steady                  state by detecting the concentration of carbon                  dioxide in the box body, and determining that the                  system is in a steady state under the condition                  that the difference value between the carbon                  dioxide concentration and the set carbon dioxide                  concentration is in a set interval and lasts for                  a set time length.

A light source chamber (54) houses the light source, a gas flow path (p52) connects sample cell to light source chamber and guides gas flowing out from sample cell into light source space, and a carbon dioxide absorption element arranged in gas flow channel. A carbon dioxide concentration adjustment unit adjusts the concentration of carbon dioxide in light source zone. The carbon dioxide absorbing element includes activated alumina filled in a column. PT P
PN CN107894386-A; CN107894386-B
TI Quantitative evaluation of the effect of supercritical carbon dioxide injection on the pore throat structure of low-permeability sandstone reservoirs comprises e.g. drilling rock core sample, washing, drying, and measuring permeability.
AU ZHAO J
   LI T
   ZHAO K
   GAO H
   DONG Y
AE UNIV XIAN SHIYOU (UXSY-C)
GA 201828841X
AB    NOVELTY - Quantitative evaluation of the effect of supercritical carbon dioxide injection on the pore throat structure of low-permeability sandstone reservoirs comprises e.g. drilling rock core sample from full diameter of rock core, washing, drying, and measuring rock core sample permeability, simulating formation of water using oilfield produced water, vacuumizing rock core sample, saturating working fluid, calculating rock core porosity and pore volume, measuring nuclear magnetic resonance T2 spectrum for core sample, and measuring irreducible water saturation by centrifugation.
   USE - The method is useful for quantitative evaluation of the effect of supercritical carbon dioxide injection on the pore throat structure of low-permeability sandstone reservoirs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Quantitative evaluation of the effect of supercritical carbon dioxide injection on the pore throat structure of low-permeability sandstone reservoirs comprises (i) drilling rock core sample from full diameter of rock core, washing, drying, and measuring rock core sample permeability, and simulating formation of water using oilfield produced water, (ii) vacuumizing rock core sample, saturating working fluid, and calculating rock core porosity and pore volume, (iii) measuring nuclear magnetic resonance T2 spectrum for core sample obtained in step (ii), and measuring irreducible water saturation by centrifugation, (iv) resaturating core sample after centrifugation, (v) placing core sample into core holder, connecting carbon dioxide flowing system, setting test temperature and pressure, and continuously injecting supercritical carbon dioxide into the core sample, (vi) drying core sample obtained in step (v), vacuumizing, and saturating working liquid, (vii) measuring magnetic resonance T2 spectrum of the core sample, and measuring irreducible water saturation by centrifugation, (viii) measuring nuclear magnetic resonance T2 before and after injecting supercritical carbon dioxide, and drawing pore throat radius distribution curve, and (ix) quantitatively comparing and evaluating changes of the moving pore throat and the distribution of pore throat radius before and after the supercritical carbon dioxide injection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the diameter of core sample is 2.5 cm and the length is 3.8 cm. In the step (v), setting temperature at 70 degrees C and pressure at 8 MPa, and injecting supercritical carbon dioxide at 0.5 ml/minute speed for 24 hours. In the step (viii), measuring nuclear magnetic resonance T2 using formula represented by r = (2 x T2 x rho )/1000, where T2 is the transverse relaxation time, r is the pore throat radius (microns), rho is the surface relaxation rate (microns/second).
DC H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)
MC H01-B03B2; S03-E07C; S03-E13D; S03-E14E7; S03-F06B
IP G01N-015/08; G01N-024/08

PT P
PN WO2022150922-A1
TI Slow down of ripening of harvested fruit involves            infusing carbon dioxide gas into water to create carbon            dioxide-infused water and repeatedly spraying the water            onto the harvested fruit.
AU ARCHIBALD J
   JULIUS M
AE CO2 GRO INC (COTW-Non-standard)
GA 202293661C
AB    NOVELTY - Slowing ripening of harvested fruit,                comprises: infusing carbon dioxide gas into water                to create carbon dioxide-infused water; spraying                the harvested fruit with the carbon dioxide-infused                water; and repeatedly spraying the fruit with                carbon dioxide infused water. The infusing step                comprises using hollow carbon fibers, which create                bubbles of carbon dioxide of 0.2 mu m in diameter.                The spraying step is carried out with a period of                30-60 minutes, each spraying is for a duration of                5-10 seconds. The harvested fruit is on display for                purchase by consumers. The infusing carbon dioxide                gas into water comprises infusing carbon dioxide                gas to a concentration of 1.4 g/l water.
   USE - The method is useful for slowing spoilage of                harvested fruit and other grocery produce, during                storage, transportation, and retail, e.g. fruits,                and vegetables.
   ADVANTAGE - By delivering high concentrations of carbon                dioxide to harvested fruit through spraying the                fruit with carbon dioxide-infused water, ethylene                production within the fruit is suppressed, leading                to a slowing2down of the ripening process, without                increasing atmospheric carbon dioxide                concentrations. This allows more time to pass                between harvesting the fruit and consumption, which                can reduce food wastage, in a way which does not                increase the risk to humans. The method can be used                with other grocery produce, so as to reduce                spoilage of the produce. The carbon dioxide will                still enter the cells of the fruit and suppress the                production of ethylene, and hence slow the ripening                process, while minimizing increasing the carbon                dioxide of the surrounding atmosphere, but will be                less than ideal than full exposure of the harvested                fruit to the carbon dioxide-infused water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                slowing spoilage of grocery produce.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-A04

PN EP3730199-A1; WO2020216967-A1
TI Method for removal of gaseous contaminants from carbon dioxide processing fluid based processing system, involves venting gaseous portion as vented gas flow from condenser volume for separating gaseous portion from liquid portion.
AU DE VRIES T J
AE FOLIUM BIOSCI EURO BV (FOLI-Non-standard)
   FOLIUM BIOSCI EURO BV (FOLI-Non-standard)
GA 2020A4876V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves providing a processing system (1) provided with main volume (3) and condenser volume which brings the main volume and condenser volume in fluid communication. The main volume and condenser volume is maintained the carbon dioxide processing fluid at a process pressure and process temperature. The process pressure and process temperature corresponds to one selected from a liquid state and supercritical state for carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide processing fluid is cooled in the condenser volume to a second temperature. The second temperature is lower than the process temperature, resulting in carbon dioxide enriched liquid portion and gaseous portioning enriched with air component in the condenser volume. The gaseous portion vented as vented gas flow from the condenser volume for separating the gaseous portion from the liquid portion.
   USE - Method for removal of gaseous contaminants from carbon dioxide processing fluid based processing system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The efficient removal of air or possibly air with other components that provide supercritical temperature lower than that of carbon dioxide from mixture comprises pressurized carbon dioxide, which accumulates in systems in which liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide is recycled or re-used. Since the method allows recycling the carbon dioxide during operation, so that the composition of the carbon dioxide processing fluid is maintained at constant level or at low level of air contaminants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide processing fluid based processing system; and
   (2) a process for re-use of liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide processing fluid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of carbon dioxide processing fluid based processing system.
   Processing system (1)
   Main volume (3)
   Outlet (4)
   Cleaning chamber (5)
   Inlet (6)

PT P
PN CN114774100-A
TI Sequestering carbon dioxide by utilizing silicon            dioxide aerogel nanofluid in oil recovery, involves            injecting silicon dioxide aerogel nanofluid and carbon            dioxide gas into formation simultaneously.
AU LU T
   LI B
   DU L
   LI Z
   CAO A
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
GA 202298734G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for sequestering carbon dioxide by                utilizing silicon dioxide aerogel nanofluid,                involves injecting silicon dioxide aerogel                nanofluid and carbon dioxide gas into formation                simultaneously, where the mass concentration of the                silicon dioxide aerogel nanofluid is 0.1-1 wt.%,                the volume ratio of silicon dioxide aerogel                nanofluid and carbon dioxide gas injected into the                formation is 1:(5-8), the bubbles generated by the                silicon dioxide aerogel nanofluid and carbon                dioxide gas in the formation will adsorb dissolved                and adsorbed carbon dioxide to further                increase.
   USE - The method is used for sequestering carbon                dioxide by utilizing silicon dioxide aerogel                nanofluid in oil recovery (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for using the carbon dioxide by utilizing silicon                dioxide aerogel nanofluid in oil recovery, which                involves simultaneously injecting the silicon                dioxide aerogel nanofluid and carbon dioxide gas to                a thin oil type oil reservoir to displace the oil                reservoir.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass                  concentration of the silicon dioxide aerogel                  nanofluid is 0.5-1 wt.%.Preferred Component: The silicon dioxide                  aerogel nanoparticles are hydrophobically                  modified silicon dioxide aerogel nanoparticles                  with a specific surface area of 900-1100 m2/g, a                  density of 0.178 cm3/g, a particle size of                  15-25nm, a porosity of greater than 90%, and a                  pore size of 5-10 nm.Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the silicon dioxide aerogel nanofluid involves                  (i) drying the silicon dioxide aerogel                  nanoparticles at high temperature, (ii) adding                  absolute ethanol and deionized water to the dried                  silicon dioxide aerogel nanoparticles, and                  ultrasonically dispersing after stirring, (iii)                  centrifuging the silicon dioxide aerogel                  nanoparticles after ultrasonic dispersion at high                  speed, pouring off the supernatant, adding                  deionized water for centrifugal washing, and                  repeating for 3-5 times, and (iv) adding the                  silicon dioxide aerogel nanoparticles processed                  in step (iii) to deionized water and uniformly                  stirring to prepare silicon dioxide aerogel                  nanofluid.Preferred Conditions: The projected surface                  area of the bubbles increases by 15-45% relative                  to the injection type at 15 minutes, and the                  projected surface area increases by 37-59%                  relative to the injection type at 30 minutes. In                  step (i), the high-temperature drying is carried                  out at 180-250&#8451;, preferably                  200&#8451; for 1-2 hours, preferably 2 hours.                  In step (ii), when the ratio of the absolute                  ethanol added to the silicon dioxide aerogel                  nanoparticles is 9-15 ml/g, the ultrasonic                  dispersion is carried out for 5-10 minutes, and                  when the ratio of the added absolute ethanol to                  the silicon dioxide aerogel nanoparticles is 10                  ml/g, and the ultrasonic dispersion is carried                  out for 5 minutes.

PN RD686042-A
TI Loop circuit for production and utilization of e-methanol as energy storage, comprises methanol production and storage tank, carbon dioxide storage tank and methanol fuel cell, where seawater is subjected to electrolysis in electrolyzer.
AU 
AE GHADRDAN M (GHAD-Individual)
   BORRESEN B T (BORR-Individual)
   BORCH J H (BORC-Individual)
   SANNER G (SANN-Individual)
   EQUINOR ENERGY AS (DENO-C)
GA 2021649684
AB    NOVELTY - The loop circuit comprises a methanol production and storage tank, a carbon dioxide storage tank and a methanol fuel cell. The seawater is fed into the system and subjected to electrolysis in an electrolyzer. The hydrogen produced is combined with carbon dioxide to produce e-methanol. The oxygen produced is routed to be utilized in the methanol fuel cell. The loop circuit allows the carbon dioxide from a carbon dioxide capture unit to be combined with purchased carbon dioxide or the carbon dioxide produced from the methanol fuel cell.
   USE - Loop circuit for use as an off-shore or on-shore facility for production and utilization of e-methanol as energy storage.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide volume requirement from the carbon dioxide capture unit is reduced. Production of sufficient carbon dioxide from the methanol fuel cell is ensured.

PT P
PN CN111282597-A; CN111282597-B
TI New zinc complex catalyst useful in preparing cyclic carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate and conventional thermodynamically carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate containing continuous carbon dioxide units.
AU ZHANG Y
   LIAO X
   HE J
AE UNIV JILIN (UYJI-C)
GA 202056005K
AB    NOVELTY - Zinc complex catalyst (I) is new.
   USE - (I) useful: in preparing cyclic carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate (claimed); and in preparing conventional thermodynamically carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate containing continuous carbon dioxide units.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: realizes alternating copolymerization of epoxy monomer and carbon dioxide (regioselective polycarbonate greater than or equal to 99%); and exhibits high activity (TOF ia greater than or equal to 3200/h.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Zinc complex catalyst of formula (I) is new.R = alkyl or alkyl derivative, preferably methyl, ethyl, isopropyl, n-butyl, t-butyl, phenyl, benzyl, -CH2OCH3,- CH2N(CH3)2, -CH2SCH3, -CH2CH2N(CH3)2,- CH2CH2OCH3 or -CH2CH2SCH3.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) cyclic carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate of formula (II); and
   (3) method for preparing (II), comprising mixing cyclohexene oxide-free aqueous solution and (I), then filling carbon dioxide, and performing polymerization under anhydrous and anaerobic conditions to obtain cyclic carbon dioxide-based polycarbonate.
   n = positive integer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises mixing 4,4-diaminodiphenylmethane, acetylacetone, p-toluenesulfonic acid and toluene to perform the first hydroxylamine condensation reaction to obtain first ligand, then mixing ethanol solution of the first ligand, hydrochloric acid solution of 1,4-dioxane and 4,4-diaminodiphenylmethane to perform second hydroxylamine condensation reaction to obtain second ligand, and mixing solution of second ligand and solution of the zinc compound to perform substitution reaction to obtain catalyst, where zinc compound in the solution of the zinc compound includes zinc compounds of alkyl zinc or alkyl derivatives. Preferred Components: In first hydroxylamine condensation reaction: ratio between 4,4-diaminodiphenylmethane, acetylacetone, p-toluenesulfonic acid and toluene is 11.6-24.0:12:1-100:1-3; and reaction is carried out under reflux conditions at 80-130 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours. In second hydroxylamine condensation reaction: ratio between first ligand, hydrochloric acid solution of dioxane and 4,4-diaminodiphenylmethane in ethanol solution of the first ligand is 3:4.2-8.4:1.65-3.3; concentration of hydrochloric acid in hydrochloric acid solution of dioxane is 4.0 mol/l; and second hydroxylamine condensation reaction is carried out under reflux condition at 80-90 degrees C for 4-12 hours. The alkyl zinc is dimethyl zinc, diethyl zinc, diisopropyl zinc, di-n-butyl zinc, di-tert-butyl zinc, diphenyl zinc or dibenzyl zinc; and alkyl derivatives is zinc di(methoxymethyl), di(methoxyethyl) zinc, di(methylthiomethyl) zinc, di(methylthioethyl) zinc, zinc bis(dimethylaminomethyl) or zinc bis(dimethylaminoethyl); molar ratio between second ligand and zinc compound solution 1:2-3; and temperature of the substitution reaction is 90-110 degrees C, and time is 24-48 hours.

AU CONSTANTZ B R

AB    NOVELTY - System comprises: a source of the carbon dioxide containing gas; a source of an aqueous medium; a reactor configured to contact the carbon dioxide containing gas with the aqueous medium under conditions sufficient to produce a bicarbonate rich product; and produce a solid carbonate composition and a product carbon dioxide gas from the bicarbonate rich product; and an injector configured to inject the product carbon dioxide gas in a subsurface geological location.
   USE - The system is useful for sequestering carbon dioxide (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for removing a non-carbon dioxide pollutant from a multicomponent gaseous stream, comprising contacting the multi-component gaseous stream with an aqueous medium under conditions sufficient to produce a bicarbonate rich product, and subjecting the bicarbonate rich product to carbonate precipitation conditions to remove a non-carbon dioxide pollutant from the multicomponent gaseous stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The non-carbon dioxide pollutant comprises nitrous oxide (NOx), sulfur oxide (SOx), volatile organic compounds, heavy metals and/or particulate matter. The aqueous medium is a bicarbonate buffered aqueous medium. The bicarbonate buffered aqueous medium has a pH of 8-10. The carbon dioxide containing gaseous stream is a multicomponent gaseous stream. The bicarbonate rich product comprises droplets of a liquid condensed phase in a bulk liquid. The carbon dioxide containing gas is contacted with the aqueous medium in the presence of a catalyst that mediates the conversion of carbon dioxide to bicarbonate. The solid carbonate composition is produced without the use of an alkalinity source. The product carbon dioxide gas comprises pure carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide containing gas is obtained from an industrial plant. The carbon dioxide containing gas is a flue gas. The system further comprises compressing the product carbon dioxide gas before injecting the product carbon dioxide gas in the subsurface geological location. The subsurface geological location is a subterranean location. The system is configured to produce a commodity from the solid carbonate composition. The commodity is a building material. The building material is an aggregate. The building material is a cement or supplemental cementitious material. Preferred Method: The method produces a mole of purified carbon dioxide for every two moles of carbon dioxide removed from the gaseous stream.

   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the jet structure (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  blowing the recirculated reducing gas, oxygen                  with coal dust separately into the blast furnace                  for combustion through the outer channel of a                  three-pass injection device, blowing coal dust                  tangentially into a cylindrical mixing chamber                  open to the outer ring channel of the injection                  device with nitrogen as the propellant gas,                  analogous to the jet gas of a spiral jet mill,                  and parallel to this, feeding the recycling gas                  into the blast furnace through the central                  channel of the injection device. The methane and                  oxygen for oxidation and generation of a                  separating jet of water and carbon dioxide is                  blown into the blast furnace to isolate the                  recycling gas core jet from the outer combustion                  jet of oxygen and dusty substances through the                  inner ring channel of the injection device in the                  stoichiometric ratio of natural gas. The recycled                  gas is heated to 350 degrees C by means of a                  metal heat exchanger and to greater than or equal                  to 900 degrees C by means of a plasma torch or                  with the help of a pebble heater without the                  upstream heat exchanger. The recycling gas is                  heated to greater than or equal to 900 degrees C                  and fed through the shaft nozzles as reducing gas                  to increase performance, another combustible gas                  is used as a reducing gas with the help of the                  three-way injection device, and the synthesis gas                  from a gasification plant or coke oven gas is                  injected through the inner tube of the injection                  device into the blast furnace. The HT gate valve,                  ceramic-lined ball valves or other fittings can                  be shut off when using plasma torches on blast                  furnaces to replace the plasma torch electrodes                  for other work on the plasma torch the supply                  line to the plasma torch and the connecting line                  of the heated reducing gas in the furnace through                  suitable shut-off valves for these operating                  conditions.

   USE - The method is useful for preparing effervescent beverage, which is used as effervescent alcoholic beverage (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares effervescent beverage with increased carbon dioxide gas pressure without impairing the foamability of the effervescent beverage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: The beverage has an alcohol content of 4.1 vol.% or more, and NIBEM value of 240 seconds or more. Preferred Method: The method involves reacting protein with proteolytic enzyme by maintaining the protein at a first temperature of 50-62 degrees C, and reacting polysaccharide with alpha -glucosidase by maintaining the polysaccharide at a second temperature of 55-80 degrees C higher than the first temperature. The method involves preparing effervescent beverage having a carbon dioxide pressure of at least 205 kPa at 20 degrees C by increasing the carbon dioxide pressure so that the carbon dioxide pressure at 20 degrees C of the beverage is 205 kPa or more, adjusting the carbon dioxide pressure of the beverage at 20 degrees C to less than 205 kPa and having the same or higher NIBEM value as when manufactured under the same conditions. Preferred Components: The raw material further comprises a protein, and the raw material liquid comprises a mixture comprising the polysaccharide and the protein.

PN CN109750083-A
TI Measuring immobilized lipase activity in supercritical carbon dioxide comprises e.g. using polyvinyl alcohol and olive oil solution as substrate and aminated silica immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase as enzyme catalyst.
AU ZHAN S
   WANG J
   SHANG X
   HOU W
AE UNIV DALIAN (UYDV-C)
GA 201944468K
AB    NOVELTY - Measuring immobilized lipase activity in supercritical carbon dioxide comprises using polyvinyl alcohol and olive oil solution as substrate and aminated silica immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase as enzyme catalyst, determining the catalytic activity of the immobilized enzyme in supercritical carbon dioxide by acid-base titration.
   USE - The method is useful for measuring immobilized lipase activity in supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes solvent, which are all green reagents; is accurate and simple; does not require large-scale instruments and equipment; and is a testing method for immobilized enzyme activity having broad application prospects.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (i) mixing polyvinyl alcohol with olive oil, adjusting the pH of the substrate solution to 6-10, and performing high-speed homogenization treatment, (ii) dissolving the aminated silica porcine pancreatic lipase sample in the phosphate-buffered saline solution, and (iii) separately preheating the reaction kettle, the substrate solution and the enzyme solution to temperature of 50 degrees C, adding the substrate solution and the enzyme solution to the reaction kettle, blowing the reaction system with carbon dioxide to close the gas outlet valve, raising the temperature to 40-110 degrees C and the pressure is 8-15 MPa, maintaining the pressure under magnetic stirring, after completing the reaction, adding 15 ml 95% ethanol solution to the kettle to terminate the reaction, titrating the reaction solution with sodium hydroxide standard solution until the color became pink, and calculating the immobilized lipase activity based on the volume of the sodium hydroxide standard solution consumed. In the step (ii), controlling the concentration of the enzyme solution, where the difference between the alkali amount of the sample and the reference substance is 1-2 ml, dissolving 0.1-0.2 g aminated silica-immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase sample in 20 ml phosphate-buffered saline solution, dissolving 0.2 g aminated silica-immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase sample in 20 ml phosphate-buffered saline solution. In the step (iii), the reaction temperature is 49-110 degrees C, preferably 90 degrees C and the reaction pressure is 8-15 MPa, preferably 12 MPa. Preferred Components: The concentration of polyvinyl alcohol in the step (i) is 4%, the volume ratio of polyvinyl alcohol to olive oil is 3:1, and the pH of the substrate solution is 9. The mass ratio of olive oil and aminated silica-immobilized porcine pancreatic lipase in the step (iii) is 100:1.

PN CN108862623-A
TI Removing nitrate nitrogen in water having low organic carbon content, involves aerating carbon dioxide with carbon dioxide-containing gas in low organic carbon content water body to acidify low organic carbon content water body.
AU FENG C
   PENG T
   CHEN N
AE UNIV CHINA GEOSCIENCES BEIJING (UYGJ-C)
GA 201895522L
AB    NOVELTY - Removing nitrate nitrogen in water having a low organic carbon content, involves aerating carbon dioxide with carbon dioxide-containing gas in low organic carbon content water body to acidify low organic carbon content water body to form an acidic water body, mixing the iron-carbon micro-electrolytic material and the inert filler, immersing in hydrogen autotrophic denitrifying sludge to form a mixed biological filter material. The obtained acidic water body is passed through mixed biological filter material.
   USE - Method for removing nitrate nitrogen in water having low organic carbon content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The iron-carbon microelectrolytic material is made of iron-carbon composite material. The iron is any one of zero-valent iron, iron filings, sponge iron or nano-iron, and the carbon is activated carbon or coal. The carbon dioxide is an industrially produced high purity carbon dioxide or an exhaust gas produced by the combustion of fossil fuels. The microbial attachment filler is zeolite, basalt, activated carbon, one or more combinations of ceramsite, porous ceramic, sponge and polyurethane foam. The microbial active filler is phosphate rock and medical stone. The inert filler is used in quartz sand, zeolite, pebbles, anthracite and garnet. Preferred Process: The method involves aerating the carbon dioxide to the acidic water body and the mixed biological filter material. The reaction of the iron-carbon coupled hydrogen autotrophic denitrification system involves ferrous oxide and 2 mole of hydrogen to give ferrous ion and hydrogen gas. The iron-carbon microelectrolytic material is mixed with iron and carbon, where mass ratio of the iron and carbon is 3:1-1:1. The mixed biological filter material is further filled with a microbial adhesion filler and a microbial active filler.

TI Carbon dioxide electrolytic reduction device for            use in field of carbon dioxide electrolysis, has            specific air inlet and other air inlet that are            communicated with pipeline type cavity, and specific            second one-way valves that are fixedly mounted in            specific air inlet and other air inlet.
AU AN C
   CHEN R
AE LIAONING INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (LSTI-C)
GA 2021D3418J
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a cathode treatment tank (1),                an anode treatment tank (2) and a flow plate (3).                An air duct (101) is arranged between an air inlet                and an air outlet of the cathode treatment tank. A                first one-way valve (102) is fixedly communicated                with the inner wall of the air duct. The air outlet                of the first one-way valve is coated with a thin                film, and an air hole is formed in the middle                position of the thin film. A pipeline type cavity                is formed in the buffer plate (4). A first air                inlet and a second air inlet are formed in two                sides of the buffer plate respectively. The first                air inlet and the second air inlet are communicated                with the pipeline type cavity. A second one-way                valves are fixedly mounted in the first air inlet                and the second air inlet. The generated hydrogen                ions migrate to the cathode through an ion exchange                membrane (302) to participate in a carbon dioxide                electroreduction reaction to generate carbon                monoxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide electrolytic reduction device                for use in field of carbon dioxide                electrolysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The spiral air duct with the spiral structure                is arranged in the cathode treatment tank, so that                the flowing stroke of air flow is increased when                carbon dioxide gas is injected, and the air flow is                released through the groups of first one-way                valves, so that the direct sample injection of                reaction gas is realized, the gas flow field and                the retention time are increased, the contact area                is increased, the liquid seepage phenomenon is                reduced, and the catalytic reaction efficiency is                greatly enhanced. The pipeline type cavity is                arranged in the slow flow plate, so that the                contact area of the organic composite electrolyte                in which carbon dioxide is dissolved is effectively                increased, the retention time is increased, and gas                is fully diffused to the surface of the catalyst.                The position of the piston plate group in the                cathode treatment tank is regulated according to                the amount of electrolyte and the amount of                electrolyte, after the first one-way valve on the                inner wall of the gas guide pipe is opened, so that                the pressure in the cathode treatment tank is                regulated, the speed of releasing carbon dioxide by                the first one-way valve is changed, and the                completeness of electrolytic reaction is                ensured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for electrolytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic sectional view                of a carbon dioxide electrolytic reduction device                for use in field of carbon dioxide                electrolysis.

PT P
PN CN112010337-A
TI Preparing large-size aragonite whiskers by pumping refined lime milk into carbonization reaction tank, adding magnesium salt crystal form control agent, heating, passing refined carbon dioxide gas, filtering, drying and dispersing.
AU PENG T
   SUN H
   LUO D
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USWS-C)
GA 2020C2018Y
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing large-size aragonite whiskers by intermittent carbonization of lime-based raw materials containing impurities, involves: crushing and grading the limestone raw material, and calcining at a high temperature to obtain impurity-containing quicklime and crude carbon dioxide gas; adding water to digest the impurity-containing quicklime, then aging to obtain crude lime milk, adding water to the crude lime milk, and stirring and removing impurities to obtain a refined lime milk; removing impurities from the crude carbon dioxide gas to obtain refined carbon dioxide gas; and pumping the refined lime milk into a carbonization reaction tank, adding a magnesium salt crystal form control agent containing magnesium ions and stirring uniformly, heating to the reaction temperature, passing the refined carbon dioxide gas into the carbonization reaction to obtain a suspension, filtering the suspension, washing, drying and dispersing.
   USE - The method is used for preparing a large-sized aragonite whisker (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high resource utilization rate, simple process, low process cost, and easy industrial realization. The obtained aragonite whiskers have the characteristics of large size, high straightness rate and uniform dispersion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing large-size aragonite whiskers by intermittent carbonization of lime-based raw materials containing impurities, involves: crushing and grading the limestone raw material, and calcining at a high temperature to obtain impurity-containing quicklime and crude carbon dioxide gas; adding water to digest the impurity-containing quicklime, then aging to obtain crude lime milk, adding water to the crude lime milk, and stirring and removing impurities to obtain a refined lime milk; removing impurities from the crude carbon dioxide gas to obtain refined carbon dioxide gas; and pumping the refined lime milk into a carbonization reaction tank with high-speed stirring and heating functions and a gas conduit, adding a magnesium salt crystal form control agent containing magnesium ions and stirring uniformly, heating to the reaction temperature, passing the refined carbon dioxide gas into the carbonization reaction to obtain a suspension, filtering the suspension, washing, drying and dispersing to obtain the large-size aragonite whiskers. In the step of obtaining the suspension, the addition amount of the magnesium salt crystal form control agent can satisfy the condition that the magnesium ion concentration in the solution reaches 0.2-0.6 mol/l and the reaction temperature is 70-90 degrees C, carrying out more than twice aeration and stirring process in sequence. In the aeration and stirring process, the process involves passing the refined carbon dioxide gas at the speed of 30-60 l/minute.kg calcium oxide for 20-40 minutes while stirring, immediately stopping the passing of the refined carbon dioxide gas, and continuously stirring for 10-20 minutes. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the large-sized aragonite whisker prepared by the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The aeration and stirring steps are performed twice or more in sequence, and the pH value of the carbonization reaction system reaches 6.25-6.75 as an end condition. The impurity removal in the step of obtaining the refined lime milk comprises separating and removing insoluble solid impurities in the crude lime milk after stirring with water by a cyclone classifier and/or filter press. The impurity removal in the step of obtaining refined carbon dioxide gas comprises removing the solid particles and sulfur dioxide in the crude carbon dioxide through a bag filter and a device equipped with a saturated sodium bicarbonate solution. Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature is 78-84 degrees C, and the speed is 40-50 l/minute.kg calcium oxide. Preferred Composition: The magnesium ion concentration is 0.35-0.45 mol/l. The amount of water added in the digestion reaction satisfies a solid-to-liquid ratio of 1:3-1:10, and the amount of calcium hydroxide in the crude lime milk is 0.1-0.4 mol/l. Preferred Components: The limestone raw materials are one or more chosen from limestone containing quartz and/or mica as impurities, raw marble ore, and tailings or tailings produced during the mining and processing of limestone mines or marble mines, where the content of calcium carbonate in the limestone-based raw materials is 70-99%. Preferred Properties: The phase of the aragonite whiskers is aragonite, the morphology is toothpick-like, the aragonite content reaches 80% or more, the middle diameter of the whiskers is 3-10 mu m, and the whisker length is 60-90 mu m.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining a to-be-detected area (S1). A sealed box is buckled on the to-be-detected area. Gas is input (S2) in the sealed box after passing outside air through the alkaline solution. The gas in the sealed box is input (S3) into a carbon dioxide measuring apparatus to measure carbon dioxide flux. A measuring value sequence is generated according to the measured carbon dioxide flux. Data of the measuring value sequence is obtained (S4) to calculate the carbon dioxide flux by using no-flow variable formula. The outside air is sent to the carbon dioxide measuring apparatus through the alkaline solution.
   USE - Non flow meter dynamic tank carbon dioxide flux measuring method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables guaranteeing measuring precision of a flow meter device, simplifying design of the flow meter device, and reducing cost of the flow meter device for better progress.

PT P
PN WO2020165048-A1
TI Preparing shaped cellulose article e.g. fiber, by contacting cellulose and ionic liquid for cellulose mass, forming mixture of activated carbon/carbonizable component, shaping into shaped cellulose article, carbonizing and precipitating.
AU OCONNOR P
AE CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
GA 202079777R
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing a shaped cellulose article containing activated carbon, involves (a) contacting cellulose with an ionic liquid to form a liquid cellulose mass, (b) forming a mixture of an activated carbon or a carbonizable component in the liquid cellulose mass, (c) shaping the mixture to form a shaped cellulose article containing activated carbon or a carbonizable component, (d) in case the mixture comprising a carbonizable component, performing in-situ carbonization of the carbonizable component in the liquid cellulose mass before or after step (c), (e) precipitating the mixture before or after step (c) to form a precipitate mixture comprising the activated carbon or carbonizable component and cellulose, (f) removing the ionic liquid before step (c) followed by chemical modification for carbon dioxide capture, where the method is suitable and adapted for carbon dioxide capture from an air stream.
   USE - The method is used for preparing a shaped cellulose article, preferably fibers, yarns, films, extruded articles, microspheres, beads, extrudate particles, granulates and articles made from the shaped articles, preferably fabrics or particle beds. The article is used for carbon dioxide adsorption. The article is used as a catalyst, a catalyst support or as an adsorbent or adsorbent support, preferably as an adsorbent or adsorbent support for capturing and/or concentrating of carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide containing sources such as flue gas, biogas and/or air (all claimed). The article includes a sheet or particles including microspheres, beads, extrudates, granulates, monoliths or structured adsorbers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces cost and waste generation, and ensures high quality control. The article has high mechanical stability, tenacity and strength combined with high adsorbing and/or catalytic activity of active carbon, preferably when used as carbon dioxide absorbent or as supports for carbon dioxide absorbent for carbon capture application. The shaped article does not easily trite or breaks and forms dust, and has high strength to provide mechanical stability to structured adsorbers and monoliths. The method is easier/simpler to modify the activated carbon in the shaped article, and the article has high molecular weight, high crystalline and strength properties, ball-mill hardness, abrasion strength, impact hardness, ball-pan hardness, T-bar hardness, crushing strength and impact-hardness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing a shaped cellulose article containing activated carbon, involves (a) contacting cellulose with an ionic liquid to form a liquid cellulose mass, (b) forming a mixture of an activated carbon or a carbonizable component in the liquid cellulose mass of step (a), (c) shaping the mixture to form a shaped cellulose article containing activated carbon or a carbonizable component, (d) in case the mixture comprising a carbonizable component, performing in-situ carbonization of the carbonizable component in the liquid cellulose mass before or after step (c), (e) optionally precipitating the mixture before or after step (c) to form a precipitate mixture comprising the activated carbon or carbonizable component and cellulose, (f) removing the ionic liquid before step (c) followed, if needed, by chemical modification for carbon dioxide capture, where the method is preferably suitable and adapted for carbon dioxide capture from an air stream by contacting the article with carbon dioxide from an air stream, then releasing the captured carbon dioxide by a temperature and/or pressure swing. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a shaped article obtainable by the above method, preferably fibers, yarns, films, extruded articles, microspheres, beads, extrudate particles, granulates and articles made from the shaped articles, preferably fabrics or particle beds; and
   (2) a method for carbon dioxide adsorption, comprising contacting a carbon dioxide containing gas with a shaped article, where the active carbon comprises a carbon dioxide adsorbent and the shaped article is in the form of fibers, such as a fabric made of the fibers, or in the form of a bed of particles or structured adsorbers or monoliths.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (a), the ionic liquid is a molten salt, comprising zinc chloride or calcium carbonate, preferably a zinc chloride hydrate, preferably zinc chloride tetrahydrate, in substantial absence of protic acid, and further comprising a proton scavenger, preferably zinc oxide, where the cellulose component of the biomass is delaminated but not dissolved by the ionic liquid. In step (c), the shaped article is shaped by extrusion or spinning, preferably to elongate or particulate shaped articles. Preferred Method: The active carbon is obtained by partial carbonization of carbonizable components of a cellulose containing biomass by heating the carbonizable components to 100 degrees C, preferably above 150, 200 or even 250 degrees C, and in case of in-situ carbonization in step (d), heating the carbonizable component in the presence of the cellulose is under 500 degrees C, preferably under 200 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In step (b), the activated carbon is provided from an external source, and the activated carbon is obtained by carbonization of carbonizable components from a cellulose containing biomass, preferably lignin, hemicellulose or peptides or their combinations, where the method comprises after step (a), separating where carbonizable components are separated from cellulose containing biomass, subjected to a separate carbonization to form activated carbon, which is provided to form the mixture in step (b). In step (a), the biomass comprising cellulose and carbonizable components is contacted with ionic liquid and the active carbon is formed in-situ in step (d) by partial carbonization of the carbonizable components in the presence of the cellulose. The carbonization is done in the presence of an activator preferably zinc chloride, potassium hydroxide, potassium carbonate, ferric oxide, zinc oxide, potassium bicarbonate, magnesium-aluminum or their mixtures, and the activator is present when the carbonization temperature is 100-350 degrees C, preferably 150-250 degrees C. A carbon dioxide adsorbent is added to the mixture before shaping step (c), preferably adsorbed in the activated carbon, or the carbon dioxide adsorbent is added to the shaped cellulose article comprising activated carbon and the carbon dioxide adsorbent is adsorbed in the active carbon in the shaped cellulose article, and/or the activation of the carbon and the functionalization as a carbon dioxide adsorbent is achieved in one process step. Preferred Conditions: In-situ carbonization in step (d) is done in the mixture after step (a) and before precipitation in step (b) or in the precipitate after precipitation step (b) and before shaping or on the shaped article after shaping step (c). Preferred Composition: The shaped article comprises a carbon dioxide adsorbent which is a carbonate, preferably potassium bicarbonate or one or more nitrogen containing-compounds preferably amine compounds or their combinations. The shaped article comprises an amount of activated carbon relative to the total weight of the shaped article of 1-90 wt.%, preferably 10-70 wt.% and in case of elongate shaped articles, the amount of active carbon is 1-30 wt.%, preferably 5-25 wt.%. Preferred Properties: The shaped article is in the form of an elongate shaped article, preferably a fiber or film, having a long axis and having a smallest diameter of a cross-section perpendicular to the long axis of smaller than 0.5 mm, preferably smaller than 0.1 mm.
