PN CN110776965-A; CN110776965-B
TI Removing water and carbon dioxide from natural gas involves entering normal temperature raw material natural gas containing water and carbon dioxide shell side of first pre-cooled dehydration heat exchanger.
AU DU H
AE DU H (DUHH-Individual)
GA 202015694F
AB    NOVELTY - Removing water and carbon dioxide from natural gas involves entering normal temperature raw material natural gas containing water and carbon dioxide the shell side of the first pre-cooled dehydration heat exchanger/second pre-cooled dehydration heat exchanger through the first intake valve/second intake valve, absorbing bottom-up flow the cooling capacity provided by the refrigerant flowing from top to bottom in the tube to cool it down, so that the water contained in the natural gas is gradually condensed and separated from the natural gas. The portion of the condensed water flows by its own gravity to the bottom of the first pre-cooling dewatering heat exchanger/the second pre-cooling dewatering heat exchanger is collected. The portion natural gas frozen is attached to the outer surface of the heat exchanger.
   USE - Method for removing water and carbon dioxide from natural gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing water and carbon dioxide from natural gas involves entering normal temperature raw material natural gas containing water and carbon dioxide the shell side of the first pre-cooled dehydration heat exchanger/second pre-cooled dehydration heat exchanger through the first intake valve/second intake valve, absorbing bottom-up flow the cooling capacity provided by the refrigerant flowing from top to bottom in the tube to cool it down, so that the water contained in the natural gas is gradually condensed and separated from the natural gas. The portion of the condensed water flows by its own gravity to the bottom of the first pre-cooling dewatering heat exchanger/the second pre-cooling dewatering heat exchanger is collected. The portion natural gas frozen is attached to the outer surface of the heat exchanger, passed through the shell side outlet of the first pre-cooling dehydration heat exchanger/second pre-cooling dehydration heat exchanger, and discharged through the first exhaust valve/second exhaust valve. The low-temperature natural gas is dewatered by the first pre-cooling dehydration heat exchanger/second pre-cooling dehydration heat exchanger continues to enter the first cryogenic refrigeration de-carbon dioxide heat exchanger/second cryogenic refrigeration de-carbon dioxide heat exchange device and cooled by the refrigerant outside the tube. The natural gas is gradually cooled from the gas phase to the gas-liquid two-phase by the cooling capacity provided by the refrigerant circulation system, until it is completely converted to liquid natural gas. The carbon dioxide in the medium is gradually cooled and precipitated, and a portion is dissolved in the liquid natural gas, while the carbon dioxide exceeding the saturated solubility is precipitated and frozen and fixed on the inner surface of the heat exchange tube. Finally, the natural gas is cooled to become supercooled liquid natural gas from the first depth. The cold-freeze carbon dioxide heat exchanger/second deep-freeze carbon dioxide heat exchanger is discharged from the tube side of the top and enters the discharge port through the first/second discharge valve to produce a liquid natural gas (LNG) product. The normal-temperature and low-pressure refrigerant is used from the refrigerant return flow outlet of the main heat exchanger enters the refrigerant inlet of the compressor of the refrigerant circulation system and is cooled by the condenser after entering the refrigerant inlet of the main heat exchanger, and cooled by the backflow low-pressure refrigerant, entered the first switching heat exchanger group/second switching heat exchanger group through the first refrigerant throttle valve/the second refrigerant throttle valve. The first switching heat exchanger group/second switching heat exchanger group is reheated, the high-temperature heating gas enters from the first heating gas inlet valve/second heating gas inlet valve, and then the first heating gas exhaust valve is turned on to heat and gasify the carbon dioxide and water existing in the heat exchanger group.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The third temperature sensor and a fourth temperature sensor are respectively arranged at the tube-side outlets of the first cryogenic refrigeration de-CO2 heat exchanger and the second cryogenic refrigeration de-CO2 heat exchanger for monitoring the temperature of the discharged LNG product.

GA 202134867U
AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide capture system (100) comprises a first electricity generation unit (110) for producing electric energy by supplying a hydrocarbon-based fuel to a fuel cell (116). A second electricity generation unit (120) is used for generating electric energy using water separated from unreacted gas discharged from the anode of the fuel cell. A carbon dioxide collecting unit (130) collects carbon dioxide from the unreacted gas from which moisture is removed by the second electricity generating unit. A recirculation unit is sued for combusting off-gas discharged after separating the carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide collecting unit. A fuel tank (112) stores the hydrocarbon-based fuel. A reformer (115) is used for reforming some of the fuel discharged from the fuel tank.
   USE - System for capturing carbon dioxide using fuel cell.

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide cycle-utilized hybrid electric power system, has carbon dioxide electric power system and biofuel extraction system that are utilized for utilizing heat exchanger for performing heat-exchange with working fluid.

A biofuel extraction system (B) extracts biofuel using the supercritical carbon dioxide as a working fluid. The supercritical carbon dioxide power generation system and the biofuel extraction system commonly utilize a heat exchanger (130) for performing heat-exchange with the working fluid for heating a ball. TI Method for calculating underground storage amount of carbon dioxide, involves calculating carbon dioxide underground storage amount using capillary number, bond number, and relative permeability of carbon dioxide in flowing state.
AU SUNG W M
   KIM J H
AE UNIV HANYANG IUCF-HYU (UHYG-C)
GA 201918854P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating (610) a capillary number representing a ratio of a viscosity of the underground medium and a capillary pressure of the underground medium. A bond number representing a ratio of gravity and a viscosity of the underground medium is calculated (620). The relative permeability of carbon dioxide in a flowing state of the underground medium is calculated (630). The carbon dioxide underground storage amount is calculated (640) using the Capillary Number, the Bond Number, and the relative permeability of carbon dioxide in a flowing state of the underground medium.
   USE - Method for calculating underground storage amount of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The storage efficiency can be maximized, and injection optimization for forming a flow pattern can be performed. The storage quantity of the carbon dioxide can be optimized.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) an apparatus for calculating underground storage amount of carbon dioxide; and
   (2) a recording medium for calculating underground storage amount of carbon dioxide.

TI Geological storage system of carbon dioxide comprises injection pipe extending to carbon storage reservoir including rock grains and brine, from ground surface, pores disposed between rock grains and storage structure.
AU KWON T
   PARK T
   KWON T H
   PARK T H
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
   KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2019213649
AB    NOVELTY - Geological storage system (100) of carbon dioxide comprises: an injection pipe (130) that extends to a carbon storage reservoir (140) that includes many rock grains and brine, from the ground surface, and supplies an injection material that includes carbon dioxide to the carbon storage reservoir; many pores that are disposed between the rock grains; and a storage structure (110) that is connected with a part of the pores where the carbon dioxide reaches through the pores and then stored, the pores and the storage structure are filled with the brine, and the carbon dioxide is separated from the brine by being surrounded by biosurfactants produced by microorganisms included in the injection material.
   USE - The system is useful for storing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The system reduces interfacial tension between brine and carbon dioxide, increases a contact brine, carbon dioxide, and rocks, reduces a capillary pressure, increases sweep efficiency of carbon dioxide in an carbon storage reservoir, and increases a contact angle between brine, carbon dioxide, and rocks.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for geologically storing carbon dioxide, comprising supplying injection material that includes carbon dioxide, microorganisms, and a bacterial growth medium through an injection pipe that extends to a carbon storage reservoir, forming a micelle structure by biosurfactants produced by the microorganisms, and forming a storage material by trapping the carbon dioxide in the micelle structure, moving the storage material through pores that are disposed between the rock grains and filled with the brine and storing the storage material by moving the storage material to a storage structure that is partially connected with the pores and filled with the brine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates cross-sectional view of carbon dioxide geological storage system.
   Carbon dioxide geological storage system (100)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (110)
   Injection portion (120)
   Injection pipe (130)
   Carbon storage reservoir (140)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The microorganism is Bacillus subtilis, and the biosurfactant is surfactin. The biosurfactants form a micelle structure, and the carbon dioxide is trapped in the micelle structure. Preferred Method: The method comprises, before the supply of the injection material to the carbon storage reservoir, converting a phase of the carbon dioxide to a liquid state or a supercritical state by using a phase conversion device. The phase of the carbon dioxide is determined corresponding to a temperature and a pressure environment of the carbon storage reservoir. In the supplying of the injection material to the carbon storage reservoir, an injection temperature, an injection pressure, and an injection amount of the injection material are controlled.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide is in a gas state, a liquid state, or a supercritical state.

TI Preparing a carbon dioxide trapping agent used in recycling carbon dioxide comprises prepolymerizing N-vinylpyrrolidone and tetravinylsilane, prepolymerizing acrylamide-based monomer e.g. 2-acrylamido-2-methylpropyltrimethylammonium chloride, and acetylenic alcohol, purifying and drying.
AU GUO X
   LIU X
   HOU Y
   WANG L
   SONG X
   CUI S
AE SHANDONG DESHI CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   DESHI ENERGY TECHNOLOGY GROUP CO LTD (DESH-Non-standard)
GA 2021C9100E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a carbon dioxide trapping agent comprises prepolymerizing N-vinylpyrrolidone and tetravinylsilane with a molar ratio of 1:5-10 to obtain a first prepolymer, where the degree of polymerization of the first prepolymer being 5-15, prepolymerizing the acrylamide-based monomer and the acetylenic alcohol with a molar ratio of 1-10:1 to obtain a second prepolymer, where the degree of polymerization of the second prepolymer is 10-40, adding the second prepolymer to the first prepolymer to polymerize for at least 3 hours, purifying and drying to obtain carbon dioxide trapping agent.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a carbon dioxide trapping agent, which is used in recycling carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide trapping agent can improve the adsorption selectivity and adsorption capacity of carbon dioxide. The method can be adsorbed and recovered the carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas using small amount of trapping agent, and can reach the purity of the adsorbed and recovered carbon dioxide of more than 90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide trapping agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The acrylamide-based monomer includes acrylamide, 2-acrylamido-2-methylpropyltrimethylammonium chloride, acrylamidopropyl trimethylammonium chloride, 2-acrylamido-2-methylpropane sulfonic acid, N,N-methylenebisacrylamide, sodium 2-acrylamido-2-methylpropane sulfonate, potassium 2-acrylamido-2-methylpropane sulfonate and/or N-(hydroxymethyl)acrylamide, preferably N-(methylol)acrylamide, acrylamide and N,N-methylenebisacrylamide. The mass ratio of the N-(hydroxymethyl)acrylamide, acrylamide and N,N-methylenebisacrylamide is 1-10:2-5:0-5. The mass ratio of the first prepolymer and the second prepolymer is 10-40:20-70. The polymerization temperature of the first prepolymer and the second prepolymer is 80-90degrees Celsius. The pore size of the trapping agent is 5-40 nm, the specific surface area is 500-2000 m2/g, and the pore volume is 1.5-6 cm3/g. The pores with a pore diameter of 15-25 nm in the trapping agent account for more than 50%.Preferred Method: In the method, adding nano-montmorillonite to the first prepolymer, maintaining at 80-90degrees Celsius for more than 1 hour, adding the second prepolymer to polymerize for 3-10 hours, and obtain the carbon dioxide trapping agent after purification and drying, where the weight of the nano-montmorillonite accounts for 5-10% of the first prepolymer. In the method, N-vinylpyrrolidone, tetravinylsilane and initiator are dissolved in a solvent, stirred uniformly, and nitrogen is introduced, the temperature is raised to 50-75degrees Celsius, and the first prepolymer is obtained after 2-10 hours of reaction, and/or dissolve the acrylamide-based monomer in the solvent and raise the temperature to 80-90degrees Celsius, and subsequently the acetylenic alcohol and the initiator are added dropwise to polymerize the second prepolymer after 1-5 hours.

AU WANG B
   JIN B

   LIU S
   ZHOU F
   ZHENG F
   LIU J
   SUN H
   DING X
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 201909141N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves utilizing a carbon dioxide gun as a source of seismic detection on a working surface. The carbon dioxide gun is excited on a side of the working surface. The carbon dioxide gun is arranged in a detonating line that is led to a drilling hole. Four detectors are distributed on a same side of the working surface and connected through a connecting line, a collecting station and a collecting host. An excitation position of the drilling hole is determined to locate the carbon dioxide gun. The carbon dioxide gun is excited in a carbon dioxide hole position by a computer according to carbon dioxide flow for locating and combining site geological data for comprehensive analysis. The working surface is configured to forecast of geologic anomaly.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables accurately locating carbon dioxide gun position for seismic signal processing so as to effectively ensure accuracy of working surface seismic acquisition.

PN CN114651631-A; CN217770945-U
TI Indoor plant cultivation device for use in a            plastic greenhouse, has light control switch for            controlling whether wet wind at carbon dioxide blowing            pipe and head is mixed with carbon dioxide, where            organic carbon fertilizer is applied to plant            cultivation.
AU NIU D
AE NIU D (NIUD-Individual)
GA 202286530E
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a plant cultivation box (1)                used to hold plant cultivation substrates. Multiple                wet wind preparation devices (2) provide wind with                gaseous water. Two ends of an oxygen delivery box                (3) are connected with the wet wind preparation                device and a bottom part of the plant cultivation                box, respectively. Two ends of a carbon dioxide                injection box (4) are respectively connected with                the wet air preparation device and a carbon dioxide                blowing pipe (6) and/or a carbon dioxide blowing                head (7). The carbon dioxide blowing pipe and the                carbon dioxide blowing head are used for planting                plants grown in the plant cultivation box. A blade                conveys wet air. A light control switch is used to                control whether the wet air at the carbon dioxide                blowing pipe and the carbon dioxide blowing head is                mixed with carbon dioxide according to light                conditions. A water-soluble small-molecule organic                carbon fertilizer is applied to the plant                cultivation substrate.
   USE - Indoor plant cultivation device for use in a                plastic greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The device makes the carbon dioxide and the                organic carbon cooperate with other growth elements                of plants, and also provides oxygen, gaseous water                and wind to plants, which is beneficial to the                growth of indoor plants, so as to solve the problem                that the closed environment planting plant carbon                source is not enough. The device inputs oxygen into                the plant substrate cultivation promoting plant                root growth together with carbon, and plants the                plant in the closed indoor to provide wind and                gaseous water, making the wind and the gaseous                water and carbon dioxide, and oxygen needed by                plant to generate the synergetic effect, which is                more beneficial for the healthy growth of indoor                plant. The air hole is not closed by the                self-protection mechanism of the blade, so that the                light effect of blade, breathing effect and                transpiration can be normally performed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an indoor                plant cultivation method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structure schematic                diagram of the indoor plant cultivation.

PN CN114428071-A
TI Rapid on-site measurement of carbon dioxide concentration in air and shallow soil, involves carrying out instrument calibration and correction coefficient determination, measuring concentration of carbon dioxide in air, soil and shallow soil and carrying out results analysis application.
AU SUN X
   HAN J
AE BEIJING URANIUM GEOLOGY RES INST (CNNU-C)
GA 2022663464
AB    NOVELTY - The method for rapid on-site measurement of carbon dioxide concentration in space air and shallow soil involves: carrying out instrument calibration and correction coefficient determination; measuring the concentration of carbon dioxide in air, soil and shallow soil; and carrying out results analysis application, if it is found that the carbon dioxide measurement value is obviously abnormal, repeating the operation of the preparing an instrument and correcting the coefficient at the abnormal point until the error between the measurement results does not exceed plus minus15%.
   USE - Method for rapid on-site measurement of carbon dioxide concentration in space air and shallow soil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a rapid on-site measurement of carbon dioxide concentration in air and shallow soil, which has been applied to the relevant scientific research work, and can also be applied to the fields of environmental survey, geological survey, geothermal survey. The measurement work is realized, and large-batch data are measured in a short time. The characteristics of universal adaptability, wide applicability and relative accuracy are realized, and the requirements of relevant specifications of field geological and environmental measurement are met. The determination of carbon dioxide in the air of public places. The relative error of the measurement result does not exceed plus minus10%, and the detection limit is 0.24 mg/m3 or 20x10-6 (volume ratio). The method has the characteristics of standardization, programming, high efficiency, simplicity, convenience and easiness in operation, and is favorable for obtaining reliable carbon dioxide under the conditions of different areas, geology, landform, hydrology, vegetation and climate. The results have lateral contrast. The measurement work has the characteristics of measuring a large amount of data in a short time, and has the characteristics of universal adaptability, wide applicability and relative accuracy, and meets the relevant specifications for field geology and environmental measurement. The method aims at short-term mass measurement of carbon dioxide concentration in air and shallow soil in open, closed and semi-closed spaces, and will greatly improve the work efficiency of carbon dioxide concentration measurement items in environmental and geological surveys, especially for production with relatively low precision requirements.

PN CN112657478-A
TI Preparing closed semi-closed space cerium modified carbon dioxide adsorption material involves adding ammonium salt and dispersant to the cerium salt solution, stirring separately to obtain a cerium salt solution system.
AU YANG B
   BAI X
   WANG L
   WU J
   WU H
   ZHOU W
   LI P
   YU X
   PAN X
   GENG M
   JING Y
   ZHAO Y
AE STATE GRID XIAN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID CORP CHINA (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID SHAANXI ELECTRIC POWER CO ELE (SGCC-C)
   STATE GRID JIBEI ELECTRIC POWER CO ELECT (SGCC-C)
GA 2021483271
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing closed semi-closed space cerium modified carbon dioxide adsorption material involves adding ammonium salt and dispersant to the cerium salt solution, stirring separately to obtain a cerium salt solution system. The cerium salt solution system is added to the adsorption among the carbon materials, the cerium modified carbon dioxide adsorbent is obtained by performing multi-stage heating and calcination.
   USE - Method for preparing closed semi-closed space cerium modified carbon dioxide adsorption material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has stronger modified material and good economic benefitand social value, large adsorption capacity and strong stability, avoids the problem of carbon dioxide escaping caused by the temperature rise of the current adsorption carbon material, satisfies the quality requirement of closed semi-closed space carbon dioxide elimination, in simple and cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using cerium modified carbon dioxide adsorbent material which involves spraying cerium modified carbon dioxide adsorbent material on the inner wall of the closed semi-closed space for carbon dioxide adsorption or arranging cerium modified carbon dioxide adsorption material on a movable carrier for carbon dioxide adsorption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The method involves preparing a cerium salt solution, dissolving and dispersing the raw material cerium to adjust the concentration to 150-300 g/L, and stirring uniformly at room temperature to obtain the cerium salt solution. The method involves configuring the cerium salt solution system, adding ammonium salt to the cerium salt solution, adding different ammonium salts or ammonia water and adjusting the pH value of the solution to 6-11 after the first stirring, then adding a dispersant, and performing the second time and stirring uniformly to obtain a cerium salt solution system. The wood straw is roasted and prepared, washed with 0.1-3 mole/L hydrochloric acid for 2 hours, and then calcined at 200-300 degrees C for 3 hours to obtain the final adsorption carbon material. The cerium salt solution system is divided into solid-liquid ratio of 3:1 and added to the adsorption carbon material for multi-stage heating and calcination. The calcination is carried out at a temperature of 120-200 degrees C for 1-4 hours, then calcined at a temperature of 250-300 degrees C for 1-4 hours, continue to calcine at a temperature of 300-400 degrees C for 1-4 hours, and finally calcined at a temperature of 400-500 degrees C for 1-4 hours. The stirring time is 1.5 hours. The ammonium salt accounts for 0.05-10% of the total weight of the raw material cerium, reacted in an oil bath environment of 30-90 degrees C, where the first stirring time is 1-3 hours, and the second stirring time is 1-3 hours. The cerium salt solution system is cooled, and the cerium salt solution system obtained after the treatment is placed in the air to cool naturally. The particle size of the cerium modified carbon dioxide adsorbent is 10-20 micrometer, and the porous large specific surface area is 1200-1390 m2/ g. Preferred Components: The raw material cerium is cerium sulfate, cerium nitrate, and cerium carbonate. The ammonium salt is at least one of ammonium carbonate, ammonium oxalate, and ammonium citrate. The dispersant is sodium citrate, sodium cetylbenzene sulfonate.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

An electrical connection is provided                for electrically connecting the generator with a                habitat, where electricity generated by the                generator is supplied to the habitat through the                connection.

Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of (CaO+Fe)/B is 0.1-0.3:1. The flow rate of carbon dioxide in the carrier gas is 10-40 centimeter/minutes, preferably, 20-30 centimeter/minutes. The deoxidation reaction is performed at a temperature of 850-950 degrees C. The ID/IG of the carbon product is 0.87-3.25. The regeneration is performed in an atmosphere containing carbon monoxide (CO) and the temperature is 850-950 degrees C.

PN US2020140270-A1; US10730749-B2
TI Co-producing carbon dioxide containing stream and syngas stream by introducing e.g. high-pressure hydrocarbon containing stream into syngas generator and introducing e.g. low-pressure hydrocarbon containing stream into oxy-combustion device.
AU GUILLARD A
   ROESCH A
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2020374629
AB    NOVELTY - Method for co-producing a carbon dioxide containing stream and a syngas stream, involves introducing a high-pressure hydrocarbon containing stream and a high-pressure oxygen containing stream into a syngas generator to produce a high-pressure syngas stream, introducing a low-pressure hydrocarbon containing stream and a low-pressure oxygen containing stream into an oxy-combustion device to produce a low-pressure carbon dioxide containing stream, and introducing the low-pressure carbon dioxide containing stream into a waste het boiler to produce steam, and introducing the steam into a work expander to generate work and a carbon dioxide containing stream.
   USE - The method is used for co-producing a carbon dioxide containing stream and a syngas stream. The high-pressure syngas stream is used for producing downstream product without further syngas compression, where the downstream product is methanol produced in a methanol reactor, and/or ammonia produced in an ammonia reactor. The carbon dioxide containing stream is used for downstream separation. At least a portion of the high-pressure syngas stream is used as feedstock for an oxy-alcohol reactor (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces overall operation and capital expenditures and reduces the direct and indirect carbon dioxide and criteria pollutants such as sulfur oxide, nitrogen oxide, carbon monoxide, volatile organic compound, and particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The syngas generator comprises a partial oxidation reactor and/or an autothermal reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The high-pressure oxygen stream, the high-pressure nitrogen stream, and the low-pressure oxygen stream are produced by vaporizing a high-pressure liquid oxygen stream, a high-pressure liquid nitrogen stream, and a low-pressure liquid oxygen stream in a main heat exchanger. The high-pressure oxygen stream and the high-pressure nitrogen stream are produced by vaporizing a high-pressure liquid oxygen stream and a high-pressure liquid nitrogen stream in the main heat exchanger, and the low-pressure oxygen stream is not vaporized in a heat exchanger. The high-pressure oxygen stream, the low-pressure oxygen stream, and the high-pressure nitrogen containing stream are produced in the same air separation unit. The high-pressure nitrogen stream is not subject to further nitrogen compression. A high-pressure nitrogen stream is combined with the high-pressure syngas stream to produce an ammonia reactor feed stream, which is then introduced into the ammonia reactor. The high-pressure hydrocarbon containing stream is at the same pressure as required by the syngas generator. The ammonia reactor feed stream is further compressed in a feed compressor. The high-pressure syngas stream has a pressure equal to or greater than 50 barg, preferably 100 barg.

PN US2019366277-A1; US11285445-B2
TI Thin film composite membrane used to capture chemical specie comprises porous polymer support e.g. polyacrylonitrile, and carbon dioxide-philic defect-free selective layer e.g. polyacrylonitrile-r-methacrylate polyethylene glycol copolymer.
AU KARUNAKARAN M
   KUMAR M
   SHEVATE R
   AKHTAR F H
   PEINEMANN K
AE UNIV KING ABDULLAH SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYIK-C)
GA 2019A1667N
AB    NOVELTY - Thin film composite membrane comprises: a porous polymer support comprising polyacrylonitrile, polysulfone, polyetherimide and polyvinyl difluoride; and a carbon dioxide-philic defect-free selective layer comprising a polyacrylonitrile-r-methacrylate polyethylene glycol (PAN-r-PEGMA) copolymer, where the selective layer is a thickness of 1.2-1.5 mu m and a molecular weight of 40-60 kDa.
   USE - The membrane is useful for capturing at least one chemical specie (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane exhibits: a gas permeability of carbon dioxide is greater than hydrogen is greater than methane is greater than oxygen is greater than nitrogen; and a selectivity of carbon dioxide/nitrogen is greater than carbon dioxide/methane is greater than carbon dioxide/hydrogen (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) fabricating a thin-film composite membrane comprising dissolving PAN-r-PEGMA copolymer in at least one of an alcohol and alcohol-water mixture to form a copolymer solution and immersing or partially immersing a porous polymer support in the copolymer solution to form, by dip-coating, a carbon dioxide-philic defect-free thin-film composite membrane; and
   (2) capturing at least one chemical specie comprising contacting the membrane comprising PAN-r-PEGMA with a fluid composition and capturing carbon dioxide from the fluid composition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The porous support includes PAN. The thickness of the porous support is 150-200 mu m. The polyether-based copolymer is at least one of PAN-r-PEGMA40, PAN-r-PEGMA60, PAN-r-PEGMA64 and PAN-r-PEGMA67. The alcohol-water mixture is an ethanol-water mixture. The ratio of alcohol to water in the alcohol-water mixture is 7:3 w/w. The fluid composition further includes at least one of hydrogen, methane, oxygen and nitrogen. Preferred Method: The step of dissolving proceeds at a temperature of 80 degrees C, where a concentration of the PAN-r-PEGMA copolymer in the copolymer solution is 1-3 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN US2019151789-A1; CN109813054-A; KR2019058985-A; KR2019058986-A; KR1997355-B1; KR2038684-B1; CN109813054-B; US11071938-B2
TI Carbon dioxide capturing apparatus for capturing carbon dioxide, has capturing cooler to capture carbon dioxide contained in flue gas by performing heat exchange between capturing coolant discharged from chiller and flue gas.
AU SUNG H
   CHA S
   CHE S
   SUNG H C
   CHA S H
AE DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
   DOOSAN HEAVY IND & CONSTR CO LTD (DOOS-C)
GA 201944829H
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (100) has a heat exchanger (121) to cool primary coolant using heat exchange between the primary coolant and liquefied natural gas (LNG). A chiller (150) is connected to the heat exchanger to discharge capturing coolant that is colder than the primary coolant by performing a heat exchange between the capturing coolant and a cooling material. A capturing cooler (141) captures carbon dioxide contained in flue gas by performing a heat exchange between the capturing coolant discharged from the chiller and the flue gas.
   USE - Carbon dioxide capturing apparatus for capturing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The coolant is efficiently cooled to a cryogenic temperature, since the coolant is cooled in two stages and a portion of the coolant is controlled to bypass. The cooling efficiency of the flue gas is improved, since the secondarily bypassed coolant is supplied to the gas cooler to cool the flue gas before flowing into the capturing cooler. The internal pressure of the bypass line is reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a power generation system using cold heat of liquefied natural gas (LNG).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the carbon dioxide capturing apparatus using cold heat of liquefied natural gas.
   Carbon dioxide capturing apparatus (100)

TI Carbon dioxide reaction accelerating-type lift ceiling ventilation device, has ventilation device provided with air inlet, air pipe and air outlet, where air inlet is fixed at top surface of ceiling and air inlet is detached with air outlet.
AU CHEN Y
AE GUANGDONG BOHO IND GROUP CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201924782T
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a lift cage (1) whose top surface is equipped with a ceiling (2) that is equipped with a ventilation system. The ventilation system we provided with a ventilation device (3) and a carbon dioxide detecting device (4). The ventilation device is electrically connected with the carbon dioxide detecting device. The ventilation device is provided with an air inlet (30), an air pipe (31), a negative pressure fan (32) and an air outlet (33). The air inlet is fixed at a top surface of the ceiling. The air inlet is detached with the air outlet through the air pipe.
   USE - Carbon dioxide reaction accelerating-type lift ceiling ventilation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device accelerates reaction of content of carbon dioxide of the lift cage with lime water in an air excess condition so as to accelerate circulation of air, thus reducing the content of the carbon dioxide in air ratio, and hence relieving air warming dryness and maintaining lift air quality.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a carbon dioxide reaction accelerating-type lift ceiling ventilation device.

TI Fuel cell system has controller that executes process for intentionally increasing carbon dioxide concentration in combustion exhaust gas during power generation period of fuel cell and inspects carbon dioxide sensor.

GA 2018759688
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a fuel cell (13) that generates power using hydrogen gas and oxidant gas. A reformer (11) generates the hydrogen gas. A combustor (15) heats the reformer. A carbon dioxide sensor (19) is positioned in the exhaust passage (17). A controller (32) monitors the combustion state in the combustor based on the detection value of the carbon dioxide sensor. The controller executes a first process for intentionally increasing the carbon dioxide concentration in the combustion exhaust gas during the power generation period of the fuel cell and inspects the carbon dioxide sensor, and executes a second process for causing the power generation amount of the fuel cell to converge with a target value.

The balance of operation of a fuel cell system is maintained, and the fluctuation of electric power generation amount is suppressed. The operation of a fuel cell system is stably continued.

GA 201850314K

The refrigerant cooling evaporator passes refrigerant and high temperature and high pressure gas to the condenser for compressing the refrigerant through the auxiliary recycle compressor to return and exchange heat of water. The refrigerant cooling evaporator passes carbon dioxide fluid of a carbon dioxide gas cooler outlet to the auxiliary circulation throttling valve. TI Liquefied natural gas cold energy utilization based-ship liquefied natural gas-carbon dioxide storage system has heat exchanger whose liquid carbon dioxide outlet is connected with carbon dioxide inlet of reserved empty tank through carbon dioxide conveying pipeline.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a combustion application device (5) provided with an oxygen inlet. A carbon capture device (7) is provided with a tail gas inlet, a decarburization tail gas outlet and a carbon dioxide outlet. The tail gas outlet of the combustion application device is connected with the tail gas inlet of the carbon capture device through the tail gas conveying pipeline. The decarburization tail gas outlet is connected with the atmosphere through the pipeline. The carbon dioxide outlet is connected with the gaseous carbon dioxide inlet of a gas compressor (8) through the carbon dioxide conveying pipeline. The gaseous carbon dioxide outlet of the gas compressor is connected with the gaseous carbon dioxide inlet of the heat exchanger through the carbon dioxide conveying pipeline. The liquid carbon dioxide outlet of a heat exchanger (4) is connected with the carbon dioxide inlet of a reserved empty tank (3) through the carbon dioxide conveying pipeline.
   USE - Liquefied natural gas cold energy utilization based-ship liquefied natural gas-carbon dioxide storage system.
   ADVANTAGE - The marine liquefied natural gas-carbon dioxide storage system can meet and greatly exceed the long-term emission reduction requirement of the International Maritime Organization (IMO). The original demand of the LNG fuel sharing tank body, it only needs to add a common tank body can further improve the economical efficiency. By rationally setting the heat exchange device, fully using the cold energy of LNG to cool the pressurized CO2 into liquid state, it is convenient to store and transport. At the same time, it uses the cold-heat management of the combustion application to pre-heat the gaseous CH4, which reduces the energy consumption and improves the energy efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the liquefied natural gas cold energy utilization based-ship liquefied natural gas-carbon dioxide storage system.

AB    NOVELTY - Combating pests which are present in a space (43), preferably insects and/or rodents, comprises providing liquid carbon dioxide, converting the liquid carbon dioxide into gaseous carbon dioxide, bringing the gaseous carbon dioxide to a desired temperature of 10-40 degrees C, preferably 10-30 degrees C and introducing the gaseous carbon dioxide into the space for combating pests.
   USE - The method is useful for combating pests (insects and/or rodents) present in a space (claimed), preferably in a space of transport container comprising a freight container; and reducing the number of hibernating or diapausing pests. No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: enables to combat rodents and insects in an efficient manner without adversely affecting the material present in the space; utilizes gaseous carbon dioxide that reaches all locations in the space, is not harmful to any products which are present in the space, and can be introduced into the space at a relatively low pressure and in a simple manner, or at least greatly reduce, the noise which accompanies the introduction of carbon dioxide into the space under pressure, hence provides a more efficient and animal-friendly method for combating pest in a relatively short time after the introduction of the gaseous carbon dioxide, while ensuring their suffering is relatively limited; prevents insects from hibernating or diapausing, thus allowing to combat even those insects which are inherently difficult to combat, in an inexpensive and efficient manner; reduces any risks to workers when entering the space; and does not utilize the gases such as methyl bromide-phosphine, hence safety for workers.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system (1) for combating pests, comprising a container (22) for containing the carbon dioxide, preferably liquid carbon dioxide, an evaporation device (3) for converting the liquid carbon dioxide into gaseous carbon dioxide, a conditioning unit for bringing the gaseous carbon dioxide to the desired temperature, and delivery means which are configured for supplying the gaseous carbon dioxide from the container to the space at the desired temperature.

   Displaceable frame (21)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The desired temperature is adapted to an average temperature of the space, preferably in which the desired temperature is substantially equal to the average temperature of the space. The method comprises the step of removing the gaseous carbon dioxide from the space after a time period of 6-48 (preferably 12-24) hours after the introduction of the gaseous carbon dioxide. The method further comprises bringing the space to and/or keeping the space at a further desired temperature, where the further desired temperature is 10-40 degrees C, preferably substantially equal to the desired temperature. The gaseous carbon dioxide is introduced into the space substantially evenly distributed over the volume of the space. The gaseous carbon dioxide is introduced into the space at a pressure of 2-4 (preferably 2-3) bar.

   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide (CO2) oxalic acid in the aromatic ester ionic liquid system has high current density and fast oxalic acid production rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The quaternary ammonium base is tetraethylammonium hydroxide or tetrabutylammonium hydroxide, and the quaternary phosphonium base is tetraethylphosphorus hydroxide or tetradecyltrihexylphosphonium hydroxide. The aprotic organic solvent is one or both of acetonitrile, N,N-dimethylformamide or dimethyl sulfoxide. Preferred Compositions: The anolyte in the H-type electrolytic cell is a concentration of 0.1 mol/L of Sulfuric acid (H2SO4) or a 0.1 mol/L sodium sulfate (Na2SO4) solution, lead (Pb) is a working electrode, platinum (Pt) is a counter electrode, and silver (Ag/Ag+) is a reference electrode. The aromatic ester ionic liquid is an aromatic ester compound dissolved in an aprotic organic solvent, and is formulated into a compound system having a concentration of 0.1-1 mol/L as a catholyte. The aromatic ester ionic liquid is obtained by reacting methyl p-hydroxybenzoate with a quaternary ammonium base or a quaternary phosphonium base. The molar ratio of the methyl p-hydroxybenzoate to the quaternary ammonium base or the quaternary phosphonium base is 1:1, the reaction temperature is 25-30 degrees C, and the reaction time is 10-12 hours.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Components: The lithium-containing                  linear negative electrode is a lithium wire, a                  lithium-plated copper wire, a highly-conductive                  carbon nanotube fiber loaded with pure metal                  lithium or lithium silicon alloy or lithium tin                  alloy, and a reduced graphene oxide fiber loaded                  with a pure metal lithium, a lithium silicon                  alloy or a lithium tin alloy. The gel electrolyte                  adheres directly to the lithium-containing linear                  negative electrode. The working electrode is a                  carbon nanomaterial thin film with catalytic                  activity, where the carbon nanomaterial thin film                  includes a highly-conductive carbon nanotube thin                  film, a reduced graphene oxide film, a carbon                  nanofiber film and a tough carbon paper.                  Preferred Properties: The lithium wire has a                  diameter of 0.5-3.4 mm and a length of 1-1000 mm.                  The lithium-plated copper wire has a diameter of                  0.1-1 mm, a length of 1-1000 mm and a lithium                  content of 5-90%. The highly-conductive carbon                  nanotube fiber loaded with pure metal lithium,                  lithium-silicon alloy or lithium-tin alloy has a                  diameter of 0.05-1 mm, a length of 1-1000 mm and                  a metal content of 5-90%. The reduced graphene                  oxide fiber loaded with pure metal lithium,                  lithium-silicon alloy or lithium-tin alloy has a                  diameter of 0.05-1 mm, a length of 1-1000 mm, and                  a metal content of 5-90%. The gel electrolyte has                  a thickness of 0.01-5 mm. The porous heat                  shrinkable tube has a diameter of 3.8-4.5 mm and                  a length of 1-1000 mm.

PN CN111747411-A; CN111747411-B
TI Low-pressure and temperature treatment of industrial carbon dioxide gas comprises e.g. passing carbon dioxide raw material gas into on-site water washing tower and into compression process after washing with water, cooling and dehydrating.
AU LI W
   XU K
   ZHENG S
   QI J
   FENG Z
AE GUANGZHOU HUADA PETROCHEMICAL CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A1192Y
AB    NOVELTY - Low-pressure and temperature treatment of industrial carbon dioxide gas comprises e.g. (i) passing the carbon dioxide raw material gas into the on-site water washing tower from the upstream through the pipeline, and the gas into the compression process after washing with the water, cooling, dehydrating, and cooling the carbon dioxide gas to below 2 degrees C; (ii) passing the low-temperature compressed carbon dioxide gas into the washing tower for water washing, using low-temperature pure water and carbon dioxide compressed gas for heat exchange and the methanol and water in the carbon dioxide compressed gas in the heat exchange process, taking the liquefied and dissolved methanol solution to the bottom of the tower, discharging to the outside through the tower bottom, returning the other part of the water into the cooler by the water washing process water circulation pump, passing into the water washing tower and circulating in this way.
   USE - The method is useful for low-pressure and temperature treatment of industrial carbon dioxide gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can dehydrate and remove impurities and greatly loses carbon dioxide; is environmentally-friendly; and saves energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Low-pressure and temperature treatment of industrial carbon dioxide gas comprises (i) passing the carbon dioxide raw material gas into the on-site water washing tower from the upstream through the pipeline, and the gas into the compression process after washing with the water, cooling, dehydrating, and cooling the carbon dioxide gas to below 2 degrees C; (ii) passing the low-temperature compressed carbon dioxide gas into the washing tower for water washing, using low-temperature pure water and carbon dioxide compressed gas for heat exchange and the methanol and water in the carbon dioxide compressed gas in the heat exchange process, taking the liquefied and dissolved methanol solution to the bottom of the tower, discharging to the outside through the tower bottom, returning the other part of the water into the cooler by the water washing process water circulation pump, passing into the water washing tower and circulating in this way; and (iii) carrying out a heat exchange cooling process using the washed carbon dioxide gas
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of control flow chart of low-pressure and temperature treatment of industrial carbon dioxide gas. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the carbon dioxide feed gas contains a variety of impurities, including moisture, light hydrocarbon impurities, methanol, and sulfur particles through the water washing tower for water washing and heat exchange, reducing the freezing point through the characteristics of methanol aqueous solution, when the water is mixed with a corresponding proportion of methanol to prevent freezing, the greater the methanol mass fraction, the lower the freezing point, maintaining the water in a supersaturated environment, reducing the evaporation temperature of the water, and always exist in the form of liquid, and the liquid water has been deposited at the bottom of the tower to facilitate the removal of water and prevents the generation of water vapor. The step (ii) further comprises controlling the reaction of carbon dioxide and water to achieve dynamic equilibrium when carbon dioxide is continuously dissolved in water forming carbonic acid and decomposing carbonic acid to replace the lost carbon dioxide. In the step (i), the method comprises controlling carbon dioxide raw material gas from the original temperature of 35 degrees C or higher, the pressure of 0.03 Mpa or more, cooling to less than or equal to 2 degrees C and lowering the air pressure to 0.028 Mpa. The method comprises carrying out the heat exchange cooling process in the cooler of the cooling system, where the heat exchange plate of the cooler adopts a plate heat exchanger, increasing the heat exchange area can reduce energy consumption for the heat exchange process, where in the heat exchange process using liquid ammonia as the refrigerant which comes from the bottom of the ammonia liquid separator of the industrial air conditioner, carrying out the heat exchange, returning the gas ammonia into the middle of the ammonia liquid separator through the pipeline, passing into the cooler inlet through the pipeline from the top of the ammonia liquid separator, and recycling.

   ZHANG J
   ZHU L
   WANG K
   LI F
   WANG X
AE LINHE CLIMATE TECHNOLOGY BEIJING CO LTD (LINH-Non-standard)
GA 202232930A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing double quaternary ammonium carbon                dioxide adsorption resin involves mixing resin with                1, 1, 3, 3-tetramethyl guanidine, after swelling                the resin, stirring and reacting for 6h to 24h at                50-60degrees Celsius, filtering and washing to                obtain the first reaction product. The first                reaction product obtained after draining water,                firstly adding tetrahydrofuran, methyl iodide and                sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, adding                tetrabutyl ammonium bromide, stirring and reacting                for 6h to 24h at 50-60degrees Celsius, filtering                and washing to obtain the second reaction product,                filtering the second reaction product obtained,                adding sodium carbonate solution for multiple times                of ion exchange reaction, at last, washing by                water, filtering and drying to obtain the double                quaternary ammonium carbon dioxide adsorption                resin.
   USE - Method for preparing double quaternary                ammonium carbon dioxide adsorption resin used as                the DAC absorbent for absorbing and desorbing                carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the adsorption performance                of the carbon dioxide adsorption resin under the                premise of not affecting the adsorption rate of the                double quaternary ammonium carbon dioxide                adsorption resin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. A double quaternary ammonium carbon dioxide                adsorption resin.
   2. A method for collecting carbon dioxide by                direct air, where the double quaternary ammonium                carbon dioxide adsorbent resin is used as the DAC                absorbent for absorbing and desorbing carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The resin is                  chloromethylated polystyrene resin. The swelling                  agent used in the swelling process is selected                  from one or more of N, N-dimethyl formamide,                  tetrahydrofuran, benzene and toluene. Preferred                  Conditions: The crosslinking degree is 5 % to 10                  %, and the content of chlorine is 2 mmol/g to 6                  mmol/g. The dosage of the swelling agent is that                  each 1g resin is swelled by 2ml-10ml swelling                  agent, the swelling time is 10h to 30h. The mol                  ratio of the resin to 1, 1, 3, 3-tetramethyl                  guanidine is 1: (2 to 4). The dosage of the                  tetrahydrofuran in the step b) is that each 1 g                  of the resin is immersed in 0.5ml to 1.5ml of                  tetrahydrofuran, the mol ratio of methyl iodide                  and resin is (2-4): 1, the volume ratio of the                  tetrahydrofuran and the sodium hydroxide aqueous                  solution is 1: (2 to 5), the molar concentration                  of the sodium hydroxide aqueous solution is                  0.8mol/l to 1.2mol/l, the mass ratio of the                  tetrabutyl ammonium bromide and the resin in the                  step a) is 1: (50 to 150).

AU HUANG Q
   GUAN W
AE GEORGES CLAUDE METHODS STUDY & DEV (GEOR-Non-standard)
GA 2021F0153X
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a main device for providing carbon dioxide product gas of first pressure, a first stage compressor, a first cooler, a water scrubbing tower and a second stage compressor. The first-stage compressor and the second-stage cooler are connected in a sequence along direction of carbon dioxide flow, where pressure of the carbon dioxide product gas after each-stage compression is not less than 8 MPa and content of methanol in the carbon dioxide product gas is not higher than 50 mg/Nm3. A water washing tower is connected with a methanol-water separating tower through a pipeline.
   USE - Device for preparing carbon dioxide product gas generated by low temperature methanol washing into high pressure carbon dioxide carrier gas. Uses include but are not limited to use in chemical industry, machinery, food, agriculture and tobacco industries.

TI Green system for removing crystal of long karst tunnel drainage pipeline comprises carbon dioxide purification device, refrigerating and washing device and carbon dioxide purification device composed of pressure swing adsorption device.
AU ZHONG Z
   XU Y
   LIU X
   GUO H
   LIU D
   ZHOU X
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
   UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2020858304
AB    NOVELTY - Green system for removing crystal of long karst tunnel drainage pipeline comprises carbon dioxide purification device, refrigerating device and washing device. The carbon dioxide purification device is composed of a pressure swing adsorption device, a PSA device and a carbon dioxide collection tank. The refrigeration device is composed of a water storage tank and a refrigerator. The flushing device is composed of a connecting tee, a water pump, a water level control valve and a smart valve. The pressure swing adsorption device is composed of a raw material gas compressor, a temperature swing preconditioner, a pressure swing adsorber, a vacuum pump and a displacement gas compressor. The bottom of water storage tank is connected to the water pump and the carbon dioxide collection tank and upper part is connected to central water ditch through a water level control valve. The water pump is connected to longitudinal drain pipe on one side of a drainage zone through the connecting tee.
   USE - Used as green system for removing crystal of long karst tunnel drainage pipeline.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: solves the crystallization problem of drainage system; utilizes the carbon dioxide waste gas and tunnel drainage inside the tunnel to achieve the self-production and self-sale of carbon dioxide gas; and provides a new application direction for automobile exhaust gas treatment and water resource utilization in karst tunnels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Green system for removing crystal of long karst tunnel drainage pipeline comprises carbon dioxide purification device, refrigerating device and washing device. The carbon dioxide purification device is composed of a pressure swing adsorption device, a PSA device and a carbon dioxide collection tank. The refrigeration device is composed of a water storage tank and a refrigerator. The flushing device is composed of a connecting tee, a water pump, a water level control valve and a smart valve. The pressure swing adsorption device is composed of a raw material gas compressor, a temperature swing preconditioner, a pressure swing adsorber, a vacuum pump and a displacement gas compressor. The bottom of the water storage tank is connected to the water pump and the carbon dioxide collection tank and the upper part is connected to the central water ditch through a water level control valve. The water pump is connected to the longitudinal drain pipe on one side of a drainage zone through the connecting tee. The horizontal drain pipe on the local side of the drainage zone is connected to the smart valve and the horizontal drain pipe on the other side maintains the normal drainage state. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for removing crystal of long karst tunnel drainage pipeline by green method comprising (i) passing the waste gas collected in the ventilation system into the carbon dioxide purification device to extract carbon dioxide gas, (ii) passing the extracted carbon dioxide gas into the water storage tank in the refrigeration device, and (iii) entering water in the water storage tank to the drainage pipe through a washing device to clean the calcium carbonate crystals attached to the inner wall of the pipe.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the green system for removing crystal of long karst tunnel drainage pipeline.

PN US2022146193-A1
TI Simultaneously liquefying carbon dioxide and cooling natural gas in e.g. power plants, involves providing portion of compressed stream required by carbon dioxide liquefaction system to pre-cooling system of natural gas liquefaction system.
AU GUILLARD A
   GUERIF P
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 202266903E
AB    NOVELTY - Simultaneously liquefying carbon dioxide and cooling natural gas, involves (i) providing a compressed carbon dioxide loop, comprises a pressurized stream, where the first compressed stream and a second compressed stream are produced by carbon dioxide compressor, (ii) providing a portion of first compressed stream to carbon dioxide liquefaction system, where first compressed stream provided a portion of the refrigeration required by carbon dioxide liquefaction system, (iii) and further providing a portion of the second compressed stream to the pre-cooling system of a natural gas liquefaction system, where second compressed stream provided a portion of the refrigeration required by the natural gas pre-cooling.
   USE - Method for simultaneously liquefying carbon dioxide and cooling natural gas used in power plants, steam methane reformers and gas turbines. Can also be used in oil recovery, underground sequestration, food and beverage applications or conversion routes such as carbon dioxide to methanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simultaneously liquefying carbon dioxide and cooling natural gas in efficient manner. The carbon dioxide exhibits thermodynamic properties with the possibility to leverage the latent heat of vaporization between -56degrees Celsius (at a corresponding pressure of approximately 5.2 bar abs) and 31degrees Celsius (at a corresponding pressure of approximately 74 bar abs), so that the carbon dioxide is used as a refrigerant instead of propane or hydrofluorocarbon (HFC) for the precooling of the natural gas liquefaction plant. The carbon dioxide is alsonon-flammable, which is beneficial in terms of safety risk and permitting aspect versus hydrocarbons.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for simultaneously producing a pressurized carbon dioxide stream and cooling natural gas, which involves:
   (1) providing a carbon dioxide compressor system, comprises a first compressed stream and a second compressed stream produced by a carbon dioxide compressor;
   (2) providing a portion of the first compressed stream to a downstream user;
   (3) providing a portion of the second compressed stream to the pre-cooling system of a natural gas liquefaction system, where second compressed stream provided a portion of the refrigeration required by the natural gas pre-cooling.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic representation of a combined system.
   Natural gas feed stream (101)
   Natural gas pre-cooler (102)
   Cooled natural gas (103)
   Carbon dioxide containing feed stream (201)
   Carbon dioxide capture and purification unit (202)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The lowest pressure within carbon dioxide loop is greater than the triple point pressure of carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide liquefaction system comprises recycle stream that is introduced into the carbon dioxide compressed carbon dioxide loop. The first compressed cooling stream and the second compressed cooling stream are at the same pressure and/or different pressures. Preferred Components: The downstream user is an enhanced oil recovery system and carbon dioxide sequestration system.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Processing method for making cellulose fibers by pre-swelling or pretreating cellulose fibers, adding additive to increase solubility of whitening drug in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and then immersing cellulose fiber in fluid.
AU ZHU W
   WANG H
   LONG J
   XIAO H
   SHI M
AE BINZHOU LOFTEX IND CO LTD (BINZ-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   NANTONG CELLULOSE FIBERS CO LTD (CNTA-C)
   SHANDONG CHUCAI ANHYDROUS FIBER DYEING (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201887211C
AB    NOVELTY - Processing method based on supercritical carbon dioxide fluid technology for making cellulose fibers involves: (1) pre-swelling or pretreating the cellulose fibers; (2) adding an additive to increase the solubility of whitening drug in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and dissolving a hydrophilic drug, which is not soluble in supercritical fluid in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; (3) adding the whitening drug to a medicine tank of a high-pressure equipment, removing air, introducing carbon dioxide, increasing the pressure in the equipment to 8-30 MPa to obtain a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and immersing the cellulose fiber in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; and (4) introducing the whitening drug into the pre-swollen cellulose fiber, carrying out relative movement between the drug-loaded supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and the cellulose fibers, and leaving still the whitening drug in the amorphous zone inside the cellulose fibers after pressure relief.
   USE - The processing method based on supercritical carbon dioxide fluid technology is useful for making cellulose fibers.
   ADVANTAGE - The processing method is capable of making the cellulose fibers with excellent whitening function.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing method based on supercritical carbon dioxide fluid technology for making cellulose fibers involves: (1) pre-swelling or pretreating the cellulose fibers; (2) adding an additive to increase the solubility of whitening drug in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and using supercritical carbon dioxide/reverse micelle method to dissolve the hydrophilic drug, which is not soluble in supercritical fluid in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; (3) adding the whitening drug to a medicine tank of a high-pressure equipment, removing air, introducing carbon dioxide, increasing the pressure in the equipment to 8-30 MPa and temperature to 32-120 degrees C to obtain a supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, and immersing the cellulose fiber in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid; and (4) introducing the whitening drug into the pre-swollen cellulose fiber by dissolving in the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, continuously carrying out relative movement between the drug-loaded supercritical carbon dioxide fluid and the cellulose fibers, and leaving still the whitening drug in the amorphous zone inside the cellulose fibers after pressure relief to form a drug-loaded cellulose fiber capable of sustained release.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is chosen from methanol, ethanol and/or acetone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: The cellulose fibers include natural cellulose fibers and regenerated cellulose fibers. The natural cellulose fibers include cotton fibers and hemp fibers. The regenerated cellulose fibers comprise viscose fiber, high wet modulus viscose fiber and solvent spun regenerated cellulose fibers. Preferred Method: The pre-swelling method involves: (A) preparing 30-80 %mass N-methylmorpholine-N-oxide aqueous solution, immersing the cellulose fiber in the aqueous solution, where the bath ratio of cellulose fiber and aqueous solution is 1:15-1:35, and setting the temperature at 60-90 degrees C for 10-80 minutes; (2) preparing 10-20% sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, immersing the cellulose fiber in the aqueous solution, where the bath ratio of cellulose fiber and aqueous solution is 1:25-1:40, and setting the temperature at -10 degrees C to 20 degrees C for 10-80 minutes; and (3) preparing an aqueous ionic liquid 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium chloride solution with a water content of 2-5%, immersing the cellulose fiber in the ionic liquid, where the bath ratio of cellulose fiber and ionic liquid is 1:25-1:40, and setting the temperature at 60-120 degrees C for 10-80 minutes. The pretreatment involves carrying out active grafting on the cellulose fibers.
