   USE - Used as factory power plant refinery flue gas                capture, conversion and application full recycling                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves air pollution and                greenhouse effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Factory power plant refinery flue gas capture,                conversion and application full recycling system                comprises energy subsystems, capture subsystems,                conversion subsystems, application subsystems,                water gas units, carbon dioxide capture units,                hydrogen production units, water recovery units,                combined depollution and dust removal units,                supercritical refining and preparation of                nanocellulose units, supercritical carbon dioxide                nanocellulose foaming material unit, where the flue                gas includes dust particles, gaseous compounds,                trace elements, carbon dioxide and moisture, where                the gaseous compound includes at least nitrogen                oxides and sulfur oxides and the flue gas full                amount resources, where the system removes dust                particles and gaseous oxides through the combined                depollution and dust removal unit which captures                carbon dioxide through the carbon dioxide capture                unit in the capture subsystem and pressurizes and                stores it as supercritical carbon dioxide and                recovers moisture in the flue gas through the water                recovery unit. The hydrogen recovery unit                decomposes the recovered water into hydrogen and                oxygen. The oxygen is sent to the water gas unit                for combustion and the high-purity hydrogen is                further obtained through the water gas unit. The                high-stability, low-energy carbon dioxide and high                energy are captured by the conversion subsystem,                where the ethylene oxide reacts to produce ethylene                carbonate (EC). The ethylene carbonate is then                catalytically reacted with hydrogen to obtain                methanol and a dihydric alcohol. The supercritical                carbon dioxide and nanocellulose are used to                prepare a supercritical carbon dioxide                nanocellulose slurry, which is supercritical carbon                dioxide nanocellulose after the concentration of                the slurry is formulated. The material particles                are extruded into a supercritical carbon dioxide                nanocellulose foaming material. The full flue gas                recycling system adopts supercritical refining and                prepares a nanocellulose unit for preparing                supercritical carbon dioxide nanometer cellulose.                The supercritical carbon dioxide nanocellulose                foaming unit is used for preparing a nanocellulose                foaming material. The energy subsystem is connected                to solar power, wind power to clean power and                recovers power through heat recovery and hydrogen                gas-fired power generation is used as a                supplementary energy source, which is stably                configured to provide the necessary power for the                various subsystems and units of the flue gas                full-resources system and at the same time the                power distribution, lighting distribution, and heat                protection within the full flue gas recycling                system. The power distribution, surveillance power                distribution and security distribution to provide                electricity, which passes carbon dioxide capture,                transform, apply and finally produce methanol,                diols and foaming materials to form the material                balance of the system through the integration of                energy subsystems. The utilization of clean energy,                hydrogen energy and system thermal energy can be                achieved to achieve the entire system energy                self-sufficiency. At the same time it can also                output part of thermal energy, electrical energy or                hydrogen energy, thus achieving full energy                quantification and full use of quantitative                quantification targets, where the 1.1 supercritical                refining and preparation of nanocellulose units                includes cellulose, refining and the cellulose has                a particle size of micron and the cellulose                includes wood fiber, carbon fiber, silicon fiber,                metal fiber and/or graphene fiber. The refining                device is a fully-sealed, high-pressure-resistant,                water-free equipment. The refining device mixes the                cellulose and the supercritical carbon dioxide                liquid and beats, and the micron-sized cellulose is                ground into nano-sized fibers through a refiner.                The nano-cellulose and supercritical carbon dioxide                constitute supercritical carbon dioxide                nano-cellulose pulp, referred to as nano-slurry for                short. The content of nano-cellulose in the                nano-slurry is greater than 1%. The refining device                includes slurry tanks, refiners and conveyors. The                slurry tanks, refiners and conveyors are sealed and                connected via three-way valves, vacuum valves and                high-pressure lines and are operated under                supercritical conditions as a whole. The internal                circulating flow is filled with a slurry composed                of a supercritical carbon dioxide liquid phase and                cellulose powder dissolved in the liquid phase of                the supercritical carbon dioxide. The conveyor is                provided with a pump motor and a fluid pump in the                chassis and is passed by the pump motor. The belt                drives the fluid pump, sucks the slurry from the                slurry tank to the pump inlet and conveys the                slurry through the high pressure pipeline to the                refiner inlet through the pump outlet and the                slurry enters via the side flow passage between the                grinding disc and the rotating disc. After the                slurry is finely ground, the slurry is led to the                material cylinder by the hollow rotating shaft and                is sent to the slurry tank through the discharge                port and the high pressure pipeline. At this point,                the slurry forms a cyclic transportation in the                supercritical state and flows.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation                of the factory power plant refinery flue gas                capture, conversion and application full recycling                system (Drawing includes non-English language                text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The lower part of the                  refiner is sealed and provided with a chassis. A                  hollow motor rotating shaft is disposed in the                  chassis by a grinding motor and the hollow                  rotating shaft drives the rotating grinding wheel                  to rotate at high speed. The rotating grinding                  wheel and the fixed grinding disk are rotated. An                  inter-coupled ultra-fine nano-grinding array is                  provided. The super-fine nano-grid array grinds                  micron-sized cellulose into nano-sized cellulose.                  The slurry tank is provided with a temperature                  sensor, a pressure sensor and a density sensor.                  The particle size detection sensors are used to                  detect the temperature, pressure, density, and                  cellulose particle size signals of the slurry in                  the slurry tank respectively, and display the                  above parameter information through a                  programmable logic controller (PLC) controller.                  The slurry tank is provided with a heater and a                  cooler outside is used to control the temperature                  of the slurry in the supercritical state in the                  slurry tank. The carbon dioxide liquid inlet on                  the slurry tank is used to feed the supercritical                  carbon dioxide liquid and through the valve group                  configured on the inlet of the carbon dioxide                  solution together with the vacuum valve. The                  carbon dioxide liquid is discharged or added to                  control the pressure of the supercritical carbon                  dioxide. The slurry tank also includes a mixer, a                  stirrer, a cleaning tank, a discharge port,                  cleaning agent and waste outlet. The mixer and                  stirrer are used to stir and mix the slurry in                  the slurry tank. The cleaning tank is filled with                  a cleaning agent for cleaning the refining                  device. The sewage outlet is used for cleaning                  the waste liquid is discharged. The refiner                  further includes a grinding body, a pressure                  gauge, and a flow meter. The grinding body is a                  shell of a refiner, and the pressure gauge and                  the flow meter are assembled on the high pressure                  pipeline of the feed inlet, which are used to                  show the pressure and flow of supercritical pulp.                  The 1.2 supercritical carbon dioxide                  nanocellulose foaming material unit includes                  auxiliary devices, deployment devices, injection                  devices, twin screw extruders, foaming devices                  and foaming materials. The preparation device                  supercritically regulates the content ratio                  between the supercritical carbon dioxide and the                  nanocellulose in the nano-paste to release the                  carbon dioxide, thus increases the nano-particles                  in the nano-paste, cellulose content, deployment                  device after the release of carbon dioxide, where                  the ratio of carbon nano-slurry of nano-cellulose                  (30-70%):(70-30%) (weight percentage wt.% ),                  which is convenient to maintain the content of                  nano-cellulose in the foaming material and                  increases the performance of the foaming                  material. The preparation device is connected                  with the nano-slurry storage tank, which is used                  for storing and supplying the prepared nano-paste                  material. The amount of carbon dioxide in the                  foamed material is positively correlated with the                  pressure of supercritical carbon dioxide, when                  the pressure is 5 Mpa, the amount of carbon                  dioxide infused is 3%. When the pressure is 15                  Mpa, the amount of carbon dioxide infusion is                  10%. The system adopts a pressure condition of                  about 20 MPa in order to increase the amount of                  infused carbon dioxide to a higher percentage.                  The application subsystem deploys the formulated                  nano-slurry, supercritical carbon dioxide, and                  accelerator to the design ratio and mixes in a                  high-pressure mixer to obtain a supercritical                  liquid material. The supercritical liquid                  material is injected into the twin-screw extruder                  through a nanocellulose injection device. The                  material particles are synchronized together,                  which is fed into a twin-screw extruder and                  melt-mixed with the supercritical liquid                  material. The extrusion product is extruded                  through a die and the extruded product is foamed                  through a foaming process. The foaming material                  product and the auxiliary device supplies the                  required supercritical carbon dioxide liquid from                  the supercritical carbon dioxide tank and is                  provided with a carbon dioxide voltage regulator,                  a high pressure pump, a carbon dioxide constant                  temperature device and a mass flow meter for the                  supercritical carbon dioxide, where the                  quantitative metering feeding the accelerating                  agent metered by a high pressure pump and mass                  flow meter. The material particles include                  plastic particles or powder, cement particles or                  powder, glass particles or powder. The foaming                  material comprises a foamed plastic, foamed                  cement and foamed glass. The foaming material                  includes sheets, plates, profiles, blocks or                  structures. The performance indicators of the                  foamed material are (a) content of nanocellulose                  is 1-5%; (b) elastic modulus of 3-10 GPa; (c)                  heat distortion temperature (HDT) of 130-150                  degrees C; (d) strength of 1000-3000 Kpa, in                  contrast, the strength of domestic corresponding                  foaming materials only reaches 150-500 Kpa, where                  the corresponding foaming materials in the united                  states only reach 1000 Kpa. The combined                  defoaming and dust removal unit passes through a                  dry adsorption tower or a lye absorption tank,                  where the class precipitator removes the dust                  particles and the nitrogen oxides, sulfur oxides                  and trace elements in the flue gas. The trace                  elements includes heavy metals e.g. mercury,                  selenium and arsenic, where the dust particles                  are used in the application subsystem. The                  supercritical carbon dioxide nanocellulose                  foaming unit is used for the manufacture of                  fillers for foamed materials. The nitrogen oxides                  and sulfur oxides are collected in the form of                  nitrates and sulfates by a nitroxide treatment                  system for extraction manufacturing fertilizer.                  The combined decontamination and dust removal                  unit adopts a dry process or a wet process for                  joint decontamination and dust removal process.                  The capturing subsystem performs the carbon                  dioxide and moisture in the flue gas after dust                  removal, desulfurization, and denitrification. In                  the treatment, the flue gas is from the flue gas                  of a factory, a power plant, and a refinery. The                  flue gas is stored in a flue gas tank, and water                  vapor recovery and carbon dioxide recovery are                  performed by a water recovery unit and a carbon                  dioxide capture unit respectively. In a water                  tank the carbon dioxide is stored in a carbon                  dioxide tank, where the carbon dioxide in the                  carbon dioxide tank is converted into                  supercritical carbon dioxide by a supercritical                  pump, and is stored in a supercritical carbon                  dioxide tank. The carbon dioxide recovery unit in                  the capture subsystem comprises absorption and                  desorption unit or ammonia spray absorption tower                  and purification unit. The absorption and                  desorption unit absorbs carbon dioxide in the                  absorption tower through the carbon absorbent and                  desorbs carbon dioxide through regenerating tower                  regenerating carbon absorbent. The carbon                  absorbent is recycled and used. The carbon                  dioxide desorbed by the regeneration tower passes                  through the double desulfurization bed in the                  purification unit. The drying bed and adsorbent                  bed are successively subjected to residual sulfur                  removal, water removal drying, and other trace                  elements removal, where the carbon dioxide is                  purified to make the purity of carbon dioxide                  reach to 99.9%, and the purified carbon dioxide                  gas is stored in a carbon dioxide tank. The                  carbon absorbent comprises a composite                  decarbonized aqueous solution or a non-aqueous                  decarbonization solution. The composite                  decarbonized aqueous solution comprises aqueous                  ammonia or comprises a main absorption component,                  an auxiliary absorption component, an activation                  component, a corrosion inhibitor, an antioxidant,                  and water. The composite aqueous solution, solute                  of the non-aqueous decarbonization solution is                  N-ethylethanolamine and the solvent is                  N,N-diethylethanolamine. The water recovery unit                  recovers the water and waste heat in the flue gas                  by using a dual-tube dual-path water heat                  recovery device. The waste heat is used for heat                  pump power generation or steam turbine power                  generation, and the water is transferred to a                  water tank for electrolysis of hydrogen or                  process water. The dual-tube dual-circuit                  hydrothermal recovery device has multiple                  applications in full flue gas recovery system and                  is applied to a flue gas source, a combined                  defoaming and dust removal unit, a capture                  subsystem, a conversion subsystem, an application                  subsystem, and an energy source. In the subsystem                  according to the design principle of the                  dual-tube and dual-path water and heat recovery                  device and the actual use of each subsystem and                  unit a variety of dual-tube and dual-path water                  and heat recovery devices are manufactured, where                  respectively applied to the full flue gas                  resource system. In each subsystem and unit the                  dual-tube dual-path hydrothermal recovery device                  includes condensate recovery, high-temperature                  heat or waste heat recovery, and waste water slag                  treatment. The condensate water is transferred                  and stored in a water tank, where the                  high-temperature heat or residual heat is used.                  The turbine power generation or heat pump power                  generation can also be used for heat supply or                  external heat supply in a flue gas full resource                  system. The dual-tube dual-circuit water and heat                  recovery device uses double heat pipe technology                  heat or waste heat in the flue gas. The dual                  refrigerant recovery techniques gaseous water in                  the flue gas, waste heat recovery and water                  reclamation integrated design and high water heat                  recovery efficiency to facilitate the procedure                  for utilization of heat and water. The hydrogen                  production unit adopts an electrolysis method to                  utilize the water captured by the water recovery                  unit to obtain hydrogen and oxygen. The water gas                  unit uses the oxygen produced by electrolysis of                  the hydrogen in the hydrogen production unit to                  help combust water gas to generate electricity                  and the electricity from the water gas unit used                  in a full flue gas recovery system. The water gas                  unit produces high-purity hydrogen gas, which is                  stored in the hydrogen tank together with the                  hydrogen produced in the hydrogen production unit                  for the non-water gas power plant. The integrated                  water gas device is adopted in the present                  invention acts as a combined supporting equipment                  for the energy, hydrogen, and oxygen of the                  full-quantity resource system. The conversion                  subsystem utilizes the hydrogen produced by the                  hydrogen production unit and the water gas unit,                  and under the catalytic action of the                  copper-based nanocatalyst, where the catalyst is                  heterogeneously chemically reacted with hydrogen,                  carbon dioxide and solid catalyst; and the fixed                  bed is adopted. The reactor serves as a catalytic                  reactor and a microplate reactor as a synthesis                  reactor, and is arranged in a double-loop nested                  configuration before and after the carbon dioxide                  is selectively hydrogenated through a                  cyclocarbonate intermediate to simultaneously                  prepare methanol and glycol. The conversion                  subsystem comprises conversion technology scheme,                  conversion reactor configuration, heterogeneous                  catalytic system, system composition and ratio,                  reaction conditions and preparation of                  copper-based active nanocatalysts. The energy                  subsystem is used to comprehensively regulate and                  configure the power consumption and heat of each                  subsystem. The purpose of distribution and                  recovery is to meet the demand for heat                  consumption of each subsystem by completely                  utilizing clean energy power generation, hydrogen                  power generation, waste heat recovery and power                  generation, and finally achieve self-sufficiency                  of the entire full-scale resource system. There                  is a solar power generation device, a wind power                  generation device, and hydrogen gas produced by                  electrolyzing water to store energy. When solar                  power generation and wind power generation are                  rich, the excess of energy is stored in the form                  of electrolytic hydrogen. When the system needs                  additional power the gas is used to generate                  electricity. The machine burns hydrogen gas to                  generate electricity to supply power to the                  system. This system makes full use of the waste                  heat of each subsystem, which uses heat pump and                  waste heat recovery technology to make full use                  of thermal energy, which stores excess heat in                  the form of supercritical carbon dioxide and                  releases through supercritical carbon dioxide                  when required where the energy is reused. The                  energy subsystem also includes electricity,                  high-purity oxygen produced during the water                  decomposing process which uses the electrolyzed                  oxygen for the coal-fired power generation of the                  water gas power plant and also obtains                  high-purity hydrogen gas after combustion of the                  water gas power plant, which is stored in                  hydrogen or used in the conversion subsystem,                  which makes the entire system run in a                  comprehensive cycle and comprehensive resource                  model.

PN DE102020215686-A1; WO2022122473-A1
TI Device for separating carbon dioxide from gas mixture using regenerative carbon dioxide absorbers on board submarine, has inlet that is arranged above solid configured for absorbing carbon dioxide present in particulate form and is configured to generate radial flow of gas mixture above solid.
AU BUECHNER R
   KUHLENCORD M
AE THYSSEN KRUPP AG (THYS-C)
   THYSSENKRUPP MARINE SYSTEMS GMBH (THYS-C)
GA 202281105R
AB    NOVELTY - The device (10) has a solid (20) that is configured for absorbing carbon dioxide. A heat exchange device (30) for the solid and housing (40) is provided. The housing is evacuatable. An inlet (50) is provided in the housing for the gas mixture. The inlet is arranged above the solid. The solid configured for absorbing carbon dioxide is present in particulate form. The inlet is configured to generate a radial flow of the gas mixture above the solid.
   USE - Device for separating carbon dioxide from gas mixture using regenerative carbon dioxide absorbers on board submarine.
   ADVANTAGE - A device is suitable for long-term stable use and reliably absorbs carbon dioxide and easily allows the necessary maintenance steps in a confined space. The heat transfer fluid circulates between two devices for separating carbon dioxide until the temperature is equalized to save energy before switching a device for separating carbon dioxide from absorption to desorption operation.

PN KR2021065398-A; KR2331408-B1
TI Device for removing and regenerating carbon dioxide in air, has oil pipe is installed on side of collecting unit, and radiating device is configured to radiate microwaves to thermally decompose carbon dioxide adsorbed on adsorbent.
AU LEE J W
   KIM Y
   LEE J H
AE KOREA AEROSPACE RES INST (KARI-C)
GA 202162845U
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1000) has a collecting unit (100) which is filled with an adsorbent to which carbon dioxide is adsorbed, and is configured to collect carbon dioxide from the air passing therethrough. A first oil pipe (400) is installed on one side of the collecting unit. A second oil pipe (500) is installed on the other side of the collecting unit. A radiating device (200) is configured to radiate microwaves to thermally decompose the carbon dioxide adsorbed on the adsorbent.
   USE - Device for removing and regenerating carbon dioxide in air through reception of air and collection of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is efficiently removed from the air in outer space. The carbon dioxide is pyrolyzed and discharged from the adsorbent, by heating the adsorbent with microwaves that increase the heating cross-sectional area, which significantly increases the regeneration efficiency of the adsorbent in extreme spaces where the number of times the adsorbent is regenerated is limited.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of removing carbon dioxide from air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the device for removing and regenerating carbon dioxide in air.
   Collecting unit (100)
   Radiating device (200)
   Pressure control unit (300)
   Oil pipes (400,500)

TI Method for deciding life of e.g. infant, in vehicle i.e. car, using carbon dioxide concentration, involves determining operation of vehicle, and delivering signal to designated terminal when life present in interior part is determined.
AU HYEONG J S
AE WISECONN CO LTD (WISE-Non-standard)
GA 201925865S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining an operation of a vehicle. Temperature of an interior part of the vehicle is measured. Carbon dioxide concentration of the vehicle interior part is measured. Predetermined carbon dioxide concentration is compared with the measured carbon dioxide concentration for producing rate of change of the carbon dioxide concentration. Life presence of the vehicle interior part is determined through the comparison of the rate of change of the predetermined carbon dioxide concentration and rate of change of the calculated carbon dioxide concentration. Signal is delivered to a designated terminal when life present in the vehicle interior part is determined.
   USE - Method for deciding life of an infant or pet in a vehicle i.e. car, using carbon dioxide concentration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables measuring the carbon dioxide concentration so as to prevent accident due to sleepiness induction to an operator as a notice and quickly determine life determined with excess carbon dioxide concentration through a carbon dioxide density variation, thus protecting life of an infant in the vehicle interior part or a pet.

TI Composite amine-absorbing solution used in apparatus for removing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide from gas, is obtained by dissolving linear monoamine, diamine and amide group-containing compound in water.
AU TANAKA H
   HIRATA T
   KAMIJO T
   TSUJIUCHI T
AE MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND CO LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI JUKOGYO KK (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
   MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND ENG LTD (MITO-C)
GA 201861403S
AB    NOVELTY - A composite amine-absorbing solution is obtained by dissolving a linear monoamine, a diamine and an amide group-containing compound in water.
   USE - Composite amine-absorbing solution is used in apparatus for removing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide from gas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite amine-absorbing solution has excellent synergistic effect, absorption effect, dissipation properties of the absorbed carbon dioxide or hydrogen sulfide when reusing the absorbing solution. The amount of water vapor from reboiler used when reusing the absorbing solution in carbon dioxide recovery device can be reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide removal apparatus, which comprises an absorption tower for removing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide by contacting gas containing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide with the composite amine-absorbing solution, and an absorption liquid regeneration tower for regenerating a solution absorbing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide. The solution regenerated by removing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide in the absorption liquid regeneration tower is reused in the absorption tower; and
   (2) removal of carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide, which involves contacting gas containing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide with the composite amine-absorbing solution, regenerating solution absorbing carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide, and recycling the solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The linear monoamine is primary linear monoamine, secondary linear monoamine and tertiary linear monoamine. The diamine is chosen from primary linear diamine, secondary linear diamine and cyclic diamine. The composite amine-absorbing solution contains 40-60 wt.% linear monoamine and diamine, in total. The ratio of total of diamine and amide group-containing compound with respect to linear monoamine is 0.16-2.1. The ratio of diamine and amide group-containing compound is 1:0.4-1:54. Preferred Conditions: When the absorption temperature of the absorption tower is 30-80 degrees C, the regeneration temperature of the absorption liquid regeneration tower is 110 degrees C or more.

PN CN110025977-A
TI Extracting Panax notoginseng with supercritical carbon dioxide involves preparation of Panax notoginseng and extracting agent, which is ethanol, followed by presoaking, and extraction control of Panax notoginseng.
AU MA F
   MING M
AE HAIAN HUADA PETROLEUM INSTR CO LTD (HAIA-Non-standard)
GA 201965184K
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting Panax notoginseng with supercritical carbon dioxide involves: (S1) preparation of P.notoginseng and an extracting agent by crushing P.notoginseng with a water content of less than 5% to 50-100 mesh, and taking 60-70% ethanol as an extracting agent, followed by adding to an extraction kettle and an ethanol storage tank, respectively, where mass ratio of the P.notoginseng to ethanol is 1:5-1:7; (S2) presoaking by soaking P.notoginseng in ethanol at room temperature for 10-15 hours; and (S3) extraction control of P.notoginseng by placing the presoaked P.notoginseng in an extraction vessel, heating at 55-65 degrees C and heating the separation vessel to 45-55 degrees C, opening a carbon dioxide storage tank, pressurizing the extraction vessel, when the pressure of the vessel reaches 25-32 MPa, adjusting the carbon dioxide flow rate to 12-24 l/hour, maintaining temperature and pressure, and extracting for 2.5-3 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting P.notoginseng with supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The extraction method is continuous, safe, environmentally-friendly and convenient to operate, improves the yield of P.notoginseng, and reduces the use of extracting agent and the extraction cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for extracting P.notoginseng by supercritical carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The step (S3) involves opening carbon dioxide storage tank, pressurizing the extraction kettle, when the pressure of the extraction vessel reaches 30 MPa, adjusting the carbon dioxide flow rate to 18 l/hour, maintaining the temperature and pressure were, and performing extraction 3 times, where the extraction time of each time is 1 hour. Preferred Conditions: In the step (S3), the extraction vessel is heated at 60 degrees C by the heating device, and the separation vessel is heated at 50 degrees C. Preferred Components: In the step (S1), the mass ratio of the crushed P.notoginseng to ethanol is 1:7.

PN CN108586422-A
TI Producing propylene carbonate involves adding carbon dioxide and propylene oxide gas into reaction vessel at normal temperature, where reaction vessel is filled with N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone and water.
AU ZHENG P
   JIANG W
   BI P
   SHI X
   ZHENG W
   WEI X
   LIU W
   LI T
   AN X
AE SHANDONG FEIYANG CHEM CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018795427
AB    NOVELTY - Producing propylene carbonate involves adding carbon dioxide and propylene oxide gas into a 1 liter reaction vessel in a volume ratio of 1.32:1 at a normal temperature, where the reaction vessel is filled with 500ml N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone and water. When the pressure reaches 9-14kg/cm2, then addition of carbon dioxide and propylene oxide is stopped and the weight loss of the two gas cylinders is measured separately. The hot oil in the jacket is heated until the temperature of the reactor reaches 180 degrees C. The heating is stopped after 1 hour. The gas phase outlet of the top of the reactor is connected to the plate separation column. The top of the column collects carbon dioxide and propylene oxide gas. The tower receives N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone. The tower receives propylene carbonate. The overhead recovery gas is sent to the reaction vessel through a booster fan.
   USE - Method for producing propylene carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method significantly reduces equipment corrosion rate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic overall structure of the method for producing propylene carbonate.
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (1)
   Propylene oxide storage tank (2)
   Carbon dioxide flow meter (3)
   Propylene oxide flow meter (4)
   Centrifugal booster fan (5)
   Reaction kettle (6)
   Separation tower (7)

PN WO2019006096-A1; US2020121875-A1; EP3645091-A1; EP3645091-A4; US2022355062-A1
TI Composition for placing within the mouth of            mammal, useful for treating mammal suffering from            hypocapnia, comprises acid and carbonate.
AU STEPANEK J
   CEVETTE M J
   PRADHAN G N
   KLEINDIENST S J
   BOGLE J M
   BLUE R S
   BREZNAK K K
AE MAYO FOUND MEDICAL EDUCATION & RES (MAYO-C)
   STEPANEK J (STEP-Individual)
   CEVETTE M J (CEVE-Individual)
   PRADHAN G N (PRAD-Individual)
   KLEINDIENST S J (KLEI-Individual)
   BOGLE J M (BOGL-Individual)
   BLUE R S (BLUE-Individual)
   BREZNAK K K (BREZ-Individual)
   MAYO FOUND MEDICAL EDUCATION & RES (MAYO-C)
   MAYO FOUND MEDICAL EDUCATION & RES (MAYO-C)
GA 201902056B
AB    NOVELTY - Composition for placing within the mouth of a                mammal, preferably at least 90% of the composition                comprises an acid and a carbonate, where the                composition releases carbon dioxide for the mammal                to inhale once the composition is placed within the                mouth of the mammal or within a cup with liquid                from which resulting carbon dioxide gas is                inhaled.
   USE - The composition is useful for treating a                mammal suffering from hypocapnia, where the mammal                is a human (claimed). No biological data                given.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: reduces level of carbon                dioxide and rapidly resolve the symptoms of                hypocapnia; allows a mammal to resume normal                activities without needing additional medical care;                and increases cerebral perfusion, enhances tissue                oxygen delivery, increases cerebral tissue oxygen                reserve time, enhances cognitive performance,                enables dislodging of carbon monoxide from                hemoglobin molecules, and/or mitigates deleterious                hypocapnia.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a capsule device for delivering carbon                dioxide to a mammal, comprising a liquid                compartment for housing a liquid, a solid                compartment for housing a solid, the liquid and the                solid, an actuator configured to allow the liquid                to contact the solid upon actuation, and a                mouthpiece portion, where the contact of the liquid                with the solid releases carbon dioxide for the                mammal to inhale through the mouthpiece;
   (2) a mask device for delivering carbon                dioxide to a mammal, comprising the liquid                compartment for housing a liquid, the solid                compartment for housing a solid, the liquid and the                solid, the actuator configured to allow the liquid                to contact the solid upon actuation, and a strap to                hold the mask device to the face of the mammal,                where the contact of the liquid with the solid                releases carbon dioxide for the mammal to inhale                through the mask;
   (3) identifying (M1) a symptom attributable to                hypocapnia within a mammal, comprising (a) reducing                the level of carbon dioxide within the mammal below                a baseline carbon dioxide level within the mammal                to create a state of reduced carbon dioxide within                the mammal, (b) determining the presence of a                symptom during the state of reduced carbon dioxide                within the mammal, (c) increasing the level of                carbon dioxide within the mammal to the baseline                carbon dioxide level, and (d) determining that                resolution of the symptom upon increasing the level                of carbon dioxide within the mammal to the baseline                carbon dioxide level, thus identifying the symptom                as being the symptom attributable to                hypocapnia;
   (4) a canister device for delivering breathing                gas to a mammal, or delivering breathing gas of                oxygen and carbon dioxide to a mammal, comprising                an oxygen module, a carbon dioxide module, and a                mouthpiece, where the carbon dioxide module                comprises a liquid compartment for housing a                liquid, a solid compartment for housing a solid,                the liquid and the solid, and a carbon dioxide                actuator configured to allow the liquid to contact                the solid upon actuation, where the contact of the                liquid with the solid releases carbon dioxide, the                mouthpiece is in fluid communication with a carbon                dioxide line connected to the carbon dioxide module                and an oxygen line connected to the oxygen module,                and the oxygen module comprises an oxygen gas                source and an oxygen actuator configured to trigger                release of oxygen gas from the oxygen module upon                actuation for the mammal to inhale through the                mouthpiece;
   (5) an oxygen system comprising a component                for supplying or generating oxygen and a capsule                for generating carbon dioxide; and
   (6) treating (M1) a mammal suffering from                hypocapnia, comprising delivering carbon dioxide to                the mammal using the composition, the capsule                device, the mask device, the canister that contains                compressed carbon dioxide gas source, or the oxygen                system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The oxygen gas source                  is pressurized oxygen, molecular sieve derived                  oxygen, or liquid oxygen. The component is a                  compressed oxygen tank, a chemical gas generation                  component, or a molecular sieve system.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: In the composition,                  at least 95% of the composition comprises the                  acid and the carbonate Preferred Components: The                  liquid is the acid. The solid is a carbonate. The                  acid is ascorbic acid. The carbonate is sodium                  bicarbonate. Preferred Method: The step (a)                  comprises reducing the level of carbon dioxide                  within the mammal of 10-20 mmHg as compared to                  the baseline carbon dioxide level.
   ACTIVITY - Metabolic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

The high purity carbon dioxide is realized to zero emission of greenhouse gas. Steam is generated by a steam turbine. High temperature low pressure gas is generated by a waste heat boiler. High temperature and high pressure steam are generated by the waste heat boiler that is introduced by the steam turbine to realize combine power generation of a gas turbine and the steam turbine. Cooled carbon dioxide gas is entered into a liquid carbon dioxide storage tank by a two-stage compressor.

AB    NOVELTY - Integrated process for manufacturing                polyethyleneimine compounds comprising                polyethyleneamines and hydroxyethylethyleneamines                comprises: (a) in adduction step, providing carbon                dioxide adduct of starting compound comprising e.g.                (-NH-CH2-CH2-NH-) moiety (I); (b) in reaction step,                reacting hydroxy-functional compound comprising                ethanolamines and dihydroxyethane with                amine-functional compound to form carbon dioxide                adduct of a product polyethyleneamine compound; (c)                in an elimination step, converting carbon dioxide                adduct of product polyethyleneamine compound to the                corresponding product polyethylene amine compound,                where a fraction comprising a recycle compound                comprising (I) or (II), or (III), or its carbon                dioxide adducts, is provided from the end of the                reaction step or the elimination step to the                adduction step or to the reaction step, the recycle                compound having per molecule on average fewer of                the total of (I) and (II) moieties than the product                polyethyleneamine compound.
   USE - The process is useful for manufacturing                polyethyleneimine compounds comprising                polyethyleneamines and hydroxyethylethyleneamines                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process provides polyethyleneimine in an                effective and industrially attractive manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Integrated process for manufacturing                polyethyleneimine compounds comprising                polyethyleneamines and hydroxyethylethyleneamines                comprises: (a) in an adduction step, providing a                carbon dioxide adduct of a starting compound                comprising a (-NH-CH2-CH2-NH-) moiety (I) or a                (-NH-CH2-CH2-OH) moiety (II) or ethylene glycol                (III); (b) in a reaction step, reacting a                hydroxy-functional compound comprising                ethanolamines and dihydroxyethane with an                amine-functional compound, where at least part of                the total of hydroxy-functional compounds and                amine-functional compounds is provided in the form                of a carbon dioxide adduct, to form carbon dioxide                adduct of a product polyethyleneamine compound; (c)                in an elimination step, converting carbon dioxide                adduct of product polyethyleneamine compound to the                corresponding product polyethylene amine compound,                where a fraction comprising a recycle compound                comprising (I) or (II), or (III), or its carbon                dioxide adducts, is provided from the end of the                reaction step or the elimination step to the                adduction step or to the reaction step, the recycle                compound having per molecule on average fewer of                the total of (I) and (II) moieties than the product                polyethyleneamine compound.

PN CN110438701-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing machine comprises dyeing machine, carbon dioxide storage tank, preheater, dye box, separator, condenser and cooling system, where carbon dioxide storage tank is connected to preheater through first pipe.
AU HUANG X
AE JIANGSU GAOKE PHARM EQUIP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201998411G
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing machine comprises dyeing machine (1), carbon dioxide storage tank (2), preheater (3), dye box (4), separator (5), condenser (6) and cooling system (7), where carbon dioxide storage tank is connected to preheater through first pipe. The preheater is connected to dyeing machine through second pipe. The condenser is connected to carbon dioxide storage tank through seventh pipe, and cooling system is connected to three pipes which are connected to dyeing machine.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing machine has low cost, improves dyeing efficiency and dyeing quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for dyeing supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing machine with preheat separator, which involves:
   (A) preparing printing and dyeing materials, adding appropriate amount of additives to pretreatment device, mixing additives with water, placing printing and dyeing materials for 8-12 minutes;
   (B) placing pretreated printed matter into dyeing machine, injecting dyeing machine with disperse dye through dye box, mass of the disperse dye is 2-3% of the printed matter, starting booster pump, booster pump boosts carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide storage tank to critical pressure and sending it to preheater, heating carbon dioxide to critical temperature and sent to dyeing machine, controlling temperature in dyeing machine at 35-50 degrees C, pressure is controlling at 10-22 megapascal, flow rate of supercritical fluid is controlling to 2-10L/h, dyeing time is controlling with 36-42 minutes, after dyeing is completed, dyeing machine is close, carbon dioxide in dyeing machine is discharge, after carbon dioxide is processing by separator, cooling gaseous carbon dioxide through condenser, then returning it to carbon dioxide storage tank for collection, after dyeing machine reaches normal pressure, starting cooling system, dyeing machine reaches normal pressure and normal temperature and pressure to taking out printed matter, taking out printed matter after dyeing, preparing clean water, washing it repeatedly 3-5 times, 5-9 minutes each time, after each cleaning of printed matter, removing printed matter to dewater, so that printed matter is dehydrating rate reaches more than 80%;
   (C) placing washed printing and dyeing materials on drying device, controlling temperature of drying device to 38-46 degrees C, drying time is 2-3 hours; and
   (D) leaving to cool naturally at normal temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing machine.
   Dyeing machine (1)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (2)
   Preheater (3)
   Dye box (4)
   Separator (5)
   Condenser (6)
   Cooling system (7)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is penetrant, NIBAOLONG, anhydrous sodium sulfate, auxiliary agent and surfactant.

GA 201848141Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbonated drink, comprises (a) preparing red pepper extract, (b) concentrating the red pepper extract at reduced pressure, (c) injecting carbon dioxide into the red pepper concentrate, and (d) packaging and sterilizing the red pepper concentrate injected with the carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for producing carbonated drink.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbonated drink has optimal preference degree.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises (a1) subdividing the red pepper into one half , and (a2) hydrolyzing the red pepper at 80-100 degrees C for 2-4 hours. The step (b) is carried out until red pepper concentrate reaches sugar content of 10-30 degrees Bx. The amount of carbon dioxide gas used in step (c) is 8 g/750 ml. Preferred Components: The red pepper concentrate of the step (b) further comprises liquid fructose, vitamin C, sodium citrate and/or citric acid, and cider flavor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN JP2022076331-A
TI Carbon dioxide reduction device for artificial photosynthesis apparatus comprises anode which oxidizes water or hydroxide ions to produce oxygen, anode solution, and cathode which reduces carbon dioxide and generates carbon dioxide reduction product.
AU SEKIZAWA K
   SATO S
   MORI T
   MORIKAWA K
AE TOYOTA CENT R & D LABS INC (TOYW-C)
GA 202269418Y
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide reduction device (1) comprises an anode (16) which oxidizes water or hydroxide ions to produce oxygen, an anode portion (10) having an anode solution flow path (18) for supplying an anode solution to the anode, and a cathode (22) which reduces carbon dioxide to produce a carbon dioxide reduction product, a cathode portion (12) having a gas flow path for supplying carbon dioxide gas to the cathode, an ion conductive polymer film sandwiched between the anode portion and the cathode portion, where the cathode is an ion conductive polymer (14), and a catalyst layer and a gas diffusion layer provided from a film side. The catalyst layer is a metal complex catalyst having a central metal and a diimine ligand into which an electron-attracting substituent is introduced, and a conductive carbon.
   USE - Carbon dioxide reduction device used for artificial photosynthesis apparatus (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide reducing device generates large reaction current density at low cell potential, and produces carbon dioxide reduction product with high selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for artificial photosynthesis apparatus, which comprises the carbon dioxide reduction device, and a solar cell for generating electric power supplied to the anode and the cathode.

PN CN112357923-A
TI Method for producing high-purity liquid carbon dioxide, involves providing liquid carbon dioxide from stripping process to enter shell side of subcooler from bottom, exchanging heat with low-pressure ammonia in tube side.
AU YANG Y
   YAO F
   DONG J
AE YANG Y (YANG-Individual)
GA 2021182799
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves passing the gas through the adsorption tower (4), deoil tower (5), desulfurization tower (6), drying tower (7) and filter (8) in sequence. The gas is purified by the adsorption process. The carbon dioxide from the adsorption process enters the plate-fin carbon dioxide condenser (9). The liquid carbon dioxide from the liquefaction process enters the high-pressure stripping tower (12) from the middle and upper portion. The high-boiling components are re-liquefied into a liquid state and settled to the bottom of the high-pressure stripping tower by gravity. The liquid carbon dioxide from the stripping process in the filling process enters the shell side of the subcooler (19) from the bottom, exchanges heat with the low-pressure ammonia in the tube side to reduce the temperature of the liquid carbon dioxide to below -25 degrees C, and then enters the carbon dioxide storage tank (20).
   USE - Method for producing high-purity liquid carbon dioxide applied to various raw material gases of different component.
   ADVANTAGE - The production method has the characteristics of significant energy-saving and consumption-reducing effects and obvious power-saving effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device used in the production method for producing high-purity liquid carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a device used in the production method for producing high-purity liquid carbon dioxide.
   Adsorption tower (4)
   Deoil tower (5)
   Desulfurization tower (6)
   Drying tower (7)
   Filter (8)
   Plate-fin carbon dioxide condenser (9)
   High-pressure stripping tower (12)
   Subcooler (19)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (20)

TI Carbon dioxide sequestering involves combining            water and foaming agent to form foaming agent mixture,            and then adding gas comprising carbon dioxide to            foaming agent mixture in mixing chamber to form foam            mixture in mixing chamber.
AU DIGGINS B J
   DIGGINS C M
   REID B R
AE INNOVATIVE CARBON TECHNOLOGIES PTY LTD (INNO-Non-standard)
   INNOVATIVE CARBON TECHNOLOGIES PTY LTD (INNO-Non-standard)
   INNOVATIVE CARBON TECHNOLOGIES PTY LTD (INNO-Non-standard)
GA 202079955S
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide sequestering involves combining                water and a foaming agent to form a foaming agent                mixture, and then adding a gas comprising carbon                dioxide to the foaming agent mixture in a mixing                chamber to form a foam mixture in the mixing                chamber, where the foam mixture comprises multiple                foam bubbles containing portion of the carbon                dioxide. The foam mixture is combined with concrete                materials in another mixing chamber to contact the                concrete materials, where the concrete materials                comprising cement. The foam mixture and the                concrete materials are mixed in the mixing chamber                to form the concrete having sequestered carbon                dioxide.
   USE - Method for sequestering carbon dioxide in                concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides sequestering of the carbon                dioxide in concrete in a simple, cost-effective and                eco-friendly manner with increased surface                temperatures and rapid climate change.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for sequestering carbon dioxide in concrete, which                comprises a mixing vessel (202) having a mixing                chamber configured to receive water, a gas                comprising carbon dioxide and a foaming agent, and                mix the water, the gas comprising carbon dioxide                and the foaming agent to form a foam mixture, and                another mixing vessel (204) having another mixing                chamber configured to receive concrete materials,                receive the foam mixture from the former mixing                vessel, and mix the concrete materials and the foam                mixture to form concrete having sequestered carbon                dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system for sequestering carbon dioxide in                concrete.
   System (200)
   Mixing vessels (202, 204)
   Mixing chamber (203)
   Water stream (206)

PT P
PN FI128319-B1; WO2020165500-A1; EP3924104-A1; US2022128460-A1
TI Method for detecting carbon dioxide in gaseous sample.
AU GENOUD G
   REINIKAINEN M
AE VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
   VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
   VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
GA 202022308A
AB    NOVELTY - Detection of carbon dioxide in gaseous sample involves flowing the gaseous sample through an anion exchange resin which is capable of selectively adsorbing carbon dioxide present in the gaseous sample, releasing the adsorbed carbon dioxide from the resin by heating the resin to a temperature of 80-250 degrees C to obtain a concentrated gaseous sample, and determining the amount of an isotopic form of carbon dioxide in the concentrated gaseous sample by infrared absorption spectroscopy.
   USE - Detection of carbon dioxide in gaseous sample e.g. atmospheric sample, biofuels, such as biodiesel or biogas, biological sample, such as breath sample, blood sample or plasma sample (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, and selectively traps carbon dioxide for isotopic analysis using laser spectroscopy without trapping unwanted contaminants such as nitrous oxide and reduces the cost and size of optical instruments.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) apparatus, which consists of unit (U1) for concentrating carbon dioxide present in a gaseous sample to obtain a concentrated gaseous sample, and unit (U2) for determining the amount of an isotopic form of CO2 present in the concentrated gaseous sample by infrared absorption spectroscopy. The unit (U1) comprises an anion exchange resin; and
   (2) use of an anion exchange resin, for concentrating carbon dioxide present in a gaseous sample before detecting an isotopic form of the carbon dioxide by infrared absorption spectroscopy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The isotopic form is 12CO2, 13CO2 and/or 14CO2. The isotopic form of carbon dioxide is isotopolog of carbon dioxide comprising isotope(s) of oxygen and isotope of carbon. The gaseous sample originates from a nuclear power plant. Preferred Process: The determining process involves measuring an infrared absorption spectrum of the concentrated gaseous sample by using a cavity down-ring laser spectroscopy. The method further comprises catalytically oxidizing 14CH4 to 14CO2 by a catalyst before the determination process. The catalytically oxidizing process involves heating palladium catalyst to a temperature of at least 300 degrees C, and contacting the gaseous sample with the heated palladium catalyst, in which the heated palladium catalyst catalyses oxidation of the 14CH4 present in the gaseous sample to 14CO2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The apparatus further comprises upstream of unit (U1), and unit for catalytically oxidizing 14CH4 present in the gaseous sample. The unit (U2) is adapted for determining the combined amount of 14CO2 present in the gaseous sample and 14CO2 converted from 14CH4 present in the gaseous sample by infrared absorption spectroscopy. The unit for catalytically oxidizing 14CH4 present in the gaseous sample comprises a catalyst bed comprising catalyst, preferably palladium catalyst. The unit (U2) comprises a cavity down-ring laser spectrometer comprising a quantum cascade laser as an infrared light source.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anion exchange resin comprises primary, secondary, and/or tertiary amino groups. The gaseous sample further comprises 14CH4.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The anion exchange resin comprises crosslinked polymeric material. Preferred Process: The method involves heating resin to a temperature of 100-200 degrees C, preferably 150-200 degrees C for 1-15 minutes, preferably 10 minutes.

TI New polyamine phosphorus dendrimer (P-dendrimer)            used for composite material for adsorbing, separating,            storing or sequestering removal of carbon dioxide from            gaseous fluid stream.
AU THOMPSON S J
   MUSTAPHA S
   SOUKRI M
AE RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
   RES TRIANGLE INST (RTRI-C)
GA 201964171T
AB    NOVELTY - A polyamine phosphorus dendrimer (P-dendrimer)                (I), is new.
   USE - New polyamine phosphorus dendrimer                (P-dendrimer) used for composite material for                adsorbing, separating, storing or sequestering                removal of carbon dioxide from gaseous fluid stream                (all claimed). Uses include but are not limited to                building, plane, automobile, submarine, and                spacecraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyamine phosphorus dendrimer has                excellent thermal stability, and carbon dioxide                capacity, and exhibit rapid reaction kinetics with                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A polyamine phosphorus dendrimer (P-dendrimer)                of formula (I), is new.
   W' = phosphorus-based dendrimer core;
   X = polyfunctional aryl linker;
   Y' = absent or polyfunctional amino phosphoryl                linked to polyfunctional aryl linker;
   Z = diaminoalkyl, polyalkylamino,                polyethyleneimino, or polypropyleneimino;
   l,m = 1-10;and
   n = 2-10.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) composite material, which comprises solid                support, and a surface coated with polyamine                P-dendrimer;
   (2) removal of carbon dioxide from gaseous                fluid stream, which involves contacting gaseous                fluid stream with polyamine P-dendrimer;
   (3) method of adsorbing, separating, storing                or sequestering carbon dioxide, which involves                contacting polyamine P-dendrimer with gaseous fluid                stream comprising carbon dioxide;
   (4) capturing and removal of carbon dioxide                from carbon dioxide-containing stream, which                involves providing hosing having dispersed in                sorbent comprising polyamine P-dendrimer, passing                carbon dioxide-containing stream through the                housing such that carbon dioxide-containing stream                contacts the sorbent, flushing the housing with                steam to cause the sorbent to desorb an carbon                dioxide retained as result of contact of sorbent                with carbon-dioxide-containing steam, and flushing                the housing with air to remove the steam from                housing; and
   (5) preparation of polyamine phosphorus                dendrimer (P-dendrimer).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gaseous fluid                  stream is flue gas, post-combustion stream, and                  ambient air. The flue gas comprises flue gas from                  natural gas power plant, coal-fired power plant,                  cement plant, or other industrial process.                  Preferred Composition: The flue gas stream                  comprises 3-45 mol%, preferably 1000-4000 parts                  per million carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preparation (claimed): The preparation of                  polyamine phosphorus dendrimer involves reacting                  a phosphoryl chloride starting material for                  phosphorus-based dendrimer core with nucleophilic                  aldehyde starting material for polyfunctional                  aromatic linker to form polyphosphoryl ester                  aldehyde compound, condensing the polyphosphoryl                  ester aldehyde compound with an polyalkylamine to                  form polyalkylimine polyphosphoryl ester, and                  reducing the polyalkylimine polyphosphoryl ester                  to form polyamine phosphorus dentrimer. Preferred                  Composition: The polyamine phosphorus dendrimer                  comprises 2 wt.%, preferably 10 wt.% carbon                  dioxide capacity. Preferred condition: The                  polyamine phosphorus dendrimer method is                  performed at 20-90 degrees C. Preferred Process:                  The method further involves regenerating the                  polyamine P-dendrimer with heating P-dendrimer at                  50-150 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The solid support                  comprises polymeric material, carbon, glass or                  metal support. The polymeric material is natural                  or synthetic material. The natural polymer                  comprises cellulose acetate, silk protein,                  soybean protein or starch. The synthetic polymer                  comprises petroleum pitch, polyacrylonitrile,                  polyamide, polyethylene, polypropylene,                  polystyrene, and/or polyvinyl chloride.
   Preferred Definitions:
   W' = phosphazenyl, thiophosphoryl, or                cyclophosphazatetraenyl;
   X = group of formula (II) or (III);
   Nu = -OH, -SH, or -NH2;
   R2 = H, Me, Et, or halo;
   Y' = group of formula (IV), or (V);
   Z = group of formula (VI), or (VII);
   Ar' = not defined;and
   p,q = 1-6.
   N.B: Ar' is not defined.
   EXAMPLE - 2 mL Acetone and 4.0 mg Kynar(RTM:                polyvinylidene fluoride resin) was ultrasonically                treated in water bath until the resin was                completely dissolved, 40 mg polyamine P-dendrimer                sorbent was added and ultrasonically treated for 10                minutes for complete dispersion. The obtained                solution was applied by 12.4 mg carbon rectangular                sheet portion. The obtained solution was dried for                1 hour to ensure complete dryness, and excellent                thermal stability.

TI Medical treatment used in e.g. wound care comprises e.g. selectively securing compressed gas cylinder containing compressed carbon dioxide to compressed carbon dioxide unit, and providing a reservoir with a medical therapy solution.
AU LEVY F
AE LEVY F (LEVY-Individual)
   LEVY F (LEVY-Individual)
GA 2018832261
AB    NOVELTY - Medical treatment comprises: (a) providing a portable compressed carbon dioxide unit (1) adapted for selective attachment of a compressed gas cylinder (27) to it; (b) selectively securing the compressed gas cylinder containing compressed carbon dioxide to the compressed carbon dioxide unit; (c) providing a reservoir with a medical therapy solution; (d) communicably interconnecting the reservoir with the compressed carbon dioxide unit; (e) initiating the compressed carbon dioxide unit to deliver compressed carbon dioxide from the compressed gas cylinder and draw the medical therapy solution from the reservoir to generate a carbon dioxide enriched medical therapy solution; and (f) applying the carbon dioxide enriched medical therapy solution for medical treatment.
   USE - The medical treatment is useful for wound care or hair growth stimulation. No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the apparatus, which does not require an impeller and incorporated fan with a foam generator to create and dispense the foam, and is portable, less expensive and compact, hence simple to manufacture and use; and utilizes the carbon dioxide, which is safer and has less complications than air or oxygen, diffuses more naturally in body tissues, and is absorbed in the body more rapidly and with less side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a carbon dioxide enriched medical therapy solution made by the steps (a)-(e) as per se.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a side perspective and partly schematic view of an apparatus.

   Compressed gas cylinder (27)

PT P
PN CN107905213-A
TI Method for reinforcing carbon dioxide acidified calcareous sand foundation, involves capturing and purifying carbon dioxide emissions, followed by mixing water with calcareous sand and introducing high-pressure carbon dioxide.
AU ZHU Q
   ZUO D
   WANG C
   FAN Z
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY XUZHOU (UYMT-C)
   TIANJIN RES INST WATER TRANSPORT ENG (TWTE-C)
GA 201830027C
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide acidified calcareous sand foundation reinforcing method involves capturing and purifying carbon dioxide emissions from fossil fuel combustion devices, mixing water with calcareous sand, introducing high-pressure carbon dioxide and allowing carbon dioxide, water and calcium sand react to produce calcium bicarbonate solution. The calcium bicarbonate solution is injected, decomposed, deposited the crystals and cemented calcareous sand particles to achieve reinforcement. The solution is recovered, filtered, and injected back into the high-pressure vessel and recycled the water.
   USE - Method for reinforcing carbon dioxide acidified calcareous sand foundation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reinforcing the carbon dioxide acidified calcareous sand foundation with high bearing performance, low transport cost, and wide application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide acidified calcareous sand foundation reinforcing method involves capturing and purifying carbon dioxide emissions from fossil fuel combustion devices (such as coal-fired boilers, gas turbines, etc.), mixing water with calcareous sand in a high-pressure vessel, introducing high-pressure carbon dioxide and allowing carbon dioxide, water and calcium sand react to produce calcium bicarbonate solution. An injection well and a discharge well are set in the area to be reinforced, injected the calcium bicarbonate solution through the injection well to maintain high pressure conditions, decomposed the calcium bicarbonate solution into calcium carbonate ground to generate calcium carbonate, deposited the crystals in the area to be reinforced and cemented the calcareous sand particles to achieve reinforcement. The solution is recovered through the extraction well after flowing through the consolidation zone, filtered, and injected back into the high-pressure vessel and recycled the water.

MC Q42-A10
IP E02D-003/12

AB    NOVELTY - The carbon dioxide concentration measurement system (1) comprises a sensor unit (2) that contains an human detection sensor (21), and the carbon dioxide sensor. A communication unit (3) communicates with the ventilation system (10), which performs an indoor ventilation. A control unit (4) controls operation of a sensor unit, and the communication unit. The battery (5) supplies electric power to the sensor unit, the communication unit, and the control unit. The communication unit is made to output the carbon dioxide concentration to the ventilation system, when the carbon dioxide sensor works by the first measurement frequency.
   USE - Carbon dioxide concentration measurement system for a ventilation system system business.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide concentration measurement system carries out reduction in power consumption, and makes possible to reduce the frequency of the battery exchange operation.

PN CN109583788-A
TI Method for calculating carbon dioxide discharge amount of coal-fired thermal power unit, involves calculating carbon dioxide emission of power generating unit, and obtaining Chinese enterprise greenhouse gas emission accounting mode.
AU WANG X
   ZHOU B
   REN J
   LIU Y
AE HUADIAN ELECTRIC POWER RES INST (HUAD-C)
GA 201933282E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting parameters of a coal burning thermal power generating unit. Carbon dioxide emission is obtained according to a type of the parameters. Carbon dioxide emission of the coal burning thermal power generating unit is calculated according to history data and unit discharge coefficient of the coal burning thermal power generating unit. The carbon dioxide emission of the coal burning thermal power generating unit is compared with the obtained carbon dioxide emission to obtain a carbon dioxide emission result. A Chinese enterprise greenhouse gas emission accounting mode and a report guide are obtained according to the type of the parameters.
   USE - Method for calculating carbon dioxide discharge amount of a coal-fired thermal power unit.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simplifying influence factor of carbon discharge, improving accuracy of accounting carbon emission, and directly obtaining carbon dioxide discharge amount and a corresponding calculation mode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for calculating carbon dioxide discharge amount of a coal-fired thermal power unit.
