PT P
PN CN113303145-A
TI Cultivating Populus euphratica seedling involves adding cultivating soil to cultivating groove on tray, filling cultivating soil with bottom water, and performing data programming on programmable logic controller in operating controller.
AU YUAN X
   LIU S
   LIU C
AE YUAN X (YUAN-Individual)
   LIU S (LIUS-Individual)
   LIU C (LIUC-Individual)
GA 2021A2569M
AB    NOVELTY - Cultivating Populus euphratica seedling involves providing incubator with multiple trays in rotatable manner. The second glass layer is upturned through sucker, cultivating soil is added to cultivating groove on tray, and cultivating soil is filled with bottom water. The water is permeated in cultivating soil, processed Populus euphratica breeding seeds are placed in cultivating groove of multiple trays, and Populus euphratica seed is slightly pressed into cultivating soil, and second glass layer is re-set.
   USE - Method for cultivating Populus euphratica seedling used in poplar seedling cultivating device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to utilize PLC controller for programming, which is safe and accurate, improve maintenance management efficiency of seedling, avoid wasting time and labor, reduce labor intensity of cultivating technical personnel so as to improve breeding and transplanting survival rate of Populus euphratica seedling.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Cultivating Populus euphratica seedling involves providing incubator with multiple trays in rotatable manner. The second glass layer is upturned through sucker, cultivating soil is added to cultivating groove on tray, and cultivating soil is filled with bottom water. The water is permeated in cultivating soil, processed Populus euphratica breeding seeds are placed in cultivating groove of multiple trays, and Populus euphratica seed is slightly pressed into cultivating soil, and second glass layer is re-set. The seeds are supplemented with required amount of water in time, and irrigation water is stored in water storage tank at top of sunshine board ceiling. The first sprayer in water storage tank is used to fill irrigation water into first L-shaped spraying pipe. The multiple trays are driven to rotate by geared motor, spraying head is used to irrigate Populus euphratica seeds on multiple trays respectively, through sunshine plate ceiling on top of incubator, and fixing block and second glass layer are provided on side of incubator to meet light requirements of seeds. A data programming is performed on programmable logic controller (PLC) in operating controller, PLC controller is electrically connected to geared motor and first sprayer through wires, and operating time of device electrically connected is set. The tray is driven to rotate through geared motor, where when any tray rotates to top, first sprayer irrigate Populus euphratica seeds on top tray, humidity sensor and temperature sensor are set to monitor temperature inside incubator, and humidity inside incubator, respectively, and temperature data is transmitted to PLC controller. The PLC controller is electrically connected with centrifugal air exchange fan is set on inner wall of incubator ventilating and extracting hot air in incubator to cool down, or second glass layer is lifted for ventilating and cooling when temperature in incubator is too high. The soil is maintained moist within 7 days after sowing. The PLC controller is programmed again, and irrigated once every other day when irrigation time reaches 7-15 days. The PLC controller is programmed, and water is refilled once every 3-4 days when the irrigation time reaches 16-30 days. The PLC controller is programmed again to adjust irrigation frequency to once a week or ten days to replenish water when Populus euphratica seedlings grow 3-4 true leaves, Populus euphratica seedlings is cultivated for one week or ten days in year, and water is added once for irrigation. A carbon dioxide collector connected to interior of incubator is fixedly set on supporting base plate to collect and emit carbon dioxide for performing photosynthesis after Populus euphratica seedlings grow true leaves. The controller is electrically connected with PLC controller through wire, and PLC controller is programmed to control working state of carbon dioxide collecting and discharging device. The carbon dioxide collecting and discharging device is set in Populus euphratica seedlings. The carbon dioxide collector provides carbon dioxide needed for photosynthesis of Populus euphratica seedlings to increase growth rate. The Populus euphratica seedlings release carbon dioxide at night, which is collected and discharged by carbon dioxide collector for collection. The Populus euphratica seedlings are cultivated to second year, and irrigated in spring of second year. The PLC controller is programmed again to arrange reasonable interval, and irrigated 7-8 times throughout the year. A liquid medicine storage tank and nutrient liquid storage tank are fixedly set on top of sun board ceiling. The liquid medicine storage box is filled with weeding and insecticide, and liquid medicine is sprayed on Populus euphratica seedlings through second sprayer in liquid storage tank after Populus euphratica seedlings is cultivated for a period of time. The nutrient solution for cultivating Populus euphratica seedlings is filled in nutrient solution storage tank before irrigating, nutrient solution for cultivating Populus euphratica seedlings is cultivated through third sprayer in nutrient solution storage tank is sprayed on Populus euphratica seedlings to facilitate growth of Populus euphratica seedlings, cultivated for one year by repeating the process, and seedlings emerges in spring of third year. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a Populus euphratica seedling cultivating device, which comprises supporting component (1), rotating component (2), auxiliary component (3), spraying component (4) and operation control component (5), where supporting component comprises supporting bottom plate, culture box and motor seat, where bottom of supporting bottom plate is fixedly set with universal wheel, culture box is fixedly set on culture box, one side of supporting bottom plate respectively is set with ventilation through hole and first round hole, first round hole is fixedly set with first bearing, first bearing is fixedly inserted with coupler, other side of supporting bottom plate is fixedly installed with first glass layer, one side of first glass layer is fixedly connected with fixing block provided with blind hole, blind hole is fixedly installed with second bearing, and both ends of supporting bottom plate are fixedly mounted with second glass layer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of Populus euphratica seedling cultivating device.
   Supporting component (1)
   Rotating component (2)
   Auxiliary component (3)
   Spraying component (4)
   Operation control component (5)
DC P11 (Soil working, planting (A01B, C).); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); T01 (Digital Computers); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC P11-C03A; P11-T99; P13-A01A; P13-A08; T01-F06; X25-N01

PN US2021130705-A1; WO2021086762-A1
TI Capturing carbon dioxide involves e.g. mixing            hydrogen stream and feedstock stream to form mixed            stream, introducing the mixed stream to hydroprocessing            unit and reacting the hydrocarbons and the hydrogen in            primary reactor of the unit to form hydroprocessing            product and carbon dioxide.
AU KOSEOGLU O R
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202145150Y
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide comprises mixing                hydrogen stream and feedstock stream to form mixed                stream; introducing the mixed stream to                hydroprocessing unit (20); reacting the                hydrocarbons and the hydrogen in the primary                reactor of the unit to form hydroprocessing product                stream (200) and carbon dioxide stream, where the                product stream comprises light products, the carbon                dioxide stream comprises carbon dioxide;                introducing water stream to the unit; collecting                the ammonium bisulfide in the water to produce a                sour water; introducing the sour water to a waste                water unit; processing the sour water in the waste                water unit to produce ammonia stream, hydrogen                sulfide stream and stripped water stream;                introducing the ammonia stream to a carbon dioxide                recovery system; introducing the carbon dioxide                stream to the carbon dioxide recovery system; and                separating carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide                stream using the ammonia in the ammonia stream to                produce a carbon dioxide product.
   USE - The processes are useful for capturing carbon                dioxide and producing a vehicle fuel (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: reduces or eliminates the carbon                dioxide released to the atmosphere; and provides                capture of carbon dioxide produced across multiple                units beyond the limits of the hydroprocessing                unit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Capturing carbon dioxide comprises mixing a                hydrogen stream and a feedstock stream to produce a                mixed stream, where the feedstock stream comprises                hydrocarbons, the hydrogen stream comprises                hydrogen; introducing the mixed stream to a                hydroprocessing unit (20), where the unit                configured to process the hydrocarbons to produce                light products in a primary reactor; reacting the                hydrocarbons and the hydrogen in the primary                reactor of the unit to produce a hydroprocessing                product stream (200) and a carbon dioxide stream,                where the product stream comprises light products,                the carbon dioxide stream comprises carbon dioxide,                the unit is further configured to produce ammonium                bisulfide; introducing a water stream to the unit,                where the water stream comprises water; collecting                the ammonium bisulfide in the water to produce a                sour water; introducing the sour water to a waste                water unit (30); processing the sour water in the                waste water unit to produce an ammonia stream, a                hydrogen sulfide stream, and a stripped water                stream, where the ammonia stream comprises ammonia;                introducing the ammonia stream to a carbon dioxide                recovery system; introducing the carbon dioxide                stream to the carbon dioxide recovery system; and                separating carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide                stream using the ammonia in the ammonia stream to                produce a carbon dioxide product.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (a) producing (P2) a vehicle fuel comprising                mixing the hydrogen stream and the feedstock stream                to produce the mixed stream, introducing the mixed                stream to the hydroprocessing unit to process the                hydrocarbons to light products in a primary                reactor, reacting the hydrocarbons and the hydrogen                in the primary reactor of the hydroprocessing unit                to produce a hydroprocessing product stream and a                carbon dioxide stream, introducing a water stream                to the hydroprocessing unit, where the water stream                comprises water, collecting the ammonium bisulfide                in the water to produce a sour water, introducing                the sour water to a waste water unit and processing                the sour water in the waste water unit to produce                an ammonia stream, a hydrogen sulfide stream, and a                stripped water stream, and the ammonia stream                comprises ammonia, the ammonia stream is operable                to be used as the vehicle fuel; and
   (b) a system for capturing carbon dioxide                comprising a mixer, where the mixer configured to                mix a hydrogen stream and a feedstock stream to                produce a mixed stream, the hydroprocessing unit                fluidly connected to the mixer and the waste water                unit fluidly connected to the hydroprocessing                unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows process flow diagram of the                system.
   Hydrogen production unit (10)
   Hydroprocessing unit (20)
   Waste water unit (30)
   Hydrocarbon feedstock (100)
   Hydroprocessing product stream (200)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (P1) further                  comprises: introducing a hydrocarbon feedstock                  (100) and an oxygen-containing stream to a                  hydrogen production unit (10), where the                  hydrocarbon feedstock comprises hydrocarbons with                  a carbon number of less than 5, where the                  hydrogen production unit is configured to convert                  hydrocarbons with a carbon number of less than 5                  and processing the hydrocarbons with a carbon                  number of less than 5 in the hydrogen production                  unit to produce the hydrogen stream and a                  byproduct stream, where the byproduct stream                  comprises carbon dioxide; and mixing the                  byproduct stream and the carbon dioxide stream to                  produce a combined stream and introducing the                  combined stream to the carbon dioxide recovery                  system.Preferred Components: The feedstock stream                  comprises a whole crude oil, a naphtha stream, a                  distillate stream, a vacuum gas oil stream, a                  vacuum residue stream and/or an intermediate                  refinery stream. The hydroprocessing unit                  comprises hydrotreating unit, a hydrocracking                  unit and/or a residue hydrocracking unit. The                  hydrocarbon feedstock comprises natural gas,                  where the carbon dioxide product is operable for                  use in enhanced oil recovery operations.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further                  comprises: the hydrogen production unit; a carbon                  dioxide recovery system fluidly connected to the                  waste water unit, where the carbon dioxide                  recovery system configured to separate carbon                  dioxide to produce a carbon dioxide                  product.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110624363-A; CN110624363-B
TI Pressurized regeneration of collecting carbon dioxide in flue gas comprises e. g. collecting carbon dioxide capture alcohol amine solution to absorb carbon dioxide in absorption tower to obtain alcohol amine rich liquid.
AU MAO S
   GUO B
   CHEN X
   WANG D
   JI Y
   YE N
   DING Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 2020026487
AB    NOVELTY - Pressurized regeneration of collecting carbon dioxide in flue gas comprises (i) collecting carbon dioxide capture alcohol amine solution to absorb carbon dioxide in the absorption tower to obtain alcohol amine rich liquid; (ii) subjecting the alcohol-amine-rich liquid to heat-exchange method and sending into an atmospheric regeneration tower; (iii) absorbing desorbed part of carbon dioxide in the atmospheric regeneration tower, desorbing the dissolved oxygen in the alcohol amine rich liquid to obtain semi-rich solution; (iv) pressurizing and heat-exchanging the semi-rich solution and sending into a pressurized regeneration tower; and (v) depressurizing the remaining carbon dioxide in the pressurized regeneration tower, heating and circulating the recycled solution to the absorption tower.
   USE - The method is useful for pressurized regeneration of collecting carbon dioxide in flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and ensures high heat rate of regeneration reaction, significantly reduces regeneration energy consumption, and ensures without introducing external high pressure air lift gas and high heat utilization rate and regenerates high carbon dioxide pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: A regeneration heat source is provided at the bottom of the pressurized regeneration tower and the atmospheric regeneration tower. The regenerative heat source is steam with pressure of 0.6 MPa. In the step (iii), the operating pressure of the atmospheric regeneration tower is 0-20 kPaG, the operating temperature of the atmospheric regeneration tower is 80-110 degrees C. In the step (v), the operating pressure of the pressurized regeneration tower is 100-200 kPaG, the operating temperature of the pressurized regeneration tower is 100-150 degrees C. The normal pressure regeneration tower and the pressurized regeneration tower produce carbon dioxide in a ratio of 0.05-0.3: 1. Preferred Components: The solute of the carbon dioxide capture alcohol amine solution includes ethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, diisopropanolamine, N-methyldiethanolamine and/or sterically hindered amine and the mass content of the solute in the solvent is 50-90%. The raw material flue gases are power plant flue gas, chemical plant flue gas, and steel plant flue gas. The carbon dioxide content in the raw material is 5-25%.

PN KR2026419-B1
TI Producing methanol from carbon dioxide-containing            methane gas, comprises e.g. removing hydrogen sulfide            contained in carbon dioxide-containing methane gas, and            controlling amount of carbon dioxide contained in steam            reforming reaction gas.
AU LEE Y J
   KWAK G J
   KANG S
   PARKHAEGU
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 2019869322
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methanol from carbon                dioxide-containing methane gas, comprises (a)                removing hydrogen sulfide contained in carbon                dioxide-containing methane gas, (b) controlling                amount of carbon dioxide contained in steam                reforming reaction gas via plasma by removing part                carbon dioxide so that content of carbon dioxide is                0.2-0.7 relative to methane on molar basis, (c)                producing syngas by steam reforming methane gas of                which the amount of carbon dioxide is controlled by                plasma, and (d) preparing methanol from the syngas                prepared in the step (c). The carbon                dioxide-containing methane gas is gas containing                siloxane as content of carbon dioxide and methane                is 30-40%, respectively based on the basis of the                number of moles of dry gas. In the step (c), the                siloxane is converted into silica particles, and                ammonia and volatile organic compounds are                pyrolyzed.
   USE - The method is useful for producing syngas and                methanol from carbon dioxide and methane containing                landfill gas or biogas using plasma steam reformer                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces methanol by converting                landfill gas or biogas containing large amount of                carbon dioxide through steam/carbon dioxide mixed                methane reforming reaction by plasma without                separate treatment device even when impurities i.e.                siloxanes have significant effect on catalytic                reaction, and is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) plasma steam reformer comprising a plasma                layer and a catalyst layer provided at rear end of                the plasma layer. The mixed gas of methane, carbon                dioxide and water vapor is converted into carbon                monoxide and hydrogen-containing syngas through a                steam reforming reaction by plasma while passing                through the plasma layer to remove unreacted                methane-containing hot gas which is passed through                the plasma layer through the catalyst bed where                additional methane reforming reactions occur. The                reformer is connected to an upstream of a separator                for removing carbon dioxide that controls the                amount of carbon dioxide contained in the steam                reforming reaction gas by plasma, and converts the                siloxane into filterable silica particles by                plasma; and
   (2) converting methane gas containing landfill                gas or carbon dioxide from biogas in the plasma                steam reformer; and
   (3) producing methanol from landfill gas or                biogas, comprising (i) dehumidifying and                desulfurization by pre-treating the landfill gas or                biogas, (ii) compressing the gas pre-treated in                step (i) to remove portion of carbon dioxide                through the separator at 2-15 atm, (iii)                humidifying the gas of step (b) containing methane                and carbon dioxide, and preparing a syngas through                a steam/carbon dioxide mixed methane reforming                reaction by plasma at 0.1-5 atm, (iv) cooling the                syngas prepared in step (iii) to 0-50 degrees C to                remove particles through a filter, separating the                water, reacting syngas by compressing to 40-100 atm                in methanol reactor to prepare methanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (c), a portion                  of carbon dioxide is removed so that molar ratio                  of hydrogen/ (2 moles carbon monoxide and 3 moles                  carbon dioxide) in syngas produced by the steam                  reforming reaction by plasma is 0.8-1.2, before                  reforming the carbon dioxide-containing methane                  gas. In the step (a), Hydrogen sulfide is removal                  is carried out by dry adsorption. In the step                  (c), the mixed reforming reaction of methane is                  carried out at reaction pressure of 0.1-5 bar,                  and molar ratio of methane, carbon dioxide and                  water vapor is 1:0.2-0.7:1-2.5; and methane                  reforming catalyst layer in addition to plasma                  layer downstream of the plasma generating unit to                  passes through methane reforming catalyst layer                  to synthesize the gas by discharging unreacted                  methane-containing gas heated by the plasma. The                  step (c) further comprises removing silica                  particles with particle removal filter or with                  water in the water removal process. In the step                  (b), carbon dioxide removal is performed by a                  separator. Preferred Components: The carbon                  dioxide-containing methane gas is landfill gas or                  biogas. The unreacted methane-containing gas                  heated by the plasma discharge of the plasma                  layer passes further through the methane                  reforming catalyst layer to further synthesize                  the gas. The unreacted methane-containing hot gas                  passed through the plasma layer has temperature                  of 700-900 degrees C. The plasma steam reformer                  utilizes electrical energy derived from renewable                  energy as a heat source for the steam reforming                  reaction by plasma.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN WO2022074293-A1; FI202005987-A
TI Power plant combustion method in which oxidizing            gas is directed to combustion reactor of power plant,            by directing oxygen containing gas to combustion,            optionally together with at least one other gas,            forming oxidizing gas, separating carbon dioxide from            flue gas and capturing carbon dioxide.
AU JUUTILAINEN T
   SILVENNOINEN J
AE CARBONREUSE FINLAND OY (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 202250932B
AB    NOVELTY - Power plant combustion method in which                oxidizing gas is directed to a combustion reactor                of a power plant, the combustion of which oxidizing                gas generates flue gas containing carbon dioxide,                involves directing oxygen containing gas to a                combustion process, optionally together with at                least one other gas, forming the oxidizing gas of                the combustion process, processing at least part of                the flue gas generated in the combustion process in                a membrane filter system to increase the carbon                dioxide concentration of the flue gas, separating                carbon dioxide from the flue gas coming from the                combustion process and the membrane filtration                system using a separation method based essentially                on interaction between water and carbon dioxide                gas, and capturing separated carbon dioxide.
   USE - As power plant combustion method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method eliminates need for production of                pure oxygen for combustion process which generally                saves energy and is cost-effective. The apparatus                is smaller in size which enables utilization of                physical separation process also at sites with                limited space.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for power                plant combustion apparatus, which has an oxidizing                gas production and/or feed unit, a combustion                reactor, a flue gas outlet system, and a carbon                dioxide separation system based essentially on                interaction between water and carbon dioxide gas,                in which carbon dioxide is separated from flue gas                and from which carbon dioxide gas obtained can be                directed to a capture system, the apparatus                comprises a membrane filter system suitable for                increasing the carbon dioxide concentration of the                flue gas, the system being located in the apparatus                after the combustion chamber before the carbon                dioxide separation system based essentially on                interaction between water and carbon dioxide                gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Method: The nitrogen is removed in                  the membrane filter system using a membrane                  filter prior to separation of carbon dioxide from                  flue gas using a separation method based                  essentially on interaction between water and                  carbon dioxide gas. The oxygen containing gas is                  produced from air by directing air through a                  membrane filter system to remove nitrogen,                  whereby the oxygen concentration of the oxygen                  containing gas it at least 21%. The part of the                  carbon dioxide gas is directed back to the                  combustion rector as part of the oxidizing gas to                  dampen the combustion process and to increase                  carbon dioxide concentration of the flue gas. The                  oxygen concentration of the oxidizing gas is at                  least 15% and at the most 40%. The separation                  method based essentially on interaction between                  water and carbon dioxide gas, flue gas is                  directed to an absorption column, in which carbon                  dioxide gas is absorbed into water using                  pressure, after which the water is pumped to at                  least one desorption column, in which the                  pressure is decreased to release pure carbon                  dioxide gas from water and to capture a carbon                  dioxide fraction, after which the water                  preferably is pumped back to the absorption                  column. In the separation method based                  essentially on interaction between water and                  carbon dioxide gas, the pressure is increased                  and/or decreased stepwise, for example by use of                  auxiliary columns. The flue gas coming from the                  combustion process has a carbon dioxide                  concentration of at the most 35% prior to                  membrane filtration and carbon dioxide                  separation.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PN KR2021096418-A
TI Device for removing carbon dioxide in flue gas of thermal power plant, has carbon dioxide absorbing unit and pressure changing unit enclosed in indoor space at room temperature, where device is implemented as independent structure or mechanical device.
AU PARK M J
   PARK C
   HYUN O
   LEE J
   BAEK S J
AE LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
GA 202190342D
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a chemical absorbent (122) for absorbing carbon dioxide. A container (121) is accommodated with the absorbent. An inlet pipe (123) is provided to diffuse external air containing the carbon dioxide into the container. A pressure changing unit is provided for sucking the external air. A control unit (130) is electrically connected to the changing unit to adjust a compression value of the air compressed by pressure. A regulator controls an amount of the air discharged from a carbon dioxide absorption unit (120). A carbon dioxide sensor detects a concentration of the carbon dioxide in the external air and provides a concentration value to the control unit. The carbon dioxide absorption unit and the pressure changing unit are enclosed in an indoor space at room temperature, where the device is implemented as an independent structure or mechanical device.
   USE - Device for removing carbon dioxide in flue gas of a thermal power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The device quickly converts indoor environment at room temperature into an environment with a carbon dioxide concentration lower than a reference value by accelerating carbon dioxide absorption through the pressure change unit. The device appropriately controls the pressure value of the pressure changing unit so as to maintain optimum absorption rate according to type and concentration of the chemical absorbent, so that absorption rate can be decreased with time, and carbon dioxide can be stably controlled, thus maintaining amount of absorption and rate of absorption.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a device for removing carbon dioxide in flue gas of a thermal power plant.
   Carbon dioxide absorption unit (120)
   Container (121)
   Chemical absorbent (122)
   Inlet Pipe (123)
   Control unit (130)

   MESSER SE & CO KGAA (MESG-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Device (1) comprises a storage container (2)                into which a feed for carbon dioxide snow and a gas                discharge line (5) for discharging carbon dioxide                gas open and which is equipped with an output unit                (3) for the metered discharge of carbon dioxide                snow. The dispensing unit has a dispensing opening                laterally arranged in the area of a dosing section                of the storage container and a horizontally movable                sliding element which cooperates with the                dispensing opening for pushing out the dispensing                section. At least two snow horns are provided, each                connected to a feed line for liquid carbon dioxide                and opening into the storage container at an                opening, which are arranged such that the streams                of carbon dioxide snow and carbon dioxide gas                entering from their mouth openings into the storage                container are directed towards one another at least                in one directional component. The gas discharge                line leads away from the storage container above                the mouth openings of the snow horns.
   USE - The device is useful for dosing carbon dioxide                snow (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device is low-maintenance device and                generates carbon dioxide snow by releasing liquid                carbon dioxide and provides precisely metered                amounts of carbon dioxide snow at short time                intervals with small losses.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                arrangement for filling refrigerant receiving                compartments of mobile cooling containers with                carbon dioxide snow, comprising a transport device                for transporting the refrigerant receiving                compartments and with the device and guide means                for transporting from the output unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic vertical                sectional view of the device.
   Device (1)
   Storage container (2)
   Output unit (3)
   Gas discharge line (5)
   Snow horns (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The snow horns are                  arranged at an acute angle, with the mouth                  openings downwards, on the storage container. A                  separating knife which can be moved horizontally                  into the interior of the storage container is                  provided for dividing a partial volume of an                  amount of snow located in the storage container.                  The storage container has a rectangular or square                  cross section, and an equal number of snow horns                  are arranged in mutually opposite vertically                  extending side walls of the storage container.                  The bottom and/or the walls of the storage                  container and/or the sliding element and/or the                  cutting knife are equipped with heating devices.                  The devices are arranged one behind the other,                  which are connected to a common feed line for                  liquid carbon dioxide and to a common gas                  discharge line.

PT P
PN CN113563489-A
TI Carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of Cordyceps militaris polysaccharide used for preparing medicine or food for increasing immunity of body, by drying Cordyceps militaris, subjecting to ultra-fine grinding treatment, performing carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, concentrating and drying.
AU JIANG G
AE ROUND TABLE PAI BIOTECHNOLOGY SUZHOU CO (ROUN-Non-standard)
GA 2021D0513G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of Cordyceps militaris polysaccharide, involves drying C.militaris, subjecting to ultra-fine grinding treatment to obtain C.militaris powder of 1200-1400 mesh, subjecting the C.militaris powder to carbon dioxide supercritical extraction treatment to obtain a supercritical extract of C.militaris, and concentrating and drying the supercritical extract of C.militaris in a vacuum under reduced pressure to obtain C.militaris polysaccharide, where the carbon dioxide supercritical extraction treatment is carried out at 20-40 MPa and 30-50degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours.
   USE - The method is used for carbon dioxide supercritical extraction of C.militaris polysaccharide for preparing medicine or food for increasing immunity of body (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a C.militaris polysaccharide prepared by the carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method of the C.militaris polysaccharide, where the extraction rate of the C.militaris polysaccharide is greater than or equal to27%, and the extraction content of C.militaris polysaccharide is greater than or equal to63%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide supercritical extraction treatment involves adding the C.militaris powder and entrainer to a supercritical extraction kettle according to a certain ratio, and extracting at 20-40 MPa and 30-50degrees Celsius for 1-3 hours to obtain the C.militaris supercritical extract.Preferred Conditions: The flow rate of the entrainer is maintained at 3-5 ml/minute. The carbon dioxide supercritical extraction treatment is carried out at 30 MPa, and 45degrees Celsius for 2 hours. When the supercritical extract of C.militaris is concentrated under vacuum and reduced pressure, the vacuum degree is controlled to be 0.06-0.10 MPa, and the temperature is maintained at 65-75degrees Celsius.Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the entrainer and the C.militaris powder is (0.5-3):1, preferably 1:1 or 2:1. The entrainer is an aqueous ethanol solution with a concentration of 95 %mass.
   ACTIVITY - Immunostimulant.

PT P
PN CN109280552-A; CN109280552-B
TI Method of using underground salt brine for sequestering carbon dioxide and sand, involves passing concentrated underground salt brine into mixing reactor, passing carbon dioxide, stirring, adjusting pH using aqueous ammonia and discharging.
AU ZHANG M
   ZHAO L
   JI J
AE UNIV NANJING (UNAJ-C)
   NANJING PUSHI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019143940
AB    NOVELTY - The method of using underground salt brine for sequestering carbon dioxide and sand, involves (1) passing concentrated underground salt brine into mixing reactor, and passing carbon dioxide of the exhaust gas collected in exhaust gas storage tank into mixing reactor, stirring and mixing uniformly to obtain a mixed slurry, (2) adjusting the pH of the mixed slurry to 6-9 using aqueous ammonia and stirring evenly, (3) discharging the pH-adjusted slurry from the bottom of the mixing reactor, and dripping in sand surface layer to form a 1-5 cm thick consolidated layer, discharging residual exhaust gas from the top of the mixing reactor, drying and sending to exhaust gas storage tank for recycling.
   USE - Method of using underground salt brine for sequestering carbon dioxide and sand.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables using underground salt brine for sequestering carbon dioxide and sand ensuring full utilization of underground salt water resources, combining carbon dioxide reduction with desertification issues, minimized impact on environment, conducive for plant growth and enhanced energy-saving effect by simple and easy process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of calcium ion is 0.05-0.5 mol/l. Preferred Conditions: The step (1) involves dripping salt brine at rate of 60-300 ml/minute, passing carbon dioxide at rate of 200-500 ml/minute, controlling pressure in mixing reactor at 0.1-0.5 MPa and agitating at 200-500 rpm. The step (2) involves dripping aqueous ammonia at rate of 1.0-5.0 ml/minute. The step (3) involves controlling the drip rate of slurry at 50-300 ml/minute.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); G04 (Miscellaneous compositions - including luminescent and tenebrescent materials, de-icing/de-misting compositions, mastics, heat transfer compositions and aerosol-can filling mixtures (C09H).)

An expansion throttle valve (4) is connected with an outlet of a cold medium circulating tank (3). An indoor heat exchanger (6) is connected with an outlet of the expansion throttle valve for converting cooling medium exchanging heat into gaseous mediumpressure normal temperature carbon dioxide cooling medium and performing cooling of a refrigerating medium.

GA 2021798987

   USE - Green and environmentally friendly carbon dioxide concentration detection device for seedling cultivation.

The device can detect the carbon dioxide concentration by matching the box body, exhaust pipe and a series of components of carbon dioxide converter, and the absorbed air, so that part of other gas is converted into carbon dioxide, so as to increase the concentration of carbon dioxide in the breeding room.

PT P
PN CN112076565-A
TI Processing gas involves collecting gas containing carbon dioxide and fine suspended particles, rinsing gas with water to obtain the rinsed gas, contacting eluted gas with the alkaline aqueous solution, making alkaline aqueous solution.
AU LIN Z
AE LIN Z (LINZ-Individual)
GA 2020C8012D
AB    NOVELTY - Processing gas involves collecting gas containing carbon dioxide and fine suspended particles, rinsing gas with water to obtain the rinsed gas, contacting eluted gas with the alkaline aqueous solution in same direction, making alkaline aqueous solution, absorbing carbon dioxide contained in the rinsed gas to obtain treated gas and weak alkaline aqueous solution. The pH of the alkaline aqueous solution is 9-14, and pH of the weak alkaline aqueous solution is 8-8.5.
   USE - Method for processing gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to process gas that reduces content of fine suspended particles and carbon dioxide in the gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline aqueous solution is sodium hydroxide aqueous solution. The weak alkaline aqueous solution comprises first weak alkaline aqueous solution and second weak alkaline aqueous solution. The microalgae is Botryococcus braunii, chlorella, Crypthecodinium cohnii, thin column algae, Dunaliella primolecta, Isochrysis Monalanthus Salina, Nannochloris sp., Neochloris oleoabundans, Phaeodactylum tricornutum, Schizochytrium sp., Tetraselmis suecica, Arthrospiraplatensis. Preferred Conditions: The concentration of the alkaline aqueous solution is 1-2 wt.%. The ratio of the flow rate of the alkaline aqueous solution to the flow rate of the rinsed gas is 1:200-1:800. Preferred Process: The method involves adding weak alkali aqueous solution to microalgae cultivation tank containing microalgae, evaporating water of the weak alkali aqueous solution to dryness to obtain sodium bicarbonate solid. The eluted gas and alkaline aqueous solution are contacted for the first time in the same direction to make alkaline aqueous solution, and absorbed the carbon dioxide contained in the rinsed gas to obtain gas after initial treatment and first weak alkali aqueous solution. The initially-treated gas and alkaline aqueous solution are contacted in the same direction again. The alkaline aqueous solution is made and absorbed the carbon dioxide contained in the gas after primary treatment. The treated gas and second weak alkaline aqueous solution are obtained.

AE KIDA K (KIDA-Individual)
   SUMITOMO SEIKA CHEM CO LTD (SEIT-C)
   SUMITOMO SEIKA CHEM CO LTD (SEIT-C)
   KIDA K (KIDA-Individual)

AB    NOVELTY - Suppressing (M1) spinal cord ischemic disorder, comprises administering a gaseous composition comprising a carbon dioxide gas to a subject in need for the carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for suppressing spinal cord ischemic disorder, where the spinal cord ischemic disorder is late-onset paraplegia (claimed), muscle weakness, paralysis, sensory disturbance, autonomic disorder, urinary incontinence, fecal incontinence, erectile dysfunction and abnormal reflex. Test details are described but no results given.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves spinal cord blood flow in an ischemic state.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for suppressing (M2) spinal cord ischemic disorder, comprising adjusting carbon dioxide partial pressure in blood of a subject to a high value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In method (M1), the step of administration of the composition is started before ischemia. Preferred Components: The composition comprises greater than or equal to 1% carbon dioxide.
   ACTIVITY - Uropathic; Neuroprotective; Muscular-Gen; Vasotropic.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PN WO2020201720-A1; AU2020251310-A1; CA3134597-A1; CN113747963-A; EP3946685-A1; IN202117048197-A; US2022184574-A1
TI Calcination process involves calcining metal carbonate by combustion of fuel in presence of mixture of water vapor to generate metal oxide, water vapor, carbon dioxide and heat and using heat generated to drive oxygen generation reaction.
AU KRUGER T
   JENKINS B
   KLUGE T
AE ORIGEN POWER LTD (ORIG-Non-standard)
   ORIGEN POWER LTD (ORIG-Non-standard)
   ORIGEN POWER LTD (ORIG-Non-standard)
   ORIGEN POWER LTD (ORIG-Non-standard)
GA 202098009F
AB    NOVELTY - Calcination process involves (a) calcining a metal carbonate by combustion of a fuel in the presence of a mixture of oxygen, water vapor and carbon dioxide to generate a metal oxide, water vapor, carbon dioxide and heat, (b) using the heat generated to drive an oxygen generation reaction and (c) using the oxygen generated in step (b) in step of calcination.
   USE - The calcination process is useful in carbon dioxide sequestration and oxygen generation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not require nitrogen, increases kinetics of the calcination reaction and is environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The heat generated in step (a) is used to compress the carbon dioxide generated in steps (a) and/or (b). The oxygen generation reaction in step (b) involves adopting a gas separation process which occurs in a pressure swing adsorption system (preferred) or an air separation unit. The process further involves sequestering the carbon dioxide generated in step (a). The process further involves hydrating the metal oxide generated in step (a) to produce a metal hydroxide and heat. The heat generated during hydration of the metal oxide is used to drive the generation of oxygen in step (b). Preferred Components: The metal of the metal carbonate in step (a) is a group II metal and/or group II metals. The metal carbonate is chosen from calcium carbonate (preferred), magnesium carbonate and/or dolomite. The fuel in step (a) comprises hydrocarbons (preferred), coal, coke, syngas, biomass and/or biogas. Preferred Composition: The oxygen and/or carbon dioxide used in the combustion reaction of step (a) is greater than 95% pure. The oxygen generated in step (c) is greater than 95% pure.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN114377429-A; CN216986376-U
TI Removal of organic matter and/or elemental sulfur            from supported waste catalyst used in pesticide,            involves removing organic matter and/or elemental            sulfur adsorbed by supported waste catalyst using            carbon dioxide supercritical extraction, and separating            carbon dioxide in non-supercritical state.
AU LIU G
   SHI Q
   CHENG L
   ZHANG M
   LIU Z
AE SHAANXI JUTAI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
   SHAANXI JUTAI NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 202265425Q
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of organic matter and/or elemental                sulfur from supported waste catalyst involves                loading supported waste catalyst into extraction                tank (1), feeding liquid carbon dioxide to                extraction tank, adjusting liquid carbon dioxide to                supercritical state, removing organic matter and/or                elemental sulfur adsorbed by supported waste                catalyst by carbon dioxide supercritical                extraction, passing extraction phase to receiving                separation tank (5) and buffer tank (6), adjusting                carbon dioxide in extraction phase to                non-supercritical state, separating carbon dioxide                in non-supercritical state, discharging carbon                dioxide from buffer tank in gaseous form,                compressing into liquid state, and storing in                liquid carbon dioxide storage tank (3). After                completing carbon dioxide separation of                non-supercritical state, reducing pressure in                separation tank and buffer tank to normal pressure,                separating organic matter and/or elemental sulfur                remaining at bottom of the tank, and discharging in                liquid form.
   USE - Removal of organic matter and/or elemental                sulfur from supported waste catalyst used in                petrochemical, coal, pesticide, pharmaceutical, and                chemical production fields.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables removal of organic matter                and/or elemental sulfur from supported waste                catalyst using carbon dioxide supercritical                extraction method without producing secondary                pollution, by simple, economical, and                environmentally friendly process. The organic                matter contained in the supported waste catalyst                reduces to less than 0.5%, elemental sulfur content                reduces to less than 0.5%, and extracted sulfur                content reaches more than 98%. The continuous                cooperation of the conveying unit can automate the                process, laying a foundation for comprehensive                utilization of the waste catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removal of organic matter and/or elemental                sulfur from supported waste catalyst involves using                device for removing organic matter and/or elemental                sulfur. The device comprises extraction tank, and                carbon dioxide inlet valve (2) at the top of the                extraction tank is connected to liquid outlet of                liquid carbon dioxide storage tank. The extraction                phase outlet valve (4) at the top of the extraction                tank is connected to the receiving separation tank                and the buffer tank through pipeline. The buffer                tank is connected to liquid inlet of liquid carbon                dioxide storage tank through compressor (7). The                method involves loading supported waste catalyst                into the extraction tank, feeding liquid carbon                dioxide to the extraction tank, adjusting liquid                carbon dioxide to supercritical state in extraction                tank, removing organic matter and/or elemental                sulfur adsorbed by supported waste catalyst by                carbon dioxide supercritical extraction method,                passing extraction phase after extraction to the                receiving separation tank and the buffer tank from                the extraction tank, adjusting carbon dioxide in                the extraction phase to non-supercritical state in                the receiving separation tank and the buffer tank,                separating carbon dioxide in the non-supercritical                state by receiving separation tank and the buffer                tank, discharging carbon dioxide from top of the                buffer tank in gaseous form, compressing into                liquid state by compressor, and storing in liquid                carbon dioxide storage tank. After completing the                carbon dioxide separation of non-supercritical                state, reducing pressure in the receiving                separation tank and buffer tank to normal pressure,                separating organic matter and/or extract of                elemental sulfur remaining at the bottom of the                tank from the receiving separation tank and the                buffer tank, and discharging temperature-regulated                phase separation through receiving separation tank                and buffer tank from the bottom valve (37) in                liquid form, respectively.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of device                for removing organic matter and/or elemental sulfur                from supported waste catalyst. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

PN CN110465268-A
TI Preparing carbon-based carbon dioxide adsorbent useful for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas, comprises e.g. preparing semi-coke, activating semi-coke, semi-coke treatment, and modifying semi-coke.
AU HAN J
   XUE Z
   SONG K
   LI K
   XU Y
   ZHAO R
AE UNIV HEBEI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKJ-C)
GA 201999076K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises (1) drying lignite, pulverizing, cooling, charging into fixed-bed reactor, carrying out pyrolysis to obtain pyrolysis semi-coke, taking pyrolysis semi-coke, loading into fixed-bed reactor, heating, activating to obtain activated semi-coke, taking activated semi-coke, adding concentrated hydrochloric acid, adding distilled water until immersed in activated coke, stirring, subjecting to ultrasonic immersion treatment by ultrasonic oscillator, suction filtering and washing with distilled water and drying, adding hydrofluoric acid to activated semi-coke, adding distilled water, stirring and immersing, subjecting to ultrasonic immersion treatment, filtering, washing with distilled water, pickling to obtain activated semi-coke, adding organic amine and absolute ethanol solution, subjecting to ultrasonic immersion treatment, filtering, washing with distilled water, and drying.
   USE - The carbon-based carbon dioxide adsorbent is useful for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent has high adsorption capacity, good cycle stability, and low energy consumption properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing carbon-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises (1) taking lignite, drying at 100 degrees C for 2-3 hours, then pulverizing with high-speed pulverizer, cooling to screen out required particle size of 16-40 mesh, charging the screened coal into fixed-bed reactor, and passing nitrogen for pyrolysis for 2-3 hours to obtain pyrolysis semi-coke, (2) taking pyrolysis semi-coke, loading into fixed-bed reactor, passing nitrogen, heating up to 800 degrees C, and passing into water vapor, activating for 60-180 minutes to obtain activated semi-coke, (3) taking 10-20 g activated semi-coke, adding 15-20 ml concentrated hydrochloric acid, then adding distilled water until immersed in activated coke, stirring for 0.5-1 hours, subjecting to ultrasonic immersion treatment by ultrasonic oscillator for 2-3 hours, suction filtering and washing with distilled water and drying, (4) adding 15-20 ml hydrofluoric acid to activated semi-coke treated in step (3), adding distilled water to immerse over half coke, stirring and immersing for 0.5-1 hour, subjecting to ultrasonic immersion treatment by ultrasonic oscillator for 2-3 hours, suction filtering, washing with distilled water, drying, and pickling to obtain activated semi-coke, and (5) weighing 10-20 g pickled active semi-coke, adding 5-10 ml organic amine and 20-25 ml absolute ethanol solution, subjecting to ultrasonic immersion treatment with an ultrasonic oscillator for 3-4 hours, suction filtering, washing with distilled water, and drying to obtain carbon-based carbon dioxide adsorbent. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) use of carbon-based carbon dioxide adsorbent for capturing carbon dioxide in flue gas; and
   (2) carbon-based carbon dioxide adsorbent, prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (2), the rate of water vapor penetration is 0.04 g of water vapor per minute per gram of carbonized material. In the step (5), the organic amine is ethanolamine (preferred), dimethylamine, 1,2-dimethylpropylamine or hexamethylenediamine. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the pyrolysis temperature is 800 degrees C. In the step (3), step (4) and step (5), the drying condition is vacuum drying for 2-3 hours, freezing and drying for 10-12 hours. The adsorption temperature is 30-40?OC, and the adsorption pressure is 0.1-1 MPa.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2021246314-A1
TI Effectively utilizing carbon dioxide in cement exhaust gas by adding hydrogen to carbon dioxide in exhaust gas discharged from cement production facility, or to carbon dioxide separated and recovered from exhaust gas to generate methane, and using it as alternative fuel for manufacturing facility.
AU TAKAYAMA Y
   KAWASAKI H
   KOMA T
   KOMATSU T
AE MITSUBISHI MATERIALS CORP (MITV-C)
GA 2021D9945M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for effectively utilizing carbon dioxide in a cement exhaust gas involves adding hydrogen to carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas discharged from a cement production facility, or to carbon dioxide separated and recovered from the exhaust gas to generate the methane, and using methane as an alternative fuel for some or all of the fossil fuels for the cement manufacturing facility.
   USE - The method is used for effectively utilizing carbon dioxide in a cement exhaust gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively uses methane which can reduce carbon dioxide from energy sources and enhance the greenhouse gas reduction effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon dioxide utilization system in cement manufacturing exhaust gas, comprising a methane conversion device that produces methane by adding hydrogen to carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas from the cement manufacturing facility or carbon dioxide separated and recovered from the exhaust gas, and a methane supply device that supplies the methane to the cement manufacturing facility as an alternative fuel for part or all of the fossil fuel to the cement manufacturing facility.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The methane supply device is a carbon dioxide utilization system in cement manufacturing exhaust gas, which is connected to a fuel supply line of at least one of a cement calcining kiln and a calcining furnace in the cement manufacturing facility. The system further comprises a carbon dioxide separation/recovery device that separates and recovers carbon dioxide by bringing the exhaust gas from the cement manufacturing facility into contact with the carbon dioxide absorbing material. The methanization device directly adds the hydrogen to the exhaust gas to produce the methane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The methane is used as an alternative fuel for at least one of a cement heating kiln and a calcining furnace in the cement manufacturing facility. The carbon dioxide is carbon dioxide obtained by contacting the exhaust gas from the cement manufacturing facility with a carbon dioxide absorber and separating and recovering it. The hydrogen is directly added to the exhaust gas to generate the methane.

PN CN111004398-A; CN111004398-B
TI New micro-porous copper-metal-organic framework material useful in selective separation of carbon dioxide/dinitrogen and carbon dioxide/methane.
AU SHI N
   LIANG L
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV SHANXI (UYSX-C)
GA 202032298D
AB    NOVELTY - Micro-porous cupper-metal-organic framework material (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful: in selective separation of carbon dioxide/dinitrogen and carbon dioxide/methane (claimed); and in capturing and separating carbon dioxide and natural gas in power plant exhaust purification fields.
   ADVANTAGE - (I): has excellent water vapor stability and organic solvent stability, has excellent selective adsorption performance for carbon dioxide, and excellent selective separation performance for carbon dioxide/dinitrogen and carbon dioxide/methane by gas adsorption test results.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Micro-porous copper-metal-organic framework (MOF) material of formula (CuTIPA.n(N,N-dimethylformamide)) (I) is new.
   n = 1-3; and
   TIPA2 = 5-(1H-1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)isophthalate is of formula (II).
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) use of micro-porous cupper-metal-organic framework material in selective separation of carbon dioxide/dinitrogen and carbon dioxide/methane, comprising soaking micro-porous Cu-MOF material in ethanol for 72 hours to exchange high-boiling dimethylformamide molecules in the pores, then degreasing the exchanged micro-porous cupper-metal-organic framework material at 100 degrees C for 8 hours, and removing dimethylformamide molecules in the pores and then filling into the device for storing carbon dioxide adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises adding copper salt and H2TIPA ligand in a molar ratio of 1:0.9-1.1 into mixed solvent, then sonicating until the ligand and copper salt are completely mixed, and reacting at 80-150 degrees C for 1-5 days to obtain final product, where the copper salt is copper(II) nitrate trihydrate; the ligand is 5-(1H-1,2,4-triazol-1-yl)isophthalate (H2TIPA); the mixed solvent is water, dimethylformamide and N,N-dimethylacetamide in a volume ratio of 1:0.5-5:1, (either water or dimethylformamide is mixed in a volume ratio of 1:1-5, or water and N,N-dimethylacetamide is mixed in volume ratio of 1:1-5).

PT P
PN CN110629245-A; CN110629245-B
TI Photoreduction of carbon dioxide by nitrogen-doped carbon-coated copper-cadmium sulfide catalyst involves using cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate, copper(II) nitrate tetrahydrate, thiourea, polyvinylpyrrolidone and anhydrous ethylene glycol.
AU CHENG J
   CEN K
   ZHOU J
   LIU J
   YANG W
   WANG Z
   ZHANG Y
   ZHOU Z
   HE Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202002541R
AB    NOVELTY - Photoreduction of carbon dioxide by a nitrogen-doped carbon-coated copper-cadmium sulfide catalyst involves: (1) taking cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate, copper(II) nitrate tetrahydrate, thiourea, polyvinylpyrrolidone and anhydrous ethylene glycol, mixing and ultrasonically dissolving to form a uniform solution, pouring the solution into a stainless steel high-pressure reaction kettle, and heating to obtain copper-cadmium sulfide nanoparticles; and (2) taking copper-cadmium sulfide nanoparticles in step (1) and dissolving in anhydrous methanol, then dimethylimidazole solution, carrying out ultrasonic treatment on the mixed solution, then pouring anhydrous methanol solution of zinc nitrate hexahydrate into the mixed solution, reacting, centrifuging the precipitate, then washing with anhydrous methanol and drying at 80 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain copper-cadmium sulfide nanoparticles coated with an imidazole organic frame as a solid mixture.
   USE - Photoreduction of carbon dioxide by a nitrogen-doped carbon-coated copper-cadmium sulfide catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The photoreduction of carbon dioxide improves the selectivity of methanol product by more than 60% compared with pure nitrogen-doped carbon catalyst or copper-cadmium sulfide nanoparticle catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Photoreduction of carbon dioxide by a nitrogen-doped carbon-coated copper-cadmium sulfide catalyst involves: (1) taking 3.5 mmol cadmium nitrate tetrahydrate, 3.5 mmol copper(II) nitrate tetrahydrate, 7 mmol thiourea, 7 mmol polyvinylpyrrolidone and 70 ml anhydrous ethylene glycol, mixing and ultrasonically dissolving to form a uniform solution, pouring the solution into a stainless steel high-pressure reaction kettle, and heating at 120 degrees C for 4 hours to obtain copper-cadmium sulfide nanoparticles; (2) taking 2.5 mg copper-cadmium sulfide nanoparticles in step (1) and dissolving in 2 ml anhydrous methanol as a solvent, then dripping 10 mM 30 ml dimethylimidazole solution, carrying out ultrasonic treatment on the mixed solution for 15 minutes, then pouring 10 mM 10 ml anhydrous methanol solution of zinc nitrate hexahydrate into the mixed solution, reacting at room temperature for 2 hours, after the reaction is completed, centrifuging the precipitate and washing with anhydrous methanol, and drying at 80 degrees C for 12 hours to obtain copper-cadmium sulfide nanoparticles coated with an imidazole organic frame as a solid mixture; (3) placing the solid mixture in step (2) in a tubular furnace, passing nitrogen and heating the furnace at 300-700 degrees C, calcining at a constant temperature for 2 hours to obtain a nitrogen-doped carbon-coated copper-cadmium sulfide catalyst, taking 10 mg catalyst, 100 mu l deionized water and 200 mu l Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution, ultrasonically mixing and brushing on 1 cm2 carbon cloth, and then placing in a vacuum oven at 80 degrees C for 8 hours to prepare a cathode electrode; (4) taking 12.3 g iron(III) nitrate nonahydrate and 12.3 nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate, pouring into 50 ml deionized water and stirring for 30 minutes to obtain a mixed solution, heating the mixed solution in a water bath at 110 degrees C for 30 minutes until the water is completely evaporated to give a solid, grinding the solid in a mortar, then pouring into a crucible, and calcining in a muffle furnace at a constant temperature of 550 degrees C for 5 hours to get a nickel oxide/iron(III) oxide catalyst; (5) adding 20.3 g graphitic carbon nitride to 25 ml absolute ethanol, and ultrasonically stirring for 0.5 hour, then pouring 6 g nickel oxide/iron(III) oxide catalyst into the graphitic carbon nitride solution and stirring for 24 hours to form a uniform suspension solution, transferring the suspension solution to the stainless steel high-pressure reaction kettle, heating at 150 degrees C for 4 hours, then naturally cooling, filtering and drying at 60 degrees C to obtain a graphitic carbon nitride-doped nickel oxide/iron(III) oxide catalyst, taking 50 mg the catalyst, 300 mu l deionized water and 300 mu l Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution, performing ultrasonic treatment and uniformly mixing and then coating on 1 cm2 carbon cloth, then placing the cloth in a vacuum oven at 60 degrees C for 12 hours to prepare an anode electrode; (6) using a reactor having a double electric tank to install the cathode electrode obtained in step (3) on one side and the anode electrode obtained in step (5) on the other side, connecting the middle of the double-tank reactor with a sand core for isolation, where the whole reactor is sealed with optical glass, irradiating the anode electrode with UV light to simulate solar UV light, and connecting the anode electrode and the cathode electrode to form an external circuit; and (7) adding a deionized aqueous solution to the anode cavity of the double cell reactor, adding anhydrous dimethylformamide organic solution to the cathode cavity, introducing carbon dioxide into the cathode cavity through a micron aerator to perform a photoreduction reaction, and converting carbon dioxide in the cathode cavity into methanol after photoreduction reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (2), the centrifugation speed is 10000 rpm, and the centrifugation time is 10 minutes. In step (3), the heating rate of the tubular furnace is 5 degrees C/minute. Preferred Properties: In step (1), the average diameter of the prepared copper-cadmium sulfide nanoparticles is 70 nm. In step (3), the prepared nitrogen-doped carbon-coated copper-cadmium sulfide catalyst has an average diameter of 370 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In steps (3) and (5), the mass concentration of the Nafion (RTM: Sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution is 10%.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); ; J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A85 (Electrical applications.)

PT P
PN US2019308137-A1; WO2019197975-A1; US10617998-B2; EP3773994-A1
TI Reclaiming carbon dioxide from air comprises e.g. absorbing solar radiation using many photovoltaic panels, chemically removing carbon dioxide from heated air, using coolant liquid and releasing air having carbon dioxide chemically removed.
AU KISS Z J
AE KISS Z J (KISS-Individual)
   KISS Z J (KISS-Individual)
   KISS Z J (KISS-Individual)
GA 2019846157
AB    NOVELTY - Reclaiming carbon dioxide from air comprises: absorbing solar radiation using many photovoltaic panels, where each of the solar photovoltaic panels includes at least one heating plate configured to heat air and, behind the heating plate, a support plate, and the photovoltaic panels are separated by a distance, forming an enclosure to provide a chimney effect; heating air between the photovoltaic panels, producing heated air, causing the heated air to rise, sucking in more air to be heated, and the air includes carbon dioxide; chemically removing the carbon dioxide from the heated air, using a coolant liquid, where the coolant liquid, in a heat exchanger, when in contact with the carbon dioxide in the heated air, forms a bicarbonate; and releasing air that has had the carbon dioxide chemically removed.
   USE - The method is useful for reclaiming carbon dioxide from air (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for reclaiming carbon dioxide from air, comprising an energy converter, the energy converter including a solar photovoltaic panel configured to absorb solar radiation and convert some of the solar radiation into electricity, the solar photovoltaic panel including at least two plates, the at least two plates are configured so that the absorbed solar radiation heats the at least two plates, heating air between the plates, producing heated air, causing the heated air to rise, sucking in more air to be heated, where the air includes carbon dioxide, a heat exchanger, the heat exchanger includes a coolant liquid configured to chemically removing the carbon dioxide from the heated air so that, when the coolant liquid is in contact with the carbon dioxide in the heated air, the coolant liquid reacts with the carbon dioxide, forming a bicarbonate, and an air exhaust opening configured to enable air that has had the carbon dioxide chemically removed to be released.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: converting some of the solar radiation into electricity; converting some heat produced by the solar radiation to electricity; exchanging some heat of the heated air with the coolant liquid; and removing the bicarbonate from the heat exchanger. Preferred Components: The coolant liquid includes potassium hydroxide. The coolant includes potassium hydroxide. The bicarbonate includes potassium bicarbonate. The energy converter includes a thermal energy converter configured to convert some heat produced by the solar radiation to electricity. The coolant liquid is configured such that the heated air exchanges some heat with the coolant liquid. The plates include a heat absorbing film. The film includes graphite.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. PN WO2022200532-A1
TI Manufacture of hydrogen- and carbon            monoxide-containing synthesis gas mixture involves            reacting gas containing hydrocarbon, oxygen and carbon            dioxide in partial oxidation reactor to form product            mixture containing carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide,            separating carbon dioxide and returning to            reactor.
AU BADER A
   GALL M
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 2022C4400X
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of hydrogen- and carbon                monoxide-containing synthesis gas mixture involves                feeding at least one reactant gas containing                hydrocarbons, an oxygen-containing reactant gas and                a carbon dioxide-containing reactant gas into a                partial oxidation reactor, and reacting at                1200-1550&#176; C to form product gas mixture                containing hydrogen, carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide, in which portion of the carbon dioxide is                preferably separated off from the product gas                mixture and returned to the partial oxidation                reactor. The carbon dioxide fed into the partial                oxidation reactor contains additional, imported                carbon dioxide and the molar ratio of hydrocarbons,                oxygen and carbon dioxide in the feed gases is                0.19-0.57:0.31-0.70:0.02-0.30. In the partial                oxidation reactor, the product gas mixture has                molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide of                0.8:1-1.6:1.
   USE - Manufacture of hydrogen- and carbon                monoxide-containing synthesis gas mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacture of hydrogen-                and carbon monoxide-containing synthesis gas                mixture with reduced overall carbon dioxide                emission, by recycling the by-product carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbons                  contain at least 80 wt.% 1-6C alkanes and an                  oxygenates. The hydrocarbon-containing reactant                  gas of the partial oxidation contains at least 80                  wt.% methane. The molar ratio of hydrogen to                  carbon monoxide in the product gas mixture is                  0.8:1-1.2:1, preferably 0.9:1-1.1:1. The educt                  gases of the partial oxidation the molar ratio of                  methane, oxygen and carbon dioxide is                  0.39-0.57:0.30-0.40:0.05-0.30, preferably                  0.39-0.57:0.31-0.38:0.05-0.30.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
