PN WO2022187640-A1
TI Extracting carbon dioxide by splitting salt            solution to provide hydroxide solution and acid            solution, contacting hydroxide solution with gas            mixture including carbon dioxide and combining            bicarbonate and acid solutions to evolve carbon dioxide            and regenerate salt solution.
AU SCHRAG D P
AE HARVARD COLLEGE (HARD-C)
GA 2022B5917C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting carbon dioxide comprises                (a) splitting an at least 0.5 M salt solution to                provide a hydroxide solution and an acid solution,                (b) contacting the hydroxide solution with a gas                mixture including carbon dioxide to produce a                bicarbonate solution and (c) combining the                bicarbonate and the acid solutions to evolve carbon                dioxide and regenerate the salt solution.
   USE - The method is used for extracting carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method employs a chemical salt splitting                cycle that allows for the efficient removal of                carbon dioxide from a gas mixture such as air, even                at ambient air concentrations and reduces the                energy demands of the capture process, and any need                for carbon-based liquid or solid fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for extracting carbon dioxide, comprising (a) an at                least 0.5 M salt solution, (b) a means for                splitting an at least 0.5 M salt solution to                provide a hydroxide solution and an acid solution,                (c) a means for contacting the hydroxide solution                with a gas mixture including carbon dioxide and (d)                a means for combining the bicarbonate and the acid                solutions to evolve carbon dioxide and regenerate                the salt solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The system further                  comprises a unit for returning the regenerated                  salt solution to the unit for splitting the salt.                  The unit for splitting the salt comprises an                  electrolytic or electrodialytic device. The unit                  for contacting the hydroxide solution and gas                  mixture comprises a bubbler or a water contactor                  or a pond. The system further comprises a unit of                  separating water vapor from the evolved carbon                  dioxide and returning it to the regenerated salt                  solution. The system further comprises a unit for                  storing or sequestering the evolved carbon                  dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises returning the resulting salt solution                  to step (a) and repeating steps (a)-(c). The                  cation of the salt is selected to provide a                  water-soluble carbonate. The salt is split using                  an electrochemical process or an electrodialysis                  process. The contacting comprises bubbling,                  flowing the hydroxide solution through a water                  contactor, or passive absorption. The method                  further comprises separating water vapor from the                  evolved carbon dioxide and returning it to the                  regenerated salt solution. The method further                  comprises storing or sequestering the evolved                  carbon dioxide. The method further comprises                  using a unit for collecting the evolved carbon                  dioxide. Preferred Components: The cation is                  sodium, potassium, or rubidium. The salt                  comprises an anion that is more acidic than                  carbonic acid when protonated. The protonated                  anion has a pKa at least 3 pKa units lower than                  carbonic acid at room temperature.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN110152735-A; CN110152735-B
TI Carbon dioxide reduction catalyst comprises metal organic framework and metal nanoparticles containing nickel-iron compound.
AU LI X
   WANG Y
   LIANG Y
   LI J
   CUI G
   CAI L
AE BEIJING MACHINERY EQUIP RES INST (CAES-C)
   BEIJING MACHINERY EQUIP RES INST (CAES-C)
GA 2019773176
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide reduction catalyst comprises metal organic framework and metal nanoparticles. The metal nanoparticles are encapsulated in the metal organic framework. The pore structure of the metal organic framework is ZIF-8 or UIO-66. The metal nanoparticles are nickel-iron compound.
   USE - Carbon dioxide reduction catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide reduction catalyst has high catalytic performance, strong selectivity, high stability, improves concentration of carbon dioxide on surface, and effectively increases the synthesis gas yield of reduction reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The carbon dioxide reduction catalyst comprises metal organic framework and metal nanoparticles. The metal nanoparticles are encapsulated in the metal organic framework. The pore structure of the metal organic framework is ZIF-8 or UIO-66. The metal nanoparticles are nickel-iron compound of formula: (NixFey). INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: preparation of carbon dioxide reduction catalyst, which involves preparing metal organic framework, preparing nickel-iron metal nanoparticles, adding metal organic framework and nickel-iron compound metal nanoparticles to solvent, stirring, dipping, washing and drying; and #carbon dioxide reduction reaction method, which involves granulating the carbon dioxide reduction catalyst by tableting, loading into isothermal zone of tubular fixed bed reactor, passing hydrogen and carbon dioxide, reacting hydrogen and carbon dioxide through the isothermal zone to form carbon monoxide and water and outputting carbon monoxide and water from product outlet.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The loading amount of metal nanoparticles is 1.0-2.5% of total mass of metal organic framework and the metal nanoparticles. Preferred Process: The preparation process involves using nickel nitrate and iron nitrate as precursors, dissolving in a solvent, and preparing nickel-iron compound metal nanoparticles by liquid phase reduction process in the presence of a protective agent and a reducing agent. Preferred Properties: The metal organic framework has pore structure having specific surface area of 1000-1400 m2/g. The particle diameter of metal nanoparticles is 2.0-3.0 nm. The flow rate of hydrogen gas is 120-180 mL/hour, and the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 70-180 mL/hour. The reaction temperature is 500-600 degrees C.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN113548992-A
TI Extracting lutein from microalgae using supercritical carbon dioxide comprises e.g. separating microalgae liquid from algae water, drying algae mud, crushing, adding microalgae powder into supercritical extraction reaction device, performing pressure relief and gasification and concentrating.
AU XIA A
   LIAO Q
   HUANG Y
   ZHU X
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2021C3640J
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting lutein from microalgae using supercritical carbon dioxide comprises (i) separating the microalgae liquid from the algae water, drying the algae mud and crushing to obtain dried microalgae powder; (ii) adding a specific amount of microalgae powder into the supercritical extraction reaction device, and adding a specific amount of organic solvent dissolved in ionic liquid, and stirring and mixing thoroughly; (iii) pumping supercritical carbon dioxide into the supercritical extraction device until the set extraction pressure is reached, and controlling the temperature of the supercritical extraction device until the extraction is complete; (iv) subjecting the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid containing lutein to pressure relief, performing gasification and scrubbing steps, releasing the lutein into the collector, washing the lutein and concentrating to obtain a high-purity lutein solution.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting lutein from microalgae using supercritical carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable in the fields of biology, and medicine.

TI Producing methane from carbon dioxide containing            industrial gases, by culturing methanogenic            microorganisms, providing industrial gases, controlling            and regulating pH, feeding methanogenic microorganism            culture, and collecting methane.
AU FONTAINE D
   HORL M
   PESIC A
   HAFENBRADL D
   TAVARES S K
   AHRENS T
   HOERL M
   TAVARES SILVA K
   SILVA K T
AE ELECTROCHAEA GMBH (ELEC-Non-standard)
   ELECTROCHAEA GMBH (ELEC-Non-standard)
   ELECTROCHAEA GMBH (ELEC-Non-standard)
   ELECTROCHAEA GMBH (ELEC-Non-standard)
GA 202036064E
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing methane from carbon                dioxide containing industrial gases in a                bioreactor, involves (a) culturing methanogenic                microorganisms in a continuous process, (b)                providing industrial gases containing carbon                dioxide and hydrogen sulfide and/or oxygen                molecule, (c) controlling and optionally                continuously regulating the pH value to a                predetermined value, (d) optional feeding the                culture of methanogenic microorganisms with                additional hydrogen molecule in a stoichiometric                ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen molecule is                1:0.6 and 1:5, and (e) collecting methane or a gas                composition enriched with methane.
   USE - The process is useful for producing methane                from carbon dioxide containing industrial gases in                a bioreactor.
   ADVANTAGE - The methane produced by the method is                essentially free of solid contaminants, e.g. foams,                small solid particles such as dust and dirt                particles in suspension, greases or gaseous                contaminants, e.g. water vapor, hydrogen sulfide,                siloxanes, ammonia and halogen compounds (chloride,                fluoride), volatile organic compounds (VOCs), e.g.                limonene and other terpenes, and other trace                contaminants.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves (i)                  maintaining the methanogenic microorganism in a                  suitable liquid culture medium which provides a                  nitrogen source and salts, (ii) maintaining the                  culture conditions i.e. either anaerobic or                  facultative anaerobic, (iii) optionally stirring                  the culture, (iv) removing continuously metabolic                  water from the culture, and (v) maintaining                  temperature of 32-85 degrees C at atmospheric                  pressure. The methanogenic microorganism is                  cultured in the presence of additionally added                  sulfide, preferably in the form of sodium sulfide                  (Na2S) and/or ammonium, preferably in the form of                  ammonium hydroxide (NH4OH). The methanogenic                  microorganism in the culture measured as optical                  density (OD)610 is cultured to a density of                  microorganisms of at least 14 and up to 60 which                  corresponds to a dry weight of the microorganisms                  in the culture of at least 2.5 g/L and up to 20                  g/L. The methanogenic microorganism culture is                  continuously stabilized and/or regulated to be                  maintained at a pH or 9 or less, or at of pH 7 by                  adding suitable amounts of an acid and/or base.                  The methanogenic microorganism culture is                  continuously stabilized and/or regulated in order                  to maintain the given pH value by metering                  suitable amounts of sodium hydroxide (NaOH) or                  NH4OH and hydrochloric acid (HCl) into the                  culture. The entire process or at least one step                  is carried out under atmospheric pressure                  conditions and/or under pressure conditions of up                  to 16-420 bar. Preferred Microorganism: The at                  least one methanogenic microorganism is chosen                  from archaea or archaebacteria comprising                  Methanobacterium, Methanobrevibacter,                  Methanothermobacter, Methanococcus,                  Methanosarcina, Methanopyrus or their mixtures.                  The methanogenic microorganism is anaerobic                  and/or oxygen-compatible. The methanogenic                  microorganism is chosen from naturally chosen or                  natively suitable microorganisms, the adapted                  halogenophilic microorganisms and the genetically                  modified microorganisms, all of which can live                  and thrive in a moderately saline environment.                  Preferred Components: The liquid culture medium                  of step (i) has moderately saline environment and                  comprises 12-300 mmol/l chlorine anion, 0.5-12                  g/l, preferably 5.6 g/l sodium chloride (NaCl).                  The carbon dioxide containing industrial gases                  are the carbon source for methane production and                  come from natural gas or treated geothermal gas,                  emissions from coal or fossil fuel combustion                  plants, emissions from waste or renewable energy                  combustion plants and emissions from geothermal                  power plants. The carbon dioxide containing                  industrial gas comprises at least 20% carbon                  dioxide, up to 5000 mg/l hydrogen sulfide, and up                  to 5% oxygen molecule or more.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN110833819-A; CN110833819-B
TI Double-brush structure organosilicon nano material carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises silicone nanoparticle material and organic amine supported on the silicone nanoparticle material.
AU LIU Q
   QIAO X
   QI L
   BAI G
   CUI M
   TANG J
   CHEN X
   FEI Z
   ZHANG Z
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
   NANJING ZIHUAN ENG TECHNOLOGY RES INST (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020188592
AB    NOVELTY - Double-brush structure organosilicon nano material carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises silicone nanoparticle material and organic amine supported on the silicone nanoparticle material. The pores of the silicone nanoparticle material are covered by a long alkyl chain and its silane organic matter.
   USE - Used as double-brush structure organosilicon nano material carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent: has better amine stability and dispersion, high stability, maximum carbon dioxide adsorption capacity of 5.34 mmol/g, and adsorption capacity of 5.24 mmol/g after 100 adsorption/desorption cycle experiments. The method is suitable for large-scale industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing double-brush structure organosilicon nano material carbon dioxide adsorbent comprising (i) taking ethyl orthosilicate as silicon source, bis(triethoxysilylpropyl)tetrasulfide as silane organic compound, and triethanolamine as catalyst, mixing with templating agent and water, performing hydrolysis reaction and drying to obtain double-brush structure organosilicon nano material, and (ii) supporting the organic amine on the obtained silicone material by a dipping method and drying to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the hydrolysis reaction temperature is 70-120 degrees C, and the reaction time is 3-12 hours. In step (ii), the dipping solvent used in the dipping method is organic compound having a boiling point of 50-90 degrees C; and the dipping time is 0.2-12 hours; and drying temperature is 40-90 degrees C, and drying time is 3-24 hours. Preferred Components: The organic amine includes polyethyleneimine, diethylenetriamine, triethylenetetramine, tetraethylenepentamine and/or triethanolamine. The double-brush structure organosilicon nano material carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises 10-80% organic amine. The templating agent includes cetyltrimethylammonium chloride and/or cetyltrimethylammonium bromide. The mass ratio between triethanolamine, ethyl orthosilicate, templating agent, and bis(triethoxysilylpropyl)tetrasulfide is 0.1-1:1:2:1.3-13. The organic compound includes methanol, ethanol, and/or acetone.

PT P
PN CN110540891-A
TI Extraction of phytosterol-rich seabuckthorn seed oil using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid by drying seabuckthorn seed to constant weight, crushing, sieving, supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extracting, and detecting sterol content.
AU ZHANG D
   ZHANG B
   YANG Y
   HU Z
   ZHANG X
AE UNIV QINGHAI (UYQH-C)
GA 2019A4372H
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of phytosterol-rich seabuckthorn seed oil using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid comprises drying the seabuckthorn seed, drying to constant weight to obtain seabuckthorn seed dry sample, crushing, sieving with 40-60-mesh sieve and aperture of 0.25-0.425 mm to obtain seabuckthorn seed powder, weighing 50 g seabuckthorn seed powder sample, taking 10 ml entrainer absolute ethyl alcohol, putting into extracting axe, supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extracting at 50-70 degrees C and 300-500 bars for 5-7 hours to obtain seabuckthorn seed oil, and detecting the sterol content by gas chromatography mass spectrometry.
   USE - The method is for extraction of phytosterol-rich seabuckthorn seed oil using supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the seabuckthorn seed oil sterol extraction yield, shortens the extraction period, is simple and suitable for industrialization, and has low cost and great economic benefits. The extracted sterol can be developed into e.g. food, health products, and additives without further purification.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extraction is conducted at 60 degrees C and 450 bars for 7 hours.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PN CN107759490-A; CN107759490-B
TI New catalyst i.e. 1,2-dimethoxybenzene-cyclohexane-1,2-diamine zinc complex useful for preparing poly(cyclohexene carbonate) by copolymerization of carbon dioxide and cyclohexene oxide.
AU YANG L
   YU X
   WANG N
   WANG Y
   LU X
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
   HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 2018199802
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst i.e. 1,2-dimethoxybenzene-cyclohexane-1,2-diamine zinc complex (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful for preparing poly(cyclohexene carbonate) by copolymerization of carbon dioxide and cyclohexene oxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) has excellent catalytic properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst i.e. 1,2-dimethoxybenzene-cyclohexane-1,2-diamine zinc complex of formula (I) is new. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I); and #use of (I) in preparing poly(cyclohexene carbonate) by copolymerization of carbon dioxide and cyclohexene oxide comprising replacing the air in the autoclave with carbon dioxide, then adding (I) and cyclohexene oxide, then adding carbon dioxide to reaction system at a pressure of 3-6 MPa, stirring and heating to 80-150 degrees C for 8-50 hours, stopping stirring and heating, cooling to the room temperature, discharging the residual gas, taking out the polymer product, dissolving product by dichloromethane, filtering to remove residual catalyst and then precipitating with methanol, carrying out centrifugal separation and vaccum drying resultant product at constant weight 80 degrees C to obtain poly(cyclohexene carbonate).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) dissolving 2,3-dimethoxybenzaldehyde and cyclohexanediamine in a methanol solution and adding solution of 2,3-dimethoxybenzaldehyde in methanol to cyclohexanediamine while stirring at room temperature, then heating mixture, refluxing, stirring for 1-2 hours, then filtering off the precipitate and drying to obtain 2,3-dimethoxybenzaldehyde cyclohexanediamine ligand, and (ii) taking 2,3-dimethoxybenzaldehyde cyclohexanediamine ligand in a flask, vacuuming and filling nitrogen for three times, then replacing air and water in flask, cooling in an ice-salt bath and adding diethyl zinc under the protection of nitrogen, stirring for 1 hours, removing ice salt bath, heating to 60 degrees C and continuously stirring for 1-2 hours to obtain (I). Preferred Components: In step (i), the molar ratio between 2,3-dimethoxybenzaldehyde and cyclohexanediamine is 1:0.4-0.6. In step (i), the molar ratio between 2,3-dimethoxybenzaldehyde cyclohexanediamine and diethyl zinc is 1:1-1.25. The mass ratio between (I) and cyclohexene oxide is 1:100-400.

PN WO2022069568-A1
TI Conversion of gas stream comprising carbon dioxide into ester, in production of polyester-based hydrogel, involves passing carbon dioxide through water bath, passing through metal ion exchange bubble column comprising metal oxalate, and reacting produced oxalic acid with mono-alcohol.
AU NENU N C
   GUO J
   KLEMT A
   SCHOLZ S
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 2022484068
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of carbon dioxide gas stream (105) comprising carbon dioxide into ester involves (i) converting carbon dioxide into oxalic acid, by passing carbon dioxide through a water bath to produce a carbonated water, and passing the carbonated water through a metal ion exchange bubble column (110) comprising a metal oxalate to produce oxalic acid and metal bicarbonate, and (ii) reacting the oxalic acid with a mono-alcohol to obtain ester.
   USE - Conversion of gas stream comprising carbon dioxide into ester e.g. dimethyl oxalate or diethyl oxalate, used in production of polyester-based hydrogel (all claimed) which is used to sequester carbon in soils and to increase and protect existing soil carbon level to promote crop growth.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and large-scale conversion of gas stream comprising carbon dioxide into ester, with minimized waste-products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Conversion of carbon dioxide gas stream comprising carbon dioxide into ester involves (i) converting carbon dioxide into oxalic acid, by passing carbon dioxide through a water bath to produce a carbonated water, and passing the carbonated water through a metal ion exchange bubble column comprising a metal oxalate of formula: M2(COO)2 to produce oxalic acid and metal bicarbonate of formula: MHCO3, and (ii) reacting the oxalic acid with a mono-alcohol to obtain ester.
   M = Na and/or K.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system (100) for manufacturing ester from carbon dioxide gas stream, which comprises a carbon dioxide conversion unit configured to convert the carbon dioxide into oxalic acid, metal ion exchange bubble column to produce oxalic acid and metal bicarbonate, and a reactor (115) for reacting the oxalic acid with mono-alcohol to obtain ester. The reactor comprises an activated carbon bed configured to receive oxalic acid and mono-alcohol to generate the ester.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the system.
   System (100)
   Carbon dioxide gas stream (105)
   Metal ion exchange bubble column (110)
   Reactor (115)
   Crystallization unit (120)
   Dryer or inert treatment reactor (125)
   Polymerization reactor (130)
   Hydrogenation tank (145)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The system further comprises a polymerization reactor (130) configured to receive the ester and combine the ester with a glycerin and acid catalyst to produce compound (I) and ethanol, a hydrogenation reactor (145) connected to the conversion unit and configured to receive the metal bicarbonate from the conversion unit and to combine the metal bicarbonate with hydrogen gas and a palladium catalyst to produce a mixture comprising metal bicarbonate and metal formate, a crystallization unit (120) configured to receive the mixture from the hydrogenation reactor and to separate the mixture through into separated metal bicarbonate and separated metal formate, and a dryer/inert treatment reactor (125) configured to receive the separated metal formate from the crystallization unit and to treat the separated metal formate with potassium hydroxide to produce hydrogen gas and dried metal oxalate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process (ii) involves passing the oxalic acid and mono-alcohol through an activated carbon bed to generate ester. The process further involves (iii) combining glycerin, an acid catalyst comprising sulfuric acid and the ester to produce a polyester-based hydrogel comprising a structure of formula (I) and a mono-alcohol, (iv) combining the metal bicarbonate with a hydrogen gas in a hydrogenation reactor comprising a palladium catalyst at a temperature of 15-100 degrees C and a pressure of 0.1-100 bar to produce a mixture comprising metal formate of formula: HCOOM e.g. potassium formate or sodium formate and metal bicarbonate, (v) separating the mixture through fractional crystallization in a crystallization unit into separated metal bicarbonate and separated metal formate, (vi) feeding the separated metal bicarbonate into the hydrogenation reactor, (vii) treating the separated metal formate with a catalytic amount of metal hydroxide of formula: MOH, preferably potassium hydroxide and/or sodium hydroxide at a temperature of 100-400 degrees C to produce a hydrogen gas and dried metal oxalate e.g. disodium oxalate or dipotassium oxalate, (viii) transferring the hydrogen gas to the hydrogenation reactor, and (ix) transferring the dried metal oxalate to the metal ion exchange bubble column.n = 1-5000, preferably plus minus 250.N.B: n is defined in the disclosure, but not in the claims.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   A method for electrochemically reducing carbon                dioxide, which involves: (a) electrochemically                reducing carbon dioxide gas and dissolving                unreacted carbon dioxide gas in an electrolyte in a                state, where the electrolyte composed of a strong                alkaline aqueous solution is accommodated in a                first liquid flow path in which an inlet and an                outlet between a first cathode and a first anode                are closed, and the carbon dioxide gas is                accommodated in a first gas flow path in which an                inlet and an outlet on a side of the first cathode                opposite to the first anode is closed, and (b)                electrochemically reducing dissolved carbon dioxide                in the electrolyte in a second liquid flow path by                moving the electrolyte in which carbon dioxide                dissolves from the first liquid flow path to the                second liquid flow path between a second cathode                and a second anode, where average value of voltages                are applied between the first cathode and the first                anode during the reduction of the carbon dioxide                gas is set to be lower than an average value of                voltages applied between the second cathode and the                second anode during the reduction of the dissolved                carbon dioxide.
   A method for producing carbon compounds by                electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide.

TI Ultra-critical carbon dioxide expansion-type cycle power generating system, has main compressor for lifting ultra-critical carbon dioxide pressure through cooler, and main expander for expanding ultra-critical carbon dioxide through heater.
AU JIANG Y
   GUO C
   HU H
   GUO Y
   LIANG S
   YUE P
   ZENG M
AE INST ENG THERMOPHYSICS CHINESE (CAET-C)
GA 2021122345
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a cooler (101) for cooling and absorbing heat of ultra-critical carbon dioxide. A main compressor (401) lifts ultra-critical carbon dioxide pressure after passing through the cooler to high pressure. A heat regenerator (301) absorbs reflux heat of the ultra-critical carbon dioxide after boosting by the main compressor. A heater (201) heats the ultra-critical carbon dioxide after passing through the heat regenerator to high temperature. A pre-compressor (402) compresses pressure of the carbon dioxide after passing through the heat regenerator to critical pressure intensity. A main expander (501) expands ultra-critical carbon dioxide through the heater.
   USE - Ultra-critical carbon dioxide expansion-type cycle power generating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can flexibly switch power generation circulation mode according to electric load size based on Brayton cycle principle so as to improve flexibility of a power generation system peak and effectively increase power generation efficiency of the cycle power generation system and peak amplitude, thus ensuring system cycle power generation efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an ultra-critical carbon dioxide expansion-type cycle power generating method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an ultra-critical carbon dioxide expansion-type cycle power generating system.
   Cooler (101)
   Heater (201)
   Heat regenerator (301)
   Main compressor (401)
   Pre-compressor (402)
   Main expander (501)

TI Carbon dioxide collecting system consists of absorption tower, regeneration tower, absorption tower exhaust gas passage component, cleaning component for injecting cleaning liquid into absorption tower exhaust gas, and injection unit.
AU HANDA Y
   FUJITA K
   UTATSU M
   MURAOKA D
   KITAMURA H
   KATO Y
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA ENERGY SYSTEMS KK (TOKE-C)
GA 202049297P
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide collecting system consists of an absorption tower (2) for discharging the absorption tower exhaust gas containing the process target gas from which the carbon dioxide is removed and contacting the process target gas containing carbon dioxide and an absorbing liquid which absorbs the carbon dioxide, regeneration tower for discharging the absorption liquid which diffuses the carbon dioxide from the absorption liquid discharged from the absorption tower and a regeneration tower exhaust gas containing the carbon dioxide, an absorption tower exhaust gas passage component comprising at least one hole through which the absorption tower exhaust gas passes, cleaning component (3) for injecting the cleaning liquid into the absorption tower exhaust gas which passes through the holes, and an injection unit for cleaning the absorption tower exhaust gas with the cleaning liquid.
   USE - Carbon dioxide collecting system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide collecting system is capable of reducing an absorption liquid component accompanying a process target gas from which carbon dioxide is removed by an absorption tower.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of operating a carbon dioxide collecting system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the structure of the carbon dioxide collecting system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Waste gas discharge installation (1)
   Absorption tower (2)
   Cleaning component (3)
   Rich liquid pump (4)
   Regenerated heat exchanger (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The absorption tower exhaust gas passage component partitions the space in the cleaning section into an area (A1) and area (A2). The absorption tower exhaust gas passes from area (A1) to the area (A2) through the holes. The cleaning liquid flows from the injection unit to the area (A1) through the holes. The cleaning component re-injects the cleaning liquid which flows to the area (A1) from the injection unit into the exhaust gas of the absorption tower passing through the holes. The injection unit comprises one or more sprays for injecting the cleaning liquid in a mist form into the absorption tower exhaust gas passing through the holes. The injection unit comprises two or more sprays for one hole of the absorption tower exhaust gas passage component. The carbon dioxide collecting system comprises spraying unit for spraying the cleaning liquid, and the direction in which the cleaning liquid is sprayed and direction in which the absorption tower exhaust gas flows are opposed to each other. The injection unit is provided in the hole and injects the cleaning liquid in a direction away from a wall surface of the hole, and in a direction toward a wall surface of the hole. The carbon dioxide collecting system further comprises packed bed through which the absorption tower exhaust gas and the cleaning liquid are passed, and cleaning component (C2) for cleaning the absorption tower exhaust gas in the packed bed with the cleaning liquid. Preferred Properties: The direction in which the cleaning liquid is injected from the injection unit is parallel to the straight line (L1). The direction in which the absorption tower exhaust gas flows in the holes is parallel to the straight line (L2). The angle between the straight lines (L1) and (L2) is 10 degrees or more, preferably 80 degrees or less.

GA 202239812B
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide recovery system comprises                adsorption tower (20) to absorb carbon dioxide in                carbon dioxide-containing gas into the absorption                liquid, regeneration tower (30) for introducing the                absorption liquid absorbed carbon dioxide in the                absorption tower (20), and regenerates the                absorption liquid by separating carbon dioxide from                the absorption liquid using the regeneration vapor,                a supply pipe (I) to supply the absorption liquid                regenerated in the regeneration tower (30) to the                absorption tower (20), and a portion of the                absorption liquid regenerated in the regeneration                tower (30) to remove the deteriorated material, and                a reclaimer to supply the absorption liquid from                which the deterioration material is removed to the                supply pipe (I).
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery system used for                reducing emissions of carbon dioxide emitted to                outside air during processing of exhaust gas in                thermal power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide recovery system for                removing a deteriorated material from an absorption                liquid by simple configuration or control.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon dioxide recovery system comprises                adsorption tower (20) to absorb carbon dioxide in                carbon dioxide-containing gas into the absorption                liquid, regeneration tower (30) for introducing the                absorption liquid absorbed carbon dioxide in the                absorption tower (20), and regenerates the                absorption liquid by separating carbon dioxide from                the absorption liquid using the regeneration vapor,                a supply pipe (I) to supply the absorption liquid                regenerated in the regeneration tower (30) to the                absorption tower (20), and a portion of the                absorption liquid regenerated in the regeneration                tower (30) to remove the deteriorated material, and                a reclaimer to supply the absorption liquid from                which the deterioration material is removed to the                supply pipe (I), an in-line viscometer (70) for                measuring the viscosity of the absorption liquid                circulating the supply pipe (I) and a control                portion (80) for controlling the treatment amount                of the absorption liquid in the reclaimer based on                the viscosity measured by the in-line viscometer                (70).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                carbon recovery system (the drawing includes                Non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide recovery system (1)
   Adsorption tower (20)
   Regenerated tower (30)
   In-line viscosity (70)
   Control portion (80)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Properties: The control portion                  (80) changes the treatment amount of the                  absorption liquid in the reclaimer in which the                  viscosity measured by the in-line viscometer (70)                  exceeds predetermined threshold value which                  starts the treatment with respect to the                  absorption liquid in the reclaimer, and increases                  the throughput of the absorption liquid in the                  reclaimer when the viscosity measured by the                  in-line viscometer (70) exceeds the threshold                  value, supply pipe (II) for supplying a portion                  of absorption liquid regenerated in the                  regeneration tower (30) to the reclaimer, and a                  flow rate (A) regulating valve installed in the                  supply piping (II), the control portion (80)                  adjusts the flow rate of the absorption liquid to                  the flow rate (A) control valve to change the                  treatment amount of the absorption liquid in the                  reclaimer, and a supply pipe (III) installed in                  supply pipe (I) to introduce a portion of the                  absorption liquid flowing in the supply pipe (I)                  and returning again to the supply pipe (I), and a                  flow rate (B) regulating valve installed on the                  upstream side of the supply pipe (III) and a                  filter installed on the downstream side of the                  supply pipe (III) when the viscosity measured by                  the in-line viscometer (70) exceeds the threshold                  value to adjusts the flow rate of the absorption                  liquid to the flow rate (B) regulating valve and                  increases the liquid passing amount of the                  absorbing liquid in the filter.

TI Carbon dioxide capture and direct utilization            using dual function catalyst-absorber comprises            injecting gas containing carbon dioxide into first            reactor including inlet and outlet and having dry            absorbent containing catalyst.
AU CHNAG K J
   LEE S C
   CHO S
   KIM T
   WOO J

   KANG H
AE UNIV KYUNGPOOK NAT IND ACAD COOP FOUND (UKYK-C)
GA 202226659R
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide capture and direct utilization                using dual function catalyst-absorber comprises                injecting a gas containing carbon dioxide into a                first reactor including an inlet and an outlet and                having a dry absorbent containing a catalyst                disposed, and collecting carbon dioxide by the dry                absorbent, disposing the dry absorbent in which the                carbon dioxide is captured in a second reactor                having a separate space from the first reactor,                hydrogen or methane gas is injected to react with                the dry absorbent in which the carbon dioxide is                captured to produce a product.
   USE - The method is useful for carbon dioxide                capture and direct utilization using dual function                catalyst-absorber.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is environmentally friendly;                prevents loss of water; and maintains the                reactivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first step and the                  second step are alternately and repeatedly                  performed to increase the carbon dioxide capture                  and product production efficiency. The first and                  second steps are carried out at 500-700 degrees                  C. Preferred Components: The catalyst is nickel,                  and ruthenium. The dry absorbent is calcium                  oxide, potassium carbonate and lithium silicate.                  The dry absorbent comprising the catalyst is                  prepared by sol-gel method specifically preparing                  a mixed solution by stirring the hydrate of the                  catalyst metal, the hydrate of the dry absorbent,                  and the gelling agent, and calcining after drying                  the mixed solution. The hydrate of the catalyst                  metal is nickel nitrate dehydrate. The hydrate of                  the dry absorbent is calcium nitrate trihydrate.                  The content of nickel is 5-10 wt.%. The dry                  absorbent comprising the catalyst prepared                  through the sol-gel method comprise                  calcium-nickel oxide.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of materials of                dry absorbent that do not support the claims.

   USE - Used as carbon clean potent gas cleaning agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The agent captures and recycles the carbon dioxide tail gas generated by the enterprise production, and reduces the discharge of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the cleaning agent, comprising (i) capturing and recycling carbon dioxide tail gas generated by enterprise production by using carbon dioxide capture system, (ii) storing the captured and recovered carbon dioxide in the reaction tank, (iii) adding nitrogen, helium and argon, and injecting nitrogen, helium and argon into the reaction tank storing carbon dioxide, and (iv) pressurizing the reaction tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the preparation method of the carbon clean potent gas cleaning agent (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The agent comprises 75% carbon dioxide, 15% nitrogen, 5% helium gas and 5% argon gas.Preferred Components: In the step (i), carbon dioxide capture is a physical absorption capture process using polyethylene glycol dimethyl ether as a capture reagent. In the step (iv), the pressure is 5.6-6.2 Mpa when the reaction tank is pressurized.Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the dosing device is used to inject it when the prepared gas cleaning agent is injected into the pipeline of the water circulation device, the dosing device comprises a gas injection pressurizing unit and a gas pressurizing connecting pipe unit fixedly connected to one end of the gas injection pressurizing unit, the gas injection and pressurization unit comprises a hydraulic cylinder and a fixed pipe fixedly welded on one end of the hydraulic cylinder, the fixed pipe is sleeved on the telescopic end of the hydraulic cylinder, and fix the welding push plate on the telescopic end of the hydraulic cylinder, a gas input assembly is also provided at one end of the hydraulic cylinder, the gas pressurization connecting pipe unit comprises a reinforced connecting pipe fixedly connected to one side of the gas input assembly and a reset blocking assembly movably installed at one end of the reinforced connecting pipe, a partition plate is fixedly welded at one end of the reinforcing connecting pipe, the partition plate divides the reinforcing connecting pipe into two parts, the pressure chamber and the installation chamber, a pipeline jack is provided at one end of the installation cavity, a blocking positioning slot is opened on one side of the partition plate, a through hole is opened in the blocking positioning groove, and an arc-shaped positioning notch is further opened on the groove wall of the blocking positioning groove.

TI Power system for generating mechanical energy from carbon dioxide in closed loop, has differential orifice that is coupled to rotary engine and heat exchanger to decrease temperature and pressure of output supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU BAKER K C
AE BAKER K C (BAKE-Individual)
GA 2020454862
AB    NOVELTY - The power system (10) has a compressor (12) that is configured to increase a pressure and flow rate of the supercritical carbon dioxide in a high pressure supercritical carbon dioxide. A heat exchanger (14) is connected to compressor and pressure differential orifice (24) and that is configured to cross hot and a cold carbon dioxide stream from the compressor and pressure differential orifice. A rotary engine is connected to the heat exchanger and is configured to convert thermal energy from the high enthalpy supercritical carbon dioxide into mechanical energy and an output supercritical carbon dioxide. The pressure differential orifice is coupled to the rotary engine and heat exchanger to decrease the temperature and the pressure of the output supercritical carbon dioxide in a low pressure low temperature subcritical carbon dioxide. The low pressure low temperature supercritical carbon dioxide is heated in the heat exchanger and then enters the compressor completing the closed loop.
   USE - Power system for generating mechanical energy from subcritical and supercritical carbon dioxide in closed loop.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide stream from the accumulator tank pressure decreases from predefined value for equalizing the pressure of the low-pressure side of system and for increasing system pressure at three-way junction.

   Compressor (12)
   Heat exchanger (14)

   Rotor (50)

The pressure control relation of a corresponding proportional valve is obtained according to the concentration level of the carbon dioxide concentration value. The proportional valve of the gas water heater is controlled according to the obtained proportional valve pressure control relationship. Determination is made to check whether the carbon dioxide concentration value is greater than the preset threshold value. The gas water heater is controlled to stop working, and the alarm information is transmitted, when the carbon dioxide concentration value is greater than the preset threshold value.
   USE - Method for controlling a gas water heater (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables adjusting the proportional valve pressure value according to the carbon dioxide concentration value to ensure that the gas water heater keeps good combustion condition when operating, thus improving combustion efficiency, solving incomplete combustion, vibration combustion, waste gas discharge, and improving safety and use experience of the heater, and hence increasing customer satisfaction.

   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for controlling a gas water heater. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN113578305-A
TI Modified indium oxide catalyst used for preparing carbon monoxide, is prepared by indium oxide annealing treatment, where atmosphere of annealing treatment is hydrogen, and ensures high catalytic activity for carbon dioxide and enhanced energy efficiency.
AU DU Z
   SHI X
   ZHAO K
   WANG J
   LI S
   XIANG X
   ZHANG B
   CHENG G
AE UNIV HENAN (UYEH-C)
GA 2021C5566N
AB    NOVELTY - A modified indium oxide catalyst is prepared by indium oxide annealing treatment, where the atmosphere of annealing treatment is hydrogen.
   USE - Modified indium oxide catalyst used for preparing carbon monoxide used in preparation of carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Since hydrogen reacts with lattice oxygen on the surface of indium oxide to produce water, the concentration of oxygen vacancies on the surface of indium oxide is increased, and the electronic structure is adjusted, and charge transport and surface properties are utilized to promote carbon dioxide dissociation process. The increase of oxygen vacancy concentration makes the modified indium oxide catalyst have more electron accumulation at Fermi level, which is more conducive to occurrence of catalytic reduction reaction of carbon dioxide, the catalytic activity for carbon dioxide is high, and the energy efficiency of carbon dioxide conversion is high, and the obtained modified catalyst component is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing carbon monoxide by catalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, which involves utilizing reaction devices comprising friction nano generators, rectifier bridges and plasma components, where plasma assembly is comprised with discharge probe and working electrode plate, and one side of the working electrode plate is comprised to accommodate modified indium oxide catalyst. The alternating current input end of rectifier bridge is electrically connected with output end of friction nano generator, and the negative direct current (DC) output end of rectifier bridge is connected with discharge probe, and the positive direct output end of the rectifier bridge is connected with working electrode plate, after the reaction device is energized, a triboelectric plasma is generated, and the triboelectric plasma is mixed with carbon dioxide to perform catalytic reduction reaction to obtain carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The temperature of annealing treatment is controlled at 200-600degreesC for 1-10 hour. The temperature of catalytic reduction reaction is controlled at 5-40degreesC and pressure is 0.1-10 MPa.Preferred Composition: The loading amount of modified indium oxide catalyst on the surface of working electrode plate is 0.01-10 mg/cm2.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The friction nano-generator is independent-layer rotating friction nano-generator.Preferred Conditions: The rotation speed of friction nano generator is 10-1000 rpm. The discharging mode of triboelectric nanogenerator is direct current discharge of power generation spark. The discharge voltage of friction nano generator is 1-10 kV, and the discharge current is 10-100 muA. The distance between discharge probe and working electrode plate is less than or equal to 1 mm. The curvature of discharge probe is 1-10 mum.

The water outlet of the heat exchange device is connected to one end of the low-temperature return water return pipeline (4). One end of the carbon dioxide injection pipeline (6) is connected to a carbon dioxide storage tank, and the other end extends to the bottom of the natural gas hydrate reservoir. One end of the methane pumping pipeline (7) is placed on the top of the gas hydrate reservoir, and the other end is connected to the methane storage tank. The carbon dioxide injection pipeline and the methane gas extraction pipeline are placed in the inner cavity of the heat exchange device.
   USE - Device for developing natural gas hydrate.
   ADVANTAGE - The device realizes the effective cooling of power plant tail water/tail steam and carbon dioxide emission reduction, improves the mining efficiency of submarine natural gas hydrate, ensures the long-term stability and development of the energy supply system and reduces the thermal excitation cost during the mining process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for developing natural gas hydrate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic diagram of the structure of the device for developing natural gas hydrate. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   High temperature water vapor injection pipeline (1)
   Low temperature return water return pipeline (4)
   Drain pump (5)
   Carbon dioxide injection pipeline (6)
   Methane pumping pipeline (7)

AB    NOVELTY - Aqueous fire extinguishing agent suitable for water mist comprises water, sodium chloride, a chlorine dioxide disinfectant or a chlorine dioxide activator and carbon dioxide gas. The fire extinguishing agent is prepared by mixing water and sodium chloride to obtain 0.9% sodium chloride aqueous solution containing sodium chloride, adding the chlorine dioxide disinfectant or the chlorine dioxide activator in a weight ratio of 0.001-0.005%, placing the obtained extinguishing agent in a storage tank, pressing 1 l aqueous fire extinguishing agent to dissolve 7 l carbon dioxide gas to dissolve carbon dioxide under pressure to obtain the extinguishing agent finished product.
   USE - As aqueous fire extinguishing agent suitable for water mist.
   ADVANTAGE - The fire extinguishing agent has no harm to human body, long-acting antibacterial effect, low manufacturing cost and strong practicability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the fire extinguishing agent involving (i) using distilled water or purified water, adding 0.9% analytically pure sodium chloride solids, fully stirring and dissolving, adding 0.001-0.005% chlorine dioxide effervescent tablet and leaving still for 20-25 minutes and (ii) injecting the fire extinguishing agent pre-configured in step (i) into a fire extinguishing agent storage tank, closing the storage tank outlet valve, starting the fire extinguishing device control system, automatically starting a carbon dioxide pressurizing pump to inject carbon dioxide gas, replacing air in the upper space of the storage tank, closing a liquid supplementing port valve after 30 seconds of replacement, pressurizing the storage tank to 1.2 MPa, automatically stopping, controlling 1 l fire extinguishing agent to dissolve 7 l carbon dioxide gas at 1MP room temperature and obtaining the finished product of canned aqueous fire extinguishing agent.

AU ZHANG H

   WANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202112361N
AB    NOVELTY - Biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyurethane elastomer comprises 60-110 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-based polyol, 22-35 pts. wt. aliphatic diisocyanate, 0.002-0.01 pts. wt. catalyst, 0.1-0.8 pts. wt. antioxidant, 0.2-0.8 pts. wt. dihydroxy compound containing sulfonate and 3.5-18 pts. wt. chain extender.
   USE - Used as biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyurethane elastomer.
   ADVANTAGE - The elastomer: improves the hydrophilicity of the polyurethane elastomer material by introducing the sulfonate with a strong hydrophilic group; enhances hydrolyzability, and the biodegradability; has number average molecular weight of the polyurethane elastomer of 5.8-82,000, and the weight average molecular weight of 7.6-115,000, tensile strength is 14-25 MPa, and elongation at break is 650-820%; and reaches the degradation rate to 92%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyurethane elastomer, comprising (i) heating the carbon dioxide-based polyol to 85-105 degrees C, reducing pressure for 1-2 hours, adding aliphatic diisocyanate, and reacting for 1-3 hours, (ii) adding catalyst, antioxidant, and sulfonate-containing dihydroxy compound to the reaction product obtained in the step (i) and continuously reacting for 0.5-1.5 hours, and(iii) heating the reaction product obtained in the step (ii) to 120-150 degrees C, and adding chain extender, reacting for 1-3 hours to obtain biodegradable carbon dioxide-based polyurethane elastomer.

PN CN111562247-A
TI Method for facilitating online analysis of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide, involves determining concentration of carbon dioxide in target natural raw material gas according to relational expression.
AU ZHU H
   SUN X
   ZHOU L
   LU D
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2020829793
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves outputting target natural raw material gas using a natural raw material gas output piece. Gas-liquid separation treatment is performed in the target natural raw material gas using a gas-liquid separator. The target natural raw material gas is filtered using a filter after performing gas-liquid separation treatment. The target natural raw material gas is stabilized by a voltage stabilizer after performing filtering process. Actual Raman spectrum of hydrogen sulfide is detected using a laser Raman analyzer in the target natural raw material gas after performing voltage stabilizing process. Actual Raman spectrum of carbon dioxide and normal butane is determined according to the actual Raman spectrum of the hydrogen sulfide. Concentration of the hydrogen sulfide is determined in the target natural raw material gas. Concentration of carbon dioxide is determined in the target natural raw material gas according to relational expression and the actual Raman spectrum.
   USE - Method for facilitating online analysis of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method determines concentration of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide in target natural raw material gas and satisfies requirement of timely adjustment of desulfurization carbon process of purifying plant.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for facilitating online analysis of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for facilitating online analysis of hydrogen sulfide and carbon dioxide (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN109675586-A; CN109675586-B
TI Catalyst for electroreduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid used to produce electroformed carbon dioxide for formic acid, comprises chalcogen element including sulfur, selenium and tellurium, and metal including indium, tin, and antimony.
AU XIE S
   MA W
   ZHANG Q
   WANG Y
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
   UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 201940788G
AB    NOVELTY - The catalyst is a chalcogen-doped metal electrocatalyst. The chalcogen element includes sulfur, selenium and tellurium. The metal includes indium, tin, lead, and antimony.
   USE - Catalyst for electroreduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid used to produce electroformed carbon dioxide for formic acid (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has high reactivity, high selectivity, stable catalytic performance, and maintains high formic acid selectivity over a wide current range.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of catalyst for preparing electroformed carbon dioxide formic acid, which involves (1) dissolving simple or chalcogen compound and a metal salt of chalcogen element in N,N-dimethylformamide, placing carbon material and finally transferring to an autoclave for solvent heat treatment, (2) heat-treating the solvent, taking out and washing carbon material with deionized water, drying, obtaining chalcogen-doped metal oxide precursor supported by carbon material, and then electrically reducing to obtain a chalcogen-doped metal electrocatalyst supported by carbon material; and
   (2) application of catalyst for electroreduction of carbon dioxide to formic acid used to produce electroformed carbon dioxide for formic acid, which involves carrying out reaction in an H-type electrolytic cell, using chalcogen-doped metal electrocatalyst as a cathode, and using platinum sheet or graphite as an anode, using saturated calomel electrode as a reference electrode, passing carbon dioxide into the catholyte, and applying negative potential for electrolysis to obtain formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon material includes carbon paper, carbon cloth, graphite flakes, foamed carbon, and/or carbon nanotubes. The carbon material is carbon paper. The metal salt is chloride, nitrate, and/or an acetylacetonate of indium, tin, lead, and antimony. Preferred Composition: The content of the chalcogen element is from 0.01-25 mol.%. The carbon dioxide flow rate is 5-100 mL/min. The electrolyte concentration is 0.1-2 M. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of solvent heat treatment is 130-200 degrees C, the heat treatment time of solvent is 12-48 hours, and the electric reduction treatment potential is -0.98 V vs.RHE. The negative potential is -0.33 to -1.23 V vs.RHE.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound of the chalcogen element is sulfur powder, thiourea, sodium sulfide, thioacetamide, selenium powder, sodium selenite, tellurium powder, and/or telluric acid. The electrolyte includes sodium hydrogen carbonate, potassium hydrogen carbonate, cesium bicarbonate, and potassium sulfate

TI System for carbon neutral cycle of natural gas            production has molten salt reactor to generate zero            carbon dioxide emissions electricity, desalination            unit, electrolysis unit, oxy-combustion unit, and            catalytic hydrogenation unit.
AU WANG Y
   AL-QASIM A S
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202293440A
AB    NOVELTY - A system for a carbon neutral cycle of natural                gas production, comprises molten salt reactor (114)                configured to generate zero carbon dioxide                emissions electricity; desalination unit (116)                configured to receive the zero-carbon dioxide                emissions electricity from the molten salt reactor                and produce a desalinated water; electrolysis unit                (105) configured to be powered by the zero-carbon                dioxide emissions electricity generated by the                molten salt reactor and generate hydrogen and                oxygen from the desalinated water; oxy-combustion                unit (108) configured to receive and combust a                hydrocarbon fuel with the oxygen from the                electrolysis unit to produce electricity and carbon                dioxide; carbon dioxide capture system adapted to                capture carbon dioxide produced by the                oxy-combustion unit; and catalytic hydrogenation                unit (103) configured to receive and convert                hydrogen from electrolysis unit and carbon dioxide                from carbon dioxide capture system to produce                hydrocarbon fuel.
   USE - A system for a carbon neutral cycle of natural                gas production.
   ADVANTAGE - Carbon dioxide may be captured and converted                into useful industrial products. The driving energy                of the carbon dioxide conversion is clean                electricity generated with zero carbon dioxide                emission operation, such as molten salt reactor                operations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon                neutral cycle of a natural gas production, which                involves generating electricity with a molten salt                reactor configured to generate zero carbon dioxide                emissions; powering a desalination unit with the                electricity from the molten salt reactor, producing                desalinated water with the desalination unit;                producing hydrogen and oxygen from the desalinated                water with an electrolysis unit; introducing the                hydrogen produced by the electrolysis unit to a                catalytic hydrogenation unit; reacting captured                carbon dioxide and hydrogen generated from the                desalination unit by catalytic hydrogenation in a                catalytic hydrogenation unit, where the reaction                produces a hydrocarbon fuel; introducing the                hydrocarbon fuel into an oxy-combustion unit;                producing carbon dioxide in the oxy-combustion unit                by reacting the hydrocarbon fuel with the oxygen                from the electrolysis unit; capturing carbon                dioxide from the oxy-combustion unit; and                introducing the captured to the catalytic                hydrogenation unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of a carbon                dioxide utilization and value creation                process.

PN CN113886461-A
TI Monitoring method to influence carbon dioxide concentration on surface temperature, involves performing similarity analysis coupling network analysis to surface temperature and carbon dioxide concentration network to obtain influence parameter of carbon dioxide concentration on surface temperature.
AU LI X
   YANG L
   CUI Q
   LIU Y
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI SCI & DEV INST (CHSC-Non-standard)
GA 202212538R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves calculating (S101) temperature related coefficient and temperature connection weight between each geographic node according to surface sampling temperature. The concentration correlation coefficient and concentration connection weight of each geographic node are calculated according to carbon dioxide sampling concentration. A ground temperature network is established (S102), according to temperature correlation coefficient, temperature connection weight and geographic distance of the geographic node, and filter geographic node. Carbon dioxide concentration network is established (S103), according to the concentration correlation coefficient, concentration connection weight and the geographic distance. Similarity analysis coupling network analysis is performed (S104) to the surface temperature network and the carbon dioxide concentration network, so as to obtain the influence parameter of the carbon dioxide concentration on the surface temperature.
   USE - Monitoring method to influence carbon dioxide concentration on surface temperature in weather climate application technology field.
   ADVANTAGE - The influence of the carbon dioxide concentration on the surface temperature in the parameter dimension is displayed through the surface sampling temperature of each geographic node and information processing of carbon dioxide sampling concentration, by means of network analysis, thus providing more scientific reference or guidance for the distribution of measurement and emission reduction responsibility of future carbon emission space.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: (1) a monitoring device to influence carbon dioxide concentration on surface temperature; (2) a computer storage medium storing program for monitoring to influence carbon dioxide concentration on surface temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the carbon dioxide concentration of surface temperature of influence monitoring method. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for obtaining the ground sampling temperature and carbon dioxide sampling concentration of each geographic node (S100)
   Step for calculating the temperature related coefficient and temperature connection weight between each geographic node according to the surface sampling temperature (S101)
   Step for establishing ground temperature network according to the temperature correlation coefficient, the temperature connection weight and the geographic distance of the geographic node, and filter geographic node (S102)
   Step for establishing carbon dioxide concentration network according to the concentration correlation coefficient, concentration connection weight and the geographic distance (S103)
   Step for similarity analysis coupling network analysis to the surface temperature network and the carbon dioxide concentration network, so as to obtain the influence parameter of the carbon dioxide concentration on the surface temperature (S104)

PT P
PN CN109809420-A; CN109809420-B
TI Preparing polymeric aluminum silicate ferric flocculant comprises e.g. pulverizing coal gangue and fly ash, mixing mixed raw material, calcining, adding calcined clinker, passing carbon dioxide-rich gas to filtrate and mixing filter residue.
AU YU Y
   SONG L
AE UNIV LINYI (UNLY-C)
   UNIV LINYI (UNLY-C)
GA 2019495037
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polymeric aluminum silicate ferric flocculant comprises (i) pulverizing coal gangue, fly ash and red mud by a ball mill to 100 mesh or more as a mixed raw material; (ii) mixing the mixed raw material in the step (i) with the industrial soda ash to prepare a raw material; (iii) calcining the raw material obtained in the step (ii) in an oxygen-enriched air atmosphere at 700-950 degrees C for 1-3 hours to obtain a clinker and subjecting the tail gas to heat exchange to obtain a carbon dioxide-rich gas, which is obtained after heat exchange; (iv) adding calcined clinker to 3 times the mass of water and continuously stirring at 90 degrees C for 2 hours; (v) passing the carbon dioxide-rich gas obtained in the step (iii) to the filtrate A for a carbonation reaction; (vi) mixing the filter residue B; (vii) adding the filtrate C obtained in the step (v) to an equal volume of water; (viii) placing polysilicic acid obtained in the step (vii) in the reaction vessel and slowly adding the solution F.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polymeric aluminum silicate ferric flocculant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, economical and environmentally-friendly, saves resources, improves the utilization rate of the heat energy, realizes the recycling of the activator and consumes low energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing polymeric aluminum silicate ferric flocculant comprises (i) pulverizing coal gangue, fly ash and red mud by a ball mill to 100 mesh or more as a mixed raw material, mixing silicon/aluminum/iron in the raw material having the weight ratio of 5-30/20-50/10-40; (ii) mixing the mixed raw material in the step (i) with the industrial soda ash to prepare a raw material, where the weight ratio of the soda ash to the mixed raw material in the raw material is 1:(1-3); (iii) calcining the raw material obtained in the step (ii) in an oxygen-enriched air atmosphere at 700-950 degrees C for 1-3 hours to obtain a clinker and subjecting the tail gas to heat exchange to obtain a carbon dioxide-rich gas, which is obtained after heat exchange, using hot gas in the concentrated crystallization step of the subsequent solution; (iv) adding the calcined clinker to 3 times the mass of water and continuously stirring at 90 degrees C for 2 hours, separating solid-liquid, immersing the filtrate in the next batch of calcined clinker and continuously stirring at 90 degrees C for 2 hours, so that the cycle is 3-10 times and obtaining the filtrate A and the filter residue B respectively; (v) passing the carbon dioxide-rich gas obtained in the step (iii) to the filtrate A for a carbonation reaction, where the reaction pressure is 0.35 MPa, the reaction temperature is 40 degrees C and after completing the reaction, sufficiently stirring the solid-liquid separation for 1 hour to obtain a filtrate C and a filter residue D; (vi) mixing the filter residue B, the filter residue D and the calcium aluminate and crushing and then adding 3-10% industrial hydrochloric acid for reaction, reacting at 60-80 degrees C and is allowing to stand the reaction for 2 hours to obtain a solution F; (vii) adding the filtrate C obtained in the step (v) to an equal volume of water and stirring uniformly and then introducing carbon dioxide-rich gas for secondary carbonation reaction, where the reaction pressure is 0.35 MPa, the reaction temperature is 40 degrees C and fully stirring the reaction for 1 hour, separating the solid and liquid to obtain a polysilicic acid and sodium carbonate solution, concentrating the sodium carbonate solution and crystallizing by the hot gas after the heat exchange in the step (iii) and then recycling to prepare the raw material in the step (ii); (viii) placing polysilicic acid obtained in the step (vii) in the reaction vessel and slowly adding the solution F, where the stirring speed is not less than 150 revolutions/minute, the reaction temperature is 40 degrees C and adjusting the pH of the solution to 3.5-4, after adding the solution F and allowing to stand for 6-12 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the soda ash to the mixed raw material in the step (ii) is 1:1.5. The mass of the calcium aluminate in the step (vi) is 2-5% of the total mass of the filter residue B and the filter residue D. Preferred Method: The step (iii) is carried out in an oxygen-enriched air at a calcination temperature of 900 degrees C. In the step (iii) passing the exhaust gas through two heat exchange processes and after the first process, obtaining a high temperature hot gas of 550-700 degrees C and after the second process, obtaining a medium temperature hot gas of 150 to 300 degrees C and finally the concentration of carbon dioxide in the carbon dioxide-rich gas is not less than 23% and using the medium-high temperature hot gas and the medium-temperature hot gas in the sodium carbonate solution concentration crystallization step in the step (vii). In the step (iv) stopping the circulation leaching of the filtrate when the sodium content in the filtrate reaches 4.8-5.5 mol/l. In the step (v) preventing the carbon dioxide-rich gas from flowing into the filtrate A, when the pH of the solution reaches 12.5-12.7. The pH of the solution F after the end of the reaction is 2.2-2.5. In the step (vii) when the pH of the solution reaches 7-8.5, the secondary carbonation reaction ends.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); Q42 (Hydraulic engineering, soil shifting and sewerage (E02, 3))
