PN CN107779434-A
TI Experimental method for efficient amplification of            human peripheral blood natural killer cell involves            diluting blood, resuspending liquid with ficoll            separation solution, centrifuging content,            resuspending, culturing and amplifying cell.
AU ZHOU Y
   MA J
   ZHAO G
   FENG D
   XU R
   DI L
AE TIANJIN KANGTING BIOENGINEERING CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201823430P
AB    NOVELTY - Experimental method for efficient                amplification of human peripheral blood natural                killer (NK) cell involves centrifuging peripheral                blood, inactivating and collecting supernatant as                autologous serum, diluting blood with physiological                saline, resuspending liquid in centrifuge tube with                ficoll separation solution, centrifuging the                content, rinsing white film layer with normal                saline, centrifuging content and collecting cell                for flow cytometry, adjusting cell concentration,                centrifuging and resuspending cell, culturing cell                in vial and amplifying total number of cell.
   USE - The experimental method is useful for                efficient amplification of human peripheral blood                NK cell.
   ADVANTAGE - The method removes heterocysts and factors,                ensures obtaining lymphoid population through a                short period of cultivation and increases activity                and quantity of NK cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Experimental method for efficient                amplification of human peripheral blood natural                killer (NK) cell involves (a) centrifuging                peripheral blood at 800 revolutions per minute                (rpm) for 10 minutes, where centrifugation is                performed with 8 l of 4 drops method, inactivating                supernatant at 56 degrees C for 30 minutes and                centrifuging content for 2000 rpm for 10 minutes                using 8 l of 8 drops and collecting supernatant as                autologous serum, (b) diluting blood with                physiological saline, resuspending the liquid                slowly in a centrifuge tube with ficoll separation                solution, where volume ratio of ficoll to liquid is                3:4, centrifuging the content at 600 rpm for 40                minutes using 1 liter 0 drop method, (c) observing                and rinsing white film layer with normal saline,                centrifuging content at 400 rpm for 10 minutes                using 8 l of 4 drops method, repeating centrifuging                process twice, resuspending cell with saline,                counting and collecting suitable amount of cell for                flow cytometry to analyze the ratio of CD3-/CD56+                cell after initial cell sorting, (d) adjusting cell                concentration with the medium KBM501, inoculating                cell at a final cell concentration of 2x 106                cell/ml, adding 10-50 U/ml cytokine IL-15, 0.5-500                ng/ml cluster of differentiation (CD3), 50-100ng/ml                CD16 and 5% autologous serum, incubating content in                carbon dioxide incubator with an air carbon dioxide                content of 5% at 37 degrees C, (e) cultivating                content, until D3-D5 days, mixing and counting                content, horizontally centrifuging content at 300                rpm for 5 minutes (9 l 9 drops), collecting                supernatant, then regulating cell concentration                using cell-free KBM501 medium, where the medium                contains 5% autologous serum and inoculating with a                final cell concentration of 1.5x 106 cell/ml,                incubating content in carbon dioxide incubator with                a 5% carbon dioxide concentration at 37 degrees C,                (f) after culturing to D7 days, suspending and                mixing to count NK cell cultured after removing                D3-D5 cytokines, collecting 2x 106 cell for flow                cytometric detection of NK phenotype (CD3-/CD56+),                transferring remaining cell to a 50 ml centrifuge                tube and horizontally centrifuging content at 300                rpm for 5 minutes using 9 l and 9 drops method to                remove the original culture fluid and replacing the                fresh medium, where the composition of the medium                is KBM501 and KBM502 in a ratio of 1:1 which                contains 1% autologous serum to regulate cell                concentration, inoculating with a final cell                concentration of 1.5x 106 cell/ml, incubating                content in carbon dioxide incubator with a 5%                carbon dioxide concentration at 37 degrees C and                (g) culturing medicum to the number of cell to meet                the required level, centrifuging the content at 300                rpm for 5 minutes using 9 l 9 drop method,                collecting supernatant, resuspending the cell with                15 ml physiological saline, horizontally                centrifuging content at 300 rpm for 5 minutes,                performing centrifugation using a 9 l of 9 drops                method, removing supernatant and repeating                centrifugation process 4 times, resuspending the                cell in 40 ml saline, filtering the cell in an                80-mesh cell sieve into a 50-ml centrifuge tube,                counting and horizontally centrifuging the content                at 300 rpm for 5 minutes using 9 l 9 drop method,                removing supernatant and resuspending cell in 20 ml                normal saline from a 100 ml saline bottle using a                20 ml syringe, transferring the cell suspension to                the original vial with a 20 ml syringe, culturing                content for 14 days, amplifying total number of                cell 100-500 times and the number of NK cell is                amplified 1000-3000 times.

PN CN107764686-A
TI Accurately measuring volatile mass in coal comprises taking thallium as volatile component, squeezing by pressing machine, pre-combusting on crucible, adding air dried coal, vibrating crucible, leveling, coking, cutting, heating, and drying.
AU CHEN F
AE CHEN F (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2018198619
AB    NOVELTY - Accurately measuring volatile mass in coal comprises selecting a device tightly matched with a porcelain crucible cover, crucible frame, a pyrometer and a temperature adjusting device, taking thallium as volatile component, squeezing by pressing machine, pre-combusting crucible, weighing the air dried coal, slightly vibrating the crucible, leveling coal sample, placing on the crucible frame, lignite and coking coal pre-pressing cake, cutting, pre-heating the furnace, opening furnace door, heating, taking out the crucible from the furnace, air cooling, drying, weighing and calculating result.
   USE - The method is useful for accurately measuring volatile mass in coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the accuracy of the detection and improves the detection efficiency, so that the tested coal can meet the expected requirements when used.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Accurately measuring volatile mass in coal comprises (a) selecting a device tightly matched with a porcelain crucible cover, taking 12-18 g thallium as volatile component, selecting a pyrometer and a temperature adjusting device, maintain the temperature at 800 plus minus 10 degrees C, and a constant temperature area (at 800 plus minus 5 degrees C) in a muffle furnace as the furnace, the where the initial thermal capacity of the furnace is 820 degrees C, placing a crucible frame and multiple crucibles at room temperature, closing the furnace door, recovering to 800 plus minus 10 degrees C within 2 minutes, inserting a row of exhaust hole and a small thermocouple at the posterior wall of the furnace, where the small hole position is such that the thermocouple is inserted into the furnace between the hot junction at the bottom of the crucible and the furnace and is 20-300 mm away from the furnace, measuring constant temperature zone of the muffle furnace under the furnace door closed state, determining at least one half, calibrating pyrometers (including millivoltmeters and thermocouples) at least six months, where the crucible frame is made of nickel winding wire or other heat-resistant metal wire, its size is such that it can enable all to be in a constant temperature zone of the muffle furnace, the bottom of the crucible is set above the hot junction of the thermocouple and at the bottom of the furnace at a distance of 20-30 mm, selecting the crucible frame clamp and an analytical balance, where the inductance of the analytical balance is 0.0001 g, using screw-type or lever type cake press as a compressor, which can compress coal samples with a diameter of about 10 mm, using a stopwatch and dryer comprising allochroic silica gel or granular anhydrous calcium chloride (b) covering a porcelain enamel, pre-combusting to a constant quality at 900 degrees C, weighing 1 plus minus 0.01 g air-dried coal sample having particle size of less than 0.2 mm, accurate to 0.0002 g, gently vibrating the crucible, leveling the coal, covering the cover, placing lignite and long flame coal on the crucible rack, pre-pressing to cake, cutting into small blocks of about 3 mm, pre-heating the muffle furnace to 920 degrees C, opening the furnace door, and the vigor of the frame into the constant temperature area and closing the furnace door, heating for 7 minutes in the crucible and the frame after the furnace temperature is reduced, restoring the furnace temperature to 900 plus minus 10 degrees C within 3 minutes, otherwise rejecting this test, where the heating time includes temperature recovery time, taking out the pot from the oven, cool in the air for 5 minutes, transferring into a dryer and cooling to room temperature (about 20 minutes) and weighing and (c) calculating the air dried coal sample volatile component using the formula: (Vad is equal to m1/mx 100-Mad), when the air carbonate dioxide content in drying coal sample is 2-12%, then using the formula: (Vad is equal to m1/mx 100-Mad-(Carbon dioxide)ad), when the air carbonate dioxide in drying coal sample content is greater than 12%, then using the formula: (Vad is equal to m1/mx 100-Mad-(carbon dioxide)ad-(carbon dioxide)ad(coke residue)), where Vad is air dried coal sample volatile yield (in %); m1is the heated mass (in g), m is coal sample mass (in g), Mad is air dried coal sample moisture content (in %), (carbon dioxide)ad content is carbonate dioxide in air dried coal sample (in %), (carbon dioxide)ad(coke residue) is carbon dioxide content in coke residue (in %).

TI Method for sodium sulfide production and carbon dioxide recovery based on carbon dioxide plasma hot blast stove, comprises e.g. calcining and reducing Glauber's salt and charcoal raw materials in calcining kiln to generate sodium sulfide.
AU BAI Y
   AYINAXI H
   DI S
   TAO L
AE SHAANXI YIFEIHANG INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 202174299L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for sodium sulfide production and carbon dioxide recovery based on a carbon dioxide plasma hot blast stove, comprises (i) using carbon dioxide gas as the gas source to form carbon dioxide plasma hot air gas under the action of the plasma hot air furnace, (ii) feeding Glauber's salt and charcoal raw materials to the calcining kiln after pretreatment, using the carbon dioxide plasma hot air gas formed to provide heat to the calcining kiln, so that the temperature of the calcining kiln is 800-1100 degrees C, and calcining the glauber's salt and carbon raw materials and reducing to generate sodium sulfide products and carbon dioxide reaction gas, and (iii) collecting and cooling carbon dioxide reaction gas, recovering directly, partially returning as a gas source or partial return to the plasma hot blast furnace to adjust the gas temperature of the carbon dioxide plasma hot blast.
   USE - The method is useful for sodium sulfide production and carbon dioxide recovery based on carbon dioxide plasma hot blast stove.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides high temperature for calcination reduction, has high heating temperature, low energy consumption, and realizes clean production, has high carbon dioxide gas concentration, and can be directly recovered or recycled without processing, shortens the process flow and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for realizing the sodium sulfide production and carbon dioxide recovery method based on the carbon dioxide plasma hot blast stove, comprising a calcining kiln (1) and a plasma hot blast furnace (3). The plasma hot air furnace includes a furnace body (4) and a plasma generator (5) placed on the outer shell of the furnace body. The plasma generator is in communication with the inside of the furnace body. The furnace body is provided with a hot air gas outlet (7). The plasma generator is provided with a starting gas source inlet. The start gas source inlet is externally connected to a carbon dioxide gas path. The hot air gas outlet is connected to the starting gas source inlet via the calcining kiln. The calcining kiln is respectively provided with a material inlet and a material outlet. The material inlet is communicated with the material outlet.

TI Methane production system used in power generating plant, has methanation reactor which is configured to react desorbed carbon dioxide with hydrogen supplied from outside to convert into methane.
AU SHIGEO H
   RYOHEI I
   HIRONOBU K
   TAKASHI S
   HATAMIYA S
   INAGAKI R
   KOBAYASHI H
   SASAKI T
AE HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
   HITACHI LTD (HITA-C)
GA 2019364141
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a boiler (101) for generating steam. A steam turbine (102) is driven by the steam. A feed water heater (106) is provided for heating and supplying water to the boiler. A carbon dioxide recovery device is provided for recovering and desorbing carbon dioxide contained in the exhaust gas of the power generation facility. A methanation reactor (301) reacts the desorbed carbon dioxide with hydrogen supplied from the outside to convert into methane. The heat exchange reaction temperature during methane conversion of the methanation reactor by the steam is discharged from the steam turbine. The heated reaction heat is supplied to the hydrogen, the desorbed carbon dioxide and the carbon dioxide recovery device, and then supplied to the feed water heater.
   USE - Methane production system used in power generating plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The methane production system is provided which consume less energy as a whole system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The system has reaction heat that heats the hydrogen and the desorbed carbon dioxide to a temperature suitable for initiating the methane conversion reaction. The carbon dioxide recovery device is cooled by the exhaust gas. The recovery material in the carbon dioxide recovery device does not come in contact with the steam. The power generation facility is a coal-fired thermal power plant. A cooler is provided for cooling the desorbed carbon dioxide and the water vapor condensed is mixed with the carbon dioxide. The steam discharged from the steam turbine is supplied to the carbon dioxide recovery device. A methanation reactor cooler is arranged for cooling the temperature of the methanation reactor, and supplying the heat of reaction recovered by the methanation reactor cooler to the feed water heater. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for methane production method, involves providing power supply facility including a feed water heater for heating and supplying water to the boiler. The carbon dioxide recovery apparatus is provided for recovering and desorbing carbon dioxide contained in the exhaust gas of the power generation facility. The desorbed carbon dioxide is reacted with hydrogen supplied from the outside to convert the carbon dioxide to methane. The heat of reaction during methane conversion of the methanation reactor is increased by the steam discharged from the steam turbine. The heated reaction heat is supplied to the hydrogen, the desorbed carbon dioxide and the carbon dioxide recovery device, and then supplied to the feed water heater.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the structure illustrating the configuration of methane production system.
   Methane production system (100)
   Boiler (101)
   Steam turbine (102)
   Feed water heater (106)
   Methanation reactor (301)

AU CHEN Y
   FANG J
   ZOU L

   YANG H

     etching the silicon dioxide template, forming the                carbonized core-shell composite material into                mesoporous structure, filtering, thoroughly washing TI Separating and purifying of 14C isotopes from            nuclear waste, comprises e.g. condensing 14C-carbon            dioxide crude product, then evaporating to obtain pure            14C-carbon dioxide, exchanging and discharging            14C-carbon dioxide, and analyzing.
AU XU Z
   SHEN J
   SHUANG J
   GUI Y
   WU W
   XU W
   JIN Z
AE JIANGSU HUAYI TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   CHANGSHU ZHIHE ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202063807A
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and purifying of 14C isotopes from                nuclear waste, comprises (i) condensing 14C-carbon                dioxide crude product in two stages at -20 to 0                degrees C and -78.5 to -170 degrees C, then                evaporating orderly at -77 to 0 degrees C and -78.5                to 170 degrees C to obtain pure 14C-carbon dioxide,                and (ii) adding the obtained pure 14C-carbon                dioxide to a exchange tower, exchanging 14C-carbon                dioxide with the liquid phase 12C-carbon dioxide                from the top of the exchange tower in the exchange                tower, discharging 14C-carbon dioxide from the                exchange tower bottom and adding to analysis tower                and analyzing to obtain ultra-pure 14C-carbon                dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and                purifying of 14C isotopes from nuclear waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple technical step, stable                raw material source and low cost; and is suitable                for industrially producing high purity 14C isotope.                14C isotope has high purity up to more than                90%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for 14C                isotope separation and purification system,                comprising a gas oxidation system, a primary                cryogenic cooling system, a secondary cryogenic                cooling system and a 14C-carbon dioxide separation                and enrichment system connecting in sequence, the                14C-carbon dioxide separation and enrichment system                includes a 12C-carbon dioxide/14C-carbon dioxide                exchange tower, an analysis tower and an absorption                tower, the outlet of the secondary low temperature                cooling system is connecting to the inlet of the                12C-carbon dioxide/14C-carbon dioxide exchange                tower body, the bottom outlet of the 12C-carbon                dioxide/14C-carbon dioxide exchange tower is                connecting to the air inlet of the tower body, the                liquid outlet at the bottom of the analysis tower                is connecting to the liquid inlet at the bottom of                the absorption tower, the liquid outlet at the top                of the absorption tower is connecting to the liquid                inlet at the top of the exchange tower, the air                outlet at the top of the resolution tower is                connecting to the air inlet at the bottom of the                exchange tower, and the air outlet on the top of                the exchange tower is connecting to the air inlet                of the absorption tower body.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the 14C isotope separation and purification                system for separating and purifying of 14C isotopes                from nuclear waste (Drawing includes non-English                language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The primary cryogenic                  cooling system comprises a low temperature cold                  trap of -20 to 0 degrees C and a cryogenic cold                  trap of -78.5 to -170 degrees C connected in                  sequence. The secondary low-temperature cooling                  system is sequentially connected with a                  low-temperature cold trap of -77 to 0 degrees C,                  a low-temperature cold trap of -78.5 to -170                  degrees C, and a low-temperature cold trap of -77                  to 0 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (ii), the                  liquid phase 12C-carbon dioxide is in a form of                  complex formed by organic base or inorganic base                  and 12C-carbon dioxide. The organic base is used                  to form complex with liquid 12C-carbon dioxide,                  and the concentration of organic base is 0.001-10                  mol/l. The inorganic base is used to form                  composite with 12C-carbon dioxide in the liquid                  phase, and the concentration of the inorganic                  alkali is 0.001-12 mol/l. The content of                  14C-carbon dioxide in the nuclear power plant                  waste is 0.01-10%, and the content of 14C-methane                  is 0.01-90%, and content of 14C-carbon monoxide                  is 0.01-90%. Preferred Method: In step (ii), the                  temperature in the exchange tower is 0-100                  degrees C, and the pressure is -0.09-0.5 MPa. The                  step (ii) comprises exchanging 14C-carbon dioxide                  and liquid phase 12C-carbon dioxide under action                  of catalytic filler, where the material of the                  catalytic filler is metal or its surface oxide,                  alloy or its surface oxide, polymer material                  and/or inorganic material.

   USE - The method is useful for recycling rubber powder using supercritical carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling rubber powder using supercritical carbon dioxide comprises (A) introducing supercritical carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide tank through a pressurizing pump to buffer tank through heat exchanger, where the buffer tank is set with a heating insulating device; (B) introducing carbon dioxide and waste rubber powder from buffer tank into 40-80 meshes sieve, transferring 1/5-1/3 waste rubber powder water to supercritical reaction kettle, maintaining the working temperature of supercritical reaction kettle upto 40-200 degrees C, the pressure is 8-30 MPa, the saturation time is 1-6 hours, continuously stirring by carrying out saturated process; (C) saturating using supercritical reaction kettle of nozzle for pressure relief, introducing rubber powder, gas and water into tank with separating function, maintaining pressure in buffer tank at the same time, continuously supplying pressure to supercritical reactor until rubber powder is completely discharged into supercritical reaction kettle, discharging pressure to the inner portion of supercritical reactor; (D) drying rubber powder in storage tank, carrying out extrusion using single-screw extruder, carrying out regeneration, to obtain regenerated rubber powder. PT P
PN WO2021050000-A1; SG11202113342-A1; CN114341324-A
TI Preparing patchouli extract, comprises grounding patchouli material into patchouli powder and pelleting to obtain patchouli granules, introducing liquid carbon dioxide to perform supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU LIANG M
AE CROP PROTECTION SINGAPORE PTE LTD (CROP-Non-standard)
GA 202128112R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (M1) patchouli extract, comprises grounding patchouli material into patchouli powder and pelleting to obtain patchouli granules, introducing liquid carbon dioxide to perform supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process on the patchouli granules, then during the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process to obtain extracted product, where based on a total weight of the introduced liquid carbon dioxide not exceeding 10 times the weight of the patchouli granules, is collected as a first patchouli extract, where the first patchouli extract comprises first patchouli oil, and during the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process to obtain extracted product, based on the total weight of the introduced liquid carbon dioxide exceeding 10 times the weight of the patchouli granules but not exceeding 15 times the weight of the patchouli granules, is collected as a second patchouli extract, where the second patchouli extract comprises second patchouli oil.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing patchouli extract.
   ADVANTAGE - The patchouli extract has excellent effects in terms of anti-inflammatory, antibacterial activity, anti-oxidation and analgesic effects, and high-purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing (M1) patchouli extract, comprises grounding patchouli material into patchouli powder and pelleting to obtain patchouli granules, introducing liquid carbon dioxide to perform supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process at 40-60 degrees C pressure of 110-180 bar on the patchouli granules, then during the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process to obtain extracted product, where based on a total weight of the introduced liquid carbon dioxide not exceeding 10 times the weight of the patchouli granules, is collected as a first patchouli extract, where the first patchouli extract comprises first patchouli oil, and during the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process to obtain extracted product, based on the total weight of the introduced liquid carbon dioxide exceeding 10 times the weight of the patchouli granules but not exceeding 15 times the weight of the patchouli granules, is collected as a second patchouli extract, where the second patchouli extract comprises second patchouli oil. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing (M2) patchouli extract, comprising grounding patchouli material into patchouli powder and pelleting to obtain patchouli granules, introducing liquid carbon dioxide to perform supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process on the patchouli granules, where extraction pressure is 110-180 bar and extraction temperature is 40-60 degrees C, a flow rate of the liquid carbon dioxide is 15 kg/hour, then during the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process to obtain extracted product within extraction time of not less than 0.5 hour and collecting first patchouli extract, where the first patchouli extract comprises first patchouli oil, and during the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction process to obtain extracted product within an extraction time of 0.5-1 hour and collecting as a second patchouli extract, where the second patchouli extract comprises second patchouli oil.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method (M1) and (M2) further comprises: drying the patchouli material such that the dried patchouli material has a moisture content of less than or equal to 15%; centrifuging the first patchouli extract to separate the first patchouli oil from patchouli wax in the first patchouli extract. In the method (M1): the extraction pressure is 120-150 bar; and the first patchouli oil has a melting point of 10-40 degrees C, and the second patchouli oil has a melting point of 40-70 degrees C. Preferred Components: In the methods (M1) and (M2): the patchouli material comprises patchouli leaf and/or patchouli stem; the patchouli powder has a size of less than or equal to 140 mesh; the patchouli granules have a diameter of about 2-10 mm, and the patchouli granules have a thickness of 2-4.5 mm; content of patchouli alcohol in the first patchouli oil is 10-40 wt.%, and content of patchouli alcohol in the second patchouli oil is 10-30 wt.%; and content of patchouli alcohol in the patchouli wax is 10-30 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - Stems and leaves of the patchouli were separately grounded by using a grinder until the powder at a size of 140 mesh was obtained, the same weight of the stem powder and the leaf powder were mixed to obtain patchouli powder, the patchouli powder was pelleted to obtain thick pressed pellets, the thick pressed pellets have a bulk density of 0.63 (g/ml), then 1.28 kg thick pressed pellets were extracted by using a supercritical fluid extraction device at 50 degrees C for 0.5 hour under pressure of 150 bar, flow rate of carbon dioxide of 15 kg/hour to obtain patchouli oil.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC D10-A05; E10-E04F; E11-Q01C; E31-N05C; J01-C01A

PT P
PN CN111116418-A
TI New aggregation-inducing luminescent material i.e. 1,1,2,2-tetraphenylethene compounds useful in e.g. preparing carbon dioxide concentration detection fluorescent probe and in carbon dioxide detection material.
AU WANG Y
   MAI Z
   ZHOU G
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA NORMAL (USCN-C)
GA 202041399H
AB    NOVELTY - Aggregation-inducing luminescent material i.e. 1,1,2,2-tetraphenylethene compounds (I) are new.
   USE - (I) are useful in preparing carbon dioxide concentration detection fluorescent probe and in carbon dioxide detection material, hydrogel and carbon dioxide concentration detection (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) responds to properties of amidino/guanidine groups that can specifically to carbon dioxide and the structure can form more stable conjugated structure and improves responsiveness of probe molecules in a carbon dioxide atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Aggregation-inducing luminescent material i.e. 1,1,2,2-tetraphenylethene compounds of formula (I) are new.
   L1, L2, L3, L4 = hydrogen, amidino group or guanidine group.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I);
   (2) carbon dioxide concentration detection method comprising placing (I) in the environment to be detected, determining concentration of carbon dioxide based on fluorescence intensity of the aggregation-inducing luminescent material; and
   (3) hydrogel comprising (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises (i) performing McMurray coupling reaction of benzophenone compounds of formulae (II) and (III) to obtain 1,1,2,2-tetraphenylethene compound of formula (IV), and (ii) reacting (IV) with compound B1 to obtain aggregation-inducing luminescent material, where compound B1 is 1,1-dimethoxy-N,N-dimethylethanamine or H2N-C(=N).M1, M2, M3 and M4 = hydrogen atom or amino group.Preferred Components: The hydrogel is polyacrylamide hydrogel.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 10 Compounds (I) are specifically claimed e.g. (Z)-N,N-dimethyl-N'-(4-(1,2,2-triphenylvinyl)phenyl)acetimidamide (Ia), (1Z)-N',N''-(4,4'-((E)-1,2-diphenylethene-1,2-diyl)bis(4,1-phenylene))bis(N,N-dimethylacetimidamide), (1Z,1'Z)-N',N''-(4,4'-((Z)-1,2-diphenylethene-1,2-diyl)bis(4,1-phenylene))bis(N,N-dimethylacetimidamide), 1-(4-(1,2,2-triphenylvinyl)phenyl)guanidine and (E)-1,1'-(4,4'-(1,2-diphenylethene-1,2-diyl)bis(4,1-phenylene))diguanidine.
DC A89 (Photographic, laboratory equipment, optical - including electrophotographic, thermographic uses.); E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); S03 (Scientific Instrumentation)

PT P
PN CN110813059-A
TI Reducing regeneration energy consumption of carbon dioxide capturing alcohol, comprises e.g. capturing alcohol amine solvent of carbon dioxide in flue gas and performing mineralization reaction of carbon dioxide with precipitating agent.
AU MAO S
   GUO B
   YE N
   WANG D
   CHEN X
   JI Y
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NANJING CHEM RES INST CO LTD (SNPC-C)
GA 202018265D
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing regeneration energy consumption of carbon dioxide capturing alcohol, comprises capturing alcohol amine solvent of carbon dioxide in flue gas, adding a precipitating agent and an auxiliary precipitating agent for mineralization reaction of carbon dioxide to occur instead of performing traditional thermal regeneration, and regenerating the rich liquid without the need of an external heating source, and subjecting simultaneously the carbon dioxide to mineralization fixation.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing regeneration energy consumption of carbon dioxide capturing alcohol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not require an external heating source during the regeneration process, can significantly reduce the energy consumption required for regeneration, and can save equipment investment and operating costs of a lean liquid pump and a lean liquid cooler.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for reducing regeneration energy consumption of carbon dioxide capturing alcohol (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precipitating agent used contains an element that can undergo a mineralization reaction with carbon dioxide, where the element is calcium and/or magnesium. The auxiliary precipitating agent used is a compound containing an element capable of undergoing mineralization reaction with carbon dioxide, where the compound is oxides and/or hydroxides. The auxiliary precipitating agent used is sodium carbonate, sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate or potassium bicarbonate. The carbon dioxide capture solvent used is an alkyl alcohol amine-containing solvent, where the alkyl alcohol amine-containing solvent is an alkyl alcohol amine-containing aqueous solution, a non-aqueous solution and phase change absorption liquid. The alkyl alcohol amine comprises monoethanolamine (MEA), diethanolamine (DEA), triethanolamine (TEA), diisopropanolamine (DIPA), N-methyldiethanolamine (MDEA) or sterically hindered amines. Preferred Method: The substance produced by the mineralization reaction is separated from the regenerated alcohol amine solution by filtration.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN CN110186233-A; CN211012034-U
TI Carbon dioxide refrigerating system, has low-pressure circulatory barrel and liquid supplying pipeline fixed to form circulation loop of liquid carbon dioxide, where pipeline circulates liquid carbon dioxide through shielding pump.
AU GU Z
   ZHOU B
   CHEN J
   RAN C
   CHEN X
AE ZHEJIANG DUNAN COLD CHAIN SYSTEM CO LTD (DUNN-C)
GA 201975965H
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a feed pipeline provided with a shielding pump for pumping liquid carbon dioxide in a low-pressure circulatory barrel and supplying liquid carbon dioxide into a liquid supplying pipeline. An end of a first bypass pipeline is connected with a low-pressure circulatory barrel. An end of the low-pressure circulatory barrel is connected with the liquid supply pipeline. The first bypass line, the low-pressure circulatory barrel and the liquid supplying pipeline are fixed to form a circulation loop of liquid carbon dioxide. The liquid supplying pipeline circulates liquid carbon dioxide through the shielding pump.
   USE - Carbon dioxide refrigerating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system measures certain amount of refrigerant flows to prevent motor overheating of the shielding pump, improves running safely and reliability and avoids shutdown or power failure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a pneumatic diagram of a carbon dioxide refrigerating system.
DC Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25))
MC Q75-A01; Q75-T09
IP F25B-041/04; F25B-041/06; F25D-017/02; F25D-003/10

PT P
PN US2019249315-A1; WO2019157507-A1; CA3090411-A1; CN111683731-A; IN202017030620-A; KR2020110705-A; AU2019218389-A1; BR112020014630-A2; EP3752276-A1; JP2021512598-W; EP3752276-A4; US11359294-B2; CN111683731-B
TI Improving of carbon conversion efficiency involves            passing carbon dioxide gas substrate to form carbon            dioxide treated gas stream which forms carbon monoxide            enriched stream, and performing carbon monoxide            consuming process.
AU MIHALCEA C
   CONRADO R
   BOURDAKOS N
   LI X
   SIMPSON S
AE LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZA TECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
   LANZATECH INC (LANZ-C)
GA 2019697069
AB    NOVELTY - Improving of carbon conversion efficiency                involves passing carbon dioxide-comprising gas                substrate from an industrial process to a removal                module (A) for removal of at least one constituent                (228) from the carbon dioxide comprising gaseous                substrate to produce a carbon dioxide treated gas                stream (A), passing carbon dioxide treated gas                stream (A) to a carbon dioxide electrolysis module                for conversion of at least a portion of carbon                dioxide treated gas stream (A) to produce carbon                monoxide enriched stream and oxygen enriched stream                (A), and passing at least a portion of the carbon                monoxide enriched stream to a carbon monoxide                consuming process (240).
   USE - Improving of carbon conversion                efficiency.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables the improvement of carbon                conversion efficiency and produces recycled product                with increased yield.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view for                integration of industrial process.
   Conduit (212)
   Constituent (228)
   Carbon dioxide electrolysis module                (230)
   Carbon monoxide consuming process                (240)
   Oxygen separation module (260)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The at least one                  constituent (228) removed from the carbon dioxide                  comprising gas substrate is chosen from sulfur                  compounds, aromatic compounds, alkynes, alkenes,                  alkanes, olefins, nitrogen compounds, oxygen,                  phosphorous-comprising compounds, particulate                  material, solids, oxygen, halogenated compounds,                  silicon-comprising compounds, carbonyls, metals,                  alcohols, esters, ketone, peroxide, aldehyde,                  ether, tar, and naphthalene. The carbon monoxide                  consuming process comprises a fermentation                  process comprising a culture of at least one                  carboxydotrophic bacterium. The fermentation                  process produces a fermentation product chosen                  from ethanol, butyrate, 2,3-butanediol, lactate,                  butene, butadiene, methyl ethyl ketone, ethylene,                  acetone, isopropanol, lipids, 3-hydroypropionate,                  terpenes, fatty acids, 2-butanol,                  1,2-propanediol, and 1-propanol. The carbon                  dioxide lean stream (A) comprises carbon monoxide                  and/or hydrogen. The carbon dioxide comprising                  gaseous substrate from the industrial process                  further comprises one or more of carbon monoxide,                  hydrogen, and methane. The industrial process is                  chosen from carbohydrate fermentation, gas                  fermentation, cement making, pulp and paper                  making, steel making, oil refining and associated                  processes, petrochemical production, coke                  production, anaerobic or aerobic digestion,                  gasification, natural gas extraction, oil                  extraction, metallurgical processes, production                  and/or refinement of aluminum, copper, and/or                  ferroalloys, geological reservoirs,                  Fischer-Tropsch processes, methanol production,                  pyrolysis, steam methane reforming, dry methane                  reforming, partial oxidation of biogas or natural                  gas, and autothermal reforming of biogas or                  natural gas. The removal module (A) is chosen                  from hydrolysis module, acid gas removal module,                  deoxygenation module, catalytic hydrogenation                  module, particulate removal module, chloride                  removal module, tar removal module, and hydrogen                  cyanide polishing module. Preferred Method: The                  carbon dioxide comprising gaseous substrate from                  the industrial process is passed to a pressure                  module to produce a pressurized carbon dioxide                  comprising gas stream, and the pressurized carbon                  dioxide comprising gas stream is passed to the                  removal module (A).The process further involves                  passing at least a portion of the oxygen enriched                  stream (A) directly to the industrial process,                  passing at least a portion of the oxygen enriched                  stream (A) to an oxygen separation module to                  produce oxygen enriched stream (B) and an oxygen                  lean stream, passing at least of portion of the                  oxygen enriched stream (B) to the industrial                  process, passing at least of portion of the                  oxygen lean stream to the carbon dioxide                  electrolysis module and passing at least of                  portion of the oxygen lean stream to the carbon                  monoxide consuming process, and passing at least                  a portion of carbon dioxide comprising gaseous                  substrate from the industrial process and/or at                  least a portion of the carbon dioxide treated gas                  stream (A) to a carbon dioxide concentration                  module (A) to produce carbon dioxide concentrated                  stream (A) and carbon dioxide lean stream (A),                  passing at least a portion of carbon dioxide                  concentrated stream (A) to one or more of removal                  module (A) and carbon dioxide electrolysis                  module, and passing at least a portion of the                  carbon dioxide lean stream (A) to the carbon                  monoxide consuming process. The process which                  involves passing a water substrate to an hydrogen                  electrolysis module to produce an hydrogen                  enriched stream and passing at least a portion of                  the hydrogen enriched stream to the carbon                  monoxide consuming process, which produces a tail                  gas comprising carbon dioxide, and passing at                  least a portion of tail gas comprising carbon                  dioxide to the removal module (A) or to a removal                  module (B) for removal of at least one                  constituent from the tail gas to produce a carbon                  dioxide treated gas stream (B) and passing at                  least a portion of the tail gas comprising CO2 to                  a carbon dioxide concentration module (B) to                  produce a carbon dioxide concentrated stream (B)                  and a carbon dioxide lean stream (B). At least a                  portion of the tail gas comprising carbon dioxide                  is passed to a pressure module to produce a                  pressurized tail gas stream, and the pressurized                  tail gas stream is passed to the removal module                  (A) and/or (B) and the process further involves                  passing at least a portion of carbon dioxide                  concentrated stream (B) to the removal module (A)                  or (B) for removal of at least one constituent                  from the tail gas to produce a carbon dioxide                  treated gas stream (B), passing at least a                  portion of the carbon dioxide treated gas stream                  (B) to the carbon dioxide electrolysis                  module.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PT P
PN US2019143261-A1
TI Method for reducing aerosol emissions during carbon dioxide capture from flue gas, involves feeding flue gas to absorber column, absorbing carbon dioxide in solvent followed by feeding solvent to cooler, and recovering solvent.
AU MABROUK R
   JOVANOVIC S
   NAUMOVITZ J
   KRISHNAMURTHY R
AE MABROUK R (MABR-Individual)
   JOVANOVIC S (JOVA-Individual)
   NAUMOVITZ J (NAUM-Individual)
   KRISHNAMURTHY R (KRIS-Individual)
GA 201942110V
AB    NOVELTY - An aerosol emissions during the carbon dioxide capture from flue gas in an amine based solvent process reducing method involves feeding a flue gas containing carbon dioxide to an absorber column, where the absorber column contains an amine solvent. Carbon dioxide in the amine solvent is absorbed and a rich solvent is formed followed by feeding the rich solvent to inter-stage cooler, recovering the rich solvent and feeding the rich solvent to a regeneration column, separating the carbon dioxide from the rich solvent and recovering the carbon dioxide and forming a lean solvent, feeding the lean solvent to the absorber column and feeding steam to the flue gas containing carbon dioxide.
   USE - Method for reducing aerosol emissions during the carbon dioxide capture from flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing aerosol emissions with high process efficiency, in simple, easy and cost effective manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The flue gas contains 2-15% carbon dioxide.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E11-Q01A; E31-N05C; J01-E02B; J01-E03C

PN WO2018178300-A1; EP3600610-A1
TI Removal of carbon dioxide from gas for preparing cyclic carbonate, involves contacting gas comprising carbon dioxide with liquid mixture comprising epoxide and ionic liquid in absence of solvent, and reacting carbon dioxide with epoxide.
AU BOBBINK F D
   DYSON P
   LAURENCZY G
AE EPFL ECOLE POLYTECHNIQUE FEDERALE LAUSAN (EPFL-C)
   EPFL ECOLE POLYTECHNIQUE FEDERALE LAUSAN (EPFL-C)
GA 201877547K
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of carbon dioxide from a gas involves providing a gas comprising carbon dioxide at ambient pressure, contacting the gas with a liquid mixture comprising an epoxide and an ionic liquid in absence of a solvent and allowing carbon dioxide to react with epoxide.
   USE - Removal of carbon dioxide from gas for preparing cyclic carbonate (claimed) for electrolyte in lithium battery, and as solvent or starting material for polycarbonate for producing bottle and container.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces cyclic carbonates without the need of an extensive instrumental effort and complex equipment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of cyclic carbonate, which involves allowing carbon dioxide in the gas to react with the epoxide to give a cyclic carbonate and separating cyclic carbonate; and
   (2) use of liquid mixture comprising the epoxide and ionic liquid for removing carbon dioxide from the gas and/or for preparing cyclic carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The gas is free of water. The epoxide is chosen from 13 compounds, e.g. compounds of formulae (I)-(V). The ionic liquid comprises an azanium-based compound chosen from compounds of formulae (VI)-(IX). The molar ratio of epoxide and ionic liquid is 50:1-1:1. The cyclic carbonate is chosen from 13 compounds, e.g. compounds of formulae (X)-(XIV).R5-R16 = organic residue;andX- = anion.Preferred Property: The epoxide has boiling point of 40-350 degrees C. Preferred Process: Manufacture of cyclic carbonate further involves dissolving the cyclic carbonate in a solvent (S2) chosen from ethyl acetate, hexane, pentane and diethyl ether, and separating the solution from the ionic liquid. Preferred Condition: Contacting process is carried out at 40-150 degrees C.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A85 (Electrical applications.); ; X16 (Electrochemical Storage)

PT P
PN US2018280887-A1; US10179310-B2
TI Natural-gas purification apparatus used to purify natural gas by separating carbon dioxide from natural gas, comprises pressure adjuster adjusting pressure of natural gas, and separator separating natural-gas liquid and carbon dioxide.
AU MATSUBARA W
   EDA M
   HONJO S
   OKINO S
   NAKATANI H
   YUSHIMA M
AE MITSUBISHI HEAVY IND CO LTD (MITO-C)
GA 201876762Y
AB    NOVELTY - Natural-gas purification apparatus (A1) (100) comprises: a pressure adjuster that adjusts a pressure of natural gas (1); and a separator (20) that separates natural-gas liquid (3) and carbon dioxide (4) from the natural gas after the pressure adjustment by the pressure adjuster through a natural-gas-liquid separation membrane and a carbon-dioxide separation membrane, where the separator includes a device body including a gas inlet port through which to receive the natural gas, a natural-gas-liquid outlet port through which to discharge the natural-gas liquid, a carbon-dioxide outlet port through which to discharge the carbon dioxide, and a gas outlet port through which to discharge the natural gas from which the natural-gas liquid and the carbon dioxide have been separated, each of the natural-gas-liquid separation membrane and the carbon-dioxide separation membrane is in a flat shape.
   USE - The natural-gas purification apparatuses are useful for purifying natural gas by separating carbon dioxide from the natural gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The natural-gas purification apparatuses: are small in size but capable of efficiently separating and removing carbon dioxide from natural gas, while preventing the generation of a liquid film over the carbon-dioxide separation membrane as compared to conventional apparatuses; and utilize the separator that performs the separation and removal of natural-gas liquid from natural gas with the natural-gas-liquid separation membrane and the separation and removal of carbon dioxide from the natural gas with the carbon dioxide separation membrane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Natural-gas purification apparatus (A1) (100) comprises: a pressure adjuster that adjusts a pressure of natural gas; and a separator that separates natural-gas liquid and carbon dioxide from the natural gas after the pressure adjustment by the pressure adjuster through a natural-gas-liquid separation membrane and a carbon-dioxide separation membrane, where the separator includes a device body including a gas inlet port through which to receive the natural gas, a natural-gas-liquid outlet port through which to discharge the natural-gas liquid, a carbon-dioxide outlet port through which to discharge the carbon dioxide, and a gas outlet port through which to discharge the natural gas from which the natural-gas liquid and the carbon dioxide have been separated, each of the natural-gas-liquid separation membrane and the carbon-dioxide separation membrane is in a flat shape, the natural-gas-liquid separation membrane and the carbon-dioxide separation membrane are arranged inside the device body in such a way as to partition an inside of the device body into a first chamber including the gas inlet port and the gas outlet port, a second chamber including the natural-gas-liquid outlet port, and a third chamber including the carbon-dioxide outlet port. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a natural-gas purification apparatus (A2) comprising a pressure adjuster that adjusts a pressure of natural gas, and a separator that separates natural-gas liquid and carbon dioxide from the natural gas after the pressure adjustment by the pressure adjuster through a natural-gas-liquid separation membrane and a carbon-dioxide separation membrane, where the separator includes a device body including a gas inlet port through which to receive the natural gas, a natural-gas-liquid outlet port through which to discharge the natural-gas liquid, a carbon-dioxide outlet port through which to discharge the carbon dioxide and a gas outlet port through which to discharge the natural gas from which the natural-gas liquid and the carbon dioxide have been separated, a first partition plate partitioning an inside of the device body into a first chamber including the gas inlet port and the gas outlet port and a second chamber including the natural-gas-liquid outlet port and a second partition plate partitioning the inside of the device body into the first chamber and a third chamber including the carbon-dioxide outlet port, each of the natural-gas-liquid separation membrane and the carbon-dioxide separation membrane is in a tubular shape, the natural-gas-liquid separation membrane is arranged inside the first chamber in such a way as to open only to the second chamber through the first partition plate and the carbon-dioxide separation membrane is arranged inside the first chamber in such a way as to open only to the third chamber through the second partition plate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic configuration diagram of the natural-gas purification apparatus.
   Natural gas (1)
   Natural-gas liquid (3)
   Carbon dioxide (4)
   Separator (20)
   Natural-gas purification apparatus (100)

TI Air regeneration system, has carbon dioxide            absorber connected with potassium hydroxide solution            outlet of mixing reaction oxygen generator, and air            inlet passage connected, where solution is mixed with            carbon dioxide in air to remove carbon dioxide.
AU WANG X
   SHI L
   WANG P
   REN X
   XU Y
   SUN S
AE BEIJING TONGFANG CLEAN TECH CO LTD (TONG-C)
GA 2021C16027
AB    (CN214390082-U)   NOVELTY - The embodiment of the invention claims an air                regeneration system, relating to the technical                field of air regeneration, the main purpose is to                improve the safety and controllability of the use                of potassium superoxide. The air regeneration                system includes: a potassium super-oxide medicine                feeder; a water feeder; a mixing reaction oxygen                generator; the feeding port respectively the mixing                reaction oxygen generator is connected with the                discharging port of the potassium super-oxide                medicine feeder and the water feeding port of the                water feeder; carbon dioxide absorber; the inlet                respectively the carbon dioxide absorber is                connected with the potassium hydroxide solution                outlet of the mixing reaction oxygen generator; the                air inlet passage is connected; the potassium                hydroxide solution for the potassium hydroxide                solution outlet is mixed with the carbon dioxide in                the air so as to remove the carbon dioxide in the                air. Compared with the existing technology,                potassium superoxide is capable of completely                reacting with water; the oxygen amount can be added                with potassium superoxide medicine and water                according to need; the reaction controllability is                strong; the reaction is complete; it can improve                the safety and controllability of using potassium                superoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                control method of the air regeneration                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the air regeneration system.
   10Potassium ultra-potassium oxide chemical                feeder
   20Water feeder
   30Mixing reaction oxygen generator
   40Carbon dioxide absorber
   51Air inlet passage
EA    NOVELTY - Air regeneration system, comprises a potassium                ultra-potassium oxide chemical feeder (10), a water                feeder (20) and a mixing reaction oxygen generator                (30). A feeding port of the mixed reaction oxygen                generator is respectively connected with the                discharge port of the potassium super oxide                medicament feeder and water feed port of the water                feeder. A carbon dioxide absorber (40) whose inlet                is respectively connected with the potassium                hydroxide solution and outlet of the mixed reaction                oxygen generator. An air inlet passage (51) is                connected for mixing the potassium hydroxide                solution flowing into the potassium hydroxide                solution outlet with carbon dioxide in the air to                remove the carbon dioxide in the air.
   USE - Used as air regeneration system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: has strong reaction                controllability and can improve the safety and                controllability of the use of potassium super                oxide.

PN CN110562921-A; CN110562921-B
TI Synthesizing lithium borohydride-carbon dioxide coordination compound comprises e.g. transferring lithium borohydride into dry reactor, introducing carbon dioxide into reactor, contacting lithium borohydride and carbon dioxide and reacting.
AU LIANG C
   WANG Y
   SUN X
   HUANG H
   ZHANG W
   GAN Y
   XIA Y
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG TECHNOLOGY (UYZT-C)
GA 2019A6679Y
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing lithium borohydride-carbon dioxide coordination compound comprises (i) transferring the lithium borohydride into a dry reactor under a vacuum or a protective atmosphere; (ii) introducing carbon dioxide into the reactor, contacting lithium borohydride and carbon dioxide fully in the dry reactor, heating to 20-200 degrees C and reacting at a constant temperature for 0.1-120 hours; and (iii) taking out solid product in the reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing lithium borohydride-carbon dioxide coordination compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and easy; and consumes less energy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The protective atmosphere described in step (i) is a gas or a mixture that does not react with the reactants and products comprises nitrogen and argon. Preferred Method: The method further comprises subjecting lithium borohydride obtained in step (i) to ball milling pretreatment according to the required particle size to use in the reaction, preferably comprises charging lithium borohydride into a sealed ball mill tank for ball milling at a speed of 200-600 r/min under vacuum or protective atmosphere, where mass ratio of abrasive and ball is 1:30-200, and the ball milling for 10-120 hours. In step (ii) introducing carbondioxide into the reactor, preferably pressure of carbondioxide in the reactor is 0.1-10 MPa.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN115184302-A
TI Method for measuring vehicle carbon emission            factor in specific area by using vehicle networking            technology, involves updating data of emission factor            for one time using existing emission factor data to            count discharge amount of carbon dioxide.
AU YANG C
   GUI L
AE UNIV SHANGHAI POLYTECHNIC (USSP-C)
GA 2022D1991N
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves assuming that the number                of vehicles running in a specific area. A                concentration sequence of the n-th vehicle emission                carbon dioxide area is recorded. Running speed                sequence of the vehicle is calculated. The carbon                dioxide emission area of the vehicle provided by a                carbon dioxide concentration monitoring system is                combined. An emission carbon dioxide emission                quality sequence is obtained. Real-time carbon                emission factors and vehicle running speed                information are combined. The vehicle according to                carbon emission factor is classified for finally                dividing the vehicle into F-type vehicle. Volume of                the discharged carbon dioxide zone of the vehicle                is set.
   USE - Method for measuring vehicle carbon emission                factor in specific area by using vehicle networking                technology. Can also be used in automatic driving,                fire alarm, gas detection field.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good real-time performance,                simple calculation, not fixing the advantages of                the vehicle type, the vehicle-mounted carbon                dioxide concentration detecting device does not                occupy space and can be fixed, and using the                semiconductor laser as the light source, using the                laser temperature current controller to realize the                high bandwidth wavelength modulation of the laser.                The method uses the ratio of the corresponding                characteristic absorption peak secondary harmonic                wave and the first harmonic wave as the                concentration measuring standard, which greatly                improves the measuring precision and stability of                the system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon                dioxide concentration monitoring system in fuel                vehicle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a carbon                dioxide concentration monitoring system in fuel                vehicle (Drawing includes a non-English language                text).

PN WO2022187336-A1
TI Method for uptake of carbon dioxide from ambient            air during weathering for global warming, involves            applying heat to calciner, contacting carbonation            medium with ambient air, adding water stream to            carbonation medium, and feeding carbon-containing            stream to calciner.
AU KELEMEN P B
   MCGILLIS W
   WILCOX J
   MCQUEEN N
   DIPPLE G
   MORTIMER T
AE UNIV COLUMBIA NEW YORK (UYCO-C)
   KELEMEN P B (KELE-Individual)
   MCGILLIS W (MCGI-Individual)
   WILCOX J (WILC-Individual)
   MCQUEEN N (MCQU-Individual)
   DIPPLE G (DIPP-Individual)
   UNIV BRITISH COLUMBIA (UYBR-C)
   UNIV PENNSYLVANIA (UPEN-C)
GA 2022B59398
AB    NOVELTY - Method (10) for uptake of carbon dioxide                involves applying heat to a calciner to decomposing                a carbon-containing stream to a gas stream and a                stream of a carbonation medium, and the gas stream                including carbon dioxide, sequestering or utilizing                (15) the gas stream, feeding the stream of the                carbonation medium to a carbonation station,                contacting (11) the carbonation medium with ambient                air at the carbonation station, such that the                carbonation medium adsorbs carbon dioxide to form                the carbon-containing stream, during contacting                adding (12) a water stream to the carbonation                medium at intervals of about 30 minutes to about 72                hours, and feeding (13) the carbon-containing                stream to the calciner.
   USE - Method for uptake of carbon dioxide from                ambient air during weathering for global                warming.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is inexpensive, and increases                carbon dioxide uptake.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system,                which comprises a carbonation station, a water                delivery device, and a calciner. The carbonation                station contains several of carbonation plots                containing a carbonation medium. The carbonation                medium contacts ambient air and adsorb carbon                dioxide from the ambient air such that at least a                portion of the carbonation medium is converted to a                carbon-containing stream. The water delivery device                affects relative humidity of the carbonation                station and/or maintains the carbonation medium                within a predetermined water content range. The                calciner receives the carbon-containing stream, and                calcines the stream to form a gas stream and a                carbonation medium stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of method                for uptake of carbon dioxide from ambient                air.
   10Method for carbon dioxide uptake
   11Contacting process
   12Addition of water process
   13Feeding carbon containing stream                process
   15Sequestering process
   18Condensing process
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The water delivery                  device contains at least one of a sprayer, a                  hose, and a mat. The system further contains a                  condenser condenses water from the gas stream, an                  air separation unit processes an air stream and                  increases the oxygen content of the air stream                  before feeding the air stream to the calciner,                  and a grinding station grinds the carbonation                  medium.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process involves                  adding the water stream through misting and/or                  spraying at a rate of 0.01-0.5 mL water per gram                  of carbonation medium, grinding an adsorbent                  material to form the carbonation medium into a                  powder through at least one of a ball mill                  crusher, impact crushers, or a cone crusher,                  condensing an amount of water from the gas                  stream, recycling the amount of water by feeding                  the amount of water to the water stream,                  processing an air stream through an air                  separation unit to produce an oxygen stream, and                  the oxygen stream having a purity of at least                  about 95% and feeding the oxygen stream to the                  calciner, applying the heat to the calciner                  through electric resistance heating powered by                  electricity, monitoring a relative humidity,                  temperature, and air velocity levels of ambient                  air in close proximity to the carbonation medium                  during the contacting, and hydrating the                  carbonation medium before feeding the carbonation                  medium to the carbonation station. Preferred                  Composition: The carbonation medium is maintained                  at a moisture content of 3-50 wt.%. The gas                  stream includes at least about 95 vol.% carbon                  dioxide. The predetermined water content range of                  the carbonation medium is 3-50 wt.%. Preferred                  Components: The carbonation medium includes at                  least one of magnesium oxide, magnesium silicate,                  silicon dioxide, calcium oxide, calcium                  hydroxide, calcium silicates, sodium oxide,                  sodium hydroxide, potassium oxide, potassium                  hydroxide, and magnesium hydroxide. The                  carbon-containing stream includes at least one of                  magnesium carbonate (MgCO3), dolomite (high                  magnesium calcium carbonate), sodium carbonate,                  sodium bicarbonate, silicon carbonate, calcium                  carbonate, calcium bicarbonate, potassium                  carbonate, potassium bicarbonate, nesquehonite,                  and hydromagnesite. The carbonation station                  includes several of carbonation contactors, and                  each carbonation contactor of the carbonation                  contactors including a sheet having thickness of                  0.5-10 mm, of the carbonation medium. The                  adsorbent material includes at least one of                  magnesium oxide, magnesium silicate, silicon                  dioxide, calcium oxide, calcium hydroxide,                  calcium silicates, sodium oxide, sodium                  hydroxide, potassium oxide, potassium hydroxide,                  and magnesium hydroxide. Preferred Properties:                  The carbonation medium has average particle size                  of less than 75 &#956; m. The relative                  humidity of the carbonation station is 5-50%                  RH.

AB    NOVELTY - A calcium reaction control method involves                measuring carbonate ion concentration value in a                solution to be tested in a water tank to obtain                related measurement results; and adjusting input                amount of carbon dioxide gas input into calcium                reactor according to measurement results, where, if                results are less than concentration preset value,                increasing input amount of carbon dioxide gas from                previous set value, and inputting said reaction                water into water tank until carbonate ion                concentration value of solution to be tested equals                the concentration preset value; and if results are                greater than concentration preset value, decreasing                input amount of carbon dioxide gas from previous                set value, and inputting calcium reaction water                into water tank until carbonate ion concentration                value of solution to be tested equals the                concentration preset value.
   USE - A calcium reaction control method for calcium                reactor used to provide calcium reaction water                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The amount of carbon dioxide input and the                amount of calcium reaction water can be controlled,                which can improve the control accuracy and ease of                use. The method can control the flow of carbon                dioxide gas to reach a stable state. The carbon                dioxide gas flow cycle time can be adjusted by                manual or automatic control procedures to adjust                the carbonate concentration, so that the water in                the breeding tank can reach the best living                environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for calcium                reaction control device configured to be coupled                to: a water tank storing a solution to be tested, a                calcium reactor, and a calcium reaction measuring                device, comprising body, carbon dioxide gas inlet                for receiving carbon dioxide gas, and carbonate ion                signal transmission unit for coupling to calcium                reaction measuring device which measures a                carbonate ion concentration value in the solution                to be tested.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Processes: When carbonate ion                  concentration value is lower than concentration                  preset value, a number of times that carbon                  dioxide gas is input is increased by one; and                  when carbonate ion concentration value is higher                  than concentration preset value, the number of                  times that carbon dioxide gas input is decreased                  by one. When carbonate ion concentration value is                  lower than concentration preset value and a                  difference between carbonate ion concentration                  value and concentration preset value is or                  exceeds a maximum value, the input amount of the                  carbon dioxide gas is adjusted to a maximum input                  percentage above the previous set value; and when                  carbonate ion concentration value is higher than                  concentration preset value and the difference                  between carbonate ion concentration value and                  concentration preset value is or exceeds the                  maximum value, the input amount of carbon dioxide                  gas is adjusted to a minimum value. The method                  further comprises inputting the calcium reaction                  water into the water tank at one time. The method                  further comprises inputting the calcium reaction                  water into the water tank at a constant flow                  rate.Preferred Parameters: A set value of the                  input amount of the carbon dioxide gas is that a                  minimum input amount of the carbon dioxide gas is                  50% of a maximum input amount of the carbon                  dioxide gas.
