GA 2021A6386C
AB    NOVELTY - Solid cosmetic product comprises at least one spherical carbon dioxide effervescent component comprises (a) salt of carbonic acid in powder form and (b) acidifying agent in powder form, where only a portion of the surface of the at least one carbon dioxide effervescent component is coated with fat containing coating component, where the fat containing coating component is 0.1-4 wt.% and the carbon dioxide effervescent component is 96-99.9 wt.% based on the combined weight of the fat containing coating component and the carbon dioxide effervescent component, and at the portion of the surface coated with a fat containing coating component is integrated with the powder particles of the salt of carbonic acid and the powder particles of the acidifying agent.
   USE - The solid cosmetic product is useful in bathing water.
   ADVANTAGE - The solid coating: reduces dust produced by powder cosmetic during transportation; enables longer storage; has improved storage and handling properties; and provides moisturising and emolliating quality to a bath, in addition to appealing multi-sensory display.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) producing of solid cosmetic product, comprising (i) providing at least one spherical carbon dioxide effervescent component comprising (a) salt of carbonic acid in powder form and (b) acidifying agent in powder form, (ii) providing liquid fat containing coating component, and (iii) contacting the carbon dioxide effervescent component in the liquid fat containing coating component to coat only a portion of the surface of 96-99.9 wt.% carbon dioxide effervescent component with 0.1-4 wt.% fat containing coating component (based on the combined weight of the fat containing coating component and the carbon dioxide effervescent component); and
   (2) product obtained or obtainable by the above process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the solid cosmetic product: the thickness of the fat containing coating component is at least 1%, preferably not greater than5% of the thickness of the solid cosmetic product; the salt of carbonic acid and the acidifying agent are present in a weight ratio of 90:10-50:50, preferably 90:10-70:30; the salt of carbonic acid comprises alkali metal carbonates and/or alkali metal bicarbonates, preferably sodium bicarbonate and/or sodium carbonate; the acidifying agent comprises monocarboxylic acids, dicarboxylic acids and/or tricarboxylic acids, preferably citric acid and/or potassium bitartrate (cream of tartar); the carbon dioxide effervescent component comprises (a) 55-90 wt.% sodium bicarbonate based on the carbon dioxide effervescent component, and (b) 5-40 wt.% citric acid based on the carbon dioxide effervescent component; the carbon dioxide effervescent component further comprises coloring, preferably water soluble coloring; the carbon dioxide effervescent component comprises 0.001-5 wt.% coloring based on the carbon dioxide effervescent component; the fat containing coating component comprises at least one vegetable butter, 50 wt.% hard vegetable butter, 1.5-25 wt.% emulsifier (based on the fat containing coating component), 0.001-5 wt.% colouring, oil soluble coloring and water soluble coloring; the vegetable butter comprises Cocoa butter, lllipe butter, murumuru butter, kokum butter, Aloe butter, avocado butter, cupuacu butter, macadamia nut butter, mango butter, olive butter, shea butter, coconut butter, pumpkin seed butter, peanut butter, almond butter, coffee bean butter, refined butter, hemp seed butter, mochacchino butter, pistachio nut butter and/or shealoe butter; the hard vegetable butter comprises cocoa butter, lllipe butter, murumuru butter and/or kokum butter; the emulsifier comprises polyoxyether of lauryl alcohol, polyethylene glycol (PEG)-6 caprylic/capric glycerides, PEG-60 almond glycerides and/or sodium cocoamphoacetate; the fat containing coating component or the carbon dioxide effervescent component further comprises at least one additional component comprising binders, fillers, opacifiers, perfumes, fragrances and/or decorative items; and the product is a bathing product.Preferred Process: In the process, the liquid fat containing coating component is provided by melting a fat containing coating component which is solid at 20degrees Celsius.

PN CN111439797-A
TI Treating water containing calcium hydroxide comprises e.g. removing calcium hydroxide, passing carbon dioxide into water containing calcium hydroxide as raw material gas and adding sulfuric acid to light calcium carbonate.
AU WANG W
AE BEIJING XINHESHENG INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202072457Q
AB    NOVELTY - Treating water containing calcium hydroxide comprises removing calcium hydroxide, passing carbon dioxide into the water containing calcium hydroxide as a raw material gas, so that the raw material gas reacts with calcium hydroxide to generate light calcium carbonate and water, adding sulfuric acid to the light calcium carbonate generated in the step of removing calcium hydroxide, reacting the light calcium carbonate with the sulfuric acid to generate calcium sulfate and carbon dioxide, recycling carbon dioxide, using the carbon dioxide generated in the step of generating carbon dioxide as the raw material gas in the step of removing calcium hydroxide into the water containing calcium hydroxide, where in the step of removing calcium hydroxide, the carbon dioxide generated in the step of generating carbon dioxide is used to react with calcium hydroxide to generate light calcium carbonate and water.
   USE - The method is useful for treating water containing calcium hydroxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: decreases the concentration of calcium ion will not bring cations into the water; reduces the pH value of the water; does not bring anions into the water, which is conducive to the reuse of water after treatment; has low cost of the water treatment and high the purity of calcium sulfate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIM are also included for:
   (1) gypsum production method comprising producing the calcium sulfate in the carbon dioxide generation step of the calcium hydroxide containing water treatment method is used for gypsum production;
   (2) sewage treatment method comprising adding calcium hydroxide, adding excess calcium hydroxide to sewage to make calcium hydroxide react with sewage, treating water containing calcium hydroxide for the sewage treated by the step of adding calcium hydroxide, the water treatment method containing calcium hydroxide used for treatment; and
   (3) water treatment device containing calcium hydroxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of removing calcium hydroxide is carried out in the first reactor, the carbon dioxide generation step is carried out in the second reactor, the light calcium carbonate generated in the step of removing calcium hydroxide performed in the first reactor is pumped into the second reactor by a pump, the carbon dioxide generated in the carbon dioxide generation step performed in the second reactor is passed into the first reactor through a gas pipe. In the step of removing calcium hydroxide, precipitation and concentration are performed after the light calcium carbonate is generated. In the step of generating carbon dioxide, the light calcium carbonate after precipitation and concentration reacts with the sulfuric acid, the concentration of the light calcium carbonate after precipitation and concentration is greater than or equal to 10% and less than or equal to 50%. Preferred Components: The concentration of the light calcium carbonate after precipitation and concentration is greater than or equal to 25% and less than or equal to 35%. The calcium carbonate outlet is in the lower part of the first reactor and the drain is in the upper part of the first reactor. The lower part of the first reactor is a sedimentation bucket, and the calcium carbonate discharge port is in the lower part of the sedimentation bucket. The device further comprises a gas filling pipe, and the output end of the gas filling pipe is in communication with the inner cavity of the first reactor and the gas filling pipe is used for filling carbon dioxide into the first reactor.

PN WO2018216017-A1
TI Sensor used in sensing system for detecting carbon dioxide in gas sample, consists of sensing layer comprising carbon dioxide absorbing poly(ionic liquid), and conductive material and electrodes in electric contact with sensing layer.
AU HAICK H
   PORAT G
   SHANI G
   ABUD HAWA M
AE TECHNION RES & DEV FOUND LTD (TECR-C)
GA 2018948795
AB    NOVELTY - A sensor (10) consists of a composite carbon dioxide sensing layer (16) comprising carbon dioxide absorbing poly(ionic liquid) comprising a diallyldimethylammonium cation and an anion, and a conductive material comprising single-walled carbon nanotubes and/or carbon black particles, two or more electrodes (14), and a substrate (12). The electrodes are arranged on the substrate or on the sensing layer and are in electric contact with the sensing layer.
   USE - Sensor is used in sensing system for detecting carbon dioxide concentration in gas sample, such as air, breath sample, modified-atmosphere packaging, fresh produce head space, and automobile emission. The sensor is in the form resistive sensor, chemiresistive sensor, capacitive sensor, impedance sensor, and field effect transistor sensor (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The sensor efficiently and accurately detects carbon dioxide with high sensitivity and selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) sensing system, which consists of the sensor(s) and a sample feeding system;
   (2) fabrication of the sensor, which involves providing the substrate, forming two or more electrodes, preparing a composite carbon dioxide sensing layer composition comprising the carbon dioxide absorbing poly(ionic liquid) and the conductive material, and applying the composition onto the substrate or the electrodes to form the sensing layer; and
   (3) determination of concentration of carbon dioxide in gas sample, which involves providing the sensing system, exposing the sensor(s) to the gas sample through a sample feeding tube (t1) to obtain a confounding signal, exposing the sensor(s) to the gas sample through the sample feeding tube (t2) to obtain a test signal, and comparing the test signal with the confounding signal.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the sensor.
   Sensor (10)
   Substrate (12)
   Electrodes (14)
   Sensing layer (16)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The sensing layer comprises 3-70 wt.% conductive material with respect to the total weight of the sensing layer. The single-walled carbon nanotubes are wrapped by the carbon dioxide absorbing poly(ionic liquid). The sensing layer comprises 30-70 wt.% single-walled carbon nanotubes, and 3-50 wt.% carbon black particles with respect to the total weight of the sensing layer. The carbon black particles are dispersed in the carbon dioxide absorbing poly(ionic liquid). The sensing layer comprises 0.001-10 mu g conductive material, and 0.001-100 mu g carbon dioxide absorbing poly(ionic liquid). Preferred Properties: The single-walled carbon nanotubes have diameter 1-5 nm, and length of 1-50 mu m. The carbon black particles have particle size of less than 500 nm and/or surface area of 50-100 m2/g. The sensing layer has area of 0.1-50 mm2. Preferred Process: The formation of the sensing layer composition involves dispersing single-walled carbon nanotubes in an organic solvent to form a mixture, adding the carbon dioxide absorbing poly(ionic liquid) to the mixture, grinding the mixture for 20 minutes or more to form a paste, and washing the paste with a solvent to form a stable dispersion comprising 5-150 ppm single-walled carbon nanotubes and the carbon dioxide absorbing poly(ionic liquid). The formation of the sensing layer composition alternately involves dispersing carbon black particles in a solvent to form a mixture, adding the carbon dioxide absorbing poly(ionic liquid) to the mixture to form a dispersion, and ultrasonically processing the dispersion for 0.5 hour or more. The method further involves applying the sensing layer composition onto the substrate or the two electrodes by drop casting of 0.1-5 mu l of sensing layer composition.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The sample feeding system comprises two sample feeding tubes in contact with the sensor(s). The tube(s) comprises a carbon dioxide adsorbent material comprising a solid amine sorbent. The adsorbent material is removable and/or reusable. The tube(s) in the sample feeding system does not include a carbon dioxide adsorbent. The sample feeding system is configured to alternately prevent the exposure of the sensor(s) to a gas sample through the sample feeding tubes. The sensing system further comprises a computing system configured for executing various algorithms stored on a non-transitory memory, a detection device for measuring a change in one or more properties chosen from resistance, conductance, direct current, alternating current, frequency, capacitance, impedance, inductance, electrical potential, and voltage threshold of the sensor(s), and a sensor array (a) or (b). The sensor array (a) comprises diallyldimethylammonium trifluoroacetate and single-walled carbon nanotube-based sensor, diallyldimethylammonium methanesulfonate and single-walled carbon nanotube-based sensor, diallyldimethylammonium benzoate and single-walled carbon nanotube-based sensor, and diallyldimethylammonium nitrate and single-walled carbon nanotube-based sensor. The sensor array (b) comprises diallyldimethylammonium trifluoroacetate and carbon black particle-based sensor, diallyldimethylammonium benzoate and carbon black particle-based sensor, and diallyldimethylammonium nitrate and carbon black particle-based sensor. The computing system receives output signals of the sensor(s). The algorithm is chosen from artificial neural network algorithm, support vector machine, discriminant function analysis, principal component analysis, multilayer perceptron, generalized regression neural network, fuzzy inference system, self-organizing map, radial basis function, genetic algorithm, neuro-fuzzy system, adaptive resonance theory, partial least squares, multiple linear regression, principal component regression, linear discriminant analysis, cluster analysis, Fisher linear discriminant analysis, soft independent modeling of class analogies, K-nearest neighbors, fuzzy logic algorithm, and canonical discriminant analysis, preferably principal component analysis. The sensing system is configured for detecting carbon dioxide concentration of 0.05-20% in the gas sample.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anion is chosen from acetate, trifluoroacetate, methanesulfonate, trifluoromethanesulfonate, benzoate, nitrate, and chloride, preferably benzoate.

TI Carbon dioxide adsorbent used for air            conditioning, comprises polymer compound that has            chemical structure in which functional group containing            amine group that is composed of at least primary amine            group is bonded and the polymer compound repeatedly            adsorbs and releases carbon dioxide when heated.

   MASAKI N
   AKIYAMA Y
   KOMURA A
   NOBUNAGA K
AE PANASONIC INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY MANAGEME (MATU-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises a polymer                compound that has chemical structure in which a                functional group containing an amine group that is                at least a primary amine group is bonded, where the                polymer compound repeatedly adsorbs and releases                carbon dioxide when heated, and is formed in a                porous shape. The temperature at which the amount                of carbon dioxide released peaks is 100&#176; C                or less.
   USE - The carbon dioxide adsorbent is used for air                conditioning and an adsorption device (claimed)                used for indoor applications, factory exhaust gas                treatment applications, agricultural facility                applications, and vehicle-mounted                applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide adsorbent improves handling                property of the adsorbent and the particles of the                adsorbent need not be uniform in shape and                size.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an air                conditioner, comprises a blowing mechanism that                generates an airflow passing through the adsorption                device, and a regeneration mechanism that causes                the carbon dioxide adsorbent in the adsorption                device to release carbon dioxide to regenerate the                carbon dioxide adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The functional group                  is such that the amine group is located at the                  end of a branched chain in the chemical                  structure. The chemical structure includes an                  aromatic ring directly or indirectly bonded to                  the amine group. The carbon dioxide adsorbent is                  a powder having an average particle size of 300                  &#956; m or more and 1.3 mm or less. The                  polymer compound includes at least a polystyrene                  resin skeleton. The amount of amine carried is                  2.0 mmol/g or more. The functional group has a                  molecular weight of 70-200. The stress at 10%                  deformation is a value of 5x 106to 14x                  106Pa.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN113773171-A
TI Processing emission reduction method for industrial carbon dioxide recycling has e.g. using calcium oxide for fixing carbon dioxide, introducing oxygen and water vapor, reacting calcium carbide with water to generate acetylene and calcium hydroxide for realizing carbon dioxide recycling.
AU SUN Y
   XUE Y
AE QINGDAO WEISHE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOG (QING-Non-standard)
GA 202200374L
AB    NOVELTY - Processing emission reduction method for industrial carbon dioxide recycling comprises (i) using calcium oxide for fixing carbon dioxide to obtain solid block of calcium carbonate, (ii) mixing calcium carbonate solid block obtained in the step (i) with the carbon source, and then introducing oxygen and water vapor, heating, taking the carbon source undergoes oxidation reaction, when the temperature rises, decomposing calcium carbonate into calcium oxide and carbon dioxide, continuously heating, and reaching specific temperature, where the substances in the furnace continuously reacting to generate calcium carbide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen, and (iii) using resulting carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the processing of methanol, methane or ethanol, reacting calcium carbide with water to generate acetylene and calcium hydroxide for realizing carbon dioxide recycling.
   USE - The method is useful for processing emission reduction method for industrial carbon dioxide recycling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes zero emission of industrial carbon dioxide; achieves the goal of reducing carbon dioxide emissions; and has low cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the molar ratio of calcium oxide and carbon dioxide is 1.1-1.2:1.Preferred Method: In the step (i), spraying calcium oxide into the carbon dioxide atmosphere by atomization. In the step (ii), the carbon source includes lump coal, blue charcoal and/or coke. The particle size of the carbon source is 6-100 mm. The mass ratio of the carbon source and the calcium carbonate solid block is 4.5-6:1. The molar ratio of carbon source and oxygen is 3.5-6:1. The molar ratio of carbon source and water vapor is 1-2:2.Preferred Method: The method comprises when heating up to 880-910degrees Celsius in the step (ii), calcium carbonate solid block decomposes, as the reaction progresses, continuously heating, and when the temperature rises to 2000-2200degrees Celsius, reacting calcium oxide with the carbon source to generate calcium carbide and carbon monoxide, reacting the carbon source with water vapor to obtain carbon monoxide and hydrogen, and reacting the carbon source with carbon dioxide to form carbon monoxide. The method comprises heating calcium hydroxide in the step (iii) and dehydrating to obtain calcium oxide.

PN CN113620293-A; CN113620293-B
TI Method for producing methane and carbon dioxide by            catalytically cracking high-concentration organic            wastewater, involves filling high-concentration organic            waste water into reaction kettle with loading type            catalyst, and collecting generated methane and carbon            dioxide from kettle.
AU ZHANG H
   WANG W
   SUN J
   JIANG Y
AE WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
   WANHUA CHEM NINGBO CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 2021D2014X
AB    NOVELTY - The method for producing methane and carbon                dioxide, involves (1) filling high-concentration                organic waste water into reaction kettle with                loading type catalyst for processing, under                condition of high temperature and high pressure,                where the raw material space velocity is 1-10/hour,                preferably 1-4/hour, (2) collecting generated                methane and carbon dioxide from gas outlet end of                reaction kettle, where the load type catalyst                comprises carrier and active component, such that                the carrier is magnetic nano iron oxide, the active                component is palladium and silver.
   USE - Method for producing methane and carbon                dioxide by catalytically cracking                high-concentration organic wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the organic carbon in high-concentration                organic wastewater is converted into methane and                carbon dioxide and further utilized for production                of synthesis gas, enhanced resource reutilization                is achieved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In catalyst, the                  content of palladium is 0.4-1 wt.% of the mass of                  iron oxide in carrier. The content of silver is                  5.0-10 wt.% of iron oxide content in carrier. The                  content of palladium in catalyst is 0.6-0.8 wt.%                  of the mass of iron oxide in carrier. The content                  of silver is 6-8 wt.% of the mass of iron oxide                  in carrier. In step (a), the mass ratio of sodium                  silicate nonahydrate and magnetic nano iron oxide                  is (0.9-5.5):1. The adding amount of zeolite is                  0.5-5 %mass of the mass of the iron oxide.Preferred Components: The carrier is                  magnetic nano iron oxide carrier modified by                  silicon dioxide and sulfhydryl. The organic waste                  water is one or more of acetone waste water, high                  aldehyde waste water, propylene glycol waste                  water, acrylic acid waste water, aniline waste                  water and phenol waste water. In step (b), the                  metal silver precursor is one or more of nitrate                  of silver, sulfate and oxalate. The metal                  palladium precursor is one or more of nitrate of                  palladium, sulfate, trifluoroacetate and                  oxalate.Preferred process: The preparation method of                  catalyst, involves (a) dispersing magnetic nano                  iron oxide into 60-90degreesC constant                  temperature water, dripping 0.5-3 mol/L sodium                  silicate nonahydrate solution to dispersion with                  nitrogen gas, mechanically stirring, adjusting pH                  to 4-6, stirring for 1-5 hours, separating solid                  with magnet, and washing to obtain silicon                  dioxide modified carrier iron oxide loaded                  silicon dioxide, (b) adding silicon dioxide                  modified carrier, metal silver precursor, and                  metal palladium precursor to alcohol solution,                  stirring for 0.5-3 hours, and processing to                  obtain solid, and calcining at 180-220degrees                  Celsius in nitrogen atmosphere for 0.5-8 hours to                  obtain loaded active group sub-catalyst                  intermediate palladium-silver/iron oxide loaded                  silicon dioxide, (c) activating catalyst                  intermediate palladium-silver/iron oxide loaded                  silicon dioxide by acid and washing with water to                  neutrality, dispersing in benzene solvent, adding                  mercaptopropyltriethoxysilane and zeolite, and                  reacting under reflux for 12-48 hours, stop                  reaction, and separating solid by magnet, washing                  and drying.Preferred Conditions: The high temperature                  is controlled at 200-300degreesC and high                  pressure at 5-10 MPa, preferably 240-270degreesC                  and 7-9 MPa.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (c), the                  adding amount of mercaptopropyl triethoxysilane                  is 0.7-3 times of the mass of iron oxide in                  carrier.

Preferred Conditions: The reaction pressure is 0.1-3000 psi. The reaction temperature of the carbon salt upon adsorption of the carbon dioxide to the LTA zeolite adsorbent impregnated with the metal salt is (-)20-100 degrees C. The mixed solution is performed for 24-72 hours at a temperature condition of from room temperature to 80 degrees C. The drying is performed under a temperature condition of 80-130 degrees C. The pre-treatment step is performed under a temperature condition of 350 degrees C or less. The mixed solution contains the zeolite adsorbent and the alkali metal salt in a ratio of 10:1-5:1.

The organic phosphonate is organic phosphonic acid sodium salt, organic phosphonic acid potassium salt or organic phosphonic acid ammonium salt. The organic phosphonic acid comprises aminotrimethylenephosphonic acid, ethylenediamine tetramethylene phosphonate, hydroxyethylidene diphosphonic acid, diethylenetriamine pentamethylphosphonic acid, 2-phosphonate butane-1,2,4-tricarboxylic acid, 2-hydroxyphosphonic acid, hexamethylene diamine tetramethylene phosphonate, polyaminopolyether methylene phosphonic acid, or diethylenetriamine penta(methylene phosphonic acid) heptasaodium salt. The organic phosphonate comprises aminotrimethylenephosphonate, ethylenediamine tetramethylene phosphonate, hydroxyethylidene diphosphonate, diethylenetriamine pentamethylphosphonate, 2-phosphonate butane-1,2,4-tricarboxylate, 2-hydroxyphosphonic acid acetate, hexamethylene diamine tetramethylene phosphonate, polyaminopolyether methylene phosphonate or diethylenetriamine penta(methylene phosphonic acid) heptasaodium salt. The foam stabilizer is alkanolamide nonionic surfactant, and cocoic acid diethanolamide (preferred). The foaming agent comprises fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate and/or alpha -olefin sulfonate. The alpha -olefin sulfonate is sodium alpha -olefin sulfonate having a carbon number distribution of 12-18, preferably 14-16. The fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate comprises fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sodium sulfate, fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether ammonium sulfate or fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether potassium sulfate. The molecular structure of fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether sulfate is RO(ethylene oxide)n-sulfur trioxide-X. The water is formation water, tap water, ground water or reservoir refilling water. The high calcium magnesium reservoir has more than 400 mg/l divalent ion content.R = alkyl group of 12-15C;n = 2-4; andX = Na, K or NH4.

PN CN108041779-A; CN108041779-B
TI Method for optimization of amber based on supercritical fluid technology, involves pretreating amber stones and placing in autoclave followed by evacuating with carbon dioxide, closing autoclave and introducing high-pressure carbon dioxide.
AU CHENG X
   CENG J
   HOU S
   ZENG J
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 201840173V
AB    NOVELTY - An amber optimization method involves pretreating amber stones to obtain pretreated amber. The pretreated amber is placed in an autoclave and evacuated with carbon dioxide. The autoclave is closed, and high-pressure carbon dioxide is introduced. The temperature is raised to 100-180 degrees C, when the pressure in the autoclave is 8-12 MPa, to form supercritical carbon dioxide, amber swelling in the supercritical carbon dioxide environment, followed by releasing pressure and cooling after processing with beeswax to obtain beeswax-treated amber.
   USE - Method for optimization of amber based on supercritical fluid technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables optimizing the amber with no influence on chemical components, and reduced transparency and glass transition temperature to form bubbles that distributed uniformly and opalescent devitrification visible light with better reflecting effect by realizing the environment-friendly foaming, in simple, efficient and safe manner.

GA 202112899N

PN WO2020006636-A1; CA3102302-A1; US2021107840-A1; EP3818028-A1; US11124459-B2; US2021380494-A1; EP3818028-A4
TI Ready-mixed composition used for producing concrete material, comprises concrete mixture containing cementitious material and carbon dioxide-sequestering chemical, and carbon dioxide-containing water which is mixed with concrete mixture.
AU GONG B
AE CRH CANADA GROUP INC (CRHC-Non-standard)
   CRH GROUP CANADA INC (CRHC-Non-standard)
   CRH GROUP CANADA INC (CRHC-Non-standard)
   CRH GROUP CANADA INC (CRHC-Non-standard)
   CRH CANADA GROUP INC (CRHC-Non-standard)
GA 202004628E
AB    NOVELTY - A ready-mixed composition comprises a concrete mixture comprising a cementitious material, aggregates and at least one carbon dioxide-sequestering chemical for accelerating a carbon dioxide sequestration speed and maximizing the captured amount of the carbon dioxide, and carbon dioxide-containing water comprising water and at least one of blended carbon dioxide gas bubbles, dissolved carbonic acid, carbonate ions, bicarbonate ions, nanosized alkaline earth metal carbonate and nanosized alkali metal carbonate particles. The carbon dioxide-containing water is mixed with the concrete mixture in conditions allowing workability of the ready-mixed composition.
   USE - Ready-mixed composition e.g. pre-mix composition used for producing concrete material such as carbonated concrete material using system e.g. ready-mixed truck for producing wet carbonated concrete mixture (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The ready-mixed composition has excellent workability and concrete properties such as strength, permeability and durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) pre-mix composition;
   (2) preparation of carbonated concrete material;
   (3) sequestering carbon dioxide in hardening concrete;
   (4) sequestering carbon dioxide in concrete material;
   (5) system; and
   (6) ready-mixed truck.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Components: The cementitious material comprises at least one of cement and supplementary cementitious materials (SCMs). The cement is hydraulic cement, optionally Portland cement. The SCMs comprise fly ash, ground granulated blast furnace slag (GGBS), silica fume, siliceous material, aluminosilicate material, calcined clay, metakaolin, and/or ground glass. The aggregates comprise sand, gravel, and/or crushed stone. The concrete further comprises admixtures comprising at least one of water reducer, air entrainment agent, superplasticizer, accelerator, viscosity modifier, shrinkage reducer, expansion agent, and waterproofing agent. The concrete mixture further comprises fibers, color pigments, and/or mineral fillers. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of carbon dioxide-containing water to total cementitious material is 0.15-2.0. The weight ratio of admixture to cementitious material is 0-10%. The weight ratio of carbon dioxide-sequestering chemical to cementitious material is 0.5-10%. The ratio of carbon dioxide to water is 2-200 g/L, optionally 5-80 g/L. Preferred Properties: The ready-mixed composition has cementitious material per cubic meter concrete of 10-2000 kg/m3 and aggregate per cubic meter concrete ratio of at most 2000 kg/m3.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide-containing water comprises at least 1 kg/m3 carbon dioxide. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-sequestering chemicals comprise at least one alkali metal compound, and at least one alkaline-earth metal compound. The carbonation of cementitious material and the carbon monoxide sequestering chemical is performed according to the three following chemical reactions: (i) carbon dioxide is reacted with water to produce bicarbonate ion and proton, (ii) bicarbonate ion is converted to carbonate ion and proton, and (iii) metal ion (MA(power(a+))) including alkali-metal ion or alkaline-earth metal ion is reacted with carbonate ion to produce alkali metal/alkaline-earth metal compound (MAxCO3).

TI Preparing synthesis gas and compressed natural gas from coke oven gas and submerged arc furnace gas comprises e.g. compressing coke oven gas, converting carbon monoxide into hydrogen and carbon dioxide, performing pressure swing adsorption, and fine degassing.
AU TIAN L
   WANG J
   ZHANG Q
   ZHONG J
   YANG H
AE YANG H (YANG-Individual)
GA 202219195T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing synthesis gas and compressed natural gas (CNG) from coke oven gas and submerged arc furnace gas comprises e.g. compressing coke oven gas to 0.4-1.8MPa, converting the carbon monoxide into hydrogen and carbon dioxide i.e., change gas 1, where the concentration of carbon monoxide in the change gas 1 is less than or equal to 0.5%, performing pressure swing adsorption to remove hydrogen sulfide, organic sulfur, carbon dioxide, CnHm, and part of methane to obtain decarbonized gas, where the sum of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the decarburized gas is less than or equal to 1%, introducing the decarbonized gas to fine desulfurization and purification to obtain sulfur less than or equal to 0.1ppm, and fine degassing to obtain regeneration gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing synthesis gas and compressed natural gas from coke oven gas and submerged arc furnace gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes stable production of the synthesis gas system using submerged arc furnace gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing synthesis gas and compressed natural gas (CNG) from coke oven gas and submerged arc furnace gas comprises compressing coke oven gas to 0.4-1.8MPa, converting the carbon monoxide into hydrogen and carbon dioxide i.e., change gas 1, where the concentration of carbon monoxide in the change gas 1 is less than or equal to 0.5%, performing pressure swing adsorption to remove hydrogen sulfide, organic sulfur, carbon dioxide, CnHm, and part of methane to obtain decarbonized gas, where the sum of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the decarburized gas is less than or equal to 1%, introducing the decarbonized gas to fine desulfurization and purification to obtain sulfur less than or equal to 0.1ppm, and fine degassing to obtain regeneration gas, where the regeneration gas flows back into the coke oven fuel gas system, introducing the fine degassing to the methanation purification unit to remove carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to the sum of less than or equal to 10ppm i.e., methanation gas, introducing the methanation gas into the pressure swing adsorption purification device to produce CNG with methane concentration of not greater than 95%, where the toxic gas concentration and calorific value of CNG meet the gas standard, and obtaining crude degassing with methane concentration of less than or equal to 8%, introducing crude degassing to the pressure swing adsorption fine degassing unit to separate methane to concentration of less than or equal to 0.5%, i.e., synthesis gas 1, introducing the degassing gas to the coke oven gas system, supplementing the shortage of coke oven gas with Part of the submerged arc furnace gas, compressing part of it, introducing into the change gas 2, where the change gas 2 removes carbon dioxide to obtain synthesis gas 2, and obtaining high-concentration carbon dioxide, directly discharging part of high-concentration carbon dioxide, adding part of carbon dioxide to oxygen-enriched or pure oxygen as combustion-supporting gas for coke ovens to adjust the flame height, when the gas flow rate of the submerged arc furnace is large, more CNG is produced, and when the gas flow rate of the submerged arc furnace is small, less CNG is produced, and realizing the stable production of the synthesis gas system.

TI Electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide comprises e.g. using copper-based amorphous alloy complex as amorphous alloy strip including copper, zirconium, aluminum, and yttrium and amorphous alloy strip is prepared under normal temperature.
AU MA G
   ZHANG H
   LU Z
AE UNIV SHENYANG (UNHE-C)
GA 201866527V
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide comprises e.g. using copper-based amorphous alloy complex as amorphous alloy strip and its composition includes copper, zirconium, aluminum, and yttrium, where amorphous alloy strip is prepared under normal temperature and pressure, and using potassium bicarbonate solution, where amorphous alloy strip is used as the working electrode, taking potassium bicarbonate solution as the electrode solution, using carbon dioxide as a carbon resource, and performing electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide, vacuuming the working chamber of the tungsten magnetron arc furnace, melting titanium metal through the strong oxidation reaction of titanium metal at high temperature to further reduce the partial pressure of oxygen in the working chamber, and then smelting the above-mentioned elemental raw materials to become a master alloy ingot and cooling the mother alloy ingot to room temperature with a tungsten magnetron arc furnace.
   USE - The method is useful for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes a high chemical activity on a surface of a copper-based amorphous alloy, has good electrical properties and stability by using a copper-based amorphous alloy; is simple and easy to control, and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide comprises using copper-based amorphous alloy complex as (Cu46Zr44.5Al7.5Y2) (I) amorphous alloy strip and its composition includes 46 at.% copper (Cu), 44.5 at.% zirconium (Zr), 7.5 at.% aluminum (Al), 2 at.% yttrium (Y), where amorphous alloy strip (I) is prepared by under normal temperature and pressure, and using potassium bicarbonate solution, where amorphous alloy strip (I) is used as the working electrode, taking potassium bicarbonate solution as the electrode solution, using carbon dioxide as a carbon resource, and performing electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide; (ii) vacuuming the working chamber of the tungsten magnetron arc furnace to 7x104Pa, and then entering the purity of 99.99 wt.% pure argon, melting titanium metal through the strong oxidation reaction of titanium metal at high temperature to further reduce the partial pressure of oxygen in the working chamber, and then smelting the above-mentioned elemental raw materials to become a master alloy ingot, cooling the mother alloy ingot to room temperature with a tungsten magnetron arc furnace, and taking out the mother alloy ingot; and (iii) descaling the above-mentioned smelted master alloy ingot, crushing into small pieces, then placing 50 g small alloy ingot into the lower belt after having a small hole (diameter 1-1.5 mm) in a quartz tube, placing a quartz tube with a small alloy into the induction furnace, waiting until the working chamber is vacuumed to 2x103 Pa, and then performing induction melting, after the small alloy changes from solid to alloy melt, and the temperature of the alloy melt reaches the preset temperature, the alloy melt in the quartz tube is sprayed onto the surface of the high-speed rotating copper roller with high-purity argon gas to form (Cu46Zr44.5Al7.5Y2) amorphous alloy strip; (iv) using above prepared amorphous alloy strip (I) as electrode, taking 1-3 ml of 40% hydrofluoric acid solution, adding deionized water; and (v) carrying out the experiment in an H-type quartz electrode cell under normal temperature and pressure, separating the electrolysis cell into a cathode chamber and an anode chamber by a proton exchange membrane, and the two chamber electrolytes are all aqueous potassium hydrogen carbonate, catalytically reducing the electrode before the carbon dioxide, purging the cathode of the chamber electrolyte with nitrogen for 30 minutes to remove oxygen from the electrolyte, introducing carbon dioxide for 30 minutes, where the concentration of carbon dioxide in the catholyte is saturated, and continuously supplying carbon dioxide, and controlling the flow rate to 10-30 ml/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The mother alloy is prepared by smelting the amorphous alloy strip (I) to ensure the chemical composition uniformity of the master alloy ingot, and refining the master alloy ingot for at least 5 times. In the method, the electrode is etched, the etched electrode is placed in anhydrous hexanol, ultrasonically cleaning for 5 minutes, and then washing in distilled water for 5 minutes, and blow-drying in an empty beaker with a hair dryer. The electrode is catalytically reduced by carbon dioxide, and amorphous alloy strip (I), the platinum sheet and the saturated calomel electrode are respectively working electrode, counter electrode and reference electrode, taking samples by timed sampling, AND using gas chromatography for qualitative and quantitative analysis.

PN CN114130398-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide hydrogenation methanol            catalyst derived from zinc-based coordination polymer            comprises adding methanol solution of zinc acetate in            chloroform, dropping solution containing copper nitrate            and zirconium nitrate, reacting, vacuuming, roasting,            and reducing catalyst matrix.
AU CHEN S
   QITIAN Q
   ZHANG Y
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2022389588
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises (a) adding methanol solution                of zinc acetate with concentration of 0.1-8.0 mol/l                into the solution with chloroform 0.02-4 mol/l                hexamethylenetetramine, reacting for 8-20 hours at                room temperature to generate solution containing                Zn-based coordination polymer of two dimensional                structure, solution of Zn-HMT, (ii) dropping the                solution containing copper nitrate and zirconium                nitrate into the solution of Zn-HMT obtained in                step (i), reacting for 6-20 hours at 30-60degreesC                to obtain the blue green suspension, (iii)                vacuuming and distilling the blue green turbid                liquid in step (ii) to remove methanol and                chloroform solvent in the turbid liquid, obtaining                the blue green powder solid, Cu-Zr/Zn-HMT powder,                (iv) drying, roasting at 300-600degreesC, sieving                to obtain the catalyst precursor for CO2                hydrogenation methanol reaction, (v) reducing the                catalyst matrix in the step (iv) in the mixture of                H2/N2 with the volume ratio of 5-20% at                200-400degreesC for 2-6 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide hydrogenation methanol catalyst derived                from zinc-based coordination polymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has a methanol space-time yield                of 140% higher than that of the Cu/ZnO/ZrO2                catalyst prepared by the impregnation method; and                has good stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for:                4
   (1) Preparing carbon dioxide hydrogenation                methanol catalyst derived from zinc-based                coordination polymer comprises (a) adding methanol                solution of zinc acetate with concentration of                0.1-8.0 mol/l into the solution with chloroform                0.02-4 mol/l hexamethylenetetramine, reacting for                8-20 hours at room temperature to generate solution                containing Zn-based coordination polymer of two                dimensional structure, solution of Zn-HMT, where                the mol ratio of zinc acetate to                hexamethylenetetramine is 0.5-5, (ii) dropping the                solution containing copper nitrate and zirconium                nitrate into the solution of Zn-HMT obtained in                step (i), reacting for 6-20 hours at 30-60degreesC                to obtain the blue green suspension, where the mol                ratio of copper nitrate and zirconium nitrate is                2-8, the concentration of copper nitrate in the                solution containing copper nitrate and zirconium                nitrate is 0.02-0.2 mol/l, the concentration of                zirconium nitrate is 0.01-1.6 mol/l, (iii)                vacuuming and distilling the blue green turbid                liquid in step (ii) to remove methanol and                chloroform solvent in the turbid liquid, obtaining                the blue green powder solid, Cu-Zr @Zn-HMT powder,                (iv) drying the Cu-Zr @Zn-HMT powder in the step                (iii), roasting at 300-600degreesC, sieving to                obtain the catalyst precursor for CO2 hydrogenation                methanol reaction, (v) reducing the catalyst matrix                in the step (iv) in the mixture of H2/N2 with the                volume ratio of 5-20% at 200-400degreesC for 2-6                hours to obtain the catalyst for preparing methanol                by CO2 hydrogenation.
   (2) Use of carbon dioxide hydrogenation                methanol catalyst in preparing methanol by CO2                hydrogenation comprising (i) performing carbon                dioxide hydrogenation to form methanol at                200-300degrees Celsius, (ii) ensuring reaction                space velocity of 3600-60000 ml/(g h), (ii)                ensuring the reaction pressure of 2-5 MPa, and (iv)                ensuring the hydrogen-to-carbon ratio of the                reaction raw material gas of 2-4.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent of the                  solution containing copper nitrate and zirconium                  nitrate in step (ii) comprises water, methanol,                  ethanol, and/or N, N-dimethyl formamide.Preferred Method: The step (iii) comprises                  vacuuming and filtering at room temperature. The                  step (iv) comprises drying at 50-80degreesC, and                  sieving mesh number of 20-40 meshes.

TI Microbial fermentation device useful for producing marine plant seaweed fertilizer, comprises e.g. box body, one side of box body set with sealing door, and other side of box body orderly set with multiple drawer ports from top to bottom.
AU WANG S
   MAO Q
   CAO L
AE SHANDONG SUNWAY SALINE-ALKALI SOIL TECHN (SUAW-C)
GA 202075968E
AB    NOVELTY - Microbial fermentation device comprises box body (1). The one side of the box body is set with sealing door (2). The other side of the box body is orderly provided with multiple drawer ports (11) from top to bottom. The lower part of drawer port passes through one side of the box body and set with transverse pipe (20). The upper part and lower part of the transverse pipe is respectively connected with spraying head. The top of the box body is connected with horn-shaped exhaust port (6). The top of the box body is fixedly connected with carbon dioxide storage tank. The aerobic bacteria liquid storage tank and anaerobic bacteria liquid storage tank is respectively connected to the two inlets of the reversing valve through pipeline. The outlet of the reversing valve is connected with the inlet of infusion pump through pipeline. The outlet of the infusion pump is connected with conveying pipe. And the one side of the conveying pipe is respectively connected with one end of transverse pipe.
   USE - The microbial fermentation device is useful for producing marine plant seaweed fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The device spays bacteria liquid directly from upper and bottom of the drawer box to facilitate the fast penetration of the bacteria liquid, saves fermentation time, and quickly and conveniently switch between oxygen-free environment and aerobic environment to improve fermentation efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for processing microbial fermentation production of seaweed fertilizer, comprising (i) crushing the prepared algae raw material to obtain algae powder, sieving the algae powder according to the diameter size of the powder particle into two groups, where group A is fine powder particle, group B is coarse powder particle, (ii) paving group B of coarse powder on the bottom mesh plate of the drawer box, laying the group A of fine powder on the coarse powder of the drawer box, installing the drawer box on the box body, using the locking hand wheel for pressing and keeping sealing, filling all the drawer opening on the box body, and pushing the box body to the glass sunshine room, (iii) controlling the air valve to open by the operation panel, slowly releasing the carbon dioxide, where the density of the carbon dioxide is greater than the air density, the carbon dioxide sink into the box body, extruding the air in the box body from the top, and forming the oxygen-free environment, (iv) controlling the reversing valve, the infusion pump pumps anaerobic bacteria liquid to spray the upper part and bottom of the drawer box, and finishing the bacterial liquid spraying from the upper surface and the lower surface, (v) fermenting for 10-15 days under the oxygen-free environment, opening the sealing door, releasing carbon dioxide for 2-3 hours, closing the sealing door, controlling the reversing valve, the infusion pump extracting aerobic bacteria liquid to spray the upper part and bottom of the drawer box, finishing the bacteria liquid spraying of aerobic bacteria, opening the sealing door, and fermenting 5-10 day in the aerobic environment, and (vi) finishing aerobic fermentation, removing out the drawer box and powder in the inner part and drying, sieving, crushing the crude powder after the bottom fermentation, obtaining the fine powder by mixing with the unfermented fine powder for fermenting or fine powder obtained by mixing with the fermented group A of fine powder to obtain seaweed fertilizer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic representation of the microbial fermentation device for producing marine plant seaweed fertilizer.
   Box body (1)
   Sealing door (2)
   Horn-shaped exhaust port (6)
   Multiple drawer ports (11)
   Transverse pipe (20)

GA 202050714E
AB    NOVELTY - An apparatus comprises supply unit (101),                synthesis gas generation unit (103) which generates                a synthesis gas containing carbon monoxide and                hydrogen from methane and carbon dioxide contained                in the source gas, while heating the catalyst                structure (C1), supply unit (106), and hydrocarbon                generation unit (107) which generates a hydrocarbon                containing a lower olefin containing propylene and                an 6-10C aromatic hydrocarbon from synthesis gas,                while heating catalyst structure (C2). The catalyst                structure (C1) includes a support (a) having a                porous structure comprising zeolite-type compound,                and catalyst substance (a1). The support (a) has a                channel (I). The catalyst substance (a1) is metal                fine particles (p1), and present in at least                channel (I) of the support (a). The catalyst                structure (C2) includes a support (b) having a                porous structure comprising zeolite-type compound,                and at least one metal fine particles (p2). A                portion of channel (II) has average inner diameter                of 0.95 nm or less.
   USE - Apparatus used for manufacturing hydrocarbon                such as lower olefin such as propylene and 6-10C                aromatic hydrocarbon (claimed) used as raw material                for manufacturing liquid and solid fuel products                such as synthetic oil and synthetic fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus provides the simple and                efficient manufacture of hydrocarbon having                excellent selectivity and suppressed decrease in                catalytic activity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An apparatus comprises supply unit (101) for                supplying a source gas containing methane and                carbon dioxide, synthesis gas generation unit (103)                which generates a synthesis gas containing carbon                monoxide and hydrogen from methane and carbon                dioxide contained in the source gas which is                supplied from unit (101), while heating the                catalyst structure (C1), supply unit (106) which                supplies the synthesis gas discharged from the unit                (103), hydrocarbon generation unit (107) which                generates a hydrocarbon containing a lower olefin                containing propylene and an 6-10C aromatic                hydrocarbon from carbon monoxide and hydrogen                contained in the synthesis gas, which is supplied                from unit (106), heating the catalyst structure                (C2), and detection unit (109) for detecting                propylene and the aromatic hydrocarbon in the                hydrocarbon discharged from the hydrocarbon                generation unit. The catalyst structure (C1)                includes a support (a) having a porous structure                comprising zeolite-type compound, and at least one                catalyst substance (a1) contained in the support                (a). The support (a) has a channel (I) which                communicating with the outside of the support (a).                The catalyst substance (a1) is metal fine particles                (p1), and present in at least the channel (I) of                the support (a). The catalyst structure (C2)                includes a support (b) having a porous structure                comprising zeolite-type compound, and at least one                metal fine particles (p2) contained in the support                (b). The support (b) has a channel (II) which                communicating with the outside of the support (b).                The portion of channel (II) has average inner                diameter of 0.95 nm or less. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is included for manufacture of hydrocarbon, which                involves supplying a source gas containing methane                and carbon dioxide to the catalyst structure (C1),                generating a synthesis gas containing carbon                monoxide and hydrogen from methane and carbon                dioxide contained in the source gas while heating                the catalyst structure (C1), supplying the obtained                synthesis gas to the catalyst structure (C2), and                producing a hydrocarbon containing lower olefin                containing propylene and 6-10C aromatic hydrocarbon                from carbon monoxide and hydrogen contained in the                synthesis gas while heating the catalyst structure                (C2) and detecting propylene and the aromatic                hydrocarbon in the hydrocarbon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of each of                the main components constituting the                apparatus.
   Apparatus (100)
   Supply unit (101,106,116,117,118)
   Synthesis gas generation unit (103)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The hydrogen                  production apparatus further consists of an                  oxygen supply unit (114) that supplies oxygen                  having a concentration below the lower explosion                  limit to the synthesis gas generation unit,                  separation unit (105) which separates methane and                  carbon dioxide in the synthesis gas discharged                  from the synthesis gas generation unit, supply                  unit (116) which supplies the methane and carbon                  dioxide separated in the separation unit (105) to                  the synthesis gas generation unit, detection unit                  (115) which detects carbon monoxide and hydrogen                  in the synthesis gas discharged from the                  synthesis gas generation unit, and separation                  unit (111) that separates the hydrocarbon, carbon                  monoxide, and hydrogen discharged from the                  hydrocarbon generation unit, and supply unit                  (117) which is configured to supply the carbon                  monoxide and hydrogen separated by the separation                  unit (111) to the hydrocarbon generation unit,                  separation unit (113) which separates the                  hydrocarbon, methane, and carbon dioxide                  discharged from the hydrocarbon generation unit,                  supply unit (118) that supplies the methane and                  carbon dioxide separated by the separation unit                  (110) to the synthesis gas generation and                  separation unit (112) that cools the hydrocarbon                  discharged from the hydrocarbon generation unit                  and separates the lower olefin and the aromatic                  hydrocarbon. The synthesis gas generation unit                  heats the catalyst structure (C1) at 600-1000                  degrees C. The hydrocarbon generation unit heats                  the catalyst structure (C2) at 350-550 degrees                  C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal fine                  particles (p1) are chosen from rhodium,                  ruthenium, iridium, palladium, platinum, iron,                  cobalt and nickel. The channel (I) is one of                  one-dimensional hole, a two-dimensional hole and                  a three-dimensional hole of the skeleton                  structure of the zeolite type compound, and has                  expanded portion (P1) that is different from                  one-dimensional hole, the two-dimensional hole,                  and the three-dimensional hole, and catalyst                  substance (105) which is present in at least the                  expanded diameter portion (P1). The enlarged                  diameter portion (P1) communicates several holes                  forming of one-dimensional hole, the                  two-dimensional hole and the three-dimensional                  hole. The average particle diameter of the fine                  metal particles (p1) is larger than the average                  inner diameter of the channel (I) and is equal to                  or smaller than the inner diameter of the                  expanded diameter portion (P1). The metal fine                  particles (p2) comprises at least one metal (i)                  chosen from ruthenium, nickel, iron and cobalt,                  or ruthenium (Ru), nickel (Ni), iron (Fe) or                  cobalt, and metal (ii) chosen from platinum,                  palladium, gold, silver, copper, ruthenium,                  iridium, rhodium, osmium, zirconium and                  manganese. The channel (II) is one of                  one-dimensional hole, a two-dimensional hole and                  a three-dimensional hole of the skeleton                  structure of the zeolite type compound, and has                  expanded portion (P2) that is different from                  one-dimensional hole, the two-dimensional hole,                  and the three-dimensional hole, and metal fine                  particles (p2) which are present in at least the                  expanded diameter portion (P2). Preferred                  Property: The portion of channel (II) has average                  inner diameter of preferably 0.39-0.75 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aromatic                  hydrocarbon includes aromatic hydrocarbon                  compound of formula (I) and at least one compound                  of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons. The compound                  (I) is preferably at least one compound chosen                  from benzene, toluene, and butylbenzene. The                  polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon contains a                  compound of at least one of azulene and                  naphthalene.R1-R6 = H or 1-4C alkyl.

PT P
PN CN108262016-A
TI Removing carbon dioxide from a mixed gas using steel slag, comprises e.g. grinding steelmaking steel slag, dissolving steel slag material of mixed activated carbon in deionized water, performing suction filtration, drying and calcining.
AU ZHU M
   YUAN J
   WANG F
   ZHAO J
   WANG J
AE UNIV NINGBO (UYNB-C)
GA 2018592107
AB    NOVELTY - Removing carbon dioxide from a mixed gas using steel slag, comprises (i) grinding steelmaking steel slag; (ii) adding a suitable amount of activated carbon material, steel slag composed of a steel slag material containing 80% or more of steel slag material and activated carbon material, and obtaining mixed activated carbon, dissolving steel slag material of mixed activated carbon in deionized water, adding steel slag mixture material and pyrrole/100 ml water, then adding ammonium persulfate, reacting, naturally reducing the temperature to room temperature, collecting the product, performing suction filtration, washing with deionized water, drying under vacuum at 60-100 degrees C to obtain a product, calcining the dry product under the protection of inert gas, cooling, washing and drying to obtain steel slag material; (iii) loading the obtained steel slag material into a reactor; and (iv) drying the steel slag material to a constant weight, performing impurity removal treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for removing carbon dioxide from a mixed gas using steel slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes low cost raw materials, close to the emission source, no pretreatment, has rapid reaction speed and mild reaction conditions and promotes good adsorption effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Removing carbon dioxide from a mixed gas using steel slag, comprises (i) grinding steelmaking steel slag; (ii) adding a suitable amount of activated carbon material, steel slag composed of a steel slag material containing 80% or more of steel slag material and 20% of activated carbon material, and obtaining mixed activated carbon, dissolving steel slag material of mixed activated carbon in deionized water, adding 2-8 g steel slag mixture material and 2-15 g pyrrole/100 ml water, then adding 3-8 g ammonium persulfate, reacting at 30-90 degrees C for 6-10 hours, naturally reducing the temperature to room temperature, collecting the product, performing suction filtration, washing with deionized water, drying under vacuum at 60-100 degrees C to obtain a product, calcining the dry product under the protection of inert gas at 500-1000 degrees C, cooling, washing and drying to obtain steel slag material; (iii) loading the obtained steel slag material into a reactor; and (iv) drying the steel slag material to a constant weight, performing impurity removal treatment, then vacuuming by applying vacuum valve (11), performing pressure reduction treatment, closing the vacuum valve, starting the molecular valve operation, and continuing the pressure reduction treatment, purging high-purity helium gas into steel slag material and placing the steel slag material into an adsorption tube (10), after vacuuming, supplying mixed gas using a mixed gas source (1), vacuuming using vacuum valve, pumping rate is -0.095Mpa, monitoring by a pressure display (2), then opening the cocks K1, K2, closing the cock K3, passing the mixed gas, passing into a drying tank (3) and a rotor flow rate, drying the mixed gas from the drying tank, and testing the mixing gas in rotor flow rate, gas flow rate is until the flow is stabilized at a flow rate of about 1570 ml/minute, cocks K3 and K4 are opened, the cocks K2 and K5 are closed, the mixed gas is introduced into the adsorption tube and is carried out in a constant temperature water bath, adsorption until the adsorption is saturated, then the gas is introduced into the gas buffer tank and finally the pressure is reduced to normal pressure by the pressure controller at room temperature, storing the desorbed inert gas in the container to obtain mixed gas for removing carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device utilized in the above mentioned method.
   Mixed gas source (1)
   Pressure display (2)
   Drying tank (3)
   Adsorption tube (10)
   Vacuum valve (11)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The steel slag is ground to about 200-250 mesh, and the steel slag material is contained in an amount of 80% or more based on the total weight of the steel slag material, and steel slag material and 20% activated carbon material, the steel slag material has a specific surface area of 2000-2300 m2/g, total pore volume is 2.3-3 cm3/g, medium pore volume is 1.4-2 cm3/g, and average pore diameter is 2.3-2.8 Nm. Preferred Method: The steel slag is obtained by (a) dissolving steel slag material of the mixed activated carbon in deionized water, adding 2-8 g steel slag mixture material and 2-15 g pyrrole per 100 ml of water, adding 3-8 g ammonium persulfate, where reaction temperature is 30-90 degrees C, the reaction time is 6-10 hours, and the temperature is naturally lowered to room temperature; (b) collecting the solid product, suction filtration, washing with deionized water, vacuum drying at 60-100 degrees C to obtain a product; and (c) calcining the dry product under the protection of an inert gas at 500-1000 degrees C, cooling, washing and drying to obtain the steel slag material. The steel slag material is heated to 320-420 degrees C in a heating furnace to remove impurities. The adsorption time is set by physical adsorption time controlled to about 5 hours. The grinding time of the step (i) is 6-8 hours. The heating furnace is an electric resistance heater, an infrared heater and a microwave heater.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Carbon compression mechanism of ship combustion machine carbon capture device, has bracket whose outer side wall is installed with heat exchange fan distributed with equal distance structure through bolt.
AU ZHOU X
AE SHANGHAI SANQING ENVIRONMENTAL SCI & TEC (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202233545R
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon compression mechanism of carbon capture device of ship combustion engine. comprising a mounting plate and a carbon dioxide compressor main body, the outer wall of the top part of the carbon dioxide compressor main body is provided with a constant temperature box through a bolt; and one end of the outer wall of the bottom of the constant temperature box is welded with an outlet pipe connected with the input end of the carbon dioxide compressor main body. The temperature sensor of the utility model can monitor the temperature of carbon dioxide, when the carbon dioxide temperature is too high, the heat exchange fan induced airflow to cool the snake-shaped pipe, and the fin greatly improves the cooling efficiency of the carbon dioxide, when the temperature sensor monitors the carbon dioxide temperature is low; the heating sleeve can heat the carbon dioxide through the snake-shaped pipe, and the distribution of the spoiler and the fin is used, which greatly improves the heat exchange efficiency of the carbon dioxide in the snake-shaped pipe, so that the carbon dioxide compressor main body avoids surge phenomenon caused by excessive carbon dioxide temperature; it reaches the effect of ensuring the device safety and personal safety

PN CN111914388-A
TI Method for calculating urban natural factor carbon dioxide concentration based on average minimum concentration, involves obtaining minimum carbon dioxide concentration value in concentration value by calculating concentration.
AU HUANG R
   LAN L
   LUO Q
   YANG C
   GUI W
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 2020B38525
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves collecting near surface carbon dioxide concentration value in a period of time of a to-be-tested city area, atmosphere boundary layer height corresponding to time. Time average calculation process is performed to obtain a set of measured concentration and a change sequence of related weather data. Data are grouped according to different hours and time. A height average value of an atmosphere boundary layer in the hour group is determined. An atmosphere boundary layer height lower than a group average value is selected. A minimum carbon dioxide concentration value in the carbon dioxide concentration value is obtained by calculating carbon dioxide concentration under a natural factor condition of the hour group. A time-concentration curve graph is determined as a natural factor carbon dioxide concentration day change curve in the period of time.
   USE - Method for calculating urban natural factor carbon dioxide concentration based on average minimum concentration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables comprehensively obtaining meteorological data, reducing calculation amount to accurately calculate ratio of the natural factor leading carbon dioxide concentration change for researching artificial carbon dioxide emission in urban area.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for calculating urban natural factor carbon dioxide concentration based on average minimum concentration. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

   DENG S
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2022A3971A
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a reaction tank whose inner                part is provided with a anode cylinder and a                cathode cylinder from outside. The anode cylinder                is driven to rotate along the axis of itself. The                inner cylinder wall of the reaction tank is                provided with a peeling body (10) attached on a                rotary cylinder anode precipitate. The reaction                tank is connected with an air storage tank through                a gas collecting pipeline (7). The reaction tank is                connected to the external soil sealing area through                a sedimentation recycling pipeline (9). The cathode                cylinder is made of the graphite.

The energy utilization efficiency                of the device can reach 62.03 percent. The cathode                and anode material with good electrochemical                performance can be used, the rotary anode is                rotated at a suitable rotating speed clockwise the                iron anode net reaches the state of the deposition                attachment saturation, after the reaction tank                inner wall stripping body, using seawater to                deposit the calcium carbonate precipitate in the                form of suspension to discharge or seal, therefore,                it can satisfy the requirement of continuous                capturing.

TI Converter for monitoring respiratory powder carbon dioxide of patient, has converter main body that is provided with space for circulating gas, and sampling tube with channel connected with interface, where diameter of channel is less than inner diameter of another interface.

   WANG Y
   WU G
AE SHANDONG QIANFOSHAN HOSPITAL (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202264360Y
AB    NOVELTY - The converter has a converter main body that is provided with a space for circulating gas. An outer wall of the converter body is provided with the first and second interfaces. The first interface and the second interface are connected through a first channel. The third interface is used for connecting a nasal oxygen tube. The fourth interface is utilized for connecting an end of a main flow type respiration tail carbon dioxide monitor sampler with a larger diameter. A first sampling tube is fixed with a second channel that is connected with the fourth interface. The diameter of the second channel is less than the inner diameter of a fourth interface, where the second sampling tube comprises a first sample tube and a second sample tube that is connected with each other.
   USE - Converter for monitoring respiratory powder carbon dioxide of a patient. Uses include but are not limited to blood oxygen saturation, cardiac output, lung perfusion quantity, and ventilation.
   ADVANTAGE - The converter realizes each type of respiratory pipeline of the multiple types present in the present world. The conversion between the oxygen-absorbing pipeline and the respiration tail carbon dioxide monitoring instrument realizes the monitoring of all patient respiring carbon dioxide used by the non-breathing loop. The converter is provided with a first interface and a second interface on the converter main body, realizing the sampling of the exhaled patient when using the oxygen mask and the nasal suction oxygen therapy pipe, at the same time, the fourth interface is used for connecting one end of the main flow type respiration carbon dioxide monitor sampler with large diameter, to realize the detection of the patient gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for using a converter for monitoring respiratory powder carbon dioxide.
