PT P
PN US2019093246-A1; US10900134-B2
TI Producing and purifying carbon and recovering lithium carbonate by reducing carbonate ions at cathode in molten carbonate salt electrolyte in electrolysis reactor to form carbon, and producing a solid reaction product.
AU REID D L
   RUSH B M
   AHUMADA D R
   REID K
AE SARATOGA ENERGY RES PARTNERS LLC (SARA-Non-standard)
   SARATOGA ENERGY CORP (SARA-Non-standard)
GA 2019281072
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon is produced and purified and lithium carbonate is recovered by reducing carbonate ions at a cathode (305) in molten carbonate salt electrolyte in electrolysis reactor (300) to form carbon, where molten carbonate salt electrolyte comprises lithium carbonate; producing a solid reaction product containing carbon, lithium oxide, and lithium carbonate from molten carbonate salt electrolyte; transferring solid reaction product to extraction vessel; providing carbon dioxide and hydrogen-donor solvent to extraction vessel at target pressure to (i) produce lithium bicarbonate from lithium carbonate, and (ii) form mixture containing purified solid carbon and solution of (a) lithium bicarbonate, (b) carbon dioxide, and (c) hydrogen-donor solvent; separating purified solid carbon from mixture; and removing carbon dioxide and hydrogen-donor solvent from solution to provide recovered lithium carbonate.
   USE - Production and purification of carbon and recovery of lithium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - Recovery and recyclability of lithium carbonate are improved. Material costs are reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) system for producing and purifying carbon and recovering lithium carbonate comprising electrolysis reactor (300) which produces reaction product by reducing carbonate ions at cathode (305) in a molten carbonate salt electrolyte; extraction vessel which holds carbon dioxide, hydrogen-donor solvent, and solid form of reaction product to (i) produce lithium bicarbonate from the lithium carbonate, and (ii) form mixture comprising purified solid carbon and solution of (a) lithium bicarbonate, (b) carbon dioxide, and (c) hydrogen-donor solvent; separator for separating the purified solid carbon from the mixture; and evaporation reactor which receives solution after the purified solid carbon is removed from the mixture where evaporation reactor evaporates the carbon dioxide and hydrogen-donor solvent from solution to provide recovered lithium carbonate; and
   (2) producing carbon in electrolytic reactor which involves reacting carbon dioxide with lithium-containing precursor and producing lithium carbonate; transferring lithium carbonate to electrolytic reactor; reducing carbonate ions formed from lithium carbonate to form elemental carbon at cathode of the electrolytic reactor, where reducing the carbonate ions is performed without introducing carbon dioxide to electrolytic reactor; and purifying the elemental carbon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the electrolysis accelerator.
   Electrolysis reactor (300)
   Container (301)
   Cathode (305)
   Anode (306)
   Scraper (312)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Method includes supplying the recovered lithium carbonate to electrolysis reactor. Removing carbon dioxide from solution precipitates the recovered lithium carbonate in hydrogen-donor solvent. After removing the hydrogen-donor solvent from solution, hydrogen-donor solvent removed from the solution is supplied into the extraction vessel. After removing the carbon dioxide from the solution, carbon dioxide removed from the solution is supplied to extraction vessel. Providing carbon dioxide to the extraction vessel comprises providing a feed stream of carbon dioxide to extraction vessel and providing the carbon dioxide removed from the solution to extraction vessel. Removing the carbon dioxide from the solution converts the lithium bicarbonate to the recovered lithium carbonate. Preferred Components: Purified solid carbon comprises greater than or equal to 1 material selected from activated carbon, amorphous carbon, carbon nanotubes, graphite, graphene, and fullerenes. Purified solid carbon contains carbon nanotubes, graphite. Solid reaction product includes 5-50 wt.% carbon, 5-50 wt.% lithium oxide, and 5-50 wt.% lithium carbonate. Solid reaction product comprises 50-100 wt.% carbon, 0-30 wt.% lithium oxide, and 0-30 wt.% lithium carbonate. Purified solid carbon comprises less than or equal to 0.1 at.%, preferably less than or equal to 0.05 at.% lithium. Cathode comprises a transition metal. Anode in the electrolysis reactor comprises a transition metal. Cathode comprises iron. Anode in the electrolysis reactor comprises stainless steel. Hydrogen-donor solvent comprises water.

comprises the second heat exchanger, and a light-removing tower, and the top of the light-removing tower is provided with a tail gas outlet, and the bottom is provided with a finished product outlet, and the bottom of the light-removing tower is provided with a sixth heat exchanger, and the light component discharge port is connected to the light-removing tower after passing through the second heat exchanger, and the carbon dioxide gas discharged from the light-removing tower is condensed by the second heat exchanger and enters the light-removing tower, and the recombination is divided into high-purity carbon dioxide gas discharged through the finished product outlet, and tail gas discharged through the tail gas outlet.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate by                sucrose-assisted carbon dioxide mineralization,                comprises carrying out saccharification reaction,                mixing desulfurization slag or steel-making blast                furnace slag with sucrose solution for reaction,                and introducing carbon dioxide gas into the clear                liquid obtained by the reaction in the                mineralization reaction to generate calcium                carbonate.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing calcium                carbonate by sucrose-assisted carbon dioxide                mineralization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method not only realizes the effective                utilization of the calcium-containing component in                the solid waste desulfurization slag, but also                generates calcium carbonate product, it has a                certain economic value and environment-friendly                meaning. The components in the desulfurized slag                are relatively complex, where the calcium oxide,                calcium hydroxide and other alkaline component is                wrapped in the insoluble component, and hidden in                the self-hardening framework, so that the alkaline                components dissolution difficulty is large,                condition is harsh. The method utilizes sucrose                solution, which can not be affected by the self                hardening tendency of the raw material. The calcium                ion almost reaches the degree of completely                extracting, and the whole process does not need to                heating the pure calcium carbonates and can be used                for circulating sucrose solutions.

AU DONG Y
   SHANGGUAN Y
   JI G
   DONG J
AE ZHEJIANG JINGYANG ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202258128D
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves injecting a main fuel into a main combustion area. Coal powder is injected into a re-combustion area. Primary air is input for fuel combustion in the main combustion area and a re-combustion area. The main fuel is burned, where smoke generated by the main fuel combustion enters into the re-combustion area to ignite superfine coal powder and ignite flue gas. Targeting cluster air injection combustion is input to the re-combustion area to form a partial vacuum area in the re-combustion area. Large carbon dioxide flue gases reflux are passed to the main combustion area for refluxing to form secondary cracking and generating carbon monoxide interventional combustion.
   USE - Combustion method for reducing carbon dioxide emission concentration of a coal burning boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables injecting the upper and lower circulation technology, combustion in the appointed area by using targeted bunching wind, forming a partial vacuum area in the area, realizing top airflow rotation, forming upper and lower circulation, upper flue gas reflux, carrying large amount of carbon dioxide flue gases to high temperature and high carbon region to form secondary cracking, and generating carbon monoxide intervention combustion, so that the carbon dioxide concentration can be reduced more than 60 percentage, thus reducing nitrogen oxide generated in the main combustion area, and inhibiting generation of new nitrogen oxides.

TI Porous gypsum manufacturing method using high concentration waste sulfuric acid, involves agitating mixture, and preparing porous gypsum having pores formed by carbon dioxide and increasing steam generated as temperature of mixture.
AU SIN Y
   KIM J
   GOON S Y
AE SIN Y (SINY-Individual)
   GOON S Y (GOON-Individual)
GA 2020C7072R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing waste sulfuric acid by matching the concentration and temperature of waste sulfuric acid to a predetermined crystal concentration and crystal temperature and introducing the waste sulfuric acid into a reactor. The limestone powder is added to the reactor in a predetermined amount. A mixture is prepared by adjusting an input amount of the limestone powder in response to a predetermined critical temperature according to the number of inputs. The mixture is agitated, and a porous gypsum having pores formed by carbon dioxide is prepared and steam generated as the temperature of the mixture is increased.
   USE - Porous gypsum manufacturing method using high concentration waste sulfuric acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can reduce harmful substances, which are essential elements when used as an interior material that is in contact with the human body. The economic efficiency is increased by recovering pure carbon dioxide by removing sulfur gas from carbon dioxide generated by the reaction between waste sulfuric acid and limestone and selling carbon dioxide. The economic efficiency can be improved by producing purified high-purity carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a manufacturing apparatus for manufacturing porous gypsum.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram showing a porous gypsum manufacturing apparatus.

   Mixing space (110)

TI Performing carbon dioxide bioconversion process            involves providing gaseous substrate to bioreactor,            where gaseous substrate comprises carbon dioxide,            providing acetogenic bacteria to bioreactor, and            providing sodium ions to bioreactor.
AU SENARATNE R
   PRICE A
   BEARD B
   SENARATNE R H
   CAMARGO N A
   LACEY R M
   PRICE A J
   BEARD B L
AE JUPENG BIO INC (JUPE-Non-standard)
   JUPENG BIO INC (JUPE-Non-standard)
   JUPENG BIO INC (JUPE-Non-standard)
   JUPENG BIO INC (JUPE-Non-standard)
GA 202013958Y
AB    NOVELTY - Performing carbon dioxide bioconversion                process involves providing a gaseous substrate to a                bioreactor, where gaseous substrate comprises                carbon dioxide containing 5-90 mole.% carbon                dioxide, providing acetogenic bacteria to                bioreactor, and providing sodium ions to bioreactor                through one or more sodium ion sources. The method                involves fermenting gaseous substrate with                acetogenic bacteria in a fermentation broth                comprising acetogenic bacteria and one or more                sodium ion sources to produce one or more organic                acids. The acetogenic bacteria includes a sodium                translocating ATPase that is active during                fermentation in the bioreactor. The fermentation                broth includes less than 0.01 grams per liter yeast                extract, and less than 0.01 grams per liter                carbohydrate. The sodium ions are provided with a                sodium feed rate of 290-8750pg/gram of                cells/minute, where fermentation broth is                maintained at a pH in a range of 4-6.9.
   USE - Method for performing carbon dioxide                bioconversion process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                composition, which comprises one or more of a                source of ammonium positive ion, phosphorus,                potassium, iron, nickel, cobalt, selenium, zinc,                tungsten, or magnesium, 875-35000mg/L of a sodium                ion source and 0.009-0.397mg/L of a molybdenum                source, where composition includes less than 0.01                grams per liter yeast extract, and less than 0.01                grams per liter carbohydrates, and has a pH of                4-6.9.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  containing gaseous substrate is selected from a                  group consist of industrial gases, fermenter gas                  streams and its mixtures. The sodium ion source                  is provided by a compound selected from a group                  consist of sodium chloride, sodium hydroxide,                  sodium phosphate, sodium sulfate, sodium nitrate,                  sodium bicarbonate, sodium bisulfite and its                  mixtures. The organic acid is one or more 1-10C                  organic acid. The organic acid is acetic acid,                  butyric acid, or its mixtures. The composition                  comprises a complexing agent selected from a                  group consist of ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid,                  ethylenediaminediacetic acid,                  ethylenediaminedisuccinic acid and its mixtures.                  The nitrogen is provided from a nitrogen source                  selected from a group consist of ammonium                  hydroxide, ammonium chloride, ammonium phosphate,                  ammonium sulfate, ammonium nitrate, and its                  mixtures. The phosphorus is provided from a                  phosphorus source selected from group consist of                  phosphoric acid, ammonium phosphate, potassium                  phosphate, and its mixtures, and potassium is                  provided from a potassium source selected from a                  group consist of potassium chloride, potassium                  phosphate, potassium nitrate, potassium sulfate,                  and its mixtures. The iron is provided from an                  iron source selected from a group consist of                  ferrous chloride, ferrous sulfate, and its                  mixtures, nickel is provided from a nickel source                  selected from a group consist of nickel chloride,                  nickel sulfate, nickel nitrate, and its mixtures,                  cobalt is provided from a cobalt source selected                  from a group consist of cobalt chloride, cobalt                  fluoride, cobalt bromide, cobalt iodide, and its                  mixtures, selenium is provided from a selenium                  source selected from a group consist of sodium                  selenite, selenocysteine, and its mixtures, zinc                  is provided from zinc sulfate, tungsten is                  provided from a tungsten source selected from a                  group consist of sodium tungstate, calcium                  tungstate, potassium tungstate, and its mixtures,                  magnesium is provided from a magnesium source                  selected from a group consist of magnesium                  chloride, magnesium sulfate, magnesium phosphate,                  and sulfur is provided from a sulfur source                  selected from a group consist of cysteine, sodium                  sulfide, and its mixtures. Preferred Conditions:                  The acetogenic bacteria is selected from a group                  consist of Acetobacterium bacteria, Acetogenium                  kivui, Acetoanaerobium noterae, Acetobacterium                  woodii, Alkalibaculum bacchi CP11 (ATCC NO:                  BAA-1772), Moorella thermoacetica, Moorella                  thermoautotrophica, Ruminococcus productus, and                  its combinations, preferably Acetobacterium                  woodii. The method involves providing a gaseous                  substrate to a bioreactor, where gaseous                  substrate comprises carbon dioxide and hydrogen                  containing 5-90 mole.% carbon dioxide. The sodium                  ion concentration is 500-8000ppm. Preferred                  Compositions: The composition comprises                  82-3280mg/L ammonium positive ion source,                  20.12-805mg/L phosphorus source or 98.33-3933mg/L                  potassium source. The composition comprises                  0.85-34mg/L iron source, 0.07-2.81mg/L nickel                  source, 0.037-1.49mg/L cobalt source,                  0.027-1.1mg/L selenium source, 0.59-23.8mg/L zinc                  source, 80.25-3210mg/L tungsten source or                  0.71-28.69mg/L magnesium source.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

GA 201971981V
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to anticorrosive protection of metal structures. Method comprises cyclically exposing a surface of solid zinc metal or an alloy based thereon in a working atmosphere containing water and carbon dioxide, wherein at the beginning of the cycle, content of carbon dioxide in the working atmosphere is increased, which is higher compared to content of carbon dioxide in the natural atmosphere of the Earth, and conditions are created for formation on said surface of a heterogeneous reaction medium containing zinc, water and carbon dioxide, with the passage of chemical reactions in said medium involving water, carbon dioxide and zinc to form zinc carbonates. In the middle of the cycle, conditions for formation of a heterogeneous reaction medium on said surface and termination of said chemical reactions and the cycle is completed with formation on said surface of deposits containing zinc carbonates formed as a result of said chemical reactions during the cycle. Said deposits are formed as constituent parts of the obtained coating, and the number of exposure cycles is selected based on the desired degree of quality of the obtained coating. Invention covers also coating and galvanized structure.
   USE - Technological processes.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention provides an increase in the degree of corrosion protection of the surface of solid zinc metal or an alloy based thereon with a smaller thickness of the coating.

PN JP2022091900-A
TI Carbon dioxide emission amount estimation system for construction material used in building, calculates total carbon dioxide discharge amount and total cover rate by integrating carbon dioxide emission and cover ratio of direct calculation item.
AU KATO A
AE TOKYU CONSTR CO LTD (TOKY-Non-standard)
GA 2022837382
AB    NOVELTY - The system (1) has a cover ratio setting portion (31) that sets a cover ratio from an estimated use weight occupying the total weight of the building for a direct calculation item selected as an item for directly calculating the discharge amount of carbon dioxide. A direct discharge quantity calculation portion (32) calculates the respective carbon dioxide emission quantity by multiplying the quantity of use of the direct calculation item as input data. An integration processing portion (33) integrates the carbon dioxide emission amount and the cover ratio of the direct calculation item having the input data, and calculates a total discharge amount and a total coverage ratio. A total discharge amount calculation portion (35) calculates a total carbon dioxide emission amount of construction materials used in the building by correcting the total discharge amount based on a ratio of construction materials used in the building not included in the total cover ratio.
   USE - Carbon dioxide emission estimation system for construction materials used in buildings.
   ADVANTAGE - The total carbon dioxide emission amount of the building is calculated easily and accurately.

In the method, washing the                  regenerated flue gas with alkali to obtain carbon                  monoxide with purity of 99-100%. In the method,                  the alkaline washing comprises absorbing an                  acidic component in gas by using aqueous solution                  of alkaline substance, preferably, the alkaline                  substance is sodium carbonate, sodium                  bicarbonate, potassium carbonate and/or potassium                  bicarbonate. Preferred Components: In the                  regeneration gas, the oxygen content is 0.8-1.2                  times the required oxygen amount V, the required                  oxygen amount V is calculated by formula is as                  specified. PN CN107811077-A
TI Preparing microencapsulated tea extract involves pre-treating tea, performing water extraction, performing supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction and adding supercritical carbon-dioxide extract with water extract.
AU ZHOU J
AE LANXI CHENMO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LANX-Non-standard)
GA 2018239754
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing microencapsulated tea extract involves pre-treating tea, performing water extraction, performing supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction, adding supercritical carbon-dioxide extract with water extract to obtain mixed solution, adding mixed solution with 0.5-0.8 times medium-chain triglycerides and 7-10 times volume of Cremophor (RTM: polyoxyethylated triglycerides) EL 35, performing ultrasonic mixing and dropping mixed solution in 0.1-0.4 times of ethanol solution under magnetic stirring to obtain microencapsulated tea extract.
   USE - Method for preparing microencapsulated tea extract (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare microencapsulated tea extract by reducing surface tension between two phases of emulsion and with high extraction rate and simple operation. The obtained microencapsulated tea extract has stable property and improved economic value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The tea is green tea, black tea, yellow tea, white tea, oolong tea and jasmine tea, where mixing weight ratio of green tea and black tea leaf is 1:0.1-0.3. The volume ratio of supercritical carbon-dioxide extract and water extract is 4-7:1. The ethanol solution is prepared by adding 0.03-0.05 times volume of kojic acid in absolute ethanol. Preferred Process: The pre-treating is performed by crushing tea after sieving with 100-300-mesh sieve, adding 10-30 times of distilled water and dipping for 2-5 hours. The water extraction is performed by adding pre-processed tea mixture with 0.001-0.002 times ethanol, heating at 40-50 degrees C for 1-4 hours, filtering to obtain filtrate and filter residue, concentrating filtrate to 1/10-1/8 of original volume, obtaining water extract and drying filter residue to obtain tea powder. Preferred Conditions: The carbon-dioxide flow rate during supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction is 30-40 kg/hour, dosage of tea powder is 70-100 g, extracting temperature is 30-40 degrees C, extracting pressure is 30-40 megaPascals, first stage separating temperature is 40-50 degrees C, second stage separation pressure is 8-8.5 megaPascals and temperature is 30-35 degrees C, separation pressure is 6-7 megaPascals, extracting time is 2-3 hours. The ultrasonic frequency is 15-25 kilohertz, power is 150-200 Watts and ultrasonic time is 5-10 minutes. The dropping speed of ethanol solution is 1-2 drops/second.

PN EP4035759-A1; WO2022162059-A1
TI Method for removing carbon dioxide from            methane-containing gas comprises separating carbon            dioxide from methane-containing gas by freezing,            reducing carbon dioxide concentration of            methane-containing gas and recycling treatment            gas.
AU JORDAN U
   VOELLMY L
   HACK G
AE HITACHI ZOSEN INOVA AG (HITF-C)
   HITACHI ZOSEN INOVA AG (HITF-C)
GA 2022964910
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing carbon dioxide from                methane-containing gas comprises: (a) providing a                methane-containing gas (103) containing at least                carbon dioxide as an impurity; (b) separating                carbon dioxide from the methane-containing gas by                freezing; (c) reducing carbon dioxide concentration                of the methane-containing gas using a separation                device (111), where a methane-enriched product gas                (117) is obtained; (d) using at least a part of the                product gas as treatment gas (129) which is passed                through the separation device to treat the                separation device, where carbon dioxide is received                by the treatment gas and removed with the treatment                gas from the separator; and (e) recycling the                treatment gas passed through the separating device                and mixing with the methane-containing gas.
   USE - The method is useful for removing carbon                dioxide from methane-containing gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, energy-efficient and                environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                device for removing carbon dioxide from the                methane-enriched gas, comprising a gas feed line, a                compression unit, a cooling unit for freezing out                carbon dioxide, a separator, a line for removing                methane-enriched product gas, and a make-up gas                line for connecting the separator and the gas feed                line.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the method for removing carbon dioxide from                methane-containing gas with p-toluenesulfonic                acid.
   Methane-containing gas (103)
   Separation device (111)
   Methane-enriched product gas (117)
   Treatment gas (129)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The separation device                  comprises a cryogenic membrane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (d), the                  product gas is liquefied by cooling, and the                  separation device is treated by optionally                  heating the treatment gas and removing carbon                  dioxide from the separation device. The liquefied                  product gas is decompressed, where the flash gas                  is obtained and passed through the separating                  device as treatment gas. In the step (b), the                  carbon dioxide concentration of the gas is                  reduced to less than 6000 ppm. In the step (c),                  the carbon dioxide concentration of the product                  gas is lowered to less than 5200 ppm, preferably                  less than 50 ppm. The recirculated processing gas                  is compressed to the pressure of the                  methane-containing gas before it is mixed with                  the methane-containing gas. At least part of the                  treatment gas or part of the product gas is used                  to cool the methane-containing gas, preferably by                  the treatment gas or the product gas, and then                  the methane-containing gas is passed through a                  heat exchanger via counter current principle. The                  methane-containing gas fed to the separating                  device at -130 to -80 degrees C, preferably -116                  to -110 degrees C with a pressure of 5-25 bar,                  preferably 14-17 bar, then cooled to -140 to -100                  degrees C, preferably -125 to -115 degrees C for                  liquefaction and is completely or partially                  liquefied. The product gas or the liquefied                  product gas is decompressed to 1 bar before the                  processing gas is branched off. Preferred                  Components: The separator includes                  p-toluenesulfonic acid for regenerating the                  separator, and the heated product gas from the                  heat exchanger is passed through the separator as                  make-up gas. In the step (a), the concentration                  of carbon dioxide in the methane-containing gas                  is up to 60%, preferably up to 2.5%.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control.    SCHMITT W
   MACELROY D
AE QUEEN ELIZABETH COLLEGE DUBLIN (QUEE-C)
GA 2021A37048

A direct air carbon dioxide is captured the process apparatus that performs a direct air carbon dioxide capture process coupled to an output of the heat pump by a second thermal transfer circuit.    ADVANTAGE - System reduces the carbon dioxide quantity in the atmosphere. Its viability relies on the development of cost-effective materials and low-cost processes.

PN EP3839015-A1; WO2021124231-A1; EP3839015-B1; CN114787326-A; ES2918173-T3
TI Processing exhaust gas having carbon dioxide and            dioxygen, having exhaust gas from cement production            plant, comprises introducing exhaust gas into            combustion reactor, burning fuel, controlling            combustion and feeding exhaust gas from reactor.
AU BOES K
   WEBER M
   STOFFEL B
AE HOLCIM TECHNOLOGY LTD (HOLF-C)
   HOLCIM TECHNOLOGY LTD (HOLF-C)
   HOLCIM TECHNOLOGY LTD (HOLF-C)
GA 2021697250
AB    NOVELTY - Processing exhaust gas containing carbon                dioxide and dioxygen, having exhaust gas from a                cement production plant, comprises: introducing the                exhaust gas into a combustion reactor; burning fuel                in the combustion reactor with the dioxygen content                of the exhaust gas which is used as an oxidizing                agent; controlling the combustion in the combustion                reactor so that the exhaust gas from the combustion                reactor contains less than 10 vol.% of oxygen and                at least 80 vol.% of a mixture of carbon monoxide                and carbon dioxide; and feeding the exhaust gas                from the combustion reactor into a carbon                dioxide/carbon monoxide conversion reactor, where                the carbon dioxide and optionally the carbon                monoxide contained in the exhaust gas is converted                into a hydrocarbon fuel.
   USE - The method is useful for processing exhaust                gas containing carbon dioxide and dioxygen, having                exhaust gas from a cement production plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: extracts and capture carbon                dioxide from flue gases for further processing the                carbon dioxide in concentrated form that are                cumbersome and costly; processes exhaust gas that                avoids any carbon dioxide emission into the                atmosphere and that allows for a carbon dioxide                concentration from the exhaust gas at a low price;                extracts the carbon dioxide fraction from a                post-combustion flue gas by flushing the gases with                an amine solvent, regenerating the solvent by steam                stripping, thus releasing nearly pure carbon                dioxide, and recycling the stripped solvent to the                absorber; and is very efficient.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates a cement production                process.
   Raw meal mill (1)
   Raw meal silo (2)
   Pre-heater (3)
   Rotary clinker kiln (4)
   Clinker cooler (6)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: At least a part of the                  exhaust gas, before introduced into the                  combustion reactor, is used to dry a solid fuel,                  preferably a solid renewable fuel, in a first                  drying unit, thus obtaining a dried fuel,                  whereupon the dried fuel is optionally ground to                  obtain a ground fuel. At least part of the                  exhaust gas is used to dry cement raw meal in a                  second drying unit, thus obtaining a dried raw                  meal, where the dried raw meal is ground to                  obtain a ground raw meal, which is fed into a                  preheater of a cement production plant. The                  by-products produced in the carbon dioxide and                  carbon monoxide conversion reactor, having                  proteins, are fed to a combustion process, having                  to the burner of a cement kiln, the pre-calciner                  burner and/or the combustion reactor burner of                  the cement production plant as a fuel. The                  hydrocarbon fuel, preferably the combustible                  fuel, obtained from the carbon dioxide and carbon                  monoxide conversion reactor is fed to a                  combustion process, having the burner of a cement                  kiln, the pre-calciner burner and/or the                  combustion reactor burner of the cement                  production plant as a fuel. The exhaust gas from                  the combustion reactor, before fed into the                  carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide conversion                  reactor, is conducted through a heat exchanger to                  cool the exhaust gas to a temperature of                  preferably 20-300 degrees C. The exhaust gas is                  taken from a cement raw meal preheater of the                  cement production plant. Preferred Components:                  The dried and optionally ground fuel is used as                  the fuel in the combustion reactor. The carbon                  dioxide and carbon monoxide conversion reactor is                  a bioreactor, a catalytic reactor or a                  power-to-fuel reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

A sensor group measures temperature and pressure values sent by the pre-heating section. An inlet of the test section is fixed with a top part of the carbon dioxide gas-liquid separator. A temperature sensor (20) is fixed between two ends of the test section. A differential pressure sensor (19) is connected in parallel with two ends of the test section. A data collecting recording instrument (23) sends data obtained by the sensor group to the temperature sensor, the differential pressure sensor and a computer (24).
   USE - High parameter supercritical carbon dioxide flow and heat exchange characteristic testing system.

PN CN110423023-A; CN212403960-U
TI Non-carbonized lime kiln gas system comprises flame burners distributed in a lime kiln calcining zone, circulating carbon dioxide introduced into flame burner for heat exchange, and vaporizer has built-in combustion chamber oxygen line.
AU CAO H
   ZHANG H
   FENG Y
   ZHANG W
AE SHIJIAZHUANG SHENGHONGDA THERMAL ENERGY (SHIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201995553L
AB    NOVELTY - Non-carbonized lime kiln gas system comprises 12 sets of flame burners distributed in a lime kiln calcining zone and divided into two layers, each layer of 6 distributed along the circumference of the lime kiln in the adjacent angle is 60 degrees, fuel is derived from carbon monoxide in carbon monoxide storage tank and is delivered via an oxygen line for a flame barrier burner. The upper layer and lower layer are staggered, circulating carbon dioxide is introduced into the flame burner for heat exchange, and heated to more than 1200 degrees C which passed directly into the kiln. The main component of the combustion of carbon monoxide in the burner is high temperature carbon dioxide, and flame burners are arranged uniformly which heats the carbon dioxide to more than 1200 degrees C and passes to a lime kiln to decompose into lime and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Used as the non-carbonized lime kiln gas system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: can adjust the temperature of the heating gas; can produce lime without causing pollution; has low calcination rate and high activity; and uses medium-low temperature carbon dioxide gas as the lime cooling medium to make gas composition of the lime production system pure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Non-carbonized lime kiln gas system comprises 12 sets of flame burners distributed in the lime kiln calcining zone and divided into two layers, each layer of 6 distributed along the circumference of the lime kiln in the adjacent angle is 60 degrees, fuel is derived from carbon monoxide in carbon monoxide storage tank and is delivered via an oxygen line for a flame barrier burner. The upper layer and lower layer are staggered, circulating carbon dioxide is introduced into the flame burner for heat exchange, and heated to more than 1200 degrees C which passed directly into the kiln. The main component of the combustion of carbon monoxide in the burner is high temperature carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide enriched lime kiln is a vertical cylindrical structure which is divided into a preheating section, combustion section, and cooling discharge section from top to bottom, flame burners are arranged uniformly which heats the carbon dioxide to more than 1200 degrees C and passes to a lime kiln to decompose into lime and carbon dioxide, a preheating section is arranged in the upper part of the lime kiln to receive the heat of the high temperature carbon dioxide from the calcining section so that the limestone which passes to the kiln is heated to 500 degrees C to reach a high temperature, and carbon dioxide temperature of the kiln is lowered to more than 200 degrees C, a cooling zone is arranged in a lower portion of the lime kiln, carbon dioxide discharged from the lime kiln is cooled to less than 100 degrees C and then the lime kiln is discharged, a gate is set between the discharge and cooled zone. The pressure environment in the lime kiln is under negative pressure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the non-carbonized lime kiln gas system (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Flame burner (2)
   Combustion chamber oxygen line (15.2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The system comprises oxygen or oxygen enrichment in the oxygen line, flame burner (2) and combustion chamber of the gasifier, the oxygen line is divided into two ways a muffle burner oxygen line leading to the combustion chamber of the gasifier, and a vaporizer that leads to the flame burner has a built-in combustion chamber oxygen line (15.2), where the oxygen used is pure oxygen, and general air or oxygen enrichment, and the purity of carbon monoxide is 80-90%.

PT P
PN TW201909731-A
TI Carbon dioxide supply system.
AU WU M
   ZHENG Z
AE UNIV KAOHSIUNG NAT SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYKA-Non-standard)
GA 201949409K
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide supply system is presented to solve the problem of inconvenient carriage of conventional carbon dioxide gas filling devices. The carbon dioxide supply system includes an electrolytic tank, an electrolytic module, a detection element and a processing module. The electrolytic tank stores an electrolyte and is connected to a cultivation pool by an exhaust pipe. The electrolytic module includes two electrodes electrically connected to a power source to form an electronic circuit. The electrolytic module is mounted in the electrolytic tank for electrolyzing the electrolyte to produce carbon dioxide. The detection unit detects the electrolyte to produce a detected value. The processing module is coupled to the electrolytic module and the detection element. The processing module receives the detected value and determines whether the detected value is not smaller than a threshold value. If yes, the electronic circuit becomes open-circuited.

TI Preparation of high performance carbon dioxide            separation composite membrane by surface cross-linking            involves coating separating layer surface with            trimesoyl chloride solution, drying and forming            cross-linking layer.
AU WANG Z
   DONG S
   SHENG M
   WANG J
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202093739K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high performance carbon dioxide                separation composite membrane by surface                cross-linking comprises coating separating layer                surface with trimesoyl chloride solution, drying                and forming cross-linking layer into separating                layer surface.
   USE - Method for preparing high performance carbon                dioxide separation composite membrane by surface                cross-linking.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy to be                amplified.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises using                  wet coating method by completely dissolving                  silicone rubber into n-heptane to form uniform                  silicone rubber solution, adding tetraethyl                  orthosilicate cross-linking agent and dibutyl tin                  dilaurate catalyst into silicone rubber solution,                  forming uniform mixed solution, reacting mixed                  solution at 20-35 degrees C for 30-50 minutes to                  form intermediate layer coating liquid, placing                  intermediate layer coating liquid into ice water                  bath to store and avoid gel, coating middle layer                  coating liquid into polysulfone ultra-filtration                  membrane surface and drying to obtain middle                  layer; scraper coating separation layer by                  preparing separating layer material, adding                  polyvinylamine as main body into 0.05-2 wt.%                  uniform solution, settling and defoaming to                  obtain separating layer coating liquid, coating                  obtained separating layer coating liquid into                  middle layer surface and drying to obtain the                  separating layer; and crosslinking surface by                  coating 0.05-0.2 wt.% trimesoyl chloride solution                  into separating layer surface and drying to                  obtain surface cross-linked composite film.                  Preferred Components: The mass ratio of silicone                  rubber, tetraethylorthosilicate and dibutyltin                  dilaurate mixed solution is 5:4:4.

PT P
PN IN201911012061-A
TI Purifying syngas and producing high purity carbon dioxide, by introducing raw syngas to raw syngas cleaning section, passing purified raw syngas through cleaned syngas conditioning section, passing purified raw syngas through acid gas removal section and passing acid gases rich in carbon dioxide.
AU DWIVEDI P
   LAKAVATH B
   MANDOWARA A
   CHAUTRE N
   JAYANTI V K
   MAITI R N
   SINGH S R
   SHUKLA V
AE ENGINEERS INDIA LTD (ENGI-Non-standard)
GA 2021A73717
AB    NOVELTY - Process for purifying syngas and producing high purity carbon dioxide, involves (a) introducing a raw syngas (stream A) to raw syngas cleaning section to attain a purified raw syngas, (b) passing the purified raw syngas through a cleaned syngas conditioning section to achieve the purified raw syngas, (c) passing the purified raw syngas through an acid gas removal section, where the syngas is treated with circulating amine solution to provide the impurity free syngas and to provide acid gases rich in carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulphide, (d) passing the acid gases rich in carbon dioxide and hydrogen sulphide through sulfur recovery unit for hydrogen sulphide removal and to provide carbon dioxide rich gas, (e) passing the carbon dioxide rich gas through carbon dioxide recovery section for further removal of hydrogen sulphide impurity by routing the carbon dioxide rich gases (Stream J) to pressure swing adsorption section in order to achieve 99.9V% or more purified carbon dioxide.
   USE - The process is useful for purifying syngas and producing high purity carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The process meets specification of syngas required for methanol production, meets specification required for methanol production, recovers high purity carbon dioxide from syngas, reduces carbon emissions to atmosphere and economical, and is carried on integrated manner in low-pressure system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system comprises raw syngas cleaning section, cleaned syngas conditioning section, acid gas removal (AGR) section, sulfur recovery unit and carbon dioxide recovery section, where the sections are linked with each other in the order specified and operate in an integrated manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process is an integrated process and carried in a low-pressure system. The treatment of acid gas stream in sulfur recovery unit is carried on a liquid redox process where the catalyst is a circulating liquid of iron chelate. The carbon dioxide rich gases are compressed in carbon dioxide recovery section up to a pressure of 6 kg/cm2g and passed through an adsorbent bed having as adsorbent an oxide of trivalent metal from elements of group VIII in periodic table to trap hydrogen sulphide. Preferred Components: The raw syngas is obtained from high ash Indian coal. The impurity free syngas meets the requirement of methanol production. The circulating amine solution is a mixture of 35-40 wt.% methyl diethanolamine, 3-8 wt.% piperazine and water. Preferred Device: The AGR comprises an absorber column having structure packing with a surface area of 125-350 m2 per m3 of packing. Preferred Composition: The impurity free syngas has ratio of hydrogen/carbon monoxide of 2.
   EXAMPLE - Example described about stream composition, so does not support the claim.

PN CN112870090-A
TI Carbon dioxide facial mask combination is a combination of cream type facial mask and dry powder type facial mask, and main component of the cream type mask is rich in vitamin C.
AU JIANG J
   GAO M
AE JIANG J (JIAN-Individual)
   GAO M (GAOM-Individual)
GA 2021640823
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide facial mask combination is a combination of cream type facial mask and dry powder type facial mask. The main component of the cream type mask is rich in vitamin C, and the main component of the dry powder type mask is sodium bicarbonate. The two types of masks are used at the same time to generate carbon dioxide.
   USE - Carbon dioxide facial mask combination.
   ADVANTAGE - The mask is applied to the face to allow the facial skin cells to absorb the active ingredients, and then cover the dry powder type mask on it, and the vitamin C reacts with sodium bicarbonate to produce carbon dioxide, and when carbon dioxide penetrates into the capillaries through the facial skin, the Bohr effect is generated. The human brain will sense the hypoxia signal of excessive carbon dioxide concentration in the facial skin, and the brain will issue instructions to increase the oxygen supply to the facial skin and promote the metabolism of skin tissue cells, and can increase collagen synthesis. The essence also has the effects of nourishment and hydration, and regular use can slow down aging and improve skin texture, and improves the appearance of the skin and achieving the effect of beauty and beauty.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Conditions: The cream type mask contains at least 15 wt.% or more of vitamin C and more than 50 wt.% of water, and the other ingredients including 56.5 wt.% deionized water, 15 wt.% vitamin C, 4 wt.% protocatechuic acid-sodium (PCA-Na)-50, 3 wt.% aloe extract, 3 wt.% jojoba oil, 2 wt.% Xanthan gum, 2 wt.% ceramide, 2 wt.% squalane, 1.5 wt.% cetyl alcohol, 1.5 wt.% stearic acid, 1.5 wt.% emulsifier (721), 1 wt.% hydroxyethyl cellulose, 1 wt.% arbutin, 1 wt.% phenoxyethanol, and 0.5 wt.% emulsifier (72). The dry powder type mask is made by using disposable sterile medical gauze with a specification of 26c yarn count as the carrier, and spraying raw materials on both sides of the gauze carrier, and then sandwiched between two layers of breathable pure cotton mask paper, and placing between the double-layer metal heating plates. The rosin glyceride is hot-melted, the other 3 ingredients, gauze carrier, and pure cotton mask paper are glued together to form the finished blank of the dry powder mask. The finished blank is cut into the shape of the face, exposing the openings of the eyes, mouth, nose, etc., and the preparation process of the dry powder mask is completed.

PT P
PN CN111830118-A; CN111830118-B
TI Method for measuring protons released by legume roots using stable carbon isotopes, comprises e.g. culturing the legumes in quartz sand, collecting first carbon dioxide gas produced by rhizosphere respiration through first lye and detecting.
AU CAO J
   YU N
   HAO J
   YUE X
AE UNIV LANZHOU (ULAN-C)
GA 2020A74442
AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises culturing the legumes in quartz sand, collecting the first carbon dioxide gas produced by rhizosphere respiration through first lye to obtain carbon dioxide released by root system, recording as carbon root system, detecting delta 13C of product of first carbon dioxide gas collected by first lye to obtain delta 13C root system, culturing the legumes in the quartz sand-carbonate mixture for second time, carrying out first culture and second culture under same environmental conditions, and second carbon dioxide gas produced in the rhizosphere through second lye to obtain total carbon dioxide release amount, recording it as C total, detecting delta 13C of product of second carbon dioxide gas collected by second lye to obtain delta 13C total, calculating protons released by roots of legumes by H+=(C totalx delta 13C total-C rootx delta 13C root)/2, where H+ is amount of protons released by roots of legumes with unit of ~ mg/h/strain, and unit of carbon root and carbon is mu g/h/strain.
   USE - Method for measuring protons released by legume roots using stable carbon isotopes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbonate in the quartz sand-carbonate mixture is calcium carbonate.

PN CN111378983-A; CN111378983-B
TI Controlling carbon dioxide electric reduction            reaction based on electrode solution system comprises            adding interface system, realizing selective modulation            of gaseous hydrocarbon product methane, applying            periodic potential.
AU MAO Q
   ZHAO J
   LI B
   LIU S
   XU K
   CAO Z
   GUO C
   HUANG Y
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 202065013Q
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling carbon dioxide electric reduction                reaction based on electrode solution system                comprises adding the interface system at 0-60                degrees C, where the control potential achieves                periodic step changes to achieve selective                preparation of catalyst crystal plane and                controllable preparation of grain size, realizing                the selective modulation of gaseous hydrocarbon                product methane, where the modulation range of                methane Faraday efficiency is 5.5-79 wt.%, applying                periodic potential steps to the carbon dioxide RR                reaction system, where an electrode solution                system, which is an interface system, where the                copper-based catalytic material, preferably at                least one of copper foil and copper mesh is in                contact with the electrolyte solution and the                electrolyte solution is at least one of 0.1-0.5                mol/l aqueous potassium bicarbonate solution,                0.1-0.5 mol/l carbon dioxide saturated potassium                bicarbonate aqueous solution and phosphate buffer                solution.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling carbon                dioxide electric reduction reaction based on                electrode solution system.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adjusts the selectivity of the                product methane; improves the stability of the                carbon dioxide electric reduction reaction; and is                suitable for the large-scale application of the                carbon dioxide electric reduction technology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process of pretreating                  the copper foil and copper mesh electrode                  comprises applying an anode current of 50-250                  mA.cm-2 in a phosphoric acid aqueous solution                  with a volume concentration of 85% for                  electropolishing and rinsing with deionized                  water, sonicating in absolute ethanol after                  taking out and rinsing with deionized water,                  realizing in-situ controllable preparation of the                  nano-copper particles on the surface of the                  copper-based catalytic material, where the                  particle size can be controlled and prepared                  between 20-700 nm, realizes the in-situ                  preparation of the tetragonal crystal plane and                  cubic crystal plane of the surface of the                  copper-based catalytic material to obtain the                  copper oxide in situ, where surface copper/copper                  oxide atomic ratio is 0.098-2.703, 5 degrees C in                  the interface system formed by copper foil and                  0.1 mol/l potassium bicarbonate solution                  saturated with carbon dioxide and periodic                  potential step operating environment having Ta of                  5 seconds, Ea at 1 V, Ec of -2.2V and Tc of 70                  seconds, where the Faraday efficiency of methane                  reaches 79% at 120 minutes.

PN US2018125100-A1; WO2018086866-A1
TI Operating drink producing apparatus, by providing e.g. drink producing apparatus, filling dispensing container with liquid, leading liquid from container, supplying carbon dioxide and decarbonizing liquid remaining in container.
AU BAYSAL K
   HAHN D
   KIRSCHEN M
   KLEINER A
   MAYERSHOFER C
   METTEL B
   WEISS C
AE BSH HAUSGERAETE GMBH (BSHB-C)
   BSH HAUSGERAETE GMBH (BSHB-C)
GA 201836239A
AB    NOVELTY - Operating a drink producing apparatus, by (a) providing the drink producing apparatus with a dispensing unit, (b) providing the drink producing apparatus with dispensing container, (c) providing the drink producing apparatus with carbon dioxide addition unit, (d) providing drink producing apparatus with a carbon dioxide line device, (e) filling the dispensing container with liquid, (f) leading liquid from container, (g) supplying carbon dioxide, and (h) decarbonizing at least liquid remaining in the dispensing container after supplying carbon dioxide into the container.
   USE - The method is useful for operating a drink producing apparatus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for operating a drink producing apparatus, involves (a) providing the drink producing apparatus with a dispensing unit for dispensing liquid from the drink producing apparatus, (b) providing the drink producing apparatus with a dispensing container, (c) providing the drink producing apparatus with a carbon dioxide addition unit, (d) providing the drink producing apparatus with a carbon dioxide line device, which is connected to the carbon dioxide addition unit for leading carbon dioxide from the carbon dioxide addition unit, (e) filling the dispensing container with the liquid before dispensing a drink from the drink producing apparatus and closing the dispensing container after filling, (f) leading the liquid from the dispensing container, thus reduction of the filling level of the liquid in the dispensing container, (g) supplying carbon dioxide through the carbon dioxide line device into the dispensing container upon leading the liquid out and decrease of the filling level in the dispensing container, and (h) decarbonizing at least the liquid remaining in the dispensing container after supplying the carbon dioxide into the dispensing container. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) drink producing apparatus; and
   (2) household refrigeration apparatus.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Method: The method involves determining degree of carbonization of the liquid and performing decarbonization depending on the degree of carbonization, after supplying the carbon dioxide into the dispensing container. The degree of carbonization of the liquid is reduced to a value less than or equal to 3.0 g/l upon decarbonization. The carbonized liquid in the dispensing container is cooled to a temperature threshold value before further dispensing from the dispensing container as the decarbonization such that a saturation pressure of the carbon dioxide in the liquid is reduced. The temperature threshold value is determined equal to the temperature, at which the liquid is dispensed from the dispensing unit in a subsequent dispensing process. The carbonized liquid is led from the dispensing container into an intermediate container and a decarbonization of the liquid is performed on the way between the dispensing container and the intermediate container. The dispensing container is arranged in a higher height position than the intermediate container and the liquid is decarbonized by fall down of the liquid on the way between the dispensing container and the intermediate container over a fall distance. The method involves charging a line connection between the dispensing container and the dispensing unit with ambient air or an inert gas before introducing the liquid present in carbonized manner in the dispensing container into the line connection and introducing the liquid into this line connection for decarbonization. The carbonized liquid is led from the dispensing container into an intermediate container and a decarbonization of the liquid is performed in the intermediate container by swirling this liquid in the intermediate container. The decarbonization is performed by swirling by a nozzle or an interface increase by a nozzle. The carbonized liquid is led from the dispensing container to the dispensing unit and a height of fall of the liquid from a dispensing opening of the dispensing unit and a vessel for receiving the liquid is set greater than or equal to to a height threshold value such that upon fall of the liquid over the height of fall, a gas exchange is effected such that the liquid is decarbonized before impingement in the vessel.

TI Method for generating synthetic gas from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, involves providing temperature of synthetic gas mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen with pre-specified minimum temperature to maximum.
AU DIPL L
AE GS GRUBER-SCHMIDT GMBH (GSGR-Non-standard)
GA 2020B60445
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves filling a carbon dioxide tank with liquid carbon dioxide, where the carbon dioxide tank has a minimum pressure of 50 bar to a maximum of 70 bar. An electrically driven piston pump (3) is used to convey the liquid carbon dioxide to an evaporator. The evaporator converts carbon dioxide into a gaseous state with a minimum temperature of 85 degrees C to a maximum of 125 degrees C. The volume flow of carbon dioxide through a control valve is minimal to 1L/h and maximum to 1000 L/h. The carbon dioxide and the hydrogen gas (25) in a mixing chamber are provided to a synthetic gas with a minimum molar ratio of carbon dioxide to hydrogen of 1:1 up to a maximum ratio of 1:4. The pressure of the synthetic gas mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen has a minimum pressure of 50 bar to a maximum of 70 bar. The temperature of the synthetic gas mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen has a minimum temperature of 25 degrees C to a maximum of 85 degrees C.
   USE - Method for generating synthetic gas from carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is scalable and has a simple structure. The expensive catalysts are not used, and expensive and complex constructions are not used. The electrical hardware in the form of the resonant circuit is simple and easy to implement. The electrical excitation by electrons has the advantage that the highly excited water has a higher conductivity and thus the plasma channel between anode and cathode is reached with significantly lower energy. The hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the liquid or gaseous phase, such as methanol or methane are stored, which stores stochastic electrical energy from wind energy or solar photovoltaics in a very simple way.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view illustrating the water tank filled with water.
   Piston pump (3)
   Control valve (5)
   Plasma chamber (8)

PN CN114247291-A; CN216755997-U
TI Working method for removing thermally stable salts in industrial carbon dioxide capture systems, involves absorbing carbon dioxide contained in carbon dioxide gas by amine liquid that is flown out from flow passage, where rich liquid is heated.
AU CHEN X
   WANG D
   SHI X
AE ZHEJIANG ZHICHENG ENVIRONMENTAL RESOURCES (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   ZHEJIANG ZHICHENG ENVIRONMENTAL RESOURCES (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202256429D
AB    NOVELTY - The working method involves absorbing the carbon dioxide contained in the carbon dioxide gas by the amine liquid that is flown out from a flow passage. The carbon dioxide is absorbed when the carbon dioxide gas is entered in a lower portion of an absorption tower (1) from the flow passage. The purified gas is discharged from another flow passage on a top of the absorbent tower, where the rich liquid is reached to a pump (61) through a fourth flow passage. The rich liquid is heated by a heat exchanger (71), where the rich liquid is reached to a regeneration tower (2). The purified amine solution reaches the heat-stable salt in the exchanger through another pump (62). The rich solution of a flowing channel is preheated, where the heat of the amine solution in the flow channel is recycled.
   USE - Working method for removing the thermally stable salts in the industrial carbon dioxide capture systems in a fossil fuel combustion process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing electrodialysis principle to remove the heat stable salt accumulated in the carbon dioxide capture system, so that the subsequent secondary processing the original concentrated solution to reach the target of minimum amine liquid loss. The method allows an ultra-filtration unit to effectively remove insoluble substance of particles and floating grease, thus greatly reducing the service life of the ion exchange membrane, and hence effectively removing the most troublesome heat-stabilizing salt of carbon dioxide recovery system, and reaching the best effect of the effect of not compromised loss of amine-liquid, reaching extremely recycling, recycling can be recycled, and is environment-friendly, saves money, and does not need more alkali storage tank and concentrated solution storage tank.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the principle of using electroosmosis method remove system of carbon dioxide. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Absorption tower (1)
   Regeneration tower (2)
   Amine liquid storage tank (3)
   Ultrafiltration unit (5)
   Electrodialysis device (6)
   Concentrated solution storage tank (7)
   Alkali storage tank (8)
   Pumps (61,62,63,64,65,66)
   Heat exchangers (71,72,73,74)
   Flow passages (81,82,83,84,85,86,87,88,89,810 to 829)
   Valves (91,92)
   Recovery system (99)
   Heat stabilizing salt removal system (100)

Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PT P
PN WO2021223609-A1; CN113621357-A; EP3995557-A1; CN113621357-B; US2022259485-A1
TI Carbon-dioxide-responsive self-thickening            intelligent fluid based on supramolecular self-assembly            useful in carbon dioxide gas flooding, comprises Gemini            surfactant, single-chain amide molecule with tertiary            amine head group, and water.
AU XIONG C
   WEI F
   YANG H
   XIE Y
   SHAO L
   PENG K
   ZENG Z
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
   PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
   PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2021C57973
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon-dioxide-responsive self-thickening                intelligent fluid based on supramolecular                self-assembly comprises Gemini surfactant                comprising amide-based compound (I), single-chain                amide molecule (II) with tertiary amine head group,                and water. The Gemini surfactant and the                single-chain amide molecule with tertiary amine                head group self-assemble to form vesicle structure                and are dispersed in water. The hydrophilic head                group is outside and the hydrophobic tail is                inside.
   USE - The carbon-dioxide-responsive self-thickening                intelligent fluid is useful in carbon dioxide gas                flooding (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon-dioxide-responsive self-thickening                intelligent fluid has high in oil displacement                efficiency; and can be prepared in economical and                simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon-dioxide-responsive self-thickening                intelligent fluid based on supramolecular                self-assembly comprises Gemini surfactant                comprising amide-based compound of formula (I),                single-chain amide molecule of formula (II) with                tertiary amine head group, and water. The Gemini                surfactant and the single-chain amide molecule with                tertiary amine head group self-assemble to form                vesicle structure and are dispersed in water. The                hydrophilic head group is outside and the                hydrophobic tail is inside.
   R1=11-21C alkyl chain, preferably CH (CH )                CH=CH(CH ) -;
   n=1-3, preferably 2;
   p=1-6, preferably 3; and
   Y1=Cl or Br, preferably Br.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                producing carbon-dioxide-responsive self-thickening                intelligent fluid, comprising (a) dissolving the                Gemini surfactant and the single-chain amide                molecule with tertiary amine head group in water in                proportion to obtain the carbon dioxide responsive                self-thickening intelligent fluid, and (b) reacting                the single-chain amide molecule with tertiary amine                head group with the methylene linking molecule of                formula (Y1-(CH2)p-Y1) (IIa) to obtain mixture of                Gemini surfactant and an unreacted single-chain                amide molecule with tertiary amine head group, and                during the reaction, control the molar ratio of the                single-chain amide molecule with tertiary amine                head group to the methylene linking molecule (IIa),                so that the ratio of the Gemini surfactant obtained                after the reaction and the unreacted single-chain                amide molecule with tertiary amine head group meets                the ratio requirement of the two in the carbon                dioxide response self-thickening smart fluid, where                the mixture obtained after the reaction is                dissolved in water to obtain the carbon dioxide                responsive self-thickening intelligent fluid.
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The                  carbon-dioxide-responsive self-thickening                  intelligent fluid comprises 1-4 wt.% Gemini                  surfactant, 0.5 wt.% single-chain amide molecule                  with tertiary amine head group, and reaming                  amount of water. Preferred Method: In the step                  (b), the molar ratio of the single-chain amide                  molecule with tertiary amine head group to the                  methylene linking molecule (IIa) is 2:(0.1-1).                  The Gemini surfactant is prepared by reacting                  single-chain amide molecule with tertiary amine                  head group with the methylene linking molecule                  (IIa) to obtain the Gemini surfactant. The                  single-chain amide molecule with tertiary amine                  head group is prepared by performing amidation                  reaction of carboxylic acid (R1-COOH)                  corresponding to R1 and the diamine of formula                  (NH2CH2(CH2)nN(CH3)2) (IIb) as raw materials to                  obtain the single-chain amide molecule with                  tertiary amine head group. The amidation reaction                  comprises dissolving carboxylic acid (R1-COOH) in                  an organic solvent at 160-180&#176; C, adding                  diamine (IIb) dropwise, and the reaction is                  continued for 6-8 hours after the addition is                  completed, after the temperature is lowered, the                  solvent is removed by vacuum rotary evaporation                  to obtain the single-chain amide molecule with                  tertiary amine head group. The process of                  reacting the single-chain amide molecule with                  tertiary amine head group and the methylene                  linking molecule (IIa) comprises dissolving the                  single-chain amide molecule with tertiary amine                  head group in organic solvent, adding methylene                  linking molecule dropwise, and after the dropwise                  addition is completed, the temperature is raised                  to react, and the Gemini surfactant is obtained                  after the reaction is completed. where the                  temperature is raised to the reflux temperature                  of ethanol, and the reaction is carried out for                  6-16 hours. Preferred Components: The molar ratio                  of the carboxylic acid (R1-COOH) and the diamine                  (IIb) is 1:(1.2-1.8). The organic solvent is                  ethanol.
   EXAMPLE - The example provides details of preparation of                carbon-dioxide-responsive self-thickening                intelligent fluid, that do not support                claims.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); H01 (Obtaining crude oil and natural gas - including exploration, drilling, well completion, production and treatment. General off-shore platform and drilling technology is included together with the treatment of tar sands and oil shales (C10G, E21B).)

PT P
PN CN110028483-A
TI Preparing cyclic carbonate by an external circulation spray type gas-liquid contact process comprises e.g. introducing alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide into reaction system and carrying out coupling reaction, discharging and recycling.
AU REN W
   LI M
   LV X
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2019651325
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate by external circulation spray type gas-liquid contact process comprises e.g. taking external circulation spray reactor, taking bifunctional catalyst as catalyst, carrying out coupling reaction of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide as raw materials to obtain cyclic carbonate, adding cyclic carbonate containing catalyst into external circulation spray reactor, heating the starting material to the reaction temperature by heat exchanger, introducing carbon dioxide into the reaction system, then introducing alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide into external circulation spray reactor, maintaining the reaction pressure, continue reacting until the total consumption of alkylene oxide is consumed, transferring the reaction material into flash tank, discharging the carbon dioxide, distilling under reduced pressure to obtain cyclic carbonate, and recycling the catalyst-containing residual liquid as starting material of the next batch reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate by an external circulation spray type gas-liquid contact process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: solves the problem that the conversion rate of the alkylene oxide in the existing method reaches certain level; improves the efficiency of coupling carbon dioxide with alkylene oxide; and is suitable for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cyclic carbonate by external circulation spray type gas-liquid contact process comprises taking external circulation spray reactor, taking bifunctional catalyst as catalyst, carrying out coupling reaction of carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide as raw materials to obtain cyclic carbonate, adding cyclic carbonate containing catalyst into external circulation spray reactor, heating the starting material to the reaction temperature by heat exchanger, introducing carbon dioxide into the reaction system, then introducing alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide into external circulation spray reactor, maintaining the reaction pressure, continue reacting until the total consumption of alkylene oxide is consumed, transferring the reaction material in the external circulation spray reactor into a flash tank, discharging the carbon dioxide, distilling under reduced pressure to obtain cyclic carbonate, and recycling the catalyst-containing residual liquid as starting material of the next batch reaction, where the mass ratio between alkylene oxide and catalyst is 50:1-50000:1, the molar ratio between alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide is 1:1, the reaction pressure is 0.3-3.0 MPa and the reaction temperature is 60-150 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is tetradentate Schiff base aluminum complex of formula (I) containing quaternary ammonium salts and/or quaternary phosphonium salt groups. The alkylene oxide is propylene oxide or ethylene oxide.R1 = benzene-1,2-diyl, cyclohexane-1,2-diyl, butane-2,3-diyl, propane-1,2-diyl, butane-1,2-diyl, 1,2-diphenylethane-1,2-diyl or ethane-1,2-diyl;R2 = H, CH3, CH2CH3 or Ph;R3 = 1-6C alkyl or phenyl;X = F-, Cl-, Br-, I-, NO3-, CH3COO-, CCl3COO-, CF3COO-, ClO4-, BF4-, BPh4-, N3-, p-methylbenzoate, p-toluenesulfonate, o-nitrophenoloxy, p-nitrophenoloxy, m-nitrophenoloxy, 2,4-dinitrophenoloxy, 3,5-dinitrophenoloxy, 2,4,6-trinitro phenoloxy, 3,5-dichlorophenoloxy, 3,5-difluorophenoloxy, 3,5-di-trifluoromethylphenoloxy or pentafluorophenol oxygen anion; andn = 1-5.
