PT P
PN CN108912493-A
TI Producing graphene hub by using fly ash carbon dioxide comprises e.g. extracting alumina, titania and magnesia from fly ash, capturing the carbon dioxide from metallurgical, coal chemical or power plant, calcining and hot-melting graphene.
AU CHENG L
   LIU X
   XIANG Y
   LIU H
AE ZHONGXI NINGXIA NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 201898402V
AB    NOVELTY - Producing graphene hub by using fly ash carbon dioxide comprises e.g. (i) extracting alumina, titania and magnesia from fly ash to obtain aluminum alloy, titanium alloy, magnesium alloy or metal aluminum, metal titanium and metal magnesium, (ii) capturing the carbon dioxide from metallurgical, coal chemical or power plant, or calcining magnesium metal with carbon dioxide dry ice to prepare graphene, and performing plasma method to obtain carbon black, (iii) hot-melting graphene and aluminum alloy, titanium alloy and magnesium alloy into graphene aluminum alloy hub, graphene titanium alloy hub and graphene magnesium alloy hub, (iv) extracting the calcium oxide from fly ash into calcium carbide island to obtain calcium carbide, then adding water, then adding a part of acetylene to prepare acetylene black to obtain graphene, and (v) adding graphene, acetylene black and polyolefin into graphene-modified olefin island.
   USE - The method is useful for producing graphene hub by using fly ash carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The product: has low cost and high strength antistatic property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing graphene hub by using fly ash carbon dioxide comprises (i) extracting alumina, titania and magnesia from fly ash to obtain aluminum alloy, titanium alloy, magnesium alloy or metal aluminum, metal titanium and metal magnesium, (ii) capturing the carbon dioxide from metallurgical, coal chemical or power plant, and sending to the island of carbon dioxide, then placing a part of carbon dioxide and magnesium metal in a burner island to burn graphene at 500-3000 degrees C; or calcining magnesium metal with carbon dioxide dry ice to prepare graphene, and performing plasma method to obtain carbon black, (iii) hot-melting 0.05-2 wt.% graphene and aluminum alloy, titanium alloy and magnesium alloy into graphene aluminum alloy hub, graphene titanium alloy hub and graphene magnesium alloy hub product, (iv) extracting the calcium oxide from fly ash into calcium carbide island to obtain calcium carbide, then adding water to prepare acetylene, then adding a part of acetylene to prepare acetylene black by chemical vapor deposition method to obtain graphene, and (v) adding graphene obtained in step (iv), 0.05-2 wt.% acetylene black and 0.05-2 wt.% polyolefin into graphene-modified olefin island to obtain graphene modified polyolefin anti- electrostatic hub, graphene carbon fiber antistatic wheel, graphene aluminum magnesium alloy antistatic hub.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), desulfurizing and heating the fly ash at 350-400 degrees C, then dehydrating and removing impurity removal. The temperature of the graphene-modified olefin island is 110-350 degrees C. The method comprises whole process opens the remote explosion-proof monitoring center power switch to perform online remote explosion-proof monitoring. In step (ii), calcining carbon dioxide with magnesium metal to obtain graphene including carbon dioxide storage tank, plasma torch, metal magnesium powder intelligent conveyor, catalytic reaction chamber, magnesium oxide recovery device, multi-wave cooling slow extension, barrier gas-solid separation mesh bag, graphene automatic packaging line and surplus carbon dioxide recovery device, (a) opening the carbon dioxide tank valve switch, placing the carbon dioxide in a plasma torch, heating at 500-3000 degrees C, then entering the catalytic reaction chamber, and rapidly reacting with magnesium metal powder fed by the metal magnesium powder intelligent conveyor to obtain magnesium oxide and graphene, (b) placing magnesium oxide in a magnesium oxide recovery device for recycling, and then recycling the metal magnesium powder, and (c) adding graphene into multi-wave cooling slower, recycling the solid graphene from the barrier of gas-solid separation mesh bag to graphene automatic packaging line for graphene packaging, and then placing excess gas carbon dioxide into surplus carbon dioxide recovery unit to recover the surplus carbon dioxide for recycling. In step (v), preparation method of graphene polyolefin antistatic hub comprises (ia) placing graphene, polyolefin and acetylene carbon black in a graphene polyolefin modification device through the graphene intelligent conveyor, polyolefin intelligent conveyor and acetylene black black intelligent conveyor, stirring and mixing modification in an electric mixer, (iia) adding mixed modified graphene polyolefin into graphene polyolefin antistatic hub die-casting production device system for die casting, and polishing to obtain molded product, and (iiia) placing graphene polyolefin antistatic hub molding product in a graphene polyolefin antistatic hub quality detecting device for product quality inspection, then placing finished product in warehouse or directly shipping, where graphene polyolefin antistatic hub includes graphene intelligent conveyor, polyolefin smart conveyor, acetylene carbon black intelligent conveyor, graphene polyolefin modification device, electric mixer, graphene polyolefin antistatic wheel hub die casting production device system and graphene polyolefin antistatic wheel quality detecting device. In step (v), preparation method of graphene aluminum-magnesium alloy antistatic hub comprises (va) placing aluminum crucible, magnesium crucible, aluminum rod, magnesium rod or aluminum and magnesium in a melting furnace, melting, and then placing in a slag furnace to remove impurities, (vb) removing the slag and molten aluminum or magnesium liquid from slag furnace, and placing in nitrogen stirring furnace 21, agitating and removing oxygen, and (vc) opening the nitrogen bottle valve and graphene powder smart metering switch, adding graphene powder into nitrogen stirring furnace with nitrogen, thoroughly stirring the mixture, and then placing in hub die casting machine for die casting to obtain graphene hub, where graphene aluminum-magnesium alloy antistatic hub includes aluminum crucible or magnesium crucible, aluminum or magnesium rod, melting furnace, slag removal furnace, nitrogen agitator, nitrogen bottle, graphene powder and hub die casting machine. Preferred Components: In step (ii), purity of carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide island is 95-9.9%. In step (v), polyolefin is polypropylene, polyethylene or polyvinyl chloride. The graphene modified olefin includes graphene polypropylene, graphene polyethylene or graphene polyvinyl chloride.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).); M22 (Casting; powder metallurgy - including foundry moulding, moulding machines, patterns, moulds, cores and metal casting (B22).); ; A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); P53 (Metal casting, powder metallurgy (B22).)

PN CN110542598-A
TI Method for collecting carbon dioxide, involves contacting aqueous solution of capture agent with carbon dioxide-containing to-be-processed object, where capture agent aqueous solution is water and capture agent.
AU CHEN Z
   WANG Z
   SHEN J
AE CHEN Z (CHEN-Individual)
GA 2019A4335C
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon dioxide collecting method involves contacting an aqueous solution of a capture agent with a carbon dioxide-containing to-be-processed object to obtain an aqueous solution of a capture agent that has captured carbon dioxide, where the capture agent aqueous solution is composed of water and capture agent for capturing carbon dioxide, the capture agent is a potassium salt of a monoprotic carboxylic acid having a total carbon number of 12 or less selected from tetrapotassium EDTA, and the mass molar concentration range of the capture agent aqueous solution is 2-30 mol/kg.
   USE - Method for collecting carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The potassium salt of a single protonic carboxylic acid having a total carbon number of 12 or less is selected from potassium acetate, potassium propionate, potassium butyrate. The capture agent is selected from potassium propionate, potassium butyrate, tetrapotassium EDTA. Preferred Method: The method further involves collecting carbon dioxide desorbed from the aqueous solution of the capture agent that has captured carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN CN109516912-A; CN109516912-B
TI Sequestering carbon dioxide comprises using inorganic alkali for capturing and absorbing carbon dioxide, forming calcium oxalate crystals by using oxalate and calcium ions in water, and realizing mineralization of carbon dioxide.
AU DONG J
   LI C
   XU H
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
   UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2019294827
AB    NOVELTY - Sequestering carbon dioxide comprises using inorganic alkali for capturing and absorbing carbon dioxide which is directly applying to the production of oxalate, where oxalate is used as a detergent builder in laundry detergents, then forming calcium oxalate crystals by using oxalate and calcium ions in the water during washing process, and then realizing the utilization and mineralization of carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for sequestering carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can avoid the large amount of energy loss during the regeneration of the adsorbent; can achieve zero emissions in entire process; and can alleviate greenhouse effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The detergent comprises 0.1-10%, preferably 2-8% nonionic surfactant, 1-30%, preferably 3-20% anionic surfactant, 1-30%, preferably 10-25% oxalate, 1-15%, preferably 3-6% silicate, 0-10%, preferably 3-10% sodium carbonate, 0.5-5%, preferably 1-3% anti-redeposition agent and excess sodium sulfate. Preferred Components: The nonionic surfactant is carbon 12-18 fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether, or carbon 12-18 alcohol and ethylene oxide/propylene oxide block copolymer. The anionic surfactant is carbon 12-18 alkylbenzene sulfonate, sodium alpha -alkenyl sulfonate, or carbon 12-16 secondary alkyl sulfonate. The oxalate sodium oxalate or potassium oxalate. The silicate is layered sodium silicate, sodium silicate, potassium silicate, or sodium metasilicate. The anti-redeposition agent is acrylic acid polymer or its sodium salt, polyvinylpyrrolidone, sodium carboxymethyl cellulose, acrylic acid-maleic anhydride copolymer or its sodium salt, polyvinyl alcohol, or styrene acrylic copolymer. Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises step (a) preparing oxalate from carbon dioxide: (a1) passing the purified power plant exhaust gas containing wet carbonate into a reaction bed, then reacting i.e. potassium or sodium carbonate reacts with carbon dioxide and water to produce two molecules of potassium or sodium bicarbonate, then in the inorganic base: the hydroxide solution absorbs unreacted carbon dioxide, i.e. two molecules of potassium or sodium hydroxide reacts with carbon dioxide to produce potassium or sodium carbonate and water, (a2) adding the bicarbonate produced in the step (a1) to water and hydrogen, then chemically reacting under the noble metal palladium/carbon i.e. potassium or sodium bicarbonate reacts with hydrogen to produce potassium or sodium formate and water, where the mass ratio of bicarbonate to water is 1-1.3, (a3) evaporating formate, centrifuging, drying, then heating the formate at 320-440 degrees C by using common industrial method for dehydrogenation to obtain oxalate, and then removing the collected hydrogen and returning to the step (a2), (b1) adding deionized water to a preparing pot, heating at 50-60 degrees C, and stirring, (b2) sequentially adding anionic surfactant, oxalate, sodium carbonate, sodium silicate, and anhydrous sodium sulfate to the preparing pot, (b3) stirring the mixture in the preparing pot uniformly, and then sequentially adding nonionic surfactant and anti-redeposition agent, (b4) stirring uniformly, then transferring the slurry to a tray and placing in an oven at 100 degrees C for drying, (b5) drying for 10-14 hours, taking out the solid, grinding in a mortar, and passing through 100 mesh sieve to obtain a powder detergent, and (c) during detergent process the calcium ions of oxalate and water forms calcium oxalate crystals having a particle size of 1-5 mu m, then realizing the utilization and mineralization of carbon dioxide.
DC D25 (Detergents - other than soap and used for cleaning (C11D)); E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.); E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); ; A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN WO2022126278-A1
TI Regenerating adsorption medium, involves receiving            source gas stream comprising hydrocarbon and carbon            dioxide, separating source gas stream into carbon            dioxide-rich gas stream, and desorbing impurities from            adsorption medium by contacting adsorption medium with            carbon dioxide-rich gas stream.
AU HU L
   LIU H
   WILLIAMS S S
AE GRANITEFUEL ENG INC (GRAN-Non-standard)
GA 202281582N
AB    NOVELTY - Method for regenerating an adsorption medium,                involves receiving a source gas stream comprising                at least one hydrocarbon and carbon dioxide,                separating the source gas stream into a carbon                dioxide-rich gas stream and a substantially carbon                dioxide-free gas stream, directing the carbon                dioxide-rich gas stream to a regeneration vessel                containing an adsorption medium having adsorbed                impurities on adsorption medium, desorbing                impurities from adsorption medium by contacting                adsorption medium with carbon dioxide-rich gas                stream to generate a carbon dioxide-rich gas                containing desorbed impurities and a regenerated                adsorption medium, and directing the carbon                dioxide-rich gas containing desorbed impurities out                of regeneration vessel.
   USE - Method for regenerating adsorption medium,                preferably for regenerating siloxane removal media                using a carbon dioxide-rich gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces power consumption, reduces                fire hazard, improves the capacity to remove                siloxanes from a gas stream and reduces high                replacement costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for regenerating an adsorption medium, comprising a                gas inlet coupled to an inlet gas line and in fluid                communication with a source gas stream, a membrane                coupled to the gas inlet line, where the membrane                is downstream from the gas inlet, and the membrane                is configured to separate the source gas stream to                a carbon dioxide-rich gas stream and a                substantially carbon dioxide-free gas stream, an                adsorption vessel coupled to the gas inlet line                between the gas inlet and the membrane, where the                adsorption vessel comprises an adsorption medium                that adsorbs impurities from the source gas stream,                a regeneration line between the membrane and a                regeneration vessel downstream from the membrane,                where the regeneration line is configured to direct                the carbon dioxide-rich gas stream to regeneration                vessel, where the regeneration vessel comprises an                adsorption medium comprising adsorbed impurities, a                heater coupled to the regeneration line between the                membrane and the regeneration vessel, where the                heater is configured to heat the carbon                dioxide-rich gas stream before it enters the                regeneration vessel, and a gas outlet coupled to                the regeneration line, where the gas outlet is                downstream from the regeneration vessel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Device: The membrane is mixed                  matrix membrane. The gas outlet is in fluid                  communication with a vent, gas storage system, or                  a flare. The gas outlet is in fluid communication                  with a system for sequestering carbon dioxide or                  desorbed impurities. The system is configured to                  switch between configurations to alternately                  regenerate the adsorption medium in the                  adsorption vessel or the adsorption medium in the                  regeneration vessel.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon                  dioxide-rich gas stream comprises at least 90                  vol.% carbon dioxide. The adsorption medium in                  adsorption vessel comprises activated carbons,                  silica gel, alumina, zeolites and/or polymeric                  resins. The adsorption medium in adsorption                  vessel and regeneration vessel comprises polymer                  beads, alumina, silica gel, activated carbon                  and/or zeolite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The separating process is                  performed using a membrane, pressure swing                  adsorption device, or any other gas separation                  process. The method further involves after                  separating and before the desorbing, heating the                  carbon dioxide-rich gas stream. The heating                  process involves raising the temperature of the                  carbon dioxide-rich gas stream to a temperature                  of 50-400 degrees C, preferably 50-150 degrees C.                  The separating process is performed using a mixed                  matrix membrane. The method further involves                  after receiving the source gas stream comprising                  at least one hydrocarbon and carbon dioxide, and                  before separating the source gas stream to carbon                  dioxide-rich gas stream and the substantially                  carbon dioxide-free gas stream by directing the                  source gas stream into an adsorption vessel                  containing an adsorption medium in the adsorption                  vessel, contacting the source gas stream with the                  adsorption medium in the adsorption vessel and                  adsorbing impurities from the source gas stream                  onto the adsorption medium in the adsorption                  vessel to generate an adsorption medium in the                  adsorption vessel containing adsorbed impurities                  on the adsorption medium in the adsorption                  vessel. Preferred Components: At least one                  hydrocarbon comprises methane. The adsorbed                  impurities comprise siloxanes. The source gas                  stream is received from a digester or                  landfill.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN113446080-A; CN217152053-U
TI Three-mode carbon dioxide waste heat power            generation and energy storage system has compressor            that is connected to cooler of terminal compression and            cooling combination, and completing waste heat direct            power generation component.
AU HE H
   ZHU C
AE BEIJING ZHONGRE INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2021B5673X
AB    NOVELTY - The three-mode carbon dioxide waste heat power                generation and energy storage system has an energy                discharging component which includes sets of                expanders, heaters (41) and high-pressure liquid                pumps (7) corresponding to the expanders                one-to-one. The electric motor drives the                multi-stage compressor to compress the carbon                dioxide, and the temperature is gradually reduced                after each stage of compression, so that the carbon                dioxide is gradually pressurized, cooled and                liquefied. The liquid carbon dioxide outlet of the                carbon dioxide storage tank (1) is in communication                with the inlet of the second throttling device                (10). The D port of the cryogenic heat exchanger                (9) is in communication with the inlet of the small                compressor (11). The outlet of the small compressor                is connected to the inlet of the cooler of the                terminal compression and cooling combination. The                waste heat direct power generation component is                completed.
   USE - Three-mode carbon dioxide waste heat power                generation and energy storage system used in carbon                dioxide energy storage technology field for energy                saving and emission reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid carbon dioxide flowing out of the                carbon dioxide liquid storage device absorbs heat                and evaporates, enters the expander to perform                external work, and completes the direct power                generation of the waste heat. The volume of the                carbon dioxide gas storage device increases, and                the volume of the carbon dioxide gas storage device                decreases, so as to achieve a constant pressure in                the carbon dioxide gas storage device. The system                can realize power peak shaving, frequency                modulation, phase modulation, voltage support,                spinning reserve, emergency response and other                energy storage services to improve the efficiency,                stability, and safety of the power system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                three-mode carbon dioxide waste heat power                generation and energy storage system. (Drawing                includes non-English language text)
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (1)
   High-pressure liquid pumps (7)
   Cryogenic heat exchanger (9)
   Second throttling device (10)
   Small compressor (11)
   Heaters (41)

PT P
PN DE102016219990-A1; WO2018069504-A1; DE102016219990-B4; CN110022964-A; EP3525918-A1; US2020047120-A1; EP3525918-B1; ES2858174-T3; US11247172-B2
TI Separation and storage of carbon dioxide/carbon            monoxide involves setting stoichiometric ratio of            carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide to hydrogen suitable for            methanation, splitting generated methane, and            processing resulting solid carbon.
AU FULDE M
AE FULDE M (FULD-Individual)
   FULDE M (FULD-Individual)
   FULDE M (FULD-Individual)
   FLD TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (FLDT-Non-standard)
   FLD TECHNOLOGIES GMBH (FLDT-Non-standard)
GA 201834721D
AB    NOVELTY - Separation and storage of carbon                dioxide/carbon monoxide involves setting a                stoichiometric ratio of carbon dioxide to hydrogen                and/or carbon monoxide to hydrogen suitable for a                methanation reaction by supplying a corresponding                amount of hydrogen or alternately carbon dioxide                and/or carbon monoxide with an additional gas (24)                to an exhaust gas (18), carrying out a catalytic                reaction to obtain methane and water, separating                the methane, splitting the methane into carbon and                hydrogen to obtain solid carbon, collecting the                eliminated carbon, and depositing the collected                carbon.
   USE - Separation and storage of carbon                dioxide/carbon monoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and                energy-efficient separation and storage of carbon                dioxide/carbon monoxide. During the process,                methane is removed so as to prevent its escape into                the atmosphere. Pre-purified exhaust gases are used                as starting material for the methanation reaction,                without the need for an upstream, complete                oxidation of methane or carbon monoxide to carbon                dioxide. The dumping of carbon in solid form                eliminates the risks of re-emission and geological                risks associated with compressing the gaseous                carbon dioxide under high pressure. The carbon                dioxide and/or carbon monoxide can be transported                without relying on a power grid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation and storage of carbon                dioxide/carbon monoxide involves setting a                stoichiometric ratio of carbon dioxide to hydrogen                and/or carbon monoxide to hydrogen suitable for a                methanation reaction by supplying a corresponding                amount of hydrogen or alternately carbon dioxide                and/or carbon monoxide with an additional gas to an                exhaust gas, carrying out a catalytic reaction in                which carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide, and                hydrogen are converted into methane and water as                starting materials, separating the methane from the                product of the catalytic reaction, splitting the                methane into carbon and hydrogen to obtain solid                carbon, collecting the eliminated carbon, and                depositing the collected carbon.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                the separation and storage of carbon dioxide/carbon                monoxide.
   Reactor (10)
   Separator (12)
   Gas network (16)
   Exhaust gas (18)
   Additional gas (24)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process, for                  adjusting the stoichiometric ratio in the exhaust                  gas the content of carbon dioxide and/or carbon                  monoxide, and hydrogen are measured. In the                  setting step, a stoichiometric ratio of four                  hydrogen molecules to one molecule of carbon                  dioxide and/or three hydrogen molecules per                  molecule of carbon monoxide in the exhaust gas is                  set. Methane is separated by condensation,                  adsorption or membrane separation. Splitting of                  methane is carried out by pyrolysis where                  hydrogen is generated and the energy required for                  hydrolysis is provided by partial utilization of                  hydrogen. During methane separation, the methane                  is fed into a gas network (16) and is taken from                  the gas network in the splitting step. The                  exhaust gas is desulfurized in a pretreatment,                  cleaned of other impurities and/or dedusted.                  Hydrogen supplied during the setting step is                  obtained by an electrolysis process where the                  resulting oxygen is supplied to a combustion                  process in which the process-treated exhaust gas                  is formed. The hydrogen generated during the                  splitting step is used as s starting material in                  the chemical industry, and as an energy source                  for the production of electric power, heat or as                  a fuel for vehicles.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).)

TI Manufacture of polyetherester foam beads for, e.g. aviation devices, by adjusting supercritical carbon dioxide to second pressure, and performing exhausting procedure to convert carbon dioxide at second pressure into gaseous carbon dioxide.
AU TAN C S
   CHUANG P K
   LIAO W C
   CHEN Y X
AE UNIV NAT TSING HUA (UNTH-C)
   CHANGCHUN PLASTICS CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
   CHANGCHUN PETROCHEM CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
   DAIREN CHEM CORP (DAIR-Non-standard)
GA 202243663U
AB    NOVELTY - A method for manufacturing polyether ester foam beads includes: (i) allowing polyether ester pellets to absorb supercritical carbon dioxide under a first pressure; (ii) decreasing the pressure of the supercritical carbon dioxide to a second pressure, and maintaining at the second pressure for a predetermined time period; and (iii) performing an exhausting procedure to convert the supercritical carbon dioxide in the polyether ester pellets at the second pressure into gaseous carbon dioxide, thereby obtaining polyether ester foam beads. A method for foaming-and-molding polyether ester is disclosed as well.

TI High-impact carbon dioxide copolymer comprises carbon dioxide-epoxycyclohexane copolymer, modified toughening agent containing thermoplastic styrene butadiene rubber, and modified inorganic particles.
AU GAO F
   CAI Y
   WANG X
   ZHOU Q
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202143584E
AB    NOVELTY - High-impact carbon dioxide copolymer comprises 100 pts. wt. carbon dioxide-epoxycyclohexane copolymer, 10-15 pts. wt. modified toughening agent comprising 100 parts mass thermoplastic styrene butadiene rubber, 5-10 parts mass dispersant and 1-2 parts mass 2-(3,4-epoxycyclohexyl)ethyltrimethoxysilane, and 1-2 pts. wt. modified inorganic particles.
   USE - As high-impact carbon dioxide copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The copolymer has improved impact resistance and excellent strength and hardness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing high impact carbon dioxide copolymer involving melt blending carbon dioxide-epoxycyclohexane copolymer, modified toughening agent and modified inorganic particles to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The modified inorganic particles comprise rigid inorganic particles surface-treated with a coupling agent. The rigid inorganic particles are silicon nitride, fumed white carbon black, neutral alumina, carbon black and/or silicon carbide. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the coupling agent to the rigid inorganic particles is 0.5-2%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dispersant comprises glyceryl triacetate, glyceryl tripropionate and/or glyceryl tributyrate. The coupling agent comprises silane coupling agent, titanate coupling agent, aluminate coupling agent and/or borate coupling agent.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The modified toughening agent is prepared by melt blending thermoplastic styrene-butadiene rubber, dispersant and 2-(3,4-epoxycyclohexyl)ethyltrimethoxysilane to obtain a modified toughening agent at 180-190 degrees C for 5-10 minutes. The modified inorganic particles is prepared by mixing and heating the rigid inorganic particles and coupling agent at 110-120 degrees C. The method involves heating the rigid inorganic particles, leaving still and adding coupling agent for mixing to obtain modified inorganic particles. Preferred Conditions: The temperature of the melt blending is 180-190 degrees C, and the time is 3-8 minutes. Preferred Properties: The number average molecular weight of the carbon dioxide-epoxycyclohexane copolymer is 40-60 kg/mol, the molecular weight distribution is 1.8-3, the thermoplastic styrene-butadiene rubber is linear styrene-butadiene rubber, and the melt flow rate is 0.1-5 g/10 minutes.

PT P
PN SE202051285-A1
TI System for thermal energy recovery in kraft pulp mill, has black liquor evaporation system that is provided with first Gas-heated Evaporation Effect (GEE) having heat exchanger arranged in stream of stripper gas downstream of desorption unit.
AU WOLF J
AE RISE INNVENTIA AB (RISE-Non-standard)
   RISE RES INST SWEDEN AB (RISE-Non-standard)
GA 202281760U
AB    NOVELTY - The system has one boiler that is provided for combustion of carbon-based fuel and for producing a flue gas containing carbon dioxide. A carbon dioxide absorption unit is configured to absorb carbon dioxide in the flue gas to a lean carbon dioxide absorption medium to produce a rich carbon dioxide absorption medium and a stream of flue gas with reduced carbon dioxide content. A desorption unit is configured to heat the rich carbon dioxide absorption medium to desorbed carbon dioxide and produce a stream (1506) of stripper gas (1012) comprising carbon dioxide, water and optionally carbon dioxide absorption medium. A black liquor evaporation system (1500) has a first GEE (1502) with a heat exchanger (1504) arranged in the stream of stripper gas downstream of the desorption unit. The GEE is configured to transfer heat from the stream of stripper gas to a stream of black liquor such that the two streams are physically separated.
   USE - System for thermal energy recovery in kraft pulp mill.
   ADVANTAGE - The systems and methods are more energy and resource efficient in capturing carbon dioxide in a kraft pulp mill. The system builds on the idea that the thermal energy of the wet carbon dioxide desorbed from the carbon dioxide medium can be efficiently used and replace freshly generated primary steam in the black liquor evaporation plant of the kraft pulp mill such that the energy losses in a kraft pulp mill implementing carbon dioxide capture technology can be reduced. The pulp mill is integrated with a post combustion carbon capture process that provides a significant potential to save energy. The carbon dioxide can be cooled to low temperatures to reduce compressor work in the next steps and to start the liquefaction process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:

   USE - The method is useful for comprehensive utilizing of carbon dioxide gas using realized carbon dioxide production system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensive utilizing of carbon dioxide gas using realized carbon dioxide production system, where the carbon dioxide production system comprises mutually connected methanol device, electric device of lime and soda lime kiln device system for producing methanol device, electric device lime and soda lime byproduct carbon dioxide gas, where the generated gas concentration is different, but both can be used as the raw material of soda device, electric device lime kiln which uses gas sleeve lime kiln technology, where the obvious characteristic of sleeve kiln technology generated by circulating gas to make material form a parallel flow calcining belt in the kiln and fuel, where the annular sleeve kiln can produce high activity lime and uses heat in the kiln to reduce heat consumption of lime product, which substantially reduces the calcinations of lime and over-burning phenomenon, the soda lime kiln device uses mixed burning lime kiln mainly limestone and coke to calcine, where its design for calcining limestone kiln is to provide pure carbon dioxide gas concentration device; the control carbon dioxide is not greater than 37%, subjecting the methanol generated by the raw material gas conversion and low-temperature methanol washing technology to obtain methanol synthetic gas, where the low temperature methanol washing is carried out for producing high and low concentration of carbon dioxide gas, where the byproduct gas has high concentration, which can be directly transferred to calcined soda device adopting pipeline compression procedure for compressing, where the gas concentration is high, thus carrying out subsequent carbonization process drastic reaction to generate the particle size of sodium bicarbonate, which is relatively small and not conducive to the follow-up soda ash requirements.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The limestone kiln gas produced by the calcium carbide plant lime kiln is sent to the soda kiln gas scrubbing plant after bag dusting, and the carbonization process is performed after passing through the compression process. The kiln gas generated during the calcination process is subjected to a subsequent process after the soda kiln gas scrubber unit to produce soda ash. The methanol plant uses East China university of science and technology to produce feed gas using coal-water slurry pressure gasification technology. The compressed carbon dioxide gas in the methanol plant and the kiln gas after washing are used for gas distribution, thus the concentration is controlled within a certain range and sent to the carbonization process. The limekiln fuel uses calcium carbide equipment to produce calcium carbide by-products calcium carbide furnace gas and coking plant coke oven gas as the main source of gas, natural gas as a spare to produce the active lime part of the calcium carbide furnace smelting production of calcium carbide and some sent to the soda plant as raw materials. The lime kiln combustion air is preheated to 450 degrees C and then sent into the combustion chamber. The exhaust gas temperature after heat exchange is reduced from 800 degrees C to less than 200 degrees C. The methanol plant cryogenic methanol wash technology is introduced by introducing the world's most advanced technology, the German Lurgi company's patented technology. The use of pressurized liquefaction rectification to produce food grade carbon dioxide provides protection for subsequent downstream customer demand and surplus carbon dioxide gas is used to provide raw materials for lithium carbonate production.

PN WO2020092534-A1; CA3117938-A1; US2021262104-A1
TI Apparatus for converting carbon dioxide and natural gas liquids into other chemicals and/or fuel, comprises at least one electrochemical cell, where electrochemical cell reduces endothermic load with electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction.
AU TREMBLY J P
AE UNIV OHIO (UOHI-C)
   UNIV OHIO (UOHI-C)
GA 202037642S
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus comprises at least one electrochemical cell, where the electrochemical cell reduces the endothermic load associated with electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction.
   USE - The apparatus is used for converting carbon dioxide and natural gas liquids into other chemicals and/or fuels.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus provides cost-effective/selective conversion of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide and ethane to ethylene at intermediate temperatures in a feasible manner. The process provides competitive process integration schemes for conversion of carbon dioxide from coal-fired power plants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for converting carbon dioxide and natural gas liquids into carbon monoxide and other chemicals and/or fuels, comprising converting carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide and converting ethane into ethylene at 650-750 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The electrochemical cell comprises a first electrochemical cell and a second electrochemical cell. Each of the first electrochemical cell and second electrochemical cell includes a cathode, a membrane, and an anode. The first electrochemical cell is adapted to convert carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide and convert ethane into ethylene, and the second electrochemical cell is adapted to offset voltage and heat requirements of the first electrochemical cell. The conversion of carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide and conversion ethane into ethylene in the first electrochemical cell occurs simultaneously. The second electrochemical cell is adapted to convert ethane into ethylene. The electrochemical cell is at least one component of an intermediate temperature solid oxide electrolyzer cell. The temperature for operation of the intermediate temperature solid oxide electrolyzer cell is 650-750 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The flue gas is the source of carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of converting carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide and converting ethane into ethylene occurs via a first electrochemical cell. The method further comprises offsetting voltage and heat requirements of the first electrochemical cell. The step of offsetting voltage and heat requirements of the first electrochemical cell occurs through use of a second electrochemical cell. The method further comprises converting ethane into ethylene via the second electrochemical cell, and reducing an endothermic load associated with electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction that occurs during the conversion of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide. The step of converting of carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide and converting of ethane into ethylene occurs simultaneously. The ethylene generated at the electrochemical cell upon conversion of ethane into ethylene subsequently converted into a fuel or fuels using an oligomerization catalyst. The method further comprises: removing nitrogen from the product of the electrochemical cell; and using a complex containing cuprous ammonium salts of organic acids to form complexes with carbon monoxide to assist in removing nitrogen.

PN CN114994243-A
TI Monitoring carbon dioxide underground sealing gas            leakage, involves obtaining weather data and gas data            under condition of confirming no leakage and under            current condition, comparing predicted data of carbon            dioxide gas with gas data of current condition after            down-sampling process.
AU CHEN H
   YANG X
   SUN J
   WANG B
   HAN P
AE UNIV SOUTHERN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (USOU-C)
GA 2022B9724F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining weather data and                gas data under the condition of confirming no                leakage and under the current condition. The                down-sampling process is performed on the weather                data. The model training data is obtained after                performing a singular spectrum decomposition                processing on the temperature data and the gas                data. A trained wide learning network model is                obtained. The carbon dioxide gas is predicted. The                predicted data of the carbon dioxide is compared                with the current temperature data after the down                sampling process is completed. The monitored                underground seal gas is leaked when the temperature                is continuously higher than the predicted                temperature data.
   USE - The method is useful for monitoring carbon                dioxide underground sealing gas leakage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method of reducing sampling and singular                spectrum decomposition processing, effectively                reducing the data amount, removing the noise data,                ensuring the data information amount and reducing                the calculation amount, which can improve the                training efficiency of the width learning network                model. The method can accurately judge whether the                underground carbon dioxide gas leakage condition                occurs, timely sending early warning, avoiding the                greater influence and harm, meeting with the                existing isotope detection method, the application                is simpler, reduces the monitoring cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                carbon dioxide underground sealing gas leakage                monitoring system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method                for monitoring carbon dioxide underground sealing                gas leakage (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

PT P
PN WO2019213705-A1; CA3098591-A1; AU2019266358-A1; CN112203745-A; SG11202010668-A1; IN202017048565-A; BR112020022847-A2; EP3813986-A1; VN77263-A; PH12020551890-A1; KR2021100006-A; JP2021524806-W; US2021370233-A1; EP3813986-A4; IN393766-B; RU2020139290-A
TI Integrated process for carbon dioxide capture,            sequestration and utilization involves contacting            carbon dioxide-containing gas stream with aqueous            slurry to dissolve magnesium from mineral, and            precipitating magnesium carbonate.
AU BENHELAL E
   BRENT G F
   KENNEDY E M
   OLIVER T K
   RAYSON M S
   STOCKENHUBER M
   BENKHELAL E
   KENNEDI E M
   REISON M S
   STOKENKHUBER M
AE MINERAL CARBONATION INT PTY LTD (MINE-Non-standard)
   MINERAL CARBONATION INT PTY LTD (MINE-Non-standard)
   MINERAL CARBONATION INT PTY LTD (MINE-Non-standard)
   MINERAL CARBONATION INT PTY LTD (MINE-Non-standard)
GA 201995373E
AB    NOVELTY - An integrated process for carbon dioxide                capture, sequestration and utilization involves (a)                providing aqueous slurry comprising aqueous liquid                and particulate solid containing activated                magnesium silicate mineral; (b) in dissolution                stage, contacting a carbon dioxide (CO2)-containing                gas stream with aqueous slurry at 1st pressure to                dissolve magnesium from mineral to provide a slurry                comprising a magnesium ion enriched carbonated                aqueous liquid and magnesium depleted solid                residue; and (c) in a precipitation stage,                precipitating magnesium carbonate from magnesium                ions dissolved in step (b) by multiple successive                stage-wise reductions in pressure, with each stage                being at a lower pressure than preceding stage.                Each successive stage-wise reduction in pressure                releases CO2 which is correspondingly stage-wise                compressed and recycled back into the dissolution                stage.
   USE - An integrated process for carbon dioxide                capture, sequestration and utilization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a permanent and safe                capture and sequestration of carbon dioxide gas                while producing products of economic value. The                method is an efficient and economically viable                integrated process for chemical conversion of                carbon dioxide and magnesium silicate feedstocks to                solid carbonates and silicates, enabling a                reduction in the amount of carbon dioxide                discharged to the atmosphere while simultaneously                producing products of economic utility and                value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for reactor                system adapted to perform the processes, comprising                greater than or equal to 2 precipitation reactors                that are each adapted to provide the stage wise                reduction in pressure and recover precipitated                magnesium carbonates.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Magnesium ion enriched                  carbonated aqueous liquid is produced by                  dissolving the activated magnesium silicate                  mineral in greater than or equal to 1 reactors                  fed by input carbon dioxide (CO2)-containing gas                  stream above atmospheric pressure, in greater                  than or equal to 2 precipitation stages, inducing                  a pH shift in liquid by the removal of dissolved                  CO2 through reducing the pressure to liquid,                  precipitating the magnesium carbonate. Magnesium                  ion enriched carbonated aqueous liquid is                  produced by dissolving the activated magnesium                  silicate mineral in greater than or equal to 1                  reactors fed by an input CO2-containing gas                  stream near, at or above atmospheric pressure, in                  greater than or equal to 2 separate precipitation                  stages, inducing a pH shift in solution by the                  removal of dissolved CO2 through the application                  of a partial vacuum or sub-atmospheric pressure                  in greater than or equal to 1 of precipitation                  stages, precipitating the magnesium carbonate.                  The dissolution stage is performed in a                  dissolution reactor and slurry exiting the                  dissolution reactor is subjected to separation                  process in a separator that separates solids from                  liquids, thus separating the slurry into a slurry                  stream comprising the magnesium ion enriched                  carbonated aqueous liquid and a slurry stream                  comprising the magnesium depleted solid residue.                  Stream comprising CO2 released from any                  stage-wise reduction in pressure is compressed                  and blended with a stream comprising CO2 released                  from any preceding stage before being further                  compressed and recycled back into the dissolution                  stage. Stream comprising CO2 that has been                  compressed is cooled before being blended with a                  stream comprising CO2 released from any preceding                  stage before being further compressed and                  recycled back into the dissolution stage. The                  cooling condenses entrained water vapor. The                  condensed water is recycled back to the process.                  Magnesium depleted solid residue is subjected to                  a wet magnetic separation process to extract an                  iron-rich portion. Magnesium depleted solid                  residue is subjected to chemical processing to                  purify silica contained. Chemical processing is                  acid leaching. Preferred Parameters: The pressure                  in each dissolution stage is 100-20000 kPa and                  the temperature is 20-185 degrees C.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN US2018252468-A1; US10465982-B2
TI Method for natural gas liquefaction and filtration of solidified carbon dioxide, involves storing purified liquefied natural gas in storage tank assembly.
AU TANG C
   HOFER D C
   LISSIANSKI V V
AE GENERAL ELECTRIC CO (GENE-C)
GA 201869345R
AB    NOVELTY - The method (100) involves directing (104) a refrigerant fluid mixture and a flow of natural gas through a first heat exchanger for exchanging heat between a natural gas flow path and a first refrigerant flow path of a refrigerant cycle subsystem. The flow of natural gas exiting from the first heat exchanger is expanded (106) through a first throttle valve resulting in formation of a cold natural gas vapor and slurry comprising a liquefied natural gas and solidified carbon dioxide. The cold natural gas vapor and the slurry comprising the liquefied natural gas and solidified carbon dioxide are directed through a filter sub-assembly. The solidified carbon dioxide is separated by the filter sub-assembly to form a purified liquefied natural gas. A pulse of a cleaning fluid comprising methane and carbon dioxide is directed through the filter sub assembly to remove the solidified carbon dioxide. The purified liquefied natural gas is stored (110) in a storage tank assembly.
   USE - Method for natural gas liquefaction and filtration of solidified carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The larger pressure ratio can result in a larger cooling capacity. The integration of the heat exchanger with the storage tank reduces the portions and simplifies assembly of the system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a method of liquefying natural gas.
   Method for natural gas liquefaction and filtration of solidified carbon dioxide (100)
   Step for directing a refrigerant fluid mixture and a flow of natural gas (104)
   Step for expanding flow of natural gas exiting from the first heat exchanger (106)
   Step for filtering the flow of natural gas for separating solid particles (108)
   Step for storing purified liquefied natural gas (110)

AU GE Q

AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202051996J
AB    NOVELTY - Simultaneous methanation of carbon monoxide                and carbon dioxide involves methanating a mixed gas                comprising carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and                hydrogen under the action of a catalyst comprising                9-20 %mass nickel as an active metal, 74-90 %mass                carbon nanofiber-modified alumina carrier, and                0.1-6 %mass metal oxide additive. The molar ratio                of hydrogen to total content of carbon dioxide and                carbon monoxide is more than 4, preferably 4-30.                The molar ratio of carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide is 0.05-4. The mixed gas optionally                comprises methane, ethane, nitrogen, argon, and                less than 20 %mass other impurity gases.
   USE - Simultaneous methanation of carbon monoxide                and carbon dioxide for preparing natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently and completely converts                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide into natural gas                with a methane selectivity of more than 98%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carbon                  nanofiber-modified alumina carrier comprises more                  than 80 %mass gamma -alumina phase and 0.5-20                  %mass, preferably 2-10 %mass carbon nanofibers.                  The carbon nanofibers optionally comprise 0.01-1                  %mass nickel and/or cobalt. The metal oxide                  additive comprises lanthanum trioxide, cerium                  trioxide, strontium oxide, magnesium oxide, and                  zirconium dioxide, preferably lanthanum trioxide.                  The methanated gas comprises less than 1% carbon                  monoxide and carbon dioxide. Preferred                  Properties: The carbon nanofiber-modified alumina                  carrier has a specific surface area of 30-300                  m2/g, preferably 50-150 m2/g and a carbon fiber                  diameter of 50-300 nm, preferably 150-250 nm.                  Preferred Process: The method further involves,                  before introducing the catalyst, performing                  reduction reaction using 600-3000 mL/(hour.g of                  catalyst) hydrogen at 400-700 degrees C under                  normal pressure for 2-12 hours. The nickel-based                  catalyst is obtained by dissolving 0.02-2 mol/L                  soluble nickel(II) and an additive salt such as                  chloride, nitrate and acetate in deionized water                  to prepare a salt solution, dissolving 0.001-2                  mol/L soluble alkali or salt such as sodium                  hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, sodium carbonate,                  sodium bicarbonate, potassium carbonate,                  potassium bicarbonate, sodium oxalate, potassium                  oxalate, sodium acetate of formula: RCOONa (I),                  potassium acetate of formula: RCOOK (II), and                  ammonia in deionized water to prepare 0.1-8 mol/L                  alkaline solution, co-precipitating the salt                  solution and alkaline solution in a suspension                  comprising the carbon nanofiber-modified alumina                  carrier at 50-80 degrees C in a parallel flow,                  maintaining the pH of suspension liquid phase to                  6-8, aging for 1-5 hours, separating the                  precipitated product from the suspension by                  centrifugation or suction filtration, washing the                  precipitated product with deionized water, drying                  at 80-130 degrees C, and baking at 400-800                  degrees C for 2-10 hours, or immersing the                  alumina carrier in the salt solution, stirring,                  maintaining still, drying, and baking at 400-800                  degrees C for 2-10 hours.R = organic functional group chosen from                  1-12C alkyl, 2-12C alkenyl, and 6-15C aryl,                  preferably methyl, ethyl or phenyl.Preferred Conditions: The mixed gas is                  methanated at 250-400 degrees C, preferably                  280-380 degrees C, pressure of 0.01-4 MPa,                  preferably 0.1-3 MPa, and a raw material gas                  space velocity of 500-50000 mL/(hour.g of                  catalyst), preferably 2000-10000 mL/(hour.g of                  catalyst).

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The injection of the                  liquefied petroleum gas cracking fluid without                  the proppant is performed at rate of 3-5                  m3/minute (preferably, 3.8-4.2 m3/minute), volume                  of 35-40 m3 (preferably, 36-38 m3), and pressure                  of 35-40 MPa, preferably 35-38 MPa. The injection                  of the liquid carbon dioxide cracking fluid                  without the proppant is performed at rate of 5-10                  m3/minute (preferably, 6.5-7.5 m3/minute), volume                  of 220-300 m3 (preferably, 240-290 m3), and                  pressure of 35-50 MPa, preferably 40-45 MPa. The                  injection of the liquid carbon dioxide cracking                  fluid containing the proppant is performed at                  rate of 5-10 m3/minute (preferably, 6.5-7.5                  m3/minute), volume of 80-150 m3 (preferably,                  80-120 m3), and pressure of 50-58 MPa, preferably                  50-53 MPa. The injection of the liquefied                  petroleum gas cracking fluid containing the                  proppant is performed at rate of 5-10 m3/minute                  (preferably, 6.5-7.5 m3/minute), volume of 4.-70                  m3 (preferably, 50-60 m3), and pressure of 35-40                  MPa, preferably 35-38 MPa. Preferred Composition:                  The liquefied petroleum gas cracking fluid                  without proppant contains 0-2 wt.% tackifier, 0-3                  wt.% crosslinking agent, and 95 wt.% pure liquid                  propane. The tackifier includes                  bis-alkylphosphate tackifier. The crosslinking                  agent includes a complex iron crosslinking agent.                  The liquid carbon dioxide cracking fluid without                  the proppant contains 0-2 wt.% thickener and more                  than 98 wt.% pure liquid carbon dioxide. The                  liquid carbon dioxide cracking fluid containing                  proppant contains 10-15 wt.% proppant including a                  low-density ceramic proppant and/or a low-density                  coated sand proppant. The liquefied petroleum gas                  cracking fluid containing the proppant contains                  20-25 wt.% proppant including large-sized                  low-density ceramic proppant and/or a large-sized                  low-density coated sand proppant.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The thickener                  includes a block copolymer of highly fluorinated                  acrylate and partially sulfonated styrene.

PN RU2019112440-A
TI Method for cleaning pipelines of hydraulic systems from oil and operational contaminants with supercritical carbon dioxide, involves cooling working medium to temperature below critical temperature for carbon dioxide in supply tank.
AU SOSNINA YU N
   POSPELOVA A V
   PATRAKEEV A N
AE ONEGA PROJECT NO TECHNO OFFICE SUIT (ONEG-Non-standard)
GA 2020A8733C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves washing the pipeline being washed using the working medium, and returning the working medium to the supply tank through the filter. The carbon dioxide is used as the working medium, and is placed in the supply tank in a liquid state. The pressure of the working medium is created and maintained by specified pump above the critical for carbon dioxide. The working medium is heated to the temperature above the critical temperature for carbon dioxide. The carbon dioxide is transferred into the supercritical liquid state. The working medium is separated from impurities on the specified adsorption filter, and is returned to the supply tank. The working medium is cooled to the temperature below the critical temperature for carbon dioxide maintained in the supply tank using a refrigeration unit. The residual carbon dioxide from the flushed pipeline after flushing is removed into the atmosphere.
   USE - Method for cleaning pipelines of hydraulic systems from oil and operational contaminants with supercritical carbon dioxide.

The                  carbon dioxide hydrogenation catalyst for                  methanol production in the first-stage reactor R1                  comprises copper-based catalysts, palladium-based                  catalysts, zirconium-based catalysts, and                  indium-based catalysts. The reaction conditions                  of the first-stage reactor R1 are 1-15 MPa, the                  reaction temperature is 180-400degrees Celsius,                  and the space velocity is 1000-20000 per hour.                  The carbon dioxide-rich synthesis gas-to-methanol                  catalyst in the secondary reactor R2 comprises                  copper-based catalysts, palladium-based                  catalysts, zirconium-based catalysts, and                  indium-based catalysts. The reaction conditions                  of the secondary reactor R2 are 1-5 MPa, the                  reaction temperature is 180-400degrees Celsius,                  and the space velocity is 1000-20000 per hour.                  The primary reactor R1 and secondary reactor R2                  is fixed bed reactor, fluidized bed reactor or                  slurry bed reactor. The amount of circulating                  reaction gas entering the secondary reactor R2 is                  adjusted according to the amount of carbon                  dioxide converted, the amount of carbon dioxide                  conversion is large, the amount of circulating                  reactant gas is adjusted to be small, the amount                  of carbon dioxide conversion is small, and the                  amount of circulating reactant gas is adjusted to                  be large. The circulation ratio of the secondary                  reactor R2 is 0.1:1-10:1.

PT P
PN US2020087590-A1
TI Making combustible gas without producing polluting emissions from fossil hydrocarbons comprises e.g. making primary gases by water electrolysis, mixing and filtering gases, internally making carbon dioxide gas and storing carbon dioxide gas.
AU PUMA N
   ILARDI G
   LI G F
AE SYNTACTIC DREAM ENERGY LLC (SYNT-Non-standard)
   PUMA N (PUMA-Individual)
   ILARDI G (ILAR-Individual)
   LI G F (LIGF-Individual)
GA 202021894V
AB    NOVELTY - Efficiently producing a combustible gas, without producing polluting emissions from fossil hydrocarbons, comprises: (a) producing primary gases by water electrolysis; (b) mixing and filtering such primary gases; (c) internally producing carbon dioxide gas using a voltage of 1-240 V (DC) and 1-400 V (AC); (d) storing such carbon dioxide gas; (e) mixing the filtered primary gases with the previously produced carbon dioxide gas (and carbon monoxide gas) in a catalytic reaction at 15-500 degrees C and at a pressure of 1-10 bar, where the gas mixture is conveyed over the surface of a desired catalyst; (f) releasing undesired and unneeded molecules; (g) collecting the final gas formulation, having the desired chemical and physical properties; (h) recovering water produced through the process; and (i) recovering any residual carbon dioxide gas for use in the process in combination with the produced/stored carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - The method and system are useful for efficiently producing a combustible gas, without producing polluting emissions from fossil hydrocarbons (claimed), where the combustible gas is useful e.g. as fuel for electricity and thermal energy production for domestic and industrial purposes and as fuel for transportation applications, and in connection with renewable energy storage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method and system: produce a combustible gas within the internal workings and can efficiently be burned at relatively lower temperatures and pressures without the production of high levels of pollutants and exhibit physical characteristics that allow the product to be produced, stored, transported and use cost effectively and safely; can intake fuel that is originally sourced from a supply network; can be operated at temperatures and pressures suitable for residences and other locations (e.g. motor vehicles), with less concern despite the potential residential setting and the smaller size of the space; and are cost-efficient and safe transport, storage and use.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a system comprising (a1) a means of producing primary gases through water electrolysis, (b1) a means of mixing and filtering such primary gases, (c1) a means of producing carbon dioxide gas, (d1) a means of storing such carbon dioxide gas, (e) a means of mixing the filtered primary gases with the previously produced carbon dioxide gas (and carbon monoxide gas) in a catalytic reactor at 15-500 degrees C and at a pressure of 1-10 bar, (f1) a means of conveying the primary gas/carbon dioxide/carbon monoxide gas mixture over the surface of a desired catalyst, (g1) a means of releasing undesired and unneeded molecules, (h1) a means of collecting the final gas formulation with the desired chemical properties, (i1) a means of recovering water produced through the process, and (j1) a means of recovering any residual carbon dioxide gas for use in the process in combination with the produced/stored carbon dioxide gas; and
   (2) a combustible fuel gas produced through the practice of the method and system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further comprises means for conveying the produced gas to the filter and means for conveying and regulating the gas flow towards the catalytic reactor; a programmable logic computer system controlling and adjusting the system and process; a combustion chamber for the generation of thermal energy containing inside the catalytic reactor, means for conveying and regulating the gas flow towards such combustion chamber, a thermoelectric generator embedded to such combustion chamber for the production of electric energy, means for using the electric energy produced to charge the battery, a heat exchanger to transfer the heat generated in such combustion chamber to a desired element, means for separating carbon dioxide from exhaust gases, a fan for conveying exhaust gases from such combustion chamber to such means of separating carbon dioxide, a programmable logic computer to control and regulate the operation of the machine and the process, means for detecting the water and the air temperature at the outlet of the heat exchanger and for the regulation of the water or air circulation, means for detecting the temperature of the incoming water and air to the heat exchanger for adjusting the flow of the water or air, means for water or air circulation through the heat exchanger with water or air, means for pushing the flue gas through the carbon dioxide separator and towards the exhaust, means for the transport of the recovered carbon dioxide to the carbon dioxide generator, and means to connect the programmable logic computer to the temperature sensor, the pressure sensor, the control valves and the electric switches.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalytic reaction produces a hot gas flow composed of methane, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and water. The final gas formulation is burned to produce heat. Preferred Components: The means of producing primary gases through water electrolysis receives a water solution from a reservoir. The filtering cleans the gases produced through electrolysis.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN113398970-A
TI Preparing zinc oxide nanowire array/three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide nanotube composite material used in field of photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises e.g. mixing zinc nitrate and urea, and calcining.
AU ZHANG J
   SHAO S
   WANG T
AE WUHAN INST TECHNOLOGY (WUHT-C)
GA 2021B0994S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing zinc oxide nanowire array/three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide (rGO) nanotube composite material comprises (i) using graphite powder as raw material to obtain graphene oxide suspension, (ii) immersing melamine sponge in the graphene oxide suspension and carrying out pretreatment, drying graphene oxide composite sponge and then calcining for the first time, and removing the polymer template to obtain three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide, (iii) dispersing zinc acetate in ethanol to obtain mixture A, soaking three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide in the mixture A, and(iv) mixing zinc nitrate, urea and hexamethylenetetramine to obtain mixed solution B, soaking the zinc-oxide seed nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide in the mixed solution B, uniformly stirring, carrying out the hydrothermal reaction in the high-pressure hydrothermal kettle, rinsing reaction product and then calcining for the third time.
   USE - The material is useful in field of photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: exhibits excellent photocatalytic activity; has good carbon dioxide adsorption performance, and good catalytic effect for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing zinc oxide nanowire array/three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide (rGO) nanotube composite material comprises (i) using graphite powder as raw material to obtain graphene oxide suspension, (ii) immersing melamine sponge in the graphene oxide suspension and carrying out pretreatment to obtain graphene oxide composite sponge, drying graphene oxide composite sponge and then calcining for the first time, and removing the polymer template to obtain three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide, (iii) dispersing zinc acetate in ethanol to obtain mixture A, soaking three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide in the mixture A, and forming zinc-oxide seed crystal nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide by the second calcinations, and(iv) mixing zinc nitrate, urea and hexamethylenetetramine to obtain mixed solution B, soaking the zinc-oxide seed nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide in the mixed solution B, uniformly stirring, carrying out the hydrothermal reaction in the high-pressure hydrothermal kettle, rinsing reaction product and then calcining for the third time, post-processing to obtain zinc-oxide nanowire array/three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide nanotube composite material. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for zinc-oxide nanowire array/three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide nanotube composite material, comprising three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide nanotube framework and a zinc-oxide nanowire array grown on the surface of the three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide nanotube framework. The framework of the three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide nanotubes is a mesh porous structure. The zinc-oxide nanowire array vertically covers the surface of the three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide nanotube nanotube skeleton.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the concentration of the graphene oxide suspension is 2.3 g/l. In the step (ii), the pretreatment includes washing the melamine sponge with ethanol and then drying at 80 degrees C for 12 hours. The calcination temperature of the first calcination is 500-600 degrees C, and the calcination time is 0.5-3.5 minutes. In the step (iii), the mass ratio of the zinc acetate and the three-dimensional nitrogen-doped reduced graphene oxide is 0.1-20%, the calcination temperature of the second calcination is 190-210 degrees C, and the calcination time is 1-25 minutes. In the step (iv), the temperature of the hydrothermal reaction is 60-120 degrees C, and the reaction time is 10-14 hours. The calcination temperature of the third calcination is 440-460 degrees C, and the calcination time is 1-1.5 hours. Preferred Components: In the step (iv), the molar ratio of the zinc nitrate, urea and the hexamethylenetetramine is (0.1-1):(0.1-1):(0.1-1).
