PN CN113322109-A
TI Vanadium inhibitor composition with high magnesium            content comprises e.g. organic solvent, low molecular            weight alcohol, phenolic substance, long-chain alkyl            benzene sulfonic acid, heavy alkyl benzene sulfonic            acid, and low molecular acid.
AU LI X
   BU W
   WANG L
   FAN J
   ZHANG C
AE XINXIANG RUIFENG NEW MATERIAL CO LTD (XINX-Non-standard)
GA 2021A40536
AB    NOVELTY - Vanadium inhibitor composition with high                magnesium content comprises 150-300 pts. wt.                organic solvent, 8-12 pts. wt. low molecular weight                alcohol, 6-15 pts. wt. phenolic substance, 4-10                pts. wt. long-chain alkyl benzene sulfonic acid,                6-24 pts. wt. heavy alkyl benzene sulfonic acid,                62-100 pts. wt. fuel oil, 5-9 pts. wt. low                molecular acid, 4-8 pts. wt. 10-18C fatty acid,                5-10 pts. wt. polyoxyethylene fatty acid ester,                10-16 pts. wt. fatty alcohol polyoxyethylene ether,                35-55 pts. wt. magnesium oxide and 20-35 pts. wt.                water.
   USE - Used as vanadium inhibitor composition with                high magnesium content.
   ADVANTAGE - The vanadium inhibitor has magnesium content                of 13-16%, base value of 550-680 mgKOH/g, viscosity                of 35-100 CST at 100 degrees C, has good oil                solubility, is brown and red and translucent in                color, does no cause layering, and does not                precipitate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing a vanadium inhibitor composition with                high magnesium content, comprising dividing                magnesium oxide into three parts according to a                certain ratio, which are respectively the first                part of magnesium oxide, the second part of                magnesium oxide and the third part of magnesium                oxide; dividing the water into four parts according                to a certain proportion, namely the first part of                water, the second part of water, the third part of                water and the fourth part of water; mixing organic                solvents, low molecular weight alcohols, phenolic                substances, long-chain alkyl benzene sulfonic acid,                heavy alkyl benzene sulfonic acid, 10-18C fatty                acids, polyoxyethylene fatty acid esters, fatty                alcohol oxyethylene ether and fuel oil with the                first part of magnesium oxide to form a mixed                liquid, heating to 40-110 degrees C, and allowing                the mixed liquid to undergo a neutralization                reaction; adding low-molecular-weight acids, the                second part of magnesium oxide, first part of                water, and carbon dioxide into the mixed solution;                adding second part of water into the mixed solution                again, continuing to feed carbon dioxide; adding                third part of magnesium oxide and third part of                water into the mixed solution, continuing to feed                in carbon dioxide; adding the fourth part into the                mixed solution, adding water, continuing to feed                carbon dioxide; aerating, maintaining the                temperature of the mixed liquid at 90-110 degrees                C, and dealing with the mixed liquid to remove the                low-molecular alcohol and water in the mixed                liquid, diluting and centrifuging to obtain                centrifugal liquid; vacuum distilling the                centrifugal liquid to remove the organic solvent                cleanly to obtain the vanadium inhibitor with high                magnesium content.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is                  toluene or xylene, and the low-molecular alcohol                  is methanol, ethylene glycol or n-butanol. The                  phenolic substance is a sulfurized alkylphenate                  or a 3-12C alkylphenol, the low-molecular acid is                  formic acid or acetic acid, and the 10-18C fatty                  acid is myristic acid. The first part of                  magnesium oxide, the second part of magnesium                  oxide and the third part of magnesium oxide in                  the ratio of 13%:55%:32%. The ratio of the first                  part of water, the second part of water, the                  third part of water and the fourth part of water                  is 27%:23%:27%:23%. The total amount of carbon                  dioxide introduced is 80-100% of the total                  consumption of the magnesium oxide; and the                  proportion of carbon dioxide input is                  0%:2-25%:36-40%:54-60%. Preferred Method: The                  aeration rate of introducing carbon dioxide is                  80-120 ml/minute. During centrifugation, the                  rotation speed is 1800 revolutions per minute,                  the centrifugation time is 40 minutes, and the                  turbidity of the clear liquid is controlled to be                  less than 5 NTU. The temperature when obtaining                  the vanadium suppressor with high magnesium                  content is less than or equal to maximum                  distillation range of the fuel oil.

PT P
PN WO2020115001-A1; EP3891831-A1; US2022029186-A1
TI Direct alcohol fuel cell (DAFC) in microelectronic device, has housing that comprises venting hole that provides fluid communication between anode section and ambient environment, and is located within spacing distance.
AU LUND-OLESEN T
   VESTENTOFT K
   TYNELIUS O
   BONDE J L
   HALES J H
   CHRISTENSEN L H
AE WIDEX AS (WIDX-C)
   WIDEX AS (WIDX-C)
   WIDEX AS (WIDX-C)
GA 202050740E
AB    NOVELTY - The DAFC (1) comprises housing (10) containing a proton exchange membrane (PEM) (44) separating an anode section from a cathode section which are contained in the housing. The cathode section comprises a cathode collection element (31) that is electrically connected to a cathode catalyst which is in diffusive communication with a gaseous oxidant. The anode section comprises an anode collection element (41) that is electrically connected to an anode catalyst. A pervaporation membrane is located at a spacing distance from the PEM and provides diffusive communication between the anode catalyst and a fuel supply. The housing comprises a venting hole that provides fluid communication between the anode section and the ambient environment and has a largest dimension in the range of 25 pm to 300 pm and is located within the spacing distance.
   USE - DAFC in microelectronic device.
   ADVANTAGE - The loss of liquid fuel through the venting hole is prevented while at the same time allowing carbon dioxide to leave the anode section by preventing access of the liquid fuel to the section having the venting hole. A single venting hole is sufficient for allowing removal of carbon dioxide from the anode section, and a single venting hole prevents a flow into the anode section due to the higher pressure in the anode section compared to the pressure in the ambient environment. The smaller the cross-sectional area of the ventilation holes, the larger the flow velocity of carbon dioxide exiting the anode section and thus the smaller the risk that unwanted particles enter the anode section. The pervaporation membrane functions together with the venting hole, and the balance between removal of carbon dioxide and prevention of loss of fuel is improved when a venting hole is employed in combination with the pervaporation membrane. The oleophobic filter minimizes penetration of liquids, e.g. sweat, into the cathode section so that when the DAFC comprises both an oleophobic filter and an anion-exchange membrane, the amount of chloride ions, e.g. from sweat, reaching the anion-exchange membrane is minimized, and thus the life time of the anion-exchange membrane is extended compared to a DAFC not having the oleophobic filter. The PEM cup functions as a gasket separating the anode section from the cathode section, and as an electrical insulator and provides mechanical support to ensure optimal assembly. The pollution of the cathode section is minimized by employing a cathode terminal at another surface than the surface having the cathode collection element.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of operating a DAFC.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded view of fuel cell components of a DAFC.
   DAFC (1)
   Housing (10)
   Cathode collection element (31)
   Anode collection element (41)
   PEM (44)

PN CN110862869-A; WO2021093459-A1
TI Preparing Artemisia argyi essential oil and its by-products by using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and secondary molecular distillation process comprises e.g. exposing fresh Artemisia argyi for rapid dehydration and drying.
AU LIN Z
   HUANG J
   LI J
   HUANG L
   XING S
   HAN H
AE GUANGDONG GEECHING BIOLOGICAL MEDICINE (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGDONG GEECHING BIOMEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020214075
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Artemisia argyi essential oil and its by-products by using supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction and secondary molecular distillation process comprises exposing fresh Artemisia argyi raw materials for rapid dehydration, and drying in shade, defoliating, and removing impurities, and crushing to obtain Artemisia argyi powder, placing the Artemisia argyi powder in supercritical CO2 extraction device for extraction, and subjecting to high-pressure separation through separation kettle I and low-pressure separation through a separation kettle II, respectively to obtain Artemisia argyi crude oil extract I and Artemisia argyi crude oil extract II separately, heating Artemisia argyi crude oil extract II to dissolve in water bath, and allowing to stand at room temperature for oil-water separation, then taking out after the solidification of upper-layer crude oil extract, and heating to dissolve in a water bath.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing Artemisia argyi essential oil and its by-products by using supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction and secondary molecular distillation process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can protects the functional components in the essential oil of Artemisia argyi; obtains Artemisia argyi essential oil i.e. pure natural, no pollution, has pure and strong smell and obtains the Artemisia argyi heavy oil and Artemisia argyi wax by-products, which can be used as cleaning raw materials for skin care daily chemical products, and increases the utilization of Artemisia argyi leaves.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Artemisia argyi essential oil and its by-products by using supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction and secondary molecular distillation process comprises (i) exposing fresh Artemisia argyi raw materials for rapid dehydration, and drying in the shade, defoliating, and removing impurities, and crushing to obtain Artemisia argyi powder; (ii) placing the Artemisia argyi powder in a supercritical CO2 extraction device for extraction, and subjecting to high-pressure separation through a separation kettle I and low-pressure separation through a separation kettle II, respectively to obtain Artemisia argyi crude oil extract I and Artemisia argyi crude oil extract II separately; (iii) heating the Artemisia argyi crude oil extract II to dissolve in a water bath, and allowing to stand at room temperature for oil-water separation, then taking out after the solidification of upper-layer crude oil extract, then heating to dissolve in a water bath, after separating by the first-stage molecular distillation, obtaining the semi-finished product of Artemisia argyi essential oil and crude oil extract heavy phase; (iv) filtering the semi-finished product of Artemisia argyi essential oil to obtain the finished product of Artemisia argyi essential oil; and (v) mixing the Artemisia argyi crude oil extract I and the crude oil extract heavy phase, dissolving by heating in a water bath and carrying out the second-stage molecular distillation separation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the leaf raw material comprises more than one of Chinese mugwort, Nanyang Artemisia, Tangyin North Artemisia and Artemisia verlotorum and the water content of the folium Artemisia argyi powder is 8-15 wt.% the particle size is 10-40 meshes. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the extraction temperature of the extraction is 45-65 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 18-30 MPa, and extraction time is 2-4 hours. The separating pressure of the high pressure separation is 10-15 MPa, and the separating temperature is 35-50 degrees C. The separating pressure of the low pressure separation is 4-7 MPa, and the separating temperature is 30-50 degrees C. In the step (iii), the water bath heating and dissolving is 80 degrees C. The distillation temperature of the first level molecular distillation is 90-120 degrees C, the vacuum degree is 15-200 Pa, the condensation temperature is 40-70 degrees C, and the cold trap temperature is -18 to 5 degrees C, the folium Artemisia argyi essential oil product collected at the cold trap. The filter medium is 0.45 mu m-aperture filtration membrane. In the step (v), the water bath heating and dissolving temperature is 85 degrees C. The second-stage molecule distillation of distillation temperature is 120-160 degrees C, the vacuum degree is 5-50Pa, condensing temperature is 20-40 degrees C, and the cold trap temperature is -18 to 5 degrees C.

TI Method for cultivating Pleurotus citrinopileatus,            involves stirring Flammulina velutipes, neem crumb,            wheat bran, maize cob and gypsum, followed by bagging,            steam-sterilizing, setting vaccination hole and placing            in inoculation chamber.
AU CHENG Y
AE GUIZHOU YITAO AGRIC DEV CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 201933027U
AB    NOVELTY - A Pleurotus citrinopileatus cultivating method                involves utilizing Flammulina velutipes, neem                crumb, wheat bran, maize cob and gypsum as                ingredients, stirring the mixture evenly to prepare                a culture material. The culture material is bagged,                steam-sterilizing the mushroom bag and setting                vaccination hole on the bag. The oyster mushroom                bag is placed in the inoculation chamber for                inoculation, followed by inserting the strain into                the inoculation hole, placing the inoculated oyster                mushroom bag in a greenhouse, providing the shed                with a shelf, providing the placing frame with a                placing plate for driving the plate to move on the                plate, controlling the lifter, providing the                placement plate with a carbon dioxide sensor,                electrically connecting the carbon dioxide sensor                with the microprocessor, placing the spinach under                the rack, ventilating, observing the growth                condition of hyphae on the mushroom bag of yew                mushroom, watering and culturing in the shed to                take out the mushrooms.
   USE - Method for cultivating Pleurotus                citrinopileatus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cultivating the Pleurotus                citrinopileatus with effectively solving the                problem that the medium dependent problem of wooden                mushroom and pleurotus death phenomenon occurs                easily.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A Pleurotus citrinopileatus cultivating method                involves utilizing 70-85 pts. wt. Flammulina                velutipes, 1-3 pts. wt. neem crumb, 5-8 pts. wt.                wheat bran, 10-15 pts. wt. maize cob and 1-2 pts.                wt. gypsum as ingredients, stirring the mixture                evenly to prepare a culture material with water                content of 60-70%. The culture material is bagged                by manually or mechanically to obtain a yellow                mushroom mushroom bag with a length of 20-25 cm and                a width of 5-10 cm, steam-sterilizing the mushroom                bag and setting 3-5 pieces vaccination hole on the                bag. The oyster mushroom bag is placed in the                inoculation chamber for inoculation, followed by                inserting the strain having a diameter of 0.9-1.2                cm into the inoculation hole, placing the                inoculated oyster mushroom bag in a greenhouse in                which spinach is grown, providing the shed with a                shelf for the oyster mushroom bag, providing the                placing frame with a placing plate for driving the                plate to move on the plate, controlling the lifter                by a microprocessor, providing the placement plate                with a carbon dioxide sensor, electrically                connecting the carbon dioxide sensor with the                microprocessor, placing the spinach under the rack,                keeping the spinach 18-25cm away from the mushroom                bag, ventilating every 3-4 days in the greenhouse                for 1-2 hours, observing the growth condition of                hyphae on the mushroom bag of yew mushroom, timely                replenishing water, and culturing in the shed for                20-40 days to take out the mushrooms.

PN IN201711019890-A
TI Nanoformulation used as e.g. dietary supplement, comprises lipophilic agro waste extract which is derived from kinnow peels, organic phase having fatty acid and aqueous dispersion phase having surfactant, co-surfactant and water.
AU SINGH M
   BAWA M
   PANNU K S
   SOOD A
   MANJU
AE PUNJAB AGRO JUICES LTD (PUNJ-Non-standard)
   UNIV PUNJAB (UYPU-Non-standard)
GA 201929090X
AB    NOVELTY - Nanoformulation of lipophilic agro waste extract with enhanced bioavailability comprises; 2-20 wt.% lipophilic agro waste extract, where the lipophilic agro waste extract is derived from kinnow peels by supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extraction process; an organic phase having 0.5-30 wt.% fatty acid or fatty acid derivatives and optionally comprising 0-5 wt.% edible oil; and 2-3 wt.% an aqueous dispersion phase having surfactant, 40-60 wt.% co-surfactant, and remaining as water, where the nanoformulation has hepatoprotective and antioxidative properties.
   USE - The nanoformulation is useful as a dietary supplement, nutraceutical supplement, health supplement, hepatoprotective property and anti-oxidants for treating and/or preventing liver diseases (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanoformulation: provides an efficient way of utilization of agro-waste, preferably peels of citrus fruits; exhibits higher stability, increased bioavailability and sustained release properties; is stable; and is capable of delivering significantly higher amount of active molecules that is limonoids to the human and/or animal system, thus rendering enhanced therapeutic efficacy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing a nanoformulation comprising lipophilic agro waste kinnow peels extract, comprising (a) subjecting the kinnow peels to supercritical fluid carbon dioxide to extract a solution rich in limonoid concentration, (b) subjecting the kinnow peels to supercritical fluid carbon dioxide to extract another solution rich in carotenoids and flavonoids concentration, (c) mixing the supercritical fluid carbon dioxide extracted kinnow peel extracts obtained in step (a) and step (b) to yield a solution having the required amounts of limonoids, carotenoids and flavonoids concentration, where the amount of kinnow peels extract is 0.5-30 wt.% based on the final formulation, (d) stirring the solution obtained in step (c) at 40 degrees C to ensure uniform mixing and optionally adding 0.5 wt.% edible oil, based on the final formulation, (e) preheating 0.5-30 wt.% fatty acid or fatty acid derivatives to its melting point and adding the required quantity of solution obtained in step (d) to it, (f) stirring and heating the mixture obtained in step (e) at 75 degrees C on magnetic stirrer to form a solubilized and uniform organic phase solution, (g) adding an aqueous phase comprising 2-3 wt.% surfactant, 40-60 wt.% cosurfactant and the balance of water dropwise to the solution obtained in step (f) under stirring conditions at 75 degrees C, (h) subjecting the mixture of step (b) to ultrasonic homogenization to obtain complete solubilization of the organic phase in the aqueous phase, where the ultrasonic homogenization is carried out with 6 mm probe at 25-35% amplitude, pulse 25 seconds on/5 seconds off for 20 minutes at 75 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fatty acid or fatty acid derivatives comprise stearic acid, stearic acid methyl ester and stearic acid (stearic acid ethyl ester) ethyl ester (stearic acid methyl ester), dodecanoic acid, myristic acid and/or palmitic acid. The edible oil comprises olive oil, canola oil and/or fish oil. The surfactants/cosurfactants comprise Tween (RTM: Polysorbate), polysorbates and/or glycerol. The kinnow peels extract comprises limonoids, flavonoids or carotenoids. The size of nanoparticle is 160-250 nm for increased aqueous dispersion and enhanced efficacy for bio-availability.
   ACTIVITY - Anabolic; Hepatotropic. The hepatotropic activity of nanoformulation (comprised of 16.67% kinnow peel extract, 43.33 % glycerol, 0.56% stearic acid, 2.22% Tween-20 (RTM: Polysorbate 20), 5% olive oil and 8.5% of water) was tested in patients with non-alcoholic fatty liver using in vivo assay by measuring aspartate transaminase (AST) and alanine transaminase (ALT) levels. The result showed that the patient treated with 85.4 wt.% nanoformulation exhibited AST levels of 41 and ALT levels of 35 (units not defined) after 2 months as compared to baseline AST levels of 75 and ALT levels 39 (units not defined).
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of nanoformulation is oral (claimed). No dosage details given.

AU KANG H J
   PARK M S
   KIM B H

GA 202040657Y
AB    NOVELTY - Fabricating polysilazane using carbon dioxide as solvent and polysilazane involves injecting silane compounds and ammonia into two independent reactors, preparing a silane compound reactant and an ammonia reactant by pressing carbon dioxide into each reactor in which the silane compound and ammonia are injected. The reactants are mixed by moving the silane compound reactants and ammonia reactants to a mixing reactor. The reaction is maintained by pressurizing by further injecting carbon dioxide into the mixing reactor. The reaction temperature of the reactor is raised to mature the reactants. The polysilazane is recovered by discharging the reactants from the mixing reactor after the aging. The pressure of each reactor after carbon dioxide injection is 40-80 bar. The pressure of the reactor after the injection of carbon dioxide is 90-120bar.
   USE - Method for fabricating polysilazane using carbon dioxide as solvent and polysilazane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to fabricate polysilazane using carbon dioxide as solvent and polysilazane that controls the solubility of reactants and products.

AE HUANENG GROUP TECHNOLOGY INNOVATION CENT (CHHN-C)

GA 202253092V
AB    NOVELTY - The system comprises coal in-situ pyrolysis                module, product separating and processing module,                direct air capture carbon dioxide module (22) and                waste heat recycling module. By injecting high                temperature and high pressure heat carrier and mild                oxidation heat supply, in-situ pyrolysis production                of underground coal bed is made. The pyrolysis                product is sent to product separating and                processing module. The high temperature steam is                fed into direct air capture carbon dioxide module.                The product separating and processing module is                used for separating and processing pyrolysis                product. The pyrolysis gas in the pyrolysis product                is used for generating electricity supplied to coal                in-situ pyrolysis system and direct air capture                carbon dioxide module. The waste heat recycling                module recycles heat supplied by residual heat of                underground coal layer after pyrolysis to supply to                direct air capture carbon dioxide module.
   USE - Used as system for in-situ coal pyrolysis and                direct air capture of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system utilizes coal resource in green and                efficient manner through underground in-situ                pyrolysis of coal, directly captures carbon dioxide                from air, and supplies energy required for                adsorption and desorption through pyrolysis gas                power generation and waste heat recovery. The                system can continuously capture carbon dioxide in                atmospheric environment, and reduces collecting                cost.

The coal in-situ                pyrolysis module is used for fracturing underground                coal bed. By injecting high temperature and high                pressure heat carrier and mild oxidation heat                supply, in-situ pyrolysis production of underground                coal bed is made. The pyrolysis product is sent to                product separating and processing module. The high                temperature steam is fed into direct air capture                carbon dioxide module. The product separating and                processing module is used for separating and                processing pyrolysis product. The pyrolysis gas in                the pyrolysis product is used for generating                electricity supplied to coal in-situ pyrolysis                system and direct air capture carbon dioxide                module. The direct air capture carbon dioxide                module collects carbon dioxide from the air, and                utilizes high temperature steam generated by coal                in-situ pyrolysis module or high temperature steam                recycled from waste heat of underground coal layer                to desorb and collect carbon dioxide. The waste                heat recycling module recycles heat supplied by                residual heat of underground coal layer after                pyrolysis to supply to direct air capture carbon                dioxide module.

PN CN110743193-A; CN110743193-B
TI Supercritical extraction process imitating ultrasonic high-speed separation comprises slicing extract, extracting with carbon dioxide, recovering gaseous carbon dioxide, storing and completely releasing carbon dioxide.
AU SHEN L
AE NANTONG KEXIN SUPERCRITICAL EQUIP CO LTD (NANT-Non-standard)
GA 202011773X
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical extraction process imitating ultrasonic high-speed separation comprises (a) slicing extract to be extracted, washing and drying, (b) passing small volume of slice and carbon dioxide into extraction tank, and adjusting temperature and pressure of extraction tank to make carbon dioxide appear in supercritical fluid state, diffusing and dissolving in small volume of slice, (c) extracting for a period of time, passing binary intermediate into dimorphic separation tank, reducing pressure to atmospheric pressure and increasing temperature, so that carbon dioxide returns to gaseous state, (d) during recovering gaseous carbon dioxide, passing micro-ultrasonic capsule at bottom continuously, so that dimorphic intermediate vibrates to accelerate release of carbon dioxide, conveying into recovery tank, and storing through internal expansion thermometry tube, and (e) completely releasing carbon dioxide, the remaining substance in dimorphic separation tank is target extract.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical extraction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method accelerates the flow rate of gaseous carbon dioxide by setting the variable volume internal expansion thermometry tube (convenient for reuse), improves the efficiency of medicinal extraction, avoids the possibility of explosion in the dimorphic separation tank due to untimely discharge of gas and improves safety.

PT P
PN CN111825085-A
TI Preparing graphene by using carbon dioxide high-entropy solution adjusted by ionic liquid by inducing cavitation field to exfoliate comprises e.g. reacting graphite powder and ionic liquid, ultrasonic treating, and drying.
AU YIN J
   XIANG H
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2020A7566L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene by using carbon dioxide high-entropy solution adjusted by ionic liquid by inducing cavitation field to exfoliate comprises e.g. (i) adding certain amount of graphite powder and ionic liquid into a reaction kettle, adjusting and controlling the surface energy of the ionic liquid-carbon dioxide mixed solution using the ionic liquid-carbon dioxide mixed solution in the reactor as stripping agent, and the solubility parameter value is 30-50 MPa 1/2, (ii) turning on the stirring system of the reactor, and making the stripping agent and graphite powder to fully mix evenly at 1000-3000 revolutions/minute, (iii) stabilizing the system pressure and temperature, and adjusting the flow rate to perform continuous circulation operation, (iv) closing the pump and valve after the operation, and collecting the graphene solution after releasing the pressure of the reactor, and (v) ultrasonic treating, and drying in an oven to obtain graphene product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing graphene by using carbon dioxide high-entropy solution adjusted by ionic liquid by inducing cavitation field to exfoliate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes ionic liquid, which is environmentally friendly; and solves problem of the van der Waals force between graphene layers to make it peel off.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing graphene by using carbon dioxide high-entropy solution adjusted by ionic liquid by inducing cavitation field to exfoliate comprises (i) adding certain amount of graphite powder and ionic liquid into a reaction kettle, where the mass ratio between ionic liquid and graphite powder is 1:10-1:20, sealing the reactor, introducing carbon dioxide into the reactor at a pressure of 10-30 MPa, heating at 40-80 degrees C, adjusting and controlling the surface energy of the ionic liquid-carbon dioxide mixed solution at 20-40 mJ/m2 using the ionic liquid-carbon dioxide mixed solution in the reactor as stripping agent, and the solubility parameter value is 30-50 MPa 1/2, (ii) turning on the stirring system of the reactor, and making the stripping agent and graphite powder to fully mix evenly at 1000-3000 revolutions/minute, (iii) stabilizing the system pressure and temperature, turning on the plunger pump of the circulation bypass, locating cavitation generator, and adjusting the flow rate to perform continuous circulation operation at a flow rate of 0.1-1 m/s for 3-12 hours, (iv) closing the pump and valve after the operation, and collecting the graphene solution after releasing the pressure of the reactor, and (v) ultrasonic treating the graphene solution, then using an organic filter membrane for suction filtration, and drying in an oven to obtain graphene product. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system for preparing graphene by using carbon dioxide high-entropy solution adjusted by ionic liquid by inducing cavitation field to exfoliate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the ionic liquid is imidazole ionic liquid, and the graphite powder is natural flake graphite having particle size of 100-200 meshes. Preferred Method: In step (i), the graphite powder is pickled with hydrochloric acid before use to remove impurities. The ionic liquid is 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium bis(trifluoromethylsulfonyl)imide or 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium acetate. In step (ii), the cavitation field is high-speed flow of the mixed liquid in the reactor through a special cavitation generator to generate the cavitation field and cooperate with the stripping agent to peel the graphene. The velocity of the fluid flowing through the cavitation generator is 0.1-1 m/s. In step (iii), the ultrasonic treatment time of the graphene solution is 30-60 minutes. The surface energy of the ionic liquid-carbon dioxide mixed solution matches that of graphene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The reactor is a high-pressure reactor, made of stainless steel, and has a discharge pipe and a feed pipe on the top. The amount of mixture is 10-20% of the total volume of the reactor. The shell of the cavitation generator has a cylindrical structure having an inner diameter of 3-10 mm; a dovetail groove is opened inside; and the groove depth is 1-10 mm.

PN CN110367541-A
TI Preparing a sesame oil soft capsule comprises e.g. placing qualified sesame seeds into automatic washing machine, rinsing, scattering, preheating, frying, sprinkling water, cooling, introducing carbon dioxide gas, sterilizing and sealing.
AU REN C
   MENG W
AE SHANDONG SHILIXIANG SESAME PROD CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019922312
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a sesame oil soft capsule comprises (i) placing the qualified sesame seeds into the automatic washing machine, rinsing, feeding into the water filter to moisten and drying, ensuring that the sesame seeds are slightly dried and scattering, (ii) preheating the wok to a temperature of 50 degrees C, adding the raw materials of the step (i), frying for 10-20 minutes, heating to 62 degrees C, stir frying for 5 minutes, sprinkling water, cooling to 40 degrees C, stir frying for 20 minutes, and drying, (iii) placing the sesame seed treated in a material basket, and placing the material frame in the extractor, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the pump chamber to pressurize, and pressure in the pump chamber after reaching 3-5 Mpa, and the pressurized carbon dioxide is condensed into the heat exchanger, turning the carbon dioxide into a liquid state and is introduced into the bottom of the extractor, and the sesame seed is extracted.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a sesame oil soft capsule.
   ADVANTAGE - The capsule has good film-forming property, high chemical stability, improves drug-loading rate and embedding rate, and is convenient to carry and quantitatively take.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a sesame oil soft capsule comprises (i) placing the qualified sesame seeds into the automatic washing machine, rinsing, feeding into the water filter to moisten and drying, ensuring that the sesame seeds are slightly dried and scattering, (ii) preheating the wok to a temperature of 50 degrees C, adding the raw materials of the step (i), frying for 10-20 minutes, heating to 62 degrees C, stir frying for 5 minutes, sprinkling water, cooling to 40 degrees C, stir frying for 20 minutes, and drying, (iii) placing the sesame seed treated in a material basket, and placing the material frame in the extractor, introducing carbon dioxide gas into the pump chamber to pressurize, and pressure in the pump chamber after reaching 3-5 Mpa, and the pressurized carbon dioxide is condensed into the heat exchanger, turning the carbon dioxide into a liquid state and is introduced into the bottom of the extractor, and the sesame seed is extracted, and the carbon dioxide liquid carrying the sesame oil is sent to the separator for separation, introducing hot water of 60-80 degrees C into one side of the separator casing to heat the separator, and a hot water outlet is provided on the other side of the separator casing, where the sesame oil flows out from the bottom of the separator, and the carbon dioxide gas is introduced into the condenser from the upper part of the separator, so that the carbon dioxide becomes liquid and then returns to the pump chamber, and the extraction time is 3-5 hours, and (iv) filling the sesame oil obtained in the step (iii) into the glutinous rice capsule, sterilizing and sealing the bottle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The slurry mass content of glutinous rice is 7.5%. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), frying is performed for 15 minutes. In the step (iii), the pressure in the pump cavity is 4 Mpa. The step (iii) is provided with hot water of 70 degrees C on one side of the separator casing. In the step (iii), the extracting time is 4 hours. In the step (iv), the glutinous rice capsule is prepared by pulverizing the clean glutinous rice to obtain glutinous rice flour of 80-100 mu m, and adding a water homogenate to obtain a glutinous rice slurry, where the glutinous rice flour in the slurry has a mass content of 5-9.5%, and subjecting to high pressure hot water melting treatment under the conditions of pressure 0.8-3 MPa, at temperature of 95-120 degrees C, rotation speed of 110-300 revolutions/minute for time 50-70 minutes, cooling the viscous slurry obtained in the step (i) to 60-80 degrees C, and extruding the atmospheric pressure screw and kept under reflux, and feeding the extruded material continuously into the inlet of the extruder for recycling, and treating for 30-40 minutes, and the extrusion reflow process is as follows: the single and double mixing screws are extruded and recirculated, and the material flows through a short-range single-screw extruder with a screw length of 30 cm and a diameter of 66 mm; then directly enters the long-axis twin-screw, the length of the screw is 140 cm, the diameter of the screw is 80 mm, and the cycle is carried out until the treatment time is reached, and the material obtained in the step B is adjusted to a pH of 6-7, and water and soy protein powder, adjusting the slurry mass concentration to 1-3%, soy protein powder concentration 0.1-0.2%, stirring evenly, maintaining the atmospheric pressure screw extrusion at a temperature of 40-50 degrees C, and feeding the extruded material continuously into the inlet of the extruder for recycling, and the treating for 10-30 minutes, drying at less than 60 degrees C to prepare a powdery capsule shell and then a capsule shell is prepared by a plastic molding machine. In the step (ii), cooling is to 70 degrees C. In the step (iii), adjusting pH value to 6.5 and adjusting slurry mass concentration to 2%.

PN KR2020001911-U; KR492664-Y1
TI Gas injection control device for endoscopy, comprises an apparatus body that is provided with discharge port, through which carbon dioxide is injected into human body, and flow control valve that controls injection amount of carbon dioxide.
AU LEE S
AE LEE S (LEES-Individual)
GA 202088746S
AB    NOVELTY - The gas injection control device comprises an apparatus body that is provided with a discharge port, through which carbon dioxide is injected into the human body. The flow rate of carbon dioxide introduced through the input port is adjusted and transferred to the discharge port to enter the human body. A flow control valve is used for controlling the injection amount of the injected carbon dioxide. A sensor unit is provided on the connection passage and includes a flow sensor configured to detect a flow rate of carbon dioxide flowing through the connection passage or a flow rate sensor to detect a flow rate of carbon dioxide flowing through the connection passage. A timer is used for counting a time, during which the carbon dioxide is discharged to the endoscope and the carbon dioxide is injected into the human body.
   USE - Gas injection control device for endoscopy.
   ADVANTAGE - Te gas injection control device for endoscopy calculates the total amount of gas injected into the human body and displays it on the display to allow the examiner and the patient to monitor the amount of carbon dioxide injected to ensure the safety of the examination, thus providing mental stability to the patient. Easily checks the amount of carbon dioxide injection and the injection time through the touch screen provided in the device body.

GA 202033633Y
AB    NOVELTY - The structure has a blast hole whose internal section is filled with a liquid carbon dioxide section (2) and an explosive section (4). The liquid carbon dioxide section is a carbon dioxide packaging tube with liquid carbon dioxide encapsulated with the liquid carbon dioxide section. The explosive section is a medicine package that is detonated and connected by a detonating component. A physical energy generated by the explosion of the explosive section destroyed a carbon dioxide encapsulated tube. A thermal energy generated by the explosion of the explosive section excited the liquid carbon dioxide to undergo a phase change inside the blast hole. The carbon dioxide gas and the explosive gas generated by the explosion of the explosive section are blasted together inside the blast hole that is sealed with the drill cuttings (5).
   USE - Mixed charge structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of the structure is simple, blasting vibration is low, cost is low, energy utilization rate is high, blasting block size is uniform and the damage to the side wall is little.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a charging method of mixed charging structure; and
   (2) a blasting method for controlling output rate of side blasting powder ore.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the mixed charge structure.
   Step slope (1)
   Liquid carbon dioxide section (2)
   Detonator (3)
   Explosive section (4)
   Drill cutting (5)

PN CN114106210-A
TI Preparing 23-valent pneumococcal polysaccharide,            comprises e.g. cultivating and fermenting pneumococcus,            sterilizing, centrifuging fermentation harvest liquid,            fermenting supernatant CTAB precipitates acidic            polysaccharides and precipitating CTAB, performing            chromatography and ultrafiltering.
AU DU L
   ZHU W
   LIU G
   LIN Y
   WANG R
   SU X
AE ANHUI ZHIFEI LONGKEMA BIOPHARMACEUTICAL (ANHU-Non-standard)
   CHONGQING ZHIFEI BIOLOGICAL PROD CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
   BEIJING ZHIFEI GREEN BAMBOO BIO-PHARM CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202237819J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 23-valent pneumococcal                polysaccharide, comprises (1) cultivating and                fermenting pneumococcus, (2) sterilizing,                precipitating and centrifuging the fermentation                harvest liquid, (3) fermenting supernatant CTAB                precipitates acidic polysaccharides or impurities,                (4) dissociating and precipitating CTAB, (5)                performing chromatography further removes nucleic                acids and proteins, and (6) ultrafiltering the                sample and freeze-drying to make pneumococcal                polysaccharide; where in step (1) the culture and                fermentation process of pneumococcus, the working                strain is started with Columbia blood agar medium,                and the culture conditions are 37plus minus1degrees                Celsius, 10% carbon dioxide; resuscitating,                expanding the culture in liquid medium, and the                culture conditions are 37plus minus1degrees                Celsius, 4-6% carbon dioxide; Bacterial                fermentation is carried out in tryptic soybean                liquid medium, culture conditions are 35-38degrees                Celsius, pH is 6.6-7.5, and the concentration of                glucose feed is 5-25 mmol/l.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 23-valent                pneumococcal polysaccharide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation, is safe,                efficient, and has excellent 23-valent pneumococcal                polysaccharide vaccine immunogenicity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method comprises                  adding 40 mg/l magnesium (II) and 0.1-0.3 ml of                  defoamer to the tryptic soybean liquid medium. In                  step (2) in the sterilization, precipitation and                  centrifugation of the fermentation harvest                  liquid, sterilizing with sodium deoxycholate, the                  final concentration of sodium deoxycholate is                  0.06-0.24%, and inactivating for more than 4                  hours, adjusting the pH value to 4-6 with acetic                  acid solution, and allowing to stand for more                  than 4 hours to precipitate impurities,                  centrifuging, adjusting the pH of the supernatant                  to neutrality, clarifying and filtering with a                  0.65 um glass fiber filter, concentrating with a                  100KD membrane bag, and performing                  ultrafiltration with not less than 5 times the                  volume of buffer solution. In step (3), adjusting                  the pH value of the feed solution to 7.2-7.6 with                  sodium carbonate solution, adding                  cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB) solution,                  until the final concentration of CTAB is 1-3%,                  mixing well and settle overnight, and                  centrifuging to collect the target components. In                  step (4), adding 0.2-0.5 mol/l sodium chloride                  solution to the precipitate to dissociate the                  polysaccharide and CTAB for more than 12 hours,                  and centrifuging to collect the supernatant to                  the obtained supernatant or the supernatant                  collected), adding sodium iodide to a final                  concentration of 0.2-1.5%, mixing well, allowing                  to stand for more than 1 hour, and centrifuging,                  clarifying the supernatant and filtering by a                  0.65 um glass fiber filter element, concentrating                  with a 100KD membrane package and performing                  ultrafiltration with not less than 5 times the                  volume of buffer solution. In step (5),                  chromatography further removs nucleic acid and                  protein, 23 types of pneumococcal polysaccharides                  all use type II hydroxyapatite as the                  chromatography medium, 0.002-0.01mol/L phosphate                  buffer is used as the equilibrium solution,                  during the purification process, the absorption                  value at 206 nm is monitored, and the main flow                  peak is collected as the polysaccharide                  component. After chromatography, the column is                  regenerated with 0.5-1.0 mol/L phosphate buffered                  saline. In step (6) dialyzing the sample with a                  100KD membrane bag and cooling water for                  injection, and the dialysis volume should not                  less than 5 times the volume of the concentrated                  feed solution, and the conductance of the                  permeate should be less than 10 micros/cm during                  online detection, after the polysaccharide                  solution is clarified and filtered,                  freeze-drying, and cooling to less than 40degrees                  Celsius for not less than 4 hours, after the                  product is completely frozen, sublimating the                  water in a vacuum state, and the sublimation                  process should 50 hours, after the ice layer of                  the product to be prepared completely disappears,                  it enters the desorption drying stage, and the                  heat preservation process should not less than 4                  hours; the temperature of the product in the                  freeze-drying process not greater than 30degrees                  Celsius; after freeze-drying, it should be white                  loose solid, and purifying pneumococcal                  polysaccharide. The 23-valent pneumococcal                  polysaccharide is further prepared into a vaccine                  with a specification of 0.5 ml/cup, 25                  microg/dose of each of 23 serotypes, and a                  diluent of 0.01mol/l phosphate buffered saline                  solution at pH of 6-7.5.Preferred Components: The pneumococcal                  capsular polysaccharide serotype is 1, 2, 3, 4,                  5, 6B, 7F, 8, 9N, 9V, 10A, 11A, 12F, 14, 15B,                  17F, 18C, 19A, 19F, 20, Types 22F, 23F and/or                  33F.
DC A96 (Medical, dental, veterinary, cosmetic.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

   ZHANG H

   LIU J
   KONG L
   LI M
   HAN J
   WU M
   XING T
   WANG Y
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
   SHANDONG ENERGY GROUP CO LTD (SNJT-C)
GA 2022231888
AB    NOVELTY - Bifunctional catalyst is claimed. The catalyst is prepared by physical blending of carbonized-oxidized metal organic framework precursor and molecular sieve; the carbonized-oxidized metal organic framework precursor is transition metal-based catalyst obtained by carbonizing and deriving oxygen-enriched vacancies of metal organic framework material.
   USE - The bifunctional catalyst is useful as reaction catalyst for preparing p-xylene by hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bifunctional catalyst: can improve the selectivity of p-xylene in the product by controlling the components of the catalyst and has low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing bifunctional catalyst, comprising (i) carbonizing metal-organic framework material in inert gas atmosphere, and then calcining in air to obtain carbonized-oxidized metal organic framework precursor, and (ii) physically blending the obtained carbonized-oxidized metal organic framework precursor and molecular sieve to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molecular sieve is zeolite socony mobil-5 (ZSM-5) i.e. aluminosilicate zeolite molecular sieve. The transition metal is iron, chromium and/or zirconium. The mass ratio between carbonized-oxidized metal organic framework precursor and molecular sieve is 1:18-18:1.Preferred Method: The carbonization is carried out at 400-600degrees Celsius for 1-4 hours and the calcination is carried out at 300-600degrees Celsius for 1-4 hours. The metal organic framework material is prepared by mixing compound containing iron, chromium or zirconium, phthalic acid and nitric acid and performing hydrothermal reaction to obtain product. The molecular sieve is prepared by either mixing aluminium nitrate nonahydrate, tetrapropyl ammonium hydroxide, ethanol and ethyl orthosilicate in distilled water and performing hydrothermal reaction, and then calcining to obtain molecular sieve or mixing sodium metaaluminate, n-butylamine, tetrapropylammonium bromide and silica sol in distilled water and performing hydrothermal reaction, and then calcining to obtain molecular sieve.

GA 2022107925
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of copper nanoparticle catalyst                involves (S1) preparing a gold seed colloidal                solution, adding sodium borohydride to the aqueous                solution of chloroauric acid and stirring rapidly,                (S2) adding copper acetate and polyvinyl                pyrrolidone in a mass ratio of 5-140 mg:0.1-0.4 g                into the colloidal solution containing 10 muL to 5                mL gold seeds, dispersing uniformly, quickly adding                ascorbic acid solution after the solution is                transparent, and (S3) centrifuging the reaction                product of (S2) and drying under the protection of                an inert gas.

PN KR2289446-B1
TI Producing saw palmetto extract e.g. in food composition comprises e.g. performing supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction of saw palmetto fruit, extracting by alcohol and mixing saw palmetto extract including lauric acid and beta-sitosterol.
AU EUN S W
AE DAEHAN CHEMTECH CO LTD (DAEH-Non-standard)
GA 202194495F
AB    NOVELTY - Producing saw palmetto extract comprises (i) performing supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction of saw palmetto fruit to obtain an extract containing lauric acid; (ii) extracting the saw palmetto fruit with alcohol to obtain an extract containing beta -sitosterol; and (iii) mixing the extract obtained in step (i) with the extract obtained in step (ii), and filtering to obtain saw palmetto extract containing 22-36 wt.% lauric acid and 2-3.5 wt.% beta -sitosterol based on the total weight of the saw palmetto extract.
   USE - The saw palmetto extract is useful in food composition for treating or preventing male menopause symptoms of decrease in number of sperm in the body, decrease in number of active sperm and decrease in muscle endurance (claimed), and for metabolic syndrome, and in medicine and quasi-drug composition.
   ADVANTAGE - The saw palmetto extract produced has safe natural substance, thus it has no side effects; and regulates nutrients or physiological actions based on the structure and function of the human body.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the supercritical carbon-dioxide extraction is carried out for 18-20 hours at 50-60 degrees C. In the step (ii), the alcohol extraction of saw palmetto fruit is performed at 70-80 degrees C for 5-6 hours, and then concentrating for 8-10 hours at 70-90 degrees C. In the step (iii), mixing is performed in a reactor at 50-60 degrees C for 1 hour.
   ADMINISTRATION - Administration of the composition, by oral or parenteral, preferably intravenous, intramuscular, intraarterial, intramedullary, intrathecal, intracardiac, transdermal, subcutaneous, intraperitoneal, intranasal, enteral, topical, sublingual or rectal route, in the form of powder, granules, tablets, pills, dragees, capsules, solutions, gels, syrups, slurries, suspensions, injections, creams, lotions, external ointments, oils, moisturizers, gels, aerosols and nasal inhalants. No dosage details given.
   EXAMPLE - The supercritical fluid carbon-dioxide was injected into 10000 kg of saw palmetto fruit (20-40 mg/g of lauric acid to the weight of the fruit) and heated at 50-60 degrees C for 18-20 hours. As a result, an extract (oil) of 950-1000 kg was obtained in which the content of lauric acid was standardized to 220-360 mg/g. Extract containing standardized beta -sitosterol was prepared with 5000 kg saw palmetto fruit (4-6 mg/g of beta -sitosterol by weight relative to the weight of fruit) with an alcohol purity of 99.33% of 10000 l tablet (fermented ethanol) was injected and heated at 70-80 degrees C for 5-6 hours. Then, it was concentrated for 8-10 hours at 70-90 degrees C, as a result, the content of beta -sitosterol to obtain a 50 kg extract standardized to 400-600 mg/g. The above extracts were mixed and filtered.

AU LUN Z

   YANG Y

   GAO Z
   SUN L
   TAO J
   WANG H
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC EXPLORATION & PRODN RES INST (SNPC-C)
GA 202178895C
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves dividing a set of well groups according to a well network structure. A gas injection well of each well group is determined. TI Foaming composition used for cleaning toilet bowl,            comprises foaming agent comprising acid, and base which            reacts with acid in presence of water to produce carbon            dioxide, and anionic surfactant.
AU DANOS C
AE CLEANEOR (CLEA-Non-standard)
   CLEANEOR (CLEA-Non-standard)
GA 202171783U
AB    NOVELTY - A foaming composition comprises a foaming                agent comprising at least one acid and at least one                base, and an anionic surfactant. The base reacts                with the acid in the presence of water to produce                carbon dioxide. The composition is in the form of a                loose powder comprising more than or equal to 3                %mass to less than or equal to 4 %mass, preferably                less than 3.5 %mass surfactant, and more than or                equal to 90 %mass acid-base mixture.
   USE - Foaming composition is used for cleaning                toilet bowl.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition is capable of being dissolved                in water of toilet bowl, and has low environmental                impact, and can be stored at room temperature and                ambient humidity for at least two months, and                quickly forms homogeneous foam that allows                excrement (urine and feces) to be hidden, and                avoids splashes, rising odors and falling noises,                when excrements fall into the toilet bowl.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                flexible waterproof and airtight sachet, which                accommodates the foaming composition.

PN CN112680481-A
TI Method for synergistically enhancing methane production from food waste by anaerobic fermentation and microbial electrocatalysis comprises performing anaerobic fermentation, and converting hydrogen and carbon dioxide into methane.
AU JIA X
   ZHAO Y
   ZHU Y
AE UNIV BEIJING TECHNOLOGY & BUSINESS (UYTB-C)
GA 202145833P
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises crushing organic waste into slurry, introducing intermittent feeding mode to anaerobic fermentation reactor for acid production and hydrogen production fermentation; (ii) using granular sludge as inoculum, taking mixed acid as the culture solution, and the single-chamber microbial electrolytic cell is used as the methanogenic phase reactor, (iii) performing solid-liquid separation, adjusting supernatant pH to 6-8, and pumping it into the methanogenic phase reactor, injecting biogas produced by fermentation into methanogenic phase reactor, maintaining the partial pressure of hydrogen in the headspace of the reactor below 1 bar, converting hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the biogas into methane under the action of cathodic methanogens, operating methanogenic phase reactor in an internal reflux mode with 20-80 % reflux ratio, 1-7 days hydraulic retention time, 2-10 kg/(m3.d) organic load of the influent, 0.2-1 V external voltage and 20-40 degrees C operating temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for synergistically enhancing methane production from food waste by anaerobic fermentation and microbial electrocatalysis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the mixed acid is composed of sodium acetate, sodium propionate, sodium butyrate, phosphate buffer solution, and/or trace element solution. In step (ii), the electrode carbon-based material of the methanogenic phase reactor is carbon brush or graphite felt, and the cathode conductive ceramic material is magneli phase titanium suboxide. Preferred Method: In the step (iii), the successful methanogenic phase reactor is the maximum current and methane output remained stable for more than 3 consecutive cycles during the start-up process of step (ii), indicating that the methanogenic phase reactor was started successfully. In the step (i), the temperature of the anaerobic fermentation reactor is controlled to 55 plus minus 1 degrees C, pH 6-6.5, organic load 6-8 kgvs/m3.d, and hydraulic retention time are 2-4 days. In the step (ii), the anode of the methanogenic phase reactor is a carbon-based material, and the cathode is a carbon-based material or a conductive ceramic material, the startup voltage is 0.2-1 v, and the operating temperature is 20-40 degrees C.In the step (ii), the granular sludge and mixed acid are added to the methanogenic phase reactor in a volume ratio of 1:1-1:10 with a solid content of 6-10%, an external voltage of 0.2-1 v, and a cathode potential controlled at -0.6 to -0.8 v, and anode potential is controlled to -0.2 to -0.1 v.

PT P
PN CN112537771-A; CN112537771-B
TI Bicontinuous nanoporous carbon material co-doped with nitrogen and oxygen used as cathode of potassium ion battery, has preset BET specific surface area and comprises carbon, nitrogen and oxygen.
AU ZHAO G
   GONG J
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 202131440W
AB    NOVELTY - A bicontinuous nanoporous carbon material co-doped with nitrogen and oxygen comprises a continuous nano mesoporous and macroporous scaffold structure with a large specific surface area, and large number of micropores. The BET specific surface area of the carbon material is 200-400 m2/g. The carbon material contains 70-90 atomic% carbon, 5-20 atomic% nitrogen, and 5-10 atomic% oxygen.
   USE - Bicontinuous nanoporous carbon material co-doped with nitrogen and oxygen used as cathode of potassium ion battery (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The bicontinuous nanoporous carbon material co-doped with nitrogen and oxygen provides cathode with excellent reversible specific capacity, multiplying power performance and cycle stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the nano porous carbon material, which involves saturating carbon dioxide in polyacrylonitrile film by supercritical carbon dioxide foaming, subjecting the film to water bath heat treatment to obtain nano porous foam, and performing pre-oxidation treatment and carbonization treatment of the foam; and
   (2) potassium ion battery cathode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The cathode comprises an active material, conductive agent, a binder and a current collector. The binder bonds the conductive agent and the current collector. The active material is the nano porous carbon material.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The solvent is dimethyl sulfoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Component: The polyacrylonitrile film is formed by dissolving polyacrylonitrile in solvent in the mass ratio of 1:8-12 to form polyacrylonitrile solution, and drying the solution at 50-70 degrees C for 22-26 hours to obtain a film. The supercritical carbon dioxide foaming is carried out at 30-50 degrees C under pressure of 10-30 MPa for 2-6 hours. The pressure release rate after foaming is 40-200 MPa.second-1, preferably 80-100 MPa.second-1. The water bath temperature is 60-100 degrees C, preferably 70-90 degrees C. The pre-oxidation is carried out at 260-280 degrees C for 6-10 hours, at a heating rate of 1-3 degrees C/minute. The carbonization is carried out at 500-1000 degrees C degrees C with a heating rate of 8-12 degrees C/minute for 0.5-1.5 hours.

Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. A carbon discharging function model is established based on type of pollutant such that an energy storage system charging and discharging instruction is obtained according to minimum cost and low-carbon dioxide emission for distribution.

   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures voltage in a microgrid is within constraint range so as to reduce system operation cost and carbon emission rate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating operations of an optimal power scheduling type micro-grid multi-layer voltage controlling system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

GA 202113668B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing anti-inflammatory fast hemostatic medical glue material involves taking n-butyl alpha-cyanoacrylate and n-octyl alpha-cyanoacrylate and mixing them to obtain medical adhesive matrix for use, taking carboxymethyl chitosan and placing in closed container, filling with ionic liquid and anti-inflammatory active ingredients, filling the container with carbon dioxide. The temperature and pressure are controlled in the airtight container to make the carbon dioxide gas in supercritical state. The carboxymethyl chitosan is not in contact with the ionic liquid and anti-inflammatory active ingredients. The ionic liquid and anti-inflammatory active ingredients are carried out to modify the carboxymethyl chitosan to obtain desired modified carboxymethyl chitosan. The modified carboxymethyl chitosan is slowly added to the medical glue matrix, stirred until system is gelatinous, and sterilized.
   USE - Method for preparing anti-inflammatory fast hemostatic medical glue material for preparing hemostatic materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for more clinical occasions, has good bacteriostatic performance, improves hemostatic performance of carboxymethyl chitosan, realizes fast hemostasis.

The added amount of the ionic liquid accounts for the carboxymethyl 5-10 wt.% of the mass of chitosan. The added amount of the anti-inflammatory active ingredient accounts for 3-8 wt.% of the mass of the carboxymethyl chitosan. The ratio of modified carboxymethyl chitosan and medical glue base is 3-8:10.

Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-based polymer is propylene oxide/carbon dioxide binary copolymer; or the carbon dioxide-based polymer is propylene oxide/ethylene oxide/carbon dioxide terpolymer; or the carbon dioxide-based polymer is terpolymer of propylene oxide/cyclohexane oxide/carbon dioxide. The catalyst II is organic carboxylate zinc catalyst, diethyl zinc, organoaluminum, rare earth catalysts and/or multiple metal mixed catalyst. The mass of the catalyst II is 0.1-10 wt.% of the mass of the carbon dioxide-based polymer. The mass of the raw material C is 0.01-2 wt.% of the total mass of the raw material A and the raw material B. The catalyst I includes organic amine and organotin; the organic amine is triethylenediamine, polyacrylamide, N,N-dimethylethanolamine and/or triethylenediamine; and the organotin is dibutyltin, dioctyltin and/or stannous octoate. The foam stabilizer is polysiloxane alkoxy ether copolymer; the foaming agent is water; and the hardener is ethylene glycol. The antistatic agent is quaternary ammonium salt type cationic surfactant, the quaternary ammonium salt type cationic surfactant is stearylamine polyoxyethylene ether diquaternary ammonium salt, dodecylamine polyoxyethylene ether diquaternary ammonium salt and/or didodecylamine polyoxyethylene ether triquaternary ammonium salt.

PT P
PN CN111926593-A; CN111926593-B
TI Dual-medium cyclic dyeing method, involves            pre-swelling fiber in padding solution, dyeing padding            fiber to release pressure, cleaning dyed fiber in            solvent of padding solution, and drying cleaned fiber            to release pressure.
AU ZHAO H
   TAN L
   WANG M
   DING X
AE UNIV DALIAN POLYTECHNIC (UYDP-C)
GA 2020B54374
AB    NOVELTY - Dual-medium cyclic dyeing method, involves (i)                pre-swelling fiber in a padding solution at 5-60                degrees C and 0.01-0.7 MPa for 30 seconds to 24                hours, (ii) placing a padding fiber in a                supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing kettle, and                dyeing the padding fiber at 40-140 degrees C and                12-35 MPa for 30-150 minutes to release the                pressure, (iii) placing the dyed fiber in a                cleaning tank and cleaning in a solvent of the                padding solution at 0-45 degrees C, and (iv)                placing the cleaned fiber in a supercritical carbon                dioxide drying kettle, and drying the cleaned fiber                at 40-140 degrees C and 12-35 MPa for 5-60 minutes                to release the pressure.
   USE - Dual-medium cyclic dyeing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method ensures high dye uptake and color                fastness, and high color depth and small color                difference of the dyed fiber, and completely                reduces the problems of dye hydrolysis, high                solvent usage, and high cycle difficulty.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - IMAGING AND COMMUNICATION - Preferred Components: The fiber comprises                  natural cellulose fiber, natural protein fiber,                  regenerated fiber, synthetic fiber or blended                  fiber. The padding solution comprises dye,                  alkali, and solvent. The dye comprises at least                  one of reactive dye, disperse dye, cationic dye,                  direct dye and acid dye, and the concentration of                  the dye is 1-100 g/l. The alkali comprises at                  least one of potassium carbonate, sodium                  carbonate, methylamine, urea, ethylamine,                  ethylenediamine, dimethylamine,                  triethylenediamine, trimethylamine,                  triethylamine, pyridine, potassium hydroxide,                  sodium hydroxide and ammonium hydroxide, and the                  concentration of the alkali is 0-100 g/l. The                  solvent comprises one or two or three of dimethyl                  sulfoxide, N,N-dimethylformamide,                  dimethylacetamide, methanol, ethanol, ethylene                  glycol, acetone, dichloromethane and chloroform.                  Preferred Method: The method involves contacting                  the solvent on the fiber in the supercritical                  carbon dioxide dyeing kettle, enabling the                  supercritical carbon dioxide drying kettle into                  the separation kettle by carbon dioxide, enabling                  the separated solvent to enter into the cleaning                  tank, cleaning the dyed fiber, filtering the                  solvent to remove insoluble impurities, enabling                  to enter the mixing tank, analyzing the content                  of dye and alkali in the filtered solvent in the                  mixing tank, adding the dye and alkali to the                  initial concentration and mixing into the padding                  solution. The method involves enabling the carbon                  dioxide containing solvent in the supercritical                  carbon dioxide dyeing kettle and the                  supercritical carbon dioxide drying kettle to                  enter the separation kettle, condensing the                  separated carbon dioxide and storing in a carbon                  dioxide storage tank. Preferred Conditions: When                  there are two solvents, the volume ratio of the                  two solvents is (10-90):(90-10). When there are                  three solvents, mix any two solvents in a volume                  ratio of (10-90):(90-10), and adding a third                  solvent, and the volume ratio of the third                  solvent to the first two solvent mixtures is                  (10-90):(90-10).
DC F06 (Chemical-type treatment of textiles (D06B, L, M, P, Q).)
MC F03-F02; F03-F03; F03-F09; F03-F18; F03-F19; F03-F20; F03-F21; F03-F22

PN CN111730794-A; CN111730794-B
TI Preparing thermoplastic elastomer foaming material used as a packaging material, comprises e.g. heating thermoplastic elastomer material in mold cavity to foaming temperature, and injecting supercritical fluid into the mold cavity.
AU ZHAO L
   CHEN Y
   LIU T
   HU D
   WU K
   XU Z
   XI Z
   ZONG Y
   LING Y
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202099459T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing thermoplastic elastomer foaming material comprises (i) heating the thermoplastic elastomer material in the mold cavity to the foaming temperature, the foaming temperature is (Tg + 180 degrees C) to (Tg + 200 degrees C); (ii) injecting supercritical fluid into the mold cavity, and keeping the supercritical state, where the supercritical fluid is a mixed gas of supercritical carbon dioxide and supercritical carbon dioxide, the pressure ratio of the supercritical nitrogen and the supercritical CO2 is (0.3-2.2):1; and (iii) relieving the pressure of the mold cavity.
   USE - The thermoplastic elastomer foam material is used as a packaging material (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The thermoplastic elastomer foam material: has simple process, low energy consumption and short total saturation time, which improves the production efficiency; has high foaming ratio and high shrinkage rate, small cell diameter, high cell density, and excellent mechanical properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for thermoplastic elastomer foaming material, is prepared by the preparation method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the form of the thermoplastic elastomer material is bead or sheet plate, the length-to-diameter ratio of the sheet plate is preferably 1:1, the thickness of sheet plate is preferably 2-10 mm, and/or, the thermoplastic elastomer is thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer rubber, thermoplastic polyester elastomer or nylon 12 elastomer, the thermoplastic polyurethane elastomer rubber is preferably thermoplastic polyether polyurethane, the Shore hardness of thermoplastic polyether polyurethane is preferably 60-90 A, further preferably 80 A. In the step (i), the heating rate of the heating is 5-20 degrees C/minute, namely 10 plus minus 1 degrees C/minute, and/or, the foaming temperature is (Tg + 185 degrees C) to (Tg + 195 degrees C), preferably is (Tg + 190 degrees C). In the step (ii), the pressure ratio of the supercritical N2 and the supercritical CO2 is (0.5-2):1, preferably (1-2):1, and/or, in the mold cavity and pressure of the supercritical fluid is 5-30 MPa, preferably 10-20 MPa. In the step (iii), the pressure relief is rapid pressure relief or slow pressure relief, the rapid pressure relief rate is 100-600 MPa/s, preferably 200-400 MPa/s, further preferably 290-310 MPa/s, the rate of the slow pressure relief is 0.01-1 MPa/s, preferably 0.01-0.5 MPa/s, further preferably 0.03 MPa/s. in the step (iii), the pressure relief is the rapid pressure relief, the maintaining supercritical pressure in step (ii) state time is 30-180 minutes, preferably 90-180 minutes for example 120-180 minutes, when the pressure relief is the slow pressure relief. The time for maintaining the supercritical state is 5-90 minutes, preferably 10-90 minutes, for example, 45-90 minutes preferably, when the pressure relief, the slow pressure relief, further includes repeating step (ii) and the rapid pressure relief operation, and the operation of slowly depressurizing the mold cavity and repeating is preferably repeated for 1-4 times, namely 2 times. The method comprises (a) heating the thermoplastic elastomer material in the mold cavity to the foaming temperature, the foaming temperature is (Tg + 180 degrees C) to (Tg + 200 degrees C), preferably (Tg + 185 degrees C) to (Tg + 195 degrees C), further preferably (Tg + 190 degrees C); (b) injecting supercritical fluid into the mold cavity, and keeping the supercritical state, the supercritical fluid is a mixed gas of supercritical CO2 and supercritical CO2, the pressure ratio of supercritical N2 and the supercritical CO2 is (0.3-2.2):1, preferably is (0.5 ~ 2): 1, more preferably is 2:1, the time for keeping the supercritical state is 5-90 minutes, preferably 10 to 90 minutes, further preferably 45-90 minutes; (c) slowly releasing pressure to the mold cavity, the speed of slow pressure relief is 0.01-1 MPa/s, preferably 0.01-0.5 MPa/s 0.03 MPa/s, further preferably (d) repeating the operation of step (b); (e) slowly releasing pressure to the mold cavity, the speed of slow pressure relief is 0.01-1 MPa/s, preferably 0.01-0.5 MPa/s 0.03MPa/s, further preferably (f) repeating the operation of step (b); and (g) vacuum extracting the mold cavity, the rate of the pressure relief is 100-600 MPa/s, preferably 200-400 MPa/s, further preferably 290-310 MPa/s.
