PT P
PN CN111557961-A
TI Using and administering volatile oil of Artemisia annua for treating respiratory diseases by extracting crushed Artemisia annua with supercritical carbon dioxide, subjecting extract to molecular distillation, and obtaining volatile oil.
AU LIU S
   WANG Z
   FU B
   LI Z
   KONG L
   XUE H
   JIANG Y
   MA H
AE YUNNAN LVXIN BIOLOGICAL PHARM CO LTD (YUNN-Non-standard)
GA 2020830723
AB    NOVELTY - Method for using and administering volatile oil of Artemisia annua for treating respiratory diseases involves crushing A.annua, placing crushed A.annua in a supercritical carbon dioxide into an extraction kettle, extracting for a certain period of time to obtain A.annua oil extract, subjecting the extract to molecular distillation, and obtaining a light component i.e. volatile oil of A.annua.
   USE - The method is useful for using and administering volatile oil of Artemisia annua for treating respiratory diseases. The respiratory diseases include pharyngitis, rhinitis, tonsillitis, bronchitis, and pneumonia, and inhibiting and killing common bacteria that cause respiratory diseases (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to use and administer highly stable volatile oil of Artemisia annua, can effectively reduce a series of problems such as antibiotic damage to patient's body and drug resistance, shortens the treatment time, and requires reduced amount of drugs, which exhibits desired therapeutic effect, avoids liver metabolism and reduces side effects of drugs on other parts of human body.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction on A.annua having a crushed particle size of 10-100 mesh at an extraction pressure of 15-30 MPa, an extraction temperature of 35-55 degrees C, a carbon dioxide flow rate of 15-35 kg/hour, and an extraction time of 0.5-3 hours. The method involves performing desorption in a desorption tank at 35-50 degrees C and 1-15 mPa. The method involves performing molecular distillation at a vacuum degree of 3-10 Pa, a film scraping speed of 150-300 revolution per minute (rpm), a temperature of 80-150 degrees C, and a flow rate of 1-5 ml/minute. The beta -cyclodextrin inclusion compound of volatile oil is prepared by accurately weighing 16 g beta -cyclodextrin, placing it in a 250 ml round-bottomed flask, adding 160 ml distilled water, stirring evenly at 100-300 rpm, heating to completely dissolve cyclodextrin, slowly lowering the temperature to 30 degrees C to obtain a saturated solution of beta -cyclodextrin, using a pipette to accurately measure 2 ml volatile oil of A.annua, diluting it with absolute ethanol at a ratio of 1:1, slowly dropping it into saturated beta -cyclodextrin solution, adding a stopper, and continually stirring at a constant temperature and uniform speed for 1 hour, placing the solution in a refrigerator for 24 hours, filtering with suction, washing filter cake for 3 times with 5 ml ethyl acetate, drying in a vacuum drying oven at 60 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain a white loose powder. The method involves using beta -cyclodextrin inclusion compound of A.annua volatile oil with an electric heater, placing beta -cyclodextrin inclusion compound of the volatile oil into heating chamber of electric heater, turning on switch of the electronic heating controller, setting temperature of the heating chamber to 300-1000 degrees C, vaporizing the drug into gas molecules and escaping it when the temperature of the heating chamber exceeds the boiling point of the compound in the volatile oil of A.annua, and inhaling the drug molecules into lesion site to achieve purpose of targeted drug delivery.
   ACTIVITY - Antiinflammatory; Antiallergic; Respiratory-Gen.; Antibacterial.
   ADMINISTRATION - The volatile oil is administered by inhalation, preferably oral inhalation or nasal inhalation (claimed).
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).)

PN WO2020153780-A1; KR2020091656-A; KR2168444-B1; US2022088578-A1
TI Bifunctional catalyst for preparing hydrocarbon from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, comprises carbon composite comprising cobalt and nitrogen atoms, and metal particles which exhibit catalytic activity of Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction.
AU HWANG S M
   JUN K W
   KIM S K
   PARK H G
   HAN S J
   KIM S G
   PARKHAEGU
   JU H
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
   KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 202071231H
AB    NOVELTY - A bifunctional catalyst comprises a carbon composite comprising cobalt and nitrogen atoms coordinated to the cobalt, and metal particles which exhibit catalytic activity of the Fischer-Tropsch synthesis reaction are dispersed on the inner pore surface and/or the outer surface of the carbon composite support.
   USE - Bifunctional catalyst used for preparing hydrocarbon from carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The bifunctional catalyst is capable of simultaneously performing reverse water gas conversion reaction and Fischer-Tropsch reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of the bifunctional catalyst, which involves (a) forming a mixture of a cobalt precursor and a porous silica template comprising cobalt, a nitrogen atom coordinating the cobalt and a carbon atom covalently bonding with the nitrogen atom, (b) pyrolyzing the mixture, (c) preparing a porous carbon composite support by removing the silica template through acid treatment of the pyrolyzed mixture, and (d) supporting a precursor of metal particles having catalytic activity of a Fischer-Tropsch reaction on the porous carbon composite support in step (c).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal particles are chosen from iron, cobalt, copper, manganese, nickel, and zinc. The metal particles are used in combination with one or more enhancers chosen from aluminum, potassium, and sodium. In the step (a), the precursor contains nitrogen and carbon containing the cobalt metal is one or more chosen from tetramethoxyphenylporphyrin cobalt(II) (Co-TMPP), cobalt(III) meso-tetraphenylporphyrin (CoTPP), cobalt acetate, and cobalt phthalocyanine. Preferred Process: The precursor is further added to form mixture. In step (c) involves removing the silica template through acid treatment of the pyrolyzed mixture. The hydrogen and carbon dioxide are reacted in the presence of the catalyst. Preferred Condition: The reduction reaction of carbon dioxide is performed at 200-450 degrees C and a pressure of 30 atm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon composite support comprises a cobalt-amine compound of formula (I).x = 1-4.

PT P
PN CN111453740-A
TI Preparing macroporous silica by air atomization carbonization, comprises preparing sodium silicate aqueous solution, adding into the dehydrating agent and reacting, atomizing the resultant material in a countercurrent manner into droplets.
AU HU Y
   HU X
   HU W
AE LENGSHUIJIANG 3A NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (LENG-Non-standard)
GA 2020727336
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing macroporous silica by air atomization carbonization, comprises (i) preparing sodium silicate aqueous solution; (ii) adding the sodium silicate aqueous solution into the dehydrating agent and reacting; (iii) heating carbon dioxide to 100-150 degrees C and passing into the reaction tower; at the same time, atomizing the resultant material of step (ii) reaction in a countercurrent manner into droplets with a diameter of 10-20 mu m through an atomizer under a pressure of 1-2 Kg/cm2 and passing into the reaction tower to react with carbon dioxide, controlling the outlet pH of the reaction tower to 7-9; (iv) washing the reaction material with water; (v) replacing water of the washed material; and (vi) drying the material to obtain large pore volume silica.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing macroporous silica by air atomization carbonization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical. The product has stable performance and is good for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i) sodium silicate is mixed with water to prepare 10-15 wt.% of silica aqueous solution. In step (ii) sodium silicate aqueous solution is added into the reaction tank, stirred at 25-30 degrees C, then dehydrating agent is added at a speed of 80-100 revolutions per minute, and the amount of the dehydrating agent added is 3-5 wt.% of the silica. In step (iv), washing with water until the resistivity of the washing water is lower than 1000 Omega /cm. In step (v), ethanol is used for water replacement to replace the water content in the silica at less than 10%. In step (iii), carbon dioxide is heated to 100 degrees C and passed into the reaction tower; at the same time, after the step (ii) reaction, the materials are atomized into droplets with a diameter of 10 mu m under a pressure of 1 Kg/cm2 through an atomizer, and then fed into the reaction tower to react with carbon dioxide, and the outlet pH of the reaction tower is controlled to 7.5. In step (iii), carbon dioxide is heated to 120 degrees C and passed into the reaction tower; at the same time, the step (ii) reaction material is atomized into droplets with a diameter of 12.5 mu m under a pressure of 1.2 Kg/cm2 through an atomizer to pass into the reaction tower to react with carbon dioxide, and control the outlet pH of the reaction tower to 8.5. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the dehydrating agent is ethanol and/or n-butanol.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC E12-A10; E31-P01
IP B82Y-030/00; B82Y-040/00; C01B-033/18

PN CN111129636-A
TI Regenerating anode material of waste lithium iron phosphate battery by discharging waste lithium-ion batteries, disassembling lithium iron phosphate cathode powder, separating, and mixing with hydrogen peroxide aqueous solution.
AU YE L
   TIAN Y
   FU T
   CHEN J
   ZHANG W
   FU D
   MIN J
   ZHANG L
   XIA L
AE UNIV TSINGHUA RES INST IN SHENZHEN (UTHR-C)
GA 2020418255
AB    NOVELTY - Regenerating anode material of waste lithium iron phosphate battery comprises discharging waste lithium-ion batteries, disassembling lithium iron phosphate cathode powder, separating through physical method, mixing with hydrogen peroxide aqueous solution, soaking lithium ion Li+, introducing carbon dioxide, controlling pH to obtain lithium salt and iron phosphate-containing mixed solution A; determining ratio of lithium, iron and phosphorus, adding lithium source, iron source or phosphorus source at molar ratio of 1-1.05:1:1 according to test results, adding carbon source at molar ratio of 0.1-4:1, and controlling carbon content at 1-10% to obtain mixture B; and spray-pyrolyzing under inert gas atmosphere.
   USE - The method is for regenerating anode material of waste lithium iron phosphate battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, does not introduce impurity elements, can recycle resources, produces carbon dioxide exhaust gas, and has short flow, gentle reaction, high leaching efficiency, low cost, and good environmental protection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrogen peroxide solution has concentration of 5-30%. The solid-liquid ratio of anode powder and hydrogen peroxide solution is 50-200 g/L. The pH value of mixed solution A is controlled between 2.5-6. The lithium source is lithium carbonate, lithium hydroxide and/or lithium acetate. The iron source is ferrous oxalate, iron oxide, ferrous acetate and/or iron phosphate. The phosphorus source is ammonium dihydrogen phosphate, ammonium phosphate, iron phosphate and/or phosphoric acid. The inert gas is nitrogen, argon or helium gas. Preferred Method: The soaking is done at 20-90 degrees C. The spray pyrolysis is done at 300-1000 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon source is glucose or sucrose.

PT P
PN CN111003720-A
TI Enriching and extracting lithium salt by sedimentary lithium potassium salt comprises e.g. isothermally evaporating brine solution containing lithium ion, potassium ion, and carbonate ion and performing carbonization treatment.
AU ZHENG S
   TAN X
   JIANG H
   ZHANG W
   WANG J
AE UNIV QINGHAI (UYQH-C)
GA 202032314C
AB    NOVELTY - Enriching and extracting lithium salt by sedimentary lithium potassium salt comprises (i) isothermally evaporating brine solution containing lithium ion, potassium ion, and carbonate ion, and analyzing the sample until lithium carbonate appears in the precipitated solid, (ii) passing solids and liquids in above system A into carbon dioxide and carrying out carbonization treatment to convert the system A into lithium ion, potassium ion/ carbonate ion/aqueous solution system to obtain solution B, (iii) repeating step (ii) until no solid precipitates out of solution B to obtain solution C, and (iv) isothermally evaporating the solution C, continuously precipitating solids at the beginning stage, and performing solid-liquid separation, and (v) repeating steps (ii), (iii) and(iv) of the solution to obtain lithium salt-enriched brine.
   USE - The method is useful for enriching and extracting lithium salt by sedimentary lithium potassium salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: realizes resource utilization of carbon dioxide and realizes the recovery and recycling of purified separation liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (v), lithium ion concentration in the solution reaches 10-30 g/l, steps (ii), (iii), and (iv) are not repeated. In step (i): concentration of brine solution containing lithium ion, potassium ion and carbonate ion is 5-10 l; mass percentage of lithium in the solution is 0.02-0.08%; mass percentage of potassium is 5-20%, and mass percentage of carbonate is 2-10%. Preferred Method: In step (iv), isothermal evaporation operation: evaporation temperature should be controlled at 25 degrees C, accompanied by intermittent exhaust operation, and wind speed is controlled at 2.8-3.8 m/second. In step (ii): carbonization treatment is performed under the condition that solution temperature is 20-25 degrees C; control pressure is 0.2-0.5 MPa; the holding time is 30 minutes to 2 hour. In step (i): isothermal evaporation process is accompanied by exhaust operation, and the wind speed of exhaust operation is 2.8-3.8 m/s. In step (i): isothermal evaporation is isothermal evaporation at 25 degrees C in a constant temperature box, during the atmospheric pressure isothermal evaporation and concentration process, monitoring the liquid phase composition and the solid phase composition until lithium carbonate appears in the solid phase, where lithium ion concentration reaches 1-3 g/l.

MC E11-K03; E11-W; E33-G

PN CN110697734-A
TI Continuously synthesizing phosphorus-free cyanamide comprises e.g. acidifying filtrate with non-phosphoric acid to obtain an acidified filtrate, and supplementing mother liquid to maintain stable volume in continuous synthesis system.
AU ZHANG C
   LIANG W
   LIU G
   CUI W
   ZHANG N
   CAO B
   JING P
AE SHANDONG EFIRM BIOCHEMISTRY & ENVIRONMEN (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020084303
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously synthesizing phosphorus-free cyanamide comprises (i) adding carbon dioxide and lime nitrogen into mother liquor under circulation state, adding stabilizer, reacting and filtering to obtain a filtrate (pH less than or equal to 6); (ii) continuously acidifying the filtrate with a non-phosphoric acid to a pH of 3-6 to obtain an acidified filtrate; (iii) returning a portion the monocyanamide in the acidified filtrate to the mother liquor after reaching a predetermined value; and (iv) introducing the other portion to the condensing system or storage system, and supplementing the mother liquid to maintain the stable volume in the continuous synthesis system, where the mass ratio of the acidified filtrate returned to the mother liquor to the acidified filtrate sent out is 2-4:1.
   USE - The method is useful for continuously synthesizing phosphorus-free cyanamide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the production process of monocyandiamide and quality of cyanamide products and reduces the carbon dioxide unit consumption and generation of impurities during the storage of cyanamide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of carbon dioxide flux and lime nitrogen is 0.2-0.4:1 (Nm3/h:Kg/h). The effective nitrogen content in the lime nitrogen is greater than or equal to 21%. The stabilizer is cuprous chloride, ferric chloride, sodium sulfate, sodium sulfide, ammonium thiocyanate and/or quaternary ammonium salt. The amount of the stabilizer is 0.01-0.05 wt.%. The non-phosphoric acid is hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid, formic acid, acetic acid and/or citric acid. The amount of monocyandiamide in the acidified filtrate is 15-50 wt.%. Preferred Method: The reaction is carried out at less than or equal to 30 degrees C with a stirring speed of 100-300 revolutions/minute. The reaction is performed in a synthesis kettle with a solid-liquid ratio of less than or equal to 0.2. The method further comprises washing the mother liquor with water or washing the filter residue.

TI Gas discharging device, has absorption tower whose top end is formed with gas pipe opening, and first sub-pipeline connected with second sub-pipeline, where absorption tower is provided with manhole.
AU WU L
   LIU Q
   XU Y
   JING L
AE CHONGQING TECHNOLOGY BRANCH SPIC YUANDA (CPIC-C)
GA 201989713W
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a gas discharging device, comprising an absorption tower and a gas pipe opening at the top end; the absorption tower is provided with a manhole, the gas pipe through the manhole into the absorption tower; the gas pipe comprises a first pipeline inside the absorption tower; the first pipeline comprises a vertical to the tower body axis of the first pipeline and the second pipeline are parallelly to the axis of tower body, the first sub-pipeline is connected with the second sub-pipeline. carbon dioxide of high concentration by gas pipe enters into the absorption tower, is mixed with the purge gas in the absorption tower to obtain the mixed gas, the mixed gas discharged to the outside of the absorption tower through the top opening of the absorption tower. Thus, the discharged to the mixed gas outside the absorption due to the purge gas, so that the concentration of carbon dioxide is reduced, which can reduce the damage to the environment.

PT P
PN CN110256381-A; CN110256381-B
TI Preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid by mixing e.g. saccharide compound and mixed solvent of 1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone solution and dimethyl carbonate, sealing and stirring, introducing carbon dioxide/oxygen mixed gas and reacting.
AU LI Y
   LONG S
   TANG P
   DU F
   DU Q
AE GUANGXI ACAD SCI (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGXI ACAD SCI (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201991954X
AB    NOVELTY - Method of one-step preparation of 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid involves (a) adding 5-20 pts. wt. saccharide compound, 0.5 pt. wt. sodium carbonate-zinc glutarate, 0.3-0.5 pt. wt. catalyst, 100 pts. wt. hydrogen peroxide, and a mixed solvent of 1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone solution and dimethyl carbonate to a high pressure reaction vessel, rapidly sealing and uniformly stirring at 300 rpm and (b) continuing stirring at 300 rpm, introducing carbon dioxide/oxygen mixed gas to a high pressure reactor at a pressure of 6-8 MPa, heating at 185-195 degrees C for 10-45 minutes for reacting, stopping the reaction and naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain a mixture of 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid as the main product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing 2,5-furandicarboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high sensitivity, adopts rapid reaction process, and is environmentally-friendly and cost-effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The saccharide compound comprises fructose, glucose, inulin, mannose, galactose or sucrose. The catalyst comprises mercaptodecanoic acid. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of zinc carbonate to zinc glutarate in the sodium carbonate-zinc glutarate is (1-4):1. The volume ratio of hydrogen peroxide, 1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone and dimethyl carbonate is 1:1:18. The volume ratio of carbon dioxide and oxygen in the carbon dioxide/oxygen mixed gas is 1:(1-3).

AB    NOVELTY - A boiler hearth structure with internally enhanced heat transfer and thermal resistance comprises hearth. The hearth comprises cooling wall (200), covering outer heat insulation layer (100) disposed outside the cooling wall and covering inner heat insulation layer (300) disposed inside the cooling wall. The cooling wall comprises multiple cooling pipes and connecting plate to form the whole of multiple cooling pipes. The inner wall of cooling pipe is provided with inner reinforcing heat transmission structure. The inner heat insulation layer comprises insulating belts provided around the hearth.
   USE - Boiler hearth structure with internally enhanced heat transfer and thermal resistance.
   ADVANTAGE - The structure prevents furnace cooling wall from overheating, and ensures safe, stable and economical operation of supercritical carbon dioxide bunker coal boiler.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for supercritical carbon dioxide bunker coal boiler.

TI Gas selective separation filter for separating            methane from carbon dioxide, comprises nanoporous            graphene membrane, porous carbon substrate, and porous            support structure on which graphene membrane and porous            carbon substrate are supported.
AU AGRAWAL K V
   HUANG S
   KUMAR V A
AE GAZNAT SA (GAZN-Non-standard)
   GAZNAT SA (GAZN-Non-standard)
   GAZNAT SA (GAZN-Non-standard)
   GAZNAT SA (GAZN-Non-standard)
GA 201980219E
AB    NOVELTY - Gas selective separation filter comprises a                nanoporous graphene membrane having thickness of                0.34-2 nm and porosity more than 0.001%, and a                porous carbon substrate (5) having porosity of                5-90% and includes porous support structure on                which graphene membrane and porous carbon substrate                (9) are mechanically supported.
   USE - Gas selective separation filter used for                separating hydrogen or methane from carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of gas selective separation filter                which involves providing graphene membrane on                sacrificial support layer (2), coating graphene                membrane (3) with organic precursor (4) of porous                substrate, subjecting organic precursor to                pyrolysis transforming organic precursor into                porous carbon substrate on graphene membrane,                mounting combined porous carbon substrate and                graphene membrane on macroporous support structure,                and removing the sacrificial support layer.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                preparation of gas selective filter.
   Support layer (2)
   Graphene membrane (3)
   Organic precursor (4)
   Porous carbon substrate (5)
   Carbon substrate supported graphene                (9)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The pores of                  nanoporous graphene membrane having mean size                  0.2-0.5 nm, preferably 0.25-0.3 nm and porous                  support structure having porosity of 2-60%,                  preferably more than 5%, formed by pores having                  mean size of 0.01-100 mu m, preferably 0.1-20 mu                  m in 1-10 mu m and thickness of 10-10000 mu m,                  typically in 20-100 mu m. Preferred Component:                  The organic precursor in solution of turanose and                  block copolymer                  polystyrene-co-poly(4-vinylpyridine) (PS-P4VP)                  dissolved in N,N-dimethylformamide and solution,                  and dried on graphene membrane, and the organic                  precursor of porous structure is amphipathic                  block copolymer, e.g. block-copolymer of                  polyvinylpyridine and polystyrene monomers,                  preferably block-copolymer                  polystyrene-co-poly(4-vinylpyridine)                  (PS-P4VP).

PN CN109970088-A
TI Preparation of high-end polyethylene blown film special nano calcium carbonate, involves adding crystal form controlling agent to calcium hydroxide solution, introducing carbon dioxide gas, and sieving cooked slurry.
AU ZHAO X
   AO Z
AE JIANGXI HUAMING NANO CALCIUM CARBONATE (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201960527S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of high-end polyethylene blown film special nano calcium carbonate, involves (A) adding a crystal form controlling agent to the calcium hydroxide solution, stirring and mixing uniformly, and introducing a carbon dioxide gas to carry out bubbling carbonization reaction to obtain a cooked pulp, (B) sieving cooked slurry obtained in step (A), heating, adding surfactant under stirring, and coating the surface to obtain a nanometer calcium carbonate suspension, (C) subjecting nano-calcium carbonate suspension formed by the step (B) to pressure filtration dehydration to obtain a filter cake and pulverizing and drying to be depolymerized.
   USE - Preparation of high-end polyethylene blown film special nano calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-end polyethylene blown film special nano calcium carbonate has excellent binding force with the polyethylene resin system, improves the melt viscous flow, and improves the mechanical properties of the film. The preparation of high-end polyethylene blown film special nano calcium carbonate strengthens tensile strength, toughness and tear strength.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The crystal form controlling agent is added to the calcium hydroxide solution, stirred and mixed uniformly. The carbon dioxide gas is introduced to carry out bubbling carbonization reaction until the pH of the solution is 7-8, to obtain a cooked pulp. The pressure filtration dehydration is performed by adding the slurry prepared in step (A) with pH of 7-8, a BET specific surface area of 20.0-50.0 m2/g, and a mass concentration of 10-20% to the reaction vessel, starting the stirrer to stir, increasing the slurry temperature to 70-90 degrees C by heat transfer oil while stirring, adding the surfactant under stirring, adjusting the pH value to 7-9, stirring at a temperature of 70-90 degrees C for 60-90 minutes, and forming a nanometer calcium carbonate suspension by surface coating. The pH is adjusted by using zinc sulfate, aluminum sulfate, phosphoric acid or acetic acid as a regulator, and the pH adjuster is added in an amount of 0.1-0.4% of the dry mass of the calcium carbonate, in step (B). The calcium hydroxide solution is prepared by (A1) calcining the limestone with a diameter of 5-12 cm in a shaft kiln to obtain calcium oxide, (A2) heating water to digest the calcium oxide, (A3) obtaining fine pulp after removing impurities and (A4) aging to obtain seminal plasma. The step (A2) is realized by (A21) uniformly adding water to ash machine to digest and form a slurry of calcium hydroxide, the temperature of the water used for control is 40-60 degrees C, the amount of water is 5-6 times the mass of lime, controlling the concentration of calcium hydroxide between 10-20%, when digesting, (A22) removing The impurities by passing through 40 mesh sieve, a hydrocyclone, 160 mesh sieve, and 200 mesh sieve in order to obtain a fine emulsion slurry. Preferred Condition: The calcination temperature is 900-1100 degrees C and the calcination time is 8-24 hours, in step (A1). The aging time is 24-48 hours, in step (A4). Preferred Composition: The mass concentration of the calcium hydroxide solution is 6-20%. The temperature is 16-32 degrees C. The volume concentration of the carbon dioxide gas is 20-40%, and the carbonization reaction time is 180-450 minutes. The amount of the crystal form controlling agent added is 0.2-1.6% of the mass of the dry basis of calcium hydroxide. Preferred Components: The crystal form controlling agent is one or more of sucrose, citric acid, sodium citrate, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA), triethanolamine, aluminum sulfate, zinc sulfate, sulfuric acid, sodium sulfate, phosphoric acid, and acetic acid.

PN KR1954971-B1
TI Preparation of water-soluble natural fragrance            involves extracting essential oil from Citrus fruit            peels, mixing with wax, treating with supercritical            carbon dioxide, adding coconut and sunflower oils,            adding fermented alcohol and cooling.
AU PARK H K
AE PARK H K (PARK-Individual)
GA 2019232828
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of water-soluble natural fragrance                involves extracting essential oil from two or more                peels of Citrus fruits chosen from grapefruit,                lemon, orange, tangerine, kumquat, lime and citron,                mixing and stirring the essential oil with wax to                produce a semi-solid refined wax mixture, passing                pure supercritical carbon dioxide solvent through                the purified oil-wax mixture at 30-35 degrees C and                3000-4000 psi at flow rate of 1.5-2.5 L/minute,                mixing the resulting degreased extract with coconut                oil and sunflower oil to prepare a refined mixture,                adding the mixture to fermented alcohol and                purified water, mixing, cooling to 2-3 degrees C                for 3-5 hours, cooling and producing                supernatant.
   USE - Preparation of water-soluble natural                fragrance.
   ADVANTAGE - The clear and transparent water-soluble                natural fragrance can be prepared economically with                high yield of 97% by simple method. Since the                fat-soluble components are effectively separated,                the requirement of separate filtering process can                be eliminated.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The wax is beeswax or                  carnauba wax. Preferred Composition: The refined                  mixture contains 10-15 pts. wt. degreased                  extract, 30-50 pts. wt. coconut oil and 30-50                  pts. wt. sunflower oil. The essential oil mixture                  is a mixture of 10-20 pts. wt. essential oil,                  70-100 pts. wt. fermented alcohol, and 100-150                  pts. wt. pure water. The fermented alcohol is                  sweet potato fermented alcohol.

TI Preparation of monochloromethane by preheating carbon dioxide-containing hydrogen chloride exhaust gas and methanol, reacting, adding generated monochloromethane reaction gas to absorption and recovery system, drying system, and compressing.
AU JIAN S
   LIAO J
   XU J
   WANG C
AE SICHUAN TIANYI SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SICH-Non-standard)
   SOUTHWEST CHEM IND RES & DESIGIN INST (CNCC-C)
GA 201913324G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of monochloromethane comprises preheating carbon dioxide-containing hydrogen chloride exhaust gas and methanol, adding to reaction system, reacting, adding generated monochloromethane reaction gas to absorption and recovery system, cooling, absorbing, condensing, washing unreacted hydrochloric acid, methanol and water, and recycling; and adding monochloromethane to drying system to remove water, trace methanol and dimethyl ether, compressing product, and purifying.
   USE - Method for preparation of monochloromethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low energy consumption and low operating cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a monochloromethane preparation device comprising, reaction system, absorption washing recovery system, drying system, compression system, and purifying system. The reaction system comprises preheater and reactor. The absorption washing recovery system comprises heat exchanger, absorption tower and water washing tower connected in series. The drying system comprises dehydration desulfurization alcohol column and sulfuric acid absorption tower. The compression comprises first-stage compressor and second-stage compressor. The purification system comprises first-stage distillation column and a second-stage distillation column.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The compressing is done by adding monochloromethane to first-stage compressor, compressing at 0.9-2 MPa, directly adding to first-stage distillation system, distilling, separating monochloromethane through bottom of distillation column while discharging small portion of monochloromethane and remaining gas from top of column and into second-stage compressor, compressing at 3-4.5 MPa, directly adding gas into second-stage distillation system, distilling, and separating monochloromethane through bottom of distillation column while discharging remaining gas free of monochloromethane from top of column.
DC E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.)

PN RU185781-U1
TI Cylindrical dental implant for dental prosthetics, has copper-containing carbon diamond-like film is synthesized in process of ion-beam processing with beam of copper ions in vacuum environment of carbon dioxide.
AU RODIONOV I V
   PERINSKAYA I V
   KUTS L E
AE UNIV SARAT TECH (UYSA-C)
GA 201904603P
AB    NOVELTY - The implant has a conical coronal portion and a cylindrical intraosseous portion combined into a permanent joint with a neck and a cylindrical intraosseous portion with two external longitudinal projections, beveled in the direction from the cervical area to the apical zone and located in the same diametral plane. Transverse grooves are located between the longitudinal projections microstructured in the form of microcavities of an elliptical shape. The surface of the intraosseous portion is located between the transverse grooves with biocompatible m nanostructured coating of zirconium dioxide. A copper-containing carbon diamond-like film is synthesized in the process of ion-beam processing with a beam of copper ions in a vacuum environment of carbon dioxide (CO2).
   USE - Cylindrical dental implant for dental prosthetics.
   ADVANTAGE - The implant can be prevented from moving along its longitudinal axis. Thus, the transverse grooves located on the intraosseous portion between the longitudinal projections provide a high resistance to axial displacement of the implant under stress.

PN CN109020450-A
TI Preparation of board for building, involves adding concentrated material prepared using e.g. phosphogypsum, quicklime, sodium metasilicate, and polyallylamine to reactor, introducing carbon dioxide, heating, cooling, molding and drying.
AU ZHAN Y
   MA J
   ZHU D
AE FOSHAN QIAN CONSTR TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 2018A5194D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of board involves mixing 30-50 pts. wt. phosphogypsum and 60-80 pts. wt. anhydrous ethanol, ball milling to obtain a slurry, mixing the slurry with 4-8 pts. wt. 96-98 mass% concentrated sulfuric acid for 2-4 hours, adding 30-40 pts. wt. 10-20 mass% calcium salt solution, adjusting the pH to alkaline, stirring for 6-8 hours, concentrating the mixture to obtain a concentrated material, adding 80-120 pts. wt. concentrated material, 40-60 pts. wt. water, 8-10 pts. wt. quicklime, 8-10 pts. wt. sodium metasilicate, 3-5 pts. wt. sodium metaaluminate and 10-15 pts. wt. polyallylamine to a hydrothermal reactor, introducing carbon dioxide gas until the pressure in the hydrothermal reactor is reached to 0.3-0.4 MPa, heating and stirring, cooling to room temperature, discharging to obtain a gypsum slurry, injecting the gypsum slurry into a mold, drying at low temperature, cooling, and demolding.
   USE - Preparation of board for building.
   ADVANTAGE - The board has excellent waterproof performance and surface hardness.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcium salt is calcium nitrate or calcium chloride. The sodium metasilicate is anhydrous sodium metasilicate, sodium metasilicate pentahydrate or sodium metasilicate nonahydrate.

PN CN108991016-A
TI Preparation of alumina-supported copper-zinc            antibacterial agent involves adding aluminum            isopropoxide to deionized water, shaking, adding            peptizer, magnetically stirring in water bath, removing            excess liquid carbon dioxide and drying.
AU LU W
AE ZHANGJIAGANG HUIDING NEW MATERIAL TECHNO (ZHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A37236
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of alumina-supported copper-zinc                antibacterial agent comprises adding aluminum                isopropoxide to deionized water, shaking for 10-20                minutes, adding peptizer, magnetically stirring for                1-3 hours at 70-80 degrees C in water bath,                removing excess liquid carbon dioxide at 4-8                degrees C to remove isopropanol and ionized water,                adjusting pH value to 3.5-5.0 to obtain aluminum                hydroxide sol, adding copper salt solution and zinc                salt solution having concentration of 0.1-1 mol/L                to aluminum hydroxide sol where molar ratio of                copper salt and zinc salt is 1:1, magnetically                stirring for 2-3 hours, taking product, aging for                8-12 hours, drying at 90-110 degrees C for 3-5                hours, calcining at 800-1000 degrees C for 1-2                hours, keeping temperature for 20-30 minutes and                grinding into powder.
   USE - Method for preparing alumina-supported                copper-zinc antibacterial agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The product has good antibacterial                effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The concentration of                  copper salt solution and zinc salt solution IS                  0.5 mol/L. The content of alumina in                  alumina-supported copper-zinc antibacterial agent                  is 85-90%. Preferred Components: The copper salt                  solution is copper nitrate solution, copper                  chloride solution or copper sulfate solution. The                  zinc salt solution is zinc nitrate solution, zinc                  chloride solution or zinc sulfate solution.                  Preferred Method: The pH value is adjusted to                  4.5. The calcination step is done at 900 degrees                  C.

PT P
PN CN108822301-A; CN108822301-B
TI Preparing functionalized covalent organic            framework material used for removing e.g. carbon            dioxide, by taking framework material as matrix and            replacing constituent units in parent framework            material with monomer having reactive group.
AU QIAN H
   YAN X
AE UNIV JIANGNAN (UYJN-C)
GA 201891904Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a functionalized covalent organic                framework material involves taking a covalent                organic framework material prepared by a direct                synthesis method as a matrix, and replacing the                constituent units in the parent covalent organic                framework material with a monomer having a reactive                group used in the preparation of the matrix. The                functionalized covalent organic framework material                is a covalent organic framework material, which is                not obtained by direct synthesis (de novo).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a                functionalized covalent organic framework material,                which is useful for removing carbon dioxide and                energy storage material such as methane or                hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes a monomer exchange method                to prepare a reactive group functionalized imine                covalent organic framework. The prepared                functionalized covalent organic framework material                has a specific crystal structure, uniform pore size                and particle size and excellent thermal                stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                functionalized covalent organic framework material                prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation method                  involves dissolving or dispersing a reactive                  monomer, which is used for preparing the covalent                  organic framework in a solvent, reacting under a                  certain condition for a period of time to obtain                  a parent covalent organic framework, then adding                  a ligand to the parent covalent organic                  framework, continuously reacting, centrifuging,                  collecting the solid and washing to obtain the                  functionalized covalent organic framework. The                  preparation method involves: (1) mixing an amine                  compound with an aldehyde compound or a boric                  acid compound for preparing a covalent organic                  framework, acetic acid, tetrahydrofuran and                  N,N-dimethylacetamide, and ultrasonically                  treating for 5 minutes to obtain uniform reaction                  solution; (2) transferring the above reaction                  solution to a reaction tube, and heating at                  70-140 degrees C for 2-4 days to obtain a parent                  covalent organic framework material; (3) cooling                  the reaction tube to room temperature, directly                  adding amino ligand, aldehyde group or boric acid                  ligand to the reaction tube, continuously                  reacting at 30-80 degrees C for 2-4 days,                  centrifuging, collecting the final solid product                  and washing. Preferred Components: The functional                  group of the functionalized covalent organic                  framework material is one or more of a group                  participating in a covalent organic framework                  reaction of an amino group, an aldehyde group and                  a boric acid group. The matrix comprises, but is                  not limited to, an imine covalent organic                  framework and a boron-based covalent organic                  framework.

PT P
PN CN108747855-A
TI Preparation of ceramic binder involves mixing            phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and modifier,            calcining, catalyzing, adding carbon dioxide,            pulverizing, crushing silica and sodium oxide,            sintering, quenching, grinding, adding nano            zirconia.
AU CHEN X
   LI Z
   WANG Q
   WANG L
AE UNIV GUIZHOU (UYGZ-C)
GA 201888657K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of ceramic binder involves (A)                mixing phosphogypsum, fly ash, additive and                modifier, grinding to form raw meal, delivering to                kiln, calcining to obtain clinker, clinking the                clinker by grinding with water, performing                solid-liquid separation, (B) separating sulfide                from precipitate, calcining separated sulfide,                catalyzing the obtained flue gas by vanadium                pentoxide, absorbing with concentrated sulfuric                acid, producing sulfuric acid, (C) adding carbon                dioxide to liquid obtained by solid-liquid                separation in step (A) until the precipitate is no                longer produced, then drying precipitate, producing                aluminum hydroxide, (D) pulverizing aluminum                hydroxide to obtain aluminum hydroxide powder, (E)                crushing silica, sodium oxide, boron trioxide,                quartz and magnesia, adding aluminum hydroxide                powder, mixing, transferring to kiln for sintering,                quenching with water, then drying, grinding and                evenly mixing with nano zirconia.
   USE - Preparation of ceramic binder.
   ADVANTAGE - The environmentally-friendly method enables                preparation of ceramic binder with high added                value, low production cost, excellent heat                resistance and high strength.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The additive is                  selected from sodium carbonate, sodium sulfate or                  caustic soda. The modifier is selected from                  anthracite, carbon or coal gangue. The additive                  addition ratio is 1:1 according-the molecular                  ratio of the sum sodium oxide, aluminum oxide and                  ferric oxide. The mixing ratio of modifier is                  11-24% of the total weight of raw meal. The step                  (E) comprises 15-30 pts. wt. silica, 2-6 pts. wt.                  sodium oxide, 2-7 pts. wt. boron trioxide, 1-3                  pts. wt. quartz, 1-3 pts. wt. magnesia, 10-15                  pts. wt. aluminum hydroxide powder and 3-8 pts.                  wt. nano zirconia. Preferred Process: The kiln is                  selected from industrial rotary kiln, industrial                  tunnel kiln or industrial shaft kiln.                  Phosphogypsum and fly ash are mixed in a weight                  ratio of 1-1.2:1-1.7. The sulfide placed in                  oxygen-enriched environment is 40-45%. Preferred                  Conditions: The step (A) comprises calcining at                  1100-1400 degrees C for 0.5-3 hours. The water                  dissolution time is 2-5:1. The step (B) comprises                  calcining at 1000-1200 degrees C for 2-5 hours.                  The step (C) comprises drying at 100-120 degrees                  C and controlling moisture content after                  precipitation and drying to less than 0.5%. The                  step (E) comprises pulverizing through 100-150                  mesh screen, sintering at a heating rate of                  200-300 degrees C/hour, calcining (I) at 600-800                  degrees C for 1-3 hours, calcining (II) at                  1100-1200 degrees C for 2-3 hours and calcining                  (III) at 1300-1350 degrees C for 1-4 hours,                  drying at 80-100 degrees C and passing sieve                  through 80-100 mesh screen.

PN CN108706566-A
TI Synthesizing tannic acid-based porous polymer without a template under mild conditions comprises e.g. adding compound containing bisamino functional group as crosslinking agent, concentrated hydrochloric acid and deionized water.
AU MENG Q
   ZHANG Q
   GAO H
   SHAN W
   SONG X
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 201886137M
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing tannic acid-based porous polymer without a template under mild conditions comprises (i) adding a compound containing a bisamino functional group as a crosslinking agent, concentrated hydrochloric acid and deionized water to a circle in the bottom flask, controlling the temperature is between 0-5 degrees C by using an ice bath, and after stirring uniformly, adding an aqueous solution of sodium nitrite and stirring is continued to completely convert the amino group into a diazonium salt, and (ii) maintaining an ice bath state, using a sodium carbonate solution after the neutralization, adding tannic acid and sodium carbonate aqueous solution, and after 12 hours, separating the resulting solid precipitate by filtration, washing successively with deionized water, methanol, tetrahydrofuran, methanol, water and freeze-drying.
   USE - The tannic acid-based porous polymer is useful for gas storage and separation, catalyst immobilization, electrochemistry and carbon dioxide adsorption (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, needs mild and no template, and the product has excellent carbon dioxide capture ability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bisamino functional group-containing compound is 4,4'-diaminobiphenyl, 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether, and 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl sulfone. The molar ratio of the diaminodiphenyl compound to tannic acid is 10:1. The concentration of the aqueous sodium nitrite solution in the step (i) is 0.1-0.15 mmol/ml. The concentration of the sodium carbonate aqueous solution in the step (ii) is 1-1.3 mol/l. Preferred Method: The tannic acid-based porous polymer is calcined in a tube furnace at 900 degrees C for 3 hours, and naturally cooled to obtain tannic acid-based porous polymer-derived carbon material. The tannic acid-based porous polymer is first calcined in a tube furnace at a temperature rising rate of 5 degrees C/minutes to 900 degrees C for 3 hours.

PT P
PN CN108218079-A; CN108218079-B
TI Harmless treatment of trichloroacetone residual liquid comprises e.g. adding tap water in trichloroacetone residual liquid, adding calcium carbide residue in batches with stirring at room temperature, feeding, heating and reacting.
AU YANG K
   ZHANG J
   WANG G
AE CHANGZHOU XINHONG PHARM CHEM TECHNOLOGY (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201853387S
AB    NOVELTY - Harmless treatment of trichloroacetone residual liquid comprises (i) adding 1-3 times of tap water in trichloroacetone residual liquid, adding calcium carbide residue in batches with stirring at room temperature, controlling the temperature at 60-75 degrees C, and feeding for 1-2 hours, (ii) continuously stirring at room temperature for 15 minutes, then heating, controlling the temperature at 60-75 degrees C, and reacting for 15-30 hours, until large bubble generate, (iii) filtering and vacuum extracting and distilling the filtrate, and (iv) performing microbial degradation to form carbon dioxide, methane and water, so that the waste water reaches the discharge standard.
   USE - Used as harmless treatment of trichloroacetone residual liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can further degrade organic matter in the wastewater by microorganisms to form methane, carbon dioxide and water, and realizes chemical oxygen demand (COD), salt and pH of wastewater reach the discharge standard, thus ensuring harmless treatment; is economical, safe and environmentally friendly; and is beneficial to industrialization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), using gas phase chromatography and mass spectrometry to determine the component and content of each peak of trichloroacetone residual liquid, then determining mass of calcium carbide residue required to react in a volume of trichloroacetone residual liquid. In the step (i), the excessive calcium carbide residue content is 10-30%. In the step (i) and (ii), when the trichloroacetone residual liquid and tap water are equal volumes, the feeding time is 2 hours, reaction temperature is 75 degrees C, the reaction time is 30 hours. In the step (i) and (ii), the volume ratio of trichloroacetone residual liquid and tap water is 1:1.5, the feeding time is 1.8 hours, reaction temperature is 70 degrees C, the reaction time is 26 hours. In the step (i) and (ii), when volume ratio of trichloroacetone residual liquid and tap water is 1:2, the feeding time is 1.5 hours, reaction temperature is 65 degrees C, the reaction time is 22 hours. In the step (i) and (ii), the volume ratio of trichloroacetone residual liquid and tap water is 1:2.5, the feeding time is 1.3 hours, reaction temperature is 60 degrees C, the reaction time is 19 hours. In the step (i) and (ii), volume ratio of trichloroacetone residual liquid and tap water is 1:3, the feeding time is 1 hour, reaction temperature is 60 degrees C, the reaction time is 15 hours. The microbial degradation is anaerobic and aerobic method.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

AU NIKBIN N N
   BABB D A
   NIKBIN N
AE DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
   DOW GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (DOWC-C)
GA 201838797T
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of polyether carbonate polyol                involves polymerizing carbon dioxide and at least                one alkylene oxide, with a starter in the presence                of a double metal cyanide polymerization catalyst                and a catalyst promoter that is devoid of halide                anions and cyanide. The catalyst promoter is                separate from the double metal cyanide                polymerization catalyst.
   USE - Manufacture of polyether carbonate polyol used                for producing polyurethane product. Uses include                but are not limited to slabstock foam, molded foam,                flexible foam, viscoelastic foam, combustion                modified foam, rigid foam, elastomer, adhesive,                sealant, coating and polyurethane composite                materials used for mattress, pillow, furniture,                seat cushion, bumper pad, helmet liners, thermal                insulation applications, antistatic packaging of                electronic goods, earplugs, automobile panels,                etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economical, consumes less                energy, and efficiently produces polyether                carbonate polyol with high equivalent weight and                selectivity and reduced formation of byproducts and                volatile organic compound. The catalyst promoter                enhances performance of the double metal cyanide                polymerization catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst promoter                  is magnesium, group 3-15 metal or lanthanide                  series compound, which is bonded to alkoxide,                  aryloxy, carboxylate, acyl, pyrophosphate,                  phosphate, thiophosphate, dithiophosphate,                  phosphate ester, thiophosphate ester, amide,                  siloxide, hydride, carbamate and/or hydrocarbon                  anion.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The starter comprises                  a monoalcohol initiator and/or a polyalcohol                  initiator.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The method involves                  adding carbon dioxide and alkylene oxide                  intermittently or continuously to a reactor in                  the presence of the double metal cyanide catalyst                  complex and the catalyst promoter, during                  polymerization. The double metal cyanide catalyst                  complex, the catalyst promoter and the starter                  form a starting reaction mixture, before                  polymerization. The method is carried out in                  batch, semi-batch and/or continuous mode.

PN JP2018073757-A
TI Solid electrolyte used in proton-conductive fuel cell, comprises layer composed of solid electrolyte material comprising lanthanum-containing complex, layer composed of material having proton conductivity higher than that of other material.
AU MIZUTANI Y
   OKUYAMA Y
AE TOHO GAS KK (TOHG-C)
   UNIV MIYAZAKI (ZAKI-C)
GA 201836179J
AB    NOVELTY - A proton-conductive solid electrolyte comprises a layer (L1) composed of a solid electrolyte material (m1) comprising a lanthanum-containing complex (I), a layer (L2) composed of a solid electrolyte material (m2) having a proton conductivity higher than that of the material (m1) and a low resistance to methane and/or carbon dioxide.
   USE - Solid electrolyte is used in proton-conductive fuel cell (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The fuel cell containing the solid electrolyte has improved durability and power generation characteristics, and low electrical resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A proton-conductive solid electrolyte comprises a layer (L1) composed of a solid electrolyte material (m1) comprising a lanthanum-containing complex of formula: La1-yMyYb1-xInxO3-d (I) (where M is barium, strontium, calcium or magnesium, x is 0-1 (excluding 0 and 1), and d is the amount of oxygen holes), a layer (L2) composed of a solid electrolyte material (m2) having a proton conductivity higher than that of the material (m1) and a low resistance to methane and/or carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The material (m2) is a barium cerium oxide-based proton-conductive solid electrolyte material.

PN CN113774408-A; CN113774408-B
TI Method for strengthening electro reduction of            carbon dioxide to generate carbon monoxide, involves            loading catalyst on sheet-shaped hydrophobic carbon            paper, where N pole of outer magnetic field points to            catalyst sheet.
AU YANG C
   BAI P
   LIU W
   WEI S
   XU L
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2021E95724
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves preparing magnetic                nickel-based metal organic framework (MOF)                catalyst. The catalyst is supported on the                sheet-like hydrophobic carbon paper. The N pole of                the external magnetic field points to the catalyst                sheet during the electrocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction process. The magnetic nickel-based MOF                catalyst is MOF-zinc (Zn)/nickel (Ni), MOF-cobalt                (Co)/Ni, MOF-iron (Fe)/Ni, MOF-lanthanum (La)/Ni,                MOF-cerium (Ce)/Ni, MOF-praseodymium (Pr)/Ni.
   USE - Method for strengthening electro reduction of                carbon dioxide to generate carbon monoxide through                external magnetic field used for artificial systems                in modern chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a Faraday efficiency of                applying different intensity of external magnetic                field to promote carbon dioxide electroreduction                reaction to generate carbon monoxide, thus                obviously increasing the current. The method                exhibits excellent carbon dioxide reducing                performance in the practice process, and greatly                enhances the current intensity. The method can                obviously improve the energy utilization rate,                reduce the energy loss, promote the conversion                efficiency of the energy, and has wide application                prospect in the energy field.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The magnetic nickel-based                  MOF catalyst is prepared by weighing and                  dissolving the magnetic metal nitrate in                  deionized water, dissolving 2-methylimidazole in                  deionized water, mixing two solutions, stirring                  at room temperature, centrifuging and drying to                  obtain a solid, weighing equal mass of nickel                  nitrate hexahydrate and the solid, dissolving                  them in the alcohol-water mixture, stirring at                  room temperature and drying, protecting by                  nitrogen atmosphere at 900?oC, firing for 3.5                  hours, cooling to room temperature, washing with                  1 M hydrochloric acid, and finally washing with                  water and drying.The reference electrode, the air outlet, the                  air inlet and the working electrode are placed in                  the same side of the cell, and the current is                  passed through, and the electrolyte solution is                  0.5 M potassium bicarbonate aqueous solution, in                  the electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction                  process.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The magnetic metal                  nitrates are zinc nitrate hexahydrate, cobalt                  nitrate hexahydrate, iron nitrate hexahydrate,                  lanthanum nitrate hexahydrate, cerium nitrate                  hexahydrate, and promethium nitrate                  hexahydrate.

AB    NOVELTY - Method for manufacturing oxygen carrier containing metal oxide, involves taking a surfactant, a metal oxide precursor that produces metal oxide, and a solvent, mixing the surfactant, the metal oxide precursor, and the solvent to prepare a mixed solution, drying and calcining the mixed solution to convert the metal oxide precursor to the metal oxide, and removing the surfactant to obtain the oxygen carrier.

   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen carrier has high conversion efficiency of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide and large specific surface area that can be used in a chemical looping method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for producing a gas, which involves contacting the oxygen carrier with a raw material gas containing carbon dioxide, and reducing the carbon dioxide to produce carbon monoxide.

The block copolymer comprises ethylene oxide and propylene oxide. The solvent comprises water and/or alcohol solvent. Preferred Composition: The amount of the surfactant contained in the mixed solution is equal to or higher than the critical micelle concentration. Preferred Conditions: The drying is carried out at 20-200 degrees C. The calcination is carried out at 300-1200 degrees C. Preferred Properties: The block copolymer has a weight average molecular weight of 3000-10000. The oxygen carrier has a Brunauer, Emmett and Teller (BET) specific surface area of 25-500 m2/g.

PT P
PN DE202021001363-U1
TI Device for monitoring room air, includes carbon dioxide sensor and microphone with adjustable sensitivity, and adder for summing two output signals, and pollution of room air is gradually indicated by at least one signal generator.
AU 
AE MSF-VATHAUER ANTRIEBSTECHNIK GMBH & CO (MSFV-Non-standard)
GA 202195620Y
AB    NOVELTY - The device includes a carbon dioxide sensor (24) and a microphone (25) with adjustable sensitivity. The permanent output signals of which are monitored and evaluated by an evaluation device (21) and which switches drivers for signal generators as a function of specifiable signal strengths of the output signals. An adder for summing the two output signals. The signal generators are formed by optical displays (29). The pollution of the room air is gradually indicated by at least one signal generator. The pollution of the room air is displayed in three stages.
   USE - Device for monitoring room air.
   ADVANTAGE - The adaptation to room size or to a number of people within a room is easily possible with appropriate scaling. Possible to select only the carbon dioxide concentration by the carbon dioxide-Sensor to measure only the noise level inside the room through the microphone.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a room air monitoring device.
   Evaluation device (21)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (24)
   Microphone (25)
   Optical displays (29)
   Driver (30)
DC Q74 (Heating, ranges, ventilating (F24)); X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)
MC Q74-A02; Q74-T20; X27-E01B
IP F24F-011/50
PD DE202021001363-U1   19 Aug 2021   F24F-011/50   202170   Pages: 7   German
AD DE202021001363-U1    DE20001363    14 Apr 2021
PI DE20001363    14 Apr 2021
UT DIIDW:202195620Y
ER

PT P
PN WO2021136813-A1; EP4084890-A1
TI Photo-capture of carbon dioxide with perovskite            oxide compounds or oxide compounds, involves exposing            perivskite compound or oxide compound to adequate light            wavelength in presence of carbon dioxide.
AU PEREZ TOMAS A
   RUBIO LORENTE C
   SAUTHIER G
   DAVID J
   VALES CASTRO P
   ARBIOL COBOS J
   SANTISO LOPEZ J
   CATALAN BERNABE G
AE FUNDACIO INST CATALA NANOCIENCIA I NANOT (CATA-Non-standard)
   INST CATALANA RECERCA I ESTUDIS AVANCATS (CATA-Non-standard)
   FUNDACIO INST CATALA NANOCIENCIA I NANOT (CATA-Non-standard)
   INST CATALANA RECERCA & ESTUDIS AVANCATS (CATA-Non-standard)
GA 2021790812
AB    NOVELTY - Photo-capturing of carbon dioxide with a                perovskite oxide compound of formula ABO3 or an                oxide compound of formula ABO2 involves                illuminating compound of formula ABO3, such as lead                zirconium oxide (PbZrO3)- lead titanium oxide                (PbTiO3) family, with a radiation of wavelength of                344-400 nanometer (nm) in the presence of carbon                dioxide (CO2), at - 10-80 degrees C, to obtain a                modified perovskite or oxide compound. The modified                compound is heated at 350-550 degrees C to obtain                regenerated perovskite or oxide compound.
   USE - Method for photo-capturing carbon dioxide with                perovskite oxide compound of formula ABO3 or an                oxide compound of formula ABO2.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses light energy to improve the                carbonation of the oxide surfaces, shows that                chemical looping and efficient oxide carbonation                take place at high temperatures and in the present                approach it takes place at a low temperature range,                with the catalytic agent being the light energy,                and the engineering of the perovskite oxide                compounds. The method implements at a temperature                between - 10-80 degrees C, and is cyclable, i.e.                the perovskite oxide compounds can be regenerated                by heat and used for subsequent CO2 capture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The radiation is                  selected from UVA-LED, LED - Light Emitting                  Diodes (UV, white, visible, NIR), sunlight,                  monochromatized sunlight or filtered sunlight,                  concentrated sunlight (broad or capped) or                  concentrated artificial illumination, sunlight                  simulator light, Laser, pulsed sources,                  fluorescent lamp, incandescent lamps,                  gas-discharge lamp. The building materials are                  selected from building facades, indoor walls,                  pavers or road surfaces, paint, cement, concrete,                  asphalt, masonry, ceramic, brick, clay, wood,                  stone, plastic, metal, polymer, glass. Preferred                  Conditions: The radiation has a wavelength of 365                  nm. The illumination step occurs at 20-30 degrees                  C for 1-48 hours. The heating step lasts between                  15 minutes and 5 hours. The perovskite oxide                  compound or oxide compound is in the form of thin                  films and sponge. The perovskite oxide compound                  or oxide compound is in the form of porous                  nanoparticles, molecular cages or other                  nanostructures categorized as (i) 0D, (ii) 1D,                  (iii) 2D, and (iv) 3D nanomaterials. The compound                  is applied on, incorporated on, or mixed with,                  building materials.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
MC E11-P; E11-Q02C; E31-N05C; E35-J; E35-K04; E35-L; J01-E02B; A11-C03; A12-H; A12-R01; A12-W11K

PT P
PN CN112919455-A; CN112919455-B
TI Preparing graphene film by carbon dioxide combined            low pressure chemical vapor deposition comprises            cleaning the metal substrate and placing into a            reaction furnace for vacuumizing and performing high            temperature annealing treatment.
AU LI X
   ZHAO B
   ZHANG Q
   YANG X
   WANG R
   ZHOU T
   WANG C
   YUAN L
AE CHIA TAI ENERGY MATERIALS DALIAN LTD (CHIA-Non-standard)
GA 2021658400
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing graphene film by carbon dioxide                combined low pressure chemical vapor deposition                comprises cleaning the metal substrate and placing                into a reaction furnace for vacuumizing until the                vacuum degree is 2-6 torr or 300-800 pa,                introducing carbon dioxide and/or helium gas to                pre-treat the gas, performing high temperature                annealing treatment to the metal substrate after                gas pre-treatment, annealing at 900-1050 degrees C                in the mixed gas of hydrogen and argon gas, keeping                for 10-60 minutes, introducing gas carbon source                and hydrogen, keeping for 1-60 minutes at 900-1050                degrees C for chemical vapor deposition.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing graphene                film by carbon dioxide combined low pressure                chemical vapor deposition.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts carbon dioxide combined with                low pressure system to prepare graphene film, which                can eliminates potholes, bumps and pores on the                surface of graphene film in normal pressure                experiments while improving the quality of                graphene, the source of carbon is wide; is suitable                for industrial production and is economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal substrate is                  at least one of copper foil, cobalt foil, nickel                  foil, and iron foil, or copper film, cobalt film,                  nickel film and iron film. The hydrogen flow in                  the high temperature annealing treatment is                  40-400 sccm and the argon flow is 100-800 sccm                  and hydrogen and argon gas flow ratio is                  (1:4)-(1:40). The gas carbon source is at least                  one of methane, ethane, ethylene, acetylene,                  propane and carbon dioxide. The gas carbon source                  is one of methane and carbon dioxide or the mixed                  gas of the two. The flow ratio of carbon dioxide,                  methane and hydrogen in the chemical vapor                  deposition is (1-20):(1-50):(1-1000). The                  hydrogen flow rate in the chemical vapor                  deposition process is 240 sccm, the flow rate of                  the methane is 20 sccm, and the flow rate of the                  carbon dioxide is 8 sccm. Preferred Method: The                  cleaning treatment method is: subjecting the                  metal substrate respectively with acetone,                  ethanol, isopropanol, hydrochloric acid, ammonium                  persulfate, acetic acid and de-ionized water for                  ultrasonic or dipping for 5-10 minutes, and                  removing the water on the surface of the metal                  substrate. The pre-treatment condition of the gas                  is: the flowrate of carbon dioxide or helium gas                  is 1-100 sccm when the single gas is pre-treated,                  and the flow ratio of the carbon dioxide to the                  helium gas is (1-100):(1-50) when the carbon                  dioxide and the helium gas are pre-treated; the                  temperature is 200-550 degrees C and the                  processing time is 10-60 minutes. The process of                  stopping introducing hydrogen and carbon source                  at the end of the growth of the graphene film and                  cooling to the room temperature is carried out at                  the same time by cutting into the argon                  gas.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

TI Indoor household intelligent ventilation system, has controller for controlling ventilation device to open ventilation function when indoor smoke concentration exceeds threshold value, where controller controls alarm device to alarm.
AU XIAO T
   ZOU K
AE UNIV CHINA ELECTRONIC SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UEST-C)
GA 202148628P
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide detector (101) for detecting carbon dioxide concentration of indoor air. A malodorous gas detector (102) detects malodorous gas concentration of indoor air. A smoke detector (103) detects indoor smoke concentration. A controller (104) receives detected carbon dioxide concentration of indoor air, malodorous gas concentration and smoke concentration of data. The controller controls a ventilation device (107) to open ventilation function when indoor smoke concentration exceeds a predetermined threshold value. The controller controls an acousto-optic alarm device (106) to alarm. The acousto-optic alarm device is selected as a TGSG06b acousto-optic alarm. The carbon dioxide detector is selected as a MH-Z19 carbon dioxide gas sensor. The smoke detector is selected as a CS-T4-B photoelectric smoke detector. The malodorous gas detector is selected as a JC-ADT-EFF type malodorous gas detector.

PN CN111729643-A
TI Preparation of modified coconut shell based adsorbent for separating methane/carbon dioxide used in coal-bed gas separation, by e.g. preparing potassium hydroxide solution, crushing coconut shell carbonized material, adding binder, mixing.
AU LIANG J
   LI X
   CHE Y
   GUO H
   CHEN L
AE CHINA COAL RES INST CO LTD (CHLY-C)
GA 202099284S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of modified coconut shell based adsorbent for separating methane/carbon dioxide, comprises (i) taking potassium hydroxide solid as modifier, adding water to prepare potassium hydroxide aqueous solution; crushing the coconut shell carbonized material, adding binder and potassium hydroxide aqueous solution, mixing uniformly, and kneading and molding to obtain adsorbent material strip, and drying; (ii) carbonizing the adsorbent material strip under the protection atmosphere; and (iii) carrying out water vapor physical activation treatment to the adsorbent material strip, and cooling.
   USE - The modified coconut shell based adsorbent for separating methane/carbon dioxide is useful in the field of coal-bed gas separation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent can realize saturated adsorption capacity for methane and carbon dioxide separation reach 2.34 mmol/g and 3.23 mmol/g, respectively; equilibrium separation coefficient reach 10.27, which is 190% higher than that under unmodified conditions; and has high application value and significance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a modified coconut shell based adsorbent for separating methane/carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of the coconut shell carbonized material, the binder, the potassium hydroxide modifier and water is 100:(35-45):2-10:15-25. In the step (i), the binder comprises high temperature coal tar. Preferred Method: The step (i) further comprises crushing the coconut shell carbonized material to the grain diameter of 150 mu m. In the step (i), the kneading and molding step is carried out under the pressure of 15-25 MPa, extruding and molding by the mold with the diameter of 3-5 mm. In the step (ii), the carbonization temperature is controlled to be 500-700 degrees C, and the carbonization time is 5-15 minutes. In the step (ii), the heating speed of the carbonizing step is controlled to be 3-8 degrees C/minute. In the step (iii) of the water vapor physical activation treatment step, controlling the volume flow of the steam at 8-12 ml/minute, controlling the activation temperature at 750-900 degrees C, the activation time is 20-55 minutes.

TI Combined catalyst used for producing xylene by coupled carbon dioxide hydrogenation and toluene alkylation, comprises molecular sieve, and zinc-zirconium oxide, zinc-chromium oxide, zinc-aluminum oxide, and/or chromium oxide.
AU YUAN Y
   ZUO J
   DUAN X
   YE L
   LIANG X
   LIN H
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
   UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
   UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 202011763K
AB    NOVELTY - A combined catalyst comprises a molecular sieve, and zinc-zirconium oxide, zinc-chromium oxide, zinc-aluminum oxide, and/or chromium oxide.
   USE - Combined catalyst is used for producing xylene by carbon dioxide hydrogenation and toluene alkylation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The combined catalyst enables replacement of methanol with carbon dioxide and hydrogen in xylene production, improves the production efficiency of xylene, inhibits isomerization of xylene, and improves the selectivity of p-xylene in the product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A combined catalyst comprises a molecular sieve, and zinc-zirconium oxide (ZnZrOx1), zinc-chromium oxide (ZnZrOx2), zinc-aluminum oxide (ZnAlOx3) and/or chromium oxide (CrOx4).
   x1 = greater than 1 and less than 2;and
   x2,x3,x4 = greater than 1 and less than 1.5.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) preparation of the combined catalyst, which involves mixing the metal oxide and molecular sieve; and
   (2) production of xylene, which involves placing the combined catalyst in a reducing atmosphere for activation, mixing the active catalyst with carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and toluene, and carrying out coupled carbon dioxide hydrogenation and toluene alkylation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molecular sieve is ZSM-5, MCM-22 and/or SAPO-34. Preferred Method: The mixing method involves grinding, ball milling, immersing, precipitation deposition, solvothermal reaction, coprecipitation, or molten salt mixing. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of metal oxide and molecular sieve is 1-9:1-9. The reducing atmosphere is a mixed gas of hydrogen and argon, or a mixed gas of hydrogen and nitrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1:1-8. The molar ratio of carbon dioxide and toluene is 1-30:2. Preferred Conditions: Activation is carried out at 200-600 degrees C for 0.5-12 hours. The space velocity of carbon dioxide is 300-6000 mL/g/hour. The reaction temperature in xylene production is 300-460 degrees C and pressure is 1-5 MPa.
