PT P
PN CN110699704-A; CN110699704-B
TI Preparing self-supporting silver sulfide-catalyzed            carbon dioxide reduction electrode material involves            using surface-pretreated material as matrix, and            performing vulcanization in prepared ionic            liquid-sulfur salt composite solution system.
AU ZHANG Q
   LEI H
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 202008383B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing self-supporting silver                sulfide-catalyzed carbon dioxide reduction                electrode material involves preparing low-eutectic                ionic liquid, adding sulfur salt precursor to the                eutectic ionic liquid, and stirring and mixing the                contents thoroughly to obtain an ionic liquid                sulfur salt composite solution system. The silver                substrate is immersed with dilute sulfuric acid,                absolute ethanol, dilute sulfuric acid, and                deionized water in order to clean the surface of                the substrate. The obtained surface-pretreated                material is used as a matrix, and vulcanization is                performed in the ionic liquid-sulfur salt composite                solution system. The vulcanized substrate is                repeatedly washed with anhydrous ethanol and                deionized water, and a silver sulfide electrode                material is obtained after drying.
   USE - Method for preparing self-supporting silver                sulfide-catalyzed carbon dioxide reduction                electrode material (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare self-supporting                silver sulfide-catalyzed carbon dioxide reduction                electrode material that has excellent catalytic                carbon dioxide reduction activity and is suitable                for aqueous solutions and ionic liquid media. The                silver sulfide material has simple preparation                method, mild conditions, low cost, and easy scale                industrialization.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sulfur salt                  precursor is thiourea or sodium sulfide.                  Preferred Conditions: The eutectic ionic liquid                  is a Reline system of a choline chloride-urea                  combination, an Ethaline system of a choline                  chloride-ethylene glycol combination, and a                  Glyceline system of a choline chloride-glycerol                  combination. The molar ratio of choline chloride                  and urea in the choline chloride-urea eutectic                  ionic liquid is 1: 2. The molar ratio of choline                  chloride and ethylene glycol in the choline                  chloride-ethylene glycol eutectic ionic liquid is                  1: 2. The choline chloride-glycerol molar ratio                  in the choline chloride-glycerol eutectic ionic                  liquid is 1: 2. The sulfur salt precursor content                  in the ionic liquid sulfur salt composite                  solution system is 0.1 mM to 1M. The                  concentration of dilute sulfuric acid is 2M, the                  concentration of anhydrous ethanol is 95%, and                  the soaking time is 10-20 minutes. The                  vulcanization is performed at 273-373 K for 5                  minutes-12 hours, preferably at 353 K for 2                  hours.
DC J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PN CN109569285-A; CN109569285-B
TI Nitrogen-doped graphene-based electrocatalyst is useful for reducing carbon dioxide in electrocatalytic reduction heterogeneous system comprises structure comprising nitrogen-doped calcined hydrotalcite as matrix.
AU LI W
   KANG P
AE TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
   UNIV CHINESE ACAD SCI (UCAS-C)
   TECH INST PHYSICS & CHEM CHINESE ACAD (CAPC-C)
GA 201933625X
AB    NOVELTY - Nitrogen-doped graphene-based electrocatalyst comprises a structure in which a nitrogen-doped calcined hydrotalcite is used as a matrix, where the substrate is loaded with graphene.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for reducing carbon dioxide in electrocatalytic reduction heterogeneous system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrocatalyst: has layered structure; catalyzes the reduction of carbon dioxide in the heterogeneous system to carbon monoxide; and has high selectivity, stability and high catalytic efficiency for carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the nitrogen-doped graphene-based electrocatalyst comprising mixing a salt solution of aluminum and a salt solution of nickel and/or zinc, adding urea and graphene oxide dispersion, uniformly mixing to obtain a mixed liquid, subjecting the liquid to microwave digestion reaction, washing, drying to obtain a precursor, grinding the precursor into a powder, and calcining the powdered precursor under a reducing atmosphere; and
   (2) taking the nitrogen-doped graphene-based electrocatalyst as electrocatalyst, and performing chronograph current analysis on an aqueous solution of sodium chloride electrolyte saturated with carbon dioxide gas in a potential range of - 0.7-- 1.2 V with respect to the reversible hydrogen electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The calcined hydrotalcite is nickel-zinc-aluminum hydrotalcite, nickel-aluminum hydrotalcite and zinc-aluminum hydrotalcite. The mixed liquid comprising a salt solution of aluminum, a salt solution of nickel and a salt solution of zinc, where the equivalent ratio of the nickel element and the zinc element in the liquid is 2:1-1:2. The equivalent ratio of the total amount of nickel element and zinc element, and the aluminum element in the mixed solution is 3:1-4:1. The equivalent ratio of the total amount of the metal elements in the mixed solution and urea is 1:3-1:5. The mass ratio of the total amount of the metal elements in the mixed liquid and the graphite oxide in the graphene oxide dispersion of the alkene is 6:1-8:1. The reducing atmosphere is an ammonia gas atmosphere. Preferred Method: The microwave digestion reaction is carried out by heating the liquid to 150-180 degrees C, preferably 150-160 degrees C for 25-40 minutes, preferably 25-30 minutes. The calcination is carried out by heating to 300-500 degrees C, preferably 300-400 degrees C with a rate of 1-4 degrees C/minute, preferably 1-3 degrees C/minute for 1-3 hours, preferably 2-3 hours.

AB    NOVELTY - Producing a poly(5-membered cycloaliphatic carbonate) comprises reacting a suspension of a powdered sugar alcohol in a source of carbon dioxide and a catalyst compound which is soluble in the source of carbon dioxide at the reaction temperature.
   USE - The process is useful for producing a poly(5-membered cycloaliphatic carbonate) which is useful for producing polyurethane formed without isocyanate and/or polycarbonate and/or polyethers (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is simple; and increases total output of the reaction by reducing the probability of formation of by-products and by decreasing the potential azeotrope formation between the source of carbon dioxide and natural additional product of the reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) the poly(5-membered cycloaliphatic carbonate) obtained by the process; and
   (2) producing polyurethane formed without isocyanate and/or polycarbonate and/or polyethers, comprising obtaining the poly(5-membered cycloaliphatic carbonate) and mixing the poly(5-membered cycloaliphatic carbonate) with a compound comprising at least one amine function or an alcohol function.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The powdered sugar alcohol is: a crystalline sugar alcohol; and erythritol, arabitol, xylitol, ribitol, D-sorbitol, dulcitol, D-mannitol, volemitol, maltitol, isomalt and/or lactitol, preferably powdered D-sorbitol. The source of carbon dioxide is a dimethyl carbonate (preferred), a diethyl carbonate, a diphenyl carbonate, dibenzyl carbonate, propylene carbonate, carbonate d ethylene, 1,2-butylene carbonate, glycerol carbonate, and/or 4,5-dimethyl, 3-dioxolan-2-one. The catalyst is a basic catalyst, preferably an organic basic catalyst. The powdered sugar alcohol is a powdered D-sorbitol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The reaction temperature is 20-250 degrees C, preferably 30-200 degrees C. The source of carbon dioxide is added by continuous feed. The process further comprises: precipitating the poly(5-membered cycloaliphatic carbonate) in an aqueous composition, optionally, washing the obtained precipitated poly(5-membered cycloaliphatic carbonate) and recovering the poly(5-membered cycloaliphatic carbonate) by filtration and/or drying of the obtained precipitate. Preferred Components: The poly(5-membered cycloaliphatic carbonate) is a bis(5-membered cycloaliphatic carbonate) (preferred) or tri(cycloaliphatic carbonate).
   EXAMPLE - In a flask having a distillation bridge or Vigreux fractionating columns or a refluxing device, a molar equivalent of D-sorbitol was charged, and 5% molar equivalent of catalyst e.g. sodium hydroxide, relative to the D-sorbitol weight and 3.5-18 molar equivalents of dimethyl carbonate, relative to D-sorbitol. The mixture was stirred and heated at 75 degrees C for 16-48 hours. At the end, the mixture was cooled to room temperature and 10 ml of distilled water was added to precipitate the product. The reaction products were filtered through a 0.45 mu m polyvinylidene fluoride membrane and washed with distilled water and then dried under vacuum at 50 degrees C overnight to obtain biscyclocarbonate.

PN CN108458512-A; CN208091001-U
TI Carbon dioxide air source heat pump system, has absorption heat interchanger provided with absorption-type heat pump machine set and heat exchanger, and machine set and exchanger whose absorber and condenser are utilized for heating water.
AU YIN H
   HU Y
AE CHINA RAILWAY ENG CONSULTING GROUP CO LT (CREN-C)
GA 2018691097
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a R744 compressor (1) orderly connected with an absorption heat interchanger (2), an electromagnetic valve (3), an outdoor evaporator (4) and a R744 gas-liquid separator (5), where the absorption heat interchanger is provided with an absorption-type heat pump machine set (21) and a heat exchanger (22). The R744 compressor compresses supercritical carbon dioxide gas to pass through a generator and an evaporator of the absorption type heat pump machine set. An absorber of the absorption-type heat pump machine set and a condenser of the heat exchanger heats return water in a water side pipe.
   USE - Carbon dioxide air source heat pump system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system utilizes an absorption-type heat exchanger, and ensures condensing temperature of a single-stage carbon dioxide system trans-critical circulating system limited to water side inlet temperature.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a hydraulic circuit diagram of a carbon dioxide air source heat pump system.
   R744 compressor (1)
   Absorption heat interchanger (2)
   Electromagnetic valve (3)
   Outdoor evaporator (4)
   R744 gas-liquid separator (5)
   Absorption-type heat pump machine set (21)
   Heat exchanger (22)

PN US2018064569-A1; US10675165-B2
TI Intragastric balloon system for treating obesity,            comprises composite wall structure configured to            maintain preset carbon dioxide leak rate, supporting            film structure, and carbon dioxide barrier layers.
AU BRISTER M C
   PATEL K A
   RASDAL A P
   QUINTANA N
   DRAKE N R
   LLEVARES A C
   MARKOVIC D
   VANDENBERG A D L
AE OBALON THERAPEUTICS INC (OBAL-Non-standard)
GA 201818889E
AB    NOVELTY - An intragastric balloon system comprises                composite wall structure configured to maintain                carbon dioxide leak rate of 40 cc/m2/day or more at                37 degrees C, supporting film structure having                Young's Modulus of 500 MPa or more, a carbon                dioxide barrier layer (L1) configured in which                barrier properties are not affected by exposure to                humidity levels within the gastric environment or                central lumen of the balloon and a carbon dioxide                barrier layer (L2) configured in which barrier                properties are not affected by mechanical forces                during processing, compacting, or application of                external gastric pressures.
   USE - Intragastric balloon system for treating                obesity.
   ADVANTAGE - The intragastric balloon system provides                structural integrity and mechanisms to reliably                control timing of self-inflation such that                premature inflation in the esophagus during                swallowing is avoided and ensures desired inflation                once in the stomach so as to prevent passage                through the pyloric sphincter.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method                for fabricating composite wall.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of                components of a swallowable, self-inflating                intragastric balloon system.
   Outer container (447)
   Vent hole (448)
   Septum (449)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The carbon dioxide                  barrier layer (L1) comprises a silicon dioxide                  layer having a thickness of 100-800 Angstrom .                  The carbon dioxide barrier layer (L2) comprises                  an ethylene vinyl alcohol layer having a                  thickness of at least 2 mu m. The thickness of                  the composite wall structure is 2.5 mu m or less.                  The silicon dioxide layer is provided as a                  coating on one side of the polyethylene                  terephthalate layer. Preferred Components: The                  supporting film structure comprises a                  polyethylene terephthalate layer. The                  intragastric balloon system, further comprises an                  inner container within the central lumen of the                  balloon, the inner container containing 0.28-4 g                  inflation agent comprising up to 80 wt.% powdered                  citric acid, and remainder of powdered sodium                  bicarbonate.

PN CN114712027-A
TI Large-environment experimental small animal carbon            dioxide euthanais device, has case provided with            ventilating pipe and carbon dioxide concentration            detector, and pressing mechanism fixedly installed on            sealing plate and extending into placing groove, where            pipe is connected with lifter.
AU FENG J
AE SUZHOU FENGSHI LAB ANIMAL EQUIP CO LTD (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 202292924A
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a machine case (1) whose inner                portion is formed with a sealing cavity. The case                is hinged with a box door and provided with a                ventilating pipe and a carbon dioxide concentration                detector (12). The pipe is connected to a lifter                (4) i.e. electric cylinder. The lifter is fixedly                connected to the case through a mounting plate (5).                A placing groove (6) is formed in the case and                located below the lifter. A sealing plate (7) is                installed at a lower end of the lifter, and covers                the groove and forms a sealing cavity in the                groove. First exhaust fan (8) is installed on the                mounting plate and connected with the pipe. Second                exhaust fan (9) is installed on the sealing plate.                The second exhaust fan communicates an inner                portion of the groove and the inner portion of the                case. An air supply mechanism communicates an outer                portion of the case and the inner portion of the                groove. A pressing mechanism (11) is fixedly                installed on the sealing plate, and extends into                the groove.
   USE - Large-environment experimental small animal                carbon dioxide euthanais device.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide concentration detector                continuously detects carbon dioxide concentration                in the placing groove. The device can close an                electromagnetic valve for directly filling carbon                dioxide in the placing groove when the carbon                dioxide concentration in the placing groove reaches                a preset value, thus effectively reducing                utilization amount of the carbon dioxide and saving                processing cost. The device avoids violent                struggling generating phenomenon during small                animal suffocation process and noise generating                phenomenon to ensure better experience range of an                operator. The device reduces animal suffocation                death rate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a                large-environment experimental small animal carbon                dioxide euthanais device in partial section.

AB    NOVELTY - The system has a freezing medium supply device fixed into an injection drilling hole through a pipeline (6), where the freezing medium supply device, a dissolving medium supply device and a displacement medium supply device orderly inject carbon dioxide liquid, hot steam and gaseous carbon dioxide into the injection drilling hole. A gas extracting pipeline (13) is connected into an extraction borehole, where the gas extracting pipeline works to extract gas when injecting gaseous carbon dioxide. The freezing medium supply device is provided with a liquid carbon dioxide tank.
   USE - System for increasing permeability of coal seam and extracting gas by freezing and thawing of carbon dioxide hot steam.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves permeability of a coal layer so as to increase efficiency of gas extraction, and has enhanced coal layer cracking effect. The coal layer anti-reflection process and gas extraction process continuity is improved, ensures that generated water can have function of reducing dust and avoiding dust of coal layer affecting the extraction of gas after high-temperature water vapor cooling. The back pressure valve prevents gas in the gas extraction pipeline from flowing into a coal bed. The freezing medium supply device injects a freezing medium into the injection drilling hole, and a dissolving medium is injected into an injection hole, using cold and hot impact and multiple cracking coal layers.

TI Method for capture of carbon dioxide with            co-generation or chemicals involves reacting carbonate            solution with halogenated compound to form metal salts            and output a stream is carbon dioxide, reacting metal            salts with water under electrolysis conditions to            regenerate the alkali solution.
AU PALMER M R
   FORREST B A
   LU X
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 202248323H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for capture of carbon dioxide with                co-generation or chemicals, which involves: i.                contacting a gaseous carbon dioxide containing                stream in a scrubbing unit with an alkali solution                to form a carbonate solution and output a carbon                dioxide lean gas stream; ii. reacting the carbonate                solution with a halogenated compound to form metal                salts and output a stream comprising carbon                dioxide; iii. reacting metal salts with water under                electrolysis conditions to regenerate the alkali                solution and form further chemicals; and iv. using                further chemicals in additional reaction effective                to form electrical energy and/or heat.
   USE - Method for capture of carbon dioxide with                co-generation or one or more chemicals.
   ADVANTAGE - Cost of sulfuric acid is low enough that its                use would offer economic benefit to the direct air                capture process. Carbon dioxide produced by direct                air capture in turn be used in the sour gas                sweetening process, offering further efficiency                and/or economic advantages. Excess chloralkali                electrolyzer capacity is beneficial in improving                the overall operating and/or economic efficiency of                the system. Hydrogen and chlorine, produced                methanol or other hydrocarbon fuels, and/or the                thermal energy of the overall system is stored as a                means of energy load leveling for the overall                system and/or for the local electrical grid. This                is used to improve the overall system economics,                the economics of a renewable electricity generation                system, or the local grid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                for capture of carbon dioxide with co-generation or                one or more chemicals.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                systems and processes wherein a gas stream is                subjected to scrubbing with an alkali solution to                remove a moiety, such as carbon dioxide, and the                removed moiety is isolated with recycling of the                alkali solution and simultaneous production of one                or more further chemicals.
   Compression unit (10)
   Scrubbing unit (15)
   Expander (20)
   Generator (22)
   Crystallization unit (25)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Gaseous carbon dioxide                  containing stream is air and/or flue gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Alkali solution                  comprises solution of metal salts, metal is                  alkali metals and/or alkaline earth metals.                  Preferred Method: Subjecting at least a portion                  of the carbonate solution to a crystallization                  process effective to separate solids from the                  carbonate solution and output an alkali solution                  suitable for recycle back to the scrubbing unit.                  Processing the stream comprising carbon dioxide                  in a carbon dioxide purification unit configured                  to output a stream of substantially pure carbon                  dioxide. Compressing the gaseous carbon dioxide                  containing stream prior to passage into the                  scrubbing unit. Expanding the carbon dioxide lean                  gas stream to generate energy. Chemicals that are                  formed include hydrogen and a halogen gas.                  Additional reaction effective to form electrical                  energy and/or heat includes reacting at least a                  portion of the hydrogen with at least a portion                  of the halogen gas in a fuel cell to produce the                  electrical energy. Additional reaction effective                  to form electrical energy and/or heat includes                  reacting at least a portion of the hydrogen with                  at least a portion of the halogen gas to produce                  the heat. Heat to generate power in a steam                  cycle. Carbon dioxide output from reacting the                  carbonate solution with the halogenated compound                  in a reaction to form a fuel material. Reaction                  comprises reacting the carbon dioxide with                  hydrogen, at least a portion of which is                  optionally taken from a product of the reacting                  of the one or more metal salts with the water                  under the electrolysis conditions.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

TI Membrane-less reactor used in system, comprises            circular counter electrode, porous bio-electroactive            filter, electrolyte, sampling ports, and tubular gas            diffusion electrode containing active layer modified            with electroactive material.
AU KUMAR M
   SANDIPAM S
   PURI S K
   RAMAKUMAR S S V
   DIELS L
   BOUWMAN L
   VANBROEKHOVEN K
   PANT D
   BOWMAN L
   VANBROEKHOVAN K
AE INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   VITO VLAAMSE INSTELLING TECHNOLOGISCH (VLAA-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   VITO NV (VLAA-C)
   VITO VLAAMSE INSTELLING TECHNOLOGISCH (VLAA-C)
   VITO VLAAMSE INSTELLING TECHNOLOGISCH (VLAA-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   VITO VLAAMSE INSTELLING TECHNOLOGISCH (VLAA-C)
GA 202162739Q
AB    NOVELTY - A membrane-less reactor comprises a tubular                gas diffusion electrode (7), a circular counter                electrode (8), a porous bio-electroactive filter                (10), an electrolyte (9) and sampling ports. The                diffusion electrode contains an active layer                modified with an electroactive material as a                working electrode. The reactor is single chambered                and operated in batch, continuous or                semi-continuous mode in a continuous stirred tank                reactor or in a sequential batch reactor.
   USE - Membrane-less reactor used in system for                converting carbon dioxide into alcohol (all                claimed), fuels and other organic                commodities.
   ADVANTAGE - The reactor has excellent design, ensures                efficient transformation of carbon dioxide into                alcohols at a faster rate by single pot microbial                electrosynthesis, efficiently avoids oxygen contact                at working electrode without using membrane,                increases carbon dioxide solubility and                bioavailability, and makes downstream processing                easy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) system, which comprises a carbon dioxide                or flue gas supply reservoir, an electrolyte supply                reservoir, a trace metal solution (TMS) for                providing essential nutrients to microbial                metabolism, a microbial separation column (5), a                product separation column (6), and the                membrane-less reactor. The carbon dioxide supply                reservoir and the electrolyte supply reservoir are                connected to the reactor to supply carbon dioxide                and electrolyte. The microbial separation column                and the product separation column are connected to                the reactor through the electrolyte supply                reservoir; and
   (2) production of alcohol (4) using carbon                dioxide (11), which involves supplying a feed of                carbon dioxide and electrolyte to the membrane-less                reactor, converting carbon dioxide to alcohols,                passing the output from the reactor through the                microbial separation column to separate the used                microbe from the product stream, and passing the                product stream through the product separation                column to obtain alcohol. The electrolyte is                recycled from the product separation column back to                the electrolyte reservoir.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                reactor.
   Alcohol (4)
   Microbial separation column (5)
   Product separation column (6)
   Tubular gas diffusion electrode (7)
   Circular counter electrode (8)
   Electrolyte (9)
   Porous bio-electroactive filler (10)
   Carbon dioxide (11)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The biocatalyst is a                  microbe selected from electrochemically active                  bacteria (EAB), chemoautotrophic bacteria,                  heterotrophic bacteria and homoacetogenic                  bacteria.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The working electrode                  comprises a gas compartment towards inner core or                  towards outside. The active layer comprises an                  activated carbon powder and graphite in a ratio                  of 60:40, and electroactive materials in a                  concentration of 0.4-0.6 mg/cm2. The electrolyte                  comprises trace metal solution and carbon dioxide                  as carbon source. The counter electrode is made                  of titanium, nickel, or mixed metal oxide                  (ruthenium or iridium)-coated titanium. The                  working electrode and the counter electrode are                  positioned perpendicular to each other to avoid                  oxygen contact at the gas diffusion electrode.                  The working electrode is vertically arranged                  inside the reactor such that it remains                  completely immersed in the electrolyte with the                  active layer facing towards the electrolyte and                  gas diffusion layer facing towards inner side of                  cylinder without contacting the electrolyte. The                  counter electrode is positioned on top of the                  electrolyte. The reactor further comprises a                  biocatalyst for microbial electrosynthesis. The                  bio-electroactive filter is provided with the                  active layer of the gas diffusion electrode to                  develop an electro-active biofilm. Preferred                  Properties: The counter electrode is a circular                  disc containing perforated arrangement having                  wider holes and central cavity. Preferred                  Process: The working electrode is treated with                  polymerized redox mediators dissolved in the                  electrolyte at a concentration of 0.4-0.6 mM. The                  microbe is grown under 3 V cell potential for 5-7                  days before inoculation. The carbon dioxide                  feedstock is introduced into the membrane-less                  reactor by sparging continuously through a flow                  meter. Preferred Composition: The coating of                  highly active materials has a concentration of                  0.6-0.8 mg/cm2. The feedstock contains carbon                  dioxide or flue gas containing 14% carbon dioxide                  along with other pollutant gases. Preferred                  Conditions: The reactor is operated at 25-32                  degrees C and 2-10 bar by maintaining the flow                  rate of gas and potential of 1-4 V, preferably                  2-4V or applied current of 50-200 A/m2.

PT P
PN EP3604210-A1; WO2020025815-A1; EP3830022-A1; US2021292163-A1
TI Producing carbon dioxide and ammonia to produce substituted amide compounds used as fertilizer involves e.g. separation-enhanced water gas shift reaction of mixture comprising basic oxygen furnace gas and/or blast furnace gas and steam.
AU VENTE J F
   SFAKIANAKIS V
   COBDEN P D
AE NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
   NEDERLANDSE ORG TOEGEPAST NATUURWETENSCH (NEDE-C)
GA 2020097915
AB    NOVELTY - Producing carbon dioxide and ammonia for the production of substituted amide compouNH2-CO-NH2 comprises: (a) subjecting a mixture comprising (i) basic oxygen furnace gas and/or blast furnace gas and (ii) steam to a separation-enhanced water gas shift reaction to obtain a first product gas comprising hydrogen gas and nitrogen gas and a second product gas comprising carbon dioxide; (b) subjecting the first product gas to ammonia synthesis to obtain a product gas comprising ammonia; and (c) optionally subjecting at least part of the carbon dioxide originating from step (a) and at least part of the ammonia originating from step (b) to the synthesis of (I).
   USE - The process is useful for producing carbon dioxide and ammonia for the production of substituted amide compounds (I), preferably urea or ammonium carbamate, where the urea is used as fertilizer. No biological data given.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: utilizes gases that are considered waste or burned for fuel and diminishes the reliance on fossil fuels, hence greatly reduces carbon dioxide emissions, while providing any carbon dioxide in a sequestration-ready state without needing any further treatment, thus reducing the overall cost of carbon capture and sequestration in the steel industry; is economical as compared to conventional urea production; and provides a more environmentally friendly by use of BOF and BF gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing carbon dioxide and ammonia for the production of substituted amide compounds of formula (H2NC(O)X) (I), comprises: (a) subjecting a mixture comprising (i) basic oxygen furnace gas and/or blast furnace gas and (ii) steam to a separation-enhanced water gas shift reaction to obtain a first product gas comprising hydrogen gas and nitrogen gas and a second product gas comprising carbon dioxide; (b) subjecting the first product gas to ammonia synthesis to obtain a product gas comprising ammonia; and (c) optionally subjecting at least part of the carbon dioxide originating from step (a) and at least part of the ammonia originating from step (b) to the synthesis of (I).
   X = NH2 (preferred) or O-NH4+.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (c) is performed. The molar ratio of ammonia to carbon dioxide in the feed of step (c) is 2-4, by using all of the ammonia originating from step (b) and part of the carbon dioxide originating from step (a). The remainder of the second product gas originating from step (a) is subjected to carbon dioxide sequestering. In the process, when the first product gas originating from step (a) comprises carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide, the first product is subjected to methanation to convert carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide into methane, prior to being subjected to step (b). The hydrogen to nitrogen gas molar ratio in the first product stream that is subjected to step (b) is set to 3 by addition of hydrogen or nitrogen gas, where 0-10 mole% hydrogen is added to the first product gas prior to step (b), based on the molar content of hydrogen in the first product gas. The water gas shift reaction of step (a) is sorption-enhanced. The mixture comprises basic oxygen furnace (BOF) gas and/or blast furnace (BF) gas, where 0-15 wt.% or 50-100 wt.% is BF gas, and the remainder is BOF gas. A pre-shift step is performed prior to step (a), where the mixture is subjected to a water gas shift reaction that is not separation-enhanced before being subjected to step (a). The step (a) involves: (a1) contacting the incoming gas with a sorbent capable of adsorbing carbon dioxide, under conditions suitable to convert carbon monoxide and water in hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide, to obtain a first product gas comprising hydrogen gas and nitrogen gas, and a sorbent loaded with carbon dioxide; and (a2) regenerating the loaded sorbent to obtain a second product gas comprising carbon dioxide. The electricity needed to perform the process is obtained from renewable resources. The step (b) involves: (b1) providing a reaction mixture comprising the first product gas originating from step (a); (b2) subjecting the reaction mixture to the equilibrium reaction in a reactor or sequence of reactors, to obtain a reactor outlet mixture comprising ammonia and at least one of the reactants; (b3) regenerating the loaded sorbent obtained in step (b5) by flushing the loaded sorbent with the reactor outlet mixture originating from step (b2), to obtain regenerated sorbent and an effluent comprising desorbed product ammonia; (b4) separating the effluent originating from step (b3) into a product stream comprising ammonia and a reactant stream comprising nitrogen gas, hydrogen gas and ammonia; and (b5) subjecting the reactant stream originating from step (b4) and/or an intermediate reactor mixture which is obtained at the outlet of a reactor not being the last reactor of the reactor sequence, prior to being subjected to the subsequent reactor of the reactor sequence, to an sorption step by contacting with a sorbent selective for ammonia, where the sorbent is not capable of sorbing nitrogen gas and hydrogen, to obtain a sorbent loaded with ammonia, and a depleted mixture comprising nitrogen gas and hydrogen gas that in case step (b5) is performed on the intermediate reactor mixture, is subjected to the subsequent reactor of the reactor sequence.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN110982967-A; CN110982967-B
TI Achieving steel slag quenching and waste heat            recovery utilizing water and carbon dioxide comprises            mixing carbon dioxide and water vapor, injecting mixed            gas into fluidized bed, adding molten slag and turning            off gas injection system.
AU ZHANG H
   DONG J
   ZUO Q
   WEI C
   CAO C
   LIAO C
AE UNIV JIANGXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYJL-C)
GA 202031416F
AB    NOVELTY - Achieving steel slag quenching and waste heat                recovery utilizing water and carbon dioxide                comprises (i) mixing carbon dioxide and water vapor                to form a mixed gas, where the volume fraction of                carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 70-90%, and the                rest is water vapor and the pressure of the mixed                gas is 0.1-1 MPa; (ii) injecting mixed gas into the                fluidized bed heat exchanger through the gas                injection system and discharging from the gas                outlet of the fluidized bed heat exchanger; (iii)                adding molten metallurgical steel slag with a                temperature of 1350-1650 degrees C into the steel                slag chute, opening the outlet of the steel slag                chute; (iv) reacting calcium-containing components                in the steel slag particles exothermically with                carbon dioxide and water vapor; and (v) decreasing                the volume fraction of carbon dioxde as the                reaction proceeds; and (vi) turning off the gas                injection system when the temperature of the                material in the fluidized bed heat exchanger drops                below 150 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for achieving steel slag                quenching and waste heat recovery utilizing water                and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high recovery efficiency of                steel slag waste heat; improves good stability,                steel slag, small floor area, short treatment cycle                and low gas consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Achieving steel slag quenching and waste heat                recovery utilizing water and carbon dioxide                comprises (i) mixing carbon dioxide and water vapor                to form a mixed gas, where the volume fraction of                carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is 70-90%, and the                rest is water vapor and the pressure of the mixed                gas is 0.1-1 MPa; (ii) injecting mixed gas into the                fluidized bed heat exchanger through the gas                injection system and discharging from the gas                outlet of the fluidized bed heat exchanger to enter                the waste heat boiler, discharging from the waste                heat boiler and passing gas circulation pipe, where                gas circulation pipe is connected with the inlet of                the gas injection system, and the middle of the gas                circulation pipe is connected with the bottom of                the fluidized bed heat exchanger, returning part of                the gas returns to the gas injection system, the                rest of the gas enters the fluidized bed heat                exchanger from the bottom of the fluidized bed heat                exchanger, and blowing into the fluidized bed heat                exchanger through the air distribution plate at the                bottom above the wind plate, the air flow of the                wind plate is formed, where Mach number of the air                flow formed by the gas injection system is 0.1 less                than or equal to M less than or equal to 2; (iii)                adding molten metallurgical steel slag with a                temperature of 1350-1650 degrees C into the steel                slag chute, opening the outlet of the steel slag                chute to allow molten metallurgical steel slag to                flow out into the fluidized bed heat exchanger,                where the molten metallurgical steel slag contacts                the gas flow formed by the gas injection system for                gas quenching, and gradually shrinks into solid                steel slag particles; the steel slag particles are                in a suspended state under the action of the air                flow of the air distribution plate, the                high-temperature gas generated in the fluidized bed                heat exchanger enters the waste heat boiler from                the gas outlet, forming a low-temperature gas after                heat exchange by the waste heat boiler, and then                enters the gas circulation pipe; (iv) reacting                calcium-containing components in the steel slag                particles exothermically with carbon dioxide and                water vapor to form calcium carbonate when the                temperature of the steel slag particles in the                fluidized bed heat exchanger drops below 800                degrees C, where the sensible heat formed by the                exothermic reaction is absorbed by the gas in the                fluidized bed heat exchanger, enters the waste heat                boiler, and forms a low-temperature gas after heat                exchange by the waste heat boiler, and then enters                the gas circulation pipe; (v) decreasing the volume                fraction of carbon dioxde as the reaction proceeds,                when the temperature of the high-temperature gas                decreases to 450 plus minus 5 degrees C,                introducing carbon dioxide introduced into the                injection system, replenishing carbon dioxide                consumed by the exothermic reaction so that the                volume fraction of carbon dioxide in the mixed gas                entering the fluidized bed heat exchanger from the                injection system is 70-90%; and (vi) turning off                the gas injection system when the temperature of                the material in the fluidized bed heat exchanger                drops below 150 degrees C, where solid materials in                the fluidized bed heat exchanger are discharged to                form cooling slag particles. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM                is also included for device for implementing                achieving steel slag quenching and waste heat                recovery utilizing water and carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the device for implementing achieving steel slag                quenching and waste heat recovery utilizing water                and carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the steps (i) and (v),                  the temperature of carbon dioxide passed into the                  gas injection system is 100-120 degrees C, where                  the temperature of the mixed gas is 100-150                  degrees C. In the steps (iii) and (iv),the                  temperature of the low temperature gas is 100-150                  degrees C. The time from the outflow of molten                  metallurgical steel slag into the fluidized bed                  heat exchanger and the closing of the gas                  injection system is 2-8 hours. Preferred                  Components: The shape of the cooling slag                  particles is spherical or ellipsoid and the                  particle size is 10 mu m to 4 mm. The gas                  circulating in the gas circulation pipe, the                  amount entering the fluidized bed heat exchanger                  from the bottom of the fluidized bed heat                  exchanger and subjecting to control that the                  solid materials in the fluidized bed heat                  exchanger are in a suspended state.

PN WO2018140326-A2; WO2018140326-A3
TI Hydrogenating carbon dioxide to methane, comprises            conditioning and exposing rhodium photocatalyst to            hydrogen gas and carbon dioxide gas and illuminating            the rhodium photocatalyst with blue light or UV            light.
AU LIU J
   YANG W
   ZHANG X
   LI L X
AE UNIV DUKE (UDUK-C)
GA 201860372D
AB    NOVELTY - Hydrogenating carbon dioxide to methane,                comprises: providing a rhodium photocatalyst;                conditioning the rhodium photocatalyst; exposing                the rhodium photocatalyst to hydrogen gas and                carbon dioxide gas; and illuminating the rhodium                photocatalyst with blue light or ultraviolet                light.
   USE - The method is useful for hydrogenating carbon                dioxide to methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the rhodium nanoparticles                reduces activation energies for carbon dioxide                hydrogenation by 35% below thermal activation                energies while producing strong selectivity towards                methane or carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: An intensity of the blue                  light or the UV light is sufficient for causing a                  reaction rate for the production of methane to                  have a super-linear dependence on the intensity.                  The step of providing a rhodium photocatalyst                  further comprises forming the rhodium nano-cubes                  via a slow-injection polyol method, determining a                  preferred edge length, or preferred range of edge                  length, for rhodium nano-cubes based on the                  wavelength of the ultraviolet or blue light that                  is illuminating the rhodium photocatalyst and                  forming the rhodium nano-cubes to have the                  preferred length or a length in the preferred                  range of length. The step of forming the rhodium                  nano-cubes further comprises forming the rhodium                  nano-cubes via a slow-injection polyol method.                  The rhodium nano-cube precursor is injected into                  a potassium bromide solution. The rhodium                  photocatalyst comprises a metal oxide for                  supporting the rhodium nanoparticles. The step of                  conditioning the rhodium catalyst further                  comprises reducing the catalyst and activating                  the catalyst. The method is conducted at 25-450                  degrees C. Preferred Components: The blue light                  or ultraviolet light is illuminated with an                  intensity of 1-10 Watts per square centimeter.                  The blue light has wavelength of not longer than                  460 nm. The wavelength of the ultraviolet light                  is not shorter than 350 nm. The rhodium                  photocatalyst comprises rhodium nanoparticles.                  The rhodium nanoparticles are in a form of                  rhodium nanocubes that have an edge length of                  1-100 nm preferably 30-40 nm or 45-55 nm. The                  metal oxide is aluminum oxide powder or titanium                  oxide powder. The rhodium nanoparticles have a                  mass loading of 0.5-2 wt.% on the metal                  oxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN114100619-A
TI New methane carbon dioxide reforming catalyst prepared by e.g. pouring citric acid into deionized water, dropping mixed aqueous solution of lanthanum(III) nitrate, cerium(III) nitrate and nickel(II) nitrate, and mixing with aqueous nitrate solution of alkali metal or alkaline earth metal.
AU XUE H
   JIANG J
   WANG H
   BAI Z
   ZHAO Q
   YU H
   WANG M
AE QILU BRANCH CO CHINA PETROCHEMICAL CORP (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 202237914G
AB    NOVELTY - Methane carbon dioxide reforming catalyst (I), is new.
   USE - Used as methane carbon dioxide reforming catalyst
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: can avoid the generation of carbon deposits in the methane-carbon dioxide reaction process; at the same time high stability; and can avoid deactivation. The method: is simple and easy preparation method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Methane carbon dioxide reforming catalyst of formula (A/LaxCe1-xNiO3) (I), is new.
   A = alkali metal (Na or K) or alkaline earth metal (Mg, Ca, Ba or Sr); and
   x = 0-1.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparation of (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I) comprises (1) pouring citric acid into deionized water, and mixing to form solution, (2) dropping the mixed aqueous solution of lanthanum(III) nitrate, cerium(III) nitrate and nickel(II) nitrate respectively into the solution prepared in step (1), and mixing to form sol, (3) heating the sol prepared in step (2), and evaporating water, and making it gradually become gel, (4) drying the gel obtained in step (3) to obtain fluffy solid, (5) calcining the solid obtained by drying in step (4) to obtain LaxCe1-xNiO, (6) mixing aqueous nitrate solution of alkali metal or alkaline earth metal and LaxCe1-xNiO obtained in step (5) to obtain suspension, and subjecting the suspension to rotary evaporation so that the alkali metal or alkaline earth metal is dispersed on the surface of LaxCe1-xNiO3, and (7) drying and calcining the solid obtained in step (6) to obtain final product.Preferred Method: In step (3), the temperature is raised to 60-80degrees Celsius. In step (4), drying is carried out under the condition of 80-150degrees Celsius. In step (5), calcination is carried out under the condition of 600-1300degrees Celsius. In step (6), the suspension is subjected to rotary evaporation on a rotary evaporator. In step (7), drying is performed at 80-120degrees Celsius, and calcined at 350-550degrees Celsius. In the catalyst prepared in step (7), alkali metal or alkaline earth metal amount is 0.1-0.5% of the catalyst mass.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN JP2020165276-A
TI Absorption method of carbon dioxide, involves absorbing carbon dioxide from surface of porous concrete pavement into porous concrete pavement having multi-layer structure consisting of porous concrete layer, impermeable layer, and roadbed.
AU HYODO H
AE TAIHEIYO CEMENT CORP (ONOD-C)
GA 202097940P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves absorbing carbon dioxide from the surface of the porous concrete pavement into a porous concrete pavement having a multi-layer structure consisting of a porous concrete layer, an impermeable layer, and a roadbed in order. The impermeable layer is air permeable, and the roadbed is a concrete waste material containing calcium hydroxide or calcium silicate hydrate.
   USE - Absorption method of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide is efficiently absorbed using porous concrete pavement that does not contain reinforcing bars, in which active utilization of the carbonation / neutralization phenomenon is not an obstacle.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the porous concrete road-surface. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); Q41 (Road, rail, bridge construction (E01))
MC J01-E02B; Q41-B50
IP B01D-053/14; E01C-007/14

PT P
PN CN111348649-A
TI Preparation of activated carbon involves using oxygen production equipment in cement production, storing nitrogen and carbon dioxide captured from cement kiln flue gas, heating, carbonizing, oxidizing, and removing volatile components.
AU ZHANG Z
   XUAN H
   WANG B
   LI Z
   ZHANG T
AE ANHUI CONCH DESIGN & RES INST BUILDING (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202063037U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of activated carbon comprises using oxygen production equipment in cement production, storing nitrogen separated during the production process, and connecting nitrogen storage tank to carbonization furnace; storing carbon dioxide captured from cement kiln flue gas and connecting carbon dioxide storage tank to activation furnace; feeding carbon-containing materials and nitrogen into carbonization furnace, heating, and carbonizing to remove non-carbon elements to prepare carbonized materials; feeding prepared carbonized material and carbon dioxide into activation furnace, oxidizing using carbon dioxide as oxidant at high temperature, partially oxidizing carbon atoms in graphite crystallites, and removing volatile components from the graphite crystallites, and a rich pore structure.
   USE - The method is used for preparing activated carbon.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PT P
PN US2019321785-A1; KR2034765-B1; DE102019110192-A1; US11027241-B2
TI Method for post-treating zeolite membrane for            separating carbon dioxide form mixture, involves            healing selectively defects in zeolite membrane by            treating defective zeolite membrane with chemical            material.
AU CHOI J
   LEE K Y
   HONG S
   LEE K
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 201988234C
AB    NOVELTY - A zeolite membrane post-treating method                involves healing selectively defects in the zeolite                membrane by treating a defective zeolite membrane                with a chemical material having a size larger than                the pores of the zeolite membrane and smaller than                the defects in the zeolite membrane.
   USE - Method for post-treating zeolite membrane for                separating carbon dioxide form mixture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables post-treating zeolite                membrane with improved performance, high separation                factor, in simple and convent manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for separating carbon dioxide form mixture,                which involves utilizing a zeolite membrane to                separate carbon dioxide, where the mixture                comprises carbon dioxide and molecule comprising                methane, nitrogen, oxygen, ethylene, ethane,                propene and propane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chemical material                  is dye. The dye comprises anthraquinone dye,                  triarylmethane dye, xanthene dye, cyanine dye,                  phthalocyanine dye, diarylmethane dye, acridine                  dye, azine dye, thiazine dye, oxazine dye,                  azomethine dye and azo dye. The xanthene dye                  comprises eosin-based dye, fluorescein-based dye,                  rhodamine-based dye, pyronine-based dye,                  calcein-based dye and rosamine-based dye. The dye                  is fluorescein sodium, martius yellow,                  (2'-(4-hydroxyphenyl)-5-(4-methyl-1-piperazinyl)-2,5'-bi(1H-benzimidazole)                  trihydrochloride), stilbene,                  (4-(dicyanomethylene)-2-methyl-6-(4-dimethylaminostyryl)-4H-pyran)                  or oxazole yellow. The fluorescein-based dye is                  fluorescein sodium salt.
DC E23 (Heterocyclic.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC E10-J02C3; E10-J02D1; E10-J02D2; E11-Q01B; E11-Q02C; E12-A04; E21-C10; E21-C15; E22-C; E23-B; E25; E31-D01; E31-H03; E31-N05C; E31-P02B; J01-E03E

PN KR2019050512-A; KR2029392-B1
TI Preparing a metal oxide based high temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent involves dispersing additive selected from zirconium dioxide, silicon dioxide, calcium zirconate and calcium silicate and metal oxide precursor in distilled water.
AU LEE K B
   YOON H J
   LEE K Y
   LEE G B
   YOON H
   LEE K
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 201944938C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a metal oxide based high temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent involves dispersing an additive selected from the group consisting of zirconium dioxide, silicon dioxide, calcium zirconate and calcium silicate, and a metal oxide precursor in distilled water, adding a gelling agent to prepare a mixture, heating the mixture to form a gel, drying and then calcining the gel.
   USE - Method for preparing a metal oxide based high temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare metal oxide based high temperature carbon dioxide adsorbent which has high thermal stability and can repetitively absorb carbon dioxide at high temperature without the degradation of the performance of the adsorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide is a mixture of calcium oxide (CaO), magnesium oxide (MgO), calcium oxide, and magnesium oxide. The additive is selected from the group consisting of zirconium dioxide, silicon dioxide, calcium zirconate and calcium silicate is bonded to the metal oxide. Preferably, the additive is zirconium dioxide, metal oxide is calcium oxide, and the structure includes a structure of calcium zirconate type.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gelling agent is selected from the group consisting of citric acid, ethylene glycol, and glycerol. Preferred Conditions: The heating temperature of the mixture is 100-120 degrees C. The drying temperature of the gel is 100-140 degrees C. The molar ratio of the metal oxide to the additive is from 3:1 to 50:1. The dried gel is calcined at 600-1000 degrees C. The adsorbent exhibits peaks at 332/cm, 357/cm, 440/cm and 546/cm when analyzed by laser Raman spectroscopy.

PN CN108424759-A; CN108424759-B
TI Carbon dioxide foam fracturing fluid comprises            foam containing carbon dioxide, and liquid comprising            thickener, foaming agent, crosslinker and water.
AU JIA Z
   QU C
   WANG Y
AE SICHUAN SHENHE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (SICH-Non-standard)
   SICHUAN SHENHE NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (SICH-Non-standard)
GA 201867336L
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide foam fracturing fluid which is                resistant to high temperatures of 110 degrees C                comprises 20-55 wt.% liquids; and 45-80 wt.% foams.                The gas in foam is carbon dioxide. The liquid                comprises 0.20-0.45 wt.% thickener, 0.01-0.04 wt.%                foaming agent, 0.3-0.8 wt.% crosslinker, and                balance water.
   USE - Carbon dioxide foam fracturing fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluid has extremely low residue content                after oil and gas production, small surface                tension, and low cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of the fracturing liquid comprising                weighing thickener, foaming agent and crosslinking                agent; slowly adding thickener to water while                stirring, stirring for 5-10 minutes, continue                adding foaming agent while stirring, and stirring                for 3-5 minutes; and adding crosslinking agent to                base liquid, and stirring for 5-8 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The liquid comprises                  0.20-0.35 wt.%, preferably 0.35-0.45 wt.%                  thickener; 0.01-0.025 wt.%, preferably 0.025-0.04                  wt.% foaming agent; 0.3-0.6 wt.%, preferably                  0.6-0.8 wt.% crosslinker; and balance water. The                  foaming agent comprises 40-60 wt.% hexadecyl                  dimethyl tertiary amine, 5-15 wt.% dimethyl                  sulfate, 0.5-2 wt.% initiator, 2-5 wt.% acetic                  acid, and balance glycol. The crosslinking agent                  comprises 5-10 wt.% ethylene glycol, 3-5 wt.%                  caustic soda and 20-25 wt.% anionic surfactant,                  and balance water.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The thickener                  comprises 15-30 wt.% acrylamide, 15-30 wt.%                  sodium acrylate, 2-7 wt.% soda ash, 0.05-0.40                  wt.% initiator, 4-8 wt.% anionic functional                  monomer methacrylic acid, 2.5-4.5 wt.%                  vinylpyrrolidone, and balance water. Preferred                  Method: The thickness is prepared by adding                  acrylamide into water, dissolving acrylate,                  adding sodium carbonate, anionic functional                  monomer methacrylic acid and vinylpyrrolidone,                  cooling to room temperature, slowly adding                  initiator of potassium persulfate, circulating                  cold water, cooling, reacting for 6 hours, drying                  and granulating. The foaming agent is prepared by                  adding hexadecyl dimethyl tertiary amine and                  dimethyl sulfate in glycol, mixing, adding acetic                  acid, mixing, heating to 45 degrees C, slowly                  adding initiator of ammonium persulfate, reacting                  for 5 hours, and cooling.

PN CN108285164-A
TI Calcium sulfate and calcium carbonate separation            involves pulverizing and mixing waste with water to            form suspension, reacting obtained suspension with            carbon dioxide, and then reacting obtained suspension            with carbon dioxide.
AU JI F
   CHEN X
AE JI F (JIFF-Individual)
GA 201857040R
AB    NOVELTY - Calcium sulfate and calcium carbonate                separation involves pulverizing and mixing waste                with water to form a suspension, reacting the                obtained suspension with carbon dioxide to lower pH                of the suspension, and then reacting the obtained                suspension with carbon dioxide. The obtained                suspension is allowed to stand, and then separated                into a precipitate and a clear liquid. The obtained                precipitate is collected and heated to discharge                carbon dioxide. The obtained clear liquid is heated                to obtain the precipitate.
   USE - Method for separating calcium sulfate and                calcium carbonate from waste.
   ADVANTAGE - The method separates the calcium sulfate and                calcium carbonate in a simple, cost-effective and                eco-friendly manner with high-purity calcium                sulfate and improved recovery rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The waste comprises                  58.2-75.4% calcium oxide and calcium carbonate,                  23.5-39.5% calcium sulfate, and 1.1-2.3% metal                  oxide, where the metal oxide is silicon oxide,                  iron oxide, magnesium oxide, and aluminum oxide.                  The waste is pulverized into particles having a                  particle diameter of 2 mm or less.

PN CN107620089-A; CN107620089-B
TI Reducing carbon dioxide to methanol on delafossite/CuInS2 composite semiconductor thin film electrode involves preparing delafossite/CuInS2 composite semiconductor film by depositing delafossite quantum dots on surface of CuInS2 thin film.
AU YUAN J
   GU C
   DING W
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
   UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2018088242
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing carbon dioxide to methanol on a delafossite/CuInS2 composite semiconductor thin film electrode involves preparing delafossite/CuInS2 composite semiconductor film by depositing delafossite quantum dots on surface of CuInS2 thin film by pulse electrodeposition. The visible light is irradiated on the film, and delafossite/CuInS2 composite film is used as the photocathode, and is placed in aqueous solution to carry out photoelectrocatalytic reaction for reducing carbon dioxide to methanol.
   USE - Method for reducing carbon dioxide to methanol on a delafossite/CuInS2 composite semiconductor thin film electrode.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to reduce carbon dioxide to methanol on a delafossite/CuInS2 composite semiconductor thin film electrode, which has high catalytic activity as compared with CuInS2 film electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The delafossite quantum dots are subjected to electrodeposition, and the copper salt and iron salt are dissolved in dimethyl sulfoxide to obtain electrodeposition solution. The delafossite quantum dots are deposited by electrodeposition where 2-5mM copper nitrate and 6-15mM ferric nitrate are used in a molar ratio of 1:3. The electrodeposition are carried out at an initial potential of pulse relative to saturated calomel electrode of -2.0 to -0.8V, where pulse termination voltage is lower than the initial voltage of 0.2V, pulse width of 0.125s, and pulse period of 0.25 seconds for electrodeposition of 10-90 seconds. The composite semiconductor material is subjected to calcination at a temperature of 350-650 degrees C for 30-120 minutes. The pH value of aqueous solution during the photoelectrocatalytic reaction is 3.6-6.0. The reduction potential is -0.4 to -1.2V relative to the saturated calomel electrode during the photoelectrocatalytic reaction. Preferred Components: The copper salt is copper chloride or copper nitrate, and iron salt is ferric chloride, ferric nitrate, or ferric perchlorate.

PT P
PN US2022186385-A1; WO2022125831-A1
TI Method for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide to methanol for e.g. fuel cell, involves electropolishing copper electrode, depositing copper oxide crystals over surface of copper electrode, and electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide to form methanol using formed electrocatalytic electrode.
AU ZAHID U
   ONAIZI S A
   JAMAL A
   KHALED M
   AL-ROWAILI F N
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202279622V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide to methanol involves electropolishing copper electrode to form an atomically smooth copper electrode, electrochemically depositing copper(I) oxide crystals over the atomically smooth surface of the copper electrode to form an electrocatalytic electrode, electrochemically reducing the carbon dioxide to form methanol using the electrocatalytic electrode, and isolating methanol.
   USE - Method for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide to methanol used for fuel cells and combustion engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxidation state of copper, the smoothness of copper surface, and the shape of crystals of copper(I) oxide increase the probability of methanol formation, to increase the yield from the electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an electrocatalytic electrode, which includes copper(I) oxide crystals electrodeposited over an atomically smooth copper electrode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of electrochemical cell for electropolishing surface of copper foil.
   Electrochemical cell (102)
   Potentiostat (104)
   Reference electrode (106)
   Counter electrode (108)
   Working electrode (110)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step of electropolishing the copper electrode is carried out by placing the copper electrode in a first electrolyte solution comprising ethylene glycol and electrode acid, coupling the copper electrode to a current source, applying current to the copper electrode to electropolish the copper electrode to form the atomically smooth copper electrode, and stopping the electropolishing when the electropolishing is completed. The first electrolyte solution is formed by mixing 85% phosphoric acid solution into water and adding the ethylene glycol to electrolyte solution. The electropolishing step further involves applying current to the copper electrode at 380 mA per 2 cm2, determining that the electropolishing is completed when the first electrolyte solution changes color to blue and after 11.5 minutes, and controlling temperature during the electropolishing at 65degrees Celsius. The step of electrochemically depositing copper(I) oxide is carried out by placing the atomically smooth copper electrode in a second electrolyte solution comprising copper(II) ions, coupling the atomically smooth copper electrode to a current source, and applying current to the atomically smooth copper electrode to deposit the copper(I) oxide crystals to form the electrocatalytic electrode. The second electrolyte solution is formed using 16 g/L copper(II) sulfate and 9.8 g/L trisodium citrate. The electrochemical depositing step further involves applying the current to the atomically smooth copper electrode at 380 mA per 2 cm2. The step of electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide is carried out by placing the electrocatalytic electrode in a third electrolyte solution, coupling the electrocatalytic electrode to a current source, and applying current to the electrocatalytic electrode to reduce carbon dioxide to methanol, and forming the third electrolyte solution using 16.7 g/L potassium bicarbonate, adjusting pH of the third electrolyte solution to 9 and applying the current to the electrocatalytic electrode at 190 mA/cm2. The atomically smooth copper electrode is formed by placing copper electrode in a first electrolyte solution comprising ethylene glycol and electrode acid, coupling the copper electrode to a current source, applying current to the copper electrode to electropolish the copper electrode to form the atomically smooth copper electrode, and stopping the electropolishing, when the electropolishing is completed. Preferred Components: The second electrolyte comprises copper(II) sulfate and trisodium citrate. Preferred Composition: The first electrolyte solution is formed at 3 M concentration of phosphoric acid and 0.2 M concentration of ethylene glycol.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step of providing the                  first gaseous stream comprises reforming gaseous                  feed stream comprising at least one hydrocarbon                  in a reforming unit to produce a syngas stream                  comprising at least carbon monoxide and hydrogen,                  and passing the syngas stream to a water gas                  shift reaction zone to convert at least a portion                  of the carbon monoxide to carbon dioxide and                  hydrogen and generate the first gaseous stream,                  where the water gas shift reaction zone comprises                  at least one unit comprising high temperature                  shift unit and/or low temperature shift unit. The                  process further comprises separating at least a                  portion of the carbon monoxide or the carbon                  dioxide present in the second gaseous stream into                  a carbon monoxide enriched gaseous stream or a                  carbon dioxide enriched gaseous stream prior to                  passing the second gaseous stream to the carbon                  dioxide to carbon monoxide conversion system. The                  process further comprises passing the carbon                  monoxide enriched gaseous stream to a location                  upstream or downstream of the carbon dioxide to                  carbon monoxide conversion system. The process                  further comprises separating at least a portion                  of the carbon dioxide present in the second                  gaseous stream into a carbon dioxide enriched                  gaseous stream and passing the carbon dioxide                  enriched gaseous stream to the reforming unit.                  The process further comprises fermenting the                  third gaseous stream in the gas fermentation unit                  to produce at least one fermentation product and                  an off-gas stream. The process further comprises                  fermenting the third gaseous stream in the gas                  fermentation unit to produce at least one                  fermentation product and an off-gas stream and                  recycling the off-gas stream to the reforming                  unit.
