TI Iron-based multifunctional catalyst useful as            reaction catalyst for direct synthesis of ethanol by            carbon dioxide hydrogenation, comprises            sodium-zinc-iron carbide catalyst coupled with            potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst.
AU CHEN H
   LI J
   GUO Z
   LI B
   CHENG Z
   WANG W
   LIU J
   HAN J
   WU M
   XING T
   WANG Y
AE UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM EAST CHINA (UYPE-C)
   SHANDONG ENERGY GROUP CO LTD (SNJT-C)
GA 2022158288
AB    NOVELTY - Iron-based multifunctional catalyst composed                of sodium-zinc-iron carbide catalyst coupled with                potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst. The                sodium-zinc-iron carbide catalyst is carbonized                iron-based metal-organic framework material                zinc-iron-metal-organic frameworks loaded with                sodium ions and potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum                catalyst is copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst loaded                with potassium ions.
   USE - The iron-based multifunctional catalyst is                useful as reaction catalyst for direct synthesis of                ethanol by carbon dioxide hydrogenation                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The multifunctional catalyst: optimizes the                coupling mode between different catalytic active                components; realizes high-selectivity synthesis of                ethanol by one-step hydrogenation of carbon dioxide                by reverse water gas shift and Fischer-Tropsch                synthesis carbon chain growth in the reaction                process and has carbon dioxide conversion rate of                39.2%, the ethanol selectivity of 35%, and the                olefin selectivity of 33%; realizes the efficient                synergy between different catalytic active                components; opens up new catalytic reaction path                for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to ethanol, and                has high economic value and social benefits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) Preparing iron-based multifunctional                catalyst, comprising (i) carbonizing iron-based                metal-organic framework, and then impregnating with                sodium ion solution to obtain sodium-zinc-iron                carbide catalyst, (ii) impregnating the                copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst with potassium ion                solution to obtain potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum                catalyst, and (iii) coupling sodium-zinc-iron                carbide catalyst and potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum                catalyst to obtain final product; and
   (2) Method for direct synthesis of ethanol by                carbon dioxide hydrogenation, where the iron-based                multifunctional catalyst is used as catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The coupling mode of                  sodium-zinc-iron carbide catalyst and                  potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst includes                  (i) mixing sodium-zinc-iron carbide catalyst and                  potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst, then                  extruding, forming, crushing and sieving, (ii)                  extruding and forming sodium-zinc-iron carbide                  catalyst and potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum                  catalyst respectively, then crushing, sieving and                  mixing the two, (iii) extruding, forming,                  crushing and sieving sodium-zinc-iron carbide                  catalyst and potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum                  catalyst respectively, then filling in a                  double-bed layer with the sodium-zinc-iron                  carbide catalyst in the upper layer and                  potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst in the                  lower layer or (iv) extruding, forming, crushing                  and sieving sodium-zinc-iron carbide catalyst and                  potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst                  respectively and then filling in a double-bed                  layer with the sodium-zinc-iron carbide catalyst                  in the lower layer, and the                  potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst in the                  upper layer. In step (i), the carbonization                  treatment temperature is 500-800degrees Celsius                  the carbonization treatment time is 1-6 hours.                  The iron-based metal organic framework is                  prepared by mixing dimethylformamide solution of                  zinc source compound, iron source compound and                  triethylenediamine with dimethylformamide                  solution of terephthalic acid, and carrying out                  hydrothermal reaction to obtain iron-based metal                  organic framework; the copper-zinc-aluminum                  catalyst is prepared by mixing copper source                  compound, zinc source compound, aluminum source                  compound and urea in deionized water, maintaining                  at 60-100degrees Celsius under stirring for 1-5                  hours, allowing to stand and aging for 12-24                  hours to obtain copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst                  precursor, calcining the obtained                  copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst precursor in air at                  300-600 for 1-3 hours to obtain                  copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst.Preferred Components: The mass ratio between                  sodium-zinc-iron carbide catalyst and the                  potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum catalyst is                  1:0-1:3. In the sodium-zinc-iron carbide                  catalyst, the loading amount of sodium ions is                  0.1-5 wt.%; In the potassium-copper-zinc-aluminum                  catalyst, the loading amount of potassium ions is                  0.1-10 wt.%. The sodium ion solution is aqueous                  solution of sodium carbonate and the potassium                  ion solution is aqueous solution of potassium                  carbonate.

The carbon nanotube matrix is an irregular or sponge structure. The unit selectively activating the adsorptive material to cause release of carbon dioxide from and a receiver connected to the chamber for receiving carbon dioxide released from the material. The unit selectively activating the adsorptive material comprises an electrical supply connected to the structure of adsorptive material for selectively applying an electric field to the material. The unit selectively activating the adsorptive material is configured to apply a pressure change to the material.
   USE - Apparatus for capturing carbon dioxide from mixture of gases.
   ADVANTAGE - The metal organic nanosheet or carbon nanotube (MON/CNT) combination is held as a unit within an exhaust or flue pipe element, with the routing of the gas flow stream internally optimized to create maximum exposure to the MON/CNT elements, thus promoting maximum adsorption capability. The ability to manipulate CNTs has improved greatly over recent years, with CNTs now able to be structured from the nano level to produce honeycombed or entangled sponges. The tanker lorry which collects the carbon dioxide (CO2) will able to discharge the CO2 at a central or regional collection and processing facility which will the use water and electricity to convert the waste CO2 into ethanol and synthetic fuel for recycling back into the transport and energy infrastructure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of capturing carbon dioxide from mixture of gases.

   n' = 2-6;and
   X = hydrogen bonding group chosen from carboxy, amido, carbamato, urea group and sulfonamido.

PN WO2018081310-A1; EP3532445-A1; CN110382435-A; IN201927016758-A; EP3532445-A4; CN110382435-B; US2022204401-A1
TI Manufacturing concrete product by extracting            calcium from solids as portlandite, forming            cementitious slurry including portlandite, shaping            slurry into structural component, and exposing to            carbon dioxide sourced from flue gas stream.
AU WANG B
   PILON L G
   NEITHALATH N
   WEI Z
   YOUNG B
   SANT G
   WEI Z H
   SIMONETTI D
   FALZONE G D
AE UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
   ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
   UNIV ARIZONA STATE (UYAS-C)
   ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
   UNIV CALIFORNIA (REGC-C)
GA 2018341372
AB    NOVELTY - A concrete product is manufactured by                extracting calcium from solids as portlandite,                forming cementitious slurry including the                portlandite, shaping the cementitious slurry into a                structural component, and exposing the structural                component to carbon dioxide sourced from a flue gas                stream.
   USE - Manufacture of concrete product.
   ADVANTAGE - The upcycled concrete product leverages a                process to secure calcium species for carbonate                mineralization using industrial wastes as                precursors or reactants, eliminating the need for                newly mined or produced materials. Also,                carbonation process can efficiently utilize both                carbon dioxide and waste heat carried by flue gas                in a coal-fired power plant. As a result, the                upcycled concrete product and process can                significantly enhance carbon dioxide capturing                capacity of a limestone-cement-based concrete                product, and can establish a                carbon-dioxide-negative process that can mitigate                carbon dioxide emission at large scales. By tapping                the flue gas stream at two discrete points,                extrinsic energy demands for upcycled concrete                processing are reduced, without imposing additional                demands for emissions control.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system                for manufacturing concrete product comprising                leaching reactor (102), precipitation reactor (106)                connected to leaching reactor, and a set of heat                exchangers thermally connected to leaching reactor                and precipitation reactor and configured to source                heat from a flue gas stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of the                manufacturing process of concrete product and its                integration into a primary exhaust stream of a                coal-fired power plant.
   Leaching reactor (102)
   Capacitive concentrator (104)
   Precipitation reactor (106)
   Mixer (108)
   Extruder (110)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Component: Solids include iron                  slag and/or steel slag. Preferred Processes:                  Extraction of calcium includes subjecting solids                  to leaching in leaching reactor to yield ion                  solution, where leaching reactor is operated                  using heat sourced from flue gas stream.                  Extraction of calcium further includes inducing                  precipitation of ion solution in precipitation                  reactor to yield portlandite, where precipitation                  reactor is operated using heat sourced from flue                  gas stream. Formation of cementitious slurry                  includes combining fly ash with portlandite.                  Shaping of cementitious slurry includes casting,                  extruding, molding, pressing, or                  three-dimensional printing of cementitious                  slurry. Exposure of structural component includes                  exposing, during an initial time period, the                  structural component to 1st gas reactant having                  1st carbon dioxide concentration, followed by                  exposing, during a subsequent time period, the                  structural component to 2nd gas reactant having                  2nd carbon dioxide concentration that is greater                  than the 1rst carbon dioxide concentration.                  Preferred Devices: The set of heat exchangers                  includes a set of finned-tube heat exchangers.                  The system further comprises a capacitive                  concentrator connected between leaching reactor                  and precipitation reactor. Capacitive                  concentrator includes a set of electrodes and an                  electrical source connected to the set of                  electrodes. The system further comprises                  carbonation reactor connected to leaching reactor                  and precipitation reactor and configured to                  source carbon dioxide from flue gas stream. The                  system further comprises mixer connected between                  leaching reactor, precipitation reactor, and                  carbonation reactor. The system further comprises                  extruder or pressing, molding, or forming device                  connected between mixer and carbonation reactor.                  Carbonation reactor includes reaction chamber and                  gas exchange mechanism connected to reaction                  chamber and configured to expose, during an                  initial time period, contents of the reaction                  chamber to 1st gas reactant having 1st carbon                  dioxide concentration; and expose, during                  subsequent time period, the contents to 2nd gas                  reactant having 2nd carbon dioxide concentration                  that is greater than the 1st carbon dioxide                  concentration.

An energy releasing component (400) is placed                between the gas storage tank and the liquid storage                tank, where the energy releasing component                comprises multiple expansion energy releasing                portions. Multiple supplemental energy heat                exchangers (422) are connected with an external                heat source. The external heat source supplies                energy to an expander through the supplemental                energy heat exchanger.
   USE - Supplementary external energy based carbon                dioxide gas liquid phase change energy storage                device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device reduces resource waste and saves                energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                supplementary external energy based carbon dioxide                gas liquid phase change energy storage                method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a                supplementary external energy based carbon dioxide                gas liquid phase change energy storage                device.
   Gas storage tank (100)
   Liquid storage tank (200)
   Energy storage component (300)
   Energy releasing component (400)
   Supplemental energy heat exchangers                (422)

PT P
PN CN112844323-A
TI Metal-metal-organic framework/silicon oxide            composite material for manufacturing carbon dioxide gas            adsorbent, has preset specific surface area,            macroporous pore diameter and pore volume.
AU XIN C
AE UNIV WEIFANG (UNWF-C)
GA 202161804C
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-metal-organic framework (MOF-74)/silicon                oxide composite material has specific surface area                of 300-600 m2/g, macroporous pore diameter and pore                volume of 0.4-0.7cm3/g.
   USE - Metal-metal-organic framework/silicon oxide                composite material for manufacturing adsorbent for                selective and rapid carbon dioxide gas adsorption                (All claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite material is prepared by a method                suitable for industrial batch production and                produces adsorbent having faster carbon dioxide                adsorption speed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) preparation of the composite material,                which involves synthesizing metal-MOF-74 by                hydrothermal method, and carrying out secondary                hydrothermal method;
   (2) use of the composite material in rapid gas                adsorption;
   (3) preparation of carbon dioxide adsorbent,                which involves pressing the composite material into                a sheet under the pressure of 5 MPa, crushing the                sheet-shaped adsorbent, and sieving to obtain the                adsorbent particles of 20-40 mesh; and
   (4) carbon dioxide adsorption method in fixed                bed reactor, which involves adding the adsorbent                prepared into a U-shaped quartz tube, embedding the                U-shaped quartz tube with quartz cotton, placing                the U-shaped quartz tube in fixed bed reactor                containing carbon dioxide flue gas. The carbon                dioxide volume ratio in the flue gas is greater                than or equal to 10%, preferably, the gas speed is                60 mL/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The synthesis of                  metal-MOF-74 involves dissolving                  2,5-dihydroxy-terephthalic acid and metal nitrate                  in N,N-dimethyl formamide, ethanol; fully mixing,                  adding deprotonation reagent, packaging the mixed                  solution into the reaction kettle, performing                  first hydrothermal reaction, washing the obtained                  product with N,N-dimethyl formamide, drying to                  obtain M-MOF-74 crystal. The metal is one of                  zinc, magnesium, cobalt, nickel, preferably                  magnesium. The mass ratio of 2,5-dihydroxy                  terephthalic acid and metal nitrate is 1:1-1:10.                  The reaction temperature of the mixed solution                  hydrothermal synthesis is 100-150 degrees C, for                  2-48 hours. The secondary hydrothermal method                  involves placing silicon oxide in the precursor                  solution of M-MOF-74 material, stirring, adding                  deprotonation reagent to the mixed solution,                  packaging the mixed solution into the reaction                  kettle, performing the secondary hydrothermal                  reaction. The temperature of the two hydrothermal                  reaction is the same, carried out at 100-200                  degrees C, preferably 125-150 degrees C for 2-48                  hours, preferably 12-15 hours. Preferred                  Components: The metal nitrate is one or more of                  nitrate of iron, cobalt, nickel, magnesium and                  other alkali metal or transition metal salt. The                  deprotonation reagent is one or more of                  triethylamine or sodium acetate, and is added in                  a mass amount of 1-4 times. The silicon oxide is                  MCF, SBA-15, KIT-6, HMS and/or MCM-41. The mass                  ratio of the selected M-MOF-74 and mesoporous                  silicon oxide is 1:1-1:10.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is                  N,N-dimethyl diamide: ethanol: water mixed                  solution in the volume ratio of                  0.1-1:1-5:1-10.

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Catalyst system used for catalyzing conversion of            carbon dioxide into hydrocarbon product such as 2C            hydrocarbon product e.g. ethylene and ethanol used in            fuel cell for generating electricity, comprises            boron-doped copper catalytic material.
AU SARGENT E
   DE LUNA P
   CHE F
   ZHOU Y
AE TOTAL SA (PETF-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   TOTAL SE (PETF-C)
   UNIV TORONTO GOVERNING COUNCIL (UTOR-C)
GA 201990462Q
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst system comprises boron-doped copper                catalytic material.
   USE - Catalyst system used for catalyzing                electrochemical conversion of carbon dioxide into                hydrocarbon product e.g. 2C hydrocarbon product                e.g. ethylene or ethanol used in fuel cell (all                claimed) for generating electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst system provides the effective                electro-catalytic conversion of carbon dioxide into                hydrocarbon product such as 1C and 2C hydrocarbon                products having excellent selectivity ratio of 930                or more during carbon dioxide electroreduction, and                conversion efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture of boron-doped copper                catalytic material, which involves incipient                wetness impregnating single crystal of copper (111)                material with boric acid aqueous solution;                and
   (2) electrochemical production of multi-carbon                hydrocarbon product, which involves contacting                carbon dioxide gas and electrolyte with electrode                comprising catalyst system comprising a catalyst                layer comprising the boron-doped copper catalytic                material, and optional gas- diffusion layer, which                is arranged for diffusing carbon dioxide through                the gas-diffusion layer and contacts the catalyst                layer, applying voltage to provide current density                to cause carbon dioxide which contacts in catalyst                layer to be electrochemically converted into the                multi-carbon hydrocarbon product, and recovering                the multi-carbon hydrocarbon product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The boron-doped copper                  catalytic material has porous dendritic                  morphology, and has boron concentration which                  decreases with depth into the material,                  preferably material comprises 4-7 mol% boron                  proximate at the external surface of the catalyst                  and 4 mol% or less boron at beyond depth of about                  7 nm from the external surface in which boron                  concentration is determined by using inductively                  coupled plasma optical emission spectrometry. The                  copper comprises copper (111). The copper system                  further comprises gas-diffusion layer and                  catalyst layer comprising the boron-doped copper                  catalytic material which is applied to the                  gas-diffusion layer. The gas-diffusion layer                  comprises carbon-based material and/or the                  gas-diffusion layer is hydrophobic. The current                  density provided in the current collection                  structure is predetermined for selective                  electrochemical conversion of the CO2 into                  hydrocarbon product. The electrolyte comprises                  alkaline potassium compound. Preferred Property:                  The boron-doped copper catalytic material has                  particle size of 30-40 nm which is determined by                  scanning electron microscopy. Preferred Process:                  The boron-doped copper catalytic material is                  prepared by impregnation followed by calcining at                  450-550 degrees C and/or in presence of gas                  comprising hydrogen and/or argon. The manufacture                  of boron-doped copper catalytic material involves                  combining 40-400 mg/ml, preferably 100-150 mg/ml                  copper chloride with 3-6 M borohydride e.g.                  sodium borohydride in solution to precipitate                  catalyst in the solution and recovering the                  precipitated catalyst from the solution, and                  drying the precipitated catalyst. The volume                  ratio of copper chloride and borohydride provided                  in solution is 10:1-1:10, preferably                  2:1-1:1.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); ; L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. TI Greenhouse biological reactor system, has outer wall provided with exhaust port, and power fan for extracting gas with carbon dioxide concentration, and shed body arranged close to ground such that carbon dioxide is uniformly distributed.
AU GAO J

   FENG H
   GUAN Y

AE NINGXIA ACAD AGRIC & FORESTRY SCI (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201965212G
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a biological reactor fixed at a bottom part of a tent body. An air conditioning system is installed on a shed body. The shed body comprises a front wall, a rear wall and a ceiling. The front wall and the back wall are formed with a cavity. An inner wall of the front wall is provided with multiple gas discharge ports. The outer wall is provided with an exhaust port. An inner wall of the rear wall is provided with multiple gas collecting ports. The outer wall is provided with the gas collecting port. A power fan extracts gas with high carbon dioxide concentration. The shed body is arranged close to a ground such that carbon dioxide is uniformly distributed.
   USE - Greenhouse biological reactor system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can collect high concentration of carbon dioxide gas near the ground to enter a gas collecting pipe under action of the power fan, so that the carbon dioxide is recycled to shed decks distributed regularly, and improves use efficiency of the biological reactor, and prevents the carbon dioxide at local aggregation with safety accident.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a greenhouse biological reactor system.

TI Production of synthesis gas involves performing            co-electrolysis of carbon dioxide and water to obtain            gas containing carbon dioxide, pre-heating gas, and            performing reverse water-gas shift reaction and steam            reforming reaction of gas.
AU RUEGER D
   RUGER D
AE SUNFIRE GMBH (SUNF-Non-standard)
   SUNFIRE GMBH (SUNF-Non-standard)
   SUNFIRE GMBH (SUNF-Non-standard)
GA 2018A1119U
AB    NOVELTY - Production of synthesis gas involves                performing co-electrolysis of carbon dioxide (2)                and water to obtain a gas (18) comprising hydrogen,                carbon monoxide, unreacted water vapor, carbon                dioxide and methane on a cathode side (17),                supplying the gas (18) to a catalytic reactor                and/or coke-filled container (20,24), pre-heating                the gas (18) using an electric heater (19, 23), and                performing reverse water-gas shift reaction and                steam reforming reaction of the gas (18).
   USE - Production of synthesis gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical and efficient                production of synthesis gas with reduced content of                carbon dioxide and methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                production apparatus for synthesis gas, which                comprises an electrolysis stack (9), catalytic                reactor and/or coke-filled container, a full                ceramic stack smaller than the electrolysis stack                (9), a gas separation device for separating water                and carbon dioxide from the gas after electrolysis,                and an electrolysis stack (e2).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                production apparatus for synthesis gas.
   Carbon dioxide (2)
   Cooling unit (6,26)
   Electrolysis stack (9)
   Cathode side (17)
   Gas (18)
   Electric heater (19,23)
   Catalytic reactor and/or coke-filled                container (20,24)
   Gas mixture (25)
   Purge gas (32)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The production                  apparatus for synthesis gas further comprises a                  temperature increase unit (6,48,55).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The production of                  synthesis gas further involves cooling the                  product after reverse water-gas shift reaction                  and steam reforming reaction using a cooling unit                  (6,26) to obtain a gas mixture (25) comprising                  hydrogen or a hydrogen-enriched gas, separating                  the hydrogen or hydrogen-enriched gas from the                  gas mixture (25) using a separation device,                  preferably membrane separation device and/or                  pressure swing absorption device. The                  co-electrolysis is performed in the full-ceramic                  stack using carbon dioxide and optional purge gas                  (32).

   KOREA ELECTRIC POWER CORP (KEPC-C)
GA 201893224A
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a first outlet that is provided                for discharging gas relatively. A separation                membrane module is provided for separating and                discharging the gas into a second outlet for                discharging a low gas into a virtual chamber. A                permeable portion is provided for discharging gas                having a relatively high concentration of carbon                dioxide discharged from the separation membrane                module to outside of a plant. A residual portion is                provided for discharging gas having a relatively                low concentration of carbon dioxide discharged from                separation membrane module to outside of plant. A                measurement unit is provided for measuring                information including flow rate of an inlet,                permeate and remaining portion, carbon dioxide                concentration, and pressure. A controller is                provided for determining an abnormality based on                information collected from measurement unit. The                controller is set to detect the plant operating                condition as an abnormal condition when calculated                reference value is less than 95%.
   USE - Abnormality detection system for carbon                dioxide separation membrane plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The area or time point where an abnormal                situation occurs in a carbon dioxide separation                membrane plant is accurately detected and the                suitability of operation is detected when the                operation process is changed.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                the abnormality detection system for carbon dioxide                separation membrane plant. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)

PT P
PN CN113161560-A; CN113161560-B
TI Use of copper-carbon catalyst in lithium-carbon dioxide battery; copper-carbon catalyst has large specific surface area, high porosity, and good conductivity.
AU WANG T
   XU Y
   KONG Y
   DING J
   YU X
   QU H
   ZHANG T
   HUANG X
   HE J
AE UNIV NANJING AERONAUTICS & ASTRONAUTICS (UNUA-C)
GA 2021914456
AB    NOVELTY - Use of copper-carbon catalyst in lithium-carbon dioxide battery, is claimed.
   USE - The copper-carbon catalyst is useful in lithium-carbon dioxide battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The copper-carbon catalyst: has large specific surface area, high porosity, good conductivity, high nitrogen content. The energy storage battery: has high coulombic efficiency of 90%, low overpotential of 1.35 V and stable cycle of 269 cycles, and is desirable long-distance power transmission energy storage device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Components: The lithium-carbon dioxide battery includes metal lithium cathode, electrolyte, membrane and anode; the cathode uses fresh metal lithium sheet, the membrane is glassfiber, and the electrolyte is 1 M lithium triflate/dimethylsulfoxide mixed electrolyte.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper-carbon catalyst is nitrogen-containing carbon fiber supported copper nanoparticle CuNPs/NCNF or nitrogen-containing carbon fiber supported copper palladium nanoparticle CuPd NPs/NCNF. Preferred Method: The preparation of nitrogen-containing carbon fiber comprises (i) using dimethylformamide as solvent, adding pyromellitic anhydride and dimethyl diphenyl ether in molar ratio of 1:1 under stirring, stirring for 11-14 hours to obtain precursor solution (20 wt.%); (ii) electrostatic spinning the prepared precursor solution, and vacuum drying the fiber membrane for 10-14 hours after spinning is completed; and (iii) pre-oxidizing the fiber membrane in air atmosphere, heating to 280-310 degrees C at heating rate of 1-4 degrees C/minute, keeping the temperature for 0.5-2 hours, graphitizing in nitrogen atmosphere, heating to 850-1050 degrees C at heating rate of 4-7 degrees C/minute, keeping the temperature for 2-4 hours, obtaining the nitrogen-containing carbon fiber carrier NCNF. The preparation of nitrogen-containing carbon fiber supported copper nano-particle CuNPs/NCNF catalyst comprises placing the foamed copper and nitrogen-containing carbon fiber carrier according to the gas flow direction, heating to 800-1000 degrees C at heating rate of 4-6 degrees C/minute in ammonia atmosphere, and keeping the temperature for 2-4 hours to obtain a nitrogen-containing carbon fiber supported copper nanoparticle catalyst. The preparation of the nitrogen-containing carbon fiber supported copper palladium nanoparticle CuPd NPs/NCNF comprises (a) adding 1-3 mL of K2(PdCl6) solution and 15-17 mg Copper(II) chloride dihydrate (CuCl2.H2O) into 80-120 mL deionized water, dissolving, and adding 40-70 mg trisodium citrate dihydrate; (b) adding 90-110 mg NCNF under stirring, stirring, and ultrasonically processing for 25-35 minutes; and (c) slowly and uniformly adding 11.5-14 mL of NaHB4 solution while stirring, stirring for 23-25 hours, using deionized water and absolute ethanol to centrifugally wash 2-4 times, vacuum drying at 55-75 degrees C for 23-25 hours, obtaining nitrogen-containing carbon fiber supported copper-palladium nanoparticle catalyst.
DC F01 (Threads and fibres - natural or artificial; spinning - including the production of mineral and carbon fibres (D01).); F04 (Braiding, knitting - including trimmings and non-woven fabrics (D04).); F07 (Other textile applications - include mechanical treatment of fabrics (D06C, F, G, H, J, L, M).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); X16 (Electrochemical Storage)
MC F01-C08; F03-A02; L03-E01B5B; X16-D; X16-E06A1

TI Oil field carbon dioxide flooding wellbore crude heavy component sediment deblocking agent comprises industrial alcohol ether and diesel oil.
AU ZHOU W
   WANG X
   LIU X
   LI G
   LI S
   WANG H
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
   DAQING OILFIELD CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 2019587101
AB    NOVELTY - Oil field carbon dioxide flooding wellbore crude heavy component sediment deblocking agent comprises 70-90 %mass industrial alcohol ether and 10-30 %mass diesel oil.
   USE - As oil field carbon dioxide flooding wellbore crude heavy component sediment deblocking agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The deblocking agent dredges wellbore to provide channel for smooth start of test instrument and flushing liquid circulation, corrosion-resistant dosing, and solves the production problems such as testing, dosing, rod, killing, and washing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The industrial alcohol ether comprises ethylene glycol butyl ether or 1,2-propylene glycol butyl ether. The diesel oil is chosen from No.0, No.10, No.20, No.35, and No.50.

PN JP2019005996-A
TI Laminated article for packaging bag e.g. pouch, comprises base material, and extrusion resin layer comprising carbon dioxide absorber.
AU SAITO F
AE DAINIPPON PRINTING CO LTD (NIPQ-C)
GA 2019062358
AB    NOVELTY - A laminated article (10) comprises a base material (11) and an extrusion resin layer (12) comprising a carbon dioxide absorber.
   USE - Laminated article used in packaging bag (claimed) e.g. pouch for foodstuffs.
   ADVANTAGE - The article has excellent antistatic property, is odorless, reduces the carbon dioxide emission at the time of incineration, and eliminates the problem of transparency and delamination.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of the laminated article.
   Laminated article (10)
   Base material (11)
   Extrusion resin layer (12)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The resin layer comprises the absorber in a dispersed state, and further comprises a dispersing agent. The laminated article comprises the base material, extrusion resin layer (A), barrier layer, extrusion resin layer (B) and a sealant layer sequentially, and the absorber is present in at least one of the resin layers. Preferred Composition: The resin layer comprises 0.1-5 %mass of the absorber and the dispersing agent. Preferred Property: The resin layer has a thickness of 5-60 mu m.

PN CN111535017-A; WO2021237519-A1; CN111535017-B
TI Flash explosion treatment of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, involves loading sample in fluid flash processing unit, connecting carbon dioxide medium device, adjusting to preset value, performing flash explosion process, and recovering.
AU LONG J
   CAI C
   SHI M
AE UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
   NANTONG TEXTILE & SILK IND TECHNOLOGY (NANT-Non-standard)
   UNIV SOOCHOW (USWZ-C)
GA 202080686G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for flash explosion treatment of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, involves (a) placing natural fibers or its products in steam treatment container, and performing moisture absorption and weight gain pretreatment, (b) loading processed sample treated in step (a) in fluid flash processing unit, and accessing flash explosion processing unit to supercritical carbon dioxide flash explosion processing system, (c) connecting carbon dioxide medium device, filling supercritical carbon dioxide flash explosion treatment system with quantitative carbon dioxide, and performing carbon dioxide fluid circulation processing on treatment sample, (d) adjusting temperature and pressure of separation and recovery system to preset value, (e) opening pressure relief valve of system, performing rapid or instantaneous pressure relief, and performing flash explosion processing on sample, and (f) opening gas recovery pump of separation recovery system, and recovering the gas in flash explosion system.
   USE - Method for flash explosion treatment of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the hydrophilicity of natural fiber and dyeing performance in supercritical carbon dioxide, utilizes the pressure difference generated inside and outside fiber phase to drive fiber chain segment to move, increases the internal pore diameter of fiber, breaks the molecular hydrogen bond in fiber, increases the freedom degree of macromolecules of fiber, increases the amorphous region, improves the hydrophilicity of fiber, and improves the dyeability of natural fiber in supercritical carbon dioxide fluid medium.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for flash explosion treatment of supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, involves (a) taking natural fibers or its products as treatment sample, placing in steam treatment container, performing moisture absorption and weight gain pretreatment on treatment sample using steam at standard atmospheric pressure, and controlling the moisture content of treatment sample, (b) loading processed sample treated in step (a) in fluid flash processing unit, and accessing flash explosion processing unit to supercritical carbon dioxide flash explosion processing system, (c) connecting carbon dioxide medium device, filling supercritical carbon dioxide flash explosion treatment system with quantitative carbon dioxide, heating, and after the internal temperature and pressure of system reaches preset value, performing carbon dioxide fluid circulation processing on treatment sample, (d) adjusting temperature and pressure of separation and recovery system to preset value to flash explosion process to achieve different temperature difference and differential pressure flash explosion, (e) opening pressure relief valve of system, performing rapid or instantaneous pressure relief to process, and performing flash explosion processing on processed sample, and (f) opening gas recovery pump of separation recovery system, recovering the gas in flash explosion system, taking out the sample after pressure is reduced to atmospheric pressure, and completing the flash explosion processing of natural fiber or its product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the fluid flash explosion processing system.
   Carbon dioxide storage tank (1)
   Globe valve (2)
   Condenser (3)
   Booster pump (4)
   Preheater (5)
EA    (WO2021237519-A1)   NOVELTY - Flash explosion treatment of natural fiber supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, comprises taking natural fiber or its product as processing sample, placing in the steam processing container, under the standard atmospheric pressure, using steam to perform moisture absorption and weight gain pre-treatment to the processed sample, and controlling the water content of the sample. The processed sample is filled in the flash explosion processing unit, and then the processing unit is connected to the supercritical CO2 Flash explosion processing system, connected with the carbon dioxide medium device, and filling quantitative carbon dioxide to the system, at the same time, heating and heating.
   USE - The method is useful for natural fiber supercritical carbon dioxide fluid flash explosion treatment in textile dyeing and finishing for textile industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides a simple operation, convenient, no waste water pollution, high processing efficiency of the natural fiber and its product supercritical CO2 fluid flash explosion treatment method, not changing the natural fiber excellent performance, at the same time, effectively improving the dyeing performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The natural fiber or its product comprises cotton, hemp, silk, wool fiber or their yarn and fabric product. The steam in step (a) includes saturated steam and superheated steam. Preferred Method: The step (b) involves, when the processed sample is fiber, extruding fluffy fiber and uniformly layering layer by layer under action of mechanical external force, and flatly packing in fluid flash explosion treatment unit, when the processed sample is yarn, uniformly winding yarn under the action of mechanical external force, and uniformly and smoothly winding in fluid flash processing unit, and when the processed sample is fabric, smoothly and uniformly winding the fabric under action of mechanical external force, and uniformly and smoothly winding in fluid flash treatment unit. Preferred Conditions: The step (a) involves performing moisture absorption and weight gain pretreatment for 1-30 minutes, and controlling the moisture content of processed sample to 1-300%. In step (c), the preset value of internal pressure of system is 0.1-30 MPa, and the preset value of internal temperature of system is 90-180 degrees C. The step (c) involves performing carbon dioxide fluid circulation treatment for 1-60 minutes under fluid circulation and static state, and the time ratio of fluid circulation and static state is 1:5-10:1. The preset temperature and pressure of separation and recovery system in step (d) is 0-120 degrees C and 0-25 MPa. In step (e), the rapid or instantaneous pressure relief is carried out under pressure relief flash explosion for 1-40 seconds. The method involves repeating step (c)-(e) for performing multiple flash explosion treatments on processed samples.

PN CN111729347-A
TI Supercritical extraction of active ingredients in Chinese herbal medicine e.g. Scutellaria, involves adding pulverized Chinese herbal medicine and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid to extraction tank, extracting, separating, and cooling.
AU LIU Y
   HAN F
AE LIU Y (LIUY-Individual)
GA 202099505L
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical extraction of active ingredients in Chinese herbal medicine involves cleaning picked Chinese herbal medicine by cleaning equipment, drying, transferring to pulverizer for pulverization, releasing carbon dioxide from cylinder, compressing and liquefying by compressor, adding to extraction tank, obtaining supercritical fluid of carbon dioxide, adding the carbon dioxide supercritical fluid and original pure carbon dioxide to a mixing device, mixing, adding the Chinese herbal medicine to extraction tank, injecting the carbon dioxide supercritical fluid, carrying out static extraction of Chinese herbal medicine and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid, carrying out semi-continuous separation and extraction of Chinese herbal medicine and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid, flowing extracted mixture from the extraction tank to heat exchanger for heat exchange and cooling, separating extract, and collecting by manual filter.
   USE - Supercritical extraction of active ingredients in Chinese herbal medicine e.g. Scutellaria, golden chamomile and Cnidium monnieri.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables quick, efficient, easy and simple supercritical extraction of active ingredients in Chinese herbal medicine, without phase change process of the material, and effectively prevents the oxidation and dissipation of heat-sensitive components, and occurrence of decomposition and precipitation reactions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical extraction of active ingredients in Chinese herbal medicine involves cleaning picked Chinese herbal medicine by cleaning equipment, removing the impurities and soil carried by Chinese herbal medicine, drying in a dryer at 35-45 degrees C for 20-40 minutes, transferring to pulverizer for pulverization, releasing carbon dioxide from cylinder, compressing and liquefying using compressor, adding to extraction tank through high-pressure liquid metering pump, obtaining supercritical fluid of carbon dioxide in which Chinese herbal medicine is soluble, adding the carbon dioxide supercritical fluid and original pure carbon dioxide to a mixing device, mixing for 30 minutes, passing mixed liquid through a purifier to adsorb and remove excess components and components which are not completely separated in the mixed liquid, adding the Chinese herbal medicine to extraction tank, injecting the carbon dioxide supercritical fluid, contacting at extraction tank conditions of temperature and pressure at more than critical point, carrying out static extraction of Chinese herbal medicine and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid for 2.5-3 hours, passing carbon dioxide supercritical fluid through extraction material bed in extraction tank, adding to separation tank, carrying out semi-continuous separation and extraction of Chinese herbal medicine and carbon dioxide supercritical fluid by controlling the temperature and pressure in the separation tank, separating extract, flowing extracted mixture from the extraction tank to heat exchanger for heat exchange and cooling, transferring resultant to separation tank from the conduit by pumping, separating extract, and collecting by manual filter. The critical temperature of carbon dioxide supercritical fluid is 308.2 degrees C. The critical pressure of the supercritical fluid is 74.8 MPa. The extraction tank and separation tank are connected in a sealed and conductive manner through a conduit. During the transfer process, the temperature and pressure of the carbon dioxide supercritical fluid gradually decrease, and dissolving capability of carbon dioxide supercritical fluid is greatly reduced.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN CN110565415-A; CN210766137-U; WO2021068277-A1; EP3825457-A1; EP3825457-A4; TW703254-B1; TW202115295-A; JP2022507993-W
TI Performing supercritical carbon dioxide printing and dyeing involves pressurizing and heating liquid carbon dioxide, converting into supercritical carbon dioxide, and mixing supercritical carbon dioxide with dye to form dye solution.
AU YANG J
AE SHANGHAI FULUDI FLUID TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI FULUDI FLUID TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
   SHANGHAI FULUDI FLUID TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A6623T
AB    NOVELTY - Performing supercritical carbon dioxide printing and dyeing involves pressurizing and heating liquid carbon dioxide, converting into supercritical carbon dioxide, and mixing supercritical carbon dioxide with a dye to form a dye solution. The dyeing solution is passed into the dyeing kettle, and dye the spindles from the input end under the action of ultrasonic and two-way circulation. The spindles are sent out from the output end. The remaining dyeing solution is collected after dyeing, and performed decompression and separation treatment. The separated dye and supercritical carbon dioxide are respectively purified and dried as the liquid carbon dioxide raw material and the solution. The dye raw materials are recycled.
   USE - Method for performing supercritical carbon dioxide printing and dyeing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enabels to perform supercritical carbon dioxide printing and dyeing, which have no generation of dyeing wastewater and other wastes, realizes green environmental protection, cleans production, good economic benefits, and significant environmental benefits, achieves recycling, flowing production, increases output and reduces industrial costs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a supercritical carbon dioxide printing and dyeing system , which comprises carbon dioxide (CO2) storage tank, a dye tank, a dyeing kettle, a dye collection tank, and reduced-pressure cooling unit and extraction separation unit, where the CO2 storage tank is connected to the dye tank through a filter unit, a drying unit, a heating unit and a pressurizing unit through a pipeline in order to pressurize and heat the carbon dioxide in the CO2 storage tank After treatment, it is converted into supercritical carbon dioxide, and the supercritical carbon dioxide is passed into the dye tank and mixed with the dye to form a dye solution, dye tank is connected to the dyeing tank through an auxiliary tank and a flow meter through a pipeline in order to send the dyeing liquid into the dyeing tank to dye the spindle from the feeding end, and the dyeing tank is provided Have at least one dual-core ultrasonic generator and at least one bi-directional circulation pump, bottom of the dyeing kettle is connected to the reduced-pressure cooling unit through the dye collection tank through a pipe, so as to collect the remaining dyeing liquid in the dyeing kettle after being dyed in the dye collection tank, and pass the reduced pressure, cooling unit performs pressure reduction and separation treatment on the remaining dyeing solution, top of the decompression cooling unit is connected to the CO2 storage tank through a purification unit, a filtering unit, and a drying unit through a pipe in order to send the separated carbon dioxide gas into the CO2 storage tank for recycling and reuse, and bottom end of the reduced-pressure cooling unit is connected to the extraction and separation unit through a pipeline to perform extraction treatment on the separated dye solution, the extracted dye is sent to the dye tank for recycling, and/or the extraction aid The agent is sent to the auxiliary tank for recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The pressure unit is used to pressurize the carbon dioxide pressure to above 16 Mega pascals (MPa). The heating unit needs to heat the carbon dioxide temperature above 80 degrees C. A dyeing aid is added to the dyeing solution. An ultrasonic generating unit with a working power of 600 Watt is used to ultrasonically treat the dyeing solution and the spindle in the dyeing kettle. The bidirectional circulating pump is used to place the dyeing solution in the dyeing kettle at a speed of 2.4 m/minute in the cycle, the spindle advances and dyes in the dyeing kettle at a width of 1.2 m/minute. The thermal insulation treatment is performed on the dyeing kettle during the dyeing process. The dyeing kettle is composed of a pipe of 24-25 meters, and is provided with a thermal insulation layer, four ultrasonic generating units and four bidirectional circulation pumps. A liquid inlet hole is respectively opened at 1000 mm, 3000 mm, 5000 mm, and 6000 mm away from the dyeing kettle, and the liquid inlet hole is connected to the dye tank through an ingot end pipe. The drainage holes are respectively provided at 375mm, 625mm, 875mm, and 1125mm from the ingot end of the dyeing kettle, and the drainage holes are connected to the dye collection tank through a pipeline. The drainage hole and the liquid inlet hole have a large and small head structure, which are each composed of an upper hole and a lower hole. The height of the upper hole is 20-30mm and the diameter is 25-30mm. The height of the lower hole is 8- 12mm, diameter is 3-8mm.

AB    NOVELTY - Oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) of ethane                involves subjecting a stream comprising ethane to                ODH conditions, (a) contacting ethane with oxygen                in presence of catalyst contains mixed metal oxide                which diluent comprising carbon dioxide fed to (a),                results in effluent containing ethylene, optionally                acetic acid, unconverted ethane, water, carbon                dioxide, and/or unconverted oxygen, and/or carbon                monoxide and/or acetylene, (b) removing water from                component(s) of effluent in stream (7), (c)                optionally removing unconverted oxygen, and/or                carbon monoxide, and/or acetylene from component(s)                of stream (7) from (b) resulting in stream (14),                (d) removing ethylene from component(s) of stream                (14) resulting in stream (17), (e) partially and                selectively removing carbon dioxide from stream                (17), resulting in stream (25) and recycling                component(s) of stream (25).
   USE - Oxidative dehydrogenation of ethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The process of ODH of ethane provides                separated unconverted ethane and carbon dioxide                together and at same time contributes to effective                recovery of ethylene product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - ODH of ethane involves subjecting a stream                comprising ethane to ODH conditions, comprising (a)                contacting ethane with oxygen in presence of                catalyst containing mixed metal oxide, feeding a                diluent comprising carbon dioxide to step (a),                resulting in effluent containing ethylene,                optionally acetic acid, unconverted ethane, water,                carbon dioxide, and/or unconverted oxygen, and/or                carbon monoxide and/or acetylene, (b) removing                water from at least a component effluent from step                (a), resulting in stream (7) comprising ethylene,                unconverted ethane, carbon dioxide, optionally                unconverted oxygen, optionally carbon monoxide,                optionally acetylene, water and acetic acid, (c)                optionally removing unconverted oxygen and/or                carbon monoxide and/or acetylene from component(s)                from step (b) resulting in stream (14) comprising                ethylene, unconverted ethane and carbon dioxide,(d)                removing ethylene from at least stream (14) from                step (b) or (c) by complexation separation method,                which involves contacting at least component of                stream with liquid solvent containing complexation                agent resulting in stream (17) comprising                unconverted ethane and carbon dioxide, (e)                partially and selectively removing carbon dioxide                from component(s) of stream (17) comprising                unconverted ethane and carbon dioxide resulting in                stream (25) comprising unconverted ethane and                carbon dioxide and having reduced content of carbon                dioxide and recycling at least a component of the                stream (25) comprising unconverted ethane and                carbon dioxide from step (e) to (a).
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of process                of ODH of ethane.
   Streams (7,14,17,25)
   Condensation unit (8)
   Separation unit (15)
   Substreams (17a,17b)
   Removal unit (22)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The complexation agent                  in step (d) is metal salt which is silver nitrate                  and contains silver(I) ion or copper (I) ion,                  preferably silver(I) ion.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (d) involves                  (dl) contacting at least portion of the stream                  comprising ethylene, unconverted ethane and                  carbon dioxide resulting from step (b) or (c)                  with a liquid solvent comprising the complexation                  agent, resulting in a stream comprising                  unconverted ethane and carbon dioxide, at least                  portion of which stream is fed to step (e) , and                  a liquid stream comprising solvent, complexation                  agent and complexed ethylene, and (d2) desorbing                  complexed ethylene from at least portion of the                  liquid stream comprising solvent, complexation                  agent and complexed ethylene resulting from step                  (dl), resulting in a stream comprising desorbed                  ethylene and a liquid stream comprising solvent                  and complexation agent, and (d3) recycling at                  least portion of the liquid stream comprising                  solvent and complexation agent resulting from                  step (d2 ) to step (dl). The liquid stream                  resulting from step (dl) comprises solvent,                  complexation agent, complexed ethylene and                  absorbed unconverted ethane and carbon dioxide,                  the absorbed unconverted ethane and carbon                  dioxide are stripped from at least portion of                  liquid stream by contacting with a stream                  comprising ethylene, resulting in a stream                  comprising ethylene, unconverted ethane and                  carbon dioxide, in which at least portion of the                  stream is fed to step (dl), and a portion of                  liquid stream comprising solvent, complexation                  agent and complexed ethylene, is fed to step                  (d2). At least portion of the stream comprising                  unconverted ethane and carbon dioxide resulting                  from step (d) is split into at least two                  substreams (17a,17b), in which at least portion                  of one split substream (17a) is fed to step (e)                  and the other portion of the split substream                  (17b) is recycled to step (a). Preferred                  Composition: The total amount of (i) carbon                  dioxide removed in step (e) and (ii) carbon                  dioxide removed in any step in which a portion of                  recycle stream is purged before recycling is of                  1-15% of amount of carbon dioxide from stream of                  step (d). The amount of carbon dioxide is 1-99                  vol.% preferably 5-95 vol.% based on total amount                  of ethylene, unconverted ethane, and carbon                  dioxide in feed to step (d). The liquid solvent                  in step (d) is water, organic solvent and/or                  ionic solvent.

TI Method for gas cyclic injection of liquid carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions in oil-producing well, involves injecting carbon dioxide into well, initiating oil production followed by injecting hydrocarbon micellar solution.
AU VOLKOV V A
   BELIKOVA V G
   PROKHOROV P E
   TURAPIN A N
   KEROSIROV V M
AE DELTAPROM LLC (DELT-Non-standard)
   DELTA-PROM CO LTD (DELT-Non-standard)
GA 202013978Q
AB    NOVELTY - A liquid carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions in an oil-producing well gas cyclic injection method involves injecting carbon dioxide into a production well, initiating oil production from the same producing well, to regulate the mobility of the filtration flow in a heterogeneous permeability formation during gas cyclic injection of supercritical carbon dioxide and at a temperature of not less than 31.1 degrees C and a pressure of not less than 7.38 MPa to increase the exposure to supercritical carbon dioxide and to interact more effectively with residual formation oil. The first rim of a hydrocarbon micellar solution is injected into the well in the amount of 5-20 m3 per meter of perforation of the treated reservoir before the carbon dioxide is injected into the well, followed by performing oil production, where the number of injection cycles the specified sequence in the production is least 1-3.
   USE - Method for gas cyclic injection of liquid carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions in an oil-producing well.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A liquid carbon dioxide under supercritical conditions in an oil-producing well gas cyclic injection method involves injecting carbon dioxide into a production well, initiating oil production from the same producing well, to regulate the mobility of the filtration flow in a heterogeneous permeability formation during gas cyclic injection of supercritical carbon dioxide and at a temperature of not less than 31.1 degrees C and a pressure of not less than 7.38 MPa to increase the exposure to supercritical carbon dioxide and to interact more effectively with residual formation oil. The first rim of a hydrocarbon micellar solution is injected into the well in the amount of 5-20 m3 per meter of perforation of the treated reservoir before the carbon dioxide is injected into the well, carbon dioxide is injected under the above conditions, and before the impregnation period, a second rim containing a gas phase permeability regulator and a foaming agent is injected in a volume of 5-20 m3 per meter of perforation of the treated reservoir followed by performing oil production, where the number of injection cycles the specified sequence in the production is least 1-3.

TI Geothermal energy driving circulation system for            use in geothermal energy development field, has power            circulating device, and geothermal energy system, where            power circulating device takes geothermal energy as            main kinetic energy.
AU XUE R
   FAN Y
   SHEN W
   LI C
   CAO Y
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 2022872245
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a power circulating device, a                carbon dioxide capturing device (10) and a                geothermal energy system. The power circulating                device takes geothermal energy as main kinetic                energy. The supercritical carbon dioxide and water                soluble fluid is used as working substance. The                pressurizing pump (11) is used for guiding the                carbon dioxide captured by the carbon dioxide                capturing device into the saline water layer (12).                The steam water separator (13) discharges the                moisture portion of the working substance inside                the entering period. The working substance after                heating through the working substance heater (1) is                sent to the high temperature regenerator (3)                through the supercritical carbon dioxide turbine                (2). The working substance is sent to the low                temperature regenerator (4) after being returned to                heat by the high temperature regenerator.
   USE - Geothermal energy driving circulation system                based on supercritical carbon dioxide and water                miscible fluid for use in geothermal energy                development field.
   ADVANTAGE - The system uses geothermal energy in the salt                water layer to make carbon dioxide obtain, reduce                carbon emission and environmental pollution of                thermal power generation, then using the technical                means to separate the carbon dioxide and water, the                current technical means can realize the mass ratio                of carbon dioxide to water is 95:5, then the                CO2-H2O mixture is put into circulation. The system                injects the CO 2 after circulating into the                salt-water layer, achieve closed circulation,                avoiding the discharge of CO 2.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                geothermal energy driving circulation system based                on supercritical carbon dioxide and water-soluble                fluid.
   Working substance heater (1)
   Supercritical carbon dioxide turbine                (2)
   High temperature regenerator (3)
   Low temperature regenerator (4)
   Carbon dioxide capturing device (10)
   Pressurizing pump (11)
   Saline water layer (12)
   Steam-water separator (13)

PN CN111595049-A; CN212619438-U
TI Trans-critical carbon dioxide refrigeration cycle system, has heat regenerator for conveying low-temperature low-pressure carbon dioxide gas in first loop to second loop for cooling main fluid in main loop.
AU ZHANG X
   ZENG M
   ZHENG Q
AE UNIV PEKING (UYPK-C)
GA 202086373X
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a heat regenerator for conveying low-temperature low-pressure carbon dioxide gas from a gas outlet at a top end of a first carbon dioxide gas-liquid separator to an outlet of a high-pressure compressor to form a first loop, where the heat regenerator conveys the low-temperature low-pressure carbon dioxide gas from the gas outlet at a top end of a second carbon dioxide gas-liquid separator to an inlet of a low-pressure compressor to form a second loop. The heat regenerator conveys the low-temperature low-pressure carbon dioxide gas from an outlet of a cold end dehumidifying device to a secondary fluid inlet of an ejector to form a third loop, where the heat regenerator conveys the low-temperature low-pressure carbon dioxide gas in the first loop to the third loop for cooling main fluid in a main loop.
   USE - Trans-critical carbon dioxide refrigeration cycle system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves circulation efficiency and uses carbon dioxide as a refrigerating medium, and has large ice making capacity, and can utilize heat of a gas cooler by cascade, adopt dual compressors and dual ejectors to improve cycle efficiency, reduce energy consumption and increase operating efficiency of a refrigeration cycle system by 18-25% so as to reduce operating loss of the refrigeration cycle system by 35-45%, thus improving heating performance coefficient and refrigeration performance coefficient, and hence increasing heat recovery effect based on a skating filed and a low evaporating temperature condition and effectively using cold energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a trans-critical carbon dioxide refrigeration cycle method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a trans-critical carbon dioxide refrigeration cycle system.

TI Upgraded supercritical carbon dioxide fluid            extraction system has recovery system that includes            recovery condenser arranged in communication with            extraction kettle, so that gaseous carbon dioxide in            kettle is liquefied in recovery condenser.
AU ZHANG Y
   ZHANG L
   WANG B
   ZHAO N
   DING S
   ZHAO H
AE QINGDAO LUHE EXTRACTION CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201992206C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has the condensing system,                extraction system, separation system and recovery                system. The condensing system includes a carbon                dioxide condensation device provided with primary                condenser (81) and secondary condenser (82). An                extraction system is included with extraction                kettles (3). The separation system is included with                decompression heat exchanger, separation kettle,                and hydrocyclone. The tube of decompression heat                exchanger is arranged in communication with carbon                dioxide flow outlet of extraction kettle. The                separation kettle is included with upper tube                structure and lower tube structure. The top end of                inner layer of hydrocyclone is arranged in                communication with outside of tube of decompression                heat exchanger. The recovery system is included                with recovery condenser arranged in communication                with extraction kettle, so that gaseous carbon                dioxide in extraction kettle is liquefied in                recovery condenser arranged in communication with                intermediate storage tank (9).
   USE - Upgraded supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                extraction system.
   ADVANTAGE - Since the gaseous carbon dioxide in extraction                kettle is liquefied in recovery condenser arranged                in communication with intermediate storage tank,                the total extraction volume of the supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid extraction system can greatly                save energy consumption, improve the utilization                rate of carbon dioxide, and achieve zero carbon                dioxide emissions. The data shows that the energy                cost of the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid                extraction system is reduced from about 5,000 yuan                for processing a single ton of material to less                than 1,000 yuan, which is a solid step for                replacing the solvent extraction process.

   Extraction kettle (3)
   Intermediate storage tank (9)

PN WO2019030688-A1
TI Treating biogas mixture comprising methane and carbon dioxide, by purifying, and cooling purified gas mixture to obtain carbon dioxide, and separating solid carbon dioxide from methane, hydrogen and carbon monoxide in gaseous state.
AU BIANCHI B
   MARCHIONNI G
AE UNIV BARI ALDO MORO (UYBA-Non-standard)
   MARCHIONNI SRL (MARC-Non-standard)
GA 201915432B
AB    NOVELTY - Process (P1) for treating biogas mixture comprising methane and carbon dioxide, involves purifying the gas mixture, cooling the purified gas mixture to obtain carbon dioxide in the solid state, and separating the solid carbon dioxide from methane, hydrogen and carbon monoxide in the gaseous state, where the steps are performed at ambient pressure.
   USE - The process is useful for treating biogas or gas mixture, or syngas (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process increases the production of methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) process (P2) for treating syngas comprising methane, hydrogen, carbon oxide and carbon dioxide, involves purifying the gas mixture, fractionally cooling the purified gas mixture to obtain gaseous carbon dioxide with high purity level, methane in the liquid state, hydrogen in the gaseous state and carbon monoxide in the liquid state, and separating the solid carbon dioxide from syngas in the gaseous state, where the steps are performed substantially at ambient pressure; and
   (2) plant comprising a scrubber to eliminate impurities and undesired gases present in the mixture, a liquid-nitrogen heat exchanger downstream of the scrubber to cool the purified gas mixture and to obtain carbon dioxide in the solid state, downstream of the heat exchanger, where the plant comprises a cyclone to separate the solid carbon dioxide from a gaseous fuel mixture comprising methane, hydrogen, carbon oxide, a sublimation system for the carbon dioxide to obtain it in the gaseous state and with high purity level, a liquid-nitrogen heat exchanger to obtain methane in the liquid state, and a reverse cycle stirling engine to obtain the fractionation of the gaseous hydrogen and the liquid carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ENVIRONMENT - Preferred Process: The cooling step to obtain carbon dioxide in the solid state is performed by introducing the purified mixture into a heat exchanger using liquid nitrogen as heat exchange fluid. The process (P1) further involves sublimating the carbon dioxide, after the step of separating the solid carbon dioxide from the methane in the gaseous state, to obtain highly pure carbon dioxide. The obtained methane has purity of 95-99 volume/volume% or more.

TI Carbon dioxide direct air capture apparatus            comprises enclosure defining interior volume, carbon            dioxide adsorbent bed contained within interior volume            of enclosure, vacuum source, source of input air, and            heater.
AU WILSON S M W
AE TERRAFIXING INC (TERR-Non-standard)
GA 202276446L
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has an enclosure that defines an                interior volume. A carbon dioxide adsorbent bed                (10) is contained within the interior volume of the                enclosure. A vacuum source is coupled to the                enclosure. A source of input air is coupled to the                enclosure. A heater (16) is capable of heating the                interior volume of the enclosure. The interior                volume of the enclosure is selectively isolated                from multiple components of the apparatus to enable                selective control of contents, pressure, and                temperature of the interior volume of the                enclosure, and the input air has a temperature                equal to less than 0 degree, or a humidity equal to                or less than 5 g of water per kg of air prior to                entering the enclosure.
   USE - Carbon dioxide direct air capture apparatus                for capturing and concentration carbon dioxide from                air.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus captures and concentrates carbon                dioxide from the air in an efficient and                cost-effective manner. The apparatus requires low                input energy, and allows for the carbon dioxide to                be captured directly from air at a low cost per                given unit. Using input air with low temperature                and/or low humidity enables a broad range of carbon                dioxide adsorbent material to be used. It allows                for capture and concentration of CO2 from air to be                performed in low cost, cyclical, and continuous                manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for carbon dioxide direct air capture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the                Carbon dioxide direct air capture apparatus.
   Carbon dioxide adsorbent bed (10)
   Fan (11)
   Vacuum pump (15)
   Heater (16)

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining a structure                parameter of a cold emitting system, an initial                state parameter and a preset working parameter,                where the initial state parameter comprises                temperature and pressure in a high pressure indoor                carbon dioxide initial filling state of the cold                emitting system. A dynamic variation process of a                carbon dioxide state in a variable-volume                low-pressure chamber is calculated. The carbon                dioxide state in the low-pressure chamber is                expanded when the pressure in the low-pressure                chamber is enough to push the aircraft to move. The                carbon dioxide density is calculated.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                computer readable storage medium, comprising a set                of instructions for method for characterizing cold                emission process by using supercritical carbon                dioxide as working medium.
