PT P
PN KR2020098873-A
TI Apparatus used for producing sodium bicarbonate, comprises reactor for receiving aqueous sodium carbonate solution and carbon dioxide gas, reactor for receiving fluid flowing out of reactor, and carbon dioxide microbubble generator.
AU HONG W
AE SUNG KWANG ENG CO LTD (SUNG-Non-standard)
GA 202081224U
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises a reactor (a) for receiving an aqueous sodium carbonate solution and a carbon dioxide gas, a reactor (b) for receiving the fluid flowing out of the reactor (a), and a carbon dioxide microbubble generator for receiving carbon dioxide gas and the fluid flowing out of the reactor (b), generating microbubbles in the fluid, and providing the microbubbles to the reactor (b). The reactor (a) comprises a generator for generating carbon dioxide macrobubbles and injecting the macrobubbles into the sodium carbonate aqueous solution. The amount of carbon dioxide gas injected into the reactor (a) is greater than the amount of carbon dioxide gas introduced into the reactor (b).
   USE - Apparatus is used for producing sodium bicarbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus enables efficient production of sodium bicarbonate using two-stage reaction (r1,r2) method in short time, with reduced investment cost and equipment size. The reaction (r1) is performed under low-concentration carbon dioxide dissolution conditions, and a large amount of carbon dioxide gas is quickly supplied and the reaction yield is rapidly increased to 70-80%. The reaction (r2) is performed to increase the reaction yield to 99.99% in a short time. The carbon dioxide gas consumption in the reaction (r2) is relatively lower than that in the reaction (r1). By dividing the optimized reaction conditions into two stages to match the reaction yield change proportional to the change in the concentration of bicarbonate ions in the reaction solution, the uniformity of produced mineralization is very high, and the production cost can be reduced by effectively producing precipitated carbonates in a short time.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount of carbon dioxide gas injected into the reactor (a) is 4 times or more the amount of carbon dioxide gas introduced into the reactor (b). Preferred Properties: The average diameter of carbon dioxide macrobubbles is 1-2 mm. The average diameter of carbon dioxide microbubbles is 50 mu m or less.

AB    NOVELTY - The closure (10) has a release rate control                layer (40) for controlling release of carbon                dioxide from a carbon dioxide emitter (30) into a                container when the closure is coupled to the                container made from mono-layer polyethylene                terephthalate, where diameter of the closure is                about 18-38 mm, capacity of the container is 12oz,                20oz, or 500ml. The carbon dioxide emitter is                seated within a bore (26), where the closure                utilizes dry lubricant that is magnesium stearate.                The release rate control layer is over-molded onto                the carbon dioxide emitter, and extends out from                within the bore. The release rate control layer                controls rate and timing of carbon dioxide released                into the container from the carbon dioxide emitter                and permits water vapor to pass through the release                rate control layer to the carbon dioxide emitter,                where the carbon dioxide emitter is formed by                direct compression or injection molding, and in                disk shape and includes bicarbonate, acid catalyst,                and humectant.

PT P
PN US2018258341-A1; US10400158-B2
TI Stable capsule dispersion useful for enhancing oil recovery, comprises a dispersed phase of many functionalized co-polymerized capsules, and a continuous phase of supercritical carbon dioxide.
AU AL-OTAIBI F
   KOKAL S
   SCHMIDT H
   CHANG Y
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 201870903P
AB    NOVELTY - Stable capsule dispersion comprises: a dispersed phase of many functionalized co-polymerized capsules, where each functionalized co-polymerized capsule contains a dense liquid with a density greater than that of supercritical carbon dioxide in an inner area within a capsule wall and is operable to be stably dispersed within supercritical carbon dioxide, where the capsule wall of each functionalized capsule defines the inner area and has an outer side functionalized with a carbon dioxide-philic compound; and a continuous phase of supercritical carbon dioxide, so that the density of the capsule dispersion is greater than the density of supercritical carbon dioxide at the same conditions and the viscosity of the capsule dispersion is greater than the viscosity of supercritical carbon dioxide at the same conditions.
   USE - The dispersion is useful for enhancing oil recovery.
   ADVANTAGE - The dispersion is stable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the stable capsule dispersion, comprising providing the capsules containing the dense liquid, functionalizing the capsules by adding the carbon dioxide-philic compound to the outer side of the capsule wall to create the functionalized co-polymerized capsules, and preparing the dispersion by adding the functionalized capsules to supercritical carbon dioxide so that the stable dispersion of capsules in supercritical carbon dioxide is achieved.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dense liquid: has a density of at least 0.5 g/cc; and is a dense aqueous liquid, water or heavy liquid filler including toluene, crude oil, ester, silicone oil, alcohols and/or acetone.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The functionalized capsules have a size of 10-1000 nm, preferably 50-250 mu m. The capsule wall of each functionalized capsule is composed of a co-polymer that is the polymerization product of a first co-monomer and a second co-monomer, where the first co-monomer includes triethylene tetramine (preferred), hexamethylene tetramine, ethylene diamine, hexamethylene diamine and/or diethylene triamine, and the second co-monomer includes polymeric diphenylmethane diisocyanate (preferred), isophorone diisocyante, hexamethylene diisocyanate, 1,4 phenylene diisocyanate and/or toluene 2,4 diisocyanate. The carbon dioxide-philic compound comprises: fluorinated carbon dioxide-soluble surfactant or oxygenated hydrocarbon carbon dioxide-philic molecules; and poly (1,1-dihydroperfluoroctyl acrylate). The capsule wall of each functionalized capsule is composed of a hydrogel including gelatin, chitosan, starch, alginate, polyvinyl alcohol, polyethylene oxide, polyvinyl pyrrolidone and/or polyisopropyl acrylamide. Preferred Process: The step of providing the capsules comprises: providing a first liquid, the first liquid having a density greater than the density of supercritical carbon dioxide, adding a first monomer to the first liquid to create a first monomer-liquid composition; providing a second liquid, the second liquid operable to form a liquid phase when combined with the first liquid; adding a second monomer to the second liquid to create a second monomer-liquid composition; adding the first monomer-liquid composition to the second monomer-liquid composition; agitating the first monomer-liquid composition and the second monomer-liquid composition to create a dispersion of first monomer-liquid composition in the second monomer-liquid composition; and allowing the first and second monomers to diffuse and to co-polymerize at a liquid-liquid interface at a pre-determined time such that co-polymerized capsules are formed encapsulating the first liquid.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); A18 (Addition polymers in general.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); 
MC A05-J04; A12-W05; A12-W10B; H01-D06C; H01-D06D

PT P
PN HK30023477-A2
TI Control system for portable greenhouse device, has            controller that outputs control signal to open valve            and release carbon dioxide gas in carbon dioxide            container into greenhouse device, so that carbon            dioxide concentration in greenhouse device at preset            value is maintained.
AU SO S W L
   TAM C C C
   LAU K T
   NG T H
   WONG T H
   LAI K L
AE K11 GROUP LTD (KONE-Non-standard)
GA 2022935614
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an outer cover (240) and a                sensor. The sensor includes a carbon dioxide                sensor, used to measure the concentration of carbon                dioxide in the outer cover. A controller includes a                control circuit for outputting a control signal. A                carbon dioxide container (211) contains carbonate                and acid to generate carbon dioxide gas. A valve                (221) is connected to the carbon dioxide container                for turn on or off according to the control signal                to control the outflow of carbon dioxide gas in the                carbon dioxide container. The controller outputs a                control signal to open the valve and release the                carbon dioxide gas in the carbon dioxide container                into the greenhouse device (200), when the control                system is working, when the carbon dioxide sensor                measures that the carbon dioxide concentration in                the housing of the greenhouse device is lower than                a preset value, so that the carbon dioxide                concentration in the greenhouse device at a preset                value is maintained.
   USE - Control system for portable greenhouse device                (claimed) used for cultivation or seedling of                plants such as temperature-loving vegetables,                flowers, and forests in low temperature season used                in places such as shopping malls/showrooms,                countertops and other places.
   ADVANTAGE - The hygroscopic material absorbs moisture in                the air in the outer cover, so that the humidity in                the outer cover is reduced. The split installation                of the greenhouse device is easy to carry, simple                to install, and easy to operate. The control system                is used in places such as shopping malls/showrooms,                countertops and other places without having to move                plants back and forth. The control system is used                in the greenhouse according to different types of                plants. The device maintains proper light                intensity, humidity and carbon dioxide                concentration, so that an environment most                conducive to the growth of plants is created. The                effect of regularly disinfesting/fertilizing plants                is achieved. The control system monitors and                accurately controls various environmental                parameters in the greenhouse device to create a                suitable micro-environment for the plants placed,                so that a simple and easy plant conservation                solution is provided. The carbon dioxide                concentration in the outer cover at the preset                value is kept. The portable greenhouse device has a                simple structure and is easy to disassemble and                assemble. Plants are placed in it during the                non-display period for on-site/single plant                conservation, which avoids the trouble of regularly                transporting plants to the greenhouse for                maintenance, for a small number of scattered                displays Plants such as flowers at the counter of a                shopping mall, or plants that only need short-term                conservation, provide a convenient and low-cost                conservation method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a structural diagram of the                portable greenhouse device.
   Greenhouse device (200)
   Carbon dioxide container (211)
   Valve (221)
   Main body (230)
   Outer cover (240)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The carbon dioxide container contains sodium                  bicarbonate and citric acid.
DC P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); T06 (Process and Machine Control)

PN WO2022015185-A1
TI System for capturing carbon dioxide from ambient air, comprises filter device that captures carbon dioxide from ambient air after temperature of ambient air is reduced by air conditioning system.
AU AMHAMED A
   SODIQ A
AE QATAR FOUND EDUCATION SCI & COMMUNITY (QATA-Non-standard)
GA 202213802L
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) comprises an input port (110) that is configured to accept ambient air into the system. An air conditioning system (120) draws the ambient air into the input port, and reduces a temperature of the ambient air. A filter device (130) captures carbon dioxide from the ambient air after the temperature of the ambient air is reduced by the air conditioning system. An output port (140) discharges the ambient air from the system after the ambient air is processed with the filter device. The filter device is configured to be regenerated to release the captured carbon dioxide, when the filter device is saturated with the captured carbon dioxide.
   USE - System for capturing carbon dioxide from ambient air in air conditioning system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has low operating cost and high energy efficiency of regeneration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for capturing carbon dioxide from ambient air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic diagram of the system for capturing carbon dioxide.
   System for capturing carbon dioxide (100)
   Input port (110)
   Air conditioning system (120)
   Filter device (130)
   Output port (140)

PT P
PN CN111403636-A; CN111403636-B
TI Soft case for batteries, has transparent identification portion that is composed of resin layer and carbon dioxide response layer, and highest or lowest critical temperature of film is changed after polymer film absorbs carbon dioxide.
AU LI Z
   FENG Y
   HE H
   ZHOU K
   WANG D
   YANG F
AE SUZHOU QINGTAO NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 2020664636
AB    NOVELTY - The case has a transparent identification portion that is composed of a resin layer and a carbon dioxide response layer. The carbon dioxide response layer is composed of a polymer film that is made to react with carbon dioxide. The polymer film is provided with the highest critical temperature or the lowest critical temperature. The polymer film is composed of carbon dioxide responsive monomer and co-soluble monomer polymerization. The highest critical temperature or the lowest critical temperature of the polymer film is changed after the polymer film absorbs carbon dioxide.
   USE - Soft case for batteries of soft-pack type lithium battery(claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The preliminary judgments on battery performance and status are made which greatly reduces the procedures and workload of battery testing. The carbon dioxide generated during the use of the battery is absorbed after the carbon dioxide responsive film is installed thus the performance of the battery is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a soft-pack type lithium battery; and
   (2) a method for preparing soft-pack type lithium battery.

PT P
PN CN109803239-A
TI ZigBee technology based linked humidity and carbon dioxide sensor, has power supply module with data processing module, wireless ZigBee module, temperature and humidity collecting module and carbon dioxide collecting module.
AU TAO Y
   LU Y
   XUE M
AE SUZHOU GLENWEI INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO (SUZH-Non-standard)
GA 201947285V
AB    NOVELTY - The sensor has a data processing module for receiving data collected by a temperature and humidity collecting module and a carbon dioxide collecting module. The data processing module processes and analyzes received data and sends processed data to a wireless ZigBee module. The wireless ZigBee module transmits processed data to a background terminal in a ZigBee protocol format. A power supply module is connected with the data processing module, the wireless ZigBee module, the temperature and humidity collecting module and the carbon dioxide collecting module. The temperature and humidity collecting module adopts an MEMS humidity sensor. The carbon dioxide collecting module adopts a carbon dioxide concentration sensor.
   USE - ZigBee technology based linked humidity and carbon dioxide sensor.
   ADVANTAGE - The sensor monitors temperature, humidity and carbon dioxide concentration information of a crops growing environment continuously, discovers abnormal condition in a timely manner, ensures agricultural production safety performance, realizes agricultural production management process in a fine manner, and reduces investment cost.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a ZigBee technology based linked humidity and carbon dioxide sensor. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

PT P
PN WO2022103631-A1; US2022154563-A1
TI Reducing water production from hydrocarbon bearing            subterranean formation by identifying high permeability            zone in formation, injecting dense carbondioxide            composition and withdrawing hydrocarbons, where            composition blocks pores in high permeability zone to            reduce flow of water into production well.
AU ALYOUSIF Z
   ALMAJID M M
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202268907V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for reducing water production from a                hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation involves                identifying a high permeability zone in the                hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation,                injecting a dense carbon dioxide composition from a                production well, where the dense carbon dioxide                composition comprises dense carbon dioxide and a                thickener soluble in the dense carbon dioxide, the                thickener comprises a copolymer, the copolymer                being the polymerized reaction product of monomers                that include alkenyl ether or dialkenyl ether                monomer, acrylate or methacrylate monomer,                structural monomer, and allyl ester monomer, and                after injecting the dense carbon dioxide                composition into the high permeability zone,                withdrawing hydrocarbons from the hydrocarbon                bearing subterranean formation through the                production well, where the dense carbon dioxide                composition blocks pores in the high permeability                zone to reduce or prevent flow of water from the                high permeability zone into the production                well.
   USE - The method is used for reducing water                production from a hydrocarbon bearing subterranean                formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can control water production from                hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation using                dense carbon dioxide compositions, provides a                pathway for breakthrough of aqueous injection                fluids from water flooding enhanced oil recovery                processes at the production well, improves water                flooding sweep efficiency, increases recovery of                hydrocarbon production, provides a beneficial                subterranean use for carbon dioxide to reduce                carbon dioxide in the atmosphere, improves natural                water resource preservation and is cost                effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for reducing water production from a                hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation involves                identifying a high permeability zone in the                hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation,                injecting a dense carbon dioxide composition from a                production well into the high permeability zone,                where the dense carbon dioxide composition                comprises dense carbon dioxide and a thickener                soluble in the dense carbon dioxide, the thickener                comprises a copolymer, the copolymer being the                polymerized reaction product of monomers that                comprise alkenyl ether or dialkenyl ether monomer,                acrylate or methacrylate monomer, structural                monomer, and allyl ester monomer, and after                injecting the dense carbon dioxide composition into                the high permeability zone, and withdrawing                hydrocarbons from the hydrocarbon bearing                subterranean formation through the production well,                where the dense carbon dioxide composition blocks                pores in the high permeability zone to reduce or                prevent flow of water from the high permeability                zone into the production well.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dense carbon                  dioxide is supercritical carbon dioxide. The                  thickener further comprises co-solvent that                  comprises propylene carbonate, allyl ethyl                  carbonate, dimethyl carbonate, white oil,                  silicone oil, petroleum ether, or their                  combinations. Preferred Composition: The dense                  carbon dioxide composition comprises 0.05-3 wt.%                  thickener based on the total weight of the dense                  carbon dioxide composition. The copolymer of the                  thickener comprises an average molecular weight                  of 500000-800000 grams per mole. The method,                  where 1 wt.% of the thickener dissolves in                  supercritical carbon dioxide in less than or                  equal to 3 minutes at 25 degrees C. Preferred                  Properties: The thickener has a pH of 5-8 and/or                  a specific gravity of 0.9-1.1. The thickener                  increases the viscosity of the dense carbon                  dioxide by at least 100 times the viscosity of                  the dense carbon dioxide without the thickener.                  The dense carbon dioxide composition has a                  viscosity of 50-150 millipascal seconds. The                  dense carbon dioxide composition reduces the                  permeability of carbonate rock by greater than or                  equal to 50%. Preferred Method: The dense carbon                  dioxide composition is injected at a pressure of                  900-3000 pounds per square inch, a temperature of                  20-150 degrees C or both. The method involves                  injecting the dense carbon dioxide composition                  comprises isolating a portion of the production                  well in fluid communication with the high                  permeability zone from other portions of the                  production well before injecting the dense carbon                  dioxide composition into the high permeability                  zone. The method involves isolating the portion                  of the production well in fluid communication                  with the high permeability zone from other                  portions of the production well comprises                  installing one or more temporary plugs in the                  production well, where the temporary plugs are                  disposed in the production well down hole of the                  high permeability zone or both up hole and down                  hole of the high permeability zone and after                  injecting the dense carbon dioxide composition,                  removing isolation of the portion of the                  production well in fluid communication with the                  high permeability zone and resuming production of                  hydrocarbons from the production well. The method                  involves injecting an aqueous treatment fluid                  from an injection well into the hydrocarbon                  bearing subterranean formation, where the                  injection well is spaced apart from the                  production well, and the injecting of the aqueous                  treatment fluid drives hydrocarbons in the                  hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation to the                  production well.

   (1) a method for making a fabric for removing carbon dioxide from a gas containing the carbon dioxide, which involves providing spherical capsules, providing a polymer surface layer on spherical capsules, where polymer surface layer is permeable to carbon dioxide, providing stripping solvents encapsulated within polymer surface layer of spherical capsules, where polymer surface layer absorbs the carbon dioxide and connecting spherical capsules with a web of support material to make the fabric;

PN US2018222761-A1; US10315926-B2
TI Generating lithium from natural brine, comprises e.g. heating natural brine in first vessel, injecting carbon dioxide gas into first vessel, holding mixture for first predetermined time, and screening out solid impurities from the resultant.
AU NAKANO J
   NAKANO A
   BENNETT J P
AE US DEPT ENERGY (USAT-C)
GA 2018614669
AB    NOVELTY - Generating lithium from natural brine, comprises: heating natural brine in first vessel to first predetermined temperature; injecting carbon dioxide gas into first vessel; holding mixture for first predetermined time after carbon dioxide injection so that impurities in natural brine precipitate as solids leaving second brine comprising dissolved lithium ions and dissolved chlorine ions; screening out solid precipitate impurities from second brine; heating second brine to second predetermined temperature; injecting carbon dioxide gas into second brine, where the carbon dioxide gas mixes with second brine forming second mixture so that carbon dioxide/phosphorus is greater than 50 g/atm; holding second mixture for second predetermined time after carbon dioxide injection so that chlorine is suppressed as dissolved ions while lithium ions precipitate out as lithium carbonate as only solid in second mixture; and removing lithium carbonate precipitate.
   USE - The method is useful for generating lithium from natural brine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method generates solid lithium carbonate that requires less time, energy and waste, and is produced at a lower cost as compared to conventional methods.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Generating lithium from a natural brine, comprises: heating a natural brine in a first vessel to a first predetermined temperature; injecting a carbon dioxide gas into the first vessel, where the carbon dioxide gas mixes with the natural brine forming a mixture so that the carbon dioxide/phosphorus is greater than 6 g/atm; holding the mixture for a first predetermined time after the carbon dioxide injection so that impurities in the natural brine precipitate as solids leaving a second brine comprising dissolved lithium ions and dissolved chlorine ions; screening out the solid precipitate impurities from the second brine; heating the second brine to a second predetermined temperature; injecting carbon dioxide gas into the second brine, where the carbon dioxide gas mixes with the second brine forming a second mixture so that the carbon dioxide/phosphorus is greater than 50 g/atm; holding the second mixture for a second predetermined time after the carbon dioxide injection so that chlorine is suppressed as dissolved ions while lithium ions precipitate out as lithium carbonate as the only solid in the second mixture; and removing the lithium carbonate precipitate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The first predetermined temperature is at least 200 degrees C, preferably greater than or equal to 260 degrees C. The carbon dioxide/phosphorus of the first mixture is greater than 18 g/atm. The first predetermined time is longer than 3 minutes, preferably longer than 20 minutes. The step of screening comprises moving the mixture into a second vessel. The step of screening comprises filtering the mixture through a sieve. The second predetermined temperature is equal to the first predetermined temperature. The second predetermined temperature is at least 200 degrees C. The second predetermined temperature is greater than or equal to 260 degrees C. The carbon dioxide/phosphorus of the second mixture is greater than 200 g/atm. The second predetermined time is at least 3 minutes, preferably at least 20 minutes. All of the lithium from the natural brine is recovered as lithium carbonate with a greater than 99% purity of the recovered lithium.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Carbon dioxide collection system used for small-scale carbon dioxide collection, comprises carbon dioxide collection devices having sorbent bed having sorbent resin, blower with sorbent bed through duct, collection tray beneath sorbent bed.
AU LACKNER K
AE ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
   LACKNER K (LACK-Individual)
   LACKNER K (LACK-Individual)
GA 2020A0298B
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide collection system comprises many carbon dioxide collection devices (100), each device comprising sorbent bed comprising sorbent resin, blower in fluid communication with sorbent bed through at least one duct, a collection tray beneath sorbent bed, where the collection tray comprises fluid drain, air intake (102) and air exhaust (104) in fluid communication with air intake through sorbent bed, the duct, and blower, capture configuration comprising air flow driven by blower and flowing from air intake to air exhaust and passing through sorbent resin of sorbent bed, the duct, and the blower, and regeneration configuration comprising the sorbent bed submerged in regeneration fluid, where moving the device from the capture configuration to regeneration configuration comprises flooding at least the sorbent resin of the sorbent bed with the regeneration fluid having a first dissolved inorganic carbon (DIC) concentration.
   USE - The system, device and method are useful for small-scale carbon dioxide collection (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system, device and method: remove carbon dioxide from ambient air; provide reduced-carbon processes, and on-site capture efforts, a significant quantity of carbon dioxide will need to be removed from the atmosphere to avoid a looming climate change crisis; are able to satisfy the need of a small-scale application without great expense or overhead; use of a simple design that is robust and efficient; are able to operate at this scale is advantageous for smaller scale carbon dioxide applications that often occur outside of industrial contexts; allow for gathering and upgrading of carbon dioxide on-site for a small-scale application, rather than relying on outside sources; and utilize the configuration that is that the air space below the sorbent bed that remains accessible for other gas flow, thus making it easy to operate multiple beds on a single return duct, in a continuous fashion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) a carbon dioxide collection device; and
   (2) small-scale carbon dioxide collection.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a perspective view of the device for small-scale carbon dioxide collection.
   Carbon dioxide collection devices (100)
   Air intake (102)
   Air exhaust (104)
   Air filter (106)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system comprises many carbon dioxide collection devices, each device comprising a sorbent bed comprising a sorbent resin, a blower in fluid communication with the sorbent bed through at least one duct, a collection tray beneath the sorbent bed, where the collection tray comprises a fluid drain, an air intake and an air exhaust in fluid communication with the air intake through the sorbent bed, the duct, and the blower, a capture configuration comprising an air flow driven by the blower and flowing from the air intake to the air exhaust and passing through the sorbent resin of the sorbent bed, the duct, and the blower, and a regeneration configuration comprising the sorbent bed submerged in a regeneration fluid, where moving the device from the capture configuration to the regeneration configuration comprises flooding at least the sorbent resin of the sorbent bed with the regeneration fluid having a first dissolved inorganic carbon (DIC) concentration, the regeneration fluid introduced to the sorbent resin via a liquid input and preventing the air flow from passing through the sorbent resin and moving the device from the regeneration configuration to the capture configuration comprises removing the regeneration fluid collected in the collection tray through the fluid drain in the collection tray, and the regeneration fluid passes through the fluid drain having a second DIC concentration higher than the first DIC concentration.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The sorbent resin is a moisture swing carbon dioxide sorbent. The regeneration fluid is one of a hydroxide solution, a carbonate solution or a bicarbonate solution.

PN WO2020002535-A1; GB2575249-A; IN202137002733-A; EP3813998-A1; CN112752609-A; US2021252481-A1
TI Preparing pellets of sorbent used for e.g. carbon dioxide capture, by calcining starting material comprising dolomite, mixing base material with water and additives, processing resulting mixture, and calcining intermediate pellets.
AU CHEN D
   HE L
   ROUT K
   BLEKKAN E
   STRAND A
AE AMTECH AS (AMTE-Non-standard)
   AMTECH AS (AMTE-Non-standard)
   AMTECH AS (AMTE-Non-standard)
GA 2020023384
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing pellets of sorbent for carbon dioxide capture, involves (a) calcining a starting material comprising dolomite to obtain a base material, (b) mixing the base material with water and additives comprising additive (A) and additive (B), and processing the resulting mixture to provide an intermediate pellets, and (c) calcining the intermediate pellets to provide the pellets of sorbent, where the additive (A) is a source of metal ions (I) comprising ions of aluminum or magnesium, and the additive (B) is a source of metal ions (II) comprising ions of aluminum, magnesium, transition metal or lanthanide, and the (I) and (II) metal ions are not both ions of aluminum or both ions of magnesium.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing pellets of sorbent is useful for carbon dioxide capture, post-combustion carbon dioxide capture, pre-combustion carbon dioxide capture from hydrogen and carbon dioxide gas mixture or capturing of sulfur-containing compounds, preferably capturing sulfur dioxide and/or hydrogen sulfide from sour gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the mechanical strength of the pellets of sorbent, and the prepared pellets of sorbent has high and excellent carbon dioxide capture performance from dolomite, which is naturally occurring and economical raw material, retains acceptable level of carbon dioxide capture capacity, and is conveniently produced using one-pot process and environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a pellets of sorbent used for carbon dioxide capture, which are obtained by the above-mentioned method;
   (2) a sorbent used for carbon dioxide capture, comprising calcium oxide, magnesium oxide, 0.5-20 wt.% metal ions (I) and 0.5-10 wt.% metal ions (II), where the sorbent is in the form of pellets;
   (3) a method for carbon dioxide capture, involving exposing the above-mentioned sorbent to carbon dioxide under conditions suitable for carbon dioxide capture, and providing a carbonated sorbent comprising the captured carbon dioxide; and
   (4) a carbonated sorbent comprising carbonated calcium oxide, magnesium oxide, 0.4-20 wt.% metal ions (I) and 0.4-10 wt.% metal ions (II).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step (a) involves calcining at 700-1200 degrees C, preferably 800-900 degrees C for 2-12 hours. The step (b) involves carrying out the mixing and processing to provide intermediate pellets in the same container for 5 minutes to 10 hours, preferably 20 minutes to 4 hours, which occur simultaneously or sequentially, preferably simultaneously. The step (b) involves mixing one or more additives with the base material, water, additive (A) and additive (B) in step (b), preferably where the one or more further additives are chosen from sources of metal ions other than the metal ions (I) and metal ions (II), or the one or more further additives do not contain metal ions. The step (c) involves directly using the intermediate pellets in step (c) without any intervening processing, or subjecting the intermediate pellets to intervening processing, such as sieving and/or spheronization, prior to step (c), and calcining at 700-1200 degrees C, preferably 800-1000 degrees C, for 2-12 hours. 23. The method further involves regenerating the sorbent by calcining the carbonated sorbent. Preferred Properties: The prepared base material has a surface area of 0.5-100 m2/g, preferably 2-40 m2/g. The formed intermediate pellets are substantially spherical having a diameter of 50-6000 mu m, preferably 500-3000 mu m, substantially cylindrical having the diameter of 500-5000 mu m, preferably 850-3000 mu m or in honeycomb form having a wall thickness of 500-5000 mu m, preferably 850-3000 mu m. Preferred Composition: The starting material in step (a) comprises 80 wt.%, preferably 90 wt.% dolomite. The molar ratio of metal ions (I) to metal ions (II) in the pellets of sorbent is 20-1, preferably 10-2. The metal ions (I) comprises 0.5-20 wt.%, preferably 2-10 wt.% pellets of sorbent and/or the metal ions (II) comprises 0.5-10 wt%, preferably 0.5-6 wt% pellets of sorbent. Preferred Device: The mixing and/or processing of step (b) is conducted by one or more of (i) shear force supplied by a manual or motor-driven impellor, (ii) centrifugal force supplied by a rotary container, (iii) extrusion force, and (iv) agitation forced by flowing gas. Preferred Components: The additive (I) and additive (II) added in step (b) as a solid or dissolved in an aqueous solution. The source of ions of aluminum comprises aluminum oxide, aluminum chloride, aluminum nitrate, calcium aluminate or their mixtures. The source of ions of magnesium comprises magnesium oxide, magnesium nitrate, magnesium chloride or their mixtures. The transition metal comprises zirconium, where the source of ions of zirconium comprises zirconium oxide, zirconium tetrachloride, zirconyl nitrate or their mixtures. The lanthanide comprises cerium, where the source of ions of cerium is dicerium trioxide, cerium nitrate, cerium trichloride or their mixtures. The additive (A) is a source of ions of aluminum and the additive (B) is a source of ions of magnesium, (ii) the additive (A) is a source of ions of aluminum and the additive (B) is a source of ions of zirconium, (iii) the additive (A) is a source of ions of aluminum and the additive (B) is a source of ions of cerium, (iv) the additive (A) is a source of ions of magnesium and the additive (B) is a source of ions of aluminum, (v) the additive (A) is a source of ions of magnesium and the additive (B) is a source of ions of zirconium, or (vi) the additive (A) is a source of ions of magnesium and the additive (B) is a source of ions of cerium.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Portable carbon dioxide absorption device for removing excess carbon dioxide from closed environment, has power supply that is mounted within housing, and that is in electrical communication with blower.
AU RILEY A D
AE ALLIED HEALTHCARE PROD INC (ALHE-Non-standard)
   ALLIED HEALTHCARE PROD INC (ALHE-Non-standard)
   ALLIED HEALTHCARE PROD INC (ALHE-Non-standard)

GA 2018A41788
AB    NOVELTY - The absorption device (200) has an exterior housing (700) with an inlet (120) and an outlet (210). A blower is in fluid communication with the inlet and with an expansion manifold (300). The blower is mounted within the housing and draws air into the exterior housing through the inlet. The expansion manifold is provided with a manifold housing defining an expansion chamber. The expansion chamber is mounted within the housing, filters and reduces a velocity of air received from the blower by expanding a control volume traversing the portable carbon dioxide absorption device. A carbon dioxide absorbing apparatus is mounted within the housing, and is in fluid communication with the expansion manifold and the outlet. The absorbing apparatus is provided to absorb carbon dioxide from the air exiting the expansion manifold. A power supply is mounted within the housing. The power supply is in electrical communication with the blower. The derivatives of ammonia is monoethanolamine.
   USE - Portable carbon dioxide absorption device for removing excess carbon dioxide from closed environments such as hospitals, laboratories, airplanes, and military applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The device operates automatically to continuously monitor and reduce the concentration of carbon dioxide in an environment. The portable carbon dioxide absorption machine (PCDMA) is turned on during de-icing and engine starting events, and reduce the number of delayed shipments, and increase the overall air transport dry ice capacity in which employed. The PCDAM is conveniently and easily placed in the cockpit and supernumerary and operated even when the plane is powered down, without the airplane's power packs or network, to maintain safe carbon dioxide concentration levels. The potential matter is prevented from being emitted to the surrounding environment through the outlet portion.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for automatically reducing concentration of carbon dioxide in enclosed environment using portable carbon dioxide absorption device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front perspective view of the portable carbon dioxide absorption system with portions of the exterior housing removed.

PT P
PN CN115107933-A
TI Carbon dioxide transport ship, has filter cover            provided with filter hole for filtering solid carbon            dioxide, where middle part of filter cover is provided            with through hole for liquid cargo pipe to pass            through.
AU WANG L
   LIU W
AE JIANGNAN SHIPYARD GROUP CO LTD (CHSB-C)
GA 2022C64954
AB    NOVELTY - The ship has a hull, and a cargo tank. A                bottom (3) of the cargo tank is provided with a                liquid accumulation well (2), and a cargo pump (1)                on the hull. The liquid cargo pump is connected                with a liquid cargo pipe (4) extending into the                liquid accumulation well. A filter cover (5) is                arranged in the liquid cargo tank. The filter cover                is covered at a wellhead (21) of the liquid                accumulation well, and is provided with filter                holes for filtering solid carbon dioxide. The                middle of the filter cover is provided with a                liquid cargo pipe perforation through which the                liquid cargo pipe passes. One of the cargo pipe and                the bottom of the cargo tank is fixed to the filter                cover.
   USE - Carbon dioxide transport ship.
   ADVANTAGE - The liquid cargo tank of carbon dioxide                transport ship in the application model is provided                with a filter cover, the filter hole on the filter                cover is used for filtering solid carbon dioxide,                it can reduce or even avoid solid carbon dioxide                entering into the liquid cargo pump, the liquid                carbon dioxide pump is not easy to be damage. The                filter cover has a large area, the cover opening                size is greater than the well opening size of the                liquid well, and the volume of the cover is not                limited, it will not affect the flow supply of the                carbon dioxide pump. The carbon dioxide transport                ship solves the technical problem that the solid                carbon dioxide doped in the liquid cargo tank is                easy to enter into the liquid cargo pump to cause                the liquid cargo pump damage the current carbon                dioxide transport ship is unloading the                cargo.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                installation structure of a liquid cargo pump and a                filter cover in the carbon dioxide transport                ship.
   1cargo pump
   2liquid accumulation well
   3Bottom of the cargo tank
   4liquid cargo pipe
   5filter cover
   21Wellhead

MC Q24-B02C; Q24-B02A

PT P
PN US2021041387-A1
TI Composite film used to configure carbon dioxide sensing, comprises carbon nanotube film and carbon dioxide absorbing layer i.e. deposited on carbon nanotube film.
AU RHOADS J F
   CHIU G T
   BOUDOURIS B W
   BAJAJ N
   MURRAY A K
   SIEFKER Z A
   ZHAO X
AE PURDUE RES FOUND (PURD-C)
GA 2021209888
AB    NOVELTY - Composite film comprises a carbon nanotube film and a carbon dioxide absorbing layer i.e. deposited on the carbon nanotube film, where the carbon dioxide absorbing layer comprises a mixture of a branched polyethylenimine, a polyethylene glycol, and poly(1-(4-vinylbenzyl)-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate) of formula (I).
   USE - The composite film is useful for configuring carbon dioxide sensing (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite film: provides another class of sensor i.e. chemiresistive sensors, which has significant promise for low-cost and low-power sensing; and provides a carbon dioxide sensor that can sense the gas at a lower cost with lower power, when compared to traditional detection methods.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Composite film comprises a carbon nanotube film and a carbon dioxide absorbing layer deposited on the carbon nanotube film, where the carbon dioxide absorbing layer comprises a mixture of a branched polyethylenimine, a polyethylene glycol, and poly(1-(4-vinylbenzyl)-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate) of formula (I).
   n = 10-300.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The thickness of the carbon nanotube film is 10-500 nm, and the carbon dioxide absorbing layer is 100-1000 nm. The carbon nanotube film comprises chlorosulfonic acid. The branched polyethylenimine has a molar molecular weight of 10 kg/mol, where the branched polyethylene glycol has a molar molecular weight of 0.5-50 kg/mol. The carbon dioxide absorbing layer comprises: 40-60 wt.% branched polyethylenimine; 40-60 wt.% polyethylene glycol; and 1-5 wt.% poly(1-(4-vinylbenzyl)-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate).
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109163880-A; CN109163880-B
TI Carbon dioxide saturated solution forming and            discharging process simulating method, involves            conveying carbon dioxide gas in carbon dioxide gas            bottle to pressure container, and opening drain valve            to discharge water in visible container.
AU LV X
   CUI Z
   ZHU J
   ZHANG W
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
   UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 2019063801
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves continuously increasing                pressure in a visible container. A video container                side exhaust valve is opened when an upper pressure                sensor measures pressure of the visible container                reaches a predetermined value. Carbon dioxide gas                in a carbon dioxide gas bottle is conveyed to a                pressure container through a pipeline. A carbon                dioxide gas inlet valve and a communication valve                are opened. A side vent valve of the visible                container is opened when pressure in the visible                container is reduced to normal pressure. A drain                valve is opened for discharging water in the                visible container.
   USE - Carbon dioxide saturated solution forming and                discharging process simulating method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing observing                operation in an effective manner and increasing                operability.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a carbon                dioxide saturated solution forming and discharging                process simulating device.
